Table crop.growing_season {
  growing_season_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each growing season record. Serves as the primary key for the growing_season data product and is referenced by all crop production activity records (planting, cultivation, harvest, loss events) as the temporal anchor.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: The growing season defines which commodity is being produced — driving enterprise budgeting, crop insurance policy selection, and USDA ARC/PLC program enrollment. commodity_id normalizes the denormali']
  contract_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Contract farming: a growing season is planned and executed for a specific buyer account (food manufacturer, grain elevator) under a forward supply contract. Links season-level organic, GMO, and variet']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Growing seasons are the top-level cost accumulation unit in farm ERP. Overhead allocation, input costs, and labor costs are rolled up to the season\'s cost center. Farm financial managers require this ']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A growing season is planned and executed for a specific crop type (e.g., Spring 2024 Corn). The crop master table is the authoritative reference for crop species, maturity windows, PHI/REI defaults, n']
  double_crop_parent_season_id varchar [note: 'For double-crop seasons, references the parent (primary) growing season that precedes this season on the same land area. Enables sequential crop tracking for double-crop rotations common in southern US (e.g., winter wheat followed by soybean). Null for primary or single-crop seasons.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agronomist or crop advisor responsible for managing agronomic decisions during this growing season. Links to the workforce record in SAP SuccessFactors. Used for accountability tracking, GAP audit documentation, and FMIS user assignment in Climate FieldView and Trimble Ag Software.']
  farm_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_operation. Business justification: USDA FSA reporting, farm operation P&L, and organic certification tracking require linking each growing season to its farm operation. Farm managers plan seasons at the operation level before assigning']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary farm or agricultural operation entity under which this growing season is conducted. Links to the farm master record in Granular Farm Management and USDA FSA farm registration. Supports multi-farm enterprise reporting and land management hierarchy.']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Season-level quality grade targets (e.g., USDA Grade 1 food corn vs. feed grade) are set during season planning and govern all downstream harvest quality decisions. A domain expert expects the season ']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic growing seasons must be traceable to the specific NOP organic certification record for USDA NOP compliance audits, organic premium pricing, and annual renewal tracking. organic_certification_i']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Growing seasons map to profit centers for segment-level P&L reporting (e.g., corn 2024 vs. soybean 2024). Farm financial reporting and GAAP segment disclosure require season-level revenue and cost att']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the growing season was formally closed, representing completion of the final harvest event and post-harvest handling. Captured from Climate FieldView harvest data or SAP S/4HANA PP production order closure. Used for season-over-season performance benchmarking.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which field operations for the growing season commenced (first planting event recorded). Captured from John Deere Operations Center telematics or Climate FieldView planting data. Used to measure planting timeliness against planned window and assess weather-related delays.']
  actual_yield_bu_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Realized yield for this growing season expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre, captured from harvest data in Climate FieldView or John Deere Operations Center yield monitors. Compared against target_yield_bu_acre for performance analysis. Used in USDA NASS yield surveys, crop insurance settlement, and FMIS season benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this growing season record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Establishes the audit trail for record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial reporting controls and GDPR data lineage requirements.']
  crop_insurance_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of USDA RMA crop insurance or farm program coverage elected for this growing season. APH = Actual Production History, RP = Revenue Protection, RP_HPE = Revenue Protection with Harvest Price Exclusion, YP = Yield Protection, ARC = Agriculture Risk Coverage, PLC = Price Loss Coverage. Determines indemnity calculation methodology and premium rates.']
  crop_year varchar [note: 'The marketing or production year associated with this growing season, expressed as a four-digit calendar year (e.g., 2024). Used for WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates) alignment, USDA FSA program reporting, and financial crop enterprise budget year-over-year comparisons.']
  fsma_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this growing season\'s production activities are documented and compliant with FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Produce Safety Rule requirements, including water testing, worker hygiene, and equipment sanitation records. Mandatory for covered produce operations under FDA jurisdiction.']
  gap_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current GAP (Good Agricultural Practices) certification status for this growing season. Indicates whether the production practices meet USDA GAP audit standards, GLOBALG.A.P. certification requirements, or buyer-mandated food safety standards (SQF, BRC, IFS). Required for retail and food manufacturer customer contracts.']
  gdd_target decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target cumulative GDD (Growing Degree Days) required for the primary crop variety to reach maturity during this growing season. Used to project harvest timing, align PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance windows, and calibrate ET (Evapotranspiration)-based irrigation scheduling. Sourced from seed company variety data and NOAA climate records.']
  ipm_program_code varchar [note: 'Reference code identifying the IPM (Integrated Pest Management) program or protocol applied during this growing season. Links to the crop protection plan governing pesticide applications, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) monitoring, and EPA pesticide registration requirements. Aligns with Trimble Ag Software scouting protocols.']
  irrigation_method varchar [note: 'Primary irrigation system used during this growing season. Determines ET (Evapotranspiration)-based water scheduling approach, water use efficiency reporting, and compliance with state water rights regulations. \'rainfed\' indicates no supplemental irrigation. Integrated with IoT (Internet of Things) soil moisture sensors and FMIS irrigation scheduling modules.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target crop variety for this season is a GMO (Genetically Modified Organism). Critical for USDA APHIS (Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service) regulatory compliance, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) requirements, export documentation, and customer segregation requirements for non-GMO supply chains.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this growing season is conducted under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified organic production standards. Triggers organic-specific input restrictions, record-keeping requirements, and supply chain segregation protocols. Linked to organic certificate management in compliance systems.']
  ndvi_baseline_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) baseline score established at the start of the growing season, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Derived from satellite or drone imagery processed through Esri ArcGIS or Climate FieldView. Used as the reference benchmark for in-season crop health monitoring, stress detection, and VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for agronomic observations, management decisions, weather events, or operational notes relevant to this growing season. Captures qualitative context not represented in structured fields, such as drought stress events, pest outbreak narratives, or regulatory inspection outcomes. Supports FMIS audit trail documentation.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days required between the last pesticide application and harvest for this growing season\'s primary crop, as mandated by EPA pesticide label requirements (PHI — Pre-Harvest Interval). Critical for food safety compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) adherence, and FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) produce safety rule compliance.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The agronomically planned end date for the growing season, representing the expected completion of harvest and post-harvest handling operations. Derived from expected days-to-maturity of the target crop variety and planned planting date. Used for financial crop enterprise budget planning in Granular Farm Management.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The agronomically planned start date for the growing season, typically aligned with the target planting window based on GDD (Growing Degree Days), soil temperature thresholds, and historical climate data. Used in Trimble Ag Software field planning and Climate FieldView planting analytics.']
  planting_method varchar [note: 'Tillage and planting system employed during this growing season. Drives equipment configuration in John Deere Operations Center, soil disturbance impact on OM (Organic Matter) and EC (Electrical Conductivity) measurements, and BMP (Best Management Practices) compliance reporting for USDA EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program) conservation programs.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated fields after pesticide application during this growing season, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (REI — Restricted Entry Interval). Drives workforce scheduling in SAP SuccessFactors and OSHA compliance documentation for field worker safety.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the growing season, used across FMIS systems such as Climate FieldView and Granular Farm Management for cross-system reference. Example: \'SPRING-2024-CORN\', \'FALL-2024-SOY\'. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA PP production order season coding conventions.']
  season_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the growing season, combining season type, year, and primary crop for operational clarity. Example: \'Spring 2024 Corn\', \'Fall 2024 Soybean\', \'Double-Crop 2024 Winter Wheat\'. Used in reporting dashboards and FMIS displays.']
  season_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the growing season. \'planned\' indicates agronomic planning is underway but field operations have not commenced. \'active\' indicates planting through harvest operations are in progress. \'completed\' indicates harvest and post-harvest handling are finalized. \'cancelled\' indicates the season was abandoned (e.g., due to extreme weather or market conditions). \'suspended\' indicates temporary halt due to regulatory or operational constraints.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the growing season by its agronomic timing and production strategy. \'double_crop\' supports sequential planting common in southern US and tropical operations. \'cover_crop\' denotes non-cash-crop seasons for soil health management per BMP (Best Management Practices). Drives planting window scheduling and FMIS season templates.']
  target_crop_variety varchar [note: 'Specific variety or hybrid designation of the primary crop planned for this season (e.g., \'DKC62-08RIB\', \'AG36X6\'). Drives seed procurement, VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescriptions, and yield benchmarking by variety. Aligns with seed catalog codes in SAP S/4HANA MM and Trimble Ag Software variety trials.']
  target_yield_bu_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Agronomically planned yield target for this growing season expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre, representing the YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) goal. Established during crop planning based on historical yield data, soil productivity ratings, and variety performance benchmarks. Used as the baseline for financial crop enterprise budgets and crop insurance APH (Actual Production History).']
  total_actual_area_acres decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total land area actually planted during this growing season, expressed in acres. May differ from planned area due to weather delays, equipment failures, or agronomic decisions. Captured from John Deere Operations Center planting telematics and Climate FieldView as-planted maps. Used for yield calculation (YPM) and insurance reporting.']
  total_planned_area_acres decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total land area planned for production under this growing season, expressed in acres. Aggregated from associated field zone assignments. Used for input planning (seed, fertilizer, crop protection), equipment scheduling, and financial crop enterprise budget calculations in Granular Farm Management. Aligns with FSA farm records.']
  total_yield_mt decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total harvested production volume for the growing season expressed in MT (Metric Tons), converted from bushel-based yield for international trade, export documentation, and supply chain logistics. Used in BOL (Bill of Lading) preparation, CIF/FOB export contracts, and WASDE supply estimates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this growing season record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline, optimistic concurrency control, and audit trail maintenance per SOX and GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping requirements.']

  Note: 'Defines each agricultural growing season (e.g., Spring 2024 Corn, Fall 2024 Soybean) including season type (spring, fall, double-crop, cover crop), start and end dates, target crop, associated field zones, and season status (planned, active, completed). Acts as the temporal anchor for all crop production activities — planting, cultivation, harvest, and loss events — and links agronomic plans to financial crop enterprise budgets. Supports multi-crop and double-crop scenarios common in southern US and tropical operations. Aligns with FMIS season management in Climate FieldView and Granular.'
}

  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A field crop plan is an agronomic plan for a specific crop on a specific field and season. The crop master provides reference data for PHI days, REI hours, nutrient requirements, planting depth, row s']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Contract farming: field crop plans under buyer contracts designate the specific customer receiving facility for planned harvest delivery. Enables pre-season logistics planning, capacity reservation at']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified agronomist responsible for authoring, approving, and overseeing execution of this crop plan. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and GAP compliance documentation.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field for which this crop plan is authored. Links to the field master record containing GIS boundaries, soil classification, and land tenure data.']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: field_crop_plan has gmo_trait_used as plain text but no FK to the formal GMO declaration.   grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Agronomists set the target grading standard at plan creation; this driv']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season (crop year) for which this plan applies. Aligns with USDA crop year definitions and SAP S/4HANA PP planning horizon.']

  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone.   sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Contract farming production planning: field crop plans are often created specifically to fulfill a forward sales contract. Linking enables contract farming management where production plans are tied t']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific crop variety (cultivar) selected for this field and season, including GMO status, maturity rating, and trait profile. Links to the crop variety master in the seed catalog.']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile.   soil_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_sample. Business justification: Field crop plans are built from specific soil test reports; soil_ph_baseline, soil_om_pct, and soil_cec on field_crop_plan are denormalized from soil_sample. Linking to soil_sample enables traceabilit']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the responsible agronomist formally approved this crop plan for execution. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and GAP compliance audit documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this field crop plan record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  globalg_ap_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field crop plan is authored and executed under an active GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification scope. Drives audit documentation requirements and customer supply chain eligibility.']
  gmo_trait_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the selected crop variety contains one or more GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) traits. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling), and export documentation to GMO-restricted markets.']
  irrigation_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of irrigation infrastructure deployed on this field. Determines ET (Evapotranspiration)-based scheduling methodology, water application efficiency, and water allocation compliance reporting.']
  irrigation_target_depth_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned total irrigation water application depth in acre-inches for the season, derived from ET-deficit modeling and soil moisture trigger thresholds. Supports water allocation compliance and USDA NRCS irrigation water management standards.']
  ndvi_baseline decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pre-season NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) baseline value for the field, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Used to establish field health benchmarks for in-season monitoring and VRA prescription calibration. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS or Climate FieldView satellite imagery.']
  nutrient_setback_zone_ft decimal(7,2) [note: 'Minimum buffer distance in feet from water bodies, drainage ditches, and sensitive areas within which nutrient applications are restricted or prohibited. Defined per EPA nutrient management regulations and state BMP requirements. Captured in Esri ArcGIS environmental layer.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crop plan is executed under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification, restricting inputs to approved organic materials and requiring organic system plan documentation.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days that must elapse between the last pesticide application and harvest, as mandated by EPA pesticide label requirements. Critical for MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) compliance and food safety certification under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.']
  plan_date date [note: 'The business date on which this crop plan was formally created and entered into the Farm Management Information System (FMIS). Distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the crop plan, used in Trimble Ag Software, Climate FieldView, and SAP S/4HANA PP module for cross-system reference and traceability. Format: FCP-{YEAR}-{ALPHANUMERIC}.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the field crop plan. Draft: under preparation; Approved: signed off by agronomist; Active: in-season execution underway; Completed: season closed; Cancelled: plan voided; Superseded: replaced by a revised plan version.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for the crop plan. Incremented each time the plan is revised and re-approved, supporting audit trail and change management requirements under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.']
  planned_area_acres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total field area in acres designated for crop production under this plan. Used for input quantity calculations, yield projections, and USDA FSA acreage reporting. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS field boundary data.']
  planned_harvest_date date [note: 'Target calendar date for harvest operations to commence on this field. Drives downstream supply chain scheduling, storage capacity planning, and logistics coordination via SAP S/4HANA SD and TMS.']
  planned_planting_date date [note: 'Target calendar date for planting operations to commence on this field, as defined in the crop plan. Used for scheduling labor, equipment, and input procurement in SAP S/4HANA PP and Trimble Ag Software.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated fields after pesticide application, as specified on EPA-registered pesticide labels. Required for OSHA worker protection standard compliance and GLOBALG.A.P. worker safety documentation.']
  row_spacing_inches decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned distance between crop rows in inches, as specified in the planting prescription. Affects canopy closure, weed suppression, and machinery configuration. Integrated with Trimble Ag Software VRA prescriptions.']
  soil_test_date date [note: 'Date on which soil samples were collected for the baseline soil test underpinning this crop plan\'s nutrient management recommendations. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and EPA nutrient management compliance documentation.']
  target_k_rate_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned potassium (K) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, based on soil test K levels and crop removal rates. Part of the NPK nutrient management plan. Influences crop quality, disease resistance, and water use efficiency.']
  target_n_rate_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned nitrogen (N) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, derived from soil test results and yield goal. Core component of the NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium) nutrient management plan. Must comply with EPA nutrient management regulations and state BMP requirements.']
  target_p_rate_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned phosphorus (P) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, based on soil test P levels and crop removal rates. Part of the NPK nutrient management plan. Subject to EPA and state environmental setback zone restrictions.']
  target_plant_population varchar [note: 'Planned seeding rate expressed as seeds per acre. Drives seed quantity procurement, VRA prescription generation, and yield modeling. Sourced from Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView planting prescriptions.']
  tillage_method varchar [note: 'Planned soil tillage approach for land preparation prior to planting. Influences soil health, erosion risk, fuel consumption, and BMP compliance. Captured in Trimble Ag Software field planning module.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this field crop plan record was most recently modified. Supports change management, version control, and audit trail requirements under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.']
  water_allocation_limit_acre_ft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted water volume in acre-feet that may be applied to this field during the season, as defined by water rights permits or irrigation district allocations. Enforced for regulatory compliance with state water authorities.']
  yield_target_bu_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Expected crop yield expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre for the planned field and season. Serves as the primary agronomic performance benchmark for YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) reporting and WASDE comparison. Null if crop is measured in MT or CWT.']
  yield_target_mt_hectare decimal(8,2) [note: 'Expected crop yield expressed in MT (Metric Tons) per hectare, used for international reporting and crops not measured in bushels. Complements BU/acre target for global supply chain and export documentation.']

  Note: 'Comprehensive agronomic plan for a specific field and growing season, serving as the single operational blueprint for all planned crop activities. Consolidates all sub-planning disciplines into one authoritative document. Captures: (1) Crop selection — intended crop variety, target planting population (seeds/acre), row spacing, tillage method. (2) Nutrient management — target NPK removal rates, soil test baseline (pH, NPK, CEC, OM), recommended fertilizer products and rates, application timing windows, environmental setback zones, EPA nutrient management compliance. (3) Irrigation strategy — ET-deficit and soil moisture trigger thresholds, target application depth (acre-inches), water allocation limits, irrigation system type. (4) IPM program — target pests and diseases, economic threshold levels, monitoring frequency, preferred intervention hierarchy (biological, cultural, chemical), approved agrochemicals, responsible agronomist. (5) Yield target — expected yield (BU/acre or YPM), benchmark comparisons. Integrates with Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView for VRA prescription generation. Supports EPA nutrient management compliance, BMP documentation, GLOBALG.A.P. crop protection standards, and IPM guidelines.'
}

Table crop.planting_event {
  planting_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each planting event record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the planting_event data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planting equipment (planter, seeder) used during this event, sourced from John Deere Operations Center fleet telematics records.']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Seed planting consumes a specific inventory commodity lot. Linking planting_event to commodity_lot enables FSMA seed traceability and inventory depletion reconciliation — a domain expert expects to tr']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Planting events generate direct costs (seed, labor, equipment fuel) that must be posted to a cost center for input cost tracking and budget variance reporting. Farm ERP systems require cost center ass']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Planting crew tracking: large-scale planting operations are crew-based. Linking planting_event to crew supports piece-rate payroll for planting crews, H2A labor compliance verification, and labor cost']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator or farm worker who executed the planting operation. Supports workforce accountability and GAP traceability.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agronomic crop plan that authorized and scheduled this planting event, linking actual execution back to the planned prescription.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field on which this planting event was executed. Links to the field master record containing boundary, acreage, and soil zone data.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Planting events are the agronomic record; field_operation is the equipment execution record. Linking enables reconciliation of prescribed vs. actual seeding rates, area coverage accuracy, and planter ']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_issue. Business justification: Seed planting consumes seed inventory recorded as a goods issue. Linking planting_event to goods_issue enables seed consumption reconciliation — confirming seed goods issued from inventory matches act']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: planting_event currently stores crop_season (STRING) and crop_year (INT) as denormalized text/integer representations of the growing season. Adding season_id as a proper FK to growing_season.growing_s']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Pre-plant field inspections (GAP, FSMA field sanitation) must be linked to planting events to verify field readiness and compliance before planting. Agronomists and food safety auditors expect traceab']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Precision planting operations execute variable seeding rates by management zone. Planting event records seeding_rate_actual vs seeding_rate_prescribed for VRA compliance and as-planted map generation.']
  seed_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seed lot (LOT) used in this planting event, enabling full traceability from seed supplier through planting to harvest per GAP and FSMA requirements.']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific crop variety or hybrid planted during this event, linking to the crop variety master for species, hybrid code, and trait information.']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile. Business justification: Planting decisions (depth, seeding rate, row spacing) are operationally driven by soil profile data (texture, drainage class, depth-to-restrictive-layer). Agronomists reference the soil profile when e']
  air_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature at the time of planting, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used alongside soil temperature for planting condition assessment and growing degree day (GDD) baseline calculations.']
  area_planted_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total field area covered by this planting event, measured in acres as GPS-tracked by John Deere Operations Center. Used for seed usage reconciliation, cost allocation, and yield per acre (YPM) calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planting event record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail creation point for data governance and lineage tracking.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this planting event, formatted as PLT-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in field records, GAP audit documentation, and FMIS reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the planting event. \'planned\' = scheduled but not started; \'in_progress\' = actively being executed; \'completed\' = fully finished; \'cancelled\' = aborted before execution; \'partial\' = field partially planted due to weather or equipment stoppage.']
  gap_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this planting event was executed in full compliance with Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) standards, including seed traceability, equipment calibration records, and operator certification. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI audit readiness.']
  gps_coverage_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON string representing the GPS-tracked boundary and coverage path of the planting operation, sourced from John Deere Operations Center or Trimble RTK guidance system. Enables as-planted map generation in Esri ArcGIS and Climate FieldView.']
  hybrid_code varchar [note: 'Seed company\'s commercial hybrid or variety code as labeled on the seed bag (e.g., \'DKC62-08RIB\'). Distinct from the internal crop_variety_id — this is the supplier-assigned commercial identifier used for agronomic benchmarking and seed procurement.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planted seed variety is a Genetically Modified Organism (GMO). Required for USDA APHIS regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and customer supply chain transparency disclosures.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seed used in this planting event is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). Required for organic certification audit trails and premium market channel eligibility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for agronomist or operator observations recorded at the time of planting, such as equipment issues, field condition anomalies, or deviations from the prescription. Supports GAP audit documentation and agronomic decision records.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days applicable to any seed treatments applied, representing the minimum number of days that must elapse between planting/treatment and harvest. Critical for MRL compliance and food safety. Sourced from EPA pesticide label.']
  planting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the planting operation was executed. Critical for PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) calculation, growing degree day (GDD) accumulation, and crop insurance compliance.']
  planting_depth_cm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual seed placement depth below soil surface in centimeters, as recorded by the planter monitor. Critical for germination uniformity and stand establishment. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center.']
  planting_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the planting operation was completed or stopped, sourced from John Deere Operations Center machine telemetry. Used with start timestamp to compute field operation duration.']
  planting_method varchar [note: 'Tillage and planting method used for this event. Supports soil health tracking, carbon sequestration reporting, and BMP compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conventional|no_till|strip_till|minimum_till|direct_seeding|transplanting — promote to reference product if additional methods are needed]']
  planting_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the planting operation commenced, sourced from John Deere Operations Center machine telemetry. Used for operational duration analysis and OEE calculations.']
  rei_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours for any seed treatments applied, specifying the minimum time workers must stay out of treated areas after planting. Required for OSHA and EPA worker protection standard compliance.']
  row_spacing_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual row spacing achieved during planting, measured in centimeters. Affects canopy closure, weed suppression, and yield potential. Sourced from planter configuration data in John Deere Operations Center.']
  seed_treatment varchar [note: 'Description of any chemical or biological seed treatments applied prior to planting (e.g., fungicide, insecticide, inoculant). Required for PHI/REI compliance tracking and EPA pesticide use reporting. Sourced from seed bag label or agronomist records.']
  seeding_rate_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average seeding rate achieved during the planting operation, expressed in seeds per acre. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center planter monitor data. Compared against the prescribed VRA seeding rate for agronomic performance analysis.']
  seeding_rate_prescribed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target seeding rate specified in the VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription for this field zone, expressed in seeds per acre. Sourced from Trimble Ag Software prescription maps.']
  soil_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volumetric soil moisture content at the time of planting, expressed as a percentage. Influences seed-to-soil contact quality and germination success. Sourced from soil moisture sensors or field scouting records.']
  soil_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Soil temperature at seed depth measured at the time of planting, expressed in degrees Celsius. Key agronomic indicator for germination risk assessment and planting timing decisions. Sourced from in-cab sensors or field weather stations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this planting event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, Silver layer reconciliation, and conflict resolution when multiple systems report the same event.']
  source_system_event_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this planting event in the originating source system (e.g., John Deere Operations Center task ID or Climate FieldView activity ID). Enables reverse lookup and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this planting event record. Supports Silver layer incremental processing and audit trail requirements.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Observed weather condition at the time of planting. Supports agronomic analysis of planting conditions versus stand establishment outcomes and crop insurance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|partly_cloudy|overcast|light_rain|heavy_rain|windy|foggy — promote to reference product if additional conditions are needed]']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an actual planting operation executed on a field, capturing planting date, operator, equipment used, seed lot number (LOT), actual seeding rate (seeds/acre), row spacing achieved, planting depth, soil temperature at planting, and GPS-tracked field coverage. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center and Climate FieldView planting data. Supports GAP traceability and PHI/REI compliance tracking.'
}

Table crop.seed_lot {
  seed_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each seed lot master record. Primary key for the seed_lot data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Seed lots require COAs documenting germination rate, purity, seed treatment active ingredients, and organic certification. seed_lot.coa_number is a denormalized reference. Normalizing to FK supports s']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A seed lot is a physical batch of seed for a specific crop species. The crop_species column (STRING) is a denormalized free-text field that duplicates the crop master\'s crop_name and scientific_name. ']
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.delivery_schedule. Business justification: Seed lots arrive per specific delivery schedule lines. Linking enables procurement-to-planting chain of custody traceability, on-time delivery performance measurement, and confirmation that the correc']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: Seed lots have is_gmo and gmo_trait_stack attributes but no FK to the formal GMO declaration. Identity preservation programs, USDA BE disclosure, and export market approvals (EU, Japan, China) require']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Seed lot germination, purity, and pathogen screening tests generate lab_sample records that feed into the COA. seed_lot links to certificate_of_analysis but not to the originating lab_sample; direct l']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Seed lots must be reconciled against material master records for ERP inventory valuation, reorder planning, and procurement cost accounting. Ag ERP systems (SAP) require seed lot → material master lin']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: NOP regulations require organic operations to use organically produced seed when commercially available. Linking seed_lot to organic_certification enables NOP compliance verification that certified or']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Seed lot traceability to the originating PO is required for GlobalG.A.P. and FSMA compliance. Replaces denormalized purchase_order_number plain text attribute with a proper FK enabling full procuremen']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop variety master record associated with this seed lot, linking the lot to its specific genetic variety, species, and crop type classification.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (warehouse bin, cold storage unit, or seed storage facility) where this seed lot is currently stored. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM storage location (LGORT) and Manhattan Associates WMS location master.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Seed lots are procured under vendor contracts specifying GMO status, organic certification, pricing, and quality terms. Linking seed_lot to vendor_contract enables contract compliance verification at ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seed supplier or vendor who provided this seed lot. Links to the supplier master for procurement traceability and vendor performance management. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM vendor master (LFA1).']
  certification_class varchar [note: 'AOSCA-defined seed certification class indicating the generation and purity level of the seed lot. \'breeder\' = source seed from plant breeder; \'foundation\' = first generation from breeder seed; \'registered\' = second generation; \'certified\' = third generation approved for commercial use; \'uncertified\' = not certified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seed lot record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit_cost of this seed lot (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'BRL\'). Required for multi-currency financial reporting under IFRS and GAAP, and for SAP S/4HANA FI currency management.']
  germination_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the germination rate certification is no longer valid and retesting is required per USDA Federal Seed Act regulations. Drives lot status transitions to \'expired\' and triggers procurement or retesting workflows.']
  germination_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of seeds in this lot that germinated under standard test conditions, as reported on the seed tag or Certificate of Analysis (COA). Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Critical for seeding rate calculations, yield forecasting (YPM), and USDA Federal Seed Act minimum germination standards compliance.']
  germination_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the germination test was conducted for this seed lot, as reported on the seed tag or COA (Certificate of Analysis). Used to determine germination validity period and compliance with USDA Federal Seed Act labeling requirements.']
  gmo_trait_stack varchar [note: 'Identifier or description of the GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) trait stack present in this seed lot (e.g., \'RR2Y/YGCB\', \'Roundup Ready 2 Yield + YieldGard Corn Borer\'). NULL if non-GMO. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and customer GMO disclosure requirements.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seed lot contains genetically modified organisms (GMO). True = GMO seed; False = non-GMO seed. Required for USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard compliance, customer labeling, and export documentation.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seed lot is certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = NOP-certified organic seed; False = conventional seed. Required for organic crop production compliance and NOP audit trails.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric lot number (LOT) assigned by the seed supplier or internal seed processing facility. Used for seed traceability from procurement through planting to harvest, and for FSMA seed traceability compliance. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM batch number (CHARG) and Granular Farm Management lot tracking.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the seed lot. \'available\' = in storage and ready for use; \'reserved\' = allocated to a planting plan; \'planted\' = fully consumed in field operations; \'depleted\' = quantity exhausted; \'quarantined\' = held pending quality or regulatory review; \'expired\' = past germination validity date.']
  moisture_content_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the seed lot expressed as a percentage at time of testing or receipt. Critical for safe storage, preventing mold and mycotoxin development, and maintaining germination viability. Monitored per USDA grain storage standards.']
  noxious_weed_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seed lot has been certified as free of noxious weed seeds as defined by USDA Federal Seed Act and applicable state seed laws. True = certified noxious weed free; False = noxious weed seeds detected or certification not obtained. Required for interstate commerce compliance.']
  purity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pure seed (by weight) in this lot as determined by seed analysis, excluding inert matter, other crop seed, and weed seed. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Required for USDA Federal Seed Act labeling compliance and AOSCA certification standards.']
  quantity_remaining decimal(12,3) [note: 'Current remaining quantity of seed in this lot available for planting, expressed in the unit_of_measure. Updated as seed is allocated to planting operations. Supports inventory planning, seeding rate calculations, and procurement replenishment decisions.']
  quantity_units decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total quantity of seed in this lot expressed in the applicable unit of measure (seeds, bags, CWT, or BU as defined in unit_of_measure). Tracks available inventory for planting planning, procurement, and WMS (Warehouse Management System) inventory control in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which this seed lot was physically received at the storage facility. Marks the start of the lot\'s custody chain for FSMA traceability, FIFO inventory management, and storage duration tracking.']
  relative_maturity_days varchar [note: 'Relative maturity rating of the crop variety in this seed lot, expressed in days or heat units (e.g., 95-day corn, 3.8 maturity group soybean). Critical for crop planning, harvest scheduling, and matching variety to growing season length in the FMIS.']
  seed_origin_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this seed lot was produced or conditioned. Required for USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, import/export documentation, and APHIS phytosanitary certification.']
  seed_rate_per_acre decimal(10,2) [note: 'Supplier-recommended seeding rate for this seed lot expressed in seeds or units per acre. Used in VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView, and for planting plan calculations in FMIS.']
  seed_treatment_product varchar [note: 'Commercial name of the seed treatment product applied to this lot (e.g., \'Cruiser Maxx\', \'Poncho/VOTiVO\', \'Apron XL\'). Required for EPA FIFRA compliance, SDS (Safety Data Sheet) reference, and agronomic record-keeping under GAP standards.']
  seed_treatment_type varchar [note: 'Type of chemical or biological coating applied to the seed lot prior to packaging. \'fungicide\' = fungicide-only coating; \'insecticide\' = insecticide-only coating; \'fungicide_insecticide\' = combined coating; \'biological\' = biological agent coating; \'untreated\' = no treatment applied. Required for EPA pesticide registration compliance, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) tracking, and PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) management.']
  test_weight_lbs_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Test weight of the seed lot expressed in pounds per bushel (BU), indicating seed density and quality. Used for quality grading, storage planning, and yield estimation. Aligns with USDA grain grading standards.']
  thousand_seed_weight_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Weight of 1,000 seeds in grams (TSW), used to calculate seeding rates by weight, estimate seed counts per bag, and assess seed size uniformity. Standard agronomic quality metric used in FMIS and precision planting systems.']
  treatment_active_ingredient varchar [note: 'Active Ingredient (AI) of the seed treatment applied to this lot (e.g., \'thiamethoxam\', \'fludioxonil\', \'metalaxyl\'). Required for EPA FIFRA compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) tracking, and food safety regulatory reporting.']
  treatment_date date [note: 'Date on which the seed treatment was applied to this lot. Used to calculate treatment age, validate treatment efficacy windows, and support EPA FIFRA compliance and PHI/REI tracking.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Procurement cost per unit of measure for this seed lot (in USD), as recorded at time of purchase. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, crop enterprise budgeting, CAPEX/OPEX reporting, and financial planning in SAP S/4HANA CO and Granular Farm Management.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the seed lot quantity (e.g., \'seeds\' for precision count, \'bags\' for packaged units, \'CWT\' for hundredweight, \'BU\' for bushels, \'kg\' or \'lbs\' for weight-based). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM base unit of measure (MEINS) and USDA commodity reporting standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this seed lot record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance for FSMA and SOX requirements.']
  vigor_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Seed vigor test score indicating the potential for rapid, uniform emergence under field stress conditions, expressed as a percentage or index (0.00–100.00). Complements germination rate for predicting field performance. Tested per AOSA/ISTA vigor testing protocols.']
  weed_seed_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of weed seed (by weight) found in this seed lot during purity analysis. Must meet USDA Federal Seed Act and state seed law maximum thresholds. High weed seed content may trigger quarantine or rejection.']

  Note: 'Master record for each seed lot (LOT) received and used in planting operations, including crop variety reference, supplier, lot number, germination rate, purity percentage, seed treatment details (fungicide/insecticide coating, treatment date), GMO trait stack, test weight, and storage location. Provides full seed traceability from procurement through planting to harvest. Supports FSMA seed traceability, USDA seed certification compliance, and AOSCA seed labeling requirements.'
}

Table crop.fertilization_event {
  fertilization_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each fertilizer application event record. Serves as the primary key for the fertilization_event data product in the crop domain Silver layer.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Fertilization events must reference the specific fertilizer product applied for nutrient management plan compliance, EPA reporting, and cost tracking. Domain experts expect fertilization events to lin']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment or machinery used to perform the fertilizer application (e.g., spreader, sprayer, fertigation system). Links to the equipment master for maintenance and OEE tracking.']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Fertilization events consume a specific fertilizer lot. Linking to commodity_lot enables lot-level nutrient management traceability, MRL compliance reporting, and inventory depletion — required for nu']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fertilization events are among the largest variable input costs in crop production. Direct cost center assignment enables fertilizer cost tracking, nutrient management budget variance reporting, and c']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Fertilization operations are routinely performed by field crews. Crew-level tracking supports H2A labor compliance reporting, payroll allocation by crew, and nutrient management audit trails. A domain']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or equipment operator who performed the fertilizer application. Used for workforce accountability and GAP compliance traceability.']
  fertilization_applicator_workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: Many states require Certified Crop Adviser (CCA) or nutrient management planner certification for commercial fertilizer application. Linking fertilization events to the applicator\'s certification supp']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: fertilization_event links to vra_prescription and growing_season but has no direct FK to field_crop_plan. The field crop plan defines target N/P/K rates (target_n_rate_lbs_acre, target_p_rate_lbs_acre']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field on which the fertilizer application event occurred. Links to the field master record containing boundary, acreage, and soil zone data.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Fertilization events record agronomic application details; field_operation records equipment execution. Linking enables nutrient management plan compliance verification, application rate accuracy audi']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_issue. Business justification: Each fertilizer application triggers an inventory goods issue in ag ERP systems to deplete fertilizer stock and post field operation costs. This link supports fertilizer inventory reconciliation, cost']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop season or growing season during which this fertilization event occurred. Enables agronomic analysis by season and year.']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Fertilization events apply specific catalog input products. Linking to input_catalog enables nutrient management reporting by product, MRL compliance verification, organic certification checks, and in']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Post-application soil and tissue lab samples are collected after fertilization events to verify nutrient uptake and NMP compliance. Agronomists and regulators require direct traceability from fertiliz']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: VRA fertilization events are executed at management zone level. Precision agriculture systems track nutrient application by zone for zone-level ROI analysis and variable-rate compliance reporting. No ']
  manure_source_herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Nutrient management plans and EPA/NRCS manure application regulations require traceability from applied manure back to the source herd. Agronomists and regulators need this link to verify setback comp']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Fertilizer applications must reference the material master for nutrient management plan compliance reporting, fertilizer cost-per-acre accounting, and inventory depletion posting in ag ERP. Agronomist']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Nutrient management audits and FSMA compliance require traceability from fertilizer application events back to the procurement PO and vendor. Replaces denormalized supplier_name. A domain expert expec']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile. Business justification: Fertilization rate decisions (actual_rate vs planned_rate, nitrogen/phosphorus/potassium_pct) are calibrated against permanent soil profile characteristics (CEC, texture, drainage class). State nutrie']
  soil_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_sample. Business justification: Nutrient management plans and state regulatory compliance require traceability from each fertilization event back to the soil test (COA) that justified the application rate. NRCS and state nutrient ma']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Fertilization events must trace the supplying vendor for nutrient management plan compliance, vendor performance scoring, and regulatory traceability. The existing purchase_order_id link requires a jo']
  actual_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual fertilizer application rate recorded during the field operation, expressed in the unit defined by rate_unit. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag as-applied data. Compared against planned_rate for VRA compliance and agronomic audit.']
  application_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the fertilizer was applied to the field. This is the principal real-world business event date used for agronomic scheduling, PHI/REI compliance tracking, and nutrient management reporting.']
  application_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the fertilizer application operation was completed in the field. Used to calculate application duration, equipment OEE, and to confirm REI start time.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'The agronomic method used to apply the fertilizer to the field or crop. Determines nutrient placement efficiency and environmental risk profile. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|banded|side_dress|fertigation|foliar|injection|top_dress — promote to reference product]']
  application_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the fertilizer application operation began in the field. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag telematics. Used for REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance and equipment utilization analysis.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fertilization event workflow. \'planned\' indicates a scheduled event not yet executed; \'in_progress\' indicates active application; \'completed\' indicates successful execution; \'cancelled\' indicates the event was called off before execution; \'voided\' indicates a data correction.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Agronomic timing classification of the fertilizer application relative to the crop growth stage. Distinguishes pre-plant, at-plant, and in-season applications for nutrient management planning and compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_plant|at_plant|side_dress|top_dress|in_season|post_harvest — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fertilization event record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data governance, lineage tracking, and Silver layer ingestion auditing.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the fertilization event, used in field records, agronomic reports, and regulatory submissions. Format: FERT-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  fertilizer_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the fertilizer product applied. Determines equipment requirements, handling procedures, and SDS (Safety Data Sheet) compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dry_granular|liquid|anhydrous_ammonia|suspension|slow_release|controlled_release — promote to reference product]']
  is_gmo_derived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fertilizer product contains or is derived from GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) sources (e.g., certain biofertilizers). Relevant for organic certification exclusion and international export compliance.']
  is_organic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fertilizer product applied is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = organic-approved input; False = conventional synthetic fertilizer. Critical for organic certification compliance and audit trails.']
  is_vra_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fertilization event was executed using Variable Rate Application (VRA) technology, applying different rates across management zones within the field. True = VRA prescription followed; False = flat-rate uniform application.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this fertilization event record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.']
  nitrogen_applied_lbs_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual pounds of elemental nitrogen applied per acre during this event, derived from actual_rate and nitrogen_pct. Stored as a raw field value from the source system for EPA nutrient management plan compliance and environmental reporting. Critical KPI for nitrogen use efficiency (NUE) analysis.']
  nitrogen_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of nitrogen (N) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for nutrient budget calculations, soil nitrogen loading analysis, and EPA nutrient management plan compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or agronomist notes related to this fertilization event. May include observations about field conditions, equipment issues, deviations from prescription, or agronomic rationale. Supports GAP documentation requirements.']
  nutrient_management_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the USDA NRCS-approved Nutrient Management Plan (NMP) that governs this fertilization event. Required for USDA conservation program compliance (e.g., EQIP) and EPA nutrient management reporting.']
  phosphorus_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of phosphorus (P2O5) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for phosphorus loading calculations, water quality compliance, and soil nutrient balance reporting.']
  planned_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The target or prescribed fertilizer application rate as specified in the agronomic plan or VRA prescription, expressed in the unit defined by rate_unit. Represents the intended nutrient loading before actual application.']
  potassium_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of potassium (K2O) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for potassium budget calculations and soil fertility management reporting.']
  rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for both planned_rate and actual_rate fields. Common units include lbs/acre (CWT-based), gallons/acre for liquid fertilizers, and metric equivalents for international operations.']
  rei_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the Restricted Entry Interval (REI) expires following the fertilizer application, after which workers may safely re-enter the treated field. Calculated from application_end_timestamp plus the product-specific REI duration. Critical for OSHA and EPA worker safety compliance.']
  soil_temperature_f decimal(6,2) [note: 'Soil temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the time of fertilizer application. Critical for nitrogen application timing decisions (e.g., nitrification inhibitor effectiveness, anhydrous ammonia fall application restrictions) and nutrient uptake efficiency.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this fertilization event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer ingestion audit for the Databricks Lakehouse pipeline.']
  source_system_event_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this fertilization event record in the originating operational system (e.g., John Deere Operations Center task ID, Trimble Ag field activity ID). Enables deduplication and reconciliation between the Silver layer and source systems.']
  total_product_applied decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total quantity of fertilizer product applied across the entire treated area during this event, expressed in the unit defined by rate_unit. Calculated from actual_rate multiplied by treated_area_acres but stored as a raw field value from the source system for inventory reconciliation and nutrient management reporting.']
  treated_area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual field area covered by the fertilizer application, measured in acres. May differ from the total field area if only a portion of the field was treated. Sourced from GPS/RTK boundary data in John Deere Operations Center.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Observed weather condition at the time of fertilizer application. Relevant for assessing nutrient runoff risk, volatilization potential, and GAP compliance. Recorded by the operator or sourced from field weather station data.']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Wind speed in miles per hour recorded at the time of fertilizer application. Used to assess drift risk for liquid and granular applications and to validate compliance with application label restrictions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a fertilizer application event on a field, capturing application date, product applied (NPK formulation), application rate (lbs/acre or gallons/acre), application method (broadcast, banded, fertigation), equipment used, operator, and VRA prescription reference. Tracks actual vs. planned nutrient application for agronomic compliance and environmental reporting (EPA nutrient management). Sourced from John Deere Operations Center and Trimble Ag.'
}

Table crop.protection_event {
  protection_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for protection_event']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the registered pesticide, herbicide, fungicide, or insecticide product master record used in this application event. Carries EPA registration number, active ingredient (AI), and label details.']
  applicator_workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: WPS/EPA regulatory compliance: EPA and Worker Protection Standard regulations require the pesticide applicator\'s license to be verified and recorded for each application event. protection_event has de']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the application equipment (sprayer, drone, aerial applicator, or ground rig) used to perform the crop protection event. Supports equipment calibration records and OEE tracking.']
  chemical_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.chemical_compliance. Business justification: Each pesticide application must reference the chemical compliance record for EPA registration, PHI, REI, MRL, and SDS compliance verification. This is a direct FIFRA and FSMA regulatory requirement. A']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Crop protection events apply a specific agrochemical lot. Linking to commodity_lot enables lot-level pesticide traceability, PHI/MRL compliance reporting, and EPA regulatory audit trails. lot_number o']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Crop protection events (pesticide/herbicide applications) generate significant input costs requiring cost center assignment for chemical cost tracking, IPM program cost analysis, and budget variance r']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Pesticide application crews must be tracked for EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) compliance — REI enforcement applies to all crew members present, not just the primary applicator. Crew-level track']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop variety or commodity being protected during this application event. Used to validate Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) compliance and Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) thresholds for the specific crop.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: protection_event has no link to field_crop_plan, yet crop protection activities are planned and governed by the field crop plan\'s IPM program (ipm_program_id on field_crop_plan). Adding field_crop_pla']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or management zone where the crop protection application event was performed. Links to the field master record containing parcel boundaries, soil data, and crop assignment.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.equipment_field_operation. Business justification: Pesticide/herbicide application events are executed as equipment field operations (sprayer passes). Linking protection_event to equipment_field_operation enables spray window compliance verification, ']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: protection_event currently has no FK to growing_season, creating a gap in the agronomic event lifecycle. Every pesticide, herbicide, fungicide, or insecticide application occurs within a specific grow']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Protection events apply specific agrochemical inputs from the input catalog. Linking enables PHI/REI compliance reporting by catalog item, MRL threshold verification, restricted-use pesticide tracking']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: IPM programs and variable rate pesticide applications are planned and recorded at management zone level. EPA and state pesticide application records require zone-level documentation of application_rat']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Protection events apply agrochemicals subject to commodity-specific MRL thresholds. The applicable MRL threshold (commodity + active ingredient combination) must be referenced at application time for ']
  primary_protection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified pesticide application operator or licensed agronomist who performed or supervised the crop protection event. Used for operator certification compliance tracking.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: EPA and MRL compliance audits require traceability from agrochemical application events to the procurement PO confirming the product was legally sourced. This is a standard regulatory traceability req']
  restricted_grazing_herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: EPA pesticide label compliance and food safety regulations require tracking which livestock herd is subject to grazing restrictions following a crop protection application. Veterinarians and agronomis']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Pesticide application events require vendor traceability for EPA/FSMA regulatory compliance, MRL tracking, and vendor performance management. Only purchase_order_id exists currently; direct vendor_id ']
  active_ingredient varchar [note: 'The chemical active ingredient (AI) in the applied pesticide product, as declared on the EPA-registered label. Used for MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) verification, Codex Alimentarius compliance, and IPM program tracking. May contain multiple AIs separated by semicolons.']
  agronomist_signoff_date date [note: 'Date on which the responsible agronomist reviewed and approved this crop protection event record. Mandatory for GLOBALG.A.P. CB 7.6 pesticide application record completeness and FSMA audit trail. Null indicates pending agronomist review.']
  application_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the crop protection product was applied to the field. Principal business event date used to calculate PHI compliance (earliest harvest date) and REI field re-entry clearance date. Aligns with Climate FieldView spraying event date.']
  application_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the crop protection application operation was completed. Combined with application_start_time to calculate total application duration and equipment utilization for OEE reporting.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'The physical method used to apply the crop protection product to the field or crop. Determines equipment calibration requirements, drift risk assessment, and label compliance. Sourced from Trimble Ag VRA prescription or Climate FieldView spraying records.']
  application_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The quantity of crop protection product applied per unit area (e.g., oz/acre, L/ha, kg/ha) as prescribed on the EPA label. Must not exceed label maximum rate. Used for MRL compliance calculation and VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription validation.']
  application_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the application rate field, specifying the quantity and area basis (e.g., oz/acre, L/ha). Required for MRL compliance calculations and cross-regional reporting. Must align with the EPA-registered label unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oz/acre|fl_oz/acre|lb/acre|kg/ha|L/ha|mL/ha|pt/acre|qt/acre — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  application_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the crop protection application operation commenced. Used for REI (Restricted Entry Interval) calculation, operator exposure tracking, and OSHA compliance. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag telematics.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle workflow status of the crop protection application event. Drives PHI compliance checks, agronomist sign-off workflows, and FSMA audit readiness. \'pending_review\' indicates awaiting agronomist approval.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the crop protection product by its mode of action or target pest category. Drives IPM program reporting, EPA pesticide use reporting, and GLOBALG.A.P. pesticide audit trail categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: herbicide|fungicide|insecticide|nematicide|rodenticide|plant_growth_regulator|bactericide|acaricide|molluscicide — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crop protection event record was first captured in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements under FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., and EPA pesticide recordkeeping regulations.']
  earliest_harvest_date date [note: 'Calculated earliest date on which the treated crop may be harvested in compliance with the PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval). Computed as application_date + phi_days_required. Enforced in harvest scheduling to prevent MRL violations and FSMA non-compliance.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'EPA registration number of the pesticide product applied, as printed on the product label. Mandatory for FIFRA compliance recordkeeping. Format: registrant-product[-state suffix]. Must match the EPA-registered label used at time of application.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this crop protection application event, formatted as CPE-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in audit trails, GLOBALG.A.P. pesticide records, EPA recordkeeping submissions, and FSMA traceability documentation.']
  gps_coverage_polygon varchar [note: 'Well-Known Text (WKT) representation of the GPS-tracked coverage polygon for this application event, as recorded by John Deere Operations Center or Trimble RTK positioning. Enables GIS-based spatial analysis in Esri ArcGIS for drift buffer compliance and field coverage verification.']
  ipm_justified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crop protection application was justified by an IPM (Integrated Pest Management) scouting threshold or economic injury level assessment, rather than a calendar-based schedule. True = IPM threshold-triggered; False = preventive/calendar application. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. IPM program compliance.']
  phi_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance determination for the PHI requirement relative to the planned harvest date. \'compliant\' = harvest date is after earliest_harvest_date; \'non_compliant\' = harvest scheduled before PHI expiry; \'pending\' = harvest date not yet confirmed; \'waived\' = regulatory exemption applied.']
  phi_days_required varchar [note: 'Number of days that must elapse between the last application of this crop protection product and the earliest permissible harvest date, as specified on the EPA-registered label. Core compliance field for FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., and MRL verification.']
  rei_clearance_datetime timestamp [note: 'Calculated datetime after which workers may safely re-enter the treated field, computed as application_start_time + rei_hours_required. Posted at field entry points per EPA Worker Protection Standard. Critical for OSHA and EPA WPS compliance.']
  rei_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance determination for the REI requirement. \'compliant\' = no worker re-entry before rei_clearance_datetime; \'non_compliant\' = documented early re-entry violation; \'pending\' = REI period still active; \'waived\' = PPE exception per label.']
  rei_hours_required decimal(5,1) [note: 'Number of hours that workers must stay out of the treated field following application, as specified on the EPA-registered label under the Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Decimal to support half-hour increments (e.g., 4.0, 12.0, 24.0, 48.0 hours).']
  relative_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at the time of application. Low humidity increases evaporation and drift risk for certain pesticide formulations. Required for label compliance documentation and GLOBALG.A.P. application condition records.']
  target_pest varchar [note: 'The specific pest, weed, disease, or pathogen that the crop protection application was intended to control, as identified through IPM scouting. Examples: Fusarium head blight, corn rootworm, Palmer amaranth. Supports IPM program efficacy tracking and GLOBALG.A.P. CB 7.2 pest monitoring records.']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient air temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of application. High temperatures can increase volatilization and drift risk.
