Some EPA labels restrict application above threshold temperatures. Sourced from field weather station or Climate FieldView weather data.']
  total_area_treated_ha decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total field area covered by this crop protection application event, measured in hectares. Derived from GPS-tracked equipment coverage via John Deere Operations Center or Trimble RTK positioning. Used for total product quantity reconciliation and EPA pesticide use reporting.']
  total_product_used decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total quantity of crop protection product consumed in this application event (application_rate × total_area_treated). Expressed in the same unit as application_rate_unit. Used for inventory depletion in SAP S/4HANA MM and EPA pesticide use reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this crop protection event record. Required for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and regulatory audit trail completeness.']
  water_volume_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Volume of water carrier used per acre in the spray mixture (gallons/acre or L/ha). Critical for spray efficacy, equipment calibration validation, and label compliance. Sourced from sprayer controller data in John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag.']
  wind_direction varchar [note: 'Compass direction of wind at time of application (8-point compass rose). Used for pesticide drift risk assessment, buffer zone compliance, and neighbor notification requirements under EPA label conditions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: N|NE|E|SE|S|SW|W|NW — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  wind_speed_mph decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured wind speed in miles per hour at the time of application. EPA label restrictions typically prohibit application above 10-15 mph to prevent pesticide drift. Sourced from on-field weather station or John Deere Operations Center weather integration.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a pesticide, herbicide, fungicide, or insecticide application event, capturing application date, agrochemical product reference (EPA registration number, active ingredient), application rate, target pest or disease, application method, equipment used, operator certification number, weather conditions at application, and GPS-tracked coverage area. Includes PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance tracking: PHI days required, calculated earliest harvest date, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) hours required, field re-entry clearance date, compliance status, and responsible agronomist sign-off. Supports IPM program execution, FSMA compliance, EPA pesticide recordkeeping, MRL verification, and GLOBALG.A.P. pesticide audit trails.'
}

Table crop.irrigation_event {
  irrigation_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each irrigation application event record in the crop domain. Serves as the primary key for this transactional entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Irrigation events are executed by specific irrigation assets (center pivots, pumps, drip systems). Tracking which asset performed each irrigation event is required for equipment utilization reporting,']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Irrigation events carry energy_cost_usd and water_cost_usd attributes indicating direct cost tracking. These costs must be posted to a cost center for water management cost reporting, energy cost allo']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Irrigation operations in large-scale agriculture are crew-based. Crew-level tracking supports H2A labor cost allocation to irrigation operations, piece-rate or hourly payroll by crew, and seasonal lab']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm operator or equipment operator who executed or supervised this irrigation event. Supports workforce accountability, GAP compliance, and training record linkage.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: irrigation_event links to growing_season and weather data but has no FK to field_crop_plan. The field crop plan defines irrigation targets (irrigation_system_type, irrigation_target_depth_inches, wate']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or management zone on which this irrigation event was applied. Links to the field master record in the crop domain.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Irrigation events record water application volumes; field_operation records pump/pivot equipment execution. Linking enables water use efficiency reporting, energy cost allocation per irrigation event,']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop growing season during which this irrigation event occurred, enabling season-level water use aggregation and agronomic analysis.']
  irrigation_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.irrigation_zone. Business justification: Irrigation events are executed within irrigation zones that define system type, permitted volume, and water right. Water right compliance reporting requires tracking volume applied per zone against pe']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: FSMA Produce Safety Rule mandates water quality lab sampling (E. coli, nitrates, heavy metals) tied to irrigation water sources. Linking irrigation events to lab samples enables direct compliance trac']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP S/4HANA work order or field operation task that authorized and scheduled this irrigation event.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time of the irrigation event in minutes, derived from start and end timestamps or IoT sensor telemetry. Used for operational efficiency analysis and equipment OEE calculations.']
  application_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the irrigation was applied. Used for day-level scheduling, PHI compliance checks, regulatory water use reporting, and agronomic calendar alignment.']
  application_rate_inches_per_hour decimal(8,4) [note: 'Rate at which water was applied during this irrigation event, measured in inches per hour. Used to assess runoff risk, soil infiltration compliance, and equipment calibration validation per USDA NRCS guidelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this irrigation event record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  crop_growth_stage varchar [note: 'Growth stage of the crop at the time of irrigation (e.g., germination, vegetative, flowering, grain fill, maturity). Critical for ET coefficient (Kc) selection, water demand modeling, and agronomic decision support.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or method by which this irrigation event record was captured. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and FMIS integration traceability in the Silver Layer.']
  distribution_uniformity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Coefficient of distribution uniformity (DU%) for this irrigation event, measuring how evenly water was applied across the field or zone. Key BMP metric for irrigation system performance and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the irrigation application concluded. Used with start_timestamp to calculate actual duration and validate against planned duration for operational efficiency analysis.']
  energy_cost_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated or actual energy cost in US dollars for pumping water during this irrigation event. Used for OPEX analysis, energy efficiency benchmarking, and sustainability reporting.']
  et_trigger_value_inches decimal(8,4) [note: 'Evapotranspiration (ET) deficit value in inches that triggered this irrigation event, as calculated from weather station data or crop model. Core metric for ET-driven irrigation scheduling and water use efficiency reporting.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this irrigation event, formatted as IRR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for field operations tracking, BMP compliance reporting, and cross-system reference in FMIS and John Deere Operations Center.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the irrigation event, tracking progression from scheduled through execution to completion or cancellation. Supports operational workflow management and reporting.']
  flow_rate_gpm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured flow rate of water delivery in gallons per minute (GPM) during this irrigation event. Used for pump performance analysis, water meter reconciliation, and system efficiency reporting.']
  irrigation_method varchar [note: 'Type of irrigation delivery system used for this event. Determines water use efficiency benchmarks, BMP compliance classification, and equipment utilization reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drip|center_pivot|flood|sprinkler|subsurface_drip|furrow|micro_sprinkler|lateral_move — promote to reference product if additional methods are needed]']
  is_bmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this irrigation event was conducted in accordance with Best Management Practices (BMP) as defined by USDA NRCS and applicable state environmental agencies. Required for conservation program reporting.']
  is_scheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this irrigation event was executed as part of a pre-planned schedule (True) or was an unplanned/emergency application (False). Used for schedule adherence reporting and agronomic audit trails.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or agronomist observations related to this irrigation event, such as equipment anomalies, field conditions, or agronomic observations not captured in structured fields.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Scheduled duration of the irrigation event in minutes as defined in the irrigation prescription or FMIS schedule. Used for variance analysis against actual duration.']
  pressure_psi decimal(7,2) [note: 'Operating pressure of the irrigation system in pounds per square inch (PSI) during this event. Used for equipment performance monitoring, uniformity assessment, and maintenance scheduling.']
  rainfall_since_last_event_inches decimal(7,3) [note: 'Total precipitation in inches recorded between the previous irrigation event and this one. Used to adjust effective irrigation need, validate ET-based scheduling decisions, and support water use efficiency reporting.']
  soil_moisture_pct_after decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volumetric soil moisture content (%) recorded by IoT sensors following irrigation application. Used to assess irrigation effectiveness, field capacity attainment, and over-irrigation risk.']
  soil_moisture_pct_before decimal(5,2) [note: 'Volumetric soil moisture content (%) recorded by IoT sensors immediately prior to irrigation application. Used to validate ET-based trigger thresholds and assess irrigation necessity per BMP standards.']
  soil_moisture_sensor_depth_cm varchar [note: 'Depth in centimeters at which the soil moisture sensor reading was taken. Required for agronomic interpretation of moisture data and root zone analysis.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the irrigation application began. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction, used for ET-driven scheduling analysis, water use reporting, and IoT sensor correlation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this irrigation event record. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  volume_applied_acre_inches decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total volume of water applied during this irrigation event, measured in acre-inches. Primary quantitative outcome for water use reporting, ET-based scheduling validation, and BMP compliance under USDA NRCS standards.']
  volume_applied_gallons decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total volume of water applied during this irrigation event, expressed in US gallons. Supports pump station metering reconciliation, water rights reporting, and utility billing where applicable.']
  water_cost_usd decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost of water applied during this irrigation event in US dollars, based on metered usage rates, well pumping costs, or municipal water tariffs. Supports COGS allocation and OPEX reporting.']
  water_source_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or identifier of the specific water source used (e.g., well name, canal name, reservoir name, municipal supply ID). Supports water rights tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  water_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the water source used for this irrigation event. Required for water rights compliance, environmental reporting, and BMP documentation under USDA and EPA frameworks.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an irrigation application on a field or zone, capturing irrigation date, method (drip, pivot, flood, sprinkler), water volume applied (acre-inches), ET-based trigger value, soil moisture reading at time of application, water source, pump station reference, and duration. Supports ET-driven irrigation scheduling, water use reporting, and BMP compliance. Integrates with IoT soil moisture sensors and John Deere Operations Center.'
}

Table crop.scouting_observation {
  scouting_observation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each field scouting observation record. Serves as the primary key for the scouting_observation data product within the crop domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Scouting observations are collected using UAVs, sensor ATVs, or other precision ag equipment. Linking identifies which asset collected the data, supporting equipment utilization reporting, calibration']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop variety or species being scouted in the field at the time of observation. Links to the crop master record in the crop management domain.']
  crop_protection_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop protection event created as a result of this scouting observation. Establishes the traceability link between IPM observation and the resulting pesticide or treatment application record.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: scouting_observation links to growing_season and protection_event but has no FK to field_crop_plan. Scouting observations are conducted to monitor crop health against the IPM program defined in the fi']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or management zone where the scouting observation was conducted. Links to the land and soil domain field master record.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Scouting observations generated by drone or sensor field operations must be traceable to the executing field operation for IPM documentation, data quality audits, and precision ag compliance reporting']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season or crop year during which this scouting observation was recorded. Enables seasonal trend analysis and year-over-year IPM benchmarking.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Field scouting observations serve as objective evidence during GAP field inspections. Inspectors reference scouting records to verify IPM compliance, pest pressure documentation, and food safety risk ']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: IPM programs track pest pressure and incidence by management zone to trigger zone-specific interventions. Scouting observations are spatially recorded at zone level for precision pest management repor']
  primary_scouting_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agronomist, crop scout, or remote sensing operator who conducted or submitted the observation. Links to the workforce domain employee or contractor record.']
  scouting_workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: GAP and GlobalG.A.P. audits require that scouting observations be performed by Certified Crop Advisers (CCA) or similarly credentialed scouts. Linking observations to the scout\'s certification support']
  action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the recommended follow-up action should be completed. Calculated considering PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval), REI (Restricted Entry Interval), and crop growth stage urgency. Used for workflow scheduling in SAP S/4HANA PP.']
  anomaly_detected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an anomaly was detected in the vegetation index or field condition relative to the seasonal baseline or surrounding field zones. True triggers priority review and potential VRA prescription adjustment.']
  bbch_growth_stage_code varchar [note: 'Crop growth stage at the time of observation expressed using the BBCH (Biologische Bundesanstalt, Bundessortenamt und CHemische Industrie) decimal scale (00–99). Critical for PHI and REI compliance calculations and timing of crop protection applications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the scouting observation record was first created in the FMIS or data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  gps_accuracy_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Estimated horizontal positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters at the time of observation. RTK-corrected readings typically achieve sub-0.05m accuracy; standard GPS may be 3–5m. Used to qualify spatial data reliability.']
  imagery_date date [note: 'Date on which the remote sensing imagery used for this observation was captured. May differ from observation_timestamp if imagery was analyzed retrospectively. Used for temporal alignment in seasonal vegetation health comparisons.']
  incidence_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sampled plants or area units showing presence of the observed pest, disease, or weed (0.00–100.00). Quantitative measure used alongside pressure rating for economic threshold analysis and IPM action decisions.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the precise location within the field where the observation was recorded. Enables GIS-based spatial analysis and precision agriculture mapping in Esri ArcGIS.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the precise location within the field where the observation was recorded. Used alongside latitude for spatial clustering and VRA zone delineation.']
  ndvi_baseline_delta decimal(5,4) [note: 'Difference between the current NDVI value and the seasonal historical baseline NDVI for the same field zone and growth stage. Positive values indicate improvement; negative values indicate decline relative to baseline.']
  ndvi_value decimal(5,4) [note: 'Normalized Difference Vegetation Index value ranging from -1.0000 to 1.0000 derived from remote sensing imagery. Values above 0.6 indicate healthy dense vegetation; values below 0.3 indicate stressed or sparse vegetation. Null for in-person observations without sensor data.']
  nutrient_deficiency_type varchar [note: 'Type of nutrient deficiency symptom observed on the crop. Applicable when observation_category is \'nutrient_deficiency\'. Aligned with NPK (Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium) and micronutrient classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nitrogen|phosphorus|potassium|sulfur|iron|zinc|manganese|boron — promote to reference product]']
  observation_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of what was observed during the scouting event. Segments observations for IPM reporting, crop protection event triggers, and VRA prescription generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pest|disease|weed|nutrient_deficiency|crop_health|vegetation_index|soil_condition|irrigation_stress — promote to reference product]']
  observation_method varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating how the scouting observation was collected: in-person field walk, satellite remote sensing, drone/UAV aerial imagery, manned aerial survey, or IoT sensor reading. Drives downstream processing logic and data quality rules.']
  observation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the scouting observation, formatted as SCO-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Used for cross-referencing with Trimble Ag Software, Climate FieldView, and SAP S/4HANA PP crop protection events.']
  observation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the scouting observation record, from initial field capture through agronomist review, action assignment, and closure. Drives workflow routing in Trimble Ag Software and SAP S/4HANA PP.']
  observation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the scouting observation was made in the field or when remote sensing imagery was captured. Principal real-world event time used for IPM decision timing and PHI/REI compliance tracking.']
  observer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field notes recorded by the agronomist or scout during the observation. Captures qualitative context, unusual conditions, or supplementary observations not captured in structured fields. Stored for audit and agronomic review.']
  pest_common_name varchar [note: 'Common name of the pest, disease, or weed species identified during the scouting observation (e.g., \'Corn Rootworm\', \'Soybean Rust\', \'Waterhemp\'). Complements the scientific code for field-user readability.']
  pest_organism_code varchar [note: 'Standardized scientific or EPPO (European and Mediterranean Plant Protection Organization) code identifying the pest species, disease pathogen, or weed species observed. Null when observation category is not pest, disease, or weed.']
  photo_reference_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path reference to photographic evidence captured during the scouting observation. Photos are stored in the enterprise document management system and linked here for audit trail and agronomist review purposes.']
  pressure_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of pest or disease pressure intensity observed in the field, ranging from none to severe. Used as the primary IPM decision trigger for crop protection event initiation and economic threshold comparison.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the scouting observation indicating urgency of follow-up action. Critical and high priorities trigger immediate crop protection event creation or VRA prescription review in the FMIS.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Recommended agronomic action based on the scouting observation findings. Drives downstream workflow creation in SAP S/4HANA PP for crop protection events or Trimble Ag Software for VRA prescriptions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_action|monitor|apply_crop_protection|adjust_irrigation|apply_fertilizer|vra_prescription|escalate_agronomist — promote to reference product]']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scouting observation was reviewed and validated by the responsible agronomist or supervisor. Part of the IPM audit trail required by GLOBALG.A.P. and FSMA compliance frameworks.']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual plant samples, transects, or observation points assessed during the scouting event. Provides statistical context for incidence and severity ratings and supports data quality validation.']
  scouted_area_ha decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total area of the field covered by this scouting observation, expressed in hectares. Used to calculate observation density, extrapolate field-wide pest pressure, and determine VRA prescription zone boundaries.']
  sensor_platform varchar [note: 'The sensor or imaging platform used to capture remote sensing data for this observation (e.g., satellite, drone/UAV, manned aerial, ground-based IoT sensor, handheld device). Null for purely in-person observations without sensor data.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative rating of the severity of damage or infection on affected plants, distinct from incidence (which measures spread). Severity measures the degree of impact on individual plants. Used in conjunction with incidence_pct for full IPM assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the scouting observation record was most recently modified. Supports data lineage tracking, change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  vegetation_health_class varchar [note: 'Classified vegetation health category derived from NDVI or other spectral indices. Used for field-level health mapping, anomaly detection, and seasonal baseline comparison in Climate FieldView and Esri ArcGIS.']
  weed_density_per_sqm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Count of weed plants per square meter observed in the scouted area. Applicable when observation_category is \'weed\'. Used to determine economic threshold for herbicide application and VRA prescription generation.']

  Note: 'Field observation record capturing crop health assessments from both in-person scouting and remote sensing sources. In-person scouting captures: agronomist or scout identity, pest identification and pressure rating, disease incidence and severity, weed species and density, nutrient deficiency symptoms, crop growth stage (BBCH scale code), GPS coordinates, photo references, and recommended action. Remote sensing captures: NDVI values, vegetation health classification, anomaly detection flags, seasonal baseline comparison, sensor platform (satellite, drone, aerial), and imagery date. Includes observation method type, priority classification, and recommended follow-up actions linking to crop protection events or VRA prescriptions. Sourced from Trimble Ag Software crop scouting module, Esri ArcGIS, and Climate FieldView. Drives IPM decision-making, crop protection event triggers, and variable rate intervention decisions.'
}

Table crop.yield_record {
  yield_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each yield record entry representing a single harvest pass or harvest event at the field-season-variety level.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the combine harvester or harvest equipment unit used for this harvest pass. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center fleet telematics.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Harvested commodity lots require a COA documenting pesticide residues, mycotoxins, moisture, and MRL compliance before sale to grain buyers or food manufacturers. Grain traceability and food safety co']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Yield records are the primary source for commodity-level production reporting, USDA FSA/RMA submissions, and grain marketing. commodity_id normalizes the denormalized crop_type string and enables join']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Yield records document harvested production; commodity lots represent that production in inventory. Direct traceability from yield record to commodity lot is required for grain traceability, crop insu']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: Actual yield records are compared against enterprise budget planned yields and revenues for farm profitability variance analysis — a mandatory farm management report. Agronomists and controllers use t']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator or farm worker who conducted the harvest operation. Links to the Workforce domain for labor tracking and compliance.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop plan that governed this field-season production cycle. Used to compute variance between planned yield targets and actual harvested yield.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field where the harvest operation was performed. Links to the field master record in the Land and Soil domain.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.equipment_field_operation. Business justification: Yield records are produced by specific combine field operations. Linking yield_record to equipment_field_operation enables yield-per-pass analysis, equipment efficiency benchmarking, and cost-per-bush']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: Yield records carry gmo_status as plain text but lack traceability to the formal GMO declaration. USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure, identity preservation program reporting, and export documentation ']
  government_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.government_program. Business justification: Yield records are the basis for APH (Actual Production History) calculations used to determine crop insurance indemnities and ARC/PLC government program payments. yield_record has a denormalized crop_']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Grain quality grading at harvest references a specific USDA grading standard that determines price discounts/premiums, elevator acceptance, and export eligibility. grading_standard_id normalizes the d']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop production season (e.g., Spring 2024, Fall 2024) under which this harvest record is captured. Enables season-level rollup reporting.']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Yield mapping by management zone is a core precision agriculture practice. Zone-level yield performance vs. soil productivity class drives future VRA prescription adjustments and zone-level ROI analys']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Yield records carry an mrl_compliant boolean flag but lack traceability to the specific MRL threshold checked. FSMA compliance, export documentation, and food safety audits require the yield record to']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic yield records must be traceable to the specific NOP certification for organic handler sales, premium pricing documentation, and NOP audit compliance. The yield record is the point of transfer ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Yield records are the basis for crop revenue recognition. Revenue from crop sales is attributed to profit centers for segment P&L reporting. Farm financial reporting requires yield records to be linke']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific crop variety or hybrid planted and harvested in this record. Enables variety-level yield benchmarking and seed performance analytics.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grain bin, elevator, or storage facility where this harvested lot was delivered. Links to the Inventory domain for grain inventory management and WMS integration with Manhattan Associates.']
  aph_yield_bu_per_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual Production History (APH) yield in bushels per acre as reported to USDA RMA for crop insurance purposes. May differ from yield_per_acre due to RMA-specific calculation rules (e.g., prevented planting adjustments, yield substitutions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this yield record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  damaged_kernels_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of heat-damaged, mold-damaged, or otherwise damaged kernels in the harvested grain sample. USDA grading factor impacting grade and market value. Elevated damage may indicate storage or disease issues requiring agronomic investigation.']
  foreign_material_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of foreign material (weed seeds, chaff, dirt, other crop) in the harvested grain sample. USDA grading factor that affects grade assignment and may result in elevator dockage charges.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'GMO identity status of the harvested crop. Determines segregation requirements, storage bin assignment, and market channel eligibility. Critical for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling), export documentation, and identity-preserved grain premiums.']
  harvest_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the harvest operation was performed. Principal business event date for this transaction. Used for PHI compliance verification, crop insurance documentation, and agronomic reporting.']
  harvest_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the harvest pass was completed on the field. Combined with harvest_start_timestamp to compute harvest duration and equipment OEE metrics.']
  harvest_loss_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of harvestable crop lost during the harvest operation due to combine settings, lodging, or field conditions. Sourced from combine loss monitor data via John Deere Operations Center. Used for equipment calibration and GAP compliance reporting.']
  harvest_method varchar [note: 'Method or header type used for the harvest operation. Affects harvest efficiency, grain loss estimates, and equipment OEE reporting. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center equipment configuration data.']
  harvest_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the harvest pass began on the field. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center GPS telematics. Used for OEE calculation and equipment utilization reporting.']
  harvest_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the yield record. \'draft\' indicates data is being captured; \'in_progress\' indicates active harvest pass; \'completed\' indicates finalized and reconciled; \'cancelled\' indicates voided record; \'pending_review\' indicates awaiting quality or agronomic sign-off.']
  harvested_area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'GPS-tracked actual harvested area in acres for this harvest pass. May differ from planted area due to field boundaries, obstacles, or partial harvests. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center precision agriculture data. Used as denominator for yield per acre (YPM) calculations.']
  moisture_adjusted_production_bu decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total production adjusted to standard moisture basis in bushels. Calculated as: total_production_bu × ((100 - moisture_pct_at_harvest) / (100 - standard_moisture_pct)). Used for grain settlement, sales contract fulfillment, and financial crop enterprise accounting.']
  moisture_pct_at_harvest decimal(5,2) [note: 'Grain moisture content percentage measured at the time of harvest (as-harvested moisture). Critical for drying cost calculation, storage decisions, and moisture-adjusted yield computation. Sourced from combine moisture sensor data via John Deere Operations Center.']
  mrl_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the harvested crop has been assessed as compliant with applicable Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for pesticide residues per EPA, Codex Alimentarius, and destination market requirements. Required for export documentation and food safety compliance.']
  ndvi_at_harvest decimal(5,4) [note: 'NDVI value recorded for the field at or near the harvest date. Ranges from -1 to +1; values above 0.6 indicate healthy dense canopy. Used to correlate remote sensing data with actual yield outcomes for predictive yield modeling and AI/ML applications.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this harvest was produced under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified conditions. Drives premium pricing, segregation requirements, and organic certificate of analysis (COA) documentation.']
  phi_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for all applied crop protection products has been satisfied prior to this harvest date. Mandatory compliance check under EPA pesticide registration requirements and FSMA produce safety rules.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this yield record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across John Deere Operations Center, Climate FieldView, Trimble Ag Software, and Granular Farm Management integrations.']
  standard_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Industry-standard moisture basis used for moisture-adjusted yield calculation (e.g., 15.5% for corn, 13% for soybeans). Defined by USDA grain grading standards and used as the reference point for dry bushel equivalents.']
  test_weight_lbs_per_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Grain test weight measured in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu) at harvest. Key grain quality indicator used for USDA grade determination. Below-standard test weight may result in grade discounts at grain elevator. Sourced from combine sensors or grain elevator COA.']
  total_production_bu decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total gross grain production in bushels (BU) recorded for this harvest pass at as-harvested moisture. Primary production quantity used for grain receipt (GRN) creation, sales contract fulfillment, and crop insurance documentation.']
  total_production_mt decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total gross grain production converted to metric tons (MT) for international trade, export documentation, and financial reporting under IFRS. Derived from total_production_bu using commodity-specific conversion factors.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this yield record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  yield_per_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual harvested yield expressed as bushels per acre (BU/acre) for this harvest pass. Core agronomic performance metric (YPM) used for benchmarking against field crop plan targets, historical averages, and regional USDA NASS yield data.']
  yield_target_bu_per_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned yield target in bushels per acre (BU/acre) from the associated crop plan for this field-season-variety combination. Used to compute yield variance and performance against plan for financial crop enterprise accounting.']
  yield_variance_bu_per_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Difference between actual yield_per_acre and yield_target_bu_per_acre. Positive value indicates above-plan performance; negative value indicates shortfall. Used for financial crop enterprise accounting, crop insurance (USDA RMA APH), and agronomic benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all yield and harvest performance data at the field-season-variety level. Captures individual harvest operation details: harvest date, combine/equipment used, operator, load ticket references, moisture content at harvest, test weight, grain quality grade, GPS-tracked harvest area, and field coverage. Aggregates production metrics: total production (BU, MT, CWT), average yield per acre (YPM), moisture-adjusted yield, quality grade distribution, and variance against the field crop plan\'s yield target. Each harvest pass on a field is recorded as a yield_record entry, with season-level rollups computed for financial and agronomic reporting. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center and Climate FieldView harvest data. Serves as the authoritative yield SSOT for financial crop enterprise accounting, grain receipt (GRN) creation, sales contract fulfillment, crop insurance (USDA RMA) documentation, and agronomic benchmarking. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA PP and Granular Farm Management.'
}

Table crop.rotation_plan {
  rotation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rotation_plan']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Quality audits (GLOBALG.A.P., organic certification, NRCS practice verification) review the multi-year rotation plan as a primary audit artifact. Auditors and certification bodies require direct linka']
  contract_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Long-term supply contracts in Agriculture often specify rotation requirements (organic transition, sustainability programs). Linking rotation_plan to the contracting buyer account supports multi-year ']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: Rotation plans drive multi-year financial planning. Farm financial managers link rotation plans to enterprise budgets for long-term cost-of-production modeling, break-even analysis across rotation cyc']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A rotation plan defines the primary crop in a multi-year rotation sequence for a field or farm unit. The crop master provides reference data for rotation compatibility (rotation_compatible flag), nitr']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm or operational unit under which this rotation plan is managed, supporting multi-field farm-level rotation planning and NRCS compliance reporting.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific field or farm unit for which this rotation plan is designed, linking to the land and soil domain field master record.']
  government_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.government_program. Business justification: Rotation plans are directly tied to NRCS conservation programs (CSP, CRP, RCPP). rotation_plan has denormalized csp_contract_number and nrcs_practice_code attributes. A direct FK to government_program']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the current active growing season within this rotation plan, linking to the season master for calendar year and crop year alignment.']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: NRCS conservation planning and CSP contracts require zone-level crop rotation documentation for soil health optimization. Rotation plans authored at management zone level use zone-specific OM%, draina']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: rotation_plan has organic_certified boolean but no FK to the specific organic certification. NOP system plan requirements mandate that organic rotation plans reference the certifying agent\'s certifica']
  primary_rotation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the certified agronomist or crop advisor responsible for designing and maintaining this rotation plan, used for GAP accountability and NRCS documentation.']
  primary_rotation_seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop variety planned for the second season/year of the rotation cycle. Supports multi-year rotation sequencing in Granular Farm Management.']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile. Business justification: Multi-year rotation plans are designed around soil profile characteristics (land capability class, drainage class, depth-to-restrictive-layer). NRCS conservation planning and CSP contracts require rot']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this rotation plan was formally approved by the responsible agronomist or NRCS agent. Required for NRCS CSP contract initiation and GAP compliance audit trails.']
  cover_crop_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more cover crop phases are included in this rotation plan. True = cover crops are part of the rotation sequence; False = no cover crop phases planned. Relevant for NRCS CSP cover crop practice eligibility.']
  cover_crop_species varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of cover crop species included in the rotation (e.g., \'Winter Rye, Hairy Vetch, Crimson Clover\'). Supports NRCS CSP practice documentation and soil health certification.']
  cover_crop_termination_method varchar [note: 'Method used to terminate cover crops prior to cash crop planting. Impacts soil disturbance, residue management, and organic certification compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: herbicide|tillage|roller_crimper|frost|mowing|grazing — promote to reference product if additional methods required]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rotation plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and NRCS CSP compliance documentation.']
  current_cycle_year varchar [note: 'The current year position within the rotation cycle (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 for a 3-year rotation). Tracks where the field is in the rotation sequence for operational scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this rotation plan expires or the final rotation cycle concludes. Nullable for open-ended plans. Used for NRCS CSP contract period management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this rotation plan becomes binding and the first rotation cycle begins. Used for NRCS CSP compliance period tracking and Granular Farm Management scheduling.']
  globalg_ap_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rotation plan is aligned with GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification requirements for soil and crop management.']
  gmo_trait_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any crop variety in this rotation plan includes genetically modified organism (GMO) traits. True = GMO varieties included; False = non-GMO rotation. Required for COOL and export compliance documentation.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent agronomic review of this rotation plan. Annual reviews are required for NRCS CSP compliance and GLOBALG.A.P. certification maintenance.']
  nitrogen_credit_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated nitrogen (N) credit in pounds per acre attributed to legume phases or cover crops in the rotation, reducing synthetic nitrogen (NPK) fertilizer requirements for subsequent cash crops.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text agronomic rationale, special instructions, or compliance notes for this rotation plan. Captures context such as pest pressure history, soil remediation objectives, or NRCS agent recommendations.']
  nrcs_practice_code varchar [note: 'USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) conservation practice standard code associated with this rotation plan (e.g., \'328\' for Conservation Crop Rotation). Required for CSP and EQIP payment eligibility documentation.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rotation plan is designed to meet USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification requirements. True = organic-compliant rotation; False = conventional rotation.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the rotation plan (e.g., \'Corn-Soybean-Wheat 3-Year Rotation - North Fields\'), used for identification in Granular Farm Management and agronomist reporting.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the rotation plan, used in agronomist communications, NRCS documentation, and Granular Farm Management records. Format: ROT-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rotation plan. \'draft\' = under development; \'active\' = currently being executed; \'suspended\' = temporarily paused; \'completed\' = all rotation cycles finished; \'cancelled\' = plan abandoned.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the rotation plan, incremented each time the plan is revised. Supports audit trail and change management for NRCS and GAP compliance documentation.']
  planned_area_acres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total field area in acres covered by this rotation plan. Used for input planning (seed, fertilizer, chemical), yield target setting, and NRCS CSP payment calculation.']
  primary_agronomic_goal varchar [note: 'The principal agronomic objective driving this rotation plan design (e.g., \'Soil organic matter improvement\', \'Soybean cyst nematode suppression\', \'Nitrogen fixation via legume phase\', \'Weed resistance management\'). Supports NRCS CSP documentation and GAP rationale.']
  rotation_cycle_years varchar [note: 'Total number of years in one complete rotation cycle (e.g., 2 for corn-soybean, 3 for corn-soybean-wheat). Defines the repeating sequence length for soil health and pest cycle management.']
  rotation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rotation plan type. \'simple\' = two-crop alternation; \'complex\' = three or more crops; \'cover_crop\' = includes dedicated cover crop phases; \'organic\' = certified organic rotation; \'conventional\' = standard agrochemical-supported rotation.']
  soil_health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Baseline composite soil health score for the field at the time of plan creation, derived from soil organic matter (OM), biological activity, and physical structure assessments. Supports NRCS CSP soil health practice documentation.']
  soil_om_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Baseline soil organic matter (OM) percentage at plan initiation, expressed as a percentage of dry soil weight. Key indicator for rotation plan design and NRCS CSP soil health improvement targets.']
  target_om_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target soil organic matter (OM) percentage to be achieved by the end of the rotation plan period. Defines the agronomic improvement goal and supports NRCS CSP outcome measurement.']
  tillage_system varchar [note: 'Tillage system applied across this rotation plan, governing soil disturbance practices. Influences soil organic matter (OM) accumulation, erosion risk, and NRCS conservation compliance scoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this rotation plan record was last modified. Supports change management, version control, and NRCS CSP audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Multi-year crop rotation plan for a field or farm unit, defining the planned sequence of crops and cover crops across growing seasons to optimize soil health, break pest and disease cycles, manage nutrient cycling, and support conservation compliance. Captures rotation cycle length (years), crop sequence by season, cover crop species and termination timing, tillage system transitions, and agronomic rationale for each rotation choice. Supports NRCS Conservation Stewardship Program (CSP) documentation, GAP soil health certification, and long-term land productivity management within Granular Farm Management.'
}

Table crop.loss_event {
  loss_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for loss_event']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Crop loss events (hail, disease, flood) must reference the affected commodity lot for RMA crop insurance claim submission, inventory write-down posting, and indemnity calculation. Adjusters and contro']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A loss event records crop damage or destruction affecting a field. The crop master provides reference data for the affected crop type, including disease susceptibility, drought tolerance, and market p']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed crop insurance loss adjuster assigned to assess and document the loss event for USDA RMA claim processing.']
  fault_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.equipment_fault. Business justification: Crop losses caused by equipment failures (combine fire, planter skip, sprayer malfunction) must link to the causative equipment fault for insurance claim documentation, root cause analysis, and warran']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active crop plan for the affected field and season, linking the loss event to the planned agronomic program, yield targets, and crop variety.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field where the crop loss event occurred. Links to the field master record for geospatial and agronomic context.']
  government_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.government_program. Business justification: Crop loss events trigger USDA government program claims (NAP, WHIP+, crop insurance indemnity). The loss_event carries insurance_policy_number as a denormalized attribute; a proper FK to government_pr']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop production season (e.g., Spring 2024, Fall 2024) during which the loss event occurred. Used for seasonal aggregation and insurance policy alignment.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Crop loss events trigger insurance indemnity journal entries and inventory write-downs. Direct traceability from loss event to journal entry is required for audit trails, SOX compliance, and RMA/USDA ']
  livestock_damage_herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Crop damage attribution: crop loss events caused by livestock (fence failure, overgrazing, trespass) require identifying the responsible herd for crop insurance claims, internal liability reporting, a']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Crop loss events caused by mycotoxin contamination, pesticide drift, or food safety hazards generate quality nonconformances. Linking loss_event to nonconformance supports insurance claim documentatio']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Force majeure and contract renegotiation: when a crop loss event occurs, the affected sales contracts must be identified immediately for renegotiation or force majeure invocation. Every grain merchand']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific crop variety affected by the loss event. Supports variety-level loss analysis and insurance claim documentation.']
  yield_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.yield_record. Business justification: loss_event has no FK to yield_record, yet crop loss events directly impact the yield outcomes captured in yield_record. Adding yield_record_id to loss_event enables direct linkage between documented l']
  adjuster_inspection_date date [note: 'The date on which the licensed crop insurance adjuster conducted the field inspection to assess the loss event. Required for USDA RMA claim documentation and loss adjustment timeline compliance.']
  adjuster_reference_number varchar [note: 'The adjuster\'s internal reference or case number assigned during the field inspection and loss assessment process. Used for cross-referencing adjuster reports with the insurance claim file.']
  affected_area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total acreage of the field or portion thereof affected by the crop loss event, measured in acres. Used for indemnity calculation and USDA RMA claim documentation. Aligns with FSA CLU (Common Land Unit) acreage records.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess and quantify the crop loss or damage. Determines the evidentiary basis for the insurance claim and agronomic documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual_inspection|drone_survey|adjuster_inspection|satellite_imagery|yield_monitor|lab_sample — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the crop loss event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, SOX compliance, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  crop_growth_stage varchar [note: 'The phenological growth stage of the crop at the time of the loss event (e.g., V6, R3 for corn; Feekes 10.5 for wheat). Critical for determining yield impact severity and insurance coverage applicability under MPCI policy.']
  estimated_indemnity_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'The estimated indemnity payment amount in US dollars calculated based on the assessed yield loss, insured price, and policy coverage level. Preliminary figure prior to final indemnity determination.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the crop loss event, used in communications with insurers, adjusters, and USDA RMA. Typically assigned by the farm management or ERP system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA QM module).']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the crop loss event from initial reporting through final indemnity determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reported|under_review|adjuster_assigned|assessed|claim_filed|settled|closed|denied — promote to reference product]']
  expected_yield_bu_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'The approved actual production history (APH) or expected yield in bushels per acre for the insured unit at the time of the loss event. Baseline for indemnity calculation under MPCI policy.']
  final_indemnity_usd decimal(14,2) [note: 'The final approved indemnity payment amount in US dollars as determined by the approved insurance provider (AIP) and USDA RMA following completion of the loss adjustment process. Used for financial loss reporting and SAP FI/CO posting.']
  gis_polygon_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the GIS polygon or spatial feature in Esri ArcGIS representing the precise geographic boundary of the affected area within the field. Enables spatial analysis and precision agriculture integration.']
  indemnity_determination_date date [note: 'The date on which the final indemnity amount was officially determined and approved by the insurance provider and USDA RMA. Triggers financial posting in SAP FI and revenue recognition under GAAP/IFRS.']
  insurance_claim_number varchar [note: 'The unique claim number assigned by the approved insurance provider (AIP) or USDA RMA upon formal submission of the loss event claim. Used for claim tracking and ADM system integration.']
  loss_cause varchar [note: 'Primary cause or peril responsible for the crop loss or damage event. Determines applicable insurance coverage type and USDA RMA cause-of-loss code. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hail|drought|flood|frost|pest|disease|wind|fire|excess_moisture|prevented_planting — promote to reference product]']
  loss_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific cause of loss, providing additional context beyond the primary cause code (e.g., \'Late-season frost event on 2024-04-15, temperatures dropped to -3°C for 4 hours\'). Supports adjuster documentation and RMA claim narrative.']
  loss_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the crop loss or damage event occurred or was first observed. This is the principal real-world event date used for insurance claim eligibility and PHI/REI compliance verification.']
  loss_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the crop loss event including observed damage symptoms, affected crop growth stage, and initial field conditions. Used for agronomic risk management and insurance documentation.']
  loss_severity varchar [note: 'Categorical severity classification of the crop loss event based on the assessed yield loss percentage. Used for prioritization of adjuster resources, financial risk reporting, and agronomic risk management KPI tracking.']
  ndvi_at_loss decimal(5,4) [note: 'NDVI value recorded for the affected field area at or near the time of the loss event, derived from satellite or drone imagery. Provides objective remote-sensing evidence of crop health degradation for insurance and agronomic documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, adjuster comments, or agronomic observations related to the loss event that do not fit structured fields. Supports comprehensive documentation for insurance and compliance purposes.']
  phi_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the crop loss event and any associated pesticide applications were compliant with the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) requirements at the time of loss. Critical for food safety and MRL compliance documentation under FSMA and EPA regulations.']
  policy_coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of crop insurance policy coverage applicable to this loss event. Determines the indemnity calculation methodology and USDA RMA reporting requirements.']
  prevented_planting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the loss event qualifies as a prevented planting claim under USDA RMA MPCI policy, where the insured was unable to plant the insured crop by the final planting date due to an insured cause of loss.']
  replanting_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agronomist or loss adjuster has recommended replanting of the affected area following the loss event. Triggers replanting payment eligibility assessment under MPCI policy.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the crop loss event was formally reported to the crop insurance agent or USDA RMA. Critical for claim filing deadline compliance under MPCI policy terms.']
  rma_adm_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the loss event claim data was electronically submitted to the USDA Risk Management Agency (RMA) Actuarial Data Master (ADM) system for official claim processing and reinsurance reporting.']
  total_field_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total insured acreage of the field at the time of the loss event. Used to calculate the percentage of field affected and to validate affected_area_acres against the insured unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the crop loss event record was last modified. Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and change management for insurance claim documentation.']
  yield_loss_bu_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated yield loss expressed in bushels per acre (BU/acre) resulting from the crop loss event. Primary quantitative metric for MPCI indemnity calculation and agronomic impact assessment. Aligns with YPM reporting standards.']
  yield_loss_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated yield loss expressed as a percentage of the expected yield for the affected area. Used for crop-hail policy claims and comparative loss analysis across fields and seasons.']

  Note: 'Record of a crop loss or damage event affecting a field or portion thereof, capturing loss date, cause (hail, drought, flood, frost, pest, disease, wind), estimated yield impact (BU/acre or % loss), affected acreage, loss assessment method (visual, drone survey, adjuster inspection), adjuster reference, crop insurance claim number, policy reference, and final indemnity determination. May be triggered by scouting observations identifying severe damage or by weather event alerts. Supports crop insurance (USDA RMA) claim processing, MPCI and crop-hail policy documentation, financial loss reporting, and agronomic risk management. Integrates with USDA RMA ADM system for electronic claim submission.'
}

Table crop.cover_crop {
  cover_crop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the cover crop planting record. Primary key for the cover_crop entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planting or seeding equipment used to establish the cover crop. Supports equipment utilization tracking, calibration records, and field operation cost allocation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cover crop programs generate direct costs (seed_cost_usd is already tracked on the record) for seed, planting, and termination that must be allocated to cost centers. CSP and EQIP program cost trackin']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Cover crop planting and termination are crew-executed field operations. Crew-level tracking supports H2A labor compliance, NRCS conservation practice cost documentation, and payroll allocation. Curren']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A cover crop planting references a specific crop species used for soil health and conservation. The species_scientific_name column (STRING) is a denormalized field that duplicates crop.scientific_name']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: cover_crop links to rotation_plan and growing_season but has no FK to field_crop_plan. Cover crop plantings are integral to the field-level agronomic plan — the field_crop_plan\'s organic_certified and']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field on which this cover crop was planted. Links to the land and soil domain field master record.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Cover crop planting and termination are executed as field operations. Linking enables equipment utilization tracking for cover crop activities, NRCS practice implementation documentation, and CSP paym']
  goods_issue_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_issue. Business justification: Cover crop seeding consumes seed inventory and requires a goods issue posting for seed cost tracking, CSP/EQIP program cost documentation, and input cost-per-acre reporting. Cover crop programs requir']
  government_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.government_program. Business justification: Cover crops are frequently enrolled in CSP, EQIP, and RCPP government programs. cover_crop has a denormalized csp_contract_number attribute. A direct FK to government_program replaces this denormaliza']
  grazing_herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Cover crop grazing management: in integrated crop-livestock operations, cover crops are commonly terminated by grazing. Tracking which herd grazed the cover crop is required for herd nutrition records']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season during which this cover crop planting is associated. Typically the off-season or inter-season period between primary cash crop seasons.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Cover crop biomass and soil health lab samples (soil organic matter, nitrogen credits, pathogen screening) are collected post-termination to validate NRCS practice compliance and nitrogen credit claim']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Cover crops in certified organic systems must use organically produced seed and approved practices. organic_certification_id links the cover crop record to the NOP certification covering the field ope']
  parent_cover_crop_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cover_crop (parent_cover_crop_id)']
  primary_cover_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agronomist or crop advisor responsible for prescribing and overseeing this cover crop planting program.']
  rotation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the crop rotation plan that prescribes this cover crop planting as part of a multi-season soil health or conservation strategy.']
  seed_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seed lot used for this cover crop planting. Enables traceability from field planting back to seed origin, certification class, germination test results, and supplier.']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Cover crop records reference seed_lot but variety selection for cover crops is made at planning stage before a specific lot is assigned. Linking to seed_variety supports cover crop performance analysi']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.work_order. Business justification: Cover crop planting and termination operations require work orders for equipment scheduling and cost tracking. NRCS/CSP practice payment documentation requires linking cover crop field activities to a']
  biomass_estimate_lbs_acre decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated above-ground dry biomass produced by the cover crop at termination, expressed in pounds per acre. Key indicator of soil organic matter contribution, nitrogen cycling potential, and weed suppression effectiveness.']
  cover_crop_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the cover crop by plant family or agronomic category. Determines nitrogen fixation potential, biomass contribution, and soil health benefit. \'mix\' indicates a multi-species blend.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cover crop planting record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  days_to_termination varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between the cover crop planting date and the actual termination date. Indicates the length of the cover crop growing window and is used to assess biomass accumulation potential and program compliance.']
  establishment_rating varchar [note: 'Field-assessed qualitative rating of cover crop stand establishment success. Evaluated by agronomist or field scout based on germination uniformity, plant density, and canopy coverage. Informs replanting decisions.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cover crop termination timing and method comply with FSMA Produce Safety Rule requirements for application of biological soil amendments. Particularly relevant for fields transitioning to fresh produce crops.']
  gps_coverage_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON polygon representing the GPS-mapped boundary of the actual cover crop planted area within the field. Supports precision agriculture spatial analysis, as-applied mapping, and NRCS program acreage verification.']
  is_bmp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cover crop planting meets applicable Best Management Practices (BMP) for water quality, nutrient management, and erosion control as defined by state or federal environmental agencies.']
  ndvi_at_termination decimal(5,4) [note: 'NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) value measured at or near the cover crop termination date. Provides an objective, remote-sensing-based indicator of cover crop biomass and canopy health at termination.']
  nitrogen_credit_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated plant-available nitrogen credit in pounds per acre attributed to legume cover crop nitrogen fixation or biomass decomposition. Used to adjust NPK fertilizer application rates for the subsequent cash crop season.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for agronomist or operator observations regarding this cover crop planting, including establishment challenges, weather impacts, pest pressure, or special management actions taken.']
  nrcs_practice_code varchar [note: 'USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service conservation practice standard code associated with this cover crop planting (e.g., \'340\' for Cover Crop). Required for EQIP, CSP, and other USDA conservation program payment documentation.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cover crop planting is conducted under a certified organic production system in compliance with USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. Affects allowable termination methods and input restrictions.']
  planned_planting_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled planting date for the cover crop as defined in the rotation plan or agronomic prescription. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual planting timing.']
  planted_area_acres decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total area in acres on which the cover crop was successfully planted. Used for seed cost accounting, NRCS program payment calculations, and soil health program reporting.']
  planting_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which the cover crop seed was planted or broadcast in the field. Used for growing degree day (GDD) accumulation tracking, establishment assessment, and program compliance verification.']
  planting_method varchar [note: 'Method used to establish the cover crop. Affects seed-to-soil contact, establishment success rate, and seeding rate requirements. \'interseeding\' refers to planting into a standing cash crop canopy.']
  planting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cover crop planting record. Tracks progression from planning through establishment and termination. \'failed\' indicates the cover crop did not establish adequately.']
  prescribed_seeding_rate_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Agronomist-prescribed target seeding rate in pounds per acre as specified in the cover crop prescription or rotation plan. Enables variance analysis against actual seeding rate applied.']
  primary_goal varchar [note: 'Primary agronomic or conservation objective for planting this cover crop. Drives species selection, seeding rate, and termination timing decisions. Used in NRCS program reporting and CSP payment calculations.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this cover crop planting record. Used in field documentation, NRCS program submissions, and CSP contract reporting. Format: CC-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.']
  seed_cost_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total seed cost in US dollars for this cover crop planting, calculated from seed lot unit cost and quantity used. Used in enterprise budget tracking, NRCS program cost-share calculations, and crop enterprise profitability analysis.']
  seeding_rate_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual seeding rate applied for the cover crop, expressed in pounds of seed per acre. Used to verify compliance with NRCS practice standards, calculate seed costs, and assess establishment adequacy.']
  soil_cover_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of soil surface covered by the cover crop canopy or residue at peak growth or at termination. Key metric for erosion control effectiveness and NRCS practice standard compliance verification.']
  soil_temperature_at_planting_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Soil temperature in degrees Celsius measured at the time of cover crop planting. Critical for predicting germination success and establishment timing, particularly for cool-season cover crop species.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this cover crop planting record originated. Supports data lineage, integration audit trails, and conflict resolution in the FMIS data lakehouse.']
  species_mix_name varchar [note: 'Common name or blend designation of the cover crop species or species mix planted (e.g., \'Winter Rye\', \'Crimson Clover\', \'Cereal Rye + Hairy Vetch Mix\'). Supports soil health program documentation and seed procurement.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the cover crop was terminated (killed or incorporated). Critical for calculating days of cover, biomass accumulation, and ensuring adequate decomposition time before primary crop planting.']
  termination_method varchar [note: 'Method used to terminate (kill) the cover crop prior to primary crop planting. Affects residue management, soil disturbance, and organic certification compliance. \'roller_crimper\' is preferred in no-till and organic systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cover crop planting record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for cover crop plantings used in conservation and soil health programs between primary cash crop seasons. Captures cover crop species or mix, seeding rate, planting date, termination date and method, biomass estimate, and associated field and growing season references.'
}

Table crop.harvest_event {
  harvest_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each harvest event record. Primary key for the harvest_event data product in the crop domain Silver layer.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary harvest equipment (e.g., combine harvester) used during this event. Supports equipment utilization, OEE tracking, and maintenance scheduling.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Harvest events are recorded against a specific commodity for grain elevator delivery, load ticket generation, and FSMA traceability. commodity_id normalizes the denormalized crop_type string and enabl']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Each harvest event produces a commodity lot in inventory. This link supports harvest-to-inventory traceability for food safety audits, grain marketing, and FSMA compliance. grn_number on harvest_event']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Harvest events generate direct costs (combine operation, drying, hauling) that must be allocated to cost centers for harvest cost tracking and cost-of-production reporting. Farm ERP systems require co']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Harvest crew tracking: harvest operations are crew-based in large-scale agriculture. Linking harvest_event to crew enables piece-rate payroll calculation, H2A compliance verification during harvest, a']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A harvest event records the actual harvest operation for a specific crop on a field. The crop master provides reference data for harvest method, yield units, test weight standards, and MRL compliance ']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Contract grain delivery: harvest events must record which customer receiving facility (elevator, processor, food manufacturer) accepted the harvested commodity. Supports FSMA traceability, weight tick']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator or farm worker who executed the harvest operation. Used for workforce accountability, GAP compliance, and operator performance tracking.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the field crop plan that governed the production cycle for this harvest event, linking agronomic planning to actual harvest execution.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field on which this harvest event was executed. Links to the field master record in the land and soil domain.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Harvest events are the agronomic record; field_operation is the equipment execution record. Linking enables combine OEE analysis, fuel cost per bushel, and harvest efficiency benchmarking. Standard pr']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: Harvest events record is_gmo boolean but lack a direct reference to the formal GMO declaration. Identity preservation programs, USDA BE disclosure requirements, and export market approvals require har']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Harvest quality grading at the event level references the applicable USDA grading standard for load ticket acceptance at grain elevators and price determination. grading_standard_id normalizes the den']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season during which this harvest event occurred, enabling season-level aggregation and year-over-year yield comparisons.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection.
