Business justification: Harvest food safety inspections (GAP harvest hygiene, FSMA produce safety, equipment sanitation checks) are a named regulatory requirement tied directly to harvest operations. harvest_event has no exi']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Harvest events trigger inventory receipt journal entries (debit grain inventory, credit production). Direct traceability from harvest event to journal entry is required for inventory valuation audits,']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Yield mapping records harvest data by management zone for precision agriculture reporting and variable rate prescription calibration. harvest_event captures gross_weight_bu and moisture_pct_at_harvest']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Food safety audits, export certificates, and FSMA produce safety rule compliance require ']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Harvest events record organic_certified boolean but lack a direct FK to the specific organic certification. NOP compliance, organic premium pricing, and weight ticket documentation require harvest eve']
  parent_harvest_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on harvest_event (parent_harvest_event_id)']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Contract fulfillment at harvest: harvest events are applied against open sales contracts in grain merchandising. Linking harvest_event to the contract being fulfilled enables real-time contract positi']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific crop variety harvested during this event, supporting variety-level yield performance analysis and seed selection decisions.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the grain bin, elevator, or storage facility where harvested commodity was delivered. Supports inventory management, traceability, and WMS integration.']
  yield_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the final reconciled yield record associated with this harvest event. Establishes the link between the operational harvest transaction and the authoritative yield data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this harvest event record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crop_growth_stage varchar [note: 'BBCH or crop-specific growth stage code at the time of harvest (e.g., R6 for corn black layer, R8 for soybean full maturity). Indicates physiological maturity and harvest readiness.']
  damaged_kernels_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of damaged, heat-damaged, or diseased kernels in the harvested grain sample. Impacts grain grade, price dockage, and storage risk assessment.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this harvest event, used in operational communications, load tickets, and cross-system references (e.g., HRV-2024-00123). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PP or John Deere Operations Center.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the harvest event, tracking progression from planning through execution to completion or cancellation.']
  foreign_material_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Percentage of foreign material (non-grain matter) present in the harvested sample. Used for grain grading, dockage calculation, and quality compliance per USDA GIPSA standards.']
  gps_coverage_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON polygon or multipolygon representing the actual GPS-tracked area covered during the harvest operation. Enables precision agriculture analysis, as-harvested mapping, and FSA acreage verification.']
  gross_weight_bu decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total gross weight of harvested grain or commodity measured in bushels (BU) at the time of harvest, prior to moisture adjustment or dockage. Primary yield quantity metric for grain crops.']
  gross_weight_mt decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total gross weight of harvested commodity in metric tons (MT), supporting international trade reporting, export documentation, and CIF/FOB calculations.']
  harvest_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the harvest operation was performed. Used for PHI compliance verification, crop insurance reporting, and seasonal analytics. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  harvest_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the harvest operation was completed on the field. Combined with start timestamp to compute actual harvest duration for OEE and productivity analysis.']
  harvest_loss_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Estimated percentage of harvestable yield lost during the harvest operation due to equipment settings, crop conditions, or field factors. Used for equipment calibration and operational efficiency improvement.']
  harvest_method varchar [note: 'Method or equipment type used to harvest the crop. Influences post-harvest handling procedures, grain quality outcomes, and harvest loss estimates.']
  harvest_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the harvest operation commenced on the field. Used for equipment utilization analysis, OEE calculation, and operator time tracking.']
  harvested_area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total field area harvested during this event, measured in acres. Used for yield-per-acre calculations, crop insurance reporting, and FSA acreage certification.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the harvested crop contains genetically modified organism (GMO) traits. Required for identity preservation, COOL labeling, export documentation, and USDA APHIS compliance.']
  moisture_pct_at_harvest decimal(6,3) [note: 'Grain moisture content percentage measured at the time of harvest. Critical for determining drying requirements, storage eligibility, and moisture-adjusted yield calculations. Industry standard for corn is 15.5%, soybeans 13%.']
  mrl_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the harvested crop meets Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) requirements for pesticide residues as established by EPA, FDA, and applicable international standards (Codex Alimentarius).']
  ndvi_at_harvest decimal(6,4) [note: 'NDVI value captured from satellite or drone imagery at or near the time of harvest. Used to correlate canopy health with final yield outcomes and validate remote sensing models.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or agronomist observations about the harvest event, including equipment issues, field conditions, crop anomalies, or other contextual information not captured in structured fields.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the harvested crop was produced under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified conditions. Required for organic premium pricing, identity preservation, and NOP compliance documentation.']
  phi_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for all applied crop protection products has been satisfied prior to this harvest event. Mandatory for food safety and MRL compliance under FSMA and EPA regulations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this harvest event data originated (e.g., John Deere Operations Center, Climate FieldView, SAP S/4HANA PP). Supports data lineage and SSOT governance.']
  source_system_event_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this harvest event in the originating operational system (e.g., John Deere Operations Center machine data ID, Climate FieldView harvest activity ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  standard_moisture_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Industry-standard moisture percentage used as the basis for moisture-adjusted yield calculations and grain pricing (e.g., 15.5% for corn, 13.0% for soybeans per USDA GIPSA standards).']
  test_weight_lbs_per_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Grain density measurement expressed as pounds per bushel (lbs/bu), indicating grain quality and maturity. Used for grade determination and price dockage calculations per USDA GIPSA standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this harvest event record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an actual harvest operation executed on a field, capturing harvest date, operator, equipment used, crop harvested, field area harvested, moisture at harvest, yield weight, and grain quality observations. Links to yield_record for final reconciled yield data.'
}

Table crop.crop {
  crop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for crop']
  parent_crop_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on crop (parent_crop_id)']
  average_yield decimal(10,2) [note: 'Historical average yield for this crop expressed in the yield_unit, used for crop planning and forecasting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crop master record was first created in the system.']
  crop_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the crop type based on agricultural use and market segment.']
  crop_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code used for crop identification in Farm Management Information Systems (FMIS) and regulatory reporting. Externally-known unique identifier.']
  crop_name varchar [note: 'Common name of the crop (e.g., Corn, Wheat, Soybeans, Cotton). Human-readable identifier used across all agronomic systems.']
  crop_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the crop in the master reference system. Active crops are available for planting schedules and field assignments.']
  crop_type varchar [note: 'Classification based on the crop\'s lifecycle duration: annual (single season), perennial (multi-year), or biennial (two-year cycle).']
  disease_susceptibility varchar [note: 'General susceptibility of the crop to disease infections, used in IPM-based disease management strategies.']
  drought_tolerance varchar [note: 'Crop\'s ability to withstand water stress and drought conditions, critical for irrigation planning.']
  frost_tolerance varchar [note: 'Crop\'s ability to withstand frost conditions during growing season.']
  gmo_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether genetically modified varieties of this crop are available and approved for cultivation.']
  growing_season varchar [note: 'Primary season during which the crop is typically cultivated, used for crop planning and rotation scheduling.']
  harvest_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used for harvesting the crop: manual labor, mechanical equipment, or combination.']
  market_price_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current market price per yield unit (e.g., price per bushel, price per kilogram), used for revenue forecasting and crop selection decisions.']
  maturity_days_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days from planting to harvest maturity for late-maturing varieties of this crop.']
  maturity_days_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days from planting to harvest maturity for early-maturing varieties of this crop.']
  ndvi_threshold_healthy decimal(4,3) [note: 'Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) threshold value indicating healthy crop vigor, used for remote sensing and field health monitoring.']
  nitrogen_requirement_kg_per_hectare decimal(8,2) [note: 'Recommended nitrogen fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield, used in Variable Rate Application (VRA) prescriptions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional agronomic notes, special cultivation requirements, or regional considerations for this crop.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crop can be grown under organic certification standards.']
  pest_susceptibility varchar [note: 'General susceptibility of the crop to pest infestations, used in Integrated Pest Management (IPM) planning.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) - minimum number of days required between last pesticide application and harvest to ensure compliance with food safety regulations.']
  phosphorus_requirement_kg_per_hectare decimal(8,2) [note: 'Recommended phosphorus fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield.']
  planting_depth_cm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended seed planting depth in centimeters for optimal germination and establishment.']
  potassium_requirement_kg_per_hectare decimal(8,2) [note: 'Recommended potassium fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval (REI) - minimum number of hours workers must wait before entering treated fields after pesticide application, ensuring worker safety compliance.']
  rotation_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crop is suitable for inclusion in crop rotation schedules to maintain soil health and reduce pest pressure.']
  row_spacing_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recommended distance between crop rows in centimeters for optimal growth and equipment access.']
  scientific_name varchar [note: 'Scientific botanical name of the crop using binomial nomenclature (e.g., Zea mays for corn, Triticum aestivum for wheat).']
  seeding_rate_kg_per_hectare decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recommended seeding rate in kilograms per hectare for standard planting density.']
  soil_ph_max decimal(3,1) [note: 'Maximum soil pH level tolerated by the crop for healthy growth.']
  soil_ph_min decimal(3,1) [note: 'Minimum soil pH level required for optimal crop growth and nutrient availability.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in Celsius tolerated by the crop before heat stress occurs.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in Celsius required for crop germination and growth.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crop master record was last modified.']
  water_requirement_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total seasonal water requirement in millimeters, used for Evapotranspiration (ET)-driven irrigation planning.']
  yield_unit varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for crop yield reporting (e.g., bushels per acre for corn, kilograms per hectare for wheat). Used in Yield Per Minute (YPM) calculations.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for crop. Referenced by crop_id.'
}

Table livestock.animal {
  animal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each individual animal record. Serves as the primary key and universal reference across all livestock management systems.']
  breed_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the breed master record for this animal\'s registered or recognized breed (e.g., Angus, Hereford, Duroc, Merino). Supports genetic performance benchmarking, EPD (Expected Progeny Difference) analysis, and breed association reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual animal acquisition costs and capitalized breeding animal depreciation require cost center assignment for livestock enterprise budgeting and USDA farm financial reporting. A livestock accoun']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'USDA-assigned Premises Identification Number (PIN) for the physical location where the animal currently resides. 10-character alphanumeric code (3-letter state abbreviation + 7 digits). Required for USDA NAIS/ADT interstate movement documentation and disease traceability.']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the herd or flock to which this animal is currently assigned. Drives management group decisions, vaccination scheduling, and regulatory reporting at the herd level. Sourced from Granular Farm Management herd master.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Individual animals undergo USDA ante-mortem, state veterinary, and welfare inspections tracked per animal for regulatory compliance (FSIS, APHIS). A domain expert expects each animal record to referen']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: animal.organic_certified is a boolean denormalization of an organic certification record. USDA NOP requires traceability from individual animals to the specific organic certification under which they ']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the current physical pen, pasture, or paddock where the animal is housed or grazing. Supports daily management, feeding operations, and biosecurity zone tracking. Linked to the facility location master.']
  primary_dam_animal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the mother (dam) animal record within this same table. Enables maternal lineage tracing for genetic performance analysis, EPD calculations, and pedigree documentation. Null for purchased animals with unknown dam.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Each purchased animal must reference the procurement PO for cost basis establishment, financial reconciliation, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) documentation. animal.source_vendor_id already lin']
  source_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the external vendor, auction house, or producer from whom the animal was purchased. Null for born-on-farm animals. Supports supplier traceability, biosecurity source tracking, and procurement analytics in SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Purchase price or capitalized cost basis of the animal at time of acquisition, expressed in USD. For born-on-farm animals, represents the capitalized cost of raising to weaning. Used for inventory valuation, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the animal was acquired by the operation — either the birth date for born-on-farm animals or the purchase/transfer-in date for externally sourced animals. Used for inventory aging, cost basis calculation, and COOL documentation.']
  antibiotic_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the animal has never received antibiotic treatments and qualifies for \'Never Ever 3\' (NE3) or antibiotic-free marketing programs. Drives premium market channel eligibility and label claim compliance.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Actual or estimated calendar date the animal was born. Used to calculate age, determine production class eligibility, schedule vaccinations, and compute days-on-feed (DOF). Required for USDA age verification programs and COOL documentation.']
  birth_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Body weight of the animal recorded at or shortly after birth, expressed in kilograms. Baseline measurement for growth performance tracking, EPD (Expected Progeny Difference) calculations, and dam/sire genetic evaluation.']
  brand_description varchar [note: 'Description of the hot iron or freeze brand applied to the animal for permanent ownership identification. Includes brand symbol, location on body, and registration state. Required for cattle ownership verification in brand-law states and theft prevention.']
  breed_composition_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the animal\'s genetics attributable to the primary breed identified in breed_id. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 75.00 for 75% Angus). Used for crossbreeding program management, breed association registration eligibility, and genetic improvement analytics.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the animal was born, raised, or slaughtered as required by USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) regulations. Mandatory for retail muscle cut labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'ISO 8601 timestamp when the animal record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and FSMA traceability documentation. Populated automatically at record creation and never updated.']
  current_weight_kg decimal(7,2) [note: 'Most recently recorded live body weight of the animal in kilograms. Updated at each weigh event. Used for feed ration calculation, marketing weight assessment, and growth performance monitoring. Distinct from birth weight and harvest weight.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the animal was removed from active inventory through harvest, sale, death, condemnation, or transfer out. Used for inventory reconciliation, mortality reporting, and lifecycle cost analysis. Null while animal is in ACTIVE disposition status.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition status representing the animal\'s standing in the operation. ACTIVE=currently on inventory; HARVESTED=sent to harvest/slaughter; SOLD=sold to external party; DIED=natural or accidental death; CONDEMNED=condemned at ante/post-mortem inspection; TRANSFERRED_OUT=moved to another facility. Core lifecycle status field for inventory reconciliation and FSIS compliance.']
  ear_tag_number varchar [note: 'Visual alphanumeric identifier printed on the physical ear tag applied to the animal. Used for visual identification in the field and pen. May be a farm-assigned number or official USDA 840 tag number. Supports COOL country-of-origin labeling traceability.']
  eid varchar [note: 'Electronic Identification number as printed on the visual ear tag and encoded in the RFID transponder. May differ from the raw RFID tag number in formatted display. Used in John Deere Operations Center and Granular Farm Management for animal-level record linkage.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the animal is derived from genetically modified genetics (GMO), confirmed non-GMO, or status is unknown. Required for non-GMO certification programs, organic certification under USDA NOP, and export documentation to GMO-sensitive markets.']
  health_status varchar [note: 'Current veterinary health status of the animal. HEALTHY=no active health concerns; SICK=active illness under observation; QUARANTINE=isolated for biosecurity or regulatory reasons; TREATED=currently under veterinary treatment with active withdrawal period; RECOVERED=returned to healthy status post-treatment; CONDEMNED=condemned by veterinary inspection.']
  last_weigh_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the most recent live weight measurement. Used to assess recency of weight data, calculate average daily gain (ADG) since last weigh, and trigger re-weigh alerts when interval exceeds operational thresholds.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current biological and production lifecycle stage of the animal. Drives nutritional requirements, veterinary protocols, and management interventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NEONATAL|WEANED|GROWING|FINISHING|MATURE|GESTATING|LACTATING|DRY — promote to reference product]']
  official_usda_tag varchar [note: 'Official USDA 840-prefix 15-digit national uniform ear tag number assigned under the USDA Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) framework. Required for interstate movement of cattle, bison, and other covered species. Begins with country code 840 (USA).']
  origin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the animal was born on the operation, purchased from an external source, or transferred in from another company-owned facility. Critical for COOL country-of-origin labeling, FSMA traceability, and biosecurity risk classification.']
  parity_number varchar [note: 'Number of times a female animal has given birth (parturitions). Parity 0 = never calved/farrowed/lambed; Parity 1 = first calf/litter; etc. Used for reproductive performance analysis, culling decisions, and production benchmarking for dairy and cow-calf operations.']
  production_class varchar [note: 'Operational production class that defines the animal\'s role in the production system. Determines applicable management protocols, feed rations, performance benchmarks, and marketing channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: COW_CALF|STOCKER|FEEDLOT|DAIRY|BREEDING|SHOW|SEEDSTOCK|REPLACEMENT_HEIFER — promote to reference product]']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number issued by a recognized breed association (e.g., American Angus Association, National Swine Registry) for purebred or registered animals. Required for seedstock marketing, EPD publication, and breed association reporting.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: '15-digit ISO 11784/11785 compliant RFID electronic tag number embedded in the animal\'s ear tag or bolus. Primary electronic identifier used for automated reading at chutes, scales, and gates. Required for USDA NAIS traceability and APHIS interstate movement compliance.']
  sex_code varchar [note: 'Biological sex and reproductive status of the animal. M=Male (intact bull/boar/ram), F=Female (cow/sow/ewe), C=Castrated male (steer/barrow/wether), S=Spayed female, B=Barrow. Drives production class assignment, feed ration, and harvest yield expectations.']
  species_code varchar [note: 'Standardized species classification of the animal. Drives applicable regulatory frameworks (FSIS, APHIS), withdrawal period rules, and feed ration formulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOVINE|SWINE|OVINE|CAPRINE|EQUINE|POULTRY|CERVID|BISON|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  tattoo_number varchar [note: 'Permanent alphanumeric tattoo applied inside the ear or on the body for permanent identification, particularly for swine, sheep, and registered cattle. Used as a backup identifier when ear tags are lost and for breed association registration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to the animal record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and Granular Farm Management, and audit trail compliance under SOX and FSMA.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every individual animal in the herd or flock. Serves as the SSOT for animal identity across the full lifecycle from birth through harvest, sale, or disposal. Captures RFID tag, ear tag, electronic ID (EID), species, breed reference, sex, birth date, birth weight, dam/sire references (self-referencing lineage), origin (born on farm vs. purchased), current herd assignment, current location (pen/pasture), production class, production group assignment, GMO status, current lifecycle stage, and disposition status. Supports APHIS traceability requirements, USDA NAIS/RFID compliance, and COOL country-of-origin documentation.'
}

Table livestock.herd {
  herd_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a managed herd or flock grouping within the livestock management system. Primary key for all animal-level record associations.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: In contract growing and custom feeding (common in swine, poultry, and fed cattle), the integrator or packer is a customer who owns or has a forward contract on the herd. Linking herd to customer.accou']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Each herd produces a specific commodity output (beef, pork, fluid milk, broilers). Linking to product.commodity enables commodity-level cost-of-production reporting, organic premium tracking against N']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In multi-entity livestock operations, herds belong to specific legal entities (company codes) for consolidated financial reporting, intercompany transfers, and entity-level P&L. This link is required ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Herds are routinely sold under forward sales contracts specifying volume, delivery window, and price. Linking herd to contract enables contract fulfillment tracking, production planning against commit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Herd-level cost center assignment is fundamental to livestock P&L reporting — feed, labor, and veterinary costs are accumulated per herd cost center for budget variance analysis and closeout profitabi']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Livestock herds are operationally assigned to farm units for FSA compliance reporting, stocking density calculations, and cost center allocation. A livestock manager expects to know which farm unit ea']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Pasture-based livestock operations assign herds to specific fields for rotational grazing management, stocking density compliance (head/ha), and NRCS conservation practice reporting. Agronomists and l']
  herd_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or contractor designated as the responsible herd manager. Used for accountability tracking, SAP SuccessFactors workforce assignment, and regulatory contact identification for APHIS inspections.']
  herd_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm, ranch, or production facility where this herd is domiciled. Links to the land/facility master record for location-based reporting, GIS mapping via Esri ArcGIS, and USDA premises registration.']
  herd_premises_facility_id varchar [note: 'USDA APHIS-assigned Premises Identification Number (PIN) for the physical location where the herd resides. Required for Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) compliance and interstate movement documentation. Format: 3-letter state code + 7-digit number.']
  herd_veterinarian_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed veterinarian of record (VOR) responsible for health oversight of this herd. Required for VCPR (Veterinarian-Client-Patient Relationship) compliance, antibiotic prescription authorization, and FSIS residue avoidance programs.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Herds undergo PAACO welfare audits, GAP biosecurity inspections, and state veterinary herd inspections as distinct events from quality audits. A domain expert expects herd to reference its current gov']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: USDA NOP regulations require organic livestock operations to maintain herd-level certification records tied to an NOP certificate. Linking herd to organic_certification enables certification renewal t']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Herds are the primary profit center unit in livestock enterprise accounting — each herd generates revenue (sales, milk) and incurs costs tracked against a profit center for enterprise P&L. Livestock m']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Custom feeding and livestock supply agreements are herd-level contracts in commercial agriculture. Linking herd to vendor_contract enables contract compliance tracking, spend allocation by herd, and a']
  avg_body_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Average live body weight in kilograms of animals in the herd at the most recent weigh event. Used for feed conversion ratio (FCR) calculations, drug dosage determination, and market readiness assessment. Supports YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) and performance benchmarking.']
  avg_daily_gain_kg decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average daily weight gain in kilograms per animal, calculated from the most recent performance measurement period. Key production KPI for finishing herds; benchmarked against breed standards and used in feed efficiency analysis.']
  biosecurity_level varchar [note: 'Assigned biosecurity risk classification for the herd facility, determining visitor access controls, quarantine protocols, and disease surveillance intensity. Enhanced level is triggered by proximity to foreign animal disease outbreaks or APHIS disease alerts.']
  breed varchar [note: 'Primary breed or genetic line of the herd (e.g., Angus, Holstein, Duroc, Cornish Cross, Merino). Influences production performance benchmarks, feed ration planning, and genetic improvement programs tracked in breeding records.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Earliest expiration date across all active certifications (organic, GAP, GLOBALG.A.P.) held by this herd. Used to trigger recertification workflows and prevent lapsed-certification sales into premium market channels.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the herd\'s production stage or management purpose (e.g., breeding, finishing, nursery, cow-calf, stocker, replacement). Drives feed ration formulation, housing allocation, and performance KPI benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: breeding|finishing|nursery|grow-out|cow-calf|stocker|replacement|farrowing|lactating|dry|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this herd master record was first created in the FMIS or ERP system. Supports data lineage, FSMA traceability audit trails, and SOX financial reporting controls for livestock asset records.']
  current_head_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of live animals currently in the herd as of the last inventory reconciliation. Drives feed ration calculations, housing capacity utilization, and USDA NASS livestock inventory reporting. Updated upon births, deaths, purchases, and transfers.']
  disease_status varchar [note: 'Current disease surveillance status of the herd as determined by USDA APHIS or state veterinarian. \'confirmed\' status triggers mandatory reporting, movement restrictions, and potential depopulation under the National Animal Health Emergency Management System (NAHEMS).']
  dispersal_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the herd was fully dispersed through sale, harvest, or depopulation. Null for active herds. Used to close the herd lifecycle record and trigger final regulatory reporting to USDA APHIS.']
  expected_market_date date [note: 'Projected date when the herd or a cohort of animals is expected to reach market weight and be ready for sale or harvest. Used for sales order planning, slaughter scheduling, and logistics coordination with TMS and WMS systems.']
  formation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this herd grouping was formally established or first registered in the FMIS. Used for herd age tracking, performance period calculations, and audit trail compliance under FSMA traceability requirements.']
  herd_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the herd, used in operational systems such as SAP S/4HANA livestock modules, FMIS records, and regulatory submissions to USDA APHIS. Serves as the cross-system reference key.']
  herd_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the herd or flock grouping (e.g., \'North Pasture Angus Cow-Calf Herd\', \'Broiler House 4 Flock\'). Used in operational reports, Granular Farm Management, and John Deere Operations Center displays.']
  herd_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the herd record. \'active\' indicates operational management; \'quarantined\' triggers APHIS disease containment protocols; \'dispersed\' indicates the herd has been sold or depopulated; \'pending\' indicates formation in progress.']
  housing_type varchar [note: 'Primary housing or production system type for the herd (e.g., pasture, confinement, feedlot, free-range). Determines applicable animal welfare standards, GAP certification requirements, and environmental compliance obligations under EPA regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pasture|confinement|semi-confinement|feedlot|free-range|barn|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_antibiotic_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd is managed under a no-antibiotics-ever (NAE) or raised-without-antibiotics (RWA) program. Drives treatment protocol restrictions, veterinary prescription controls, and premium market channel eligibility.']
  is_gap_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd operation holds a current Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) certification. Enables premium market access and satisfies retailer food safety audit requirements under GFSI-recognized schemes.']
  is_globalg_ap_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd holds a current GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) certification for livestock. Required for export market access to EU and other international buyers demanding GFSI-recognized certification.']
  is_gmo_free boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd is managed under a non-GMO feed program, qualifying for Non-GMO Project Verified or equivalent labeling claims on derived products. Affects feed procurement specifications and supply chain segregation.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd is certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). When True, enforces organic feed sourcing, prohibits synthetic antibiotic use, and triggers organic premium pricing in sales contracts.']
  last_health_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent veterinary health inspection or herd health assessment. Used to ensure compliance with VCPR requirements, schedule next health review, and support APHIS accredited veterinarian documentation.']
  morbidity_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling percentage of animals in the herd that have been treated for illness during the current production period. Tracks herd health status, antibiotic use patterns, and biosecurity effectiveness. Supports FSIS residue avoidance program reporting.']
  mortality_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling mortality rate expressed as a percentage of the herd, calculated over the current production period. Key animal welfare and production efficiency KPI; triggers veterinary investigation when thresholds are exceeded. Reported to USDA NASS.']
  movement_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this herd is currently subject to a USDA APHIS or state-imposed movement restriction order (e.g., due to disease quarantine, regulatory hold, or embargo). When True, blocks interstate movement documentation and sale order processing.']
  pasture_area_ha decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total pasture or grazing area allocated to this herd in hectares. Used for stocking density calculations, EPA CAFO nutrient management planning, and USDA NRCS conservation program compliance. Integrated with Esri ArcGIS land parcel data.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Primary agricultural production purpose of the herd (e.g., beef, dairy, swine, poultry, sheep). Determines applicable FSIS inspection requirements, HACCP plans, and market channel routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: beef|dairy|swine|broiler|layer|turkey|sheep|goat|mixed|other — promote to reference product]']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species classification of the animals in the herd (e.g., bovine for cattle, swine for pigs, poultry for chickens/turkeys). Drives regulatory reporting pathways under USDA APHIS and FSIS, and determines applicable withdrawal period and MRL rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|swine|poultry|ovine|caprine|equine|aquaculture|other — promote to reference product]']
  stocking_density_head_per_ha decimal(8,2) [note: 'Current stocking density expressed as number of animal head per hectare of allocated pasture or housing area. Monitored against regulatory thresholds for EPA CAFO permits, organic certification land requirements, and animal welfare standards.']
  target_head_count varchar [note: 'Planned or budgeted number of animal head for this herd grouping, used for capacity planning, feed procurement forecasting, and financial budgeting in Granular Farm Management. Compared against current_head_count for variance analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this herd master record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail compliance, and detecting stale records requiring review.']
  welfare_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent animal welfare audit for this herd. Used to determine audit currency, schedule next audit, and satisfy retailer supply chain assurance requirements.']
  welfare_audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score from the most recent animal welfare audit conducted against an approved standard (e.g., Animal Welfare Approved, Certified Humane, GLOBALG.A.P.). Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Required by major retail customers and GFSI-aligned buyers.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a managed herd or flock grouping — the primary operational unit for livestock management. Captures herd name, species, production type (beef, dairy, swine, poultry, sheep, etc.), home location (farm/facility), responsible manager, target head count, current head count, herd classification (breeding, finishing, nursery, etc.), and certification status (organic, GAP, GLOBALG.A.P.). Acts as the organizational anchor for all animal-level records.'
}

Table livestock.pen_location {
  pen_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each pen, paddock, pasture, barn, feedlot, farrowing room, milking parlor, or processing facility unit within the livestock operation. Serves as the primary key and SSOT reference for all animal movement, feed delivery, welfare audit, and biosecurity zone records.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pen locations are physical infrastructure units with depreciation, maintenance, and utility costs allocated to cost centers. Facility cost allocation by pen is standard in feedlot and confinement live']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent facility or premises in the land domain that physically contains this pen or housing unit. Enables cross-domain linkage between livestock-operational pen records and land-parcel or premises-level records managed in the land domain.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Farm unit capacity planning, pen inventory reporting, and biosecurity zone management require direct pen-to-farm-unit association. Farm managers need all pens under their farm unit without joining thr']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Outdoor pens and pasture pen locations are physically situated within fields. This link supports manure nutrient management planning (EPA/state regulations require knowing which field receives manure ']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Pen locations undergo biosecurity, sanitation, and welfare inspections (PAACO, state vet, GAP field sanitation modules). A domain expert expects pen_location to reference its most recent governing ins']
  pen_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Each pen in a feedlot or dairy is assigned a responsible pen rider or pen manager for daily welfare monitoring, morbidity detection, and regulatory compliance. This accountability link is required for']
  aisle_row_number varchar [note: 'Aisle or row designation within a building where this pen is located (e.g., \'Aisle 3\', \'Row B\'). Supports precise pen location navigation for feed delivery crews, veterinary staff, and welfare auditors within large confinement facilities.']
  area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Physical area of the pen, paddock, or pasture measured in acres. Used to calculate stocking density (head per acre), grazing rotation schedules, and welfare compliance metrics. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS geospatial measurements or Granular Farm Management land records.']
  area_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Physical area of the pen or indoor housing unit measured in square feet. Used for indoor confinement facilities (barns, farrowing rooms, milking parlors) where square footage per animal head is the applicable welfare compliance metric.']
  biosecurity_zone varchar [note: 'Biosecurity zone designation assigned to the pen per the operation\'s biosecurity plan. Governs personnel access controls, equipment movement restrictions, and disease containment protocols. Critical for APHIS foreign animal disease response planning and GLOBALG.A.P. biosecurity compliance.']
  building_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the building or structure within the parent facility that contains this pen. Used for facility layout navigation, maintenance work order routing in SAP S/4HANA PM module, and emergency response planning.']
  capacity_head varchar [note: 'Maximum number of animal head this pen or housing unit is designed to hold at one time, based on species-specific stocking density standards and welfare regulations. Used for occupancy management, welfare compliance audits, and feed ration planning.']
  commissioned_date date [note: 'Date the pen or housing unit was first placed into operational service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, maintenance scheduling, depreciation calculations (CAPEX), and historical occupancy trend analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pen location record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and Silver Layer lakehouse record management. Populated automatically at record insertion.']
  current_occupancy varchar [note: 'Current number of animal head present in the pen at the time of last update. Enables real-time occupancy monitoring, stocking density compliance checks, and feed delivery quantity calculations. Updated upon each animal movement event.']
  decommissioned_date date [note: 'Date the pen or housing unit was permanently taken out of service. Nullable for active pens. Supports asset lifecycle management, historical capacity reporting, and regulatory premises deregistration with USDA APHIS.']
  feed_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which feed rations are delivered to animals within this pen. Drives feed delivery scheduling, TMR (Total Mixed Ration) batch targeting, and feed management system integration with Granular Farm Management and SAP S/4HANA PP module.']
  flooring_type varchar [note: 'Type of flooring surface within the pen or housing unit. Impacts animal welfare assessments (lameness, injury risk), manure management practices, and compliance with GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI welfare audit criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concrete|slatted|dirt|rubber_mat|bedded_pack|pasture|grated — promote to reference product]']
  gis_polygon_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the pen boundary polygon stored in the Esri ArcGIS geospatial system or John Deere Operations Center field mapping layer. Enables spatial analysis, boundary-based animal tracking, and integration with precision agriculture platforms.']
  housing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical housing or containment structure type. Drives welfare audit requirements, ventilation standards, biosecurity protocols, and regulatory reporting under USDA APHIS and GFSI frameworks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pen|paddock|pasture|barn|feedlot|farrowing_room|milking_parlor|processing_facility — promote to reference product]']
  is_biosecurity_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pen requires restricted personnel access under the operation\'s biosecurity plan. When true, access logs, PPE requirements, and visitor protocols are enforced per APHIS biosecurity guidelines and GLOBALG.A.P. standards.']
  is_climate_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pen or housing unit has active climate control systems (heating, cooling, or humidity regulation). Relevant to welfare compliance for sensitive species/life stages (farrowing, nursery), energy cost tracking, and IoT sensor deployment planning.']
  is_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the pen or housing unit has a roof or overhead shelter structure. Affects weather protection assessments, welfare audit scoring, and heat/cold stress risk classification for animal health management.']
  last_cleaned_date date [note: 'Date the pen was most recently cleaned and sanitized. Supports biosecurity compliance tracking, disease prevention scheduling, and welfare audit documentation. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI audit trails.']
  last_disinfected_date date [note: 'Date the pen was most recently disinfected with an approved disinfectant product. Distinct from cleaning — disinfection is a separate biosecurity step required between animal groups and after disease events. Tracked for APHIS and FSIS compliance.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent animal welfare or facility inspection conducted at this pen location. Supports audit scheduling, compliance gap identification, and regulatory reporting to USDA APHIS and GFSI certification bodies.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the pen centroid or primary access point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Enables spatial tracking of animal locations, feed delivery routing, and GIS-based biosecurity zone mapping in Esri ArcGIS and John Deere Operations Center.']
  lighting_type varchar [note: 'Type of lighting available within the pen or housing unit. Relevant to animal welfare assessments, photoperiod management for reproductive performance, and energy efficiency reporting under CAPEX/OPEX tracking.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the pen centroid or primary access point in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Enables spatial tracking of animal locations, feed delivery routing, and GIS-based biosecurity zone mapping in Esri ArcGIS and John Deere Operations Center.']
  manure_management_type varchar [note: 'Method used to collect, store, or manage manure generated within this pen. Required for EPA nutrient management plan compliance, USDA NRCS BMP (Best Management Practices) reporting, and environmental permit documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this pen location that does not fit structured fields. Examples include temporary capacity restrictions, ongoing construction notes, or special biosecurity instructions for staff.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pen or housing unit. Controls whether the pen is available for animal placement, feed delivery scheduling, or welfare inspections. \'quarantined\' status triggers biosecurity protocols per APHIS and FSIS requirements.']
  pen_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code or label assigned to the pen or housing unit, used in operational signage, feed delivery manifests, animal movement records, and FMIS integrations (e.g., Granular Farm Management, SAP S/4HANA PP module). Must be unique within the parent facility.']
  pen_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the pen or housing unit (e.g., \'North Farrowing Room 3\', \'Pasture B-12\', \'Feedlot Pen 7A\'). Used in operational reports, welfare audit documentation, and staff communications.']
  production_stage varchar [note: 'Livestock production stage for which this pen is designated. Determines applicable feed ration programs, health protocols, withdrawal period tracking, and welfare audit criteria. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA PP production order classification and Granular Farm Management livestock records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nursery|grower|finisher|breeding|gestation|farrowing|lactation|dry_lot|backgrounding — promote to reference product]']
  species_type varchar [note: 'Primary livestock species for which this pen or housing unit is designed and designated. Determines applicable welfare standards, ventilation requirements, stocking density limits, and regulatory reporting frameworks (APHIS, FSIS, USDA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cattle|swine|poultry|sheep|goat|equine|mixed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stocking_density_limit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable stocking density for this pen expressed as head per acre (outdoor) or square feet per head (indoor), per applicable welfare and regulatory standards. Used to enforce compliance thresholds and trigger alerts when current occupancy approaches or exceeds limits.']
  stocking_density_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the stocking density limit field. Distinguishes between outdoor (head per acre) and indoor (square feet per head) density metrics to ensure correct welfare compliance calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pen location record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  ventilation_type varchar [note: 'Type of ventilation system installed in or applicable to the pen or housing unit. Affects air quality management, heat stress mitigation, disease transmission risk, and welfare compliance under USDA APHIS and GLOBALG.A.P. standards.']
  water_source_type varchar [note: 'Type of water delivery system or source available within the pen. Critical for animal welfare compliance, daily water consumption monitoring, and drought contingency planning. Supports GLOBALG.A.P. and USDA APHIS welfare audit requirements.']
  welfare_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current animal welfare certification status for this pen location under applicable certification programs (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P., Certified Humane, Animal Welfare Approved). Drives audit scheduling, corrective action tracking, and customer-facing welfare claims.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical housing and containment units within the livestock operation — pens, paddocks, pastures, barns, feedlots, farrowing rooms, milking parlors, and processing facilities. Captures location name, facility reference (FK to land domain for parent facility/premises), GPS coordinates, area (acres/sq ft), capacity (head count), housing type, ventilation type, flooring type, biosecurity zone designation, current occupancy, and operational status. Enables spatial tracking of animal movements, feed delivery targeting, welfare audit location references, and biosecurity zone management. Owned by the livestock domain because pen-level granularity is livestock-operational — pen attributes (capacity, biosecurity zone, housing type, ventilation) are livestock-specific and not managed at the land-parcel level. Cross-references the land domain for parent premises/facility identification.'
}

Table livestock.breeding_event {
  breeding_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each breeding event or semen/embryo inventory record. Serves as the primary key for the breeding_event data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the liquid nitrogen (LN2) cryogenic storage tank in which the semen straws or embryos are stored. Used for physical inventory location management and tank maintenance scheduling.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AI and embryo transfer breeding programs are budgeted cost line items in livestock enterprise accounting. Cost center assignment on breeding_event enables reproductive program ROI analysis and budget ']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: AI synchronization and mass breeding programs use dedicated breeding crews. Crew-level tracking enables piece-rate payroll for AI technicians, labor cost allocation per breeding event, and H2A complia']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or contractor who performed the breeding procedure (AI technician, embryo transfer specialist, or natural service supervisor). Supports labor accountability and technician performance KPIs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Breeding events occur at specific facilities (AI labs, breeding barns). Facility-level biosecurity zone compliance, USDA premises registration, and breeding program audits all require knowing which fa']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the herd or flock to which the female animal belongs at the time of the breeding event. Supports herd-level reproductive performance reporting and APHIS premises identification.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Semen quality analysis, embryo viability testing, and post-thaw motility verification require lab samples. Breeding programs track lab results to validate genetic material quality before use. A domain']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Semen and embryo lots require FSMA-compliant lot traceability for genetic material recalls and regulatory audits. breeding_event.lot_number is a denormalized lot reference; a proper FK to lot_trace en']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Semen and embryo inventory management is a core livestock genetics operation. Linking breeding_event to material_master enables lot traceability, storage temperature compliance, expiry tracking, and i']
  primary_breeding_female_animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dam (female animal) involved in the breeding activity. Links to the animal master record for the cow, heifer, sow, ewe, or other female subject of the breeding event.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Semen and embryo lots are procured via purchase orders. Linking breeding_event to purchase_order enables traceability from genetic material PO through breeding outcomes, supports lot-level cost alloca']
  semen_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: breeding_event.semen_supplier is a plain-text denormalized vendor name. Normalizing to semen_vendor_id → procurement.vendor supports vendor scorecarding, APHIS permit verification for imported genetic']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Semen and embryo suppliers operate under vendor supply contracts in commercial livestock breeding. Linking breeding_event to vendor_contract enables contract spend tracking, volume commitment monitori']
  actual_parturition_date date [note: 'Actual recorded date on which the dam gave birth (calved, farrowed, lambed, etc.). Used to calculate gestation length, compare against expected date, and update reproductive performance records. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  breeding_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the breeding activity (natural service, artificial insemination, or embryo transfer) was performed. Principal real-world event date for the breeding transaction. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  breeding_method varchar [note: 'Method used to achieve conception. \'natural_service\' indicates direct mating; \'artificial_insemination\' (AI) uses processed semen; \'embryo_transfer\' (ET) implants a flushed embryo; \'in_vitro_fertilization\' (IVF) uses laboratory-produced embryos.']
  breeding_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording additional observations, complications, or contextual information about the breeding event or inventory record (e.g., synchronization protocol used, heat detection method, embryo quality grade, technician observations).']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the semen or embryo was collected from the donor animal. Used for lot age tracking, quality assessment, and regulatory compliance with USDA semen import/export requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  conception_check_date date [note: 'Date on which pregnancy diagnosis (rectal palpation, ultrasound, or blood test) was performed to confirm or deny conception following the breeding event. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  conception_rate_history_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded historical conception rate (as a percentage) for this sire or semen lot based on prior breeding events. Derived from field performance data and used in sire selection and AI program planning. Distinct from the result of any single breeding event.']
  conception_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the pregnancy diagnosis check. \'confirmed_pregnant\' indicates successful conception; \'open\' indicates the animal did not conceive; \'recheck_required\' indicates inconclusive result requiring follow-up; \'not_checked\' indicates no pregnancy check has been performed.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Purchase cost per semen straw or embryo unit in USD. Used for breeding cost accounting, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) allocation to offspring, and ROI analysis of genetic improvement programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this breeding event or inventory record was first created in the system. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  epd_birth_weight decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for birth weight for the sire, expressed in pounds. Positive values indicate heavier-than-average offspring. Used in sire selection to manage calving difficulty and neonatal survival.']
  epd_calving_ease decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for calving ease for the sire. Expressed as a percentage advantage in unassisted births compared to breed average. Used in sire selection to minimize dystocia risk.']
  epd_marbling decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for marbling (intramuscular fat) for the sire. Expressed as a USDA marbling score unit advantage. Used in beef quality grade prediction and premium market targeting.']
  epd_milk decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for milk production for the sire, expressed in pounds. Reflects the milking ability transmitted through daughters. Used in maternal breed selection for calf nutrition and growth.']
  epd_rea decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for Ribeye Area (REA) for the sire, expressed in square inches. Indicates genetic merit for muscle expression and cutability. Used in carcass merit prediction and sire selection for yield grade improvement.']
  epd_weaning_weight decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for weaning weight for the sire, expressed in pounds. Indicates the sire\'s genetic merit for pre-weaning growth. Key metric in beef and sheep genetic improvement programs.']
  epd_yearling_weight decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for yearling weight for the sire, expressed in pounds. Indicates the sire\'s genetic merit for post-weaning growth rate. Used in feedlot performance prediction and sire selection.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breeding event or inventory record. \'planned\' indicates a scheduled breeding activity; \'completed\' indicates the event occurred; \'cancelled\' indicates the event was voided; \'pending_confirmation\' indicates awaiting conception check result.']
  expected_parturition_date date [note: 'Calculated expected date of birth (calving, farrowing, lambing, etc.) based on breeding date and species-specific gestation length. Used for calving/farrowing schedule planning, nutritional management, and labor allocation. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.']
  genetic_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official breed association registration number for the sire. Serves as the externally-known unique identifier for the sire in breed registry databases, enabling EPD lookup and genetic verification.']
  genomic_enhanced_epd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EPD values for this sire are genomic-enhanced (GE-EPD), meaning they incorporate DNA marker information in addition to traditional pedigree and performance data. Genomic-enhanced EPDs have higher accuracy, especially for young sires.']
  offspring_count varchar [note: 'Number of live offspring born from this breeding event (e.g., 1 for singleton calf, 2 for twins). Recorded at parturition. Used for litter size tracking, multiple birth analysis, and reproductive performance KPIs.']
  post_thaw_motility_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality grade expressed as the percentage of progressively motile sperm cells observed after thawing a representative straw from the lot. Minimum acceptable threshold is typically 30-35% per USDA AMS standards. Used to assess semen quality and predict conception rates.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current count of semen straws or embryos available in inventory for this lot. Decremented upon each use in an AI or ET breeding event. Used for inventory planning and reorder decisions.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator field that separates the two concerns managed by this data product: \'breeding_activity\' for actual mating/insemination events, and \'semen_inventory\' for semen or embryo inventory management records.']
  sire_breed varchar [note: 'Breed designation of the sire (e.g., Angus, Hereford, Holstein, Simmental). Used for crossbreeding program management, breed composition tracking, and genetic diversity analysis.']
  sire_name varchar [note: 'Registered name of the sire used in the breeding event or associated with the semen/embryo inventory record. Used for genetic reporting, catalog references, and breeder communications.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Precise physical location within the storage tank, expressed as canister, goblet, and cane identifiers (e.g., \'C2-G3-CN4\'). Enables rapid physical retrieval of specific semen straws or embryos during AI procedures.']
  synchronization_protocol varchar [note: 'Hormonal synchronization protocol used to coordinate estrus and ovulation timing prior to the AI or ET event. Common protocols include CIDR (Controlled Internal Drug Release), OvSynch, CoSynch, Select-Synch, and MGA (melengestrol acetate). \'none\' indicates natural estrus detection was used. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CIDR|OvSynch|CoSynch|Select_Synch|MGA|none|TAI|FTAI — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this breeding event or inventory record was most recently modified. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for the breeding program — encompassing both breeding activities and semen/embryo inventory management. For breeding activities: captures breeding date, female animal ID, sire ID or semen lot reference, breeding method (natural service, AI, embryo transfer), technician, conception check date, conception result, expected parturition date, and breeding program reference. For semen/embryo inventory: captures sire ID, sire name, breed, genetic registration number, EPD values (calving ease, birth weight, weaning weight, yearling weight, milk, marbling, REA), genomic-enhanced EPD flag, semen supplier, collection date, lot number, quantity on hand (straws/embryos), storage tank ID, storage location (canister/goblet/cane), conception rate history, cost per unit, and quality grade (post-thaw motility). Record type discriminator (breeding_activity vs semen_inventory) separates the two concerns. Supports genetic improvement tracking, EPD analysis, reproductive performance KPIs, AI program planning, and breeding cost accounting.'
}

Table livestock.parturition_record {
  parturition_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each parturition (birth/calving/farrowing/lambing/kidding) event record. Serves as the primary key for this transactional entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Parturition events use specific equipment assets (calving pen monitoring systems, obstetrical equipment, calf pullers). Linking to asset enables maintenance scheduling based on birthing event frequenc']
  breeding_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.breeding_event. Business justification: A parturition_record (birth/calving event) is the direct outcome of a breeding_event. Linking parturition_record.breeding_event_id → livestock.breeding_event.breeding_event_id closes the reproductive ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Parturition events with veterinary intervention (dystocia, C-section) incur direct costs requiring cost center allocation for livestock enterprise P&L. Calving/farrowing cost tracking by cost center i']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Calving/farrowing crews handle parturition events in large livestock operations. Crew-level tracking supports H2A labor compliance, piece-rate payroll for calving assistance, and labor cost allocation']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm facility, barn, or pen where the parturition event occurred. Supports location-based performance analysis and biosecurity management.']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the herd or flock to which the dam belongs at the time of parturition. Used for herd-level performance reporting and APHIS traceability.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Colostrum quality testing, neonatal blood sampling for pathogen screening (BVD, Johne\'s), and placental pathology samples are collected at parturition. A domain expert expects parturition_record to re']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: Parturition (birth/calving/farrowing) events occur in a specific physical pen or maternity area. Linking parturition_record to pen_location enables traceability of where births occurred, supports bios']
  primary_parturition_animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the female parent (dam) animal record that gave birth. Links to the animal master record for the dam, enabling lineage tracing and dam performance analytics.']
  primary_parturition_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm worker or veterinary staff member who attended and recorded the parturition event. Used for workforce accountability and SOP compliance tracking.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the parturition event occurred. Used as the primary date dimension for herd performance reporting, age calculation, and regulatory records.']
  birth_time timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the first offspring was born during the parturition event. Enables time-of-day analysis for staffing optimization and dystocia risk assessment.']
  birth_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the parturition by number of offspring born in the event (e.g., single, twin, triplet). Impacts dam recovery protocols, colostrum management, and offspring survival rates.']
  birth_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average birth weight in kilograms across all live offspring born in this parturition event. For individual offspring weights, see the offspring detail records. Used for growth performance benchmarking and genetic evaluation (EPD).']
  breeding_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the dam was bred that resulted in this parturition: natural service, artificial insemination (AI), or embryo transfer (ET). Used for genetic program performance analysis and sire utilization reporting.']
  colostrum_administered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether colostrum was administered to the newborn offspring following birth. Critical for passive immunity transfer and neonatal survival; required for animal welfare compliance documentation.']
  colostrum_source varchar [note: 'Source of colostrum provided to the newborn offspring: from the dam directly, pooled from other animals, commercial colostrum supplement, or frozen colostrum bank. Relevant for biosecurity and disease transmission risk management.']
  colostrum_volume_ml decimal(7,2) [note: 'Volume of colostrum administered to the newborn offspring in millilitres. Used to verify adequate passive immunity transfer per species-specific protocols and animal welfare standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parturition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  dam_body_condition_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Body Condition Score (BCS) of the dam at the time of parturition, assessed on a standardized scale (e.g., 1–5 for cattle/sheep, 1–9 for swine). Indicates dam nutritional status and predicts post-partum reproductive performance.']
  dam_parity varchar [note: 'The parity number (number of times the dam has given birth, including this event) at the time of parturition. First-parity animals carry higher dystocia risk; used for reproductive lifecycle analytics.']
  dam_post_partum_status varchar [note: 'Health status of the dam immediately following the parturition event. Drives post-partum care protocols, veterinary treatment scheduling, and culling decisions.']
  dystocia_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parturition involved dystocia (difficult birth requiring intervention). Drives veterinary follow-up workflows and is used in sire EPD (Expected Progeny Difference) calculations.']
  ease_of_birth_score varchar [note: 'Standardized numeric score (typically 1–5 scale) indicating the difficulty of the parturition: 1=No assistance, 2=Easy pull, 3=Hard pull, 4=Mechanical assistance, 5=Caesarean/Veterinary. Used for dystocia tracking, sire selection, and animal welfare compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5 — promote to reference product]']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the parturition event, used for cross-referencing with farm management systems (e.g., Granular, SAP) and regulatory reporting. Format: PART-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  gestation_length_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from confirmed conception or breeding date to the birth date. Compared against species-standard gestation norms to flag premature or overdue births and assess breeding program accuracy.']
  intervention_type varchar [note: 'Type of veterinary or assisted intervention performed during the parturition event. Used for dystocia severity classification, cost allocation, and sire/dam selection analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for attending staff or veterinarian to record additional observations about the parturition event not captured in structured fields (e.g., unusual presentations, environmental conditions, dam behavior).']
  offspring_born_alive varchar [note: 'Count of offspring born alive during this parturition event. Key metric for reproductive efficiency reporting and herd performance benchmarking.']
  offspring_stillborn varchar [note: 'Count of offspring born dead (stillborn) during this parturition event. Used for mortality analysis, genetic program evaluation, and animal welfare compliance reporting.']
  placenta_expelled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the placenta (afterbirth) was fully expelled following parturition. Retained placenta is a significant post-partum health risk requiring veterinary intervention; tracked for dam health monitoring.']
  placenta_expulsion_hours decimal(5,1) [note: 'Number of hours after birth until the placenta was fully expelled. Values exceeding species-specific thresholds (e.g., >12 hours in cattle) trigger retained placenta alerts and veterinary follow-up workflows.']
  presentation_type varchar [note: 'Describes the position of the fetus at the time of delivery (anterior/normal, posterior/backward, transverse/sideways). Relevant for dystocia risk assessment and veterinary intervention records.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the parturition record within the farm management system. DRAFT indicates pending verification; CONFIRMED indicates validated entry; AMENDED indicates a corrected record; VOIDED indicates a data entry error.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Agricultural season in which the parturition event occurred. Used for seasonal performance benchmarking, feed planning, and herd management scheduling.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this parturition record originated (e.g., Granular Farm Management, SAP S/4HANA, John Deere Operations Center, or manual entry). Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  species_code varchar [note: 'Livestock species involved in the parturition event. Determines the applicable regulatory framework (FSIS, APHIS) and terminology (calving, farrowing, lambing, kidding, foaling).']
  total_offspring_born varchar [note: 'Total count of offspring born during this parturition event, including stillborn. Used for litter size analytics and dam reproductive performance KPIs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parturition record was most recently modified. Used for change detection in incremental Silver layer loads and audit compliance.']
  veterinary_intervention boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a licensed veterinarian was required to intervene during the parturition event (e.g., caesarean section, manual correction of malpresentation). Drives post-event health monitoring and cost tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each birth or calving/farrowing/lambing/kidding event. Records dam ID, sire ID, birth date, birth time, number of offspring, individual offspring IDs, birth weight per offspring, birth type (single, twin, triplet), ease of birth score, colostrum administration, dam body condition score at birth, and attending staff. Initiates the animal master record for newborns and feeds into herd performance reporting.'
}

Table livestock.health_treatment {
  health_treatment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each health treatment record or protocol template in the livestock health management system. Primary key for the health_treatment data product.']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual animal record in the livestock master data product. Populated for individual animal treatments; may be null for group-level treatments where group_id is used instead. Supports APHIS individual animal traceability requirements.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Treatment equipment (squeeze chutes, hydraulic restraint systems, dosing guns) are tracked assets. Linking health_treatment to asset enables maintenance scheduling based on treatment frequency, equipm']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Veterinary treatment costs are the second-largest variable cost in livestock operations. Cost center assignment on health_treatment enables health cost per head reporting, antibiotic program cost trac']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Health treatments must be linked to the performing employee for antibiotic stewardship compliance, VFD regulatory audits, and labor cost tracking. \'treated_by_name\' is a denormalized string that viola']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm, barn, pen, or facility location where the treatment was administered. Supports GIS-based disease mapping in Esri ArcGIS and pen-level health analytics for APHIS disease surveillance.']
