  health_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing foreign key to the parent protocol_template record in this same health_treatment product. Populated when this treatment_instance was executed following a standardized health management protocol. Null for ad-hoc treatments or for protocol_template records themselves.']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: health_treatment records veterinary interventions that occur within a herd context. The herd_id is essential for herd-level morbidity/mortality reporting, APHIS regulatory compliance, and antibiotic u']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Veterinary drugs and biologicals are managed in the procurement input catalog with PHI days, withdrawal periods, and EPA/APHIS registration data. Linking health_treatment to input_catalog enforces app']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Culture and sensitivity testing, diagnostic blood panels, and pathogen identification samples are collected to confirm diagnosis before or during treatment. A domain expert expects health_treatment to']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: FDA/USDA drug and vaccine lot traceability is mandatory for withdrawal period compliance and product recall management. health_treatment.vaccine_lot_number is a denormalized lot reference; FK to lot_t']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Drug inventory management and withdrawal-period compliance reporting require linking each treatment to the inventory material master. Enables MRP-driven drug stock replenishment, cost-per-treatment ac']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Health treatments involve drugs with regulatory MRL thresholds governing withdrawal periods and earliest harvest dates. Linking health_treatment to product.mrl_threshold enables automated withdrawal p']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Drug residue exceedances, antibiotic withdrawal violations, and treatment protocol failures generate nonconformance records. USDA FSIS and FSMA require traceability from treatment event to quality non']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: Health treatments are administered to animals in specific pens or treatment areas. Linking health_treatment to pen_location enables pen-level disease tracking, biosecurity zone analysis, and morbidity']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Animal health treatment costs are significant livestock expenses requiring profit center attribution for enterprise P&L. Veterinary and drug costs by profit center are standard reporting requirements ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Medications used in treatments are procured via PO. Linking health_treatment to purchase_order enables drug lot traceability from procurement through treatment application, supports withdrawal period ']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Pharmaceutical and veterinary product suppliers operate under vendor contracts. Linking health_treatment to vendor_contract enables contract-based drug spend tracking, formulary compliance auditing, a']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Drug/vaccine supplier traceability per treatment record is required for product recall response, FDA/USDA regulatory audits, and withdrawal period compliance tied to specific supplier lots. Livestock ']
  veterinary_prescription_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.veterinary_prescription. Business justification: health_treatment.prescription_reference is currently a STRING field storing the prescription reference number. veterinary_prescription is the master record for VFDs and prescriptions. Normalizing by a']
  booster_due_date date [note: 'The date on which a follow-up booster vaccination is due for the treated animal, based on the vaccine manufacturer\'s protocol. Applicable only for vaccination treatment types requiring multi-dose schedules. Drives vaccination schedule alerts in the FMIS.']
  clinical_signs varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the observable clinical signs or symptoms presented by the animal or group at the time of treatment, as recorded by the attending veterinarian or farm staff. Supports HACCP documentation and veterinary case history.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when this health treatment record was first created in the FMIS or SAP QM system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping compliance.']
  diagnosis_code varchar [note: 'Standardized veterinary diagnosis code identifying the disease or condition being treated, aligned with USDA APHIS disease reporting codes or ICD-10-CM veterinary adaptations. Used for APHIS disease surveillance reporting and herd health analytics.']
  dosage_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric quantity of the drug or vaccine product administered per animal or per unit of body weight, as prescribed. Used in conjunction with dosage_unit for complete dosage documentation required by FDA VFD and FSIS withdrawal compliance.']
  dosage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the administered dosage amount (e.g., mL, mg, mg/kg body weight). Required for accurate withdrawal period calculation and FDA VFD documentation.']
  ear_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical ear tag identifier of the treated animal, denormalized from the animal master for rapid field lookup and cross-referencing with paper-based treatment logs. Supports USDA NAIS (National Animal Identification System) traceability.']
  earliest_harvest_date date [note: 'Calculated date on which the animal first becomes eligible for harvest or product collection after completing the mandatory withdrawal period. Derived from treatment_date plus withdrawal_days; stored as a business-critical compliance field to prevent premature slaughter and FSIS violations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, special instructions, or contextual information recorded by the veterinarian or farm staff at the time of treatment. Supports HACCP documentation and veterinary case history completeness.']
  number_of_animals_treated varchar [note: 'Count of individual animals included in this treatment event. Value is 1 for individual animal treatments; greater than 1 for group-level treatments such as metaphylaxis or arrival processing. Used for drug inventory reconciliation and treatment cost allocation.']
  product_used_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total_product_used quantity (e.g., mL, mg, doses). Required for drug inventory reconciliation and cost reporting.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an actual treatment event applied to an animal or group (treatment_instance) or a standardized health management protocol template used as a reference for future treatments (protocol_template). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting.']
  retreatment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment record represents a re-treatment of an animal that did not respond adequately to a prior treatment for the same condition. True when this is a follow-up treatment; False for initial treatments. Supports antibiotic stewardship and treatment efficacy analytics.']
  rfid_tag varchar [note: 'Electronic RFID tag identifier associated with the treated animal, used for automated identification in chute-side treatment systems and integration with John Deere Operations Center livestock modules. Supports ISO 11784/11785 RFID standards.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The method by which the drug or vaccine was delivered to the animal (e.g., subcutaneous injection, intramuscular injection, oral drench, topical pour-on, intranasal spray). Affects withdrawal period applicability and FSIS residue compliance.']
  total_product_used decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the drug or vaccine product consumed for this treatment event, calculated as dosage_amount multiplied by number_of_animals_treated. Stored for drug inventory depletion recording in SAP MM and cost-of-treatment reporting.']
  treatment_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of the treatment event in the farm\'s operating currency, including drug product cost and veterinary service fees where applicable. Used for herd health cost-of-production analysis, OPEX reporting, and Granular Farm Management financial planning integration.']
  treatment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the veterinary treatment, vaccination, or health intervention was administered to the animal or group. This is the principal real-world event date used for withdrawal period calculations, FSIS compliance, and HACCP records.']
  treatment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the treatment event or protocol template, used for cross-referencing with veterinary records, VFD documentation, and SAP QM notifications. Format typically follows farm-assigned numbering conventions (e.g., TRT-2024-00123).']
  treatment_outcome varchar [note: 'Clinical outcome of the treatment as assessed at the end of the treatment course or at follow-up evaluation. Used for herd health analytics, treatment efficacy reporting, and antibiotic stewardship program monitoring.']
  treatment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the treatment record. \'draft\' indicates a record in preparation; \'active\' indicates an ongoing treatment; \'completed\' indicates the treatment course is finished; \'cancelled\' indicates the treatment was voided; \'pending_approval\' indicates awaiting veterinarian sign-off for VFD-regulated products.']
  treatment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the treatment was administered, captured for IoT-integrated barn management systems and time-sensitive drug administration protocols requiring exact timing records.']
  treatment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the treatment purpose. \'therapeutic\' treats an existing disease; \'prophylactic\' prevents disease in healthy animals; \'vaccination\' administers a biological vaccine; \'metaphylaxis\' treats an entire group when a portion shows disease; \'arrival_processing\' covers standard processing at receiving; \'parasite_control\' targets internal or external parasites.']
  trigger_criteria varchar [note: 'For protocol_template records, describes the specific conditions that trigger execution of this protocol (e.g., \'rectal temperature > 104°F\', \'morbidity rate > 10% in pen within 7 days\', \'BRD pull rate exceeds threshold\'). Null for treatment_instance records.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to this health treatment record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  veterinarian_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the supervising veterinarian reviewed and approved this treatment record or protocol template. Required for VFD-regulated antibiotic treatments and GLOBALG.A.P. animal health certification audits.']
  veterinarian_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued veterinary license number of the attending or supervising veterinarian responsible for the treatment authorization. Required for VFD documentation and VCPR compliance. Null when treatment is administered by non-veterinary farm staff under a standing order.']
  vfd_reference_number varchar [note: 'The official Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) document reference number issued by the licensed veterinarian authorizing the use of a VFD antibiotic in animal feed. Mandatory for medically important antimicrobials used in feed per FDA VFD regulations. Null for non-VFD treatments.']
  withdrawal_days varchar [note: 'The mandatory number of days that must elapse after the last treatment before the animal or its products (meat, milk, eggs) may enter the food supply, as specified on the drug label. Also referred to as Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for meat animals. Critical for FSIS residue avoidance compliance.']
  withdrawal_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the animal with respect to drug withdrawal period. \'in_withdrawal\' indicates the animal cannot yet be harvested; \'cleared\' indicates the withdrawal period has elapsed; \'not_applicable\' applies to non-withdrawal products or protocol templates.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every veterinary treatment, medical intervention, vaccination, and health protocol template for individual animals or groups. Captures treatment date, animal ID, record type (treatment_instance or protocol_template), treatment type (therapeutic, prophylactic, vaccination, metaphylaxis, arrival processing, parasite control), diagnosis code, clinical signs, drug/product name, active ingredient (AI), dosage, route of administration, withdrawal period (PHI/REI), booster due date (for vaccinations), vaccine manufacturer lot number, treated-by (veterinarian or farm staff), prescription/VFD reference, protocol reference (self-referencing FK for treatments following a protocol template), trigger criteria (temperature threshold, morbidity rate for protocol templates), treatment outcome, re-treatment flag, withdrawal status, earliest eligible harvest date, and veterinarian approval date. Serves as the consolidated SSOT for all animal health interventions and standardized health management procedures. Critical for FSIS antibiotic withdrawal period compliance, FDA VFD documentation, HACCP records, USDA residue avoidance programs, APHIS disease surveillance, GLOBALG.A.P. animal health certification, and herd health protocol management.'
}

Table livestock.vaccination_record {
  vaccination_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each vaccination record. Primary key for the vaccination_record data product in the livestock domain.']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual animal receiving the vaccination. Links to the animal master record in the livestock domain. Null when vaccination is administered at group/herd level.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Vaccination events use tracked equipment assets (squeeze chutes, automatic syringes, restraint systems). Linking to asset supports maintenance scheduling (sterilization cycles), regulatory audit trail']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vaccination programs are budgeted line items in livestock enterprise health cost budgets. Cost center assignment on vaccination_record enables vaccine program cost tracking, budget vs. actual variance']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Vaccination processing days are crew events in livestock operations. Crew-level vaccination tracking supports labor cost allocation and crew productivity reporting (head vaccinated per crew per day), ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce record of the person who administered the vaccination (veterinarian, veterinary technician, or trained farm employee).']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Official USDA National Premises Identification Number (PIN) for the location where vaccination occurred. Required for APHIS Animal Disease Traceability reporting and national disease surveillance programs.']
  health_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.health_protocol. Business justification: Vaccination records should reference the standardized health protocol that governs the vaccination program. health_protocol is the master record defining vaccination schedules, dosage, withdrawal peri']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Vaccinations are frequently administered at the herd or group level (mass vaccination events), not just to individual animals. Linking vaccination_record to herd enables herd-level vaccination complia']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Vaccines are managed in the procurement input catalog with PHI days, withdrawal periods, and regulatory registration data. Linking vaccination_record to input_catalog enforces approved vaccine formula']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Post-vaccination serology and titer testing verify vaccine efficacy (e.g., BVDV, IBR titers). USDA-accredited herd health programs require documented titer results linked to vaccination events. A doma']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: USDA APHIS and FDA require vaccine lot traceability for adverse event reporting and recall compliance. vaccination_record.manufacturer_lot_number is a denormalized lot reference; FK to lot_trace enabl']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Vaccine inventory management and lot traceability require linking each vaccination event to the specific material_master record for the vaccine used. Enables withdrawal period compliance, cold-chain v']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: Vaccination events occur in specific pens or processing areas. Linking vaccination_record to pen_location enables location-based vaccination coverage analysis, biosecurity zone compliance tracking, an']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Vaccines are procured via purchase orders with lot and expiry tracking. Linking vaccination_record to purchase_order enables vaccine lot traceability from PO receipt through animal administration, sup']
  vaccine_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Vaccine products belong in the product catalog (product.master) for lifecycle management, cost tracking, and regulatory compliance. vaccine_name is a denormalized product reference. Linking enables va']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Vaccine suppliers operate under vendor contracts with volume commitments and cold-chain requirements. Linking vaccination_record to vendor_contract enables contract spend tracking, cold-chain complian']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Vaccine manufacturer/supplier traceability per vaccination record is required for lot recall response, cold chain compliance audits, and USDA/APHIS regulatory reporting. vaccination_record.manufacture']
  veterinary_prescription_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the veterinary prescription or Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) authorizing use of the vaccine, particularly for prescription-only biologics. Null for over-the-counter vaccines.']
  administration_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the vaccine was administered to the animal or group. Principal business event date for regulatory reporting, withdrawal period calculation, and booster scheduling.']
  administration_location varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the farm, barn, pen, or facility location where the vaccination was administered. Supports geographic disease surveillance, APHIS premises identification, and GLOBALG.A.P. site-level audit trails.']
  administration_route varchar [note: 'The anatomical route by which the vaccine was administered (e.g., subcutaneous, intramuscular, intranasal). Must match the label-approved route of administration per APHIS biologics regulations.']
  administration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the vaccine was administered, captured from the farm management system or mobile device. Supports time-sensitive withdrawal period calculations and audit trail integrity.']
  adverse_reaction_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any adverse reaction observed following vaccination (e.g., anaphylaxis, injection site swelling, fever). Populated only when adverse_reaction_observed is True. Supports APHIS CVB adverse event reporting.']
  adverse_reaction_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adverse reaction was observed in the animal(s) following vaccination. A value of True triggers creation of an adverse event report to USDA APHIS Center for Veterinary Biologics (CVB) and initiates veterinary follow-up.']
  animal_count varchar [note: 'Count of animals vaccinated in a group/herd-level vaccination event. Populated when herd_group_id is set and individual animal records are not created. Used for herd immunity coverage calculations.']
  booster_due_date date [note: 'Calculated or label-specified date by which the next booster dose must be administered to maintain protective immunity. Drives automated alerts in the herd vaccination schedule and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance monitoring.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued by the vaccine manufacturer for the specific lot, confirming potency, purity, and safety testing results. Corresponds to COA in common agricultural jargon.']
  cold_chain_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required cold chain storage conditions were maintained for the vaccine from receipt through administration. A value of False triggers a non-compliance flag and may invalidate the vaccination record for regulatory purposes.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of this vaccination record against the herd vaccination schedule and regulatory requirements. Drives APHIS disease surveillance alerts and GLOBALG.A.P. certification audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliant|non_compliant|pending|overdue|waived|under_review — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this vaccination record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  disease_target varchar [note: 'The disease, pathogen, or condition the vaccine is intended to prevent or control (e.g., \'Bovine Respiratory Disease\', \'Porcine Reproductive and Respiratory Syndrome\', \'Newcastle Disease\'). Supports disease surveillance analytics and APHIS reportable disease tracking.']
  dosage_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'Volume of vaccine administered in milliliters (mL) per animal or per dose. Must conform to label-approved dosage. Deviations from label dosage require veterinary authorization and are flagged for compliance review.']
  dose_number varchar [note: 'Sequential dose number within a multi-dose vaccination series (e.g., 1 = primary, 2 = booster). Enables tracking of vaccination series completion and booster compliance.']
  ear_tag_number varchar [note: 'Official ear tag or visual identification number of the individual animal at time of vaccination. Denormalized for rapid lookup and regulatory reporting without joining to the animal master. Supports APHIS Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) requirements.']
  earliest_harvest_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which the animal may be harvested for human consumption, calculated as administration_date plus withdrawal_period_days. Critical for FSIS pre-harvest food safety compliance and scheduling harvest operations.']
  injection_site varchar [note: 'Specific anatomical location on the animal where the vaccine was injected (e.g., \'neck\', \'behind ear\', \'rump\'). Supports Beef Quality Assurance (BQA) compliance and carcass quality tracking to avoid injection site lesions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, deviations from standard protocol, animal behavior notes, or other contextual information recorded by the administering technician or supervising veterinarian.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this vaccination event, used in veterinary documentation, APHIS reporting, and GLOBALG.A.P. audit trails. Format: VAC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'RFID electronic identification number of the animal at time of vaccination, conforming to ISO 11784/11785 standards. Enables automated identification during chute-side vaccination and integrates with John Deere Operations Center livestock tracking.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'USDA-assigned serial number for the specific biologics product lot, distinct from the manufacturer lot number. Required for APHIS Center for Veterinary Biologics (CVB) compliance and product release verification.']
  species varchar [note: 'Species of the animal(s) vaccinated. Determines applicable regulatory framework (APHIS, FSIS), withdrawal period tables, and label compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|porcine|poultry|ovine|caprine|equine|aquaculture|other — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the vaccine was stored prior to administration. Verified against label-required cold chain specifications (typically 2–8°C for refrigerated biologics). Out-of-range values trigger cold chain breach alerts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this vaccination record. Supports change detection, data quality monitoring, and regulatory audit requirements.']
  vaccine_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date printed on the vaccine vial or packaging. Used to verify that the vaccine was not expired at time of administration and to flag non-compliant records for regulatory review.']
  vaccine_type varchar [note: 'Biological classification of the vaccine formulation. Determines handling requirements, storage conditions, and regulatory classification under APHIS biologics licensing.']
  withdrawal_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that must elapse after vaccination before the animal or its products (meat, milk, eggs) may enter the food supply. Derived from the vaccine label and used to calculate the earliest eligible harvest date for FSIS pre-harvest food safety compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each vaccination administered to an individual animal or group. Captures vaccination date, animal or group ID, vaccine product name, manufacturer lot number, dosage, route, booster due date, administering technician, veterinary prescription reference, and compliance status against the herd vaccination schedule. Supports APHIS disease surveillance, FSIS pre-harvest food safety, and GLOBALG.A.P. animal health certification.'
}

Table livestock.weight_measurement {
  weight_measurement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual weight measurement record. Serves as the primary key for the weight_measurement data product in the livestock domain Silver Layer.']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual animal record being weighed. Populated for individual-scale measurements; null for group/pen-level measurements. Links to the animal master record in the livestock domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the weighing equipment (scale, load cell, or weigh tape device) used to capture the measurement. Links to the equipment asset master for calibration traceability.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Feedlot weigh events are performed by processing crews for piece-rate payroll and H2A labor compliance reporting. Crew-level tracking enables cost-of-gain calculations per crew and supports labor comp']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce employee or contractor who performed and recorded the weight measurement. Required for audit trail and operator performance tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm, feedlot, barn, or production facility where the weight measurement was taken. Supports geographic and facility-level performance analytics.']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: weight_measurement records individual and group weights at production checkpoints. The herd context is essential for herd-level average daily gain (ADG) reporting, performance benchmarking, and market']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: weight_measurement records are taken at specific pen/scale locations within the livestock facility. Adding pen_location_id → livestock.pen_location.pen_location_id enables location-based weight perfor']
  adg_kg decimal(6,4) [note: 'Average Daily Gain (ADG) in kilograms per day, calculated as the difference between current and previous body weight divided by the number of days elapsed. A key Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for growth performance tracking and feed conversion ratio (FCR) analysis. Stored as a captured/confirmed value from the source system, not recomputed.']
  adjusted_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Body weight after applying shrink percentage adjustment, representing the net pay weight used for sale, transfer, or harvest transactions. Calculated as body_weight_kg × (1 - shrink_pct/100) and stored for traceability.']
  age_days varchar [note: 'Age of the animal in days at the time of measurement, calculated from the recorded birth date. Used to compute age-adjusted weight benchmarks, frame scores, and to assess growth performance relative to breed and production stage expectations.']
  body_condition_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Body Condition Score (BCS) assigned to the animal at the time of weighing, typically on a 1–9 scale for beef cattle or 1–5 scale for dairy/sheep. Assesses fat cover and muscle mass relative to skeletal frame. Critical for animal welfare compliance, breeding readiness, and nutritional management decisions.']
  body_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The measured live body weight of the individual animal or average body weight per head for a group measurement, expressed in kilograms. This is the principal quantitative outcome of the measurement event. Used in ADG calculations, FCR analysis, and harvest readiness assessment.']
  body_weight_lbs decimal(10,3) [note: 'The measured live body weight expressed in pounds (lbs), the primary unit of measure used in US domestic livestock markets, USDA reporting, and sale/harvest transactions. Maintained alongside kg for dual-unit reporting compliance.']
  breed_code varchar [note: 'Standardized breed code for the animal being weighed (e.g., ANGUS, HEREFORD, DUROC, HOLSTEIN). Used to apply breed-specific growth curves, expected weight benchmarks, and frame score standards in performance analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this weight measurement record was first created in the system of record. Distinct from measurement_timestamp which captures the real-world event time. Required for data lineage and Silver Layer audit trail.']
  days_on_feed varchar [note: 'Number of days the animal or group has been on the current feed ration since feedlot entry or last production stage transition. Used in conjunction with ADG to assess feed conversion efficiency and project harvest readiness date.']
  ear_tag_number varchar [note: 'Visual ear tag identifier recorded at the time of weighing for cross-verification with RFID and as a backup identification method. Used in manual weighing operations and visual lot reconciliation.']
  frame_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numerical frame score (typically 1–9 for beef cattle) based on hip height measurement at a standard age, used to predict mature body size and optimal harvest weight. Supports breed selection, feeding program design, and market endpoint targeting.']
  harvest_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to True when the animal\'s current body weight, BCS, and days on feed meet the defined harvest readiness criteria for the production program. Triggers harvest scheduling workflow and FSIS pre-harvest notification processes.']
  head_count varchar [note: 'Number of animals included in the measurement event. For individual measurements this is 1. For group/pen measurements this is the total number of animals weighed, used to calculate average body weight per head and total pen weight.']
  hip_height_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured hip height of the animal in centimeters at the time of weighing, used to calculate frame score and assess skeletal development relative to body weight. Supports growth curve modeling and breed standard compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot Number (LOT) identifying the production batch or consignment group to which the animal belongs at the time of measurement. Enables batch-level traceability from farm through harvest, supporting FSMA and FSIS compliance requirements.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the weight measurement was taken, stored as a DATE type for day-level partitioning, reporting, and production checkpoint scheduling. Derived from measurement_timestamp but retained for query performance.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'The technique or equipment type used to obtain the body weight. Affects measurement precision and confidence. Individual scale and load cell are most accurate; weigh tape and estimated methods carry higher variance and are flagged accordingly in analytics.']
  measurement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator observations recorded at the time of weighing, such as animal behavior, equipment anomalies, environmental conditions, or reasons for weight variance. Supports audit trail and veterinary follow-up.']
  measurement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the weight measurement event, used for cross-referencing with farm management systems, scale printouts, and audit trails. Format: WM-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the weight measurement record. \'recorded\' = initial capture; \'verified\' = validated by supervisor or QA; \'rejected\' = failed validation rules; \'voided\' = cancelled after entry error.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the weight measurement was physically taken. This is the principal real-world event timestamp, distinct from record audit timestamps. Critical for Average Daily Gain (ADG) calculations and production checkpoint tracking.']
  previous_measurement_date date [note: 'The date of the immediately preceding weight measurement for the same animal or group. Used to calculate the interval in days for ADG computation and to validate measurement frequency compliance.']
  previous_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The body weight recorded at the immediately preceding measurement event for the same animal or group, in kilograms. Stored on this record to support ADG validation and trend analysis without requiring a self-join.']
  production_stage varchar [note: 'Defined production checkpoint at which the weight measurement was taken, representing the animal\'s lifecycle stage. Drives growth performance benchmarking, feed conversion ratio (FCR) calculations, and harvest readiness assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: birth|weaning|backgrounding|feedlot_entry|mid_feed|pre_harvest|harvest — promote to reference product]']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'The Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) electronic tag number read from the animal at the time of weighing, used to confirm animal identity and enable automated data capture from walk-over scales and electronic identification systems. Supports USDA APHIS traceability requirements.']
  sex_code varchar [note: 'Sex classification of the animal: M=Male (intact bull/boar/ram), F=Female (heifer/cow/gilt/ewe), C=Castrated male (steer/barrow/wether), S=Spayed female. Affects expected growth rates, weight benchmarks, and market endpoint targets.']
  shrink_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated or measured percentage of body weight lost due to gut fill, stress, or transit prior to weighing (pencil shrink or actual shrink). Applied in sale weight calculations and market price negotiations. Standard industry practice in US livestock markets.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this weight measurement record was ingested into the Silver Layer (e.g., Granular Farm Management, Climate FieldView, SAP S/4HANA PP, John Deere Operations Center, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  species_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the livestock species being weighed. Determines applicable weight benchmarks, BCS scales, frame score standards, and regulatory reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BEEF|DAIRY|SWINE|SHEEP|GOAT|POULTRY|EQUINE — promote to reference product]']
  total_group_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total combined live weight of all animals in a group measurement event, expressed in kilograms. Null for individual animal measurements. Used for pen-level inventory valuation and feed planning.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure in which the weight was recorded at the point of capture (kg or lbs). Determines which weight field is the source-of-truth value; the alternate unit field is a conversion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this weight measurement record was last modified, including status changes, corrections, or verification actions. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer pipeline.']
  weigh_condition varchar [note: 'Describes the gut-fill or fasting condition of the animal at the time of weighing, which directly affects the accuracy and comparability of weight measurements. Used to apply appropriate shrink adjustments and flag outlier weights.']
  weight_variance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator set to True when the measured body weight deviates from the expected weight by more than the configured tolerance threshold (e.g., ±15% from breed/age/stage benchmark). Triggers data quality review and potential animal health investigation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual or group weight measurements taken at defined production checkpoints (weaning, backgrounding, feedlot entry, pre-harvest). Captures measurement date, animal ID or group ID, scale ID, body weight (lbs/kg), average daily gain (ADG) since last measurement, body condition score (BCS), frame score, measurement method (individual scale, load cell, weigh tape), and operator. Feeds into growth performance tracking, feed conversion ratio (FCR) calculations, and harvest readiness assessment.'
}

Table livestock.feed_ration {
  feed_ration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each feed ration record (formulation or delivery event). Primary key for the feed_ration data product in the livestock domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Feed rations are mixed using specific TMR mixer assets. Linking feed_ration to the mixing asset supports calibration tracking (accurate ingredient inclusion rates), maintenance scheduling based on mix']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Feed rations are approved and managed at the facility level for FDA VFD medicated feed compliance and organic certification scope. A facility_id FK enables facility-specific ration approval workflows ']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Medicated and antibiotic-containing feed rations (antibiotic_flag, withdrawal_period_days) fall under HACCP/FSMA Preventive Controls for Animal Food. Linking feed_ration to its governing haccp_plan do']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Feed rations are formulated for specific herds or production classes within a herd. Linking feed_ration to herd enables herd-level nutritional management, cost-per-head analysis by herd, and supports ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Medicated feed rations (antibiotic_flag=true) are subject to FDA VFD requirements and MRL thresholds for drug residues in food animals. Linking feed_ration to product.mrl_threshold enables automated w']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pen or animal group to which this ration is assigned (formulation) or to which feed was delivered (delivery event). Links ration records to specific animal housing units for FCR tracking and feed cost allocation per animal group.']
  primary_feed_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed animal nutritionist or feed specialist responsible for formulating or approving this ration. Supports accountability, NRC compliance documentation, and audit trail for regulatory inspections.']
  antibiotic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ration contains any antibiotic or medicated feed additive subject to VFD (Veterinary Feed Directive) or withdrawal period requirements. True = medicated; False = non-medicated. Critical for FSIS antibiotic residue compliance and withdrawal period tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the ration formulation in the approval lifecycle: draft (in preparation), pending_review (submitted to nutritionist), approved (cleared for use), rejected (not approved), superseded (replaced by newer version). Applicable to formulation records.']
  bunk_score varchar [note: 'Standardized numeric score (typically 0–4) assessing the feed bunk condition at the time of delivery: 0=empty/slick, 1=light cover, 2=normal, 3=heavy cover, 4=full refusal. Applicable to delivery records. Guides feeding rate adjustments and animal health monitoring.']
  calcium_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Calculated calcium (Ca) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Critical macro-mineral for bone development, milk production, and metabolic health. NRC compliance metric for all species.']
  cost_per_head_per_day decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated feed cost in USD per animal per day based on ingredient prices and inclusion rates in the formulation. Applicable to formulation records. Key input for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) allocation, OPEX tracking, and ROI analysis per production class.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feed ration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  crude_protein_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Calculated crude protein content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Key NRC nutritional compliance metric for protein adequacy across species and production classes. Applicable to formulation records.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost_per_head_per_day field (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international agriculture enterprises.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver feed to the animal group: TMR (Total Mixed Ration) mixer wagon, bunk feeder, automated feeding system, hand feed, or conveyor. Applicable to delivery records. Impacts feed uniformity and labor cost allocation.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which feed was physically delivered to the pen or bunk. Principal business event timestamp for delivery records. Supports feeding schedule compliance, FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) tracking, and daily intake analysis.']
  dry_matter_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Dry matter content of the ration expressed as a percentage of as-fed weight. Used to convert as-fed quantities to DM basis for NRC nutrient requirement compliance and ration balancing.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this ration formulation becomes active and approved for use in feed delivery operations. Supports ration plan management and ensures animals receive the correct approved formulation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this ration formulation is no longer valid for use. Triggers review or replacement of the formulation. Applicable to formulation records.']
  formulation_date date [note: 'Date on which the ration formulation was created or last revised by the nutritionist. Applicable to formulation records. Used for version control and audit trail of recipe changes.']
  gmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ration formulation contains any GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) ingredients. True = contains GMO ingredients; False = non-GMO. Supports USDA National Organic Program (NOP) compliance, COOL labeling, and customer transparency requirements.']
  mixing_instructions varchar [note: 'Operational instructions for preparing the TMR or batch mix, including ingredient loading sequence, mixer run time, and any special handling notes. Applicable to formulation records. Supports SOP compliance and feed uniformity.']
  neg_mcal_per_cwt decimal(8,4) [note: 'Net Energy for Gain (NEg) value of the ration expressed in megacalories per hundredweight (CWT) of dry matter. Used for NRC-compliant growth performance prediction and ration optimization in beef cattle finishing programs.']
  nem_mcal_per_cwt decimal(8,4) [note: 'Net Energy for Maintenance (NEm) value of the ration expressed in megacalories per hundredweight (CWT) of dry matter. Used for NRC-compliant energy adequacy assessment in beef and dairy cattle rations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional nutritionist comments, special handling instructions, or operational observations related to the ration formulation or delivery event. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.']
  nrc_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ration formulation meets all NRC (National Research Council) nutrient requirement thresholds for the target species and production class. True = compliant; False = one or more NRC thresholds not met. Applicable to formulation records.']
  phosphorus_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Calculated phosphorus (P) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Key macro-mineral for energy metabolism and bone structure. NRC compliance metric; also tracked for environmental nutrient management under EPA guidelines.']
  production_class varchar [note: 'Specific production class or physiological stage of the target animal group (e.g., \'Finishing Steers\', \'Lactating Dairy Cows\', \'Nursery Pigs\', \'Broiler Grower\'). Used for NRC-compliant nutrient requirement matching and ration cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  quantity_delivered_dm_lbs decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of feed delivered expressed on a dry matter (DM) basis in pounds. Calculated from as-fed quantity and dry matter percentage. Used for NRC-compliant nutrient intake calculations and ration balancing.']
  quantity_delivered_lbs decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of feed delivered to the pen on an as-fed basis, measured in pounds (lbs). Applicable to delivery records. Used for feed cost allocation per animal, FCR tracking, and daily intake monitoring.']
  ration_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the feed ration formulation (e.g., \'Grower Phase 2 TMR\', \'Lactating Cow High-Energy\'). Used for identification in feed management systems and nutritionist communications.']
  ration_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the ration formulation or delivery event, used for cross-system reference and traceability (e.g., RAT-2024-00123). Sourced from Granular Farm Management or SAP PP.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator field separating the two concerns captured in this product: \'formulation\' for master ration recipes and \'delivery\' for transactional feed delivery events. Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.']
  refusal_amount_lbs decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of feed remaining uneaten in the bunk at the time of the next feeding, measured in pounds. Applicable to delivery records. Used to calculate actual intake, adjust ration quantities, and monitor animal health and appetite.']
  target_daily_intake_lbs decimal(8,3) [note: 'Planned daily feed intake per head per day on an as-fed basis, in pounds. Applicable to formulation records. Derived from NRC nutrient requirements for the target species and production class. Drives delivery quantity planning.']
  target_species varchar [note: 'Animal species for which this ration is formulated or delivered (e.g., cattle, swine, poultry). Drives NRC nutritional compliance checks and species-specific regulatory requirements under APHIS and FSIS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cattle|swine|poultry|sheep|goat|equine|other — promote to reference product]']
  tdn_pct decimal(6,3) [note: 'Total Digestible Nutrients (TDN) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Primary energy metric for ruminant rations used in NRC compliance and ration balancing. Applicable to formulation records.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feed ration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the ration formulation, incremented each time the recipe is revised. Enables tracking of formulation changes over time and ensures delivery events reference the correct recipe version.']
  withdrawal_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days animals must not receive this medicated ration prior to slaughter to ensure antibiotic residue levels are within FDA/FSIS MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) tolerances. Applicable when antibiotic_flag is True. Critical for FSIS compliance and food safety.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for feed nutrition management — encompassing both ration formulations (master) and feed delivery events (transactional). For formulations: captures ration name, version number, target species and production class, formulation date, nutritionist reference, ingredient list with inclusion rates (percent as-fed and DM basis), calculated nutrient profile (crude protein, TDN, NEm, NEg, calcium, phosphorus, trace minerals, vitamins), dry matter basis, target daily intake (lbs/head/day), cost per head per day, mixing instructions, and approval status. For delivery events: captures delivery date and time, pen/group ID, ration reference, quantity delivered (lbs/kg as-fed and dry matter), delivery method (TMR mixer, bunk feeder, automated system), operator, refusal amount, and feed bunk score. Record type discriminator (formulation vs delivery) separates the two concerns. Serves as the SSOT for feed formulation and delivery, supporting NRC nutritional compliance, feed cost allocation per animal, FCR tracking, and ration plan management.'
}

Table livestock.feed_delivery {
  feed_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each feed delivery event. Primary key for the feed_delivery data product in the livestock domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the TMR mixer wagon or automated feeding equipment used for this delivery. Supports equipment utilization tracking, maintenance scheduling, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis, and feed delivery cost allocation.']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the feed inventory lot (LOT) consumed in this delivery. Enables batch-level traceability, feed cost allocation, and withdrawal period tracking for medicated feeds per APHIS and FDA requirements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Feed is the largest cost in livestock operations (60-70% of total cost). Cost center assignment on feed_delivery enables feed cost per head per day reporting, ration cost variance analysis, and cost c']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Feed delivery in large feedlots is performed by feed crews operating mixer wagons. Crew-level tracking enables labor cost allocation per delivery, piece-rate payroll for feed drivers, and H2A complian']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the farm operator or employee who performed or supervised the feed delivery. Supports workforce accountability, SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) compliance, and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the farm facility or barn where the feed delivery took place. Supports multi-site operations, feed cost allocation by facility, and regulatory site-level reporting.']
  feed_ration_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prescribed feed ration formulation delivered. Used to verify nutritional compliance against the prescribed ration, support GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices) audits, and enable deviation analysis.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Feed ingredient testing for mycotoxins (aflatoxin, DON), pathogens (Salmonella), and nutritional analysis is performed at delivery receipt. FDA FSMA Preventive Controls require feed safety testing. A ']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Feed ingredient lot traceability is required for medicated feed VFD compliance, FSMA Preventive Controls, and withdrawal period management. Linking feed_delivery to lot_trace enables full chain-of-cus']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Contaminated, out-of-spec, or adulterated feed deliveries generate nonconformances requiring quarantine, rejection, or corrective action. FDA FSMA and HACCP programs mandate nonconformance tracking fo']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pen or housing unit where the feed was delivered. Links to the pen/group master record for animal group association, capacity tracking, and feed cost allocation per pen.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Feed deliveries must be matched to procurement goods receipts for three-way match (PO/GR/invoice) and FSMA supply chain documentation. Linking feed_delivery to procurement_goods_receipt enables quanti']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Feed costs are the largest single expense in livestock operations (50-70% of total cost). Profit center attribution on feed_delivery records enables feed cost allocation to livestock enterprise P&L se']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Feed deliveries must reconcile against the originating procurement PO for three-way match (PO→GRN→pen delivery), feed cost allocation per production group, and FSMA supply chain traceability. A livest']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Feed deliveries from external suppliers arrive as inbound supply chain shipments. Linking feed_delivery to shipment enables FSMA feed ingredient traceability, medicated feed VFD compliance verificatio']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Feed delivery operations require direct reference to the source silo/bin storage location for inventory depletion, feed dispatch routing, and FIFO/FEFO rotation compliance. Role prefix \'source_\' disti']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: The specific mixer wagon or feed truck used for each delivery is tracked for equipment maintenance scheduling, feed contamination investigations, and FSMA sanitary transport compliance. feed_delivery ']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Feed is delivered under vendor supply contracts with volume commitments and price agreements. Linking feed_delivery to vendor_contract enables contract fulfillment tracking, price verification against']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Feed supplier identity per delivery is required for vendor performance scorecarding (on-time delivery, fill rate), FSMA preventive controls traceability, and feed recall response. Linking feed_deliver']
  animal_count_fed varchar [note: 'Number of animals present in the pen or group at the time of feed delivery. Used to calculate per-head feed intake, normalize FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) metrics, and detect anomalies in feed allocation relative to group size.']
  bunk_score varchar [note: 'Standardized numeric score (typically 0–4 scale) assessing the amount of feed remaining in the bunk at the time of delivery or pre-delivery inspection. Score 0 = empty/slick bunk; Score 4 = heavy refusal. Used to calibrate next delivery quantity and monitor animal appetite and health status.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the feed delivery record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the feed cost amounts recorded in this delivery (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency operations for global agriculture enterprises.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver feed to the pen or group. Options include TMR (Total Mixed Ration) mixer wagon, bunk feeder, automated feeding system, hand delivery, or conveyor. Affects feed uniformity, labor cost allocation, and equipment utilization tracking.']
  delivery_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator observations, exceptions, or special conditions recorded at the time of feed delivery (e.g., equipment malfunction, animal behavior observations, ingredient substitutions). Supports SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) deviation documentation and veterinary review.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the feed delivery event, used for cross-referencing with feed management systems, SAP production orders, and operator logs. Follows the format FD-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  delivery_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order number of this delivery within the daily feeding schedule (e.g., 1st feeding, 2nd feeding). Supports multi-feeding-per-day operations, feeding frequency analysis, and nutritional timing compliance.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feed delivery event, tracking progression from scheduled through completion or cancellation. Supports operational monitoring and FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) completeness checks.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world date and time when the feed delivery event occurred at the pen or group location. Used for feed scheduling compliance, nutritional timing analysis, and FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) tracking.']
  delivery_variance_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Difference between the prescribed quantity and the actual quantity delivered on an as-fed basis, in kilograms (actual minus prescribed). Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery. Used for nutritional compliance verification and ration adherence KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting.']
  dry_matter_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Dry matter percentage of the delivered ration at the time of delivery, expressed as a percentage. Used to convert as-fed quantities to dry matter basis and to verify ration moisture content against the prescribed formulation.']
  feed_cost_per_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Unit cost of the delivered feed ration per kilogram on an as-fed basis, sourced from the feed inventory lot or procurement record. Used for feed cost allocation per animal group, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  feed_source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the feed source for this delivery. Indicates whether the ration was mixed on-farm (TMR), purchased as a complete feed, custom-mixed by a feed mill, or derived from co-products. Supports feed cost analysis, supply chain traceability, and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the feed ration delivered contains GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) ingredients, is certified non-GMO, or has unknown GMO status. Required for USDA National Organic Program (NOP) compliance, GLOBALG.A.P. certification, and customer supply chain transparency reporting.']
  is_medicated_feed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivered ration contains a medicated additive subject to FDA Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) or APHIS withdrawal period requirements. When True, triggers mandatory withdrawal period tracking and regulatory documentation requirements.']
  mixing_duration_min varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes that the TMR (Total Mixed Ration) mixer was operated to prepare the ration batch prior to delivery. Mixing time affects ration uniformity and particle size distribution; tracked for GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices) compliance and equipment OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivered feed ration is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). When True, the feed must originate from certified organic sources and the delivery must be documented for NOP audit compliance.']
  prescribed_quantity_asfed_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The ration-prescribed target quantity of feed to be delivered on an as-fed basis, in kilograms. Compared against actual quantity delivered to calculate delivery variance and assess nutritional compliance against the prescribed ration.']
  quantity_delivered_asfed_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of feed delivered to the pen or group on an as-fed basis, measured in kilograms. As-fed weight includes moisture content and is the primary operational quantity recorded at delivery. Used for feed cost allocation and FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) calculations.']
  quantity_delivered_dm_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of feed delivered expressed on a dry matter (DM) basis, measured in kilograms. Dry matter quantity normalizes for moisture variation across ration ingredients and is the standard basis for nutritional compliance verification and FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) analysis.']
  ration_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the feed ration delivered (e.g., \'Grower Phase 2 TMR\', \'Lactating Cow Ration\'). Provides operational context for operators and supports reporting without requiring a ration master lookup.']
  refusal_quantity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of feed remaining uneaten in the bunk or feeder at the time of the next delivery or scheduled bunk reading, measured in kilograms. Refusal data is critical for adjusting ration delivery amounts, monitoring animal health, and calculating net intake for FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) analysis.']
  scheduled_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time for the feed delivery as defined in the feeding schedule. Compared against actual delivery_timestamp to calculate delivery timing variance and assess adherence to the nutritional management plan.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this feed delivery record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PP, Granular Farm Management, Climate FieldView, Trimble Ag Software, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, Silver Layer reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.']
  total_feed_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total cost of feed delivered in this event, calculated as quantity_delivered_asfed_kg multiplied by feed_cost_per_kg. Used for feed cost allocation per pen/group, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) reporting, and EBITDA analysis. Stored as a transactional fact to preserve the cost at time of delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the feed delivery record was last modified. Supports audit trail, data quality monitoring, and change detection in the Silver Layer.']
  vfd_number varchar [note: 'The FDA Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) authorization number associated with the medicated feed delivered. Required for regulatory compliance when is_medicated_feed is True. Supports APHIS and FSIS audit traceability.']
  withdrawal_period_end_date date [note: 'The date after which animals that received medicated feed are eligible for harvest, calculated from the delivery date plus the required withdrawal period for the active ingredient (AI). Critical for FSIS pre-harvest compliance and antibiotic residue avoidance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each feed delivery event to a pen or group. Captures delivery date and time, pen/group ID, ration ID, quantity delivered (lbs/kg as-fed and dry matter), delivery method (TMR mixer, bunk feeder, automated system), operator, refusal amount recorded, and feed bunk score. Enables feed cost allocation per animal, FCR tracking, and nutritional compliance verification against prescribed rations.'
}

Lin']

  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: FSMA Traceability Rule and USDA animal traceability programs require linking animal movement events to lot trace records for forward/backward traceability in disease outbreak or food safety investigat']

Linking animal_movement to transport_order enables FSMA Sanitary Transport compliance tr']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: The specific trailer used for livestock transport is tracked for FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance (cleaning/disinfection records between loads), animal welfare audits, and biosecurity investiga']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: Animal movements to sale barns, feedlots, or packing plants generate weight tickets at the destination documenting head count and weight for commercial settlement. Linking animal_movement to weight_ti']
  average_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average live weight in kilograms of animals in this movement at time of loading. Used for transport payload calculations, sale weight documentation, and performance benchmarking against YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) targets.']
  biosecurity_zone_crossing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this movement crosses a defined biosecurity zone boundary. True triggers mandatory cleaning and disinfection (C&D) protocols for transport vehicles and equipment per APHIS biosecurity guidelines.']
  brand_inspection_number varchar [note: 'State brand inspection certificate number issued for livestock movements in brand-law states. Required for cattle and horse movements in western US states to verify ownership and prevent theft.']
  cool_country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of origin for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance. Required for covered commodities sold at retail under USDA AMS COOL regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this movement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record-keeping compliance (records must be maintained for 2 years).']
  destination_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the destination location of this movement. Required for APHIS interstate movement reporting and COOL compliance documentation.']
  head_count_arrived varchar [note: 'Actual number of animals confirmed received at the destination location upon arrival. May differ from head_count_moved due to in-transit mortality or escapes. Used for shrink calculation and inventory reconciliation.']
  head_count_moved varchar [note: 'Number of individual animals included in this movement transaction. For individual animal movements this is 1; for group movements this reflects the total head count transferred. Critical for inventory reconciliation and APHIS traceability.']
  health_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the veterinary health certificate accompanying this movement. Movements occurring after this date are non-compliant with APHIS interstate movement regulations.']
  interstate_movement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this movement crosses state lines. True triggers mandatory APHIS official identification and Certificate of Veterinary Inspection (CVI) requirements under the Animal Disease Traceability rule.']
  movement_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the animal or group movement physically occurred. Principal business event date used for APHIS traceability reporting, COOL compliance, and withdrawal period calculations.']
  movement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or observations recorded at time of movement. May include animal condition observations, loading difficulties, or regulatory exceptions noted by the handler.']
  movement_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or purpose for the animal or group movement. Drives downstream processing workflows, APHIS reporting categories, and biosecurity zone management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weaning|backgrounding|feedlot_placement|sale|harvest|death|breeding|health_treatment|pasture_rotation|quarantine — promote to reference product]']
  movement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this movement transaction, used for cross-referencing with transport documents, USDA premises records, and operational systems such as SAP S/4HANA livestock movement orders. Serves as the human-readable transaction code.']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the movement transaction. Tracks the workflow from planning through physical completion, supporting operational visibility and exception management.']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the animal or group movement was initiated or completed, captured from FMIS or IoT gate sensors. Provides sub-day precision for biosecurity event sequencing and audit trails.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the movement scope: internal (within same premises), inter-premises (between registered USDA premises), external sale (to buyer), or external purchase (from seller). Determines APHIS reporting obligations and COOL documentation requirements.']
  official_id_number varchar [note: 'The official identification number (e.g., RFID tag number, ear tag number, brand registration number) recorded at time of movement. Required for APHIS interstate movement documentation and disease traceability investigations.']
  official_id_type varchar [note: 'Type of official animal identification applied or verified at time of movement. RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) ear tags are the USDA-approved official identification for interstate movements under ADT rule.']
  origin_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for the origin location of this movement. Required for APHIS interstate movement reporting, COOL country/state of origin labeling, and state brand inspection compliance.']
  species_code varchar [note: 'Standardized species classification of the animals being moved. Used for APHIS species-specific traceability requirements, FSIS inspection routing, and biosecurity protocol selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOVINE|SWINE|OVINE|CAPRINE|EQUINE|POULTRY|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total live weight in kilograms of all animals in this movement at time of loading. Used for transport billing, sale contracts, and FSIS ante-mortem documentation.']
  transport_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance in kilometers traveled during this movement. Used for animal welfare compliance (EU transport regulations, USDA 28-Hour Law), transport cost allocation, and carbon footprint reporting.']
  transport_duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total hours animals were in transit during this movement. Critical for USDA 28-Hour Law compliance (animals must be unloaded for rest, water, and feed after 28 consecutive hours of transport) and animal welfare audits.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this movement record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements, data quality monitoring, and change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  vehicle_license_plate varchar [note: 'License plate number of the transport vehicle or trailer used for this movement. Recorded for biosecurity traceability, DOT compliance, and chain-of-custody documentation when the vehicle is not registered in the fleet asset system.']
  veterinary_health_cert_number varchar [note: 'Certificate of Veterinary Inspection (CVI) or health certificate number issued by an accredited veterinarian for interstate or international movements. Required by APHIS for regulated species and disease-control movements.']
  withdrawal_period_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any animal in this movement is currently within an antibiotic or chemical withdrawal period (Pre-Harvest Interval - PHI). True prevents movement to harvest facilities and triggers FSIS compliance holds. Supports antibiotic stewardship and FSMA compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every animal or group movement between pens, pastures, facilities, or external premises. Captures movement date, animal or group ID, origin location, destination location, movement reason (weaning, backgrounding, feedlot placement, sale, harvest, death), head count moved, transport vehicle ID, driver, and USDA premises ID for inter-premises moves. Supports APHIS traceability, COOL compliance, and biosecurity zone management.'
}

Table livestock.animal_disposition {
  animal_disposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for animal_disposition']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Animal disposition (harvest/slaughter) records must reference the packer/processor as a customer account for FSMA traceability, COOL compliance, and accounts receivable. Farm-to-processor traceability']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual animal record in the livestock master. Links this disposition event to the animal\'s full lifecycle history including breeding, health, and feed records.']

The existing ar_invoice_id covers the invoice but not the b']
  broker_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.broker. Business justification: Livestock dispositions (sales) are frequently facilitated by licensed livestock brokers. Linking disposition to broker supports commission calculation, broker performance reporting, and 1099 tax repor']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Slaughter disposition generates a COA documenting residue compliance, pathogen testing, and carcass quality for food safety release. Export markets require COAs linked to individual animal disposition']
