  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Farm-to-table traceability and USDA/FSIS compliance require linking each animal disposition (harvest) to the resulting commodity lot in inventory. Enables carcass yield reconciliation, antibiotic with']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Animal dispositions (sales, deaths, culls) are legal-entity-level financial events requiring company code assignment for statutory financial reporting, intercompany elimination, and USDA/FSA program c']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Animal dispositions (slaughter, sale) are executed against specific sales contracts. Traceability from individual disposition to the governing contract is required for contract fulfillment reporting, ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Mortality and condemnation events are significant cost items in livestock operations. Cost center assignment on animal_disposition supports mortality cost reporting by herd/facility, insurance claim d']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Harvest and euthanasia events involve dedicated crews. animal_disposition has individual employee FKs but no crew_id. Crew-level disposition tracking supports labor cost allocation at harvest and anim']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Disposition records reference the specific processing establishment (USDA-inspected facility) where harvest occurs. Linking to customer.delivery_location (which carries usda_establishment_number and f']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: When animals are disposed (sold/transferred to packer), a delivery order governs the physical handoff. Linking disposition to delivery_order enables end-to-end traceability from individual animal to d']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility (farm, feedlot, slaughter plant, or processing facility) where the disposition event occurred. Required for FSIS establishment-level reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Animal dispositions (death loss, condemnation, harvest) require GL postings to inventory write-down, mortality loss, or COGS accounts. GAAP and USDA reporting require proper GL classification of dispo']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: USDA AMS grading compliance requires linking each carcass disposition record to the applicable grading_standard that defines USDA quality/yield grade criteria. Enables grade-based pricing, USDA shield']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the herd or flock group from which the animal originated at the time of disposition. Supports traceability and group-level regulatory reporting.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: USDA ante-mortem and post-mortem inspections at harvest are formal inspection events. animal_disposition captures usda_quality_grade, usda_yield_grade, condemnation_reason_code — all outputs of the go']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: USDA FSIS residue testing at slaughter (NRMP program) and carcass condemnation pathology samples are collected at disposition. A domain expert expects animal_disposition to reference the residue/patho']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Animal harvest creates a supply chain lot trace record for the resulting carcass/product lot. Linking animal_disposition to lot_trace enables farm-to-harvest traceability required by FSMA, COOL, and f']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Condemned carcasses, failed withdrawal compliance, and carcass condemnation_reason_code directly generate nonconformance records. USDA FSIS post-mortem inspection outcomes require formal nonconformanc']
  price_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.price_agreement. Business justification: Animal sale prices are governed by price agreements (formula pricing, grid pricing). Linking disposition to price_agreement enables accurate revenue calculation, price compliance auditing, and settlem']

Business justification: Animal disposition pricing (per-cwt live, dressed weight, yield-grade premiums) is governed by a price basis record. Linking disposition to price_basis enables accurate carcass valuation, grade-based ']
  primary_animal_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the FSIS or USDA-accredited veterinary inspector who conducted ante-mortem and/or post-mortem inspection. Required for regulatory audit trail and inspection record integrity.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Animal disposition events (cull sales, death losses, harvest) are the primary revenue and loss recognition events in livestock operations. Profit center attribution on dispositions is required for liv']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: FSMA Subpart S traceability requires linking harvest/disposition records to sales orders for carcass lot tracking. When an animal is harvested and sold, the disposition record must reference the sales']
  settlement_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.settlement_statement. Business justification: Packer settlement statements reconcile animal dispositions — documenting carcass weights, quality grades, premiums/discounts, and net proceeds per head. Linking disposition directly to settlement_stat']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Harvest/disposition events where animals ship to a packer are executed as supply chain shipments. Linking animal_disposition to shipment enables carcass traceability, COOL documentation, packer settle']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: Animal disposition (slaughter/sale) generates a commercial weight ticket documenting hot carcass weight or live weight at point of sale. This weight ticket is the basis for packer/buyer settlement and']
  age_days varchar [note: 'Age of the animal in days at the time of disposition, calculated from birth date. Used for regulatory age-at-slaughter compliance (e.g., veal, bob veal), carcass quality grading, and performance benchmarking.']
  antibiotic_withdrawal_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all antibiotic and veterinary drug withdrawal periods were satisfied prior to harvest. Critical food safety compliance flag. False triggers mandatory hold and regulatory notification under FSIS residue testing protocols.']
  aphis_report_number varchar [note: 'Official USDA APHIS report number assigned when this disposition event triggers a mandatory regulatory notification (e.g., foreign animal disease suspicion, mass mortality event). Null if no APHIS report was required.']
  breed_code varchar [note: 'Standardized breed identifier for the animal. Relevant for carcass quality grading, yield prediction, and genetic performance analytics. Sourced from the animal master record at time of disposition.']
  carcass_disposition_code varchar [note: 'FSIS post-mortem inspection outcome for the carcass. Passed indicates approved for human consumption; condemned indicates rejected and must be destroyed or rendered; retained indicates held pending further inspection.']
  cause_of_death_code varchar [note: 'Standardized veterinary code identifying the primary cause of death for non-harvest disposition types (natural death, euthanasia, condemnation). Null for planned harvest events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: respiratory_disease|digestive_disorder|injury_trauma|metabolic_disorder|infectious_disease|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  cause_of_death_description varchar [note: 'Free-text veterinary narrative describing the cause of death or reason for condemnation. Supplements the standardized cause_of_death_code with clinical detail for mortality investigations and APHIS reporting.']
  condemnation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized FSIS code identifying the regulatory reason for carcass condemnation. Null for passed carcasses. Required for FSIS annual condemnation reporting and food safety analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: septicemia|pneumonia|peritonitis|abscess|contamination|bruising|cancer|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this disposition record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for data lineage, SOX compliance, and regulatory record retention requirements.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the terminal disposition event occurred. Used as the principal business event date for regulatory reporting, withdrawal period compliance, and financial period allocation.']
  disposition_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this disposition event, used in regulatory submissions, veterinary records, and supply chain documentation. Formatted as DISP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the disposition record. Tracks progression from initial capture through regulatory reporting completion. Pending indicates awaiting inspection; condemned indicates regulatory rejection of carcass.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the terminal disposition event was executed. Required for Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) and Restricted Entry Interval (REI) compliance calculations and ante-mortem inspection sequencing.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the terminal lifecycle event. Determines downstream regulatory reporting obligations, carcass handling procedures, and financial treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: harvest|natural_death|euthanasia|condemnation|salvage_sale|emergency_slaughter — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  dressing_yield_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of hot carcass weight to live weight expressed as a percentage. Key performance indicator for harvest efficiency and carcass value. Calculated as (hot_carcass_weight_kg / live_weight_kg) * 100.']
  ear_tag_number varchar [note: 'Visual ear tag identifier affixed to the animal. Used as the primary visual identification method at harvest facilities and for manual reconciliation against RFID records. May differ from RFID if tag was replaced.']
  establishment_number varchar [note: 'USDA FSIS-assigned official establishment number for the slaughter or processing facility. Required on all FSIS regulatory submissions, labels, and inspection records. Distinct from internal facility_id.']
  hot_carcass_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the carcass immediately after slaughter and evisceration, before chilling, expressed in kilograms. Primary basis for yield calculations, grading, and producer payment in harvest events.']
  last_treatment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent veterinary drug or antibiotic treatment administered to the animal prior to disposition. Used to verify withdrawal period compliance and calculate days-off-drug at time of harvest.']
  last_treatment_drug varchar [note: 'Name of the most recent veterinary drug or antibiotic administered to the animal. Used in conjunction with last_treatment_date to verify withdrawal period compliance against FDA-approved withdrawal intervals.']
  live_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Live body weight of the animal recorded at the time of ante-mortem inspection or mortality event, expressed in kilograms. Used for yield calculations, payment settlement, and performance analytics.']
  necropsy_findings varchar [note: 'Summary of pathological findings from the post-mortem examination. Documents lesions, disease conditions, and contributing factors identified during necropsy. Supports disease surveillance and herd health management.']
  necropsy_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-mortem examination (necropsy) was conducted on the animal. Required for APHIS disease surveillance reporting and mortality investigation protocols.']
  phi_days_required varchar [note: 'Number of days required between last drug treatment and harvest as specified on the drug label or veterinary prescription. Used to calculate and verify withdrawal period compliance at time of disposition.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the disposition event was officially reported to the regulatory authority. Used to verify timely reporting compliance with FSIS and APHIS notification deadlines.']
  regulatory_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this disposition event has been submitted to the applicable regulatory authority (FSIS, APHIS, or state agency). Supports compliance monitoring and audit readiness.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'RFID electronic identifier read at the time of disposition. Provides automated, tamper-resistant animal identification at harvest or mortality processing points. Supports USDA 840 official identification requirements.']
  salvage_sale_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value received for the animal or carcass in a salvage sale disposition. Applicable only when disposition_type is salvage_sale. Used for financial loss recovery accounting and insurance claim support.']
  sex_code varchar [note: 'Sex classification of the animal at disposition: M=Male (intact), F=Female, C=Castrated male, S=Spayed female. Affects carcass grading, yield estimates, and USDA quality grade assignment.']
  species_code varchar [note: 'Standardized species classification of the animal at disposition. Drives applicable regulatory framework (FSIS vs. APHIS), slaughter inspection requirements, and carcass grading standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this disposition record. Supports audit trail integrity, data lineage tracking, and change detection in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']
  usda_quality_grade varchar [note: 'Official USDA quality grade assigned to the carcass based on marbling, maturity, and other palatability factors. Drives pricing, market channel selection, and producer premium payments. Applicable primarily to beef. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Prime|Choice|Select|Standard|Commercial|Utility|Cutter|Canner — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  usda_yield_grade decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numerical USDA yield grade (1.0–5.9) indicating the estimated percentage of boneless, closely trimmed retail cuts from the round, loin, rib, and chuck. Lower values indicate higher cutability. Used for pricing and market channel decisions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every terminal animal lifecycle event — including harvest (slaughter), natural death, euthanasia, condemnation, and sale for salvage. Records disposition date, animal ID, disposition type, cause of death (for mortality events), necropsy results, carcass disposition, and regulatory reporting status.'
}

Table livestock.veterinary_prescription {
  veterinary_prescription_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each veterinary prescription or Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) record. Serves as the primary key for the veterinary_prescription data product.']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific animal or group of animals for which the prescription was issued. Links to the livestock animal master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed veterinarian (DVM) who issued the prescription. Required for VCPR validation and FDA VFD compliance.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: FDA VFD regulations require veterinary prescriptions (especially VFDs) to be associated with a specific premises/facility and kept on file there. A facility_id FK enables regulatory compliance reporti']
  health_protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.health_protocol. Business justification: veterinary_prescription is issued in accordance with established health protocols. Linking veterinary_prescription.health_protocol_id → livestock.health_protocol.health_protocol_id enables protocol-le']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the herd or flock for which the prescription applies when issued at the group level rather than individual animal level.']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Prescribed drugs must be in the approved procurement input catalog with PHI, withdrawal, and EPA registration data. Linking veterinary_prescription to input_catalog enforces approved formulary prescri']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Culture and sensitivity lab results are required to justify extra-label drug use and antibiotic selection under VCPR requirements. FDA VFD regulations and antibiotic stewardship programs require diagn']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: VFD and prescription compliance requires linking each veterinary prescription to the specific drug material_master for dispensing verification, quantity_authorized vs quantity_dispensed reconciliation']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Veterinary prescriptions, especially VFDs, drive specific purchase orders for medicated feed or drugs. Linking veterinary_prescription to purchase_order enables prescription-to-procurement traceabilit']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Veterinary prescriptions drive pharmaceutical procurement under vendor contracts. Linking veterinary_prescription to vendor_contract enables formulary contract compliance, ensures prescribed drugs are']
  animal_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'The average or representative body weight in kilograms of the animals at the time of prescription. Used for weight-based dosage calculations and to verify appropriate dosing.']
  approved_species varchar [note: 'The animal species for which the drug is approved and prescribed (e.g., cattle, swine, poultry, sheep, goats). Must align with FDA label approval or AMDUCA extra-label use authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cattle|swine|poultry|sheep|goats|equine|aquaculture — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this veterinary prescription record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FSIS record retention compliance (VFD records must be retained for 2 years).']
  dosage_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric quantity of drug to be administered per dose or per unit of feed (e.g., grams per ton of feed, mg per kg body weight). The principal quantitative fact for this prescription.']
  dosage_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency or schedule at which the prescribed drug is to be administered (e.g., once daily, continuous feed, single dose). Required on the prescription label per FDA regulations.']
  dosage_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the prescribed dosage amount (e.g., g/ton for medicated feed, mg/kg for injectable drugs). Required for accurate compounding and dispensing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: g/ton|mg/kg|mg/lb|g/head/day|oz/ton|ppm|mL/head — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  drug_class varchar [note: 'Antimicrobial or pharmacological class of the prescribed drug (e.g., tetracycline, macrolide, ionophore, beta-lactam). Used for antibiotic stewardship analytics and WHO critically important antimicrobial (CIA) classification tracking.']
  dvm_license_number varchar [note: 'The state-issued veterinary license number of the prescribing veterinarian (DVM). Required on all VFD documents per FDA regulation and for FSIS antibiotic stewardship audit trails.']
  dvm_state varchar [note: 'The US state in which the prescribing veterinarian holds their active license. Required for cross-state regulatory compliance verification.']
  egg_withdrawal_days varchar [note: 'The number of days eggs must be withheld from sale following drug administration in laying poultry. Applicable only for poultry operations; null for non-poultry species.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date after which the prescription is no longer valid and the authorized drug or medicated feed may not be dispensed or used. FDA VFD regulations require an expiration date not to exceed 6 months from issue date for most VFDs.']
  extra_label_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this prescription involves extra-label drug use (use of an FDA-approved drug in a manner not specified on the approved label). Extra-label use requires a valid VCPR and is regulated under AMDUCA (Animal Medicinal Drug Use Clarification Act).']
  indication varchar [note: 'The disease, condition, or clinical indication for which the drug is being prescribed (e.g., bovine respiratory disease, necrotic enteritis, mastitis prevention). Required for VCPR documentation and FSIS antibiotic use justification.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the veterinarian issued the prescription or VFD. This is the principal real-world event date for the prescription transaction and is required on all VFD documents per FDA regulation.']
  milk_withdrawal_days varchar [note: 'The number of days milk must be withheld from sale following drug administration in dairy animals. Separate from meat withdrawal period as milk and meat withdrawal periods often differ for the same drug.']
  number_of_animals varchar [note: 'The total count of animals covered under this prescription or VFD. Required on VFD documents per FDA regulation and used for dosage calculation and antibiotic use density metrics.']
  prescription_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the prescription or VFD at issuance. Used for regulatory filing, pharmacy dispensing, and audit trail purposes.']
  prescription_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the veterinary prescription. Active indicates the prescription is valid and may be dispensed; expired indicates the expiration date has passed; cancelled indicates the prescribing veterinarian voided the order; filled indicates the full authorized quantity has been dispensed.']
  prescription_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the prescription by regulatory category. VFD (Veterinary Feed Directive) applies to medicated feed products; prescription_drug covers legend drugs requiring a valid VCPR; OTC_extra_label covers over-the-counter drugs used in an extra-label manner; compounded covers compounded preparations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VFD|prescription_drug|OTC_extra_label|compounded — promote to reference product]']
  production_class varchar [note: 'The production class or use category of the animals covered by the prescription (e.g., beef cattle, dairy cattle, broiler, layer). Determines applicable withdrawal periods and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: beef_cattle|dairy_cattle|swine_market|swine_breeding|broiler|layer|turkey|sheep|goat|other — promote to reference product]']
  quantity_authorized decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total quantity of drug or medicated feed product authorized under this prescription (e.g., total grams, total pounds of medicated feed, total vials). Controls dispensing limits and prevents over-use.']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(12,3) [note: 'The cumulative quantity of drug or medicated feed product actually dispensed against this prescription to date. Used to track partial fills and ensure authorized quantity is not exceeded.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the authorized quantity (e.g., lbs of medicated feed, mL of injectable solution, number of vials). Required for dispensing accuracy and inventory reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lbs|kg|g|tons|mL|vials|tablets|oz — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The method by which the drug is to be administered to the animal (e.g., oral via medicated feed, intramuscular injection, subcutaneous injection). Required for proper drug use and withdrawal period determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|injectable_IM|injectable_IV|injectable_SQ|topical|intramammary|intrauterine — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for any additional veterinarian instructions, warnings, or conditions associated with the prescription (e.g., do not use in animals producing milk for human consumption, refrigerate after opening). Captures label-specific or VCPR-specific guidance.']
  treatment_duration_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the prescribed drug treatment is to be administered. Used to calculate total drug quantity needed, withdrawal period start date, and antibiotic use days for stewardship reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this veterinary prescription record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory compliance.']
  vcpr_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number or identifier for the established Veterinarian-Client-Patient Relationship (VCPR) under which this prescription was issued. A valid VCPR is a legal prerequisite for issuing any veterinary prescription or VFD under FDA regulations.']
  vfd_distributor_name varchar [note: 'The name of the licensed feed manufacturer or distributor authorized to produce and supply the medicated feed under this VFD. Required on VFD documents per FDA regulation for medicated feed traceability.']
  withdrawal_end_date date [note: 'The calculated calendar date on which the withdrawal period ends and the animal or animal products are cleared for harvest or sale. Derived from last treatment date plus withdrawal_period_days; stored for operational scheduling and FSIS compliance verification.']
  withdrawal_period_days varchar [note: 'The mandatory Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) — the number of days that must elapse after the last drug administration before the animal or animal products (meat, milk, eggs) may enter the food supply. Critical for FSIS food safety compliance and MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) adherence.']

  Note: 'Master record for veterinary prescriptions and Veterinary Feed Directives (VFDs) issued by a licensed veterinarian for use on the operation. Captures prescription date, issuing veterinarian (DVM license number), drug or medicated feed product, approved species and indication, dosage instructions, withdrawal period, quantity authorized, expiration date, VCPR (Veterinarian-Client-Patient Relationship) reference, and regulatory filing status. Required for FDA VFD compliance and FSIS antibiotic stewardship documentation.'
}

Table livestock.animal_transaction {
  animal_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each livestock commercial transfer record (purchase or sale). Primary key for the animal_transaction data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']

  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pen or lot assigned to receive and hold purchased animals upon arrival at the operation\'s premises. Populated only for purchase transactions. Links to facility management records for biosecurity and animal welfare compliance.']

Linking animal_transaction to vendor_contract e']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: Livestock sale settlement is driven by scale weight tickets — price per cwt requires a certified weight ticket. animal_transaction.weight_ticket_number is a denormalized reference; a proper FK to invo']
  antibiotic_withdrawal_clear_date date [note: 'The date on or after which the animals are cleared from any antibiotic withdrawal period and are eligible for harvest or sale. Critical for FSIS food safety compliance and prevention of violative drug residues in meat products. Populated based on most recent treatment records.']
  avg_weight_per_head_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average live weight per individual animal in pounds at the time of transfer. Derived operationally at point of transaction (scale ticket) and stored as a business fact. Supports performance benchmarking, feed efficiency analysis, and grading decisions.']
  brand_inspection_cert_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by the state brand inspection authority verifying ownership of branded livestock at time of transfer. Required in brand law states (e.g., TX, CO, MT, WY) for cattle and horse transactions to establish legal title.']
  breed varchar [note: 'Breed designation of the animals in the transaction (e.g., Angus, Hereford, Duroc, Hampshire). Relevant for cost basis accounting, genetic program tracking, and premium pricing in sales contracts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this animal transaction record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for data ingestion pipelines from source systems (SAP S/4HANA, Granular Farm Management).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction value (e.g., USD for US Dollar, CAD for Canadian Dollar, MXN for Mexican Peso). Supports multi-currency operations for cross-border livestock imports and exports.']
  grade_yield_basis varchar [note: 'The pricing and grading basis applied to outbound sale transactions. Indicates whether the sale price is based on live weight, dressed weight, USDA yield grade, USDA quality grade, or a formula-based pricing arrangement. Populated only for sale transactions.']
  head_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual animals included in this transaction. Used for inventory reconciliation, cost-per-head calculations, and APHIS movement reporting. Combined with weight and price fields to derive per-unit economics.']
  health_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official USDA APHIS Veterinary Services (VS) health certificate number issued by an accredited veterinarian certifying the health status of animals at time of movement. Required for interstate and international livestock shipments per 9 CFR Part 86.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot tracking identifier assigned to the group of animals in this transaction for traceability purposes. Supports APHIS animal movement traceability, FSIS recall readiness, and COOL chain-of-custody documentation from farm to harvest.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, or remarks associated with the livestock transaction. May include auction lot descriptions, special handling instructions, or negotiated contract terms not captured in structured fields.']
  origin_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the animals. Required for COOL compliance, import permit processing, and APHIS international traceability. Populated for all transactions; defaults to USA for domestic transfers.']
  origin_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the origin premises from which the animals were shipped. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) chain-of-custody documentation and APHIS interstate movement reporting.']
  price_per_cwt decimal(10,2) [note: 'Purchase or sale price expressed per hundredweight (CWT = 100 lbs) in USD. Standard pricing basis for cattle and hog transactions in North American livestock markets. Populated when the transaction is priced on a weight basis.']
  price_per_head decimal(12,2) [note: 'Purchase or sale price expressed on a per-head basis in USD. Populated when the transaction is priced per individual animal rather than per CWT. Mutually exclusive with price_per_cwt for a given transaction; one or both may be populated depending on transaction channel.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM purchase order number associated with this livestock purchase transaction. Populated for inbound (purchase) transactions only. Enables three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) in accounts payable processing.']
  quarantine_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the purchased animals must remain in quarantine isolation before integration into the main herd or flock. Populated only for purchase transactions where quarantine_required = True. Supports antibiotic withdrawal period tracking and biosecurity compliance.']
  quarantine_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the purchased animals are subject to a mandatory quarantine period upon arrival at the receiving premises. Applicable to purchase transactions only. Drives receiving pen assignment and health monitoring workflows.']
  rfid_tag_range varchar [note: 'Range or list of RFID electronic identification tag numbers (e.g., 840 USDA official ear tags) assigned to the animals in this transaction. Supports APHIS electronic animal identification traceability requirements and individual animal tracking.']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species classification of the animals involved in the transaction (e.g., bovine for cattle, swine for hogs, ovine for sheep). Drives regulatory reporting requirements, withdrawal period tracking, and FSIS/APHIS compliance rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|swine|ovine|caprine|equine|poultry|other — promote to reference product]']
  total_transaction_value decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross total monetary value of the livestock transfer in USD, representing the full purchase cost (for inbound) or gross sale revenue (for outbound). Used for cost basis accounting, revenue recognition, and enterprise profitability analysis in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  total_weight_lbs decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate live weight in pounds of all animals in the transaction at the time of transfer (purchase weight for inbound; sale weight for outbound). Used for CWT (hundredweight) pricing calculations, cost basis accounting, and yield analysis.']
  transaction_channel varchar [note: 'The commercial channel through which the livestock transfer was executed. Direct = farm-to-farm; Auction = physical livestock auction market; Video Auction = remote video-based auction (e.g., Superior Livestock); Formula = price tied to a market index or formula; Private Treaty = negotiated private sale.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The principal real-world business event date on which the livestock transfer (purchase or sale) was executed — i.e., the date of ownership transfer or market transaction. Distinct from transport or arrival dates.']
  transaction_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents an inbound livestock purchase (animals acquired from an external party) or an outbound livestock sale (animals transferred to an external buyer). Drives which conditional attributes are populated (e.g., quarantine period for purchases; grade/yield basis for sales).']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the livestock transfer event, typically sourced from SAP S/4HANA (purchase order number for inbound or sales order number for outbound) or the auction/market reference number. Used for cross-system reconciliation and counterparty communication.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the livestock transfer event. Tracks the lifecycle from initial draft through confirmation, transit, completion, or cancellation. Drives downstream accounting and traceability processes.']
  transport_carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed livestock transport carrier responsible for moving the animals between premises. Required for APHIS traceability, FMCSA compliance, and freight cost allocation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this animal transaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all livestock transfer events — both purchases (inbound) and sales (outbound) — between the operation and external parties. Captures transaction date, transaction direction (purchase/sale), counterparty premises ID (vendor for purchases, buyer for sales), species, breed, head count, weight (purchase weight or sale weight), price per head or per CWT, total transaction value, transaction channel (direct, auction, video auction, formula), origin state/country, health certificate number, brand inspection certificate, import permit (if applicable for purchases), quarantine period required (for purchases), receiving pen assignment (for purchases), transport carrier, BOL reference, grade and yield basis (for sales), and COOL chain-of-custody documentation. Purchase-specific attributes (quarantine period, receiving pen, import permit) are populated only for purchase transactions. Sale-specific attributes (grade/yield basis, sale channel) are populated only for sale transactions. Serves as the consolidated SSOT for all livestock commercial transfers. Supports livestock cost basis accounting, revenue recognition, enterprise profitability analysis, COOL compliance, and APHIS traceability. Distinguished from procurement/sales domain records by being animal-specific with livestock traceability attributes (health certificates, brand inspections, premises IDs) that generic commercial transaction records do not carry.'
}

Table livestock.milk_production_record {
  milk_production_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each milk production record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete milking event or Dairy Herd Improvement Association (DHIA) test-day observation for an individual animal or group.']
  animal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual dairy cow or animal record in the livestock animal master. Serves as the primary party reference linking milk yield to a specific animal\'s lifecycle record. Supports DHIA enrollment and individual cow performance tracking.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the milking parlor, milking robot (automatic milking system), or milking unit where the event occurred. Supports parlor throughput analysis, equipment performance benchmarking (OEE), and robotic milking interval compliance tracking.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Milk is a traded commodity (fluid milk, cream, cheese milk) with USDA Federal Order pricing, MRL thresholds, and organic premium classifications. Linking milk_production_record to product.commodity en']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Dairy quality management and FDA PMO compliance require linking each milk production record to the bulk tank commodity lot. Enables somatic cell count and antibiotic residue traceability per lot, supp']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Dairy operations sell milk under long-term supply contracts with processors or cooperatives. Linking milk production records to the governing sales contract enables contract volume tracking, milk chec']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Dairy enterprise P&L reporting requires milk production attributed to cost centers for milk revenue recognition and cost-per-hundredweight analysis. DHIA and farm management software universally link ']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Milking parlor operations are crew-based. milk_production_record has no crew_id. The milking crew responsible for each session is tracked for labor cost allocation, food safety accountability (SCC iss']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: Milk pickups by tanker trucks are executed as delivery orders. Linking milk production records to delivery orders enables traceability from production batch to delivery fulfillment, required for FSMA ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Individual milker accountability is required in dairy operations for food safety traceability — somatic cell count anomalies and mastitis events are traced to specific milkers. No employee_id exists o']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the herd or dairy enterprise group to which the animal belongs at the time of milking. Enables herd-level aggregation of milk production performance and supports enterprise benchmarking.']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: DHIA test-day samples (dhia_test_day_flag) and bulk tank samples are formal lab samples. Linking milk_production_record to lab_sample supports milk quality traceability for PMO compliance, DHIA report']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Milk lot traceability from individual cow production records to bulk tank to processor shipment is required by PMO (Pasteurized Milk Ordinance) and FSMA. Linking milk_production_record to lot_trace en']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Abnormal milk (is_abnormal_milk), somatic cell count exceedances, and diverted milk (divert_reason) trigger nonconformance records under PMO Grade A milk regulations. Dairy quality managers require th']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: milk_production_record captures milking events at specific parlor/pen locations. The parlor_id field exists as a BIGINT but has no FK assignment. Adding pen_location_id → livestock.pen_location.pen_lo']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Dairy enterprise revenue recognition requires milk production attributed to profit centers for dairy P&L reporting. Milk revenue is the primary income stream in dairy operations; profit center attribu']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Milk production batches are sold via sales orders to processors or cooperatives. Linking production records to sales orders enables volume reconciliation, revenue attribution per milking session, and ']
  settlement_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.settlement_statement. Business justification: Dairy cooperatives and processors issue monthly milk settlement statements based on production records. Linking milk_production_record to settlement_statement enables producer payment reconciliation, ']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Dairy milking operations run on defined shifts (AM/PM/overnight). Linking milk records to shift enables shift-level yield reporting, labor cost per hundredweight by shift, and quality traceability (SC']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Milk is collected and shipped via bulk tanker shipments to processors. Linking milk_production_record to shipment enables farm-to-processor traceability for Grade A milk program compliance, somatic ce']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Milk collection operations require direct reference to the bulk tank or storage location where milk is deposited. Supports DHIA test-day reporting, PMO regulatory compliance, milk quality management b']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: The milk tanker truck used for each pickup is tracked for FDA Grade A Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO) compliance — tanker cleaning records, temperature logs, and somatic cell count investigations req']
  conductivity_ms_cm decimal(6,3) [note: 'Electrical conductivity (EC) of the milk sample measured in millisiemens per centimeter (mS/cm). Elevated EC is an early indicator of subclinical mastitis and udder inflammation. Captured by inline sensors in automatic milking systems and modern parlor equipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milk production record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Audit trail field supporting FSMA record-keeping requirements, SOX data governance controls, and DHIA data submission audit logs. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this milk production record was ingested. Supports data lineage, silver layer reconciliation, and source system audit trails. Values align with the enterprise\'s operational systems: Climate FieldView, Granular Farm Management, DHIA testing service, parlor management system, SAP S/4HANA, or manual entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CLIMATE_FIELDVIEW|GRANULAR|DHIA_SERVICE|PARLOR_MGMT|MANUAL|SAP_S4HANA|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  days_in_milk varchar [note: 'Number of days elapsed since the animal\'s most recent calving event at the time of this milking record. DIM (Days in Milk) is the primary axis for lactation curve modeling, peak milk prediction, and dry-off scheduling. Calculated from calving date but stored as a business-critical operational field.']
  dhia_test_day_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is an official DHIA (Dairy Herd Improvement Association) test-day observation conducted by a certified DHIA supervisor. True = official DHIA test-day; False = routine parlor or non-official record. Determines eligibility for DHIA genetic evaluation and official production records.']
  divert_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for diverting milk from the bulk tank when is_abnormal_milk is True. COLOSTRUM: first-milking colostrum; ANTIBIOTIC_WITHDRAWAL: animal within withdrawal period (PHI — Pre-Harvest Interval); MASTITIS: clinical mastitis; BLOOD_TINGED: visible blood contamination; DRUG_TREATMENT: other veterinary drug treatment; NONE: not diverted. Supports antibiotic residue compliance and FSIS regulatory reporting.']
  fat_pct decimal(5,3) [note: 'Percentage of butterfat content in the milk sample, measured via DHIA test-day analysis or inline sensor. Used for component-based milk pricing (fat premium/deduction), breed performance benchmarking, and nutritional ration adjustment. Regulatory threshold compliance per FDA Grade A Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO).']
  fat_yield_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of butterfat produced in this milking event, calculated as milk_yield_kg × fat_pct / 100. Stored as a business field for DHIA component reporting, Federal Milk Marketing Order (FMMO) component pricing, and dairy enterprise financial reconciliation.']
  is_abnormal_milk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milk from this event was classified as abnormal (e.g., colostrum, mastitic milk, antibiotic-treated milk, blood-tinged milk) and therefore diverted from the bulk tank. True = abnormal/diverted; False = normal saleable milk. Critical for milk quality compliance and antibiotic residue tracking under FSIS and FDA PMO.']
  lactation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of the animal\'s completed and current lactations (parity). First-calf heifers are parity 1. Used for stratified performance benchmarking, culling decision support, and genetic evaluation. Aligns with parity_number in the animal master record.']
  lactose_pct decimal(5,3) [note: 'Percentage of lactose in the milk sample. Elevated lactose deviation can indicate early-stage mastitis or metabolic disorders. Used in conjunction with SCC and MUN for udder health monitoring and milk quality compliance.']
  linear_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Logarithmic transformation of somatic cell count expressed on a 0–9 linear scale, as defined by DHIA. Provides a normalized, statistically stable measure of udder health for genetic evaluation, herd benchmarking, and DHIA enrollment reporting.']
  milk_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature of the milk at the point of collection, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for milk quality compliance (FDA PMO requires rapid cooling to ≤10°C within 4 hours of milking), cold chain monitoring, and detection of fever-related health events in the animal.']
  milk_yield_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of milk produced by the animal during this milking event, expressed in kilograms. Primary quantitative outcome of the milking transaction. Used for lactation curve modeling, DHIA test-day reporting, and dairy enterprise revenue estimation.']
  milk_yield_lbs decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of milk produced during this milking event expressed in pounds (lbs). Retained alongside kg for compatibility with USDA NASS reporting, DHIA test-day records, and domestic dairy market pricing conventions (hundredweight/CWT basis).']
  milking_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the milking event or DHIA test-day observation occurred. Principal business event date used for daily, monthly, and lactation-period reporting. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.']
  milking_duration_sec varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds for the milking event from unit attachment to detachment. Used for parlor throughput optimization, teat condition monitoring, and robotic milking system performance analysis (OEE — Overall Equipment Effectiveness).']
  milking_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this milking event or test-day record, typically assigned by the parlor management system or DHIA testing service (e.g., DHIA test-day report number, parlor event ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation with source systems such as Climate FieldView or Granular Farm Management.']
  milking_session varchar [note: 'Identifies the milking session within the day: AM (morning), PM (evening), MID (midday for 3x programs), or labeled 3X variants for three-times-daily milking schedules. Critical for normalizing yield comparisons across different milking frequency programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AM|PM|MID|3X_AM|3X_PM|3X_MID — promote to reference product]']
  milking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the milking event was recorded, including timezone offset. Enables intra-day sequencing of milking sessions, parlor throughput analysis, and robotic milking interval tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mun_mg_dl decimal(6,2) [note: 'Concentration of urea nitrogen in milk expressed in milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL). MUN (Milk Urea Nitrogen) is an indicator of dietary protein-energy balance and rumen fermentation efficiency. Used for ration formulation adjustments and environmental nitrogen management compliance.']
  peak_flow_rate_kg_min decimal(6,3) [note: 'Maximum milk flow rate recorded during the milking event, expressed in kilograms per minute. Indicator of teat canal health, milking unit vacuum performance, and potential mastitis risk. Captured by parlor management systems and robotic milking units.']
  protein_pct decimal(5,3) [note: 'Percentage of true protein content in the milk sample. Used for component-based milk pricing (protein premium/deduction), cheese yield estimation, and feed ration optimization. Key performance indicator for dairy enterprise profitability.']
  protein_yield_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Absolute quantity of true protein produced in this milking event, calculated as milk_yield_kg × protein_pct / 100. Stored for DHIA component reporting, FMMO protein pricing, and cheese yield estimation in downstream processing.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this milk production record. ACTIVE: valid and in use; VOIDED: invalidated due to error or equipment malfunction; CORRECTED: superseded by a corrected record; PENDING_REVIEW: flagged for quality review; DHIA_SUBMITTED: officially submitted to DHIA testing service. Supports data governance and audit trail requirements.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source and method of this milk production record. DHIA_TEST_DAY: official DHIA supervisor test; DAILY_PARLOR: routine parlor management system record; ROBOT_AMS: automatic milking system (robotic) record; MANUAL_ENTRY: hand-entered observation; ESTIMATED: interpolated or estimated value. Drives data quality weighting in analytics and DHIA enrollment eligibility.']
  somatic_cell_count varchar [note: 'Number of somatic cells per milliliter of milk (cells/mL). Primary indicator of udder health and mastitis status. Regulatory threshold: FDA PMO Grade A standard ≤750,000 cells/mL; EU standard ≤400,000 cells/mL. Directly impacts milk quality premiums/penalties and bulk tank SCC compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milk production record was last modified in the silver layer lakehouse. Supports change data capture (CDC), correction audit trails, and data governance compliance. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  withdrawal_clear_date date [note: 'Date on which the animal\'s antibiotic or drug withdrawal period expires and milk is eligible to re-enter the bulk tank. Populated when divert_reason is ANTIBIOTIC_WITHDRAWAL or DRUG_TREATMENT. Critical for FSIS antibiotic residue compliance and FDA PMO Grade A milk eligibility. Aligns with withdrawal_clear_date in the animal master.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of milk yield measurements for individual dairy cows or groups, captured at each milking event or DHIA test-day. Captures milking date, animal ID, milking session (AM/PM/3x), milk yield (lbs/kg), milk fat percentage, milk protein percentage, somatic cell count (SCC), linear score, milk urea nitrogen (MUN), days in milk (DIM), lactation number, and milking parlor/robot ID. Supports DHIA enrollment, milk quality compliance, and dairy enterprise performance management.'
}

Table livestock.health_protocol {
  health_protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the herd health protocol record. Primary key for the health_protocol data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Health protocols define treatment programs with standard costs (drug, labor) that are pre-budgeted to cost centers. Linking protocols to cost centers enables protocol-level budget planning and actual-']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed veterinarian who authored and approved this health protocol. Required for VCPR (Veterinarian-Client-Patient Relationship) compliance under FDA CVM and APHIS accreditation requirements.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Health protocols are scoped to specific facilities for biosecurity zone compliance and USDA/FDA regulatory audits. health_protocol.facility_scope is a denormalized text reference; a proper FK to facil']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Health protocols specify approved drugs and biologicals that must be in the procurement input catalog. Linking health_protocol to input_catalog enforces approved product list management, ensures proto']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Health protocol formulary management requires linking each protocol to the specific drug/vaccine material_master. Enables withdrawal period calculation, VFD compliance verification, antibiotic steward']
  administration_route varchar [note: 'The prescribed route of drug administration as specified on the FDA-approved label (e.g., subcutaneous injection, intramuscular injection, oral drench, pour-on). Deviations from the approved route constitute extra-label drug use (ELDU) requiring a valid VCPR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: subcutaneous|intramuscular|intravenous|oral|intranasal|topical|pour_on — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  antibiotic_class varchar [note: 'Pharmacological class of the antibiotic used in this protocol (e.g., macrolide, tetracycline, beta-lactam). Required for antibiotic stewardship reporting, resistance monitoring, and FDA GFI #213 medically important antimicrobial tracking. Null or not_applicable for non-antibiotic protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: macrolide|tetracycline|beta_lactam|fluoroquinolone|sulfonamide|aminoglycoside|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol was formally approved and signed by the responsible veterinarian. Establishes the VCPR-compliant authorization date for regulatory audit purposes under APHIS, FSIS, and FSMA requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health protocol record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX/FSMA record-keeping compliance requirements.']
  dosage_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The prescribed dose quantity of the drug product per unit of animal body weight or per head as specified in the protocol. Used in conjunction with dosage_uom to calculate total drug volume for treatment events.']
  dosage_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the prescribed dosage amount (e.g., ml per hundredweight (CWT), ml per head, mg per kg body weight). Ensures accurate dose calculation and label compliance per FDA NADA/ANADA approved labeling.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this health protocol becomes operationally effective and may be applied to animals. May differ from approved_date if a transition period is required. Drives protocol applicability checks in treatment and vaccination records.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this health protocol expires and must be reviewed and re-approved by the veterinarian. Null for protocols with no defined expiry. Supports VCPR annual review requirements and GLOBALG.A.P. protocol currency compliance.']
  injection_site varchar [note: 'Anatomical location for injection administration as specified by BQA guidelines to minimize carcass damage and maximize meat quality. Neck injection is the BQA-preferred site for cattle. Not applicable for non-injectable routes.']
  is_antibiotic_protocol boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protocol involves the use of medically important antimicrobials (antibiotics). True triggers mandatory antibiotic stewardship tracking, VFD requirements where applicable, and antibiotic-free program compliance checks per FDA GFI #213 and GFI #209.']
  is_extra_label_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this protocol involves extra-label drug use (ELDU) — use of an FDA-approved drug in a manner not specified on the approved label (different species, dose, route, or indication). ELDU requires a valid VCPR and veterinarian authorization per 21 CFR Part 530.']
  is_prescription_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the drug product in this protocol requires a valid veterinary prescription (Rx) for procurement and use. Drives procurement compliance checks in SAP MM and vendor management workflows.']
  is_vfd_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) is required for the drug product used in this protocol. True for medically important antimicrobials administered via feed or water under 21 CFR Part 558. Drives VFD documentation workflow in SAP QM.']
  max_treatments_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this protocol may be applied to a single animal within a production cycle before escalation to a veterinarian or chronic/realizer designation. Supports antibiotic stewardship and chronic animal management decisions.']
  milk_withdrawal_days varchar [note: 'Mandatory milk discard period in days following drug administration for dairy animals, as specified on the FDA-approved label. Applicable only to dairy production classes; null for beef/non-dairy protocols. Required for Grade A Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO) compliance.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum time that must elapse between the last application of a pesticide or parasiticide and animal harvest. Distinct from drug withdrawal period; applies specifically to EPA-registered pesticide products used in parasite control protocols.']
  production_class varchar [note: 'The specific production class or stage within the species for which this protocol applies (e.g., feedlot cattle, cow-calf pairs, broiler flocks). Ensures protocol applicability is correctly scoped to avoid misapplication across production stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cow_calf|stocker|feedlot|dairy|breeding|farrow_to_finish|broiler|layer|pullet|gilt|sow|boar — promote to reference product]']
  protocol_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the health protocol, used for cross-referencing in treatment records, vaccination logs, and veterinary documentation. Serves as the business identifier referenced in FMIS and SAP QM.']
  protocol_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name of the health protocol (e.g., \'Arrival Processing BRD Metaphylaxis\', \'Spring Vaccination Schedule - Cow-Calf\'). Used in reporting, treatment records, and veterinary documentation.']
  protocol_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for veterinarian or protocol administrator notes, special instructions, contraindications, or clinical observations relevant to protocol application. Supports clinical decision-making and audit documentation.']
  protocol_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the health protocol. Draft indicates under development; active indicates approved and in use; suspended indicates temporarily halted pending review; retired indicates no longer in use; pending_review indicates awaiting veterinarian re-approval.']
  protocol_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the protocol by its operational purpose. Arrival processing covers new animal intake procedures; metaphylaxis covers mass antibiotic treatment at arrival; vaccination_schedule covers routine immunization programs; parasite_control covers deworming and ectoparasite management; treatment_guideline covers individual animal therapy; biosecurity covers disease prevention procedures.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours specifying the minimum time workers must wait before re-entering treated areas or handling treated animals, as required by EPA pesticide label regulations. Applicable primarily to parasite control and topical pesticide protocols.']
  retreatment_interval_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days that must elapse between successive applications of this protocol to the same animal. Prevents over-treatment, supports antibiotic stewardship, and ensures withdrawal period compliance between treatments.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled review interval in days for this protocol (e.g., 365 for annual review). Drives automated review reminders and protocol currency compliance tracking. Supports VCPR maintenance requirements and GLOBALG.A.P. audit readiness.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) associated with this health protocol. Links to the detailed procedural documentation in the document management system for training and audit purposes.']
  target_species varchar [note: 'The animal species for which this health protocol is designed and approved. Drives species-specific drug dosage calculations, withdrawal period requirements, and APHIS/FSIS regulatory compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|swine|poultry|ovine|caprine|equine|mixed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trigger_morbidity_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Pen or herd morbidity rate percentage at or above which this metaphylaxis or mass treatment protocol is triggered (e.g., 10% pen pull rate triggers mass treatment). Null if trigger_type is not morbidity_rate. Supports evidence-based metaphylaxis decision-making.']
  trigger_temperature_threshold_c decimal(4,1) [note: 'Rectal temperature threshold in degrees Celsius at or above which this protocol is triggered for an individual animal (e.g., 39.7°C for BRD treatment initiation). Null if trigger_type is not temperature_threshold. Used by pen riders and veterinary staff to standardize pull decisions.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'The type of trigger criterion that initiates application of this protocol. Temperature threshold triggers are based on rectal temperature readings; morbidity_rate triggers are based on pen pull rates; arrival_processing triggers apply automatically at animal intake; scheduled triggers follow a calendar-based vaccination schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health protocol record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping requirements under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.']
  withdrawal_period_days varchar [note: 'The mandatory withdrawal period in days from last drug administration to slaughter or milk harvest, as specified on the FDA-approved label. Critical for FSIS residue compliance, MRL adherence, and antibiotic-free program certification. Drives the withdrawal_clear_date calculation on the animal record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining standardized herd health protocols and treatment guidelines established by the operation\'s veterinarian. Captures protocol name, target species and production class, protocol type (arrival processing, metaphylaxis, vaccination schedule, parasite control), drug products authorized, dosages, administration routes, withdrawal periods, trigger criteria (temperature threshold, morbidity rate), and veterinarian approval date. Serves as the SSOT for standardized health management procedures referenced by treatment and vaccination records.'
}

Table livestock.ration_ingredient {
  ration_ingredient_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ration_ingredient association']
  feed_ration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the feed ration formulation that contains this ingredient']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Feed ingredients must be in the approved procurement input catalog with GMO status, organic certification, and regulatory compliance data. Linking ration_ingredient to input_catalog enforces approved ']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Individual feed ingredients (corn, soybean meal, distillers grains) are sampled and tested for nutritional content, mycotoxins, and contaminants before inclusion in rations. FDA FSMA Preventive Contro']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the material master record representing the feed ingredient']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Each feed ration ingredient is sourced under a vendor supply contract with price and volume commitments. Linking ration_ingredient to vendor_contract enables contract-based ration cost modeling, suppl']
  cost_per_unit decimal [note: 'The cost of this ingredient per unit of measure at the time this ration formulation was created or last revised. Captured at the ration-ingredient level to support feed cost allocation and historical cost tracking independent of current material_master standard_price.']
  dry_matter_basis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inclusion_rate_pct for this ingredient in this ration is expressed on a dry matter (DM) basis (true) or as-fed basis (false). The same ingredient may be expressed differently across rations depending on nutritionist preference.']
  inclusion_rate_pct decimal [note: 'The percentage of this ingredient in the ration, expressed as a proportion of the total ration weight. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination — the same ingredient may have different inclusion rates across different rations.']
  ingredient_list varchar [note: 'Structured text or JSON-serialized list of feed ingredients included in the ration formulation, with inclusion rates on both as-fed and dry matter basis (e.g., \'corn silage 45%, distillers grains 15%, hay 20%\'). Applicable to formulation records. Supports feed cost analysis and ingredient traceability. [Moved from feed_ration: The ingredient_list STRING/JSON blob on feed_ration is a denormalized encoding of the ration-ingredient M:N relationship. Once the ration_ingredient association product is in place, this attribute becomes redundant and should be deprecated. The structured ration_ingredient records replace it with properly typed, queryable, and auditable individual ingredient rows.]']
  ingredient_sequence varchar [note: 'The order in which this ingredient is added to the TMR mixer or batch during ration preparation. Critical for mixing quality and nutritional homogeneity. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination, not to the ingredient or ration alone.']
  quantity_per_batch_kg decimal [note: 'The physical weight in kilograms of this ingredient required per batch mix of the ration. Derived from inclusion rate and batch size but stored explicitly for feed mill operations. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination.']
  substitution_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ingredient in this specific ration may be substituted with an alternative ingredient when the primary ingredient is unavailable. A ration-ingredient-level flag — the same ingredient may be substitutable in one ration but not in another.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Ration Ingredient role between feed_ration and material_master. It captures the bill-of-materials composition of each feed ration formulation — specifically, which ingredients (material_master records) are included in a given ration, at what inclusion rate, in what quantity per batch, and at what cost. Each record links one feed_ration formulation to one material_master ingredient with attributes that exist only in the context of that specific ration-ingredient combination. Serves as the SSOT for ration composition, supporting NRC nutritional compliance calculations, feed cost allocation, and feed mill mixing operations.. Existence Justification: A feed ration formulation is composed of multiple feed ingredients (material_master records such as corn silage, soybean meal, mineral premix), and each ingredient appears across many different ration formulations. Nutritionists and feed mill operators actively manage this composition daily — creating, adjusting, and versioning ingredient inclusions with specific rates, quantities, and costs per ration-ingredient combination. This is a classic bill-of-materials / recipe composition relationship that the livestock feed industry universally recognizes as \'ration ingredients\' or \'feed formulation lines\'.'
}

Table livestock.breed {
  breed_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for breed']
  parent_breed_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on breed (parent_breed_id)']
  average_birth_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Typical birth weight in kilograms for offspring of this breed, used for calving/farrowing management and health monitoring.']
  average_daily_gain_kg decimal(6,3) [note: 'Expected daily weight gain in kilograms during the growth phase, used for performance benchmarking and feed efficiency calculations.']
  average_litter_size decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of offspring per birth event for the breed, used for production planning and facility capacity management.']
  average_mature_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical adult body weight in kilograms for the breed at maturity, used for feed planning and facility design.']
  breed_category varchar [note: 'Primary production purpose classification of the breed, indicating its intended agricultural use and economic function.']
  breed_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the breed across systems and external registries. Used for regulatory reporting and industry data exchange.']
  breed_name varchar [note: 'Official common name of the breed as recognized by breed associations and industry standards.']
  breed_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the breed indicating its availability and conservation state within breeding programs.']
  breeding_season varchar [note: 'Natural or managed breeding cycle pattern for the breed, indicating optimal reproductive timing for breeding program management.']
  carcass_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected dressed carcass weight as a percentage of live weight for meat breeds, used for harvest planning and economic valuation.']
  climate_adaptability varchar [note: 'Environmental climate conditions to which the breed is best suited, used for facility design and geographic placement decisions.']
  coat_color_pattern varchar [note: 'Characteristic coat color and pattern description for the breed, used for visual identification and breed verification.']
  conservation_priority varchar [note: 'Conservation status classification indicating the urgency of genetic preservation efforts for the breed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this breed record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crossbreeding_compatibility varchar [note: 'Notes on breeds commonly crossed with this breed and expected hybrid vigor outcomes, used for breeding program design.']
  disease_resistance_profile varchar [note: 'Summary of known genetic resistance or susceptibility to common diseases, used for health management and breeding selection.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this breed record became active and available for use in breeding programs and animal registration.']
  egg_production_potential_per_year varchar [note: 'Average annual egg production count for poultry layer breeds, used for production forecasting and flock management. Null for non-poultry breeds.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this breed record was retired or became inactive. Null for currently active breeds.']
  feed_conversion_ratio decimal(6,3) [note: 'Average feed conversion efficiency expressed as kilograms of feed required per kilogram of weight gain, used for economic analysis and ration planning.']
  genetic_marker_profile varchar [note: 'Summary of key genetic markers or DNA characteristics used for breed verification and parentage validation in breeding programs.']
  gestation_period_days varchar [note: 'Average length of pregnancy in days for the breed, used for breeding program scheduling and calving/farrowing date estimation.']
  is_certified_organic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breed meets eligibility criteria for certified organic production programs.']
  is_heritage_breed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the breed is classified as a heritage or traditional breed eligible for conservation programs and specialty marketing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this breed record was most recently updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  market_age_target_days varchar [note: 'Typical age in days at which animals of this breed reach market readiness, used for production cycle planning and facility turnover scheduling.']
  market_weight_target_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Optimal live weight in kilograms at which animals of this breed are typically marketed or harvested, used for production scheduling and economic planning.']
  milk_production_potential_kg_per_year decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average annual milk yield in kilograms for dairy breeds, used for production forecasting and economic analysis. Null for non-dairy breeds.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the breed, including historical context, special considerations, or operational guidance.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic origin or development location of the breed.']
  physical_characteristics varchar [note: 'Detailed description of distinguishing physical traits and conformation standards for the breed, used for breed verification and selection.']
  registry_association_name varchar [note: 'Name of the official breed registry or association that maintains pedigree records and breed standards for this breed.']
  regulatory_restrictions varchar [note: 'Any import/export restrictions, quarantine requirements, or regulatory limitations applicable to this breed, used for compliance and trade planning.']
  scientific_name varchar [note: 'Taxonomic scientific name of the breed, including genus and species classification.']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Any unique care, housing, or management requirements specific to this breed, used for facility design and staff training.']
  species_type varchar [note: 'Primary species classification of the breed, categorizing the animal type for operational and regulatory purposes.']
  temperament varchar [note: 'Typical behavioral disposition of the breed, used for handler safety planning and animal welfare management.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for breed. Referenced by breed_id.'
}

Table land.field {
  field_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each agricultural field or paddock record in the land domain. Primary key for all field-level spatial identity, boundary history, and precision management zone definitions.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Fields are designated for specific commodity production (corn, soybeans, wheat). Field-level commodity designation drives FSA acreage reporting, crop insurance unit assignment, rotation planning, and ']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent farm or agricultural operation to which this field belongs. Supports farm-level aggregation of field data for reporting and compliance.']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: NOP organic certification is issued at the field/operation level. Field-level organic certification FK enables compliance tracking, certifying agent audit scheduling, transition period management, and']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Fields are planted with specific seed varieties each season. FMIS seed placement records, yield benchmarking by variety, and VRA seeding prescriptions all require knowing which seed variety is assigne']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Field-to-territory assignment supports precision ag service delivery routing, territory-based agronomic service planning, and sales rep field visit scheduling. Ag sales operations experts expect field']
