  arcgis_feature_code varchar [note: 'Integration key referencing the corresponding feature object ID in the Esri ArcGIS geodatabase. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the authoritative GIS system of record.']
  aspect_direction varchar [note: 'Predominant compass direction that the field slope faces, derived from DEM analysis in Esri ArcGIS. Influences solar radiation exposure, frost pocket risk, and crop variety selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: N|NE|E|SE|S|SW|W|NW|flat — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  boundary_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current boundary version is considered authoritative and operationally active. Used for temporal field geometry queries and FSA acreage compliance reporting.']
  boundary_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON-encoded polygon or multipolygon representing the RTK-accurate field boundary geometry sourced from Esri ArcGIS. Stores the current active boundary version. Supports FSA compliance boundary reporting and precision VRA zone delineation.']
  boundary_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number of the field boundary geometry, supporting temporal boundary change tracking for FSA compliance and historical spatial analysis. Incremented each time the boundary is updated in ArcGIS.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the field centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84), derived from RTK-accurate boundary geometry. Used for weather data integration, GIS spatial queries, and precision agriculture applications.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the field centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84), derived from RTK-accurate boundary geometry. Used for weather data integration, GIS spatial queries, and precision agriculture applications.']
  conservation_practice_code varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS conservation practice standard code (e.g., 340 Cover Crop, 412 Grassed Waterway) applied to the field. Supports BMP (Best Management Practices) compliance tracking, EQIP program reporting, and ISO 14001 environmental management.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which the field is located. Supports international operations, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, and multi-jurisdiction regulatory reporting.']
  county_name varchar [note: 'Name of the county or administrative district in which the field is located. Used for USDA FSA county office routing, state regulatory reporting, and geographic aggregation in analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the field record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and FSMA traceability compliance.']
  drainage_class varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS standard drainage class describing the natural drainage condition of the field\'s dominant soil. Drives tile drainage planning, irrigation scheduling, and BMP selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excessively_drained|well_drained|moderately_well_drained|somewhat_poorly_drained|poorly_drained|very_poorly_drained — promote to reference product]']
  elevation_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean elevation of the field above sea level in metres, derived from digital elevation model (DEM) data in Esri ArcGIS. Used for frost risk assessment, drainage planning, and microclimate modelling.']
  field_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the field, used in operational systems such as John Deere Operations Center, Trimble Ag Software, and SAP S/4HANA PP module for field-level planning and task assignment.']
  field_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the field or paddock by the farm operator, used for identification in farm management information systems (FMIS), field maps, and operational communications.']
  field_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the field. \'active\' indicates the field is in production; \'fallow\' indicates a rest period; \'retired\' indicates the field is no longer in use; \'quarantine\' indicates a regulatory hold; \'conservation\' indicates enrollment in a conservation program such as CRP.']
  field_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the field by its primary agricultural use type. Drives applicable crop planning, irrigation, and compliance workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cropland|pasture|orchard|vineyard|greenhouse|paddock|wetland|woodland — promote to reference product]']
  fsa_field_number varchar [note: 'USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) assigned field number within the tract, uniquely identifying the field for federal acreage reporting, crop insurance, and USDA program compliance.']
  fsa_tract_number varchar [note: 'USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) assigned tract number for the field, used for federal program enrollment, acreage reporting, and crop insurance compliance. Required for USDA commodity program participation.']
  gmo_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field is designated as GMO-restricted, prohibiting the planting of genetically modified organism (GMO) seed varieties. Supports identity preservation (IP) programs and non-GMO supply chain segregation.']
  irrigated_area_ha decimal(12,4) [note: 'Area of the field that is equipped and actively managed for irrigation, measured in hectares. Used for water resource management planning, evapotranspiration (ET) calculations, and irrigation scheduling.']
  irrigation_system_type varchar [note: 'Type of irrigation infrastructure installed on the field. Drives irrigation scheduling, water use efficiency calculations, and evapotranspiration (ET)-based management in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView.']
  jd_ops_field_code varchar [note: 'External integration key referencing the field record in John Deere Operations Center (precision agriculture and fleet telematics platform). Enables cross-system field-level data linkage for planting, spraying, and harvest operations.']
  land_use_code varchar [note: 'Standardized land use classification code assigned to the field per USDA NASS or local regulatory authority (e.g., USDA CLU land use code). Used for regulatory reporting, conservation program eligibility, and GIS land use mapping.']
  lease_rate_per_ha decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual cash rent or equivalent lease cost per hectare for this field, used in land cost budgeting, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) allocation, and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis in Granular Farm Management and SAP CO.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the land tenure arrangement for this field. Drives financial planning in Granular Farm Management, land cost allocation in SAP CO, and lease obligation reporting under IFRS 16.']
  ndvi_last_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Most recent NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) score for the field, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0, derived from satellite or drone imagery. Used for crop health monitoring, yield forecasting, and VRA prescription generation.']
  ndvi_observation_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NDVI score was captured via satellite or drone imagery. Used to assess the currency of vegetation health data for crop scouting and agronomic decision-making.']
  slope_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average slope of the field expressed as a percentage (rise over run × 100). Used for erosion risk assessment, irrigation design, machinery safety planning, and BMP (Best Management Practices) compliance.']
  soil_series_name varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS official soil series name for the dominant soil type in the field (e.g., \'Drummer\', \'Tama\', \'Clarion\'). Used for soil health benchmarking, input rate calibration, and agronomic planning.']
  soil_texture_class varchar [note: 'USDA soil texture classification for the dominant topsoil horizon, describing the relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay. Influences water-holding capacity, tillage timing, and fertilizer application rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|loamy_sand|sandy_loam|loam|silt_loam|silt|clay_loam|silty_clay_loam|sandy_clay|silty_clay|clay — promote to reference product]']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'Two- or three-letter ISO 3166-2 state or province code where the field is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction routing, state-level compliance reporting, and geographic segmentation.']
  total_area_ha decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total gross area of the field measured in hectares, derived from RTK-accurate GIS boundary geometry in Esri ArcGIS. Used for yield-per-acre metric (YPM) calculations, input rate planning, and FSA acreage reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the field record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and audit trail in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  vra_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of sub-field management zones delineated within this field for variable rate application (VRA) prescriptions, based on EC (Electrical Conductivity), yield history, and NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) data. Supports precision agriculture planning in Trimble Ag Software.']

  Note: 'Master record for each agricultural field or paddock — the foundational spatial and operational entity of the land domain. Captures RTK-accurate GIS boundary geometry (polygon/multipolygon from Esri ArcGIS) with versioned boundary history, field name, code, total area, irrigated area, GPS centroid, elevation, slope, aspect, drainage class, status (active/fallow/retired), sub-field management zones (VRA zones delineated by EC/yield/NDVI), and integration keys to John Deere Operations Center and ArcGIS feature IDs. Supports boundary change tracking for FSA compliance and temporal field geometry. SSOT for all field-level spatial identity, boundary history, and precision management zone definitions.'
}

Table land.parcel {
  parcel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the legal land parcel record in the enterprise data platform (Silver Layer primary key).']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational farm unit to which this parcel is administratively assigned for crop planning, workforce, and equipment management.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total purchase price or capitalized cost at which the enterprise acquired fee-simple title to the parcel, recorded in USD for fixed asset accounting, CAPEX tracking, and land valuation analysis.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the enterprise acquired operational control of the parcel (purchase closing date for owned parcels, lease commencement date for leased parcels), used for depreciation schedules, lease accounting (ASC 842), and tenure analysis.']
  annual_property_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Most recent annual property tax levy assessed by the county on this parcel in USD, used for OPEX budgeting, land cost-per-acre analysis, and financial planning in Granular Farm Management.']
  apn varchar [note: 'County assessor-assigned Assessor Parcel Number (APN) uniquely identifying the parcel within the county tax roll. Primary government-issued cadastral identifier used for title searches, deed recording, and property tax assessment.']
  arcgis_layer_code varchar [note: 'Unique feature identifier within the Esri ArcGIS parcel boundary layer, enabling direct linkage to the authoritative GIS polygon geometry for spatial analysis, field mapping, and environmental monitoring.']
  assessed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'County assessor\'s most recent assessed market value of the parcel in USD, used for property tax calculation, financial reporting, and land portfolio valuation.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS84 decimal-degree latitude of the parcel centroid as derived from Esri ArcGIS or John Deere Operations Center RTK-accurate field mapping, used for geospatial analysis, weather data correlation, and GIS integration.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'WGS84 decimal-degree longitude of the parcel centroid as derived from Esri ArcGIS or John Deere Operations Center RTK-accurate field mapping, used for geospatial analysis, weather data correlation, and GIS integration.']
  clu_identifier varchar [note: 'USDA FSA Common Land Unit (CLU) identifier — the smallest unit of land with a permanent, contiguous boundary used for farm program administration, acreage determination, and geospatial analysis.']
  conservation_program_enrollment varchar [note: 'USDA conservation program in which this parcel is currently enrolled (e.g., Conservation Reserve Program (CRP), Environmental Quality Incentives Program (EQIP), Conservation Stewardship Program (CSP)), affecting land use restrictions and payment eligibility.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the parcel is located, supporting international operations and Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance.']
  county varchar [note: 'Name of the county in which the parcel is physically located, used for tax jurisdiction, FSA county office assignment, regulatory compliance, and geospatial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the parcel record was first created in the enterprise data platform, used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  deed_book varchar [note: 'County recorder deed book reference (book number or volume) where the current title instrument for this parcel is recorded, supporting title chain verification and encumbrance searches.']
  deed_page varchar [note: 'County recorder deed page reference within the deed book where the current title instrument is recorded, used in conjunction with deed_book for precise title document retrieval.']
  deed_recording_date date [note: 'Date the current deed or title instrument was officially recorded with the county recorder, establishing the effective date of ownership transfer and priority in the title chain.']
  flood_zone_designation varchar [note: 'FEMA National Flood Insurance Program (NFIP) flood zone designation for the parcel, used for crop insurance underwriting, lender requirements, conservation planning, and drainage infrastructure investment decisions.']
  fsa_cropland_acres decimal(12,4) [note: 'USDA FSA-determined cropland acres within the parcel eligible for commodity program enrollment, ARC/PLC payments, and conservation program base acre calculations.']
  fsa_farm_number varchar [note: 'USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) assigned farm number linking this parcel to the FSA farm record for program eligibility, commodity reporting, and conservation compliance.']
  fsa_tract_number varchar [note: 'USDA FSA tract number subdividing the FSA farm into contiguous land units under common ownership or control, used for acreage reporting and program payment calculations.']
  irrigated_acres decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total acres within the parcel served by an irrigation system (surface, drip, pivot, or flood), used for water right allocation, irrigation scheduling, and USDA program reporting.']
  land_use_designation varchar [note: 'Primary land use classification for the parcel as designated by the county assessor or USDA FSA, determining eligible farm programs, conservation requirements, and operational planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cropland|pasture|rangeland|woodland|wetland|farmstead|idle|conservation|orchard|vineyard|fallow — promote to reference product]']
  legal_description varchar [note: 'Full legal description of the parcel as recorded in the county deed or land registry, including metes-and-bounds, lot-block-subdivision, or Public Land Survey System (PLSS) section-township-range narrative.']
  nrcs_land_capability_class varchar [note: 'USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) land capability class (I–VIII) and subclass (e, w, s, c) rating the parcel\'s suitability for cultivation, indicating erosion risk, drainage limitations, and conservation treatment needs.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational control arrangement for this parcel — whether the enterprise holds fee-simple title (owned), operates under a cash rent lease, crop-share lease, or custom farming agreement.']
  parcel_name varchar [note: 'Operational or colloquial name assigned to the parcel by the farm operation (e.g., \'North Quarter\', \'River Bottom Field\') for internal identification and communication.']
  parcel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parcel record indicating its operational and administrative state within the enterprise land management system.']
  plss_range varchar [note: 'Range designation (e.g., R5E) within the PLSS grid, representing the east-west position relative to a principal meridian.']
  plss_section varchar [note: 'Section number (1–36) within the Public Land Survey System (PLSS) township-range grid identifying the one-square-mile land division containing this parcel.']
  plss_township varchar [note: 'Township designation (e.g., T12N) within the PLSS grid, representing the north-south position relative to a principal meridian baseline.']
  prime_farmland_class varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS prime farmland classification indicating the land\'s capability for sustained agricultural production, used for conservation program eligibility (ACEP, RCPP), environmental impact assessments, and land use planning.']
  soil_survey_map_unit varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS Web Soil Survey map unit symbol (e.g., \'HrA\', \'MnB2\') identifying the dominant soil series and slope class for the parcel, used for soil health planning, conservation program eligibility, and variable rate application (VRA) prescriptions.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the parcel is located, used for regulatory jurisdiction, FSA state office routing, and geospatial classification.']
  total_deeded_acres decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total land area of the parcel as recorded in the county deed or land registry, expressed in acres. Represents the legal boundary area and is the authoritative acreage for title, tax assessment, and FSA program enrollment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the parcel record in the enterprise data platform, supporting change data capture (CDC), audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.']
  wetland_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether any portion of the parcel contains USDA NRCS-delineated wetlands subject to Swampbuster provisions (Food Security Act), EPA Section 404 permitting, and conservation program restrictions.']
  zoning_classification varchar [note: 'County or municipal zoning designation for the parcel (e.g., A-1 Agricultural, A-2 Rural Residential, EFU Exclusive Farm Use) governing permitted land uses, structure placement, and development rights.']

  Note: 'Legal land parcel (cadastral unit) as defined by county assessor or land registry records. Stores APN (Assessor Parcel Number), legal description, section-township-range (PLSS), county, state, country, total deeded acres, zoning classification, land use designation, FSA farm/tract number, CLU (Common Land Unit) identifier, and USDA FSA administrative data. One parcel may contain multiple fields and belongs to a farm_unit for operational management. Links to land_ownership for title chain, to lease for tenant agreements, to easement for encumbrances, and to water_right for entitlement tracking. SSOT for legal land identity and government-assigned identifiers.'
}

Table land.ownership {
  ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each land ownership record in the system. Primary key for the land_ownership data product.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the land parcel that is subject to this ownership record. Links to the parcel master record in the land domain.']
  prior_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediately preceding ownership record in the chain of title for this parcel. Enables traversal of the full ownership history and chain-of-title lineage.']
  assessor_parcel_number varchar [note: 'The Assessor Parcel Number (APN) assigned by the county assessor\'s office for property tax assessment purposes. Also known as a parcel identification number (PIN) or tax parcel number. Used for cross-referencing with county tax records.']
  chain_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential integer indicating the position of this ownership record in the chain of title for the parcel. A value of 1 represents the earliest recorded ownership; higher values represent more recent ownership records. Supports chain-of-title history and ownership lineage analysis.']
  conservation_easement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parcel is subject to a conservation easement that restricts certain land uses in perpetuity or for a defined term. Conservation easements are commonly held by land trusts or government agencies under USDA NRCS programs.']
  conveyance_date date [note: 'The date on which the ownership interest was conveyed (transferred) from the prior owner to the current owner, as stated in the deed instrument. Distinct from recording_date (when filed) and effective_date (when legally binding).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which the parcel is located. Supports international operations and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.']
  county_name varchar [note: 'Name of the county in which the parcel is located and where the deed is recorded. Used for jurisdictional routing of legal documents and regulatory reporting to USDA FSA county offices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the data quality and verification status of this ownership record. \'Verified\' means the record has been validated against official county recorder or title company sources; \'unverified\' means the record has not yet been validated; \'under_review\' means validation is in progress; \'rejected\' means the record failed validation.']
  deed_book varchar [note: 'The deed book (volume) number in the county recorder\'s office where the deed instrument is officially recorded. Used in conjunction with deed_page for precise legal document retrieval.']
  deed_instrument_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal deed instrument used to convey ownership. Warranty deeds provide full title guarantees; quitclaim deeds convey only the grantor\'s existing interest; grant deeds include implied warranties; trustee and sheriff deeds arise from trust or court proceedings.']
  deed_page varchar [note: 'The page number within the deed book at the county recorder\'s office where the deed instrument is officially recorded. Used in conjunction with deed_book for precise legal document retrieval.']
  deed_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned to the deed instrument conveying ownership of this parcel. Used to cross-reference with county recorder and title company records for chain-of-title verification.']
  dispute_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature of any ownership dispute, legal challenge, or title defect associated with this ownership record. Populated when ownership_status is \'disputed\'. Supports legal team review and resolution tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the ownership interest legally became effective, which may differ from the recording date. For example, a deed may be executed and effective on one date but recorded later. This is the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM field for this ownership record.']
  encumbrance_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any encumbrances, liens, easements, mortgages, conservation easements, or other title restrictions affecting this ownership record. Populated when encumbrance_flag is True.']
  encumbrance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership record is subject to any encumbrances, liens, easements, mortgages, or other claims against the title that may affect the owner\'s full use and enjoyment of the parcel.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the ownership interest expires or terminates, applicable for term interests such as life estates or time-limited ownership arrangements. Null for perpetual fee simple ownership. This is the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL field.']
  farm_service_agency_tract_number varchar [note: 'The USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) tract number assigned to this parcel for farm program eligibility, commodity program enrollment, and conservation program participation tracking.']
  legal_description varchar [note: 'Full legal description of the parcel as recorded on the deed, typically expressed in metes and bounds, Public Land Survey System (PLSS) section-township-range notation, or lot-block-subdivision format. This is the authoritative legal boundary description for the parcel.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General free-text notes or remarks related to this ownership record, including special conditions, legal caveats, or administrative observations not captured in structured fields.']
  owner_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity type of the owner. Distinguishes between natural persons and legal entities for regulatory reporting, USDA farm program eligibility, and compliance with FSMA and COOL requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual|corporation|llc|partnership|trust|government|cooperative|nonprofit — promote to reference product]']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this ownership record. \'Current\' indicates active legal ownership; \'historical\' indicates a past ownership interest that has been transferred or extinguished; \'disputed\' flags records under legal challenge; \'pending\' indicates a transfer in progress; \'transferred\' indicates completed conveyance to a new owner.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Legal classification of the ownership interest held by the owner in this parcel. Fee simple is the most complete form of ownership; joint tenancy includes right of survivorship; tenancy in common allows fractional interests without survivorship; life estate is limited to the owner\'s lifetime; trust and LLC membership reflect entity-held interests.']
  percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'Fractional ownership interest held by this owner in the parcel, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.0000 for a 50% undivided interest). Supports multi-owner parcels with tenancy-in-common or LLC membership fractional interest tracking. All ownership records for a parcel should sum to 100%.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this ownership record, used for cross-referencing with county recorder offices, title companies, and legal documentation. Serves as the business identifier for chain-of-title tracking.']
  recording_date date [note: 'The date on which the deed or ownership instrument was officially recorded with the county recorder\'s office. Establishes the legal priority of the ownership claim in the chain of title.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this ownership record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage and master data management. Typical sources include Granular Farm Management, Esri ArcGIS, SAP S/4HANA, Oracle NetSuite, or manual entry.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the parcel is located. Used for jurisdictional compliance, USDA FSA state office routing, and regulatory reporting.']
  title_insurance_company varchar [note: 'Name of the title insurance underwriter or company that issued the title insurance policy for this ownership record. Used for claims processing and title defect resolution.']
  title_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'The policy number of the title insurance policy issued to protect the owner\'s interest in this parcel against title defects, liens, or encumbrances not discovered during the title search. Issued by a licensed title insurance underwriter.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'The reason or mechanism by which ownership was transferred to the current owner. Used for USDA FSA farm program eligibility determination, estate planning analysis, and land tenure reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sale|inheritance|gift|foreclosure|condemnation|partition|exchange|court_order — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and data lineage for compliance with SOX and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  usda_farm_number varchar [note: 'The USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) farm number associated with this parcel, used for enrollment in commodity support programs, conservation programs, and crop insurance. A single farm number may encompass multiple tracts and parcels.']

  Note: 'Ownership record linking parcels to legal owners (individuals, entities, trusts, LLCs, partnerships). Captures owner name, ownership type (fee simple, joint tenancy, tenancy in common, life estate, trust, LLC membership), ownership percentage share, deed reference number, deed book and page, recording date, effective date, expiration date (for term interests), title insurance policy number, and ownership status (current/historical/disputed). Supports multi-owner parcels with fractional interest tracking and ownership chain history. Note: land valuation and acquisition financial transactions are owned by the finance domain — this product captures legal ownership records only. SSOT for who legally owns each parcel and the chain of title.'
}

Table land.lease {
  lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the agricultural land lease agreement record. Primary key for the lease data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Lease agreements specify commodity restrictions (e.g., no GMO crops, corn only). lease.crop_restriction is a plain-text denormalization of the commodity entity. Proper FK enables lease compliance moni']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landowner (lessor) party record. Identifies the individual or entity that owns the land and is granting the lease.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operational farm unit to which the leased land is assigned. Provides operational context for crop planning and field operations.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the land parcel(s) covered under this lease agreement. Links to the parcel master record in the land domain for legal and GIS boundary context.']
  tenant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Leases are executed between legal entities (accounts). The tenant operator account is required for billing, FSA farm operator designation, crop insurance enrollment, and lease payment processing. Role']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement contains an automatic renewal clause. When True, the lease renews for an additional term at expiry unless either party provides written notice of termination within the required notice period.']
  cash_rent_rate_per_acre decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual cash rent rate expressed in US dollars per acre. Applicable for cash_rent and flex_rent lease types. Represents the base rental obligation per acre per year. Sensitive commercial term.']
  conservation_practice_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement mandates adherence to specific conservation practices (e.g., cover cropping, no-till, buffer strips) as a condition of tenancy. Supports BMP and ISO 14001 environmental compliance tracking.']
  county_fips_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit FIPS code identifying the US county in which the leased land is located. Used for USDA program enrollment, regulatory reporting, and geographic analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lease record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  crop_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of crop proceeds or harvested commodity allocated to the landowner under a crop share lease arrangement. Typically ranges from 25% to 50%. Applicable only when lease_type is \'crop_share\'.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this lease record (e.g., USD, CAD, BRL). Supports international operations and multi-currency reporting.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the executed lease agreement document stored in the document management system. Enables retrieval of the original signed contract for legal and audit purposes.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the lease agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended or evergreen leases. Used for renewal planning and land tenure reporting.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the lease agreement was formally signed and executed by both parties. May differ from the start date for leases signed in advance of the crop year.']
  flex_rent_base_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The commodity base price (in $/bushel or $/MT) used as the reference point for flex rent adjustment calculations. When the actual commodity price exceeds this threshold, additional rent is triggered. Applicable only for flex_rent lease type.']
  flex_rent_cap_per_acre decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable flex rent payment per acre per year, capping the upside obligation for the tenant regardless of commodity price or yield performance. Applicable only for flex_rent lease type.']
  flex_rent_trigger_mechanism varchar [note: 'The mechanism that activates the flex rent adjustment above the base cash rent. Price trigger: based on commodity market price. Yield trigger: based on actual field yield (YPM). Revenue trigger: based on gross revenue per acre. None: not applicable for non-flex leases.']
  fsa_farm_number varchar [note: 'USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) assigned farm number for the leased land. Required for federal program enrollment, crop insurance, and USDA compliance reporting. Links to FSA farm records.']
  fsa_tract_number varchar [note: 'USDA FSA tract number identifying the specific tract of land within the FSA farm record. A farm may contain multiple tracts; this field identifies the specific tract(s) covered by this lease.']
  improvements_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement permits the tenant to make permanent improvements to the land (e.g., drainage tile installation, building construction, irrigation infrastructure). Relevant for CAPEX planning and land stewardship.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the lease agreement. Used in correspondence with landowners, FSA filings, and internal reference. Format: LSE-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lease agreement. Active: in force and binding. Expired: past end date without renewal. Terminated: ended early by either party. Pending: executed but not yet effective. Under negotiation: terms being discussed. Draft: not yet executed.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease agreement structure. Cash rent: fixed $/acre payment. Crop share: landlord receives a percentage of crop proceeds. Flex rent: base rent adjusted by commodity price or yield triggers. Custom farming: operator provides services for a fee. Pasture lease: grazing rights. Hunting lease: recreational use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash_rent|crop_share|flex_rent|custom_farming|pasture_lease|hunting_lease — promote to reference product]']
  legal_description varchar [note: 'Formal legal description of the leased land parcel(s) as recorded in the deed or title document. Typically expressed in Public Land Survey System (PLSS) notation (e.g., Section-Township-Range) or metes and bounds.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional terms, special conditions, negotiation history, or operational notes related to the lease agreement that are not captured in structured fields.']
  payment_due_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1–12) in which the primary annual rent payment is due to the landowner. For semi-annual schedules, this represents the first payment month. Used by the finance domain to schedule AP obligations.']
  payment_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency at which cash rent payments are due to the landowner under the lease agreement. Drives accounts payable scheduling in the finance domain. Common agricultural practice is annual or semi-annual.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to the lease end date by which written notice of non-renewal or termination must be provided by either party. Typically 30, 60, or 90 days for agricultural leases. Drives automated renewal alert workflows.']
  right_of_first_refusal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement grants the tenant/operator a right of first refusal (ROFR) to purchase the land if the landowner decides to sell. When True, the operator must be offered the opportunity to match any third-party purchase offer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this lease record was ingested. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across Granular Farm Management, SAP RE-FX, and Oracle NetSuite.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the lease agreement becomes effective and the tenant/operator gains the right to use the land. Typically March 1 or the start of the crop year for agricultural leases.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation (or ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for international operations) identifying the state in which the leased land is located.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the lease was terminated early, if applicable. Populated only when lease_status is \'terminated\'. Distinct from end_date which is the scheduled expiry.']
  total_leased_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total land area covered under this lease agreement, expressed in acres. Used for cash rent calculation, FSA reporting, and land tenure analytics. Sourced from FSA farm records or GIS boundary measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the lease record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Agricultural land lease agreement record capturing the contractual relationship between landowner and farm operator/tenant for use of agricultural parcels. Stores lease type (cash rent, crop share, flex rent, custom farming agreement), lease start date, lease end date, annual cash rent rate ($/acre), crop share percentage, flex rent base price and trigger mechanism, total leased acres, payment schedule (annual/semi-annual/quarterly), auto-renewal flag, right-of-first-refusal flag, lease document reference, FSA farm number, and lease status (active/expired/terminated/pending/under negotiation). Links to parcel and farm_unit for legal and operational context. Note: lease payment transactions and accounts payable are owned by the finance/invoice domains — this product captures the lease agreement terms only, not financial transactions. SSOT for land tenure agreements and rental obligation terms.'
}

Table land.soil_profile {
  soil_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the soil profile record. Primary key for the soil_profile data product within the land domain.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or parcel to which this soil profile belongs. Links the soil profile to its geographic land unit as mapped in Esri ArcGIS and John Deere Operations Center.']
  agronomist_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for certified crop adviser (CCA) or agronomist observations about this soil profile, including anomalies, historical land use context, or special management considerations not captured in structured fields. Supports GLOBALG.A.P. and USDA NRCS conservation planning documentation.']
  awc_cm_cm decimal(5,3) [note: 'Available Water Capacity (AWC) expressed as centimeters of water per centimeter of soil depth (cm/cm), representing the volume of water held between field capacity and permanent wilting point. Critical input for irrigation scheduling and evapotranspiration (ET) modeling.']
  bulk_density_g_cm3 decimal(5,3) [note: 'Soil bulk density expressed in grams per cubic centimeter (g/cm³), representing the mass of dry soil per unit volume including pore space. Indicates soil compaction level; values above 1.6 g/cm³ typically restrict root growth. Informs tillage and cover crop decisions.']
  cec_meq_100g decimal(6,2) [note: 'Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) expressed in milliequivalents per 100 grams of soil (meq/100g). Measures the soil\'s ability to hold and supply positively charged nutrient ions (Ca, Mg, K, Na). Higher CEC indicates greater nutrient-holding capacity and influences fertilizer recommendations.']
  conservation_practice_code varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard code(s) applied to this soil mapping unit (e.g., 340 Cover Crop, 600 Terrace, 412 Grassed Waterway). Supports EQIP/CSP program documentation, BMP compliance reporting, and ISO 14001 environmental management system records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this soil profile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  depth_to_restrictive_layer_cm decimal(6,1) [note: 'Depth in centimeters from the soil surface to the first restrictive layer (e.g., fragipan, claypan, bedrock, seasonal high water table). Limits effective rooting depth and influences crop selection, drainage tile placement, and irrigation design.']
  drainage_class varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS drainage class describing the natural soil drainage condition (e.g., well drained, poorly drained). Critical for tile drainage design, planting date decisions, and ISO 14001 environmental management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: excessively drained|somewhat excessively drained|well drained|moderately well drained|somewhat poorly drained|poorly drained|very poorly drained — promote to reference product]']
  ec_ds_m decimal(6,3) [note: 'Soil electrical conductivity (EC) measured in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m), indicating soil salinity level. Values above 4 dS/m may indicate salinity stress for most crops. Used for precision agriculture zone delineation and irrigation water quality management.']
  erosion_risk_class varchar [note: 'Qualitative erosion risk classification for this soil profile based on USDA NRCS Universal Soil Loss Equation (USLE/RUSLE) factors including erodibility (K-factor), slope, and cover. Drives conservation practice selection and FSMA Produce Safety Rule compliance for water quality.']
  gis_map_unit_area_ha decimal(10,4) [note: 'Area of the soil mapping unit or management zone in hectares as calculated from the GIS polygon in Esri ArcGIS. Used for proportional nutrient application calculations, yield per acre metric (YPM) normalization, and land valuation.']
  is_hydric boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this soil is classified as a hydric soil by USDA NRCS criteria, meaning it formed under conditions of saturation, flooding, or ponding long enough to develop anaerobic conditions. Hydric soils are associated with wetlands and trigger regulatory review under Section 404 of the Clean Water Act and USDA NRCS wetland conservation provisions.']
  is_prime_farmland boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this soil mapping unit is designated as Prime Farmland, Farmland of Statewide Importance, or Unique Farmland by USDA NRCS. Prime farmland designation affects land use planning, conservation program eligibility (CRP, EQIP), and regulatory review for development.']
  k_factor decimal(5,3) [note: 'USDA NRCS soil erodibility K-factor from the Universal Soil Loss Equation (USLE/RUSLE), ranging from 0.02 (least erodible) to 0.69 (most erodible). Quantifies the susceptibility of soil to detachment and transport by rainfall and runoff. Used in conservation planning and NRCS EQIP program applications.']
  lab_reference_number varchar [note: 'External laboratory sample reference or accession number assigned by the certified soil testing laboratory. Enables traceability back to the original Certificate of Analysis (COA) and laboratory report for audit and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance verification.']
  land_capability_class varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS Land Capability Classification (LCC) rating from Class I (fewest limitations) to Class VIII (unsuitable for cultivation). Used for conservation planning, crop insurance rating, and USDA program eligibility determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: I|II|III|IV|V|VI|VII|VIII — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  land_capability_subclass varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS Land Capability Subclass modifier indicating the primary limitation type: \'e\' (erosion), \'w\' (wetness/drainage), \'s\' (soil limitations), \'c\' (climate). Complements the land_capability_class for conservation practice planning under BMP guidelines.']
  management_zone varchar [note: 'Agronomic management zone designation within the field (e.g., \'Zone 1 - High Productivity\', \'Zone 3 - Low OM\'). Used for variable rate application (VRA) prescriptions in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView to optimize input use efficiency.']
  mukey varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS SSURGO (Soil Survey Geographic Database) Map Unit Key (MUKEY) — the unique national identifier for the soil map unit polygon. Enables direct linkage to the SSURGO database for detailed soil property lookup, Web Soil Survey integration, and NRCS program eligibility verification.']
  nitrogen_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available nitrogen (N) concentration in the soil expressed in parts per million (ppm), typically measured as nitrate-N or ammonium-N. Core component of NPK (Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium) soil fertility assessment driving fertilization event planning.']
  nrcs_soil_series varchar [note: 'Official USDA NRCS soil series name assigned to this mapping unit (e.g., \'Drummer\', \'Tama\', \'Clarion\'). Derived from the SSURGO/Web Soil Survey database and used for agronomic benchmarking and land valuation.']
  organic_matter_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of organic matter (OM) in the soil by weight, measured by loss-on-ignition or Walkley-Black method. Key indicator of soil health, water-holding capacity, and nutrient cycling. Supports carbon sequestration reporting and ISCC sustainability compliance.']
  ph_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory method used to determine the soil pH value (e.g., 1:1 water suspension, 1:2 CaCl2 solution). Required for accurate interpretation and cross-laboratory comparability of pH results per USDA NRCS and university extension standards.']
  ph_value decimal(4,2) [note: 'Soil pH (Potential of Hydrogen) measurement representing soil acidity or alkalinity on a 0–14 scale, typically measured in a 1:1 soil-water suspension. Drives lime application decisions, nutrient availability, and crop selection. Target range for most crops: 6.0–7.0.']
  phosphorus_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available phosphorus (P) concentration in the soil expressed in parts per million (ppm), typically measured by Bray-1, Mehlich-3, or Olsen extraction method. Core NPK component driving phosphate fertilizer recommendations and environmental runoff risk assessment.']
  potassium_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available potassium (K) concentration in the soil expressed in parts per million (ppm), typically measured by Mehlich-3 or ammonium acetate extraction. Core NPK component influencing crop quality, drought tolerance, and potash fertilizer application rates.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this soil profile or management zone within the farm management information system (FMIS). Used for cross-referencing with Granular Farm Management and Trimble Ag Software records.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the soil profile or management zone (e.g., \'North Field Zone A\', \'River Bottom Unit 3\'). Used for display and reporting in FMIS dashboards.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the soil profile record. \'active\' indicates the profile is the current SSOT for agronomic decisions; \'superseded\' indicates a newer profile has replaced it; \'pending_review\' indicates awaiting agronomist validation.']
  restrictive_layer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the soil restrictive layer type encountered at the depth recorded in depth_to_restrictive_layer_cm. Determines the agronomic and engineering implications for root penetration, drainage, and deep tillage feasibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fragipan|claypan|bedrock|hardpan|water table|caliche|permafrost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sampling_date date [note: 'Date on which the soil sample was collected for laboratory analysis that established or last updated this soil profile. Determines the currency of the soil data for agronomic decision-making; most university extension services recommend resampling every 3–4 years.']
  sampling_depth_cm decimal(5,1) [note: 'Depth in centimeters at which the soil sample was collected (e.g., 0–15 cm for surface, 15–30 cm for subsoil). Sampling depth affects interpretation of pH, OM, and nutrient results and must be consistent across sampling events for trend analysis.']
  slope_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average slope of the soil mapping unit expressed as a percentage (rise/run × 100). Influences erosion risk assessment, tillage method selection, irrigation system design, and conservation practice requirements under USDA NRCS and EPA BMP guidelines.']
  soil_order varchar [note: 'Highest-level USDA Soil Taxonomy classification order for this soil profile (e.g., Mollisol, Alfisol). Determines broad agronomic suitability and management approach. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Alfisol|Andisol|Aridisol|Entisol|Gelisol|Histosol|Inceptisol|Mollisol|Oxisol|Spodosol|Ultisol|Vertisol — promote to reference product]']
  soil_suborder varchar [note: 'Second-level USDA Soil Taxonomy classification suborder for this soil profile (e.g., Udoll, Aquoll). Provides finer agronomic classification for crop suitability and drainage management planning.']
  texture_class varchar [note: 'USDA soil texture classification based on the relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay as determined by the USDA textural triangle. Drives irrigation scheduling, tillage decisions, and nutrient management planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|loamy sand|sandy loam|loam|silt loam|silt|sandy clay loam|clay loam|silty clay loam|sandy clay|silty clay|clay — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this soil profile record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks data currency for agronomic decision support and supports audit trail requirements under GLOBALG.A.P. and ISO 14001.']

  Note: 'Authoritative soil health profile for a defined soil mapping unit or management zone within a field. Stores USDA NRCS soil series name, soil texture class (USDA triangle), soil order/suborder, drainage class, available water capacity (AWC), organic matter percentage (OM), cation exchange capacity (CEC meq/100g), electrical conductivity (EC dS/m), bulk density, soil depth to restrictive layer, NRCS hydric soil flag, prime farmland designation, and profile creation/update dates. SSOT for baseline soil physical and chemical characteristics.'
}

Table land.soil_sample {
  soil_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual soil sampling event record. Primary key for the soil_sample data product in the land domain.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field from which this soil sample was collected. Links to the land domain field master record for GIS boundary and parcel context.']
  lab_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Soil testing laboratories are qualified vendors in the procurement system. Linking soil_sample to the lab vendor enables vendor qualification tracking, invoice matching for lab services, COA verificat']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sub-field management zone from which this sample was drawn. Management zones are delineated areas within a field with homogeneous soil characteristics used for Variable Rate Application (VRA) prescription planning.']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile. Business justification: A soil sampling event is conducted within a specific soil mapping unit or management zone that is authoritatively described by a soil_profile record. Linking soil_sample to soil_profile establishes th']
  base_saturation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the soil\'s Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) occupied by base cations (calcium, magnesium, potassium, sodium). Calculated from individual cation concentrations relative to CEC. Used to assess nutrient balance and guide lime and fertilizer application strategies.']
  buffer_ph decimal(4,2) [note: 'Buffer pH value measured using a buffered solution (e.g., Shoemaker-McLean-Pratt or Sikora buffer) to determine the lime requirement for acidic soils. Used in conjunction with water pH to calculate the amount of agricultural lime needed to raise soil pH to target levels.']
  calcium_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available calcium (Ca) concentration in parts per million (ppm). Calcium is a secondary macronutrient essential for cell wall development and root growth. Also used in calculating percent base saturation and Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) balance.']
  cec_meq_100g decimal(7,2) [note: 'Cation Exchange Capacity (CEC) of the soil expressed in milliequivalents per 100 grams of soil (meq/100g). CEC measures the soil\'s ability to hold and supply positively charged nutrient ions (Ca, Mg, K, Na, H). Higher CEC indicates greater nutrient-holding capacity and influences fertilizer efficiency.']
  coa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued by the laboratory for this soil sample. The COA is the official document certifying the analytical results and chain of custody.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this soil sample record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  crop_season_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit crop production year associated with this soil sampling event (e.g., 2024). Used to align soil test results with the corresponding growing season for fertilizer prescription planning and year-over-year soil health trend analysis.']
  ec_ds_m decimal(7,3) [note: 'Electrical Conductivity (EC) of the soil measured in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m). EC is an indicator of soil salinity and total dissolved salt concentration. High EC values indicate salinity stress risk and influence irrigation management and crop selection decisions.']
  gps_accuracy_m decimal(6,3) [note: 'Horizontal positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters at time of sample collection. RTK-corrected readings typically achieve sub-0.05m accuracy. Used to assess spatial data quality for precision agriculture applications.']
  lab_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Official accreditation or certification number of the laboratory that analyzed the sample, issued by a recognized accreditation body (e.g., A2LA, NELAP). Supports regulatory compliance and audit traceability.']
  lab_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the laboratory issued the official analytical results report for this soil sample. Used to calculate lab turnaround time and determine data currency for fertilizer prescriptions.']
  lab_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the collected soil sample was submitted to the analytical laboratory for processing. Used to track turnaround time and chain-of-custody compliance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84 datum) of the soil sample collection point. Captured via RTK-accurate GPS equipment for precise spatial referencing in GIS systems and VRA prescription mapping.']
  lime_recommendation_lbs_ac decimal(8,2) [note: 'Laboratory-recommended agricultural lime application rate in pounds per acre, calculated from soil pH and buffer pH results to raise soil pH to the target level for the planned crop. Directly used in fertilizer prescription and VRA lime application planning.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84 datum) of the soil sample collection point. Captured via RTK-accurate GPS equipment for precise spatial referencing in GIS systems and VRA prescription mapping.']
  magnesium_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available magnesium (Mg) concentration in parts per million (ppm). Magnesium is a secondary macronutrient and central component of chlorophyll. Used in CEC base saturation calculations and dolomitic lime application decisions.']
  nitrate_n_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available nitrate nitrogen (NO3-N) concentration in parts per million (ppm) as measured in the soil sample. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Directly informs nitrogen fertilizer application rates and VRA prescriptions.']
  nitrogen_recommendation_lbs_ac decimal(8,2) [note: 'Laboratory or agronomist-recommended nitrogen (N) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil nitrate-N test results, organic matter nitrogen credit, and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.']
  organic_matter_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of organic matter (OM) in the soil sample, measured by loss-on-ignition or Walkley-Black method. Organic matter is a key indicator of soil health, water-holding capacity, and biological activity. Drives nitrogen credit calculations and carbon sequestration assessments.']
  ph_water decimal(4,2) [note: 'Soil pH measured using the 1:1 soil-to-water suspension method, representing the Potential of Hydrogen (pH) — the primary indicator of soil acidity or alkalinity. Values range from 0-14; optimal crop production typically falls between 6.0-7.0. Drives lime application recommendations.']
  phosphorus_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory extraction method used to determine plant-available phosphorus concentration. Bray-1 is standard for acidic soils; Mehlich-3 is a universal extractant; Olsen is used for calcareous/alkaline soils. Method must be recorded as results are not directly comparable across methods.']
  phosphorus_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available phosphorus (P) concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Bray-1 or Mehlich-3 extraction method. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Drives phosphate fertilizer recommendations and VRA prescription maps.']
  phosphorus_recommendation_lbs_ac decimal(8,2) [note: 'Laboratory or agronomist-recommended phosphate (P2O5) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil phosphorus test results and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.']
  potassium_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available potassium (K) concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Mehlich-3 or ammonium acetate extraction. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Drives potash fertilizer recommendations and VRA prescription planning.']
  potassium_recommendation_lbs_ac decimal(8,2) [note: 'Laboratory or agronomist-recommended potash (K2O) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil potassium test results and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.']
  sample_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the soil sample was physically collected from the field. Critical for seasonal trend analysis and determining the agronomic context (pre-plant, post-harvest, etc.).']
  sample_depth_in decimal(5,2) [note: 'Depth in inches at which the soil sample was collected from the surface. Standard agronomic sampling depths are 0-6 inches (topsoil) or 6-12 inches (subsoil). Critical for interpreting nutrient availability relative to root zone.']
  sample_method varchar [note: 'Method used to collect the soil sample. Grid sampling uses a systematic spatial grid; zone sampling collects within defined management zones; composite combines multiple cores; benchmark uses fixed reference points for longitudinal tracking.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable sample identifier assigned at time of collection, used for lab submission tracking and field cross-referencing. Typically formatted as a lab accession or farm-assigned code (e.g., FARM-2024-F01-Z3).']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the soil sample record, tracking progression from field collection through laboratory analysis to agronomic approval for use in fertilizer prescriptions.']
  sampling_technician varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the agronomist, certified crop advisor (CCA), or field technician who physically collected the soil sample. Used for chain-of-custody documentation and quality assurance traceability.']
  soil_texture_class varchar [note: 'USDA soil texture classification based on the relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay particles. Texture class influences water-holding capacity, drainage, tillage requirements, and nutrient management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|loamy_sand|sandy_loam|loam|silt_loam|silt|sandy_clay_loam|clay_loam|silty_clay_loam|sandy_clay|silty_clay|clay — promote to reference product]']
  sulfur_ppm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Plant-available sulfur (S) concentration in parts per million (ppm). Sulfur is a secondary macronutrient critical for protein synthesis and enzyme function. Deficiencies are increasingly common in high-yield cropping systems and drive sulfur fertilizer recommendations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this soil sample record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.']
  zinc_ppm decimal(8,3) [note: 'Plant-available zinc (Zn) micronutrient concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Mehlich-3 or DTPA extraction. Zinc deficiency is common in high-pH soils and drives micronutrient fertilizer recommendations for corn and other sensitive crops.']

  Note: 'Individual soil sampling event record capturing laboratory-analyzed nutrient and chemical results for a specific field zone or GPS-referenced sample point. Stores sample date, lab submission date, lab report date, lab name, sample depth (inches), GPS coordinates, management zone ID, pH (1:1 water), buffer pH, organic matter (%), nitrate-N (ppm), phosphorus (P Bray/Mehlich-3 ppm), potassium (K ppm), calcium (Ca ppm), magnesium (Mg ppm), sulfur (S ppm), zinc (Zn ppm), CEC (meq/100g), EC (dS/m), percent base saturation, and COA reference number. Core to NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.'
}

Table land.management_zone {
  management_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the management zone record in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent agricultural field within which this management zone is spatially defined. Links to the land domain field entity.']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season for which this management zone configuration is active. Management zone boundaries and soil attribute values may be updated between growing seasons as new soil sampling and yield data become available.']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Variable-rate seeding (VRS) prescriptions assign specific seed varieties per management zone. management_zone already has vra_eligible flag. Precision agriculture operations require zone-level variety']
  soil_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_profile. Business justification: A management zone is delineated based on soil characteristics (EC, pH, OM, CEC, texture) that are authoritatively captured in the soil_profile entity. Linking management_zone to soil_profile formalize']
  agronomist_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for agronomist or crop consultant observations about the management zone, including special management considerations, historical problem areas, or recommendations for upcoming season. Captured in Granular Farm Management or Trimble Ag Software during field scouting.']
  area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated spatial area of the management zone expressed in acres, derived from the GIS polygon geometry. Used for VRA prescription rate calculations, input cost allocation, and yield-per-acre (YPM) reporting. Precision to four decimal places supports sub-acre zone granularity.']
  area_hectares decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated spatial area of the management zone expressed in hectares for international reporting and metric-system compliance. Supports GLOBALG.A.P. and ISO 14001 environmental reporting requirements.']
  cec_meq_100g decimal(6,2) [note: 'Mean cation exchange capacity (CEC) of the management zone soil expressed in milliequivalents per 100 grams (meq/100g). Measures the soil\'s ability to hold and supply positively charged nutrient ions (Ca, Mg, K, Na). Higher CEC indicates greater nutrient-holding capacity. Used for fertilizer rate calibration and soil amendment planning.']
  centroid_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the management zone polygon centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS (Global Positioning System) navigation, weather station proximity matching, and spatial analytics in Esri ArcGIS.']
  centroid_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the management zone polygon centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for GPS navigation, weather data correlation, and spatial analytics in Esri ArcGIS.']
  conservation_practice_code varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS conservation practice standard code applied to this management zone (e.g., 590 - Nutrient Management, 329 - Residue and Tillage Management, 340 - Cover Crop, 412 - Grassed Waterway). Supports USDA EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program) compliance reporting and ISO 14001 environmental management documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this management zone record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  delineation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to spatially delineate the management zone boundary. EC (Electrical Conductivity) scanning uses Veris or EM38 sensors; yield history uses multi-year combine yield monitor data from Climate FieldView or John Deere Operations Center; satellite NDVI uses remote sensing imagery; manual uses operator-drawn boundaries in Esri ArcGIS or Trimble Ag; soil sampling uses interpolated soil test point data; topographic analysis uses digital elevation model (DEM) data.']
  delineation_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the management zone boundary was originally delineated or most recently revised. Used to assess zone data currency and schedule periodic re-delineation based on updated EC scanning, yield history, or satellite imagery data.']
  drainage_class varchar [note: 'USDA NRCS soil drainage class for the management zone, indicating the natural rate of water removal from the soil profile. Affects crop selection, planting date decisions, and tile drainage infrastructure planning. Poorly drained zones may qualify for wetland conservation programs.']
  ec_deep_ds_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Apparent soil electrical conductivity (EC) reading at deep depth (typically 0-90 cm) expressed in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m). Measured via Veris 3100 or EM38 sensor. Reflects subsoil characteristics including clay pan depth and water-holding capacity. Complements shallow EC for comprehensive zone delineation.']
  ec_scan_date date [note: 'Date on which the soil electrical conductivity (EC) scanning survey was conducted for this management zone. Used to assess data currency and schedule re-scanning intervals (typically every 3-5 years per BMP guidelines).']
  ec_shallow_ds_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Apparent soil electrical conductivity (EC) reading at shallow depth (typically 0-30 cm) expressed in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m). Measured via Veris 3100 or EM38 sensor during EC scanning. Correlates with soil texture, clay content, and salinity. Used as primary input for EC-based zone delineation and VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation.']
  external_zone_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this management zone in the originating source system (e.g., Trimble Ag zone GUID, John Deere Operations Center zone ID, Esri ArcGIS feature OID). Enables bidirectional synchronization and traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational precision agriculture platforms.']
  gis_polygon_wkt varchar [note: 'Spatial boundary of the management zone stored as Well-Known Text (WKT) polygon geometry in WGS84 coordinate reference system (EPSG:4326). Sourced from Esri ArcGIS or John Deere Operations Center RTK-accurate field mapping. Enables spatial joins with soil sampling points, yield data, and satellite imagery layers.']
  last_soil_sample_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent composite soil sampling event conducted within this management zone. Used to assess soil data currency and trigger re-sampling workflows. GLOBALG.A.P. and USDA NRCS BMP guidelines recommend soil sampling every 2-4 years.']
  ndvi_image_date date [note: 'Date on which the satellite or aerial imagery used to calculate the current NDVI value was captured. Critical for assessing data currency and seasonal relevance of the vegetation index reading.']
  ndvi_value decimal(5,4) [note: 'Most recent NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) value for the management zone, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Derived from satellite or aerial multispectral imagery. Values above 0.5 indicate dense, healthy vegetation; values below 0.2 indicate sparse or stressed vegetation. Used for in-season crop monitoring and zone reclassification.']
  nitrogen_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean available nitrogen (N) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. Component of NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium) nutrient profile. Used to calculate nitrogen fertilizer application rates in VRA prescriptions and comply with nutrient management plan requirements.']
  organic_matter_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean soil organic matter (OM) content expressed as a percentage of dry soil weight for the management zone. Measured via loss-on-ignition or Walkley-Black method from composite soil samples. Higher OM improves water retention, nutrient cycling, and soil structure. Key indicator for carbon sequestration reporting under ISCC and sustainability programs.']
  phosphorus_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean available phosphorus (P) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. Component of NPK nutrient profile. Used to calculate phosphorus fertilizer application rates in VRA prescriptions and support EPA water quality compliance for phosphorus runoff management.']
  potassium_lbs_acre decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean available potassium (K) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. Component of NPK nutrient profile. Used to calculate potassium fertilizer application rates in VRA prescriptions.']
  productivity_class varchar [note: 'Agronomic productivity classification of the management zone based on historical yield performance and soil capability. High productivity zones receive premium input rates; low productivity zones may receive reduced inputs or conservation treatment under BMP (Best Management Practices) guidelines.']
  slope_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean slope gradient of the management zone expressed as a percentage (rise over run × 100), derived from digital elevation model (DEM) analysis in Esri ArcGIS. Used for erosion risk assessment, drainage planning, and equipment operation safety. Slopes above 8% may require conservation practices under USDA NRCS BMP guidelines.']
  soil_ph_mean decimal(4,2) [note: 'Mean soil pH (Potential of Hydrogen) value for the management zone, representing average soil acidity or alkalinity on a 0-14 scale. Calculated from composite soil samples within the zone. Critical for nutrient availability assessment and lime application rate determination in VRA prescriptions. Optimal range for most crops is 6.0-7.0.']
  soil_texture_class varchar [note: 'USDA soil texture classification for the dominant soil type within the management zone, based on relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay particles. Determines water infiltration rate, water-holding capacity, and tillage characteristics. Used for irrigation scheduling and equipment operation planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|loamy_sand|sandy_loam|loam|silt_loam|silt|sandy_clay_loam|clay_loam|silty_clay_loam|sandy_clay|silty_clay|clay — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this management zone record was originated or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when zone data is synchronized across multiple precision agriculture platforms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this management zone record was most recently modified in the lakehouse Silver Layer, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect stale zone configurations and trigger re-synchronization with source precision agriculture systems.']
  vra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this management zone is eligible for variable rate application (VRA) prescription generation in Trimble Ag Software or John Deere Operations Center. Zones must meet minimum area thresholds and have current soil data to qualify for VRA. True = eligible for VRA prescriptions; False = not eligible.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the management zone within the farm management information system (FMIS). Used as the business key in Trimble Ag Software and John Deere Operations Center for variable rate application (VRA) prescription generation.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the management zone (e.g., \'North Field High Productivity Zone\', \'EC Zone 3 - Sandy Loam\'). Used in FMIS reporting and VRA prescription labeling.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the management zone. Active zones are used in current-season VRA prescriptions; inactive zones are temporarily suspended; archived zones are retained for historical analysis; draft zones are pending agronomist review and approval.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone based on the primary agronomic criterion used to define it. Soil type zones are delineated by soil series; EC (Electrical Conductivity) zones by apparent soil EC readings; yield zones by multi-year yield history; topographic zones by elevation and slope; NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) zones by satellite-derived vegetation index; management zones by operator-defined boundaries.']
  zone_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the management zone boundary definition, incremented each time the zone polygon or classification attributes are revised. Supports audit trail and historical comparison of zone configurations across growing seasons.']

  Note: 'Spatially defined sub-field management zone used for variable rate application (VRA) prescriptions, precision nutrient management, and yield zone analysis. Captures zone name, zone code, zone type (soil type, EC zone, yield zone, topographic zone), delineation method (EC scanning, yield history, satellite NDVI, manual), zone area (acres), GIS polygon geometry, NDVI index value, EC shallow/deep readings, zone classification (high/medium/low productivity), and active flag. Integrates with Trimble Ag and John Deere Operations Center for VRA prescription generation.'
}

Table land.field_boundary {
  field_boundary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each versioned field boundary record in the land domain. Primary key for the field_boundary data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm or operation that owns or manages this field boundary. Supports multi-farm enterprise hierarchies in Granular Farm Management and SAP S/4HANA land management.']
  primary_field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent agricultural field entity that this boundary version describes. Multiple boundary versions may exist for the same field over time as boundaries are split, merged, or re-surveyed.']
  surveying_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Field boundary surveys are procured services — GIS/survey vendors are contracted to establish legal boundaries for FSA compliance, title insurance, and precision ag setup. The existing surveyed_by pla']
  tertiary_trimble_field_id varchar [note: 'Field identifier assigned by Trimble Ag Software for variable rate prescription (VRA) and crop scouting integration. Enables linkage of boundary geometry with Trimble-generated field plans and prescription maps.']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Positional accuracy classification of the boundary geometry. \'RTK\' indicates centimeter-level accuracy from Real-Time Kinematic GPS survey; \'sub_meter\' indicates accuracy better than 1 meter; \'meter\' indicates accuracy within 1-5 meters; \'multi_meter\' indicates accuracy greater than 5 meters (e.g., from manual digitization or low-resolution imagery).']
  accuracy_meters decimal(8,4) [note: 'Quantitative positional accuracy of the boundary geometry expressed in meters (root mean square error or circular error probable). Complements accuracy_class with a precise numeric value for spatial quality assessment and compliance reporting.']
  arcgis_feature_class_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the corresponding feature class and object ID within the Esri ArcGIS geodatabase. Enables direct linkage between this data product and the authoritative GIS source layer for spatial queries and map rendering.']
