  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this category are subject to mandatory regulatory inspection requirements (e.g., EPA pesticide application equipment calibration, OSHA safety inspections for operator-driven equipment, USDA/APHIS compliance checks for livestock handling equipment). True = regulatory inspection is required.']
  jd_ops_center_category_code varchar [note: 'Equipment category code as defined in John Deere Operations Center fleet telematics platform. Used for cross-system data reconciliation between the enterprise asset taxonomy and John Deere precision agriculture fleet management data feeds.']
  oee_benchmark_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Industry benchmark target for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) expressed as a percentage (0–100) for assets in this category. Used as the KPI baseline for fleet performance monitoring in John Deere Operations Center and operational reporting dashboards. OEE = Availability × Performance × Quality.']
  operator_license_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operating assets in this category requires a specific operator license, certification, or training qualification (e.g., CDL for transport vehicles, pesticide applicator license for sprayers, OSHA-mandated forklift certification for material handling equipment). Supports workforce compliance management in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  opex_budget_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO to which operational and maintenance expenditure for assets in this category is posted. Supports OPEX budget tracking, maintenance cost analysis, and EBITDA reporting by equipment category.']
  pesticide_applicator_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operating assets in this category requires a valid EPA-certified pesticide applicator license (e.g., self-propelled sprayers, aerial application equipment). Supports IPM (Integrated Pest Management) compliance and EPA regulatory audit readiness.']
  pm_interval_days varchar [note: 'Calendar-day interval between scheduled preventive maintenance events for assets in this category, used when calendar-based (rather than usage-based) PM scheduling is applicable (e.g., annual inspections, seasonal service). Complements pm_interval_hours for dual-trigger PM strategies.']
  pm_interval_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours interval between scheduled preventive maintenance (PM) events for assets in this category (e.g., 250 hours for tractor engine service). Used to auto-generate PM work orders in SAP S/4HANA PM/CMMS and John Deere Operations Center maintenance scheduling.']
  power_rating_hp decimal(7,2) [note: 'Typical or standard power rating in horsepower (HP) for assets in this category, representing the principal quantitative performance characteristic. Used for equipment matching (tractor-implement compatibility), fleet specification planning, and fuel consumption benchmarking.']
  precision_ag_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this category support precision agriculture hardware integration, including RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) GPS receivers, VRA (Variable Rate Application) controllers, NDVI sensors, or FMIS (Farm Management Information System) data connectivity. Supports precision ag fleet planning.']
  salvage_value_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated residual (salvage) value at end of useful life expressed as a percentage of original acquisition cost, used in depreciation base calculation for assets in this category. Supports CAPEX planning and asset disposal valuation in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA.']
  sap_asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding asset class code in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA (Fixed Asset Accounting) module that maps to this equipment category. Enables direct integration between the equipment taxonomy and SAP financial asset accounting for depreciation, CAPEX tracking, and balance sheet reporting.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric display sort order for presenting asset categories in user interfaces, reports, and dropdown lists within the equipment domain. Allows business-defined ordering independent of alphabetical or ID-based sequencing.']
  standard_depreciation_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard useful economic life in years assigned to all assets within this category for CAPEX depreciation scheduling and financial reporting. Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO asset accounting to default depreciation key and useful life at asset creation. Aligned with GAAP/IFRS IAS 16 useful life estimation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this asset category record was last modified. Supports change tracking, taxonomy governance audit trails, and Silver Layer incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  vra_controller_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether assets in this category are compatible with VRA (Variable Rate Application) controllers for precision input management (seed, fertilizer, pesticide). Supports VRA prescription execution tracking in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView.']
  weight_class varchar [note: 'Standardized weight class of assets in this category, used for transport logistics planning, road permit requirements, soil compaction risk assessment, and field access restrictions. Aligns with USDA and DOT weight classification standards for agricultural equipment.']

  Note: 'Reference classification hierarchy for agricultural equipment types and categories. Defines the taxonomy of equipment classes (e.g., tillage, planting, spraying, harvesting, irrigation, precision ag hardware, livestock handling, transport fleet) with category codes, parent-child hierarchy for rollup reporting, OEE benchmark targets per category, standard depreciation life, and applicable regulatory inspection requirements. Used for CAPEX/OPEX budgeting and fleet planning.'
}

Table equipment.telematics_reading {
  telematics_reading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each telemetry event record streamed from IoT-enabled agricultural equipment. Primary key for the telematics_reading data product. Role: EVENT_LOG.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment asset (tractor, combine, planter, sprayer, irrigation system) that emitted this telemetry event. EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE per EVENT_LOG role minimum.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the operator or machine driver logged into the equipment at the time of the telemetry reading. Supports hours-of-service compliance and operator performance analytics.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or land parcel where the equipment was operating at the time of the reading. Supports field coverage monitoring and VRA audit trails.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Telematics readings are streamed continuously from equipment during field operations. telematics_reading has asset_id, field_id, and vra_prescription_id but no FK to the specific field_operation conte']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: VRA as-applied vs. prescription comparison by management zone is a core precision agriculture reporting process. Telematics readings carry GPS coordinates that map to management zones; linking directl']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_order. Business justification: Telematics readings from transport vehicles (GPS position, temperature, fuel) must be associated with the transport order being executed for FSMA cold-chain monitoring, route compliance verification, ']
  application_rate_actual decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual rate of material (seed, fertilizer, agrochemical) being applied by the implement at the time of the reading, in the unit defined by the active VRA prescription. Supports compliance verification against prescribed rates and MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) audit trails.']
  application_rate_target decimal(10,3) [note: 'Prescribed target rate of material application from the active VRA prescription at the time of the reading, in the same unit as actual_application_rate. Enables deviation analysis between prescribed and actual application for compliance and agronomic reporting.']
  application_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the actual and target application rate fields (e.g., kg/ha for fertilizer, L/ha for liquid agrochemicals, seeds/ha for planting). Ensures correct interpretation of application rate values across different implement types.']
  coolant_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Engine coolant temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the reading. Elevated values trigger predictive maintenance alerts and can indicate impending engine failure.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or IoT platform that streamed this telemetry record. EVENT_TYPE_OR_CHANNEL per EVENT_LOG role minimum. Supports multi-source fleet management and data lineage.']
  dtc_code varchar [note: 'Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) reported by the equipment\'s onboard diagnostics system at the time of the reading. Null when no fault is active. Triggers corrective maintenance work orders in SAP S/4HANA PM and supports predictive maintenance analytics.']
  dtc_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the active Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) at the time of the reading. \'Critical\' faults require immediate equipment shutdown; \'warning\' faults require scheduled maintenance; \'informational\' faults are advisory only.']
  engine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative engine operating hours recorded on the equipment\'s hour meter at the time of the reading. Primary trigger metric for preventive maintenance scheduling (PM) and asset lifecycle management per ISO 55000.']
  engine_load_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Engine load as a percentage of maximum rated capacity at the time of the reading. Supports OEE calculation, fuel consumption benchmarking, and predictive maintenance triggers.']
  engine_rpm varchar [note: 'Engine speed in revolutions per minute at the time of the reading. Used for fuel efficiency analysis, operator behavior monitoring, and predictive maintenance trigger evaluation.']
  field_boundary_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment\'s GPS position at the time of the reading falls within the registered boundary of the assigned field. False values indicate potential off-field operation, boundary drift, or GPS error.']
  fuel_consumption_rate_lph decimal(8,3) [note: 'Instantaneous fuel consumption rate in liters per hour at the time of the reading. Supports OPEX cost allocation, carbon footprint reporting, and fuel efficiency benchmarking across the fleet.']
  fuel_level_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Remaining fuel level in the equipment\'s tank expressed as a percentage of full capacity at the time of the reading. Supports dispatch planning, refueling logistics, and downtime prevention.']
  gps_altitude_m double [note: 'Altitude of the equipment above mean sea level in meters at the time of the reading, as reported by the onboard GPS/RTK receiver. Supports terrain-aware precision agriculture analytics.']
  ground_speed_kph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Actual ground speed of the equipment in kilometers per hour as measured by the GPS receiver at the time of the reading. Used for field coverage rate calculations, operator compliance, and OEE analysis.']
  heading_degrees decimal(5,2) [note: 'Direction of travel of the equipment in degrees from true north (0–360) at the time of the reading. Supports field pass mapping, coverage analysis, and auto-steer validation.']
  hydraulic_pressure_kpa decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hydraulic system pressure in kilopascals at the time of the reading. Abnormal values indicate hydraulic system faults and trigger corrective maintenance work orders.']
  implement_depth_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Working depth of the attached implement (e.g., planter, tillage tool) in centimeters at the time of the reading. Supports agronomic compliance verification, VRA prescription audit trails, and soil management analytics.']
  implement_width_m decimal(6,2) [note: 'Working width of the attached implement in meters at the time of the reading. Used for field coverage area calculations and pass efficiency analysis.']
  ingested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when this telemetry record was received and ingested into the lakehouse Silver layer from the source system (John Deere Operations Center, Trimble, or other IoT platform). Supports data latency monitoring and pipeline auditing.']
  latitude double [note: 'RTK-corrected WGS84 latitude coordinate of the equipment at the time of the reading, in decimal degrees. Supports fleet position tracking, field coverage monitoring, geofence evaluation, and theft detection.']
  load_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the load carried by the equipment (e.g., grain in combine hopper, fertilizer in applicator) in kilograms at the time of the reading. Supports yield estimation, logistics planning, and axle load compliance.']
  longitude double [note: 'RTK-corrected WGS84 longitude coordinate of the equipment at the time of the reading, in decimal degrees. Supports fleet position tracking, field coverage monitoring, geofence evaluation, and theft detection.']
  ndvi_reading decimal(5,4) [note: 'Normalized Difference Vegetation Index value captured by the onboard crop sensor at the time of the reading, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Supports variable rate application (VRA) decisions, crop health monitoring, and precision agriculture analytics.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the equipment at the time of the reading. Drives OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation by classifying time into productive, transport, idle, and parked categories. EVENT_PAYLOAD_OR_VALUE per EVENT_LOG role minimum.']
  pto_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Power Take-Off (PTO) shaft was engaged at the time of the reading. PTO engagement signals active implement operation (e.g., mower, spreader, pump) and is used for productive-time classification in OEE calculations.']
  pto_rpm varchar [note: 'Power Take-Off shaft speed in revolutions per minute at the time of the reading when PTO is engaged. Used to verify correct implement operating speed and diagnose PTO-related faults.']
  reading_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Data quality classification for this telemetry record assigned during Silver layer ingestion. \'Valid\' records are used in analytics; \'suspect\' records require review; \'invalid\' records are excluded from reporting. Supports data governance and AI/ML feature engineering.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise UTC timestamp when the telemetry event was recorded by the equipment\'s onboard controller. EVENT_TIMESTAMP per EVENT_LOG role minimum. Distinct from ingestion audit timestamps.']
  rtk_correction_status varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the GPS/RTK positional correction applied at the time of the reading. \'Fixed\' indicates sub-inch accuracy; \'float\' and \'dgps\' indicate degraded precision. Critical for VRA prescription audit trails and auto-steer accuracy validation.']
  signal_strength_dbm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Cellular or satellite communication signal strength in decibel-milliwatts (dBm) at the time of the reading. Supports data quality assessment, connectivity gap analysis, and telematics infrastructure planning.']
  transmission_delay_ms varchar [note: 'Latency in milliseconds between the equipment recording the telemetry event and the data being received by the cloud platform. Supports data quality scoring and near-real-time fleet dashboard reliability assessment.']

  Note: 'High-frequency telemetry records streamed from John Deere Operations Center, Trimble, and other IoT-enabled agricultural equipment. Captures machine hours, engine RPM, fuel consumption rate, ground speed, PTO engagement, hydraulic pressure, GPS coordinates (RTK-corrected latitude/longitude), heading, NDVI sensor readings, implement depth/width, load weight, fault codes (DTC), operational status (field working/transport/idle/parked), geofence zone, RTK correction status, field boundary match flag, data source identifier, and timestamp. SSOT for all equipment telemetry including fleet position tracking — latest-reading queries provide current fleet position for dispatch dashboards, field coverage monitoring, theft detection, and operator hours-of-service compliance (replacing standalone fleet_position). Supports OEE calculation, predictive maintenance triggers, precision agriculture VRA audit trails, and fleet dispatch. One record per telemetry event per asset.'
}

  quality_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.corrective_action. Role-prefix \'quality_\' distinguishes this fro']

  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work was completed and the equipment was returned to service. Used to calculate MTTR, downtime duration, and OEE impact. Maps to SAP PM Actual Finish Date/Time (IDAT3/IUZEIT2).']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours recorded against the work order upon completion. Used for OPEX cost tracking, technician productivity analysis, and variance reporting against planned hours. Maps to SAP PM Actual Work (ISMNW).']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when maintenance work physically commenced on the equipment. Used to calculate response time, schedule adherence, and Mean Time to Repair (MTTR). Maps to SAP PM Actual Start Date/Time (IDAT2/IUZEIT).']
  breakdown_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this work order was triggered by an unplanned equipment breakdown (True) versus a scheduled maintenance activity (False). Drives MTBF calculations and reliability analytics. Maps to SAP PM Breakdown Indicator (MSAUS).']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text technician notes recorded at work order completion describing the root cause identified, corrective actions taken, and any follow-up recommendations. Supports knowledge management and recurring fault analysis. Maps to SAP PM Technical Findings (LTXT).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was first created in the CMMS (SAP PM). Audit trail field for record lineage and SLA response time measurement. Maps to SAP PM Creation Date/Time (ERDAT/ERUHR).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary cost fields on the work order (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international agricultural enterprises. Maps to SAP FI currency key (WAERS).']
  equipment_downtime_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total hours the equipment asset was unavailable for field operations due to this maintenance event. Critical input for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation and harvest/planting window impact analysis. Maps to SAP PM Breakdown Duration.']
  external_service_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of external contractor or third-party service charges applied to the work order (e.g., OEM dealer service, specialist repair). Supports make-vs-buy analysis and total maintenance cost reporting. Maps to SAP PM external service orders (PM03).']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Standardized equipment fault or failure code from the CMMS fault code catalogue (e.g., SAP PM Damage Code or John Deere diagnostic trouble code). Enables structured failure mode analysis, MTBF calculations, and reliability reporting. Maps to SAP PM Damage Code (QMCOD).']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the reported equipment fault, defect, or maintenance requirement as entered by the operator or maintenance planner. Supports root cause analysis and failure mode trending. Maps to SAP PM Malfunction Description (QMTXT).']
  labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of labor charged to the work order, calculated from actual labor hours multiplied by the applicable labor rate. Contributes to total maintenance OPEX. Maps to SAP CO actual cost settlement for labor activity type.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order record was most recently modified in the CMMS. Supports incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements. Maps to SAP PM Change Date (AEDAT).']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next scheduled maintenance activity is due for this asset based on the PM plan interval or counter reading. Supports proactive maintenance scheduling and equipment availability planning. Maps to SAP PM Next Planned Date.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the work order as assigned by the CMMS (SAP PM). Used for cross-system reference, technician communication, and audit trails. Maps to SAP PM Order Number (AUFNR).']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work order within the CMMS workflow. Drives operational dashboards, KPI reporting, and SLA compliance tracking. Maps to SAP PM system status (STAT). Values: open, in_progress, completed, cancelled, on_hold.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance work order by activity type: PM (Preventive Maintenance), CM (Corrective Maintenance), INSP (Inspection), CALIB (Calibration), OVERHAUL, or SAFETY. Drives scheduling logic, cost allocation, and OPEX reporting. Maps to SAP PM Order Type (AUART). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PM|CM|INSP|CALIB|OVERHAUL|SAFETY — promote to reference product]']
  parts_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of spare parts or material consumption line items recorded against this work order. Provides a quick header-level indicator of parts complexity and supports inventory replenishment trigger logic without requiring line-level joins.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which maintenance work is planned to be completed. Used for downtime window management, equipment availability planning, and SLA target setting. Maps to SAP PM Scheduled Finish Date (GLTRP).']
  planned_labor_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete the maintenance work order, as planned by the maintenance scheduler. Used for workforce capacity planning and budget forecasting. Maps to SAP PM Planned Work (ARBEI).']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which maintenance work is planned to commence, as set by the maintenance planner. Used for resource scheduling, parts pre-staging, and downtime planning. Maps to SAP PM Scheduled Start Date (GSTRP).']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned to the work order indicating urgency and scheduling precedence. 1=Emergency/Critical, 2=High, 3=Medium, 4=Low/Routine. Drives resource allocation and response time SLAs. Maps to SAP PM Priority (PRIOK).']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment fault or maintenance requirement was first reported by the operator or detected by telematics (John Deere Operations Center). Principal business event timestamp for the work order lifecycle. Maps to SAP PM Malfunction Start Date/Time (QMDAT/QMZEIT).']
  safety_permit_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the safety permit (lock-out/tag-out, confined space, hot work) issued for this maintenance work order. Required for OSHA compliance documentation and safety audit trails.']
  safety_permit_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a safety permit (e.g., lock-out/tag-out, confined space entry, chemical handling permit) is required before maintenance work can commence. Supports OSHA compliance and safety management workflows.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was formally signed off and closed by the authorizing supervisor. Marks the official end of the work order lifecycle and triggers cost settlement in SAP CO.']
  signed_off_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the supervisor or maintenance manager who formally approved and closed the work order. Required for quality assurance, regulatory compliance, and audit trail purposes. Maps to SAP PM Completed By (ERNAM).']
  total_maintenance_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total OPEX cost of the work order encompassing labor, parts, and external service charges. SSOT for maintenance cost reporting by asset, cost center, and crop enterprise. Maps to SAP CO total actual costs settled to the maintenance order (TKOSTEN).']

  Note: 'Corrective and preventive maintenance work orders for agricultural equipment managed through CMMS (e.g., SAP PM). Header captures work order number, order type (PM/CM/inspection), priority, asset reference, fault description, reported fault code, assigned technician, planned and actual start/end dates, labor hours, total maintenance cost (OPEX), work order status (open/in-progress/completed/cancelled), and sign-off details. Embedded parts consumption line items (replacing standalone parts_usage) capture part number, quantity used, unit cost, total cost, issue date, issuing warehouse/bin, and technician ID — SSOT for actual material consumption data supporting OPEX cost tracking, inventory replenishment triggers, and maintenance cost analysis by asset and crop enterprise. Core CMMS transactional record for all maintenance activities. Integrates with SAP MM goods issue and SAP CO cost allocation.'
}

Table equipment.maintenance_plan {
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the preventive maintenance (PM) plan record. Primary key for the maintenance_plan entity within the equipment domain SSOT.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Maintenance plan approval workflow requires linking the approving employee for OSHA compliance audit trails and accountability reporting. \'approved_by\' is a denormalized text field; a proper FK enable']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: maintenance_plan currently stores asset_category as a free-text STRING column, which is a denormalized reference to the asset_category lookup table. PM schedules in agricultural CMMS are defined at th']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment asset this plan is assigned to, when the plan is asset-specific rather than category-wide. Links to the equipment asset master record in the CMMS/SAP PM module.']
  billing_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_schedule. Business justification: Recurring OEM or third-party maintenance service contracts in agriculture are billed on a schedule. Linking maintenance_plan to billing_schedule enables automated invoice generation for contracted ser']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maintenance plans carry estimated costs that must be pre-assigned to GL accounts for budget planning and accrual posting. Agriculture finance requires GL account assignment on maintenance plans to gen']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: HACCP plans mandate equipment maintenance as prerequisite programs (PRPs) under FSMA Preventive Controls. Linking maintenance plans to the governing HACCP plan enables auditors to verify PRP complianc']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: MRO inventory planning: maintenance plans specify required materials (filters, lubricants, belts) that must be stocked. Linking maintenance_plan to material_master enables automated reorder triggering']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plant. Business justification: Maintenance plans in agriculture operations are scoped to a specific plant or maintenance location for seasonal scheduling, resource allocation, and compliance reporting. Without plant_id, maintenance']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Preventive maintenance plans in agriculture are often governed by OEM dealer service contracts or extended warranty agreements. Linking maintenance_plan to vendor_contract enables contract-driven PM s']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this maintenance plan was formally approved by the responsible maintenance manager or engineer. Establishes the official activation baseline for compliance and audit purposes.']
  auto_wo_generation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is configured to automatically generate work orders in SAP PM when the maintenance cycle due date or usage threshold is reached. When False, work orders must be created manually.']
  cmms_plan_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier linking this plan to the corresponding maintenance plan record in the CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System), such as SAP PM maintenance plan number or John Deere Operations Center service plan ID. Enables cross-system traceability.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code to which OPEX maintenance costs for this plan are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by farm unit, equipment type, or operational division.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this maintenance plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this plan (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency operations for international agricultural enterprises.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this maintenance plan becomes active and eligible to generate work orders. Aligns with asset commissioning date or seasonal equipment activation.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this maintenance plan expires or is decommissioned. Null indicates an open-ended plan with no scheduled end. Used for asset lifecycle management and plan version control.']
  epa_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan includes tasks subject to EPA regulatory requirements (e.g., sprayer calibration for pesticide application equipment, emissions checks, fluid disposal). Triggers environmental compliance documentation.']
  estimated_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor cost to execute one cycle of this maintenance plan, in the base currency. Calculated from estimated_labor_hours and applicable labor rates. Supports OPEX cost allocation.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total standard labor hours required to complete all tasks in this maintenance plan. Aggregated from individual task labor hour estimates. Used for workforce planning and OPEX budgeting.']
  last_completed_date date [note: 'Date the most recent maintenance cycle was completed and signed off. Used to calculate next_due_date and track compliance with PM schedules.']
  last_completed_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine/operating hour meter reading at the time the most recent maintenance cycle was completed. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics. Used for usage-based interval tracking.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the maintenance due date that a work order should be automatically generated, allowing time for parts procurement and labor scheduling. Supports seasonal readiness for planting and harvest windows.']
  maintenance_cycle_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance cycle interval. \'hours\' refers to engine/operating hours from John Deere Operations Center telematics; \'acres\'/\'hectares\' refer to field coverage metrics; calendar units support time-based strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|days|weeks|months|years|acres|hectares|kilometers — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maintenance_cycle_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric interval defining how frequently the maintenance plan recurs. Interpreted in conjunction with maintenance_cycle_unit (e.g., 250 engine hours, 12 months, 500 acres). Sourced from OEM specifications and SAP PM maintenance strategy.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next maintenance cycle is due based on last completion and the maintenance cycle interval. Drives scheduling dashboards and work order auto-generation in SAP PM.']
  next_due_hours decimal(10,1) [note: 'Engine/operating hour threshold at which the next maintenance cycle is due. Calculated as last_completed_hours plus the usage-based cycle interval. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics data.']
  oem_service_bulletin varchar [note: 'Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) service bulletin or technical service document number that this maintenance plan is based on or references (e.g., John Deere Technical Manual TM-XXXX). Ensures alignment with manufacturer specifications.']
  osha_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan includes tasks subject to OSHA regulatory requirements (e.g., lockout/tagout procedures, confined space entry, hazardous energy control). Triggers mandatory safety documentation on generated work orders.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the maintenance plan (e.g., \'Annual Combine Harvester PM\', \'250-Hour Tractor Engine Service\'). Used in operator dashboards and scheduling reports.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance plan, used in CMMS work order generation, SAP PM module references, and cross-system communication. Follows the PM-XXXXXX naming convention.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the maintenance plan. \'draft\' indicates plan is under development; \'active\' means plan is in use and generating work orders; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused; \'completed\' means all cycles fulfilled; \'cancelled\' means plan is no longer applicable.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the maintenance plan document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\'). Incremented when plan tasks, intervals, or compliance requirements are revised. Supports audit trail and change management per ISO 55000.']
  precision_ag_hardware boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan includes calibration or servicing tasks for precision agriculture hardware such as GPS/RTK receivers, VRA controllers, NDVI sensors, or auto-steer systems. Triggers specialized calibration task workflows.']
  rei_hours decimal(5,1) [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval in hours applicable to maintenance tasks performed on sprayer or pesticide application equipment. Specifies the minimum time after pesticide application before maintenance personnel may safely enter treated areas. Mandated by EPA/FIFRA.']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Minimum skill level required to execute the maintenance plan tasks. \'operator\' indicates basic tasks performable by equipment operators; \'certified_mechanic\' requires OEM-certified technicians. Drives workforce assignment in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  required_tools varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of specialized tools and equipment required to execute the maintenance plan tasks (e.g., torque wrench, hydraulic press, RTK calibration kit, diagnostic scanner). Supports tool reservation and kitting.']
  safety_precautions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of mandatory safety precautions required before and during execution of maintenance tasks in this plan (e.g., lockout/tagout procedures, PPE requirements, confined space protocols, chemical handling). Referenced in generated work orders.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Agricultural season associated with this maintenance plan when seasonal_flag is True. Drives scheduling alignment with the crop calendar to ensure equipment is ready for critical operational windows.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this maintenance plan is tied to a specific agricultural season (e.g., pre-planting inspection, post-harvest winterization). When True, scheduling is aligned with the crop calendar to ensure equipment readiness.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the maintenance strategy driving this plan. \'time_based\' triggers on calendar intervals (e.g., annually); \'usage_based\' triggers on engine hours or acreage; \'condition_based\' triggers on sensor thresholds (e.g., oil pressure); \'predictive\' uses IoT/telematics analytics from John Deere Operations Center.']
  task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual maintenance task line items defined within this plan (e.g., lubrication, filter replacement, calibration, inspection). Provides a quick summary of plan complexity for scheduling and labor estimation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this maintenance plan record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Preventive maintenance (PM) schedules and plans for agricultural equipment assets. Header defines maintenance strategy (time-based, usage-based, condition-based), maintenance cycle (e.g., every 250 engine hours, annually), applicable asset categories, and compliance requirements (OSHA, EPA, local equivalents). Embedded task list line items (replacing standalone maintenance_item) specify individual maintenance tasks including task type (lubrication, calibration, filter replacement, inspection, adjustment), task description, standard labor hours, required skill level, safety precautions (REI/OSHA), required tools, required parts/materials with quantities, and completion status. SSOT for all PM task definitions and maintenance scheduling. Linked to CMMS maintenance plans. Drives automatic work order generation and ensures equipment readiness for planting and harvest seasons.'
}

Table equipment.fault {
  fault_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment fault record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the equipment_fault data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete business event (fault occurrence) with its own lifecycle from detection through resolution.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural equipment asset (tractor, combine, planter, sprayer, irrigation system, etc.) that experienced the fault. Links to the equipment asset master record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment operator or field technician who reported the fault, when detection source is operator-reported. Links to the workforce/employee master. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Farm unit-level equipment reliability reporting, insurance claim filing, and operational risk assessment are named agricultural management processes. Domain experts expect fault/breakdown events to ro']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or land parcel where the equipment was operating at the time of fault detection. Supports geospatial analysis and field-level OEE impact assessment via Esri ArcGIS.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Equipment faults occur during specific field operations (e.g., a combine breakdown during harvesting, a sprayer fault during application). equipment_fault has field_id but no FK to the specific field_']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: Equipment faults are reported by growing season for crop insurance indemnity claims (RMA requires seasonal downtime documentation), seasonal equipment reliability analysis, and yield impact attributio']
  precision_device_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the onboard telematics unit (John Deere Operations Center MTG or equivalent) that transmitted the fault signal. Used for data lineage tracing from telematics source to fault record and for diagnosing telematics data quality issues.']
  telematics_reading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.telematics_reading. Business justification: An equipment fault detected via telematics should reference the specific telematics_reading record that triggered the DTC/fault detection. equipment_fault already has telematics_device_id (→ precision']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_order. Business justification: FSMA sanitary transport compliance and carrier performance reporting require direct traceability from a vehicle fault event to the transport order it disrupted. Direct FK enables incident reporting wi']
  warranty_vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Equipment faults flagged as warranty claims (fault.is_warranty_claim=true) are processed against the vendor warranty contract. Linking fault to vendor_contract enables warranty claim tracking, vendor ']
  acres_lost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of acres that could not be worked due to equipment downtime from this fault. Quantifies the production impact in the primary agricultural unit of measure. Used for OPEX impact analysis, crop planning adjustments, and insurance claims. Expressed in acres (US standard).']
  actual_repair_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual total cost in US dollars incurred to repair the fault, as confirmed upon SAP PM work order settlement. Includes labor hours, spare parts consumed, and external contractor charges. Used for OPEX reporting, cost center allocation, and maintenance budget variance analysis per GAAP/IFRS.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective maintenance action performed to resolve the fault, as recorded in the SAP PM work order completion. Includes parts replaced, adjustments made, software updates applied, or temporary workarounds implemented. Essential for maintenance history and warranty claims.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center code to which the repair costs for this fault are allocated. Enables OPEX tracking by farm unit, operational division, or geographic region. Supports financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS and SOX internal controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fault record was first created in the source system (SAP PM notification or John Deere Operations Center alert). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for data lineage and audit trail purposes in the Databricks silver layer.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the fault was first detected. Telematics = automatically detected via John Deere Operations Center or onboard ECU; Operator_Report = reported by equipment operator; Inspection = identified during scheduled preventive maintenance inspection; Sensor_Alert = triggered by IoT sensor threshold breach; Third_Party = reported by external service provider.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the fault was first detected, either by telematics system or reported by the operator. This is the principal real-world business event timestamp (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP). Used as the start reference for downtime duration calculations and OEE availability metrics.']
  downtime_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of equipment downtime in minutes, calculated from downtime_start_timestamp to downtime_end_timestamp. Stored as a business field (not a real-time derived metric) once the fault is closed. Core input for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability calculations and reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.']
  downtime_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment was returned to operational service following corrective action. Null if the fault is still open or equipment has not yet been restored. Used with downtime_start_timestamp to calculate total downtime duration for OEE availability metrics.']
  downtime_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the equipment became operationally unavailable due to the fault. May differ from detection_timestamp if the equipment continued limited operation after fault detection. Used directly in OEE availability calculations and production impact assessments.']
  dtc_code varchar [note: 'Standardized Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) as transmitted by the equipment\'s onboard telematics system (John Deere Operations Center, SAE J1939 protocol). Identifies the specific electronic control unit (ECU) fault condition. Used for automated fault classification and RCM analysis.']
  engine_hours_at_fault decimal(10,1) [note: 'Total cumulative engine hours recorded on the equipment at the time the fault occurred, as reported by the telematics system. Critical for condition-based maintenance (CBM) analysis, warranty validation, and mean time between failures (MTBF) calculations.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural equipment type that experienced the fault. Enables fault trend analysis by equipment category and supports fleet-level OEE benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tractor|combine|planter|sprayer|irrigation_system|harvester|tillage|other — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_repair_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost in US dollars to repair the fault, including labor, parts, and external service charges. Populated at fault creation for budget planning; updated with actuals upon work order completion. Supports OPEX cost allocation and maintenance budget management per GAAP/IFRS financial reporting.']
  fault_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the fault type used for reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis and spare parts planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|software|structural|operator_error|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the equipment fault, breakdown, or abnormal condition. May be auto-populated from DTC lookup table or manually entered by the operator/technician. Provides context for maintenance planning and root cause analysis.']
  fault_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the fault record, used in maintenance work orders, operator reports, and SAP PM notifications. Format: EF-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Serves as the human-readable reference across SAP S/4HANA PM, John Deere Operations Center, and field communications.']
  fault_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fault record from initial detection through resolution and closure. Drives workflow routing in SAP PM and maintenance scheduling. Open = detected, not yet actioned; In_Progress = corrective action underway; Resolved = fix applied; Closed = verified and closed; Recurring = fault has reoccurred after prior resolution.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the equipment\'s location at the time of fault detection, as recorded by the onboard GPS/RTK receiver. Enables geospatial fault mapping in Esri ArcGIS and field-level downtime analysis.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the equipment\'s location at the time of fault detection, as recorded by the onboard GPS/RTK receiver. Used with gps_latitude for geospatial fault mapping and field-level impact analysis.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fault poses a safety risk to the equipment operator or bystanders. Safety-critical faults trigger mandatory OSHA incident reporting workflows and immediate equipment lockout/tagout (LOTO) procedures. True = safety risk present; False = no immediate safety concern.']
  is_warranty_claim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this fault has been submitted or is eligible for a manufacturer warranty claim. True = warranty claim initiated or applicable; False = out-of-warranty or not eligible. Supports CAPEX/OPEX cost recovery tracking and vendor performance management.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fault record in the source system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks silver layer.']
  maintenance_team varchar [note: 'Name or code of the maintenance team or work center responsible for resolving this fault, as assigned in SAP PM. Examples: internal_fleet_team, dealer_service, oem_field_technician. Supports workload balancing and maintenance team performance KPI reporting.']
  odometer_km_at_fault decimal(10,1) [note: 'Total cumulative distance in kilometers recorded on the equipment odometer at the time of fault, as reported by telematics. Applicable to mobile equipment (tractors, combines, sprayers). Used alongside engine hours for condition-based maintenance scheduling and warranty claims.']
  recurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this same fault (same DTC code or fault category on the same asset) has recurred within the current operating season. A value greater than 1 indicates a recurring fault pattern requiring escalated RCM investigation. Supports chronic failure identification and maintenance strategy optimization.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fault was confirmed as resolved and the equipment returned to full operational service. Distinct from downtime_end_timestamp (which marks when equipment restarted) — resolution_timestamp marks formal closure confirmation in SAP PM. Used for mean time to repair (MTTR) calculations.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized root cause classification code assigned after fault investigation, aligned with SAP PM cause catalog and ISO 14224 failure taxonomy. Examples: wear_and_tear, improper_maintenance, manufacturing_defect, operator_misuse, environmental_damage, age_related_failure. Used for RCM analysis and preventive maintenance optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wear_and_tear|improper_maintenance|manufacturing_defect|operator_misuse|environmental_damage|age_related_failure|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the identified root cause of the fault following investigation. Complements root_cause_code with detailed findings from the technician or maintenance engineer. Supports knowledge base development and recurring fault prevention.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the fault indicating operational impact and urgency of corrective action. Critical = immediate equipment stoppage; High = significant performance degradation; Medium = reduced efficiency; Low = minor issue, can continue operating. Drives maintenance prioritization and OEE availability calculations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this fault record originated. SAP_PM = SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance notification; JD_OPS_CENTER = John Deere Operations Center telematics alert; TRIMBLE = Trimble Ag Software field report; MANUAL = manually entered; CLIMATE_FIELDVIEW = Climate FieldView event. Supports data lineage and silver layer reconciliation.']
  yield_impact_bu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated yield loss in bushels (BU) attributable to the equipment downtime caused by this fault. Calculated based on acres lost and expected yield per acre (YPM). Supports crop loss reporting, insurance claims, and financial impact assessment. BU (Bushel) is the standard grain yield unit per USDA WASDE reporting.']

  Note: 'Record of equipment faults, breakdowns, and diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) detected via telematics or reported by operators. Captures fault code, fault description, severity level, detection source (telematics/operator/inspection), asset affected, field/location at time of fault, downtime start/end, estimated production impact (acres lost, yield impact), root cause classification, and corrective action taken. Feeds into OEE availability calculations and reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.'
}

Table equipment.parts_inventory {
  parts_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each spare part or consumable record in the MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) inventory master. Primary key for the parts_inventory data product.']
  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: parts_inventory currently stores equipment_category as a free-text STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the asset_category classification hierarchy. Adding asset_category_id as a FK to asset_c']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Parts inventory is physically stocked at specific facilities (maintenance shops co-located at grain elevators or processing plants). Linking parts_inventory to supply.facility enables inventory visibi']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Parts inventory valuation is posted to GL accounts (spare parts inventory asset account on receipt; maintenance expense on consumption). Agriculture operations require this for inventory balance sheet']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Agricultural spare parts and consumables (filters, lubricants, belts) are catalogued in the input catalog. Linking parts_inventory to input_catalog enables catalog-driven reorder pricing, unified mate']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Spare parts are product master records in ERP/CMMS systems. Linking parts_inventory to product.master enables unified product costing, valuation, procurement catalog management, and BOM integration. A']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Parts inventory management requires linking spare parts stock to the material master for unified procurement valuation, hazmat classification, reorder management, and SAP inventory integration. Agricu']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plant. Business justification: In agriculture maintenance operations, spare parts inventory is managed at the plant level for MRP, stock replenishment, and inter-plant transfers. parts_inventory.plant_code is a denormalized plain-t']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Parts procurement is governed by vendor supply agreements specifying pricing tiers, lead times, and MOQs. Maintenance planners reference the active vendor contract when replenishing parts to ensure co']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary vendor/supplier from whom this part is procured. Links to the supplier master for automated purchase requisition routing and supplier performance tracking in SAP MM procurement.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC analysis classification of the part based on annual consumption value: A = high-value/high-priority (top 80% of spend), B = medium-value (next 15%), C = low-value (bottom 5%). Drives differentiated inventory control policies, cycle count frequencies, and safety stock levels in SAP MM.']
  annual_consumption_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of this part consumed in the trailing 12-month period, expressed in the base unit of measure. Used as the primary input for reorder point and safety stock calculations, and for identifying slow-moving inventory candidates for write-down.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'SAP AM (Asset Management) asset class code indicating whether this part is capitalized as a CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) item or expensed as OPEX (Operational Expenditure). Drives accounting treatment and depreciation scheduling for high-value replacement components.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or rack location within the storage facility where this part is physically located (e.g., \'A-12-03\' = Aisle A, Rack 12, Shelf 3). Managed by Manhattan Associates WMS for precise pick-and-put-away operations.']
  compatible_asset_models varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific equipment model numbers or model families this part is compatible with (e.g., \'John Deere 8R 410, John Deere 8R 370, Case IH Magnum 380\'). Enables technicians to quickly identify applicable parts during field maintenance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO (Controlling) cost center code to which the cost of this part is allocated when issued against a maintenance work order. Enables OPEX (Operational Expenditure) tracking by farm, equipment fleet, or business unit for financial reporting and budgeting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this parts inventory master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per SOX internal controls.']
  criticality_classification varchar [note: 'Operational criticality rating of the part: critical (stockout causes immediate equipment downtime during planting or harvest), non_critical (equipment can operate temporarily without), or safety_critical (required for operator safety per OSHA standards). Drives safety stock levels and emergency procurement protocols.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the unit cost and total stock value are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency operations for global agricultural enterprises.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part or consumable is classified as a hazardous material requiring special storage, handling, or disposal procedures (e.g., hydraulic fluids, battery acid, pesticide concentrates). When True, triggers compliance with EPA and OSHA hazardous materials regulations and requires an associated Safety Data Sheet (SDS).']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the part record in the inventory master: active (stocked and available), inactive (temporarily not stocked), obsolete (superseded by a newer part number), discontinued (no longer available from OEM), or on_order (replenishment in progress). Governs whether the part can be issued against work orders.']
  last_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which this part was most recently issued from inventory to a maintenance work order or field technician. Used to identify slow-moving or obsolete inventory and support inventory rationalization decisions.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count for this part. Used to assess inventory accuracy, identify shrinkage, and schedule next count per the annual physical inventory plan. Aligns with SAP MM inventory management cycle count functionality.']
  last_receipt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent goods receipt (GRN) for this part from a supplier. Supports inventory freshness analysis, supplier delivery performance tracking, and shelf-life management for perishable consumables.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order (PO) placement to goods receipt (GRN) at the warehouse. Critical input for reorder point calculation to ensure parts availability during planting and harvest seasons.']
  maximum_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The upper inventory limit for this part, beyond which overstocking is flagged. Used in SAP MM min/max replenishment planning to prevent excess capital tied up in slow-moving MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) inventory.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'The original equipment manufacturer\'s (OEM) part number as published in the equipment service manual or parts catalog. Used for cross-referencing with supplier catalogs, warranty claims, and ordering from authorized dealers (e.g., John Deere, CNH Industrial, AGCO).']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the spare part by functional category. Drives inventory segmentation, procurement routing, and storage requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|pneumatic|consumable|lubricant|filter|wear_part|structural|electronic — promote to reference product]']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the spare part including specifications, dimensions, material composition, and application context. Supports accurate identification during procurement and maintenance activities.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name or title of the spare part or consumable (e.g., \'Air Filter Element\', \'Hydraulic Pump Seal Kit\', \'Combine Header Drive Belt\'). Used for display in work orders, purchase requisitions, and inventory reports.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Distinguishes the nature of the inventory item: spare_part (replacement component), consumable (single-use item such as oil or filter), tool (maintenance tooling), assembly (multi-component unit), or sub_assembly (partial assembly). Drives MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) inventory policy and cost allocation between CAPEX and OPEX.']
  reorder_point decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum stock quantity threshold at which a replenishment purchase requisition is automatically triggered in SAP MM MRP (Material Requirements Planning). Calculated based on average daily consumption during peak seasons (planting/harvest) and supplier lead time to prevent equipment downtime.']
  reorder_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard replenishment order quantity triggered when stock falls to or below the reorder point. Optimized to balance procurement costs, storage capacity, and supplier minimum order quantities (MOQ). Drives automated purchase requisition creation in SAP MM.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained above the reorder point to absorb demand variability and supply disruptions during critical agricultural seasons (planting, harvest). Particularly important for critical-classified parts where stockout causes immediate equipment downtime.']
  sds_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this hazardous part or consumable. Required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) for all hazardous chemicals stored in the workplace. Null for non-hazardous parts.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum storage duration in days before the part or consumable degrades or expires (applicable to lubricants, chemicals, rubber seals, and other perishable MRO items). Null for non-perishable parts. Supports FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.']
  stock_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current on-hand stock quantity of the part expressed in the base unit of measure (UOM). Represents unrestricted-use stock available for maintenance work orders. Updated in real time via goods receipts (GRN) and goods issues from SAP MM or Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP MM storage location code identifying the warehouse or farm facility where this part is physically stored (e.g., \'WH01\' = Central Parts Warehouse, \'FRM-NORTH\' = North Farm Shop). Supports multi-site inventory management across farm operations.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own catalog or part number for this item, as used in purchase orders (PO) and goods receipt notes (GRN). Enables accurate ordering and receiving reconciliation with supplier invoices and packing lists.']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of current on-hand stock, calculated as stock_quantity multiplied by unit_cost. Represents the balance sheet carrying value of MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) inventory for financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS. Refreshed upon each inventory transaction.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of measure for this part, expressed in the local currency. Used for inventory valuation, OPEX/CAPEX cost allocation to maintenance work orders, and total stock value calculation. Sourced from SAP MM material master valuation data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The base unit of measure (UOM) in which the part is stocked and transacted (e.g., EA = each, KG = kilogram, L = liter, M = meter, SET = set, BOX = box). Aligns with SAP MM base UOM configuration and drives quantity calculations for reorder and valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|SET|BOX|ROLL|PAIR|GAL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this parts inventory master record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing (delta loads) and change audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'The cost accounting method used to value this part\'s inventory: standard_cost (fixed standard price), moving_average (weighted average updated on each receipt), fifo (first-in first-out), or lifo (last-in first-out). Configured in SAP MM material master and governs financial reporting treatment.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'OEM or supplier warranty duration in months from the date of installation or purchase. Supports warranty claim tracking and cost recovery from suppliers for premature part failures. Null if no warranty applies.']

  Note: 'Inventory master for spare parts, consumables, and replacement components held for agricultural equipment maintenance. Captures part number (material number), part description, OEM part number, compatible asset models/categories, unit of measure, current stock quantity, reorder point, reorder quantity, storage location (bin/warehouse), unit cost, total stock value, lead time from supplier, and criticality classification (critical/non-critical). Supports MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) inventory management, prevents equipment downtime due to parts stockouts during planting and harvest seasons, and integrates with procurement for automated replenishment.'
}

Table equipment.field_operation {
  field_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each field-level equipment operation record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the field_operation data product.']
  asset_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_assignment. Business justification: field_operation records the actual execution of equipment work in the field, while asset_assignment is the governing planning record that authorizes equipment use for a specific field, operator, and c']
  conservation_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.conservation_practice. Business justification: Field operations implementing conservation practices (cover crop seeding, contour tillage, filter strip maintenance) must be linked to the NRCS conservation_practice record for cost-share payment docu']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Field operations (planting, spraying, harvesting) are executed by crews in agriculture. Linking field_operation to crew enables crew-level labor cost allocation, H2A compliance reporting by crew, and ']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: Field operations are executed against a crop enterprise budget — linking them enables actual vs. budgeted cost tracking per operation type (planting, spraying, harvest). Agriculture farm managers requ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce employee or contractor who performed the field operation. Used for labor cost allocation, operator certification compliance (OSHA, pesticide applicator licensing), and performance analytics.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: Field operations execute against specific field crop plans (tillage, application, harvest passes). Linking equipment_field_operation to field_crop_plan enables plan-vs-actual operational reporting, ag']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific field or parcel where the operation was conducted. Links to the land and soil domain for GIS-based spatial analysis, crop rotation planning, and GLOBALG.A.P. field registration.']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the growing season during which this operation was performed. Enables seasonal aggregation of as-applied records, yield correlation analysis, and crop plan compliance verification.']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Field operations apply inputs defined in the input_catalog (PHI, REI, MRL, EPA registration). Linking field_operation to input_catalog enables real-time compliance verification of application rates ag']
  irrigation_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.irrigation_zone. Business justification: Irrigation field operations (pivot runs, drip events) are executed against specific irrigation zones. Water use reporting, water right permit compliance, and irrigation scheduling all require linking ']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Variable-rate field operations (seeding, fertilizer, chemical application) are planned and executed at management zone level. Zone-level input tracking and yield attribution reports require linking ea']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seed, agrochemical, or fertilizer product applied during this operation. Nullable for non-application operations (e.g., tillage, scouting). Links to agrochemical_product or material_master for MRL compliance and FSMA traceability.']
  precision_device_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.precision_device. Business justification: Field operations in precision agriculture are directly executed using specific precision devices (GPS/RTK receivers, VRA controllers, section control units). Linking equipment_field_operation directly']
  primary_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary equipment asset (tractor, combine, sprayer, planter, irrigation system) used in this operation. Links to the equipment asset master for OEE tracking and CAPEX/OPEX cost allocation per ISO 55000.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Field operations consume procured inputs (seeds, chemicals, fertilizers). Linking field_operation to the sourcing purchase_order enables input traceability from procurement through field application —']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Harvest field operations fulfill specific forward sales contracts in grain farming. Contract fulfillment tracking requires linking each harvest run to the sales contract it delivers against, enabling ']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Harvest and application field operations are scheduled to fulfill specific sales orders. Agriculture operations managers track which field operations (harvest runs) fulfill which sales orders to ensur']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Planting field operations must record the specific seed variety planted for variety performance analysis, yield attribution, and seed lot traceability. Agronomists and seed dealers require variety-lev']
  soil_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.soil_sample. Business justification: Fertilizer and lime application operations are directly triggered by soil sample recommendations (nitrogen_recommendation_lbs_ac, lime_recommendation_lbs_ac). Nutrient management plans and 4R complian']
  water_right_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.water_right. Business justification: Irrigation field operations must be traceable to the authorizing water right permit for annual water use reporting, curtailment compliance, and state water agency audits. Regulators in prior-appropria']
  workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: EPA WPS and state pesticide laws require pesticide application field operations to be performed by a licensed applicator. Linking to the specific workforce_certification record enables automated licen']
  application_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of product applied per unit area (e.g., oz/acre, lbs/acre, gallons/acre, seeds/acre). As-applied value from VRA controller or flow meter. Compared against VRA prescription for compliance and efficacy analysis.']
  application_rate_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the application_rate field. Ensures dimensional consistency across operations involving different product types (seeds, liquids, granulars). Required for MRL compliance calculations and FSMA records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oz/acre|lbs/acre|gal/acre|seeds/acre|kg/ha|L/ha|mt/acre — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  area_covered_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total field area covered during the operation, measured in acres. Sourced from GPS track data (John Deere Operations Center / Trimble RTK). Used for application rate normalization, cost-per-acre analysis, and USDA FSA reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center to which the operational costs (fuel, labor, product) of this field operation are allocated. Enables OPEX tracking by farm unit, crop type, or management zone for financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this field operation record was first created in the system, either via telematics auto-ingestion or manual entry. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and FSMA record-keeping compliance.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the field operation was completed or stopped. Used with start_timestamp to compute operation duration, machine utilization, and OEE metrics.']
  equipment_hours_used decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total machine hours logged by the primary asset during this operation, sourced from John Deere Operations Center engine hour meter. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling (PM triggers), OEE calculation, and CAPEX depreciation tracking.']
  fuel_consumed_gal decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total fuel consumed by the equipment during the operation, in US gallons. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics. Used for OPEX cost allocation, carbon footprint reporting (ISCC), and equipment efficiency benchmarking.']
  gps_track_file_ref varchar [note: 'File path or URI reference to the GPS/RTK track file recorded during the operation (Trimble .agdata, Climate FieldView .shp, or John Deere Operations Center export). Supports precision agriculture audit trails, pass efficiency calculation, and Esri ArcGIS spatial analysis.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the seed, chemical, or fertilizer product applied. Critical for FSMA forward/backward traceability, product recall management, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) linkage. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue records.']
  ndvi_reference decimal(5,4) [note: 'NDVI value of the field at the time of operation, sourced from satellite or drone imagery (Esri ArcGIS / Climate FieldView). Used to correlate operation timing with crop health status and validate VRA prescription zones.']
  operation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the field operation was performed. Used for day-level scheduling, PHI/REI compliance countdown, weather correlation, and FSMA as-applied record dating.']
  operation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operator or agronomist observations recorded during or after the field operation (e.g., equipment issues, unusual field conditions, pest observations). Supports scouting follow-up and SOP deviation documentation.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the field operation, sourced from John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag Software. Used for cross-system traceability and FSMA audit trail references. Format: FOP-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  operation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the field operation. Drives operational dashboards, work order closure in SAP PM, and FSMA as-applied record completeness checks.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural field operation performed. Determines applicable compliance rules (e.g., PHI/REI for spraying, FSMA traceability for planting/harvesting) and links to relevant crop management records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planting|spraying|tillage|harvesting|irrigation|fertilization|scouting|seeding|cultivation|cover_crop — promote to reference product]']
  pass_efficiency_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of effective area covered to total area traversed, expressed as a percentage. Measures overlap and skip rates from GPS track analysis. Key precision agriculture KPI for equipment calibration and operator performance.']
  phi_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required Pre-Harvest Interval was observed between the last chemical application and the scheduled harvest date. Critical for MRL compliance, FSMA food safety records, and GLOBALG.A.P. certification audits.']
  rei_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Restricted Entry Interval was enforced after pesticide application, preventing unauthorized field re-entry. Required for OSHA worker protection standard compliance and EPA FIFRA label adherence.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system from which this field operation record was ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements for FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping.']
  speed_avg_mph decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average ground speed of the equipment during the operation in miles per hour, derived from John Deere Operations Center telematics. Used to assess application uniformity, operator compliance with label speed requirements, and equipment performance benchmarking.']
  spray_window_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operation was conducted within the approved spray window conditions (wind speed, temperature, inversion risk) as defined by EPA label requirements and IPM protocols. Populated for spraying operations; null for non-spray operations.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the field operation commenced, recorded from John Deere Operations Center telematics or operator log. Principal business event timestamp for operation duration calculation and FSMA traceability records.']
  target_area_acres decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned area to be covered as specified in the field operation work order or crop plan. Compared against area_covered_acres to compute pass efficiency and identify coverage gaps.']
  total_product_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for total_product_used. Aligns with the product label unit and SAP MM material master UOM for inventory reconciliation and FSMA lot traceability.
