[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oz|lbs|gal|seeds|kg|L|mt|bu — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_product_used decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total quantity of product consumed during the operation (application_rate × area_covered_acres, adjusted for actual flow meter readings). Used for inventory depletion in SAP MM, cost accounting, and chemical usage reporting to EPA/state agencies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this field operation record. Supports Silver Layer change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit trail requirements for FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. record integrity.']

  Note: 'Records of field-level equipment operations including planting, spraying, tillage, harvesting, and irrigation runs. Captures operation type, asset used, operator, field/parcel reference, start/end datetime, total area covered (acres), application rate (for VRA operations), product applied (seed/chemical/fertilizer), pass efficiency, GPS track file reference (Trimble/Climate FieldView), fuel consumed, and operation status. SSOT for as-applied and as-planted records supporting FSMA traceability and precision agriculture audit trails.'
}

Table equipment.precision_device {
  precision_device_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each precision agriculture hardware device record in the master registry. Primary key for the precision_device entity.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent equipment asset (tractor, combine, sprayer, planter, irrigation system, etc.) to which this precision device is attached or integrated. Supports asset-device association for telematics and precision agriculture hardware lifecycle management.']
  calibration_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Precision agriculture calibration compliance (ISO 11783/FMIS standards) requires tracking which certified employee performed each calibration. \'calibration_technician\' is a denormalized text field; a ']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Precision agriculture devices (RTK receivers, VRA controllers, yield monitors) are capitalized fixed assets with individual depreciation schedules. Linking precision_device to fixed_asset enables depr']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Precision devices are acquired via purchase orders. The precision_device has a denormalized po_number string. Linking to procurement.purchase_order enables full procurement-to-deployment traceability ']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Precision ag devices (GPS receivers, VRA controllers, section controllers) are procured from specific vendors. Linking precision_device to procurement.vendor enables vendor performance tracking for pr']
  accuracy_class varchar [note: 'Standardized accuracy classification tier for the precision device, enabling fleet-level reporting and field assignment decisions. Sub-inch RTK devices are required for high-precision VRA and planting operations.']
  accuracy_specification varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-stated positional or measurement accuracy specification for the device (e.g., \'<2.5 cm RTK\', \'<1 inch RTK sub-inch\', \'±0.5% flow rate\'). Critical for VRA prescription compliance, RTK guidance validation, and GLOBALG.A.P. precision agriculture certification.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Internal fixed asset tag or barcode number affixed to the physical device for inventory control and asset audits. Corresponds to the asset tag in SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Accounting (FI-AA) and physical inventory records.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required calibration events for this device, as specified by the OEM or internal calibration policy. Used to auto-calculate next_calibration_date and generate preventive maintenance schedules.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration compliance status of the precision device. \'due_soon\' is triggered when next_calibration_date is within the lead-time threshold. \'overdue\' blocks device use in compliance-sensitive operations per GLOBALG.A.P. and FSMA requirements.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary data communication protocol used by the precision device to transmit data to the FMIS, telematics platform, or ISOBUS terminal. Determines integration architecture and data pipeline design. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISOBUS|CAN_bus|NMEA_0183|NMEA_2000|RS232|Bluetooth|WiFi|cellular_4G|cellular_5G|LoRaWAN — promote to reference product]']
  correction_source varchar [note: 'Differential correction signal source used by GPS/RTK receivers to achieve sub-inch or sub-decimeter accuracy (e.g., RTK base station, WAAS, John Deere StarFire, Trimble CenterPoint RTX). Determines achievable accuracy class and subscription cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RTK_base_station|SBAS|WAAS|OmniSTAR|StarFire|CenterPoint_RTX|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the precision device record was first created in the master registry. Provides audit trail for data governance and ISO 55000 asset lifecycle management compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the purchase_cost and any financial values on this record (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'CAD\'). Required for multi-currency CAPEX reporting.']
  data_output_format varchar [note: 'Primary data output format generated by the precision device for integration with FMIS platforms, GIS systems, and agronomic analytics tools (e.g., ISOXML for ISOBUS-compliant devices, shapefile for GIS integration with Esri ArcGIS).']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the precision device was permanently removed from service and decommissioned. Marks the end of the device\'s operational lifecycle. Null for active devices. Required for asset lifecycle reporting and CAPEX write-off processing.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the precision device for operational identification (e.g., \'RTK Receiver - Combine 4\', \'NDVI Sensor - Field 12 Sprayer\'). Supports field crew identification and FMIS integration.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the precision device. Used for warranty tracking, calibration records, and hardware lifecycle management. Sourced from device nameplate or manufacturer documentation.']
  device_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the precision device. Drives maintenance scheduling, calibration compliance alerts, and asset utilization reporting. \'calibration_due\' triggers workflow in CMMS/FMIS.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the precision agriculture hardware device by functional category. Determines applicable calibration standards, accuracy specifications, and integration protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gps_rtk_receiver|vra_controller|ndvi_sensor|yield_monitor|flow_meter|section_control_module|isobus_terminal|guidance_display|soil_ec_sensor|variable_rate_controller — promote to reference product]']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Current firmware version installed on the precision device (e.g., \'3.12.1\'). Critical for compatibility validation with ISOBUS terminals, VRA controllers, and FMIS platforms. Tracked to ensure OEM-recommended firmware levels are maintained.']
  fmis_device_code varchar [note: 'External device identifier assigned by the connected FMIS platform (e.g., John Deere Operations Center device ID, Climate FieldView device ID, Trimble Ag Software device ID). Used for cross-system data reconciliation and telematics data linkage.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the precision device was physically installed on or integrated with the parent equipment asset. Marks the start of the device\'s operational lifecycle on that asset. Used for warranty period calculation and CAPEX depreciation tracking.']
  ip_rating varchar [note: 'IEC 60529 Ingress Protection rating indicating the device\'s resistance to dust and water (e.g., \'IP67\' for dust-tight and immersion-resistant). Critical for field deployment suitability assessment in agricultural environments.']
  isobus_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the precision device holds official ISOBUS (ISO 11783) certification, enabling plug-and-play interoperability with ISOBUS-compliant tractors and implements. Required for VRA controller and section control module compatibility validation.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent calibration of the precision device was performed. Used to calculate calibration compliance status and trigger next calibration scheduling. Mandatory for GLOBALG.A.P. and FSMA traceability compliance.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the precision device (e.g., Trimble, John Deere, Raven Industries, Ag Leader, Topcon). Used for warranty management, technical support escalation, and procurement analytics.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned model or part number for the precision device (e.g., \'Trimble NAV-900\', \'Raven Viper 4+\'). Used for spare parts procurement, firmware compatibility checks, and OEM service bulletin tracking.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the next calibration of the precision device must be completed to maintain compliance. Calculated from last_calibration_date plus calibration_interval_days. Drives CMMS preventive maintenance work order generation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, known issues, configuration remarks, or special handling instructions related to the precision device. Used by field technicians and precision agriculture specialists for contextual documentation.']
  operating_temp_max_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Maximum ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at which the precision device is rated to operate reliably. Used for summer deployment planning and heat-stress operational risk assessment in field conditions.']
  operating_temp_min_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Minimum ambient temperature in degrees Celsius at which the precision device is rated to operate reliably. Used for seasonal deployment planning and cold-climate operational risk assessment.']
  power_source varchar [note: 'Primary power supply method for the precision device. Determines installation requirements, field operational constraints, and maintenance considerations for remote or battery-powered sensors.']
  purchase_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Original acquisition cost of the precision device in the operating currency. Used for CAPEX tracking, asset depreciation calculations, and ROI analysis of precision agriculture hardware investments.']
  purchase_date date [note: 'Date the precision device was purchased or procured. Used as the anchor date for warranty period calculation, CAPEX depreciation schedules, and asset lifecycle reporting.']
  section_count varchar [note: 'Number of independently controllable sections on a section control module or VRA controller (e.g., 12-section boom sprayer, 24-row planter). Determines granularity of variable rate application and overlap reduction capability.']
  signal_frequency_mhz decimal(10,4) [note: 'Operating signal frequency in megahertz for GPS/RTK receivers and radio-based precision devices (e.g., 1575.42 MHz for GPS L1, 1227.60 MHz for GPS L2). Used for interference analysis and regulatory spectrum compliance with FCC/USDA requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the precision device record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']
  warranty_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the manufacturer warranty for the precision device expires. Used to determine whether repair costs are covered under warranty or charged to OPEX. Triggers procurement alerts for extended warranty decisions.']

  Note: 'Master registry of precision agriculture hardware devices attached to or integrated with equipment assets. Includes GPS/RTK receivers, VRA controllers, NDVI sensors, yield monitors, flow meters, section control modules, and ISOBUS terminals. Captures device type, manufacturer, model, serial number, firmware version, calibration date, calibration status, accuracy specification (RTK sub-inch), communication protocol, parent asset assignment, and activation status. Supports precision agriculture hardware lifecycle management and calibration compliance.'
}

Table equipment.asset_inspection {
  asset_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each asset inspection record. Primary key for the asset_inspection data product within the equipment domain.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural equipment asset (tractor, combine, planter, sprayer, irrigation system, etc.) that was inspected. Links to the equipment asset master record.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.corrective_action. Business justification: Equipment inspections finding deficiencies on food-contact or CCP equipment generate quality CAPAs under FSMA prerequisite programs. Linking asset_inspection to the resulting corrective_action support']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce record of the individual who conducted the inspection. May be an internal technician, certified safety officer, or third-party inspector.']
  fault_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.fault. Business justification: An asset inspection is frequently triggered by or discovers a specific equipment fault (DTC, breakdown, safety deficiency). Adding fault_id to asset_inspection creates a direct traceable link between ']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Pesticide applicator equipment inspections and sprayer calibration checks are conducted in-field per EPA and state regulatory requirements. Recording which field the inspection occurred in supports co']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Equipment inspections conducted as HACCP plan verification activities (e.g., CCP equipment checks, sanitation verification) must reference the governing HACCP plan. This is a named FSMA Preventive Con']
  inspector_workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: DOT/OSHA/EPA compliance requires verifying the inspector held a valid certification at inspection time. \'inspector_certification_number\' is a denormalized text field; a FK to workforce_certification e']
  maintenance_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.maintenance_plan. Business justification: Scheduled asset inspections in agricultural equipment management are typically triggered by preventive maintenance plans (e.g., a 500-hour PM plan includes a safety inspection checklist). asset_inspec']
  primary_asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance work order associated with this inspection, if the inspection was triggered by or resulted in a corrective or preventive maintenance work order in the CMMS.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Third-party inspection services (DOT compliance, EPA equipment inspections) are performed under vendor service contracts. Linking asset_inspection to vendor_contract enables contract utilization track']
  asset_grounded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset has been taken out of service (grounded) as a result of this inspection. A True value means the asset must not be operated until corrective actions are completed and a re-inspection passes.']
  asset_hours_at_inspection decimal(10,1) [note: 'The cumulative engine or operating hours recorded on the asset at the time of inspection, as read from the telematics system (John Deere Operations Center) or hour meter. Critical for hour-based maintenance interval tracking per ISO 55000 and OEM service schedules.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory compliance certificate issued as a result of this inspection expires. Drives the scheduling of the next mandatory inspection to maintain continuous compliance.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The official certificate or compliance document number issued by the regulatory authority or internal compliance function upon successful inspection. Used for regulatory audit trails and insurance documentation.']
  checklist_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual checklist items evaluated during the inspection. Provides a completeness indicator and supports audit verification that all required inspection points were assessed.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required or taken to address deficiencies identified during the inspection. May reference specific repair procedures, parts replacement, or regulatory remediation steps.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether corrective actions are mandated as a result of this inspection. True triggers the creation of a corrective maintenance work order or formal corrective action plan in the CMMS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the inspection record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  critical_deficiency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more critical safety or regulatory deficiencies were identified during the inspection. A True value typically triggers immediate asset grounding, mandatory corrective action, and escalation to the safety or compliance officer.']
  deficiency_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of all deficiencies, non-conformances, or safety hazards identified during the inspection. Captures the nature, location, and severity of each issue found. Used for corrective action planning and regulatory reporting.']
  dot_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether DOT roadworthiness and transport safety requirements were specifically verified as part of this inspection, applicable to road-mobile agricultural equipment and transport vehicles.']
  epa_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EPA environmental compliance requirements (e.g., engine emissions, fuel system integrity, chemical containment) were specifically verified as part of this inspection.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the inspection was conducted. This is the principal real-world business event date for the inspection record and is used for regulatory compliance tracking and certificate validity calculations.']
  inspection_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the inspector completed all checklist evaluations and closed the inspection. Used for duration calculation and audit trail purposes.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'The physical location or functional location code where the inspection was conducted, such as a farm field identifier, workshop bay, or GPS-referenced field location. Aligns with SAP PM functional location hierarchy and Esri ArcGIS field mapping.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'The primary method used to conduct the inspection. Visual inspections rely on direct observation; operational tests involve running the equipment through functional checks; instrument measurement uses calibrated tools (e.g., emissions analyser, torque wrench); document review verifies maintenance records and certifications; combined uses multiple methods.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the inspector to record additional observations, contextual information, or recommendations that do not fit within the structured checklist items. Supports comprehensive documentation for audit and insurance purposes.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the inspection record, used in regulatory submissions, insurance documentation, and CMMS cross-referencing. Typically generated by SAP QM or the CMMS system.']
  inspection_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the inspector began conducting the inspection. Used for duration calculation and audit trail purposes.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record. Scheduled indicates the inspection is planned but not yet started; in_progress indicates the inspector is actively conducting the inspection; completed indicates all checklist items have been evaluated and the record is closed; cancelled indicates the inspection was voided; pending_reinspection indicates deficiencies were found requiring a follow-up inspection.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by its regulatory or operational purpose. Pre-season inspections verify readiness before planting or harvest campaigns; OSHA safety inspections verify compliance with occupational safety standards; EPA emissions inspections verify engine and exhaust compliance; DOT roadworthiness inspections verify road-legal status for transport; insurance inspections support coverage underwriting; post-repair inspections verify corrective action effectiveness; ad-hoc inspections are unscheduled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_season|osha_safety|epa_emissions|dot_roadworthiness|insurance|post_repair|ad_hoc — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspector who conducted the inspection. Internal indicates a company-employed technician or safety officer; third_party indicates an independent certified inspector engaged by the company; regulatory_authority indicates an inspector from a government body such as OSHA, EPA, or DOT.']
  items_failed_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual checklist items that received a fail evaluation during the inspection. A non-zero value triggers deficiency documentation and corrective action requirements.']
  items_passed_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual checklist items that received a pass evaluation during the inspection. Used in conjunction with checklist_item_count and items_failed_count to assess inspection completeness and asset condition.']
  next_scheduled_inspection_date date [note: 'The calculated or manually assigned date for the next scheduled inspection of this asset, based on regulatory intervals, OEM recommendations, or the preventive maintenance plan. Drives the forward maintenance calendar.']
  osha_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether OSHA occupational safety requirements were specifically verified as part of this inspection. Supports OSHA compliance reporting and audit readiness under 29 CFR 1910 and 29 CFR 1928 (agricultural operations).']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'The aggregate pass/fail determination for the entire inspection. Pass indicates all checklist items met required standards; fail indicates one or more critical items did not meet standards; conditional_pass indicates minor deficiencies noted but asset is operationally cleared with corrective actions required; incomplete indicates the inspection was not fully executed.']
  regulatory_certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory compliance certificate (e.g., OSHA safety certificate, DOT roadworthiness certificate, EPA emissions certificate) was issued as a result of this inspection passing.']
  reinspection_due_date date [note: 'The date by which a follow-up re-inspection must be completed to verify that corrective actions have been implemented and deficiencies resolved. Mandatory when overall_result is fail or conditional_pass.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the inspection was scheduled to occur, as defined in the preventive maintenance plan or regulatory compliance calendar.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the inspection record was most recently modified. Supports data lineage tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and ad-hoc inspection records for agricultural equipment assets covering safety, regulatory, and operational readiness checks. Captures inspection type (pre-season, OSHA safety, EPA emissions, DOT roadworthiness, insurance), inspection date, inspector identity, asset inspected, checklist items evaluated, pass/fail per item, overall inspection result, deficiencies noted, corrective actions required, re-inspection due date, and regulatory certificate issued. Supports OSHA compliance, insurance requirements, and ISO 55000 asset condition monitoring.'
}

Table equipment.asset_assignment {

Business justification: Custom hire equipment assignments for regulated operations (pesticide application, organic farming) require verification of the customer\'s active certification (EPA pesticide applicator, USDA organic)']

Business justification: Custom hire assignments (equipment hired out to another party) are governed by a vendor contract specifying rates and terms. asset_assignment has custom_hire_flag but no contract reference. Role-prefi']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Asset assignments cover deployment of equipment to supply facilities (e.g., a forklift assigned to a grain elevator during harvest season). This link enables facility managers to track which assets ar']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm unit or farm location to which the equipment asset is assigned. Supports multi-location fleet scheduling and CAPEX/OPEX allocation by farm operation.']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: Equipment assignments are made against specific field crop plans — a planter is assigned to execute a specific crop plan for a field/season. Business justification: Asset assignments to fields and crop operations must be linked to profit centers to allocate equipment utilization costs to the correct crop enterprise P&L. Agriculture management accounting requires ']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Custom hire equipment assignments generate sales orders for billing the customer. Agriculture operations teams link equipment assignments to sales orders to track custom hire revenue, invoice accuracy']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: Multi-shift harvest and planting operations require assigning equipment to specific shifts (day/night). Asset utilization reporting and shift-based scheduling depend on this link. Agriculture operatio']
  actual_area_hectares decimal(12,4) [note: 'The actual land area in hectares covered by the equipment asset during the assignment period, sourced from GPS/RTK telematics data in John Deere Operations Center or Trimble Ag Software.']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the equipment asset was physically returned or released from the assignment. May differ from the planned end_date due to operational delays or early completion.']
  actual_utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual number of engine/operational hours the equipment asset accumulated during the assignment period, sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics. Used for OEE calculation and OPEX cost allocation.']
  assigned_to_entity_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating the type of business entity to which the equipment asset is assigned. Determines which reference ID (field_id, farm_unit_id, operator_id, crop_enterprise_id) is the primary assignment target.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information related to the equipment assignment (e.g., specific field conditions, equipment configuration requirements, custom hire terms).']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the equipment assignment record, following the format ASGN-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with John Deere Operations Center, SAP PM, and Granular Farm Management systems.']
  assignment_purpose varchar [note: 'The operational purpose or activity for which the equipment asset is assigned. Supports fleet scheduling, utilization analysis, and OPEX allocation by agricultural activity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planting|tillage|spraying|harvesting|irrigation|transport|custom_hire|maintenance_support|other — promote to reference product]']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the equipment asset assignment. \'planned\' indicates a future assignment; \'active\' indicates the asset is currently deployed; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary pause; \'completed\' indicates the assignment period has ended; \'cancelled\' indicates the assignment was voided.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the asset assignment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the hourly rate and cost values for this assignment (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency operations for international farm operations.']
  custom_hire_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment assignment is a custom hire arrangement (True) where the equipment is operated for a third-party customer or contracted service, or an internal farm operation (False). Affects billing and revenue recognition.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the equipment asset assignment is scheduled to end or was actually completed. Null for open-ended assignments. Used for fleet availability planning.']
  functional_location_code varchar [note: 'The SAP PM functional location code representing the physical or logical location where the equipment asset is deployed during the assignment (e.g., farm site, field block, irrigation zone). Supports maintenance planning and asset hierarchy.']
  gap_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this equipment assignment is conducted in compliance with Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) standards, including equipment calibration, chemical application records, and operator hygiene requirements.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The internal charge-out or cost allocation rate per operational hour for the equipment asset during this assignment. Used for OPEX cost allocation to crop enterprises and custom hire billing.']
  maintenance_plant_code varchar [note: 'The SAP PM maintenance plant code identifying the organizational unit responsible for maintaining the equipment asset during this assignment period. Used for PM work order routing and spare parts procurement.']
  operator_license_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary operator\'s equipment operating license and certifications have been verified as current and valid for the assigned equipment type prior to assignment activation. Supports OSHA compliance.']
  planned_area_hectares decimal(12,4) [note: 'The planned land area in hectares that the equipment asset is expected to cover during the assignment (e.g., acres to be planted, sprayed, or harvested). Supports YPM and operational efficiency analysis.']
  planned_utilization_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned or budgeted number of engine/operational hours the equipment asset is expected to accumulate during the assignment period. Used for CAPEX/OPEX cost allocation and fleet scheduling.']
  precision_ag_hardware_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned equipment asset is equipped with precision agriculture hardware (True), such as GPS/RTK receivers, VRA controllers, or NDVI sensors, relevant for variable rate application prescriptions.']
  safety_induction_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned operator has completed the required safety induction and equipment-specific SOP training prior to commencing the assignment. Required for OSHA and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance.']
  start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the equipment asset assignment becomes effective and the asset is deployed to the assigned entity. Used for fleet scheduling and seasonal planning.']
  total_assignment_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'The total OPEX cost incurred for this equipment assignment, calculated as actual utilization hours multiplied by the hourly rate plus any additional direct costs. Used for crop enterprise cost allocation and EBITDA reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the asset assignment record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'The SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure element code associated with this assignment for project-based CAPEX cost tracking (e.g., equipment deployment for a new irrigation project or precision ag hardware rollout).']

  Note: 'Records of equipment asset assignments to farm operations, fields, operators, and cost centers over time. Captures asset reference, assigned-to entity type (field, farm unit, operator, crop enterprise), assignment start date, assignment end date, primary operator, backup operator, assigned cost center (SAP CO), assignment purpose (planting season, harvest, custom hire), and utilization hours during assignment period. Enables CAPEX/OPEX cost allocation by crop enterprise and supports fleet scheduling across multiple farm locations.'
}

Table equipment.rental_lease {
  rental_lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each rental, lease, or custom hire agreement record in the equipment domain. Primary key for the rental_lease data product.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice. Business justification: Equipment rental/custom hire agreements generate AR invoices billed to the hiring customer. Agriculture operations routinely invoice neighbors for custom hire equipment use. This link supports AR bill']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment asset record (tractor, combine, planter, sprayer, irrigation system, etc.) covered by this agreement. May be null for custom hire inbound where the counterparty supplies the asset.']
  billing_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_schedule. Business justification: Equipment rental and lease agreements with periodic payment structures (monthly, seasonal) are governed by billing schedules. Agriculture lease agreements for land-use equipment require automated recu']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Custom hire and equipment rental agreements in agriculture are governed by sales contracts. Linking rental_lease to sales.contract enables contract compliance tracking, billing reconciliation, and cus']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Custom hire service billing: when a farm provides equipment hire services to neighboring operations or agribusiness customers, the rental_lease counterparty must be linked to a customer account for in']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Equipment rental delivery scheduling requires knowing the customer\'s specific delivery location constraints (dock availability, max vehicle weight, receiving hours). This is a real logistics process i']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Equipment rental and lease agreements for grain handling or processing equipment specify the supply facility where the equipment is deployed or must be returned. This link supports contract management']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Custom hire and equipment rental agreements in agriculture are negotiated and budgeted at the farm unit level for enterprise cost tracking. Farm unit-level rental cost aggregation is a named process i']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific agricultural field or land parcel associated with this agreement. Applicable when the lease or custom hire is tied to a defined field boundary. Links to the land and soil domain field master.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Equipment rental and custom hire agreements generate recurring expense postings to GL accounts (operating lease expense, custom hire/contract services). Agriculture operations heavily use custom hire ']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: Equipment rental and custom hire agreements (planters, combines, sprayers) are negotiated and budgeted by growing season. Linking rental_lease to growing_season enables seasonal equipment cost allocat']
  lease_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Rental/lease agreements in agriculture require a named customer contact for contract execution, equipment pickup/return coordination, and operational scheduling. rental_lease has customer_account_id b']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Equipment rental/lease income (custom hire) and lease expense must be assigned to profit centers for crop enterprise P&L reporting. Agriculture operations track custom hire revenue by profit center to']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Agriculture operators lease grain bins and storage facilities; the rental/lease agreement must reference the specific storage location being leased for contract management, capacity planning, and stor']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Equipment rental/lease agreements in agriculture are formalized under vendor outline agreements governing pricing, terms, and renewal conditions. Linking rental_lease to vendor_contract enables contra']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external party involved in the agreement — the lessor/rental provider for inbound agreements, or the lessee/customer for outbound agreements. Links to the vendor or customer master record.']
  acreage_cap decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum contracted acreage covered under the agreement before excess-use charges apply. Applicable to per-acre custom hire and seasonal lease agreements. Supports fleet cost optimization and field coverage planning.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the rental, lease, or custom hire agreement, used for cross-referencing with counterparty documents, SAP FI lease accounting, and IFRS 16 disclosures.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rental or lease agreement. Draft indicates pending execution; active indicates in-force; suspended indicates temporarily paused; terminated indicates early exit; expired indicates natural end-of-term; closed indicates fully settled and archived.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the equipment agreement: operating_lease (off-balance-sheet lease per IFRS 16 short-term exemption), finance_lease (on-balance-sheet right-of-use asset), rental (short-term ad hoc hire), custom_hire_inbound (external operator performs field operations for the enterprise), or custom_hire_outbound (enterprise provides equipment/operators to a third party). Drives CAPEX/OPEX treatment and revenue recognition.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO cost center to which the rental or lease expense (OPEX) or right-of-use asset depreciation (CAPEX) is allocated. Supports management accounting and EBITDA reporting by operational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rental_lease record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this agreement (rate_amount, total_agreement_value, security_deposit_amount). Supports multi-currency operations for international agricultural enterprises.']
  custom_hire_service_type varchar [note: 'For custom hire agreements (inbound or outbound), specifies the field operation service being contracted: planting, spraying, harvesting, hauling, tillage, or fertilizing. Set to not_applicable for pure equipment leases and rentals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planting|spraying|harvesting|hauling|tillage|fertilizing|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  early_termination_penalty decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contractual penalty amount payable if the agreement is terminated before the end_date. Used for lease exit cost analysis and IFRS 16 lease modification accounting. Null if no early termination clause exists.']
  end_date date [note: 'The contractual end date of the rental, lease, or custom hire agreement. For open-ended agreements this may be null. Used for lease term calculation, renewal planning, and IFRS 16 lease liability amortization schedule.']
  equipment_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the equipment type covered by the agreement. Supports fleet cost optimization analysis and CAPEX/OPEX planning by equipment class. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tractor|combine|planter|sprayer|irrigation_system|hauling_vehicle|tillage|grain_cart|header|other — promote to reference product]']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the agricultural machinery or equipment covered by the agreement (e.g., \'John Deere 8R 410 Tractor\', \'60-ft Case IH 4440 Sprayer\'). Used when the asset is not registered in the internal asset master or for custom hire where the counterparty supplies equipment.']
  execution_date date [note: 'The date on which the agreement was formally signed and executed by both parties. May differ from the start_date (effective date). Used as the principal business event date for audit and legal reference.']
  hours_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum contracted engine or operational hours allowed under the agreement before excess-use charges apply. Applicable to hourly-rate rentals and operating leases. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics for monitoring.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering the equipment under this agreement. Used for claims processing and compliance verification with contractual insurance requirements.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement contractually requires the enterprise or counterparty to maintain equipment insurance coverage. True if insurance is a contractual obligation.']
  invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the most recent invoice issued or received under this agreement. Used for accounts payable/receivable matching in SAP FI and payment reconciliation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contractual terms, special conditions, operational instructions, or compliance notes not captured in structured fields (e.g., GPS/RTK equipment requirements, VRA controller compatibility, PHI/REI obligations for custom hire spraying).']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'The schedule at which lease or rental payments are due: monthly, quarterly, seasonal (aligned to crop season), annual, per_use (invoiced per event), or upfront (single prepayment). Drives accounts payable scheduling in SAP FI.']
  performance_metric_type varchar [note: 'The type of quality or performance metric tracked under this agreement. For custom hire, this may be acres_completed, hours_logged, yield_achieved, or application_rate. Supports service-level verification and payment milestone validation.']
  performance_metric_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual recorded value for the performance_metric_type tracked under this agreement (e.g., 450.5 acres completed, 320 hours logged). Used for milestone billing, service verification, and OEE benchmarking.']
  purchase_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes an option for the enterprise to purchase the equipment at or before the end_date. True if a purchase option exists. A reasonably certain purchase option affects IFRS 16 finance lease classification.']
  purchase_option_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The contractual price at which the enterprise may purchase the leased equipment if the purchase_option is true. Used for lease vs. buy analysis and IFRS 16 lease classification assessment.']
  rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The contracted rate per unit as defined by rate_structure (e.g., dollars per acre, dollars per hour, monthly payment amount). Used for invoice validation and cost benchmarking against owned-equipment OPEX.']
  rate_structure varchar [note: 'The billing rate basis for the agreement: per_acre (common for custom hire spraying/planting), per_hour (equipment rental), monthly (operating lease), seasonal (crop-season lease), annual (finance lease), or per_day (short-term rental). Drives invoice calculation and cost allocation.']
  renewal_option boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a contractual option to renew at or before the end_date. True if a renewal option exists. Relevant for IFRS 16 lease term assessment and fleet planning.']
  return_condition_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the contractual conditions under which the equipment must be returned at agreement end (e.g., \'normal wear and tear acceptable\', \'full service required\', \'maximum 2000 hours\'). Used for asset return inspection and dispute resolution.']
  sap_lease_contract_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI lease accounting contract reference number (RE-FX or IFRS 16 module) associated with this agreement. Enables reconciliation between the operational rental_lease record and the financial accounting right-of-use asset and lease liability entries.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Refundable security deposit paid or received at agreement commencement. Recorded as a financial asset (inbound) or liability (outbound) in SAP FI. Null if no deposit is required.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the rental, lease, or custom hire agreement becomes effective and the enterprise gains right-of-use or service access. Used for IFRS 16 commencement date determination and CAPEX/OPEX period allocation.']
  total_acreage_covered decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total number of acres covered or serviced under this agreement. For custom hire, this is the contracted field area to be planted, sprayed, or harvested. Used for per-acre billing reconciliation and yield-per-acre (YPM) cost analysis.']
  total_agreement_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total contracted monetary value of the agreement over its full term. For IFRS 16 finance leases, this is the undiscounted sum of lease payments. Used for CAPEX/OPEX planning, budget allocation, and lease liability recognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rental_lease record was last modified. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Records of external equipment agreements including rentals, leases, and contract farming services (custom hire) for agricultural machinery. Covers equipment obtained from external providers, leased out to third parties, and contract services where external operators perform field operations or the enterprise provides equipment/operators to others. Captures agreement type (operating lease/finance lease/rental/custom hire inbound/custom hire outbound), agreement number, counterparty identity, asset or equipment description, service type for custom hire (planting, spraying, harvesting, hauling), lease terms (start/end dates, payment schedule, mileage/hours cap, return conditions, early termination), rate structure (per acre, per hour, monthly, seasonal), total agreement value, field/acreage covered, quality/performance metrics, insurance requirements, invoice reference, and SAP FI lease accounting reference (IFRS 16). Supports fleet cost optimization decisions between owned, leased, rented, and custom-hired equipment, revenue recognition for outbound contract services, and CAPEX/OPEX planning.'
}

Table equipment.parts_issue {
  parts_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the parts_issue association']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Parts issuance accountability in agriculture maintenance requires recording which technician received parts from inventory. Supports inventory shrinkage control, labor cost allocation per technician, ']
  parts_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this parts issue line to the specific spare part or consumable record in the MRO inventory master.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking this parts issue line to the parent maintenance work order that consumed the part.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the part was physically drawn from the warehouse and issued to the work order. Belongs to the issue event, not to the work order header (which has planned/actual start dates) or the inventory master.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this parts issue record within the work order, matching the SAP PM component line or Maximo material usage line number. Enables ordering and referencing of individual parts lines on a work order.']
  parts_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary cost of spare parts and materials consumed against the work order, aggregated from embedded parts consumption line items. Supports OPEX cost tracking and inventory replenishment analysis. Maps to SAP MM goods issue cost (DMBTR) settled to the work order. [Moved from work_order: The work_order.parts_cost is a derived aggregate (sum of all parts issue line costs) and should be computed from parts_issue records rather than stored redundantly on the work order header. If retained on work_order for performance, it should be treated as a calculated/denormalized field, not a source-of-truth attribute. The authoritative per-part cost data lives in the parts_issue association.]']
  quantity_issued decimal [note: 'Actual quantity of the part issued from inventory to the work order, expressed in the part\'s base unit of measure. Belongs to the issue event, not to the work order header or the inventory master.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this material reservation/issue line: planned (on work order plan, not yet reserved), reserved (stock reserved but not yet drawn), issued (physically drawn from warehouse), partially_issued (partial quantity drawn), returned (unused quantity returned to stock), cancelled (line removed from work order). Drives inventory availability calculations and OPEX accruals.']
  return_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of the part returned to inventory after work order completion (unused material). Drives inventory replenishment recalculation and OPEX cost adjustment.']
  unit_cost_at_issue decimal [note: 'Unit cost of the part at the time of issue, captured from the inventory valuation at issue date. Differs from the current unit_cost on parts_inventory due to price fluctuations; belongs to this issue event for accurate historical OPEX costing.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Parts Issue (Material Reservation) event between work_order and parts_inventory. It captures each line-item-level consumption of a spare part or consumable against a specific maintenance work order, as recorded in the CMMS (SAP PM goods issue / IBM Maximo material usage). Each record links one work_order to one parts_inventory item and carries the quantity issued, unit cost at time of issue, issue date, reservation status, and return quantity — data that exists only in the context of this specific work-order-to-part consumption event. Serves as the SSOT for actual material consumption supporting OPEX cost tracking, inventory replenishment triggers, and maintenance cost analysis by asset and crop enterprise.. Existence Justification: In agricultural CMMS operations, a single work order consumes multiple spare parts from inventory (e.g., a combine harvester repair may require an air filter, hydraulic seal, and drive belt simultaneously), and each part number is consumed across many different work orders over time. This is a textbook operational M:N: the \'parts issue\' or \'material reservation\' is a well-established CMMS business concept actively created, updated, and closed by maintenance planners and technicians. The relationship carries its own data — quantity issued, unit cost at time of issue, issue date, reservation status, and return quantity — none of which belong solely to the work order header or the parts inventory master.'
}

Table supply.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each agricultural shipment record. Primary key for the shipment data product within the supply domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party legally identified as the consignee (recipient) on the BOL/AWB. The consignee takes legal possession of the goods upon delivery per 49 CFR Part 373.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (trucking company, rail operator, airline, or shipping line) responsible for executing this shipment. Used for carrier performance analytics and FSMA carrier compliance tracking.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Every agricultural shipment moves a specific commodity. This link enables MRL threshold lookup, FSMA coverage determination, storage requirement validation, and commodity-specific freight rate applica']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Shipments must be attributed to a legal entity (company code) for intercompany freight billing, customs declarations, and statutory financial reporting. Agriculture exporters require company-code-leve']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Agricultural shipments are delivered to customer-registered delivery locations (grain elevators, food processors). Linking enables delivery appointment scheduling, receiving-hours validation, dock ass']
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.delivery_schedule. Business justification: Inbound shipments in agriculture fulfill procurement delivery schedule lines. Linking shipment to delivery_schedule enables on-time delivery tracking, inbound logistics planning, and procurement perfo']
  destination_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving facility (food manufacturer, retailer distribution center, or warehouse) where the shipment is to be delivered. Drives route planning in TMS and delivery confirmation workflows.']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: GMO commodity shipments require GMO declaration reference for export compliance to EU, Japan, and China markets. This FK supports BE Act disclosure requirements, export documentation compliance, and G']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Agricultural commodity shipments are graded at origin and destination for buyer-seller settlement. This FK links shipments to the applicable USDA grading standard, enabling grade-based contract compli']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: Grain merchandisers and logistics managers track all shipments by crop season for season-level P&L reporting, logistics cost allocation, and crop insurance claim support. growing_season provides organ']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule requires food shipments to comply with applicable HACCP/preventive controls plans. Linking shipment to its governing HACCP plan supports FSMA compliance documentation']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Pre-shipment and arrival inspections are mandatory in agriculture supply chains. FSMA traceability requires linking each shipment to the inspection that cleared it for transport or acceptance. Agricul']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic commodity shipments require organic certification reference for NOP chain-of-custody compliance. This FK enables organic integrity tracking at the shipment level, organic premium billing verif']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating facility (farm, grain elevator, processing plant, or warehouse) from which the shipment departs. Used for traceability, FSMA compliance, and supply chain network analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Shipment revenue and freight costs must be attributed to a profit center for segment P&L reporting. Agriculture commodity trading companies track profitability by crop type or business unit at the shi']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Sales Order (SO) in SAP S/4HANA SD that triggered this shipment. Enables order-to-delivery traceability and revenue recognition linkage.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Shipments originate from a specific storage location (e.g., grain bin, cold room). Inventory depletion reconciliation and FSMA lot traceability require knowing the exact storage location from which a ']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_order. Business justification: A shipment is authorized and managed by a transport order in the TMS. Adding transport_order_id to shipment creates the direct link between the physical shipment execution record and the TMS authoriza']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: A shipment is physically executed using a specific transport unit (truck, railcar, container). Adding transport_unit_id to shipment normalizes the assignment of the physical conveyance to the shipment']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Shipments in agriculture execute against vendor contracts (grain purchase agreements, input supply contracts). Contract performance monitoring, payment terms application, and FSMA compliance reporting']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (organization or business entity) legally identified as the shipper on the BOL/AWB. The shipper is responsible for tendering the goods to the carrier per 49 CFR Part 373.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the shipment arrived at the destination facility, captured via GPS telematics or gate check-in scan. Used for on-time delivery performance, dwell time analysis, and proof of delivery.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the shipment departed from the origin facility, captured via GPS telematics or manual confirmation. Used for on-time departure performance measurement and FSMA transport time tracking.']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Air Waybill number for air freight shipments of agricultural products. Serves as the contract of carriage for air transport per IATA standards. Null for non-air transport modes.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Legally binding Bill of Lading number issued by the carrier, serving as the contract of carriage and receipt of goods per 49 CFR Part 373. Required for road and rail freight movements of agricultural commodities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA or TMS). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement from order creation to shipment execution.']
  delivery_gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) captured at the point of delivery confirmation. Provides geospatial proof of delivery location for dispute resolution and route analytics via Esri ArcGIS.']
  delivery_gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) captured at the point of delivery confirmation. Provides geospatial proof of delivery location for dispute resolution and route analytics via Esri ArcGIS.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment destination. Required for export/import customs documentation, phytosanitary certificates (APHIS), and international trade compliance.']
  electronic_signature_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an electronic signature was captured from the consignee at the point of delivery per 49 CFR requirements. Required for legal proof of delivery and invoice payment authorization.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the exception event (delay, damage, refusal, temperature breach) affecting this shipment. Captures details for carrier claims, insurance, and food safety incident reporting.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the most recent or active exception event affecting this shipment. Null if no exception has occurred. Drives exception management workflows and carrier claims processing.']
  freight_cost_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight cost in US Dollars (USD) charged by the carrier for this shipment. Used for logistics cost analysis, COGS allocation, and carrier rate benchmarking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM freight condition records.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual freight payment terms defining when title and risk transfer between shipper and consignee. FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are standard Incoterms used in agricultural commodity trade.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment has been verified as compliant with the FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O). Covers vehicle cleanliness, temperature controls, and documentation requirements.']
  gross_weight_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in Metric Tons (MT), including commodity weight and packaging. Used for freight billing, carrier capacity planning, and regulatory weight compliance on road transport.']
  max_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments during transport. Breach of this threshold triggers an exception event and potential food safety hold per HACCP and FSMA requirements.']
  min_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments during transport. Breach of this threshold triggers an exception event and potential food safety hold per HACCP and FSMA requirements.']
  net_weight_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net weight of the agricultural commodity in Metric Tons (MT), excluding tare weight of containers and packaging. Used for commodity pricing, yield reconciliation, and USDA grading.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin. Required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, customs documentation, and international trade reporting.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when proof of delivery was captured at the destination, including electronic signature, GPS coordinates, and/or photo evidence. Triggers invoice payment release in SAP S/4HANA FI and closes the delivery lifecycle.']
  quantity_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of grain or seed commodity expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard unit of measure for grain trading in North American agricultural markets. Null for non-grain commodities.']
  requires_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled cold chain management throughout transport. True for fresh produce, dairy, meat, and certain agricultural inputs requiring refrigeration per FDA and FSMA requirements.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier attached to the shipment or container for automated tracking and traceability throughout the supply chain. Enables real-time location visibility and automated gate check-in/out at facilities.']
  scheduled_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination facility. Used for receiving dock scheduling, workforce planning, and on-time delivery (OTD) KPI measurement.']
  scheduled_departure_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the shipment is scheduled to depart from the origin facility. Used for transportation planning, carrier booking, and on-time departure KPI measurement.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Tamper-evident seal number affixed to the transport container or trailer at point of loading. Required for food safety chain-of-custody verification and documented on the BOL per 49 CFR and FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment from planning through final delivery. Drives operational workflows in SAP S/4HANA SD and Manhattan Associates WMS. Lifecycle: planned → confirmed → in_transit → delivered (or exception/cancelled).']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this shipment. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (FSMA, DOT, IATA, IMO) and documentation requirements for agricultural commodity movements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'SSOT for every agricultural shipment — grain, produce, livestock feed, and processed food movements from farm to food manufacturer or retailer. Master record capturing shipment identity, BOL/AWB legal documentation (shipper, consignee, freight terms, seal numbers, electronic signatures per 49 CFR), origin and destination facility, commodity lot (LOT), carrier assignment, transport mode (road, rail, air, sea), shipment status lifecycle, scheduled and actual departure/arrival dates, total weight (MT/CWT/BU), temperature requirements for cold chain, FSMA Sanitary Transportation rule compliance flag, container identifiers, delivery milestone events (pickup confirmation, in-transit checkpoints, border crossing, proof of delivery with GPS coordinates, photo evidence, and electronic signature capture), and exception events (delay, damage, refusal). Single authoritative record for shipment identity, legal shipping documentation, execution tracking, and delivery event history within the supply domain. Subsumes BOL document data and delivery event tracking — no separate products exist for these concepts.'
}

  shipment_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shipment line record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the shipment_line data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE.']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Linking to grading_standard enables automated grade compliance verification, rejection rea']

Business justification: Shipment lines for processed/finished agricultural goods must reference the product master for SKU-level FSMA traceability, COA requirements, label compliance verification, and organic certification s']

Business justification: Each agricultural shipment line must document which specific MRL threshold was tested against for regulatory traceability, export market compliance, and food safety audit requirements. The mrl_complia']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Individual shipment lines rejected for MRL exceedance, grade failure, or contamination generate nonconformance records. Linking shipment_line to nonconformance supports line-level disposition, recall ']

  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety.
