  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment header record. Establishes the header/detail relationship linking this line to its parent shipment. Required TRANSACTION_LINE category: HEADER_REFERENCE.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Shipment lines in agriculture commodity trading fulfill specific vendor contracts. Line-level vendor contract traceability is required for contract performance reporting, FSMA traceability, quality sp']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Air Waybill document number for shipment lines transported by air freight. Used for air cargo tracking, customs clearance, and carrier reconciliation for high-value or perishable agricultural commodities.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading document number associated with this shipment line. The BOL is the primary legal document for freight transport, serving as a receipt of goods and contract of carriage. Used for carrier reconciliation and customs documentation.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the agricultural commodity was produced or harvested. Required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance under USDA Agricultural Marketing Act.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record origination.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the line value (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Ensures consistent financial reporting across international agricultural trade transactions.']
  fsma_tlc_source varchar [note: 'Description of the location or point in the supply chain where the FSMA Traceability Lot Code was assigned (e.g., farm, packing house, processor). Required field under FSMA Section 204 Key Data Element (KDE) for shipping events.']
  fsma_traceability_lot_code varchar [note: 'FSMA Section 204 Traceability Lot Code (TLC) assigned to the food commodity on this shipment line. Mandatory for foods on the Food Traceability List (FTL) to enable one-up/one-back recall response within 24 hours.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment line including packaging, pallets, and commodity in kilograms. Used for freight billing, carrier compliance, and weight-based regulatory reporting.']
  harmonized_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized Tariff Schedule code classifying the agricultural commodity for customs and international trade purposes. Required for import/export documentation, duty calculation, and USDA APHIS phytosanitary compliance.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent shipment, used to order and identify individual commodity lots loaded on a single shipment. Required TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_SEQUENCE.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the shipment line. Tracks the lifecycle from initial creation through loading, transit, delivery, and any rejection or cancellation events.']
  line_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value of the commodity on this shipment line in the transaction currency. Used for customs declarations, insurance valuation, and financial reconciliation. Required TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot tracking identifier assigned to the agricultural commodity lot on this shipment line. Used for one-up/one-back traceability and recall response per FSMA Section 204. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA batch number.']
  moisture_content_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content percentage of the commodity on this shipment line as measured at time of loading or grading. Critical quality parameter for grain commodities affecting storage life, weight dockage, and pricing under USDA grade standards.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net weight of the commodity on this shipment line excluding packaging and tare weight in kilograms. Used for commodity valuation, yield reconciliation, and food safety compliance reporting.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets used to load the commodity on this shipment line. Used for warehouse space planning, carrier capacity management, and loading dock operations in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of the Pre-Harvest Interval observed before harvest for the commodity lot on this shipment line. Confirms that the required waiting period after pesticide application was met before harvest, ensuring MRL compliance.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the commodity loaded on this shipment line expressed in the designated unit of measure (BU, MT, CWT, etc.). Required TRANSACTION_LINE category: LINE_QUANTITY.']
  required_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum required storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for this shipment line. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and FSMA sanitary transportation rule adherence.']
  required_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required storage and transport temperature in degrees Celsius for this shipment line. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and FSMA sanitary transportation rule adherence.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier assigned to the pallet or container for this shipment line. Enables automated lot-level traceability scanning at receiving docks, storage locations, and delivery points per FSMA traceability requirements.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code identifying the specific commodity product variant (e.g., crop type, grade, packaging format) loaded on this shipment line. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA material number and Manhattan Associates WMS item master.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this shipment line record originated. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated systems in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature zone classification assigned to this shipment line for cold chain management. Determines storage and transport temperature requirements to maintain food safety and commodity quality.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the shipment line quantity. BU = Bushel, MT = Metric Ton, CWT = Hundredweight, LB = Pound, KG = Kilogram, TON = Short Ton. Aligns with USDA commodity measurement standards and SAP S/4HANA base unit of measure.']
  upc_code varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code barcode identifier for the commodity SKU on this shipment line. Used for retail scanning, inventory management, and point-of-sale identification of packaged agricultural products.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment line record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture, audit trails, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'SSOT for line-level detail within each agricultural shipment, representing individual commodity lots loaded on a single shipment. Records commodity SKU, lot number (LOT), quantity (BU/MT/CWT), unit of measure, gross and net weight, pallet count, RFID tag identifiers, temperature zone assignment, COA reference, MRL compliance status, country of origin (COOL), and organic/non-GMO certification reference. Enables lot-level traceability from farm field through delivery to food manufacturer for FSMA Section 204 compliance and one-up/one-back recall response. Single authoritative record for shipment line-item detail within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.transport_order {
  transport_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a single transportation order record within the supply domain. Primary key for the transport_order data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned vehicle unit number, trailer number, or intermodal container ID assigned to execute this transportation order. Used for RFID tracking, GPS telematics integration, and FSMA vehicle inspection records.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the assigned motor carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for executing this transportation order. Links to the carrier master record for FMCSA authority, insurance, and rate information.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Transport orders in agriculture are planned around commodity type to determine temperature zones, equipment requirements, and FSMA sanitary transport compliance. A domain expert expects transport orde']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Transport orders are raised by a specific legal entity. Required for intercompany freight billing (e.g., one entity ships on behalf of another) and entity-level freight cost reporting in multi-entity ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Transport orders represent committed freight spend before invoice receipt. Cost center assignment enables commitment accounting and budget consumption reporting — standard in agriculture logistics whe']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Agriculture transport orders require a loading/unloading crew assignment (grain elevator crew, harvest loading crew) for labor costing, H2A compliance tracking, and FSMA traceability. The existing dri']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Transport orders in agriculture require the customer delivery location for route planning, carrier assignment, dock scheduling, and FSMA sanitary transport compliance. No current FK on transport_order']
  driver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FSMA Sanitary Transport requires tracking which CDL-holding, trained driver executed each transport order. Agriculture logistics managers use this link for driver compliance audits, hours-of-service t']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Transport orders generate freight cost postings to a specific GL account for cost accounting and AP accrual. Agriculture logistics operations require GL account assignment on transport orders for auto']
  primary_transport_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin facility (farm, grain elevator, processing plant, or warehouse) from which the agricultural commodity shipment originates. Used for FSMA traceability and BOL generation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Transport order freight costs are attributed to a profit center for logistics P&L reporting by business segment. Agriculture companies segment freight spend by grain division, oilseed division, or geo']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Inbound transport orders in agriculture are created to execute purchase order deliveries (moving purchased seeds, chemicals, or grain to farm/facility). Linking transport_order to purchase_order enabl']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TMS-generated route plan that defines the optimized path, waypoints, and leg sequence for this transportation order. Supports route optimization analytics and fuel cost allocation.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: TMS-to-OMS integration: transport orders are created to fulfill customer sales orders in grain/commodity logistics. Dispatchers and planners must reference the originating sales order when creating tr']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.service_contract. Business justification: A transport order is executed under the terms of a service contract with the carrier or 3PL. Adding service_contract_id to transport_order links the TMS order to the governing contract, enabling valid']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Transport orders in grain/ag operations specify the exact storage bin or silo for pickup. Load planning, FIFO/FEFO compliance, and WMS task generation require the transport order to reference the spec']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: A transport order authorizes movement using a specific transport unit. transport_order.equipment_type is a string denormalization of the unit type; with transport_unit_id FK, the unit_type, capacity, ']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading number issued by the carrier as the legal contract of carriage and receipt of goods. Required for FSMA traceability, customs clearance, and dispute resolution. Cross-references the physical BOL document.']
  booked_volume_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'The confirmed booked cargo volume in cubic meters committed with the carrier. Used for load planning, space utilization optimization, and LTL rate calculation based on dimensional weight.']
  booked_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The confirmed booked cargo weight in kilograms committed with the carrier for this transportation order. Used for freight rate calculation, weight distribution compliance, and carrier capacity management. Expressed in metric tons (MT) equivalent for bulk grain reporting.']
  booking_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The carrier or 3PL-issued booking confirmation reference number acknowledging capacity commitment for this transportation order. Used for capacity management, cancellation processing, and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the carrier capacity booking associated with this transportation order. Tracks the booking lifecycle from initial request through confirmation, amendment, or cancellation.']
  cancellation_terms varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the carrier booking cancellation terms applicable to this transportation order, including notice period requirements and penalty charges. Sourced from the carrier rate agreement.']
  center_of_gravity_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the load plan for this transportation order meets DOT center-of-gravity and axle weight distribution requirements. Non-compliant loads must be re-planned before dispatch authorization.']
  confirmed_delivery_date timestamp [note: 'The carrier-confirmed appointment date and time for cargo delivery at the destination facility. Used for consignee receiving dock scheduling and cold chain compliance window validation.']
  confirmed_pickup_date timestamp [note: 'The carrier-confirmed appointment date and time for cargo pickup at the origin facility. May differ from requested pickup date due to carrier availability. Drives dock assignment and load planning execution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this transport order record was first persisted in the data platform. Used for data lineage, SLA measurement, and audit trail compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the freight rate and charges on this transportation order (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency freight cost reporting and IFRS financial consolidation.']
  dock_assignment varchar [note: 'The specific dock door or bay number assigned at the origin facility for loading this transportation order. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS dock scheduling. Used for load sequencing and yard management.']
  freight_rate decimal(14,4) [note: 'The agreed freight rate applied to this transportation order, expressed in the transaction currency per unit (per CWT, per MT, per mile, or flat rate). Sourced from carrier rate agreements in SAP S/4HANA MM. Business-confidential pricing data.']
  freight_rate_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure basis for the freight rate applied to this order. CWT (Hundredweight) and MT (Metric Ton) are standard agricultural commodity freight rate bases; flat rate applies to FTL movements.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Commercial freight terms (Incoterms) governing risk transfer and cost responsibility between shipper and consignee. FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are standard in agricultural commodity trade. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB_origin|FOB_destination|CIF|DAP|EXW|FCA — promote to reference product]']
  fsma_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Compliance status of this transportation order against FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O). Tracks whether required vehicle cleanliness, temperature control, and shipper/carrier agreement requirements are met.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Transportation mode and capacity utilization classification. FTL (Full Truckload) and LTL (Less Than Truckload) are primary modes for agricultural commodities; intermodal, rail, and barge are used for bulk grain movements.']
  loading_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence defining the order in which commodity lots are loaded onto the vehicle for this transportation order. Optimized for delivery stop sequence (last-in-first-out) and center-of-gravity compliance.']
  max_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable cargo temperature in degrees Celsius during transport. Exceedance triggers FSMA non-compliance flag and may require cargo rejection or quality hold upon delivery.']
  min_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable cargo temperature in degrees Celsius during transport. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring, reefer set-point configuration, and FSMA temperature excursion alerting.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'The business event timestamp when the transportation order was officially created and entered into the TMS. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transportation order from creation through execution and closure. Drives workflow routing in TMS and SAP S/4HANA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|booked|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Directional classification of the transportation order indicating whether it moves agricultural commodities inbound from suppliers/farms, outbound to food manufacturers/retailers, between internal facilities, or as a return movement.']
  planned_load_end timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for completion of cargo loading operations at the origin dock. Used to calculate planned loading duration, driver detention time, and dispatch readiness.']
  planned_load_start timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for commencement of cargo loading operations at the origin dock. Used for labor planning, dock scheduling coordination with Manhattan Associates WMS, and on-time loading KPI measurement.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority classification assigned to this transportation order, influencing carrier selection, equipment allocation, and dispatch sequencing. Critical priority is used for perishable commodities with tight PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) windows.']
  requested_delivery_date timestamp [note: 'The consignee-requested date and time for cargo delivery at the destination facility. Establishes the contractual delivery commitment and drives SLA measurement for on-time delivery performance.']
  requested_pickup_date timestamp [note: 'The shipper-requested date and time window start for cargo pickup at the origin facility. Used for dock scheduling, carrier appointment booking, and on-time pickup KPI measurement.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier attached to the shipment or pallet for automated traceability tracking throughout the supply chain. Enables real-time lot-level visibility from loading through delivery in compliance with FSMA traceability requirements.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing commodity-specific handling requirements such as hazmat placarding, organic segregation requirements, GMO separation protocols, or fragile produce stacking restrictions. Printed on BOL and communicated to carrier.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone classification for the cargo during transport. Drives reefer equipment selection, cold chain monitoring requirements, and FSMA temperature control documentation. Critical for perishable produce and dairy commodities.']
  tms_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable transportation order number generated by the TMS (e.g., SAP TM or Manhattan Associates TMS). Used for carrier communication, BOL cross-reference, and customer-facing shipment tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording when this transport order record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change detection, incremental ETL, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'SSOT for TMS-generated transportation orders authorizing and managing the full lifecycle of agricultural commodity movements — from order creation through carrier booking, load planning, and dispatch execution. Captures order type (inbound/outbound/inter-facility), requested and confirmed pickup/delivery windows, priority level, freight terms (FOB/CIF), carrier or 3PL assignment, route plan reference, load type (FTL, LTL, intermodal), FSMA compliance checklist status, equipment type (reefer, dry van, flatbed, hopper), freight rate applied, booking confirmation details (booked capacity, appointment times, cancellation terms, booking status), and load planning details (loading sequence, commodity lot assignments per position, weight distribution, center-of-gravity compliance, temperature zone mapping, dock assignment, vehicle/container ID, planned load start/completion times). Single authoritative record for carrier dispatch authorization, freight capacity commitment, booking management, and load execution planning within the supply domain. Subsumes freight booking and load plan data — no separate products exist for these concepts.'
}

Table supply.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the freight carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider record within the agricultural supply chain.']
  quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Food-grade carriers must hold quality/food safety certifications (FSMA Sanitary Transport, SQF Transport). carrier has fsma_sanitary_transport_certified flag and fsma_cert_expiry_date (denormalized) b']
  sap_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Vendor master record number assigned to this carrier in SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used for cross-referencing freight invoices, purchase orders (PO), and goods receipt notes (GRN) in the ERP system.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier was formally approved to transport trial materials to or from this specific trial site. Belongs to the approval relationship, not to the carrier or site master records.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier\'s approval for this trial site. Controls whether the carrier may be tendered loads for this site. Derived from the approval management process.']
  approved_commodity_types varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of agricultural commodity categories this carrier is approved to transport (e.g., \'grain\', \'fresh_produce\', \'livestock\', \'fertilizer\', \'pesticides\'). Derived from carrier certifications and equipment capabilities.']
  audit_date date [note: 'The specific date on which audit activities were conducted for this carrier. Relevant when a multi-carrier audit spans multiple site visits on different dates.']
  audit_outcome varchar [note: 'The audit outcome determined specifically for this carrier within the audit engagement. A multi-carrier audit may yield different outcomes per carrier.']
  audit_scope_for_carrier varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific operations, routes, facilities, or practices within scope for this carrier in the context of this audit. May be a subset of the overall audit scope when the audit covers multiple carriers.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier record within the agricultural supply chain master data. Controls whether the carrier is eligible for new shipment assignments in the TMS.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation the carrier operates. Drives TMS routing logic, BOL/AWB document selection, and FSMA Sanitary Transportation rule applicability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trucking|rail|air_freight|ocean|intermodal|3pl — promote to reference product if additional modes are needed]']
  cold_chain_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has certified refrigerated or temperature-controlled transport capability for cold chain agricultural commodities (e.g., fresh produce, dairy, meat). Required for perishable shipment routing in TMS.']
  cold_chain_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier\'s cold chain capability has been specifically verified and validated for this trial site\'s requirements. Distinct from the carrier-level cold_chain_capable flag, which is a general capability indicator. A carrier may be generally cold-chain capable but not yet verified for a specific site\'s temperature profile or containment requirements.']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite compliance score (0-100) reflecting the carrier\'s adherence to regulatory requirements (FSMA, DOT, FMCSA) and contractual obligations. Calculated from documentation currency, audit results, and violation history.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current master carrier agreement or rate contract. Triggers renewal workflow alerts in SAP S/4HANA MM. Null indicates an open-ended evergreen agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current master carrier agreement or rate contract between the agricultural enterprise and the carrier. Defines the period during which contracted rates and service commitments apply.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier is required to submit or implement corrective actions as a result of findings identified during this audit. Drives follow-up workflow in the compliance domain.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the carrier is legally registered. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (FMCSA, Transport Canada, etc.) and COOL compliance requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  damage_claim_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rolling ratio of shipments resulting in a cargo damage claim to total shipments executed. Used to assess carrier handling quality for sensitive agricultural commodities and inform carrier tier scoring.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'USDOT number issued by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) to identify commercial motor carriers operating in interstate commerce. Required for FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance verification.']
  edi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports EDI transaction sets (e.g., 204 Motor Carrier Load Tender, 214 Shipment Status, 210 Freight Invoice) for automated load tendering and status updates in the agricultural supply chain.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier\'s site-specific approval expires and must be renewed. Approval periods may align with annual carrier qualification cycles or cooperator agreement renewal dates.']
  fsma_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carrier\'s FSMA Sanitary Transportation certification. Triggers compliance alerts when approaching expiry to prevent use of non-compliant carriers for food-grade agricultural shipments.']
  fsma_sanitary_transport_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food certification (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O). Mandatory for carriers transporting food-grade agricultural products to food manufacturers.']
  gps_tracking_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides real-time GPS-based shipment location tracking integrated with the TMS for visibility into in-transit agricultural commodity shipments.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds USDOT hazardous materials (HAZMAT) certification for transporting regulated agricultural inputs such as pesticides, fertilizers, and fumigants with Safety Data Sheet (SDS) requirements.']
  insurance_cert_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carrier\'s current insurance certificate (cargo liability, general liability, and auto liability). Carrier is ineligible for load assignment if this date has passed. Monitored for proactive renewal alerts.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cargo liability insurance coverage amount in USD as stated on the carrier\'s current insurance certificate. Used to validate coverage adequacy for high-value agricultural commodity shipments.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the carrier master record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit compliance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered business name of the freight carrier or 3PL provider as recorded with the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FMCSA, DOT). Used for contracts, BOL documentation, and compliance filings.']
  max_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the carrier\'s temperature-controlled units can maintain. Defines the upper bound of the carrier\'s cold chain capability for fresh produce and ambient-chilled agricultural shipments.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Operating authority number (MC number) issued by FMCSA to carriers transporting regulated commodities for hire in interstate commerce. Validates carrier authority to haul agricultural products.']
  min_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the carrier\'s refrigerated units can maintain. Used for TMS routing of frozen or deep-chilled agricultural products requiring specific temperature ranges.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling percentage of shipments delivered within the committed delivery window. Primary KPI for carrier performance evaluation, tier assignment, and preferred carrier status in TMS routing for agricultural logistics.']
  on_time_pickup_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling percentage of shipments where the carrier completed pickup within the agreed pickup window. Key performance indicator (KPI) for carrier scorecard evaluation and performance tier assignment in the agricultural supply chain.']
  performance_eval_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter and year (e.g., \'2024-Q3\') for which the current rolling performance metrics (on-time rates, damage claim rate, compliance score, performance tier) were last evaluated and updated.']
  performance_rating_at_site decimal [note: 'Performance rating (0.00–5.00) assigned to the carrier based on observed performance specifically at this trial site. Distinct from the carrier\'s aggregate rolling performance metrics, which span all sites and shipments. Enables site managers to flag carriers that perform well globally but poorly at their specific location.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Current performance tier classification assigned based on the carrier\'s rolling KPI scores (on-time rates, damage claims, compliance score). Drives TMS primary/secondary/tertiary carrier selection logic for agricultural shipment routing.']
  preferred_lane_assignments varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of preferred origin-destination lane codes (e.g., \'MIDWEST-SOUTHEAST\', \'CA-TX\') where this carrier has committed capacity or preferred routing agreements. Used by TMS for primary carrier selection in route optimization.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the carrier\'s operational contact used for load tender notifications, BOL transmissions, and compliance document exchange.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the carrier organization responsible for load tendering, dispatch coordination, and issue resolution.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the carrier\'s operational contact used for dispatch coordination, load tendering, and exception management in the agricultural supply chain.']
  rejected_loads_count varchar [note: 'Count of loads rejected or refused by the carrier after tender acceptance within the current evaluation period. High rejection rates indicate capacity reliability issues and impact carrier tier scoring.']
  rfid_tracking_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports RFID-based shipment and lot (LOT) tracking for end-to-end traceability of agricultural commodities from farm to food manufacturer. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS RFID infrastructure.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Unique 2-4 character Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to identify the carrier in EDI transactions, BOL documents, and TMS routing.']
  service_modes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of transportation service modes offered by the carrier (e.g., FTL, LTL, refrigerated, flatbed, bulk, tanker, intermodal). Used for TMS lane matching and load tendering in agricultural logistics.']
  temperature_excursion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rolling ratio of cold chain shipments where a temperature excursion (deviation outside the specified range) was recorded to total cold chain shipments. Critical metric for FSMA compliance and food safety risk management.']
  total_shipments_executed varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of shipments executed by this carrier within the current evaluation period. Provides volume context for interpreting rate-based KPIs (on-time, damage, temperature excursion) in carrier performance analysis.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) trade name of the carrier, if different from the legal name. Used in day-to-day communications and TMS lane assignments.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all approved freight carriers and 3PL providers in the agricultural supply chain. Master record storing carrier legal name, SCAC code, DOT/MC number, carrier type (trucking, rail, air freight, ocean), service modes, insurance certificate expiry, FSMA Sanitary Transportation certification status, cold chain capability, RFID tracking capability, preferred lane assignments, and rolling performance metrics (on-time pickup/delivery rates, damage claim rate, temperature excursion rate, compliance score, performance tier by evaluation period, total shipments executed, rejected loads count). Single authoritative record for carrier identity, qualification, and operational performance within the supply domain. Subsumes carrier performance tracking — no separate product exists for this concept.'
}

Table supply.route {
  route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a transportation route definition within the agricultural supply chain network. Primary key for the route data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural routes are commodity-specific (grain corridors, cold-chain produce lanes). commodity_class is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to commodity enables commodity-specific route planning']
  destination_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination facility node (distribution center, food manufacturer, retailer DC, port terminal) where the route ends. Used for delivery planning, customer service level management, and COOL compliance.']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the origin facility node (farm, elevator, warehouse, processing plant) where the route begins. Used for network planning, freight cost estimation, and FSMA traceability. Links to the facility/location master.']
  preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred carrier or 3PL assigned to this route based on contracted rates, service performance, and commodity handling capabilities. Used by TMS for automated carrier selection and freight tendering.']
  avg_transit_delay_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Historical average delay in hours experienced on this route beyond the estimated transit time, calculated from actual shipment performance data. Used for realistic delivery promise date calculation, carrier performance benchmarking, and TMS transit time buffer planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SOX financial reporting controls for transportation cost records.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for freight cost values on this route (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN). Required for multi-currency international route cost management and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the route destination location. Required for COOL compliance, import/export documentation, APHIS permits, and international freight cost calculation.']
  distance_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total planned distance of the route in kilometers, as calculated from GPS/RTK route geometry or GIS network analysis. Used for freight cost estimation, fuel consumption planning, driver hours-of-service compliance, and carbon footprint calculation.']
  distance_miles decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total planned distance of the route in miles, the standard unit for US domestic trucking freight rate calculations, FMCSA compliance, and carrier contract negotiations. Maintained alongside kilometers for dual-unit reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this route definition is no longer valid for TMS planning. Null indicates an open-ended route with no planned expiry. Used for seasonal route deactivation, road closure management, and contract expiry alignment.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this route definition becomes valid and available for TMS transportation planning. Supports seasonal route activation (e.g., harvest season routes, post-spring-thaw routes) and contract-aligned route management.']
  estimated_transit_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated total transit time in hours for the route under standard operating conditions, accounting for transport mode, distance, and typical delays. Used for delivery scheduling, customer promise dates, and cold chain temperature exposure calculations.']
  freight_cost_per_km decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard contracted freight cost per kilometer for this route in the base currency (USD). Used by TMS for freight cost estimation, carrier rate benchmarking, and transportation spend analytics. Derived from carrier contracts negotiated in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route and its designated carriers have been validated as compliant with FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rules (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O), including vehicle sanitation, temperature control, and shipper-carrier agreement requirements.']
  gps_geometry_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URI pointing to the GPS/RTK route geometry definition stored in Esri ArcGIS or the GIS layer repository. Contains the spatial polyline representation of the route for map visualization, precision agriculture VRA delivery planning, and geofencing. Follows GeoJSON or Esri feature service reference convention.']
  has_border_crossing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route includes one or more international border crossings requiring customs documentation, phytosanitary certificates, APHIS import/export permits, or COOL compliance. Triggers additional documentation requirements in SAP S/4HANA GTS.']
  has_harvest_congestion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route experiences significant traffic congestion during harvest season due to high agricultural vehicle volumes. Used by TMS for seasonal transit time adjustments, route optimization, and harvest logistics planning.']
  has_spring_weight_restriction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is subject to seasonal spring weight limit restrictions (spring thaw load restrictions) that reduce allowable gross vehicle weight on roads during spring months. Affects load planning, carrier selection, and delivery scheduling during spring planting season.']
  has_toll_road boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route includes toll roads, bridges, or tunnels that incur additional freight costs. Used for accurate freight cost estimation, carrier rate negotiations, and transportation spend analytics.']
  hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is approved for transportation of hazardous agricultural materials including pesticides, fertilizers (ammonium nitrate), and fumigants under DOT/EPA hazmat regulations. Drives carrier certification requirements and routing restrictions through populated areas.']
  is_cold_chain_corridor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is designated as a cold chain corridor requiring temperature-controlled transport equipment (refrigerated trailers, insulated containers) throughout the entire route. Critical for perishable agricultural commodities, fresh produce, and dairy products under FSMA Sanitary Transportation rules.']
  max_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius that must not be exceeded throughout the cold chain corridor route. Null for non-cold-chain routes. Used for carrier equipment specification, temperature monitoring alert thresholds, and FSMA compliance documentation.']
  max_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross vehicle weight in kilograms for this route, determined by the most restrictive bridge weight limit or road weight restriction along the route. Critical for load planning of heavy agricultural commodities (grain, fertilizer, equipment) to prevent infrastructure damage and regulatory violations.']
  min_temp_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius that must be maintained throughout the cold chain corridor route. Null for non-cold-chain routes. Used for carrier equipment specification, temperature monitoring alert thresholds, and FSMA compliance documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, known road hazards, carrier-specific requirements, or regulatory advisories relevant to this route. Used by dispatchers, logistics planners, and carrier coordinators for route-specific guidance.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the route origin location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for COOL compliance, APHIS phytosanitary documentation, customs declarations, and international freight rate determination.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) lot tracking infrastructure is available along this route for real-time shipment visibility and LOT traceability. Supports FSMA traceability requirements and Manhattan Associates WMS RFID integration.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transportation route, used in TMS, BOL documentation, and carrier communications. Serves as the business-facing identifier across SAP S/4HANA SD/MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the transportation route (e.g., \'Midwest Grain Corridor - Chicago to Memphis\'). Used in TMS planning screens, carrier briefings, and operational reporting.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route definition. \'active\' indicates the route is available for TMS planning; \'seasonal\' indicates availability limited to specific periods; \'restricted\' indicates regulatory or physical constraints are in effect; \'under_review\' indicates pending validation.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route by its supply chain function: \'inbound\' for farm inputs to storage/processing, \'outbound\' for finished goods to customers, \'inter_facility\' for transfers between company facilities, \'last_mile\' for final delivery to farm or retailer, \'backhaul\' for return load optimization.']
  seasonal_restriction_end_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month number (1-12) when seasonal restrictions end on this route. Used alongside seasonal_restriction_start_month for automated TMS seasonal planning windows and carrier capacity pre-booking.']
  seasonal_restriction_start_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month number (1-12) when seasonal restrictions (spring weight limits, harvest congestion, winter road closures) begin on this route. Used for automated TMS seasonal planning and proactive carrier notification.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this route (e.g., truck for over-the-road, rail for grain shuttle, intermodal for container movements, barge for river grain transport). Drives carrier selection, freight cost estimation, and transit time calculation in TMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this route record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data synchronization between TMS and SAP S/4HANA, and audit compliance.']
  vra_delivery_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is designated for precision agriculture VRA (Variable Rate Application) input deliveries, requiring GPS-guided last-mile delivery to specific field zones. Integrates with Trimble Ag Software and John Deere Operations Center for precision delivery coordination.']
  waypoint_count varchar [note: 'Number of intermediate waypoints (stops, transfer points, relay stations, grain elevators) along the route between origin and destination. Used for multi-stop delivery planning, driver relay scheduling, and route complexity assessment in TMS.']

  Note: 'SSOT for planned transportation routes used in agricultural commodity and input movements across the supply chain network. Defines origin facility node, destination facility node, intermediate waypoints, distance (miles/km), estimated transit time by transport mode, preferred carrier assignments, seasonal road restriction flags (spring weight limits, harvest season congestion), cold chain corridor designation, toll and border crossing points, bridge weight limit constraints, and GPS/RTK route geometry reference. Used by TMS for route optimization, freight cost estimation, and VRA delivery planning for precision agriculture inputs. Single authoritative record for transportation route definitions within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.freight_booking {

  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Freight bookings in agriculture are made on behalf of a specific customer account for billing, credit exposure checking, and freight cost allocation. Bookings can precede shipment confirmation; credit']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider assigned to execute this freight booking.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Freight bookings in agriculture are commodity-specific for rate determination, equipment type selection, and food-grade certification requirements. Linking to commodity enables automated commodity-spe']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Freight bookings in ag are made for specific commodity lots to enforce GMO segregation, organic certification, and temperature zone requirements. Booking a reefer for a certified organic lot requires ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Freight bookings are made under a specific legal entity for intercompany billing and regulatory compliance. Agriculture multinationals book freight under distinct company codes for cross-border shipme']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight bookings represent committed freight spend at the point of carrier engagement. Cost center assignment enables accrual accounting and budget consumption tracking before invoice receipt — critic']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Freight bookings must specify the customer delivery location for dock appointment scheduling, receiving-hours compliance, and FSMA facility registration verification. The existing destination_location']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: A freight booking is created to arrange carrier transport for a commercial delivery order. TMS-to-OMS integration in agriculture logistics requires this link for delivery order fulfillment tracking, f']
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.delivery_schedule. Business justification: Freight bookings in agriculture inbound logistics are triggered by procurement delivery schedule lines becoming due. Linking freight_booking to delivery_schedule enables inbound freight planning again']
  destination_location_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination facility, customer delivery point, or distribution center where the freight is to be delivered.']

Adding freight_rate_id to freight_booking links the booking to the rate schedule used for pricing. This enables rate audit (comparing booked ra']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Freight bookings generate payable amounts requiring GL account coding for AP accrual and cost posting. Agriculture commodity logistics operations code freight bookings to specific GL accounts (e.g., f']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic commodity freight bookings must reference organic certification to ensure certified transport conditions are booked (food-grade, non-contaminated equipment). This FK supports NOP organic chain']
  primary_freight_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin facility, farm, warehouse, or storage location from which the freight will be picked up.']

Agriculture operations segment freight spend by commodity type or business unit at booking level for manage']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Freight bookings are created to fulfill confirmed sales orders in agriculture logistics. TMS-to-OMS integration and freight cost allocation to sales orders require this direct link. No existing FK on ']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.service_contract. Business justification: A freight booking is made against a service contract that defines the committed volume, rate basis, and service level with the carrier. Adding service_contract_id to freight_booking normalizes the con']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated shipment record once the booking transitions to active carrier execution and a Bill of Lading (BOL) is issued.']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent transport order that initiated this freight booking. Links the booking to the originating logistics request.']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: A freight booking reserves capacity on a specific transport unit (container, reefer truck, railcar). freight_booking.equipment_type is a string that denormalizes the unit type; with transport_unit_id ']
  accessorial_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total additional charges beyond the base freight rate, including fuel surcharges, detention, lumper fees, layover, and FSMA-required temperature monitoring fees.']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Air Waybill (AWB) number for air freight shipments. Serves as the air transport equivalent of the BOL. Populated only for air mode bookings.']
  base_freight_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross base freight charge for this booking before accessorial charges, fuel surcharges, or discounts. Core component of the freight cost monetary triplet.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading (BOL) number issued upon carrier pickup. Legal document of title and receipt for agricultural goods in transit. Populated after pickup confirmation.']
  booked_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Number of units, pallets, cases, or bulk quantity (in applicable unit of measure) booked for transport. Supports lot traceability and inventory reconciliation.']
  booked_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of cargo booked, expressed in cubic meters. Used alongside weight for dimensional weight calculations and equipment capacity utilization.']
  booked_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of cargo booked with the carrier, expressed in kilograms. Used for freight rate calculation, weight-based compliance checks, and capacity planning.']
  booking_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable booking confirmation number assigned upon carrier acceptance. Used for cross-system reference and carrier communication.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight booking. Tracks progression from initial draft through carrier confirmation, active transit, completion, or cancellation.']
  booking_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the freight booking was confirmed with the carrier or 3PL. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction.']
  cancellation_deadline timestamp [note: 'The latest date and time by which this booking can be cancelled without incurring cancellation penalties. Defined by carrier contract terms.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for booking cancellation. Populated only when booking_status is cancelled. Used for carrier performance analysis and dispute resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this freight booking record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which freight amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency operations and IFRS financial reporting.']
  delivery_appointment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Confirmed date and time of the scheduled delivery appointment at the destination. Used for receiving dock scheduling and customer SLA compliance tracking.']
  freight_rate decimal(14,4) [note: 'Contracted or spot freight rate applied to this booking, expressed as a monetary amount per unit of measure (e.g., per MT, per mile, per load). Commercially sensitive pricing data.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations between shipper and consignee. FOB and CIF are common in agricultural commodity trade. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_food_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking requires food-grade transport equipment and handling procedures compliant with FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule. Critical for edible agricultural commodities.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials (e.g., agricultural chemicals, pesticides, fertilizers) requiring special handling, placarding, and regulatory documentation under DOT HazMat regulations.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot Number (LOT) identifying the specific production batch of agricultural commodity being transported. Enables end-to-end traceability from farm to table and supports recall management.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode used for this freight booking. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, documentation (BOL vs AWB), and rate structures.']
  pickup_appointment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Confirmed date and time of the scheduled pickup appointment at the origin location. Used for dock scheduling and carrier arrival coordination.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the booked quantity. Agricultural-specific units include Bushel (BU), Hundredweight (CWT), and Metric Ton (MT) for bulk commodities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|BU|CWT|PAL|EA|CS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Unit basis on which the freight rate is applied. Determines how total freight charges are calculated from the rate and booked quantity or distance.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tag identifier associated with the shipment for real-time traceability tracking throughout the supply chain. Supports lot-level traceability per FSMA Traceability Rule.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier contracted for this booking, defining transit time commitments and priority handling. Spot bookings are ad-hoc market-rate engagements outside contracted lanes.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for carrier-specific handling instructions, including loading sequence requirements, fragile cargo notes, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance notes, or customer-specific delivery requirements.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain compliance during transport. Paired with minimum temperature to define the acceptable cold chain range for perishable goods.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain compliance during transport. Mandatory for perishable agricultural products under FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule.']
  total_freight_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Net total freight cost for this booking, calculated as base freight amount plus all accessorial charges. Used for COGS allocation, OPEX reporting, and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this freight booking record was most recently modified.']

  Note: 'Records the confirmed booking of freight capacity with a carrier or 3PL for a specific transport order. Captures booking confirmation number, carrier assignment, booked capacity (weight/volume), equipment type (reefer, dry van, flatbed, hopper), pickup appointment date/time, delivery appointment date/time, freight rate applied, accessorial charges, booking status, and cancellation terms. Bridges transport order to actual carrier execution and BOL issuance.'
}
