  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOL record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record retention compliance (minimum 2-year retention for traceability records).']
  electronic_signature varchar [note: 'Captured electronic signature data (encoded string or reference token) from the driver or consignee acknowledging receipt of goods. Supports paperless BOL workflows and serves as legal proof of delivery for dispute resolution.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Commercial freight payment terms indicating who bears the cost and risk of transportation. FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are standard Incoterms used in agricultural commodity trade. Determines title transfer point and insurance responsibility.']
  fsma_traceability_lot_code varchar [note: 'FSMA Section 204 Traceability Lot Code (TLC) assigned to the food commodity on this BOL. Mandatory for foods on the Food Traceability List (FTL) under the FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials (e.g., agricultural chemicals, pesticides, fertilizers classified as hazmat). When true, DOT hazmat regulations and EPA reporting requirements apply.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the Bill of Lading was officially issued and signed, representing the principal business event date for the shipment contract. Required for FSMA traceability records and COOL compliance documentation.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Internal facility or location code for the shipment origin point (farm, warehouse, processing plant). Maps to the storage location master in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS for inventory reconciliation.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in the shipment. Used for trailer space planning, dock scheduling, and warehouse receiving operations in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Indicates who is responsible for paying the freight charges: prepaid (shipper pays), collect (consignee pays), or third-party billing. Drives accounts payable and receivable processing in SAP S/4HANA FI.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number used to track the shipment within the carrier\'s system. Distinct from the BOL number; used for freight claims, shipment tracing, and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  required_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum required temperature in degrees Celsius for the shipment during transit. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and FSMA sanitary transportation rule adherence for perishable agricultural products.']
  required_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required temperature in degrees Celsius for the shipment during transit. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and FSMA sanitary transportation rule adherence for perishable agricultural products.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Tamper-evident seal number affixed to the trailer or container at point of loading. Used for food safety integrity verification, FSMA compliance, and customs/border security. Verified at delivery against the BOL.']
  shipper_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the shipper/consignor origin location including street, city, state, and postal code. Required on the BOL for FSMA traceability and carrier routing.']
  shipper_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the party tendering the agricultural goods for shipment (the shipper/consignor). Typically the farm operation, processing facility, or distribution center. Required field on the BOL legal document.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this BOL record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements for the agricultural shipment, such as temperature control, fragile commodity handling, hazmat requirements, or FSMA-mandated sanitary transport conditions (e.g., \'Keep refrigerated 2-4°C; no co-loading with non-food items\').']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Required temperature zone classification for the shipment during transit. Critical for cold chain management of perishable agricultural commodities. Drives carrier equipment selection and FSMA sanitary transportation compliance.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of all goods on the BOL in kilograms, including packaging and pallets. Used for freight rate calculation, carrier weight compliance (DOT limits), and inventory reconciliation.']
  total_net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total net weight of the agricultural commodity only (excluding packaging and pallets) in kilograms. Used for commodity pricing, yield calculations, and USDA reporting.']
  total_piece_count varchar [note: 'Total number of handling units (cases, bags, pallets, or other packaging units) included in the shipment as listed on the BOL. Used for carrier receipt verification and warehouse receiving in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOL record was last modified in the source system. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and change data capture (CDC) processing.']
  vehicle_number varchar [note: 'Carrier vehicle or trailer identification number (license plate, DOT vehicle number, or fleet unit number) used to transport the shipment. Required for FSMA sanitary transportation compliance and carrier audit trails.']

  Note: 'Legal shipping document (BOL) issued for each agricultural shipment, serving as contract of carriage, receipt of goods, and title document. Stores BOL number, shipper and consignee details, carrier name, origin and destination addresses, commodity description, lot numbers (LOT), total weight and piece count, freight terms (FOB/CIF), special handling instructions (temperature, fragile, hazmat), seal numbers, driver and vehicle details, and electronic signature capture. Mandatory for FSMA food safety traceability and COOL compliance.'
}

Table supply.lot_trace {
  lot_trace_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key for each lot traceability record in the supply chain. Primary key for the lot_trace data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: FSMA forward traceability and recall management require knowing which customer account received a commodity lot. Regulators and food safety teams query \'which customer received lot X?\' directly. The e']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Certificate of Analysis (COA) record associated with this lot, documenting quality test results including MRL, moisture, and grade assessments.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Lot traceability is fundamentally commodity-specific. Linking to commodity enables MRL compliance enforcement, grading standard application, FSMA TLC source validation, and commodity-specific storage ']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master commodity lot record in the inventory domain (inventory.commodity_lot) that this trace record tracks through the supply chain.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Lot traces must be attributed to a legal entity for FSMA regulatory traceability and financial reporting. Agriculture food safety regulations require lot-level company attribution for recall managemen']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lot traceability records track commodity movement costs by cost center for crop enterprise costing and COGS allocation. Agriculture cost accounting requires tracing commodity lots to cost centers to c']
  current_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility (warehouse, processing plant, cold storage, distribution center) currently holding custody of this lot. Updated at each chain-of-custody transfer event.']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: FSMA Section 204 requires traceability records to capture the \'ship to\' location as a Key Data Element. Linking lot_trace to customer.delivery_location directly satisfies this regulatory requirement, ']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: FSMA traceability requires Key Data Elements linking a commodity lot back to the originating field crop plan (field, planned inputs, agronomic practices). Food safety audits and recall investigations ']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: FSMA Subpart S traceability requires linking harvested commodity lots to the specific field operation (harvest event) that created them. Auditors and food safety teams use this to trace a recalled lot']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Lot trace records support inventory valuation and COGS posting to GL accounts. Agriculture inventory accounting requires linking commodity lot movements to GL accounts for accurate COGS recognition, i']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: Lot traceability for GMO/non-GMO status requires linking to the specific GMO declaration for BE Act disclosure compliance, IP protocol chain-of-custody documentation, and export market approval verifi']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Lot traceability records must reference the grading standard applied to the lot for quality dispute resolution, regulatory audits, and grade-based pricing verification. Agriculture domain experts expe']
  harvest_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.harvest_event. Business justification: FSMA traceability and commodity recall investigations require linking a lot trace record directly to the harvest event (date, field, equipment, operator, moisture at harvest). This is a standard suppl']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: FSMA lot traceability requires linking each lot trace record to the inspection that assessed it. lot_trace has certificate_of_analysis_id but no direct inspection FK. Inspection is the quality event; ']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_master. Business justification: Lot traceability records must reference the product master for product-level recall management, COA requirements, organic certification verification, and FSMA preventive controls applicability. Agricu']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Lot traceability for MRL compliance requires recording the specific MRL threshold tested against for export market eligibility, recall management, and regulatory audit defense. Different export market']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic lot traceability requires linking to the specific NOP organic certification covering the lot for USDA organic integrity verification, organic premium pricing validation, and chain-of-custody d']
  origin_field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific agricultural field or farm unit where this lot was harvested or produced. Supports farm-to-fork traceability and GLOBALG.A.P. chain of custody requirements.']
  origin_management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Precision agriculture traceability and sustainability certification programs (e.g., identity-preserved grain, carbon credit programs) require tracing commodity lots to the specific management zone of ']
  parent_lot_lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent lot_trace record from which this lot was derived through a split or merge event. Null for original lots. Enables reconstruction of the full lot genealogy tree for recall tracing.']
  product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_certification. Business justification: Lot traces for certified commodities (non-GMO Project verified, GlobalG.A.P., fair trade) must reference product certifications for full chain-of-custody. lot_trace already has organic_certification_i']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Lot traces are attributed to a profit center for commodity P&L tracking by business segment. Agriculture companies track grain lot profitability (purchase cost vs. sale price) by profit center for seg']
  protection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.protection_event. Business justification: MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) compliance verification requires tracing a commodity lot back to the specific pesticide/agrochemical application event. lot_trace has mrl_threshold_id and mrl_test_date but']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: FSMA lot traceability and recall management in agriculture require anchoring each lot to the originating purchase order. MRL compliance audits and regulatory investigations trace lots back to the PO f']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Contract-specific lot segregation: grain elevators must prove lots shipped satisfy contracted grade, GMO status, and origin requirements of a specific sales contract. FSMA and GlobalG.A.P. traceabilit']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: FSMA forward traceability requires linking commodity lot movements directly to sales orders. Recall management and regulatory reporting need lot_trace→sales.order without joining through shipment. Agr']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Full farm-to-fork lot traceability in agriculture requires linking commodity lots back to the seed variety used to grow the crop. This FK enables IP protection for licensed varieties, variety-specific']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: lot_trace is the SSOT for end-to-end traceability of agricultural commodity lots moving through the supply chain. Adding shipment_id directly links each lot trace event to the shipment carrying that l']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Lot trace tracks the current physical location of a commodity lot. FSMA traceability and lot disposition decisions require the precise storage location (bin/silo level), not just the facility. Enables']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: FSMA lot traceability and recall management in agriculture require knowing the originating vendor contract for each lot — it governs quality specs, GMO status, organic certification, and MRL threshold']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: FSMA traceability and recall management require identifying the vendor/supplier source of every lot. origin_farm_name is a denormalized vendor name. Replacing with vendor_id enables vendor scorecardin']
  yield_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.yield_record. Business justification: FSMA farm-to-fork traceability and food safety audits require linking each commodity lot trace to the originating harvest yield record. Grain merchandisers and food safety auditors expect this link to']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot traceability record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and regulatory record retention compliance.']
  custody_transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent chain-of-custody transfer event for this lot. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the current custody state. Updated at each facility handoff.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date on which the final disposition of this lot was recorded (sold, consumed, destroyed, etc.). Used for lot lifecycle closure reporting and regulatory record retention.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'The ultimate disposition of this lot upon closure of the traceability record. \'sold\' = delivered to customer; \'consumed\' = used in internal processing; \'destroyed\' = disposed due to quality failure or recall; \'donated\' = donated to food bank or relief; \'returned\' = returned to supplier; \'pending\' = disposition not yet determined.']
  fsma_tlc varchar [note: 'The FSMA Section 204-compliant Traceability Lot Code (TLC) assigned to this lot at the first point of land-based receiving or transformation. Required for FDA food traceability rule compliance and one-up/one-back recall response.']
  fsma_tlc_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the point in the supply chain where the FSMA Traceability Lot Code (TLC) was first assigned. Required by FDA food traceability rule to establish the Critical Tracking Event (CTE) origin.']
  harvest_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this lot was harvested from the originating field. Critical for Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) compliance calculations and shelf-life determination. Sourced from Climate FieldView or John Deere Operations Center harvest records.']
  lot_event_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the lot creation or transformation event. \'original\' = first-time lot creation at harvest; \'split\' = divided from a parent lot; \'merge\' = combined from multiple parent lots; \'repack\' = repackaged without commodity change; \'reclassify\' = grade or classification change.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known, human-readable lot identifier assigned at origin (farm or first point of aggregation). Serves as the primary business identifier for traceability across the supply chain. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM batch number and FSMA Traceability Lot Code (TLC).']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lot within the supply chain traceability record. Drives operational workflows, recall response, and final disposition reporting. \'on_hold\' indicates quarantine or quality hold pending investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_transit|in_storage|processing|on_hold|sold|consumed|destroyed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  moisture_content_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The moisture content of the commodity in this lot expressed as a percentage. Critical for storage safety (mold/mycotoxin prevention), weight adjustment calculations, and grade determination. Measured at intake and updated after drying operations.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this lot was produced or harvested. Required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance and import/export documentation.']
  phi_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) was observed between the last pesticide application and the harvest date for this lot. True = PHI compliance confirmed; False = PHI violation detected. Mandatory for food safety and EPA compliance.']
  phi_days_observed varchar [note: 'The actual number of days elapsed between the last recorded pesticide application and the harvest date for this lot. Used to verify PHI compliance against the label-required interval.']
  quantity_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'The current net quantity of commodity in this lot expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit. Maintained alongside MT for domestic market reporting and USDA WASDE alignment.']
  quantity_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'The current net quantity of commodity in this lot expressed in Metric Tons (MT). Updated at each custody transfer or split/merge event. Principal quantitative measure for the lot.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is currently subject to an active recall or withdrawal action. True = lot implicated in recall; False = no active recall. Triggers immediate operational holds and one-up/one-back notification workflows.']
  recall_reference varchar [note: 'The official FDA or USDA recall reference number associated with this lot if recall_flag is True. Enables cross-referencing with regulatory recall databases and internal recall management records.']
  requires_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot requires continuous cold chain management throughout storage and transportation. True = cold chain mandatory; False = ambient storage acceptable. Drives carrier selection and facility assignment.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'The current active RFID tag EPC (Electronic Product Code) associated with this lot for automated identification and tracking through the supply chain. Supports real-time location tracking in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this lot traceability record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this lot. Exceedance triggers a cold chain exception event and potential quality hold.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this lot to maintain food safety and quality. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and facility assignment validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this lot traceability record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change detection for downstream consumers.']

  Note: 'SSOT for end-to-end traceability of agricultural commodity lots (LOT) moving through the supply chain from farm origin through storage, processing, and delivery to final customer. Captures lot number, commodity type, originating field/farm, harvest date, GRN reference, current custody facility, chain-of-custody transfer events with timestamps, RFID tag history, split/merge lot events, COA linkage, MRL test results, PHI compliance status, and final disposition (sold, consumed, destroyed). Single authoritative record for lot-level supply chain traceability supporting FSMA Section 204 food traceability rule, GLOBALG.A.P. chain of custody, and one-up/one-back recall response.'
}

Table supply.delivery_event {
  delivery_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each discrete delivery milestone event record within the supply chain execution lifecycle.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transport vehicle (truck, rail car, vessel, or aircraft) executing the shipment at the time of this event. Supports fleet telematics integration with John Deere Operations Center and carrier compliance tracking.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.bill_of_lading. Business justification: delivery_event has bol_number (STRING) as a text reference to the BOL. Adding bill_of_lading_id FK normalizes this to the BOL master record. Delivery events (pickup confirmation, delivery confirmation']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) organization responsible for executing the shipment at the time of this event. Supports carrier performance analytics and FSMA carrier compliance tracking.']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: FSMA traceability requires linking each delivery event to the specific commodity lot physically transferred. Regulators and food safety auditors need one-step-forward traceability from lot to delivery']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Delivery events (detention, demurrage, accessorial charges) generate costs posted to a cost center. Agriculture logistics operations track delivery exception costs by cost center for operational cost ']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Delivery events (proof of delivery, exceptions, custody transfers) occur at customer delivery locations in agriculture. Linking enables delivery performance reporting by customer site, appointment com']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: A delivery event records physical execution of a commercial delivery order. Proof-of-delivery reconciliation and delivery performance reporting require linking physical events to the authorizing deliv']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual driver or carrier agent who recorded or executed this delivery event. Used for accountability, proof-of-delivery validation, and workforce compliance tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, warehouse, port, border crossing, or waypoint location where this delivery event occurred. Enables geospatial traceability and facility-level compliance reporting.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Delivery events trigger financial postings (detention fees, demurrage charges) to specific GL accounts. Agriculture commodity delivery operations require GL account coding on delivery events for autom']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Delivery events in agriculture trigger receiving inspections (grading, condition assessment, FSMA compliance check). Linking the delivery event to the inspection conducted at receipt supports food saf']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: delivery_event has fsma_tlc_reference (STRING) referencing a traceability lot code. Adding lot_trace_id FK directly links delivery milestone events (pickup, delivery, custody transfer) to the specific']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Delivery events may directly generate nonconformances (cold chain breach, seal damage, temperature exceedance on arrival). Linking delivery_event to nonconformance supports FSMA food safety incident t']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Customer-facing delivery status reporting requires linking delivery events directly to sales orders. Sales teams track order fulfillment milestones without joining through shipment. FSMA traceability ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment record against which this delivery event is recorded. Links the event to the full shipment context including BOL, carrier, and commodity details.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Delivery events trigger stock position updates in the WMS/ERP (goods receipt posting). Inventory reconciliation and proof-of-delivery workflows require knowing which stock position was created or upda']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Delivery events record physical drop-off at a specific storage bin or silo. WMS and FSMA traceability require the exact storage location (not just facility) where delivery occurred for FIFO/FEFO compl']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_order. Business justification: A delivery event (pickup confirmation, in-transit scan, proof of delivery) occurs during the execution of a transport order. delivery_event currently links to shipment and bill_of_lading, but not dire']
  transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_unit. Business justification: A delivery event captures discrete milestones (pickup, in-transit scan, delivery) associated with a specific transport unit. delivery_event has asset_id -> equipment.asset for the broader equipment re']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'The Air Waybill number for air freight shipments at the time of this event. Provides cross-reference to the air transport document for perishable agricultural commodity air shipments.']
  border_crossing_point varchar [note: 'Name or code of the official border crossing or port of entry where the shipment crossed an international boundary. Relevant for USDA APHIS phytosanitary inspection, FDA import screening, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.']
  cold_chain_breach boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recorded temperature at this event exceeded the allowable temperature range for the shipment, constituting a cold chain breach. Triggers food safety exception workflows and FSMA non-conformance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery event record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement of event reporting latency.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance at the time of a border crossing or customs clearance event. Applicable for international agricultural commodity shipments subject to USDA APHIS phytosanitary inspection and FDA import controls.']
  customs_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official customs entry or declaration reference number assigned by the customs authority at border crossing or clearance events. Required for international agricultural trade compliance and USDA APHIS import documentation.']
  delay_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The number of minutes by which this delivery event was delayed relative to the scheduled event timestamp. Populated for exception events of type \'delay\'. Used for carrier performance KPI calculation and SLA breach reporting.']
  delivery_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of delivery attempts made for this shipment at the destination. Increments with each failed delivery attempt. Used to trigger escalation workflows and carrier penalty clauses after maximum attempts are exceeded.']
  electronic_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or storage path to the captured electronic signature obtained at delivery confirmation. Serves as legally admissible proof of delivery for food safety compliance and commercial dispute resolution.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes recorded by the driver, carrier agent, or dispatcher at the time of the event. Captures contextual information such as access instructions, recipient comments, or site conditions not covered by structured fields.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this event within the shipment\'s delivery event chain. Enables chronological ordering of all milestone events for a given shipment to reconstruct the full delivery timeline for FSMA audit trail and analytics.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the delivery event record, indicating whether the milestone was successfully completed, failed, or is awaiting confirmation.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the delivery milestone event physically occurred in the real world. This is the principal business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Critical for FSMA transportation compliance audit trail and cold chain monitoring.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the delivery milestone event. Drives workflow routing, compliance audit trail, and real-time shipment visibility dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pickup_confirmed|in_transit_checkpoint|border_crossing|customs_clearance|delivery_attempt|proof_of_delivery|exception — promote to reference product]']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the nature, cause, and immediate actions taken for an exception event. Populated when exception_type is not null. Supports carrier claims, insurance documentation, and FSMA corrective action records.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exception condition when event_type is \'exception\'. Identifies the nature of the delivery failure or deviation for root cause analysis, carrier claims, and FSMA incident reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delay|damage|refusal|temperature_breach|lost|partial_delivery — promote to reference product]']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery event record satisfies all applicable FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rule requirements, including temperature control documentation, shipper-carrier agreement coverage, and traceability lot code capture.']
  fsma_tlc_reference varchar [note: 'The FSMA Traceability Lot Code associated with the commodity at this delivery event. Required under FSMA Section 204 for Foods on the Food Traceability List (FTL) to enable rapid traceback in food safety incidents.']
  geofence_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the GPS coordinates recorded at this event fall within the expected geofenced zone for the scheduled waypoint or facility. Used to detect route deviations and unauthorized stops in agricultural supply chain security monitoring.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the location where this delivery event was recorded. Captured via GPS-enabled device on the transport vehicle or driver mobile device. Supports real-time shipment visibility and route compliance verification.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the location where this delivery event was recorded. Paired with gps_latitude to provide precise event geolocation for route analytics and compliance audit.']
  photo_evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or storage path to photographic evidence captured at the time of the delivery event. Used for damage documentation, delivery confirmation, and FSMA food safety incident records.']
  pod_captured boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electronic proof of delivery has been captured for this event. True when an electronic signature, photo evidence, or digital confirmation has been recorded. Critical for FSMA transportation compliance and customer dispute resolution.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who accepted or refused the delivery at the destination. Captured as part of proof of delivery documentation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR and applicable privacy regulations.']
  rfid_scan_code varchar [note: 'The RFID tag code scanned at this delivery event checkpoint. Enables automated lot-level traceability and real-time inventory visibility across the agricultural supply chain without manual barcode scanning.']
  scheduled_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which this delivery milestone event was expected to occur. Compared against event_timestamp to calculate on-time performance and identify delays for carrier KPI reporting and SLA management.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number on the container or trailer at the time of this delivery event. Verified at pickup, border crossing, and delivery to detect tampering. Required for food safety chain-of-custody documentation under FSMA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this delivery event record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer ingestion audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  temperature_reading_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or cargo temperature in degrees Celsius recorded at the time of this delivery event. Critical for cold chain compliance monitoring of perishable agricultural commodities. Compared against shipment-level min/max temperature thresholds to detect cold chain breaches.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery event record was last modified. Supports data lineage tracking and change detection in the Silver layer lakehouse pipeline.']

  Note: 'Captures discrete delivery milestone events during shipment execution — including pickup confirmation, in-transit checkpoints, border crossing, customs clearance, delivery attempt, proof of delivery (POD), and exception events (delay, damage, refusal). Records event type, event timestamp, GPS coordinates, driver/carrier agent ID, facility or waypoint, event notes, and electronic signature or photo evidence reference. Feeds real-time shipment visibility and FSMA transportation compliance audit trail.'
}

Table supply.freight_rate {
  freight_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a freight rate record. Primary key for the freight_rate data product within the supply domain.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider with whom this freight rate is contracted. Links to the carrier master record for carrier selection optimization and freight audit.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural freight rates are commodity-specific — grain, produce, and livestock rates differ significantly based on handling requirements, temperature needs, and regulatory compliance costs. Linking']
  destination_location_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination facility, food manufacturer, retailer distribution center, or port for the freight lane covered by this rate. Defines the endpoint of the origin-destination (O-D) lane.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Freight rates are used to pre-accrue freight costs to GL accounts during rate-based accrual processes. Agriculture logistics finance teams use rate cards to generate freight expense accruals by GL acc']
  primary_freight_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin facility, farm, elevator, or distribution center for the freight lane covered by this rate. Defines the starting point of the origin-destination (O-D) lane for carrier selection and route optimization.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.route. Business justification: Freight rates in agricultural logistics are negotiated per lane/route. Adding route_id to freight_rate normalizes the lane definition, linking the rate schedule directly to the route\'s origin/destinat']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent freight contract or rate agreement under which this rate schedule is established. Supports contract lifecycle management and volume commitment tracking.']
  base_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The contracted base freight rate amount, expressed in the unit defined by rate_type (e.g., USD per mile, USD per CWT, USD per MT, USD per BU, or flat USD amount). This is the principal monetary fact for the rate record, excluding fuel surcharge and accessorial charges. Used as the foundation for freight cost estimation and carrier invoice audit.']
  commitment_discount_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base rate when the contracted volume commitment tier is met or exceeded. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5% discount). Incentivizes volume commitment with carriers and 3PLs for agricultural commodity lanes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight rate record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the freight rate is denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, CAD for Canadian Dollar, EUR for Euro). Required for multi-currency freight cost estimation and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination of the freight lane. Required for export documentation, customs compliance, and international freight rate applicability.']
  destination_zip_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code of the destination point for the freight lane. Used for lane-level rate matching in TMS when a specific facility ID is not available. Supports zone-based and mileage-based rate calculations.']
  detention_rate_per_hour decimal(10,4) [note: 'Accessorial charge rate applied per hour when a carrier\'s equipment is detained beyond the contracted free time at origin or destination. A key component of the accessorial rate schedule for agricultural commodity shipments where loading/unloading delays are common.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this freight rate becomes binding and applicable for freight cost estimation and carrier selection. Aligns with contract start date in carrier agreements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of transportation equipment required for this rate. Refrigerated (reefer) equipment is required for cold chain management of perishable agricultural commodities. Hopper trailers are used for bulk grain. Tankers for liquid commodities. Critical for cold chain compliance under FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this freight rate is no longer valid for new shipment cost estimation. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no defined expiration. Retained post-expiry for freight audit variance analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  free_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours of free loading/unloading time included in the base rate before detention charges begin to accrue. Standard free time for agricultural commodity shipments is typically 2 hours. Critical for managing detention/demurrage cost exposure at grain elevators and processing facilities.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual freight payment terms defining who bears the freight cost and when title transfers. FOB (Free On Board) origin and destination are standard agricultural commodity trade terms. Prepaid = shipper pays; Collect = consignee pays; Third Party = billed to a third party. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA SD billing and SAP MM purchasing conditions.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this freight rate is associated with a carrier that has been verified as compliant with the FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O). True = carrier has confirmed FSMA compliance for this lane and equipment type. Required for food safety audit and carrier qualification.']
  fuel_surcharge_formula varchar [note: 'Description or formula code defining how the fuel surcharge is calculated, typically indexed to the US Department of Energy (DOE) weekly diesel retail price index. Examples: \'DOE_TABLE_LOOKUP\', \'DOE_PERCENT_OF_BASE\', \'FIXED_CPM\' (cents per mile). Drives dynamic fuel surcharge computation in TMS freight cost estimation.']
  fuel_surcharge_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The current or baseline fuel surcharge rate expressed as a percentage of the base rate or as a fixed amount per mile (depending on fuel_surcharge_formula). Updated periodically based on DOE index triggers. Used in freight cost estimation and carrier invoice audit.']
  lane_distance_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Contracted or calculated mileage for the origin-destination freight lane. Used for per-mile rate calculations, fuel surcharge computation, and route optimization analysis. Typically derived from PC*MILER or equivalent mileage engine integrated with TMS.']
  lumper_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Accessorial charge for lumper (third-party unloading labor) services at destination facilities such as food manufacturer warehouses and retail distribution centers. Included in the accessorial rate schedule when the carrier is responsible for arranging unloading labor.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum freight charge applicable regardless of shipment weight, volume, or distance. Ensures carrier cost recovery for small or short-haul shipments. Applied during freight cost estimation when the calculated base rate falls below this threshold.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin of the freight lane. Required for cross-border agricultural commodity movements and Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance.']
  origin_zip_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code of the origin point for the freight lane. Used for lane-level rate matching in TMS when a specific facility ID is not available. Supports zone-based and mileage-based rate calculations.']
  rate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the freight rate record, used in carrier negotiations, freight audit, and SAP S/4HANA condition record references. Enables cross-system traceability between TMS, ERP, and carrier portals.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight rate record. Active rates are available for freight cost estimation and carrier selection. Expired rates are retained for freight audit variance analysis against historical invoices. Pending rates are awaiting carrier confirmation. Suspended rates are temporarily unavailable.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the freight rate is calculated and applied. Per mile applies to truckload movements; per CWT (Hundredweight) and per MT (Metric Ton) apply to weight-based commodity pricing; per BU (Bushel) applies to grain and oilseed movements; flat rate applies to fixed-price lane agreements. Drives freight cost estimation logic in TMS.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this freight rate record originated. SAP_S4HANA indicates the rate was created in SAP S/4HANA MM condition records; TMS indicates origin from the Transportation Management System; MANUAL indicates direct entry. Supports data lineage and freight audit traceability.']
  stop_off_charge decimal(10,4) [note: 'Accessorial charge applied for each additional stop beyond the primary origin-destination pair on a multi-stop shipment. Common in agricultural distribution routes serving multiple farm locations or delivery points. Part of the accessorial rate schedule.']
  temp_max_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered by this rate. Applicable to refrigerated equipment rates for perishable agricultural commodities. Enforces FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule cold chain requirements.']
  temp_min_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum required temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered by this rate. Applicable to refrigerated equipment rates for perishable agricultural commodities (produce, dairy, meat). Enforces FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule cold chain requirements.']
  transit_days varchar [note: 'Contracted standard transit time in calendar days for the origin-destination lane at this rate. Used for delivery scheduling, cold chain management planning, and carrier service level evaluation. Critical for perishable agricultural commodity shipments.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for which this freight rate applies. Truckload (TL) and less-than-truckload (LTL) are primary modes for domestic agricultural commodity movements; rail and intermodal for bulk grain; ocean for export; air for high-value perishables. Aligns with BOL and AWB documentation requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight rate record was last modified. Supports change tracking, freight audit variance analysis, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  volume_tier_max decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum contracted volume (in the unit defined by rate_type) applicable for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper bound. Defines the upper boundary of the volume commitment tier for tiered discount rate structures.']
  volume_tier_min decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum contracted volume commitment (in the unit defined by rate_type, e.g., CWT, MT, BU, or number of loads) required to qualify for this rate tier. Supports volume-based discount structures in carrier contracts. Used for commitment tracking and carrier performance management.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume tier commitment quantities (volume_tier_min, volume_tier_max). Aligns with the rate_type unit. CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), and BU (Bushel) are standard agricultural commodity units. Ensures consistent volume commitment tracking across carrier contracts.']

  Note: 'SSOT for freight rate schedules negotiated with carriers and 3PLs for agricultural commodity transportation. Stores rate type (per mile, per CWT, per MT, per BU, flat rate), origin-destination lane, commodity class (NMFC), equipment type, effective and expiry dates, base rate, fuel surcharge formula (DOE index-linked), accessorial rate schedule (including detention/demurrage, lumper, stop-off charges), minimum charge, currency, and contracted volume tier with commitment discount. Used for freight cost estimation, carrier selection optimization, and freight audit variance analysis against actual invoices. Single authoritative record for contracted freight pricing within the supply domain.'
}

Table supply.service_contract {
  service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_contract']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.carrier. Business justification: A supply chain service contract is primarily established with a carrier or 3PL provider. service_contract.vendor_id links to the procurement vendor record, but carrier_id provides direct linkage to th']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Carrier service contracts in agriculture are commodity-specific, defining temperature requirements, FSMA obligations, and committed volumes per commodity type. A domain expert expects service contract']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Carrier service contracts are executed by a specific legal entity. Multi-entity agriculture operations require company code on contracts for contract liability reporting, intercompany freight allocati']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carrier service contracts commit freight spend to a cost center for commitment accounting and budget consumption tracking. Agriculture logistics controllers assign cost centers to contracts to monitor']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or team responsible for managing this service contract, including SLA monitoring, renewal negotiations, and counterparty relationship management. Links to workforce/HR master data.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Carrier service contracts drive freight cost accruals and committed spend that must map to GL accounts for budget vs. actual freight expense reporting. Agriculture finance teams use contract GL assign']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: Logistics service contracts are negotiated by and scoped to specific sales organizations in multi-org agriculture companies. Contract performance reporting, freight spend analysis by sales org, and ca']
  parent_contract_service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this contract to a master framework agreement or umbrella contract of which this is a sub-agreement or addendum. Null for standalone contracts. Enables contract hierarchy navigation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Carrier service contracts are attributed to a profit center for logistics contract cost tracking and segment P&L reporting. Agriculture logistics contracts are managed at profit center level to enable']
  quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Service contracts for food-grade agricultural transport require counterparties to hold specific quality certifications (GFSI, organic, FSMA) as contract conditions. Linking service_contract to quality']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Logistics service contracts with carriers are established to fulfill volume commitments in sales contracts. Contract performance reporting, freight cost allocation, and committed-volume tracking requi']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/carrier master record in the supply domain representing the contracted service provider. Links to the carrier or 3PL party master for SLA tracking, payment processing, and performance analytics.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract automatically renews upon expiry unless either party provides notice of termination within the notice period defined in termination_notice_days. Drives contract expiry alerting and renewal workflow in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  base_freight_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contracted base freight rate per unit of measure (per MT, per load, per km, or per CWT as defined by freight_rate_uom). Represents the primary commercial term of the agreement. Confidential as it reflects negotiated pricing strategy.']
  cargo_insurance_min_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required cargo insurance coverage amount in USD that the carrier or 3PL must maintain. Validated against certificate of insurance (COI) during contract onboarding and annual review.']
  cargo_insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty is contractually required to maintain cargo insurance coverage for agricultural commodities transported under this agreement. True triggers insurance certificate validation during onboarding and annual renewal.']
  committed_loads_per_period varchar [note: 'Minimum number of loads (truck, rail car, container) committed per contract period (weekly, monthly, or annually as defined by volume_period_type). Complements committed_volume_mt for load-based capacity planning and take-or-pay enforcement.']
  committed_volume_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed volume in Metric Tons (MT) per contract period that the shipper commits to tender to the carrier or 3PL. Used for take-or-pay penalty calculations and capacity planning. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM outline agreement target quantity.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned at execution (e.g., SC-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference in SAP S/4HANA MM, TMS, and legal document management. Aligns with SAP outline agreement number.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service contract. Draft indicates the contract is under negotiation; active indicates it is binding and operational; expired indicates the term has lapsed without renewal; terminated indicates early cancellation; suspended indicates temporarily paused due to dispute or regulatory hold.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service contract by counterparty category. Distinguishes carrier agreements (trucking, rail, ocean, air), third-party logistics (3PL) contracts, cold storage operator agreements, freight broker arrangements, customs broker engagements, and general logistics service provider contracts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier|3pl|cold_storage|freight_broker|customs_broker|logistics_service|intermodal — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this service contract record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this contract (base_freight_rate, penalty amounts, incentive amounts). Typically USD for domestic US agricultural logistics; EUR or other codes for international operations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the service contract becomes legally binding and operationally active. Used for SLA enforcement start, rate applicability, and FSMA compliance obligation commencement.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the service contract term ends. Nullable for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow and rate renegotiation processes in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  freight_rate_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure against which the base_freight_rate is applied. Agricultural logistics commonly uses per Metric Ton (MT), per Bushel (BU), per Hundredweight (CWT), per load, or per kilometer depending on commodity and mode.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counterparty has acknowledged and agreed to comply with FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O) requirements within this contract. Mandatory for all food-grade agricultural logistics agreements.']
  fsma_obligation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of specific FSMA Sanitary Transportation obligations assigned to the carrier or 3PL under this contract, including vehicle cleaning and sanitization requirements, temperature monitoring, and written procedures for food safety.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country/state code identifying the legal jurisdiction whose laws govern this contract (e.g., US-IL for Illinois, USA for federal US law). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and dispute resolution venue.']
  incentive_clause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of financial incentive or bonus terms awarded for exceeding SLA targets (e.g., OTD above 98%, zero temperature exceedances, volume over-performance). Confidential commercial term used in carrier performance management.']
  liability_insurance_min_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount in USD. Covers third-party bodily injury and property damage claims arising from logistics operations under this contract.']
  otd_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted minimum on-time delivery (OTD) percentage the carrier must achieve over the measurement period (e.g., 95.00%). Triggers SLA penalty clauses when actual OTD falls below this threshold. Key KPI for carrier performance scorecards.']
  penalty_clause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of financial penalty terms triggered by SLA breaches (OTD failures, temperature exceedances, volume shortfalls, cargo damage). Includes penalty calculation methodology (per-load flat fee, percentage of freight charge, or liquidated damages). Confidential commercial term.']
  service_scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the logistics services covered under this contract, including lane definitions (origin-destination pairs), commodity types handled (grain, produce, livestock feed, refrigerated goods), equipment types, and any special handling requirements such as HACCP-compliant cold chain or FSMA-regulated food transport.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this service contract record originated (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for MM outline agreements, ORACLE_NETSUITE for NetSuite contract records, MANUAL for paper-based contracts digitized into the platform). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  temp_compliance_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contracted minimum percentage of cold chain shipments that must remain within the contracted temperature range (temp_min_celsius to temp_max_celsius) throughout transit. SLA breach triggers penalty clauses and FSMA corrective action requirements.']
  temp_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered under this contract. Defines the upper bound of the contracted temperature compliance threshold. Exceedances trigger SLA penalties and FSMA non-compliance events.']
  temp_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered under this contract. Defines the lower bound of the contracted temperature compliance threshold for refrigerated or frozen agricultural commodities (produce, dairy, meat). Null for ambient/dry contracts.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the contract was terminated early (prior to expiry_date). Null for contracts that expire naturally or are still active. Used for financial accrual cutoff and carrier performance record closure.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days advance written notice required by either party to terminate or elect not to renew this contract. Drives contract management alerts and legal review workflows.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for early contract termination. Used for carrier relationship analytics, legal risk reporting, and procurement sourcing strategy. Null for active or naturally expired contracts.']
  transit_time_target_hours varchar [note: 'Contracted maximum transit time in hours from pickup to delivery for standard lanes covered under this agreement. Core SLA metric used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement and penalty trigger evaluation.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation covered by this service contract. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (FMCSA for road, FRA for rail, FMC for ocean, FAA/IATA for air) and FSMA sanitary transportation requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) of the most recent modification to this service contract record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  volume_period_type varchar [note: 'Time period granularity to which committed_volume_mt and committed_loads_per_period apply. Determines the cadence for take-or-pay calculations and volume shortfall penalty assessments.']

  Note: 'SSOT for supply chain service contracts with carriers, 3PLs, cold storage operators, and logistics service providers. Captures contract number, counterparty legal name and type, contract effective and expiry dates, service scope (lanes, modes, commodity types covered), committed volume (MT/loads per period), SLA terms (transit time targets, on-time delivery percentage, temperature compliance thresholds), penalty and incentive clauses, FSMA compliance obligations, insurance requirements (cargo, liability, workers comp), and contract status (draft/active/expired/terminated). Single authoritative record for logistics service agreements within the supply domain — distinct from procurement input supply contracts owned by the procurement domain.'
}

Table supply.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each supply chain network node (grain elevator, cold storage warehouse, processing plant, distribution center, port terminal, cross-dock facility, fumigation station). Primary key for the facility master record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Facilities are owned and operated by a specific legal entity (company code) for asset ownership, regulatory registration (USDA/FDA), and financial reporting. Agriculture groups with multiple legal ent']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each supply facility (grain elevator, cold storage, processing plant) is managed as a cost center in agriculture financial management. Facility operating costs (labor, utilities, maintenance) are trac']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Facility management in agriculture (grain elevators, cold storage, processing plants) requires tracking the responsible employee for FSMA registration, OSHA compliance, and operational accountability.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: Grain elevators, storage facilities, and processing plants are physically located on land parcels. Land management, property tax assessment, easement compliance, and real-estate records require linkin']
  primary_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Logistics facilities (grain elevators, cold storage warehouses) are commodity-specialized. This FK enables commodity-specific storage capacity planning, FSMA facility registration by commodity type, a']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the facility used for carrier routing, BOL documentation, regulatory registration, and logistics network mapping.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, building, dock area) for the facility, supplementing the primary street address for precise carrier and logistics routing.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the facility is located, used for logistics zone assignment, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and carrier rate calculation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the facility is located. Used for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, import/export documentation, and international logistics routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  dock_count_crossdock varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for cross-docking operations where inbound shipments are transferred directly to outbound without storage. Relevant for distribution centers and cross-dock facilities in the agricultural supply chain.']
  dock_count_inbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for inbound receiving operations (GRN processing). Used for inbound logistics scheduling, carrier appointment planning, and WMS receiving capacity management.']
  dock_count_outbound varchar [note: 'Number of dock doors designated for outbound shipping operations (SO fulfillment). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, carrier appointment planning, and WMS shipping capacity management.']
  dock_count_total varchar [note: 'Total number of loading and unloading dock doors at the facility. Used for throughput capacity planning, dock scheduling in TMS/WMS, and carrier appointment management.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the facility used across TMS, WMS, and ERP systems (e.g., SAP plant code, Manhattan WMS location code). Unique across the logistics network and referenced on BOL, shipment, and transport order documents.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the supply chain facility as registered with USDA/FDA and used in logistics documentation, carrier communications, and BOL/AWB records.']
  facility_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the facility within the supply chain network. Drives eligibility for shipment routing, carrier assignment, and logistics planning in TMS and WMS.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply chain network node by its primary operational function. Drives logistics routing rules, compliance requirements, and capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain_elevator|cold_storage|processing_plant|distribution_center|port_terminal|cross_dock|fumigation_station — promote to reference product]']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA-issued food facility registration number required under FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food for human or animal consumption. Must be renewed biennially.']
  fsma_registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the FDA FSMA food facility registration expires. FSMA requires biennial renewal in even-numbered years (October 1 – December 31). Used for compliance monitoring and renewal workflow triggers.']
  fsma_registration_status varchar [note: 'Current FDA FSMA food facility registration status. Facilities with suspended or cancelled registration cannot legally ship food products. Drives compliance gating in shipment and transport order processing.']
  has_rfid_infrastructure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is equipped with RFID reader infrastructure for automated lot (LOT) tracking, pallet identification, and real-time inventory visibility. Enables FSMA traceability lot code (TLC) scanning at facility entry/exit points.']
  has_weighbridge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has a certified weighbridge (truck scale) for gross and tare weight measurement of inbound and outbound agricultural commodity shipments. Required for accurate net weight determination on BOL and GRN documents.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), used for geospatial route optimization in TMS, GIS network mapping in Esri ArcGIS, and RTK-based precision logistics.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), used for geospatial route optimization in TMS, GIS network mapping in Esri ArcGIS, and RTK-based precision logistics.']
  operates_24_hours boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility operates on a 24-hour, continuous basis. When true, overrides operating_hours_start and operating_hours_end for scheduling purposes. Common for major port terminals and large grain elevators during harvest season.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Standard daily closing time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for carrier appointment scheduling, TMS delivery window planning, and dock availability management.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Standard daily opening time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for carrier appointment scheduling, TMS delivery window planning, and dock availability management.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the facility location, used for carrier rate zones, geographic clustering in GIS analysis, and regulatory reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this facility master record originates (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master, Manhattan Associates WMS Location Master, Granular Farm Management). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the facility is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping (USDA, FDA, EPA, OSHA), tax determination, and logistics lane planning.']
  storage_capacity_bu decimal(16,2) [note: 'Maximum rated storage capacity of the facility expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit of measure. Particularly relevant for grain elevators and on-farm storage. Complements MT capacity for dual-unit reporting.']
  storage_capacity_mt decimal(14,3) [note: 'Maximum rated storage capacity of the facility expressed in Metric Tons (MT). Used for inventory planning, shipment routing, and capacity utilization analytics across the agricultural supply chain network.']
  temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the facility\'s primary temperature zone. Used to validate cold chain compliance for inbound and outbound shipments and to configure IoT temperature monitoring alerts.']
  temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the facility\'s primary temperature zone. Used to validate cold chain compliance for inbound and outbound shipments and to configure IoT temperature monitoring alerts.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature zone classification of the facility\'s primary storage environment. Drives cold chain management requirements, carrier equipment type selection, and FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance for temperature-sensitive agricultural commodities.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., America/Chicago, America/Los_Angeles). Used for accurate scheduling of carrier appointments, operating hours, and timestamp normalization across multi-timezone logistics networks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility master record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in Databricks lakehouse Silver layer processing and audit compliance.']
  usda_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official USDA-issued facility registration number assigned by APHIS or AMS for grain elevators, processing plants, and other regulated agricultural facilities. Required for USDA-inspected operations and federal market reporting.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all supply chain network nodes used in agricultural logistics — grain elevators, cold storage warehouses, processing plants, distribution centers, port terminals, cross-dock facilities, and fumigation stations. Stores facility name, type, physical address, GPS coordinates, storage capacity (MT/BU by commodity), temperature zone configuration, USDA/FDA facility registration number, FSMA facility registration status, operating hours, dock count and type (inbound/outbound/cross-dock), RFID reader infrastructure, weighbridge availability, and facility manager contact. Single authoritative record for logistics network node identity within the supply domain.'
}

Business justification: Agricultural processing plants operate under specific USDA grading standards (grain grading, produce grading). This FK supports quality control operations, USDA inspection compliance reporting, and gr']

Business justification: Plants (grain elevators, processing facilities) operate under specific HACCP plans as required by FSMA Preventive Controls rules. supply.plant is distinct from supply.facility and has no existing qual']

  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: In agriculture ERP (SAP-style), a plant is assigned to a sales organization for order fulfillment determination and revenue attribution. This organizational link drives order routing, pricing procedur']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: Processing plant siting requires linking the plant to its land parcel for property tax assessment, deed records, zoning compliance, and USDA/FSA registration. supply.facility already has parcel_id; su']
  parent_plant_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plant (parent_plant_id)']
  primary_commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural plants (grain elevators, packing houses, cotton gins) are specialized for primary commodity types. This FK enables commodity-specific capacity planning, USDA/FSMA registration by commodit']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Plants (processing/storage facilities) are attributed to a profit center for plant-level P&L reporting.   quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Plants in agriculture must maintain quality certifications (SQF, BRC, GFSI) for customer audits and regulatory compliance. supply.plant has organic_certified_flag and fsma_compliant_flag (denormalized']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building, suite, or unit number.']

  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the plant facility, used for documentation transmission.']
