  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant facility is certified to handle hazardous materials such as pesticides or fertilizers.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant facility record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant facility for route optimization and logistics planning.']
  loading_dock_count varchar [note: 'Number of loading docks available at the plant facility for inbound and outbound logistics operations.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant facility for route optimization and logistics planning.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours of the plant facility, used for shipment scheduling and carrier coordination.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the plant facility in its lifecycle.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant facility is certified for organic product handling and storage.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the plant facility.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the plant facility, used across supply chain systems and documentation.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Official business name of the plant facility.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the plant facility based on its primary operational function within the agricultural supply chain.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the plant facility location.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant facility is equipped with RFID technology for lot tracking and traceability.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the plant facility is located.']
  storage_capacity_cubic_meters decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total storage capacity of the plant facility measured in cubic meters, critical for warehouse and cold storage planning.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant facility has temperature-controlled storage capabilities for cold chain management.']
  third_party_logistics_provider_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant facility is operated by a third-party logistics provider rather than owned directly.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant facility location, used for scheduling and shipment coordination.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table supply.transport_unit {
  transport_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for transport_unit']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or third-party logistics provider (3PL) responsible for operating this transport unit.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Transport units in agriculture are often commodity-dedicated (food-grade tankers, grain hopper cars, refrigerated containers). This FK supports FSMA sanitary transport compliance, cross-contamination ']
  parent_transport_unit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on transport_unit (parent_transport_unit_id)']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the transport unit was acquired or entered into service by the organization.']
  capacity_volume_m3 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the transport unit measured in cubic meters.']
  capacity_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the transport unit measured in kilograms.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the food safety certification for this transport unit expires and requires renewal.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification or registration number issued by regulatory authority for food safety compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transport unit record was first created in the system.']
  current_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the current physical location or facility where the transport unit is stationed.']
  food_grade_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transport unit meets food safety certification standards for transporting agricultural products and food.']
  gps_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transport unit is equipped with GPS tracking capability for real-time location monitoring.']
  height_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'External height dimension of the transport unit measured in meters.']
  home_location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the primary or home base location where the transport unit is normally stationed when not in use.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection performed on the transport unit.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance service was performed on the transport unit.']
  length_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'External length dimension of the transport unit measured in meters.']
  manufacture_year varchar [note: 'Year in which the transport unit was manufactured.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the transport unit.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory safety or compliance inspection of the transport unit.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance service on the transport unit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or operational notes about the transport unit.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the ownership model of the transport unit.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the transport unit was retired from active service and removed from the operational fleet.']
  rfid_tag_number varchar [note: 'Unique RFID tag identifier attached to the transport unit for automated tracking and traceability.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Empty weight of the transport unit itself, excluding any cargo, measured in kilograms.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transport unit has refrigeration or climate control capability for cold chain management.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature threshold that the transport unit can maintain for temperature-sensitive cargo, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature threshold that the transport unit can maintain for temperature-sensitive cargo, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  transport_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transport unit indicating its operational state.']
  unit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or registration number for the transport unit (e.g., container number, trailer number, railcar number).']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transport unit by physical form factor and mode of transport.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transport unit record was last modified in the system.']
  width_m decimal(8,2) [note: 'External width dimension of the transport unit measured in meters.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for transport_unit. Referenced by transport_unit_id.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each stock position record in the agricultural inventory system. Primary key for the stock_position data product.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Stock position release status and valuation in grain/commodity agriculture depend on the associated COA (moisture, grade, MRL compliance). Inventory managers and food safety auditors require this link']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: USDA warehouse position reporting and commodity-level inventory management require direct commodity identification on stock positions. Elevator operators and grain handlers organize all position repor']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity lot or batch record (LOT/GRN) associated with this stock position, enabling FIFO/FEFO rotation, traceability, and pre-harvest interval (PHI) compliance tracking.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Stock positions are valued and reported within a legal entity (company code) for consolidated financial statements. Multi-entity agriculture operations require company code assignment on inventory pos']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory valuation postings (balance sheet stock accounts) require GL account assignment per valuation class. Agriculture finance teams reconcile stock value to GL for period-close and GAAP/IFRS inve']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Stock positions with qty_in_quality_inspection must reference the triggering QM inspection record. QM stock release decisions, SAP QM usage decisions, and regulatory hold reporting all require this di']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: USDA NOP certified handlers must maintain organic chain-of-custody at the stock position level. Linking stock_position to organic_certification enables real-time organic segregation compliance verific']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP plant (facility or farm site) under which this stock position is managed. Supports multi-site inventory visibility and plant-level reporting.']
  pledged_loan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.loan. Business justification: USDA CCC commodity loans and operating lines of credit require tracking which stock positions are pledged as collateral. Agriculture lenders and FSA programs mandate this linkage for loan administrati']
  primary_stock_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the agricultural commodity, seed, chemical, or fertilizer held in this stock position. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM material master.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (grain bin, cold storage, warehouse, field stockpile) where this inventory position is held. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM storage location and Manhattan Associates WMS location master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for this stock position. For harvested commodities, this may align with the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) or shelf-life date. Drives FEFO rotation, quality holds, and food safety compliance. Null for non-perishable materials.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date the material was received into inventory at the storage location (GRN posting date). Used for FIFO rotation sequencing, aging analysis, and inbound supply chain performance reporting.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number from SAP S/4HANA MM or Manhattan Associates WMS that originated this stock position. Provides the inbound receiving document reference for audit and traceability.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-14) identifying the agricultural product for global supply chain interoperability, retail scanning, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material in this stock position is a Genetically Modified Organism (GMO). Required for USDA NOP organic certification compliance, COOL labeling, and customer segregation requirements.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material in this stock position holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification. Drives segregation requirements, premium pricing, and organic supply chain compliance.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent WMS-driven cycle count performed for this stock position. Used to assess inventory accuracy, schedule next count, and support audit compliance for GAAP/IFRS inventory verification.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory movement (goods receipt, goods issue, transfer, or adjustment) recorded against this stock position. Used for slow-moving and obsolete inventory identification.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the material in this stock position was manufactured, processed, or harvested. Used for FIFO/FEFO rotation, shelf-life calculation, and traceability under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last pesticide or chemical application and harvest for this lot. Ensures Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) compliance and food safety regulatory adherence.']
  price_variance_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Variance between the standard cost and actual purchase price for this stock position in USD. Captured at period-end for GAAP/IFRS inventory valuation adjustments and commodity price risk reporting.']
  qty_available decimal(18,4) [note: 'Available-to-promise (ATP) quantity calculated as on-hand quantity minus reserved quantity in the primary unit of measure. Represents the uncommitted inventory available for new sales orders or production requirements.']
  qty_blocked decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material in blocked stock status (not available for use) in the primary unit of measure. Includes recalled, quarantined, or damaged inventory pending disposition decision.']
  qty_in_quality_inspection decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material currently held in quality inspection stock pending QM release in the primary unit of measure. Supports HACCP, FSMA, and GLOBALG.A.P. quality hold management.']
  qty_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material currently on hand at the storage location in the primary unit of measure (CWT, BU, or MT). Represents the gross inventory position before reservations or holds.']
  qty_reserved decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the material reserved against open sales orders, production orders, or transfer orders in the primary unit of measure. Reduces available-to-promise (ATP) quantity for new commitments.']
  qty_shrinkage decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity lost to shrinkage (moisture loss, spoilage, handling losses) recorded against this stock position in the primary unit of measure. Used for shrinkage analysis, COGS adjustment, and post-harvest handling performance reporting.']
  rotation_method varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation method applied to this stock position for picking and consumption sequencing. FIFO (First In First Out) and FEFO (First Expired First Out) are standard for agricultural commodities and perishables to minimize spoilage and ensure food safety compliance.']
  sap_accounting_doc_number varchar [note: 'SAP FI accounting document number (BELNR) generated for the inventory valuation posting associated with this stock position. Provides the audit trail reference for GAAP/IFRS financial reporting and SOX compliance.']
  sscc varchar [note: 'GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC-18) assigned to the logistics unit (pallet, bin, container) holding this stock position. Enables RFID-based tracking and WMS integration for supply chain traceability.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock position record. Active indicates normal inventory; expired indicates stock past its shelf life or PHI; recalled indicates a regulatory or quality recall event; quarantined indicates hold pending investigation; disposed indicates stock written off.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA stock type classification indicating the usability of the inventory. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection stock is pending QM release; blocked stock is not available; in-transit stock is between locations; returns stock is pending disposition.']
  total_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total book value of this stock position in US dollars (USD), calculated as on-hand quantity multiplied by unit price. Used for balance sheet inventory reporting, period-end valuation snapshots, and GAAP/IFRS financial reporting.']
  unit_price_usd decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory valuation price per primary unit of measure in US dollars (USD). For standard cost materials, this is the standard price; for moving average materials, this is the current moving average price. Used for period-end inventory valuation and COGS calculation.']
  uom_primary varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for quantity reporting on this stock position. Agricultural commodities use industry-standard units: CWT (Hundredweight), BU (Bushel), MT (Metric Ton). Seeds and chemicals may use KG, LB, BAG, or EA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CWT|BU|MT|KG|LB|TON|EA|BAG — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this stock position record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage, and audit trail compliance.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'Date as of which the inventory valuation snapshot was captured for this stock position. Used for period-end financial reporting, GAAP/IFRS inventory valuation, and commodity position reporting.']
  valuation_method varchar [note: 'Inventory valuation method applied to this stock position. Standard cost uses a predetermined price; moving average recalculates price with each goods movement; FIFO (First In First Out) values inventory based on oldest cost layers. Supports GAAP and IFRS inventory accounting.']
  wms_position_number varchar [note: 'Manhattan Associates WMS internal position or license plate number for this stock position. Used for WMS-to-SAP reconciliation, cycle count management, and real-time inventory visibility.']

  Note: 'Core master record of current inventory positions across all agricultural storage locations (grain bins, cold storage, warehouses, field stockpiles). Tracks on-hand, reserved, and available-to-promise quantities in CWT, BU, and MT by material, commodity lot, and location. Includes stock type (unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked), valuation class, unit price, total value, and valuation method (standard cost, moving average, FIFO). Supports period-end valuation snapshots capturing valuation date, quantity, unit price, total value in USD, price variance, and SAP accounting document reference for GAAP/IFRS inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and commodity position reporting. Serves as the SSOT for real-time stock levels, inventory valuation, and period-end financial inventory reporting. Supports GS1 GTIN and SSCC identifiers for global supply chain interoperability. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.'
}

Table inventory.commodity_lot {
  commodity_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each commodity lot record in the agricultural inventory system. Primary key for the commodity_lot data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a physical lot/batch of agricultural commodity tracked through the supply chain.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Commodity lots must reference the commodity master for USDA grade standards, MRL thresholds, storage requirements, and exchange pricing. Grain elevator operations require this link for grade complianc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Commodity lots are assigned to cost centers for crop enterprise cost accounting — e.g., corn lot from field 5 maps to the corn enterprise cost center. Standard agriculture ERP configuration for crop P']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: Commodity lots represent actual harvested production. Linking to the enterprise budget that planned the crop enables actual vs. planned yield and cost variance analysis — a fundamental agriculture fin']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: FSA farm program reporting, crop insurance claims, and farm-level P&L require tracing commodity lots to the producing farm unit. origin_field_id covers field-level traceability but farm_unit is the FS']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.equipment_field_operation. Business justification: Harvest traceability (GlobalG.A.P., farm-to-table) requires linking each commodity lot to the field operation that produced it. Agriculture traceability audits demand knowing which equipment operation']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: BE Act (Bioengineered Food Disclosure) requires GMO commodity lots to reference a formal GMO declaration for disclosure compliance. Linking enables export market eligibility checks (EU, China, Japan a']
  government_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.government_program. Business justification: Commodity lots under USDA CCC loan programs or ARC/PLC must be tracked against enrolled program records for payment calculation, compliance verification, and FSA reporting. Agriculture domain experts ']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Commodity lots are graded at elevator receipt against USDA grading standards. Linking to grading_standard enables grade compliance verification, USDA grade certificate generation, and contract settlem']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: A commodity lot is produced in a specific growing season. Linking commodity_lot to growing_season enables grain traceability back to the originating season for crop insurance, quality reporting, and g']
  loan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.loan. Business justification: Commodity lots are pledged as collateral for FSA commodity loans and operating credit lines. Agriculture lenders require lot-level collateral tracking; FSA loan administration mandates identifying whi']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: USDA NOP requires organic commodity lots to be traceable to a valid organic certification for identity preservation and premium pricing. Linking enables certification validity checks at lot level, NOP']
  origin_field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm field or parcel from which this commodity lot was harvested, enabling farm-to-table traceability per FSMA Section 204 and GLOBALG.A.P. Traceability Standard. Links to field/parcel master data managed in John Deere Operations Center and Esri ArcGIS.']
  origin_management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Sub-field grain traceability for identity-preserved and premium grain programs: commodity lots harvested from specific management zones (high/low productivity) are segregated and priced differently. A']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center assignment on commodity lots enables crop enterprise P&L reporting by commodity type or farm unit — a standard agriculture segment reporting requirement for management accounting and inv']
  reserved_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer-specific lot reservation is a core agriculture process: grain elevators and food manufacturers reserve specific commodity lots for named buyers to satisfy forward contracts, organic/GMO segre']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Contract position management and FSMA traceability: commodity lots are procured or harvested against forward sales contracts in grain trading. This FK supports contract fulfillment tracking, basis con']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed commodity lots require variety-level traceability for seed certification compliance, germination rate tracking, and replanting eligibility decisions. Seed dealers and farm operations must link se']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the current storage location (grain bin, cold storage unit, warehouse bay, or field stockpile) where this commodity lot is physically stored. Managed by Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP S/4HANA MM storage location master.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: In agriculture grain and input trading, commodity lots are received against specific vendor contracts. Linking commodity_lot to vendor_contract enables contract volume utilization tracking, contracted']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or grower who delivered this commodity lot. Required for procurement traceability, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, and vendor quality scorecarding in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  best_before_date date [note: 'The best-before date for this commodity lot, after which quality may degrade but the product may still be safe for consumption. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation logic in WMS and for customer shipment eligibility checks. Distinct from expiration_date which represents a hard safety cutoff.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued for this commodity lot by an accredited laboratory, documenting quality test results including moisture, protein, mycotoxins, pesticide residues, and heavy metals. Required for customer shipment documentation and regulatory compliance.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this commodity was produced or harvested. Required for USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, export documentation (BOL, AWB), and customs declarations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity lot record was first created in the system, typically upon GRN posting in SAP S/4HANA MM or inbound receipt confirmation in Manhattan Associates WMS. Serves as the audit trail anchor for record lineage and FSMA traceability documentation.']
  days_to_expiry varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining until the lot\'s expiration_date as of the last inventory snapshot date. Used for FEFO prioritization alerts, customer minimum shelf-life requirement checks, and proactive disposition planning. Negative values indicate the lot is past expiration.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Prescribed disposition action for this commodity lot, particularly for lots in quarantine, hold, or expired status. Determined by quality management review in SAP QM. Drives downstream WMS and logistics workflows for lot resolution.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Hard expiration date for this commodity lot, after which the product must not be sold or used for food/feed purposes. Triggers automatic lot status change to \'expired\' in WMS and SAP MM. Used as the primary FEFO sort key for lot selection in Manhattan Associates WMS pick logic.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'GMO status of the commodity lot indicating whether the crop is genetically modified, non-GMO, or identity-preserved (IP) non-GMO with documented segregation. Required for export documentation, customer labeling compliance, and USDA NOP organic certification. Identity-preserved lots require full chain-of-custody documentation.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Official USDA grade designation assigned to this commodity lot based on moisture, test weight, damaged kernels, foreign material, and other quality factors per FGIS Official Grain Standards. Grade determines market price, storage eligibility, and end-use suitability.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note reference number generated in SAP S/4HANA MM upon physical receipt of the commodity lot at a storage facility. Links the lot to the inbound delivery and purchase order for procurement traceability and inventory valuation.']
  gs1_sscc varchar [note: '18-digit GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code assigned to the pallet or container carrying this commodity lot, enabling RFID and barcode scanning for pallet-level traceability from farm to table. Supports EDI and WMS integration with Manhattan Associates.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason this commodity lot has been placed on hold or quarantine status. Examples include: failed mycotoxin test, suspected pesticide contamination, pending COA, supplier dispute, or regulatory investigation. Required when lot_status is \'hold\' or \'quarantine\'.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity lot is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP) or equivalent international organic certification. Organic-certified lots require strict segregation from conventional lots and carry premium pricing. Must be supported by a valid Certificate of Analysis (COA) from an accredited certifier.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent WMS-driven cycle count or physical inventory verification for this commodity lot. Used to assess inventory accuracy, trigger recount workflows, and support FSMA traceability audit readiness. Managed through Manhattan Associates WMS cycle count module.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric lot identifier assigned at receipt or production, used for traceability across the agricultural supply chain. Corresponds to SAP MM batch number and Manhattan WMS lot number. Serves as the primary business-facing identifier for FIFO/FEFO rotation, recall management, and FSMA Section 204 traceability.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity lot within the inventory management system. Drives FIFO/FEFO rotation logic, WMS pick eligibility, and disposition workflows. \'quarantine\' and \'hold\' statuses block lot from fulfillment until quality release. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|consumed|expired|disposed — promote to reference product]']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity lot was produced, processed, or packaged. For harvested commodities, this is the harvest date. For processed products (feed, flour, oil), this is the processing date. Used as the anchor date for shelf-life and expiration calculations.']
  moisture_content_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the commodity lot expressed as a percentage of total weight, measured at receipt or post-drying. Critical quality parameter for grain grading, shrinkage calculation, storage eligibility, and price discounting. USDA standard moisture for corn is 15.5%, wheat 13.5%, soybeans 13%.']
  quantity_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit of measure. Used for grain elevator accounting, CBOT futures hedging, and WASDE supply reporting. One bushel equals 56 lbs for corn/sorghum, 60 lbs for wheat/soybeans.']
  quantity_cwt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in hundredweight (CWT = 100 lbs), commonly used for livestock feed, cotton, and specialty crop pricing and billing. Supports SAP MM inventory valuation in CWT-denominated contracts.']
  quantity_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in metric tons (MT), the standard international trade unit. Used for export documentation, CIF/FOB contracts, and Codex Alimentarius reporting. Derived from BU quantity using commodity-specific conversion factors but stored for operational efficiency.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity lot was physically received at the storage facility and the GRN was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM. Serves as the FIFO anchor date for first-in-first-out rotation logic and the start of the storage tenure calculation.']
  received_quantity_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'Original quantity of this commodity lot at the time of GRN receipt, expressed in bushels. Preserved as the immutable receipt quantity for shrinkage calculation, inventory reconciliation, and audit trail. Distinct from quantity_bu which reflects current on-hand quantity after consumption and adjustments.']
  rotation_method varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation method applied to this commodity lot for pick sequencing in Manhattan Associates WMS. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for grain; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is required for perishable produce and feed with expiration dates. FEFO_FIFO applies FEFO when expiration dates exist, otherwise FIFO.']
  shrinkage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of quantity lost from this lot due to moisture evaporation, handling losses, or storage shrink since receipt. Calculated as (received_quantity - current_quantity) / received_quantity * 100. Used for inventory valuation adjustments, storage contract billing, and loss reporting.']
  storage_type varchar [note: 'Type of storage facility where this commodity lot is currently held. Determines applicable storage conditions, monitoring requirements, and handling procedures. Cold storage lots require temperature and humidity monitoring per FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule.']
  test_weight_lbs_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Test weight of the commodity lot measured in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu), a key grain quality indicator reflecting kernel density and market grade. Minimum test weights per USDA GIPSA: corn 56 lbs/bu (No.1), wheat 60 lbs/bu (No.1), soybeans 56 lbs/bu (No.1). Affects price premiums and discounts.']
  uom_primary varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure used for inventory management and reporting of this commodity lot (e.g., BU for grain, MT for export commodities, CWT for feed). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure configuration and Manhattan WMS UOM settings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|MT|CWT|LB|KG|TON|BAG|UNIT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commodity lot record, including quantity adjustments, status changes, quality updates, or location transfers. Supports audit trail requirements and Silver Layer delta processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Master record for each commodity lot (LOT/GRN) tracked through the agricultural supply chain. Captures lot number, GRN reference, GS1 SSCC for pallet/container-level tracking, commodity type (grain, produce, feed), crop year, origin field, GMO status, organic certification flag per USDA NOP, grade, moisture content, test weight, and expiration management (PHI date, best-before date, manufacture date, expiration status, days to expiry, disposition action). Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation logic with expiration-driven lot selection, quarantine/hold management, and full traceability from farm to table per FSMA Section 204, EU General Food Law Regulation 178/2002, and Codex Alimentarius traceability guidelines.'
}

Table inventory.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical storage location record in the inventory master. Primary key for the storage_location data product.']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Grain bins, silos, and storage structures are equipment assets requiring maintenance plans, inspections, and work orders. Linking storage_location to equipment.asset enables maintenance scheduling, de']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Storage locations belong to a legal entity for financial reporting, asset ownership, and regulatory licensing. Multi-entity agriculture operations require company code assignment on storage locations ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Storage locations (grain bins, silos, cold storage) are assigned to cost centers for overhead allocation — storage operating costs (utilities, maintenance, depreciation) are charged to the responsible']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.facility']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Farm-unit-level storage capacity planning and USDA/FSA reporting: operators must know total storage capacity per farm unit for harvest logistics, crop insurance unit assignments, and FSA farm records.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Grain elevators, warehouses, and storage facilities are major capitalized fixed assets. Linking storage_location to fixed_asset supports depreciation cost allocation to inventory operations, asset imp']
  location_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Agriculture storage facilities (grain bins, cold storage, warehouses) require a designated responsible employee for USDA licensing, GAP/HACCP food safety audits, and regulatory inspections. A domain e']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: On-farm storage structures (grain bins, silos) are assessed as improvements on a specific parcel for property tax, crop insurance, and FSA farm records. Agriculture domain experts expect storage facil']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Storage locations are assigned to profit centers for segment reporting — e.g., on-farm grain storage vs. commercial elevator storage. Supports agriculture enterprise P&L by business segment and manage']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the storage facility. Used for logistics, carrier routing, regulatory filings, and emergency services. Classified as confidential organizational address data.']
  capacity_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum rated storage capacity of this location expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard volumetric unit for grain commodities (corn, soybeans, wheat). Used for grain bin and elevator capacity planning and USDA reporting.']
  capacity_cwt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum rated storage capacity of this location expressed in Hundredweight (CWT), a unit commonly used for livestock feed, fertilizer, and certain commodity pricing in North American agricultural markets.']
  capacity_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum rated storage capacity of this location expressed in Metric Tons (MT). Used for international trade reporting, export documentation, and compliance with Codex Alimentarius and EU food safety standards.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the storage facility is located. Used for regional reporting, logistics zone assignment, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was officially commissioned and made available for operational use. Marks the start of the location\'s active lifecycle for asset management and depreciation purposes.']
  commodity_class varchar [note: 'Primary commodity classification that this storage location is designed or certified to store. Drives applicable regulatory requirements, handling procedures, and inventory segregation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|oilseed|produce|livestock_feed|seed|chemical|fertilizer|mixed — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code for the country where the storage location is situated (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'BRA\'). Used for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance and international trade documentation.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for WMS-driven physical inventory cycle counts at this storage location. Determines how often inventory accuracy is verified against system records to support FIFO/FEFO compliance and shrinkage detection.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which this storage location was or is scheduled to be permanently decommissioned and removed from active inventory operations. Nullable for active locations.']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA facility registration number assigned under the Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) for food storage facilities. Required for facilities storing human or animal food commodities subject to FDA jurisdiction.']
  fifo_fefo_method varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation method enforced for this storage location. FIFO (First-In-First-Out) is standard for grain; FEFO (First-Expired-First-Out) is required for perishables and chemicals with Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) constraints. Drives WMS pick sequencing.']
  food_safety_certification varchar [note: 'Primary food safety certification standard held by this storage facility (e.g., Safe Quality Food (SQF), British Retail Consortium (BRC), International Featured Standards (IFS), ISO 22000, HACCP). Drives customer eligibility and audit requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|IFS|GLOBALG.A.P.|ISO22000|HACCP|none — promote to reference product]']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) — a 13-digit internationally standardized identifier uniquely identifying this physical storage location within the global supply chain. Used for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), traceability, and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance.']
  gmo_segregation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is designated for Genetically Modified Organism (GMO)-segregated storage, requiring physical separation from non-GMO commodities. True = GMO segregation enforced; False = no segregation requirement.']
  humidity_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location has active humidity control systems. True = humidity-controlled; False = ambient humidity. Critical for seed storage, dried commodities, and chemical storage.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed physical inventory cycle count at this storage location. Used to track compliance with cycle count schedules and identify locations overdue for verification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or food safety inspection conducted at this storage location by USDA, FDA, or a third-party auditor. Used for compliance tracking and audit readiness.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the storage location as captured via GPS/GIS. Used for Esri ArcGIS spatial analysis, logistics routing, and emergency response planning.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric business identifier for the storage location as defined in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) and SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used for operational referencing, labeling, and system integration across Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the storage location. Controls whether inventory can be received, stored, or dispatched from this location. \'quarantined\' indicates a hold pending quality or regulatory inspection.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical storage location by its structural and functional type. Drives applicable capacity units, temperature controls, and regulatory compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain_bin|cold_storage|dry_warehouse|refrigerated_warehouse|field_stockpile|chemical_storage|seed_vault|fertilizer_bay — promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the storage location as captured via GPS/GIS. Used for Esri ArcGIS spatial analysis, logistics routing, and supply chain mapping.']
  max_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum relative humidity level (%) designed or required for this storage location. Exceeding this threshold triggers alerts for potential spoilage, mycotoxin risk, or chemical degradation.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable or designed operating temperature for this storage location in degrees Celsius. Defines the upper thermal boundary for safe storage of perishable commodities, chemicals, and seeds.']
  min_humidity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum relative humidity level (%) designed or required for this storage location. Controls moisture conditions to prevent commodity degradation, mold growth, and seed viability loss.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable or designed operating temperature for this storage location in degrees Celsius. Critical for cold storage, refrigerated warehouses, and seed vaults to maintain product viability and food safety compliance.']
  next_inspection_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory regulatory or food safety inspection at this storage location. Drives compliance calendar management and proactive audit preparation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this storage location, including special handling instructions, known constraints, maintenance history summaries, or regulatory conditions. Used by warehouse operations and compliance teams.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification, permitting storage of certified organic commodities. True = organic certified; False = not certified for organic storage.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA plant code to which this storage location belongs. A plant represents a manufacturing or distribution facility in SAP MM. Required for inventory valuation and supply chain planning.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the storage facility address. Used for logistics carrier rate determination, regional analytics, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping. Classified as confidential organizational address data.']
  sap_storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP S/4HANA MM storage location code (Lagerort) that uniquely identifies this location within a SAP plant. Used for inventory postings, Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing, and material movements in SAP.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two or three-character state or province code where the storage facility is located (e.g., \'IA\' for Iowa, \'IL\' for Illinois). Used for USDA state-level reporting, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory compliance.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location has active temperature control systems (refrigeration, heating, or climate control). True = temperature-controlled environment; False = ambient/uncontrolled temperature.']
  usda_license_number varchar [note: 'USDA Warehouse Act license number issued to this storage facility, required for federally licensed grain warehouses storing commodities under USDA oversight. Mandatory for issuing warehouse receipts and participating in USDA loan programs.']
  wms_zone_code varchar [note: 'Zone assignment code within the Manhattan Associates Warehouse Management System (WMS) that groups storage locations for pick/put-away routing, cycle counting, and labor management. Aligns with WMS zone master configuration.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical storage locations including grain bins, cold storage units, refrigerated warehouses, dry warehouses, and field stockpile areas. Captures location code, GS1 GLN (Global Location Number), facility name, storage type, capacity in BU/MT/CWT, temperature range, humidity controls, GIS coordinates, WMS zone assignment, and regulatory compliance certifications (USDA, FDA, EU food safety). Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS location master.'
}

Table inventory.material_master {
  material_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each material master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the material_master data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Linking the SAP material master to the EPA-registered agrochemical product record enables PHI/REI compliance enforcement, pesticide use reporting, and label requirement lookups. material_master.active']
  chemical_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.chemical_compliance. Business justification: Agriculture material masters for agrochemicals carry EPA registration, MRL limits, and compliance data that is authoritatively owned by chemical_compliance. This link enforces 3NF, enables regulatory ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Material master valuation class determines the GL account for all inventory postings (GR, GI, transfers). Direct FK replaces the denormalized inventory_valuation_class and supports automated GL accoun']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Material master records for commodities specify the applicable USDA grading standard for procurement specifications and quality management. This enables automated grade compliance checking during good']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: In agriculture, the approved input catalog drives material master creation — every inventory material for seeds, chemicals, and fertilizers originates from a catalog-approved input. This link enables ']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_master. Business justification: SAP material master records are created from product catalog entries. This link enables product lifecycle management, cost rollup to product catalog, and procurement planning. Agriculture ERP implemen']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Material master stores static MRL values (mrl_limit_eu, mrl_limit_codex) that become stale as regulations change. Linking to mrl_threshold enables dynamic compliance checking for export market eligibi']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: USDA NOP compliance requires verifying that materials used in certified organic operations appear on the allowed materials list (OMRI). Linking material_master to organic_certification enables allowed']
  preferred_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Approved Vendor List (AVL) process: each agricultural input material (seed, chemical, fertilizer) has a preferred/approved vendor. This supports vendor selection during requisition creation and SAP MM']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed inventory management requires linking the SAP material record to the seed variety for variety-specific seeding rate, trait package verification, and PHI compliance lookups. Seed dealers and ag re']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure (UOM) in which the material is managed in inventory (SAP MEINS). Agricultural materials use industry-standard units: BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), KG (Kilogram), GAL (Gallon). Drives quantity calculations across procurement, WMS, and sales. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|BAG|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commodity_class varchar [note: 'Agricultural commodity classification code aligned with USDA and CME Group commodity designations (e.g., \'CORN\', \'SOYBEAN\', \'WHEAT\', \'COTTON\', \'ANHYDROUS_AMMONIA\'). Used for WASDE reporting, market price linkage, and supply chain analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material master record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  fifo_fefo_indicator varchar [note: 'Inventory rotation strategy applied to this material in WMS (Manhattan Associates). FIFO (First In First Out) for commodities without expiry; FEFO (First Expired First Out) for perishables, seeds, and chemicals with shelf life. Drives pick sequencing and stock rotation compliance.']
  gmo_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is derived from or contains Genetically Modified Organisms (GMO). Required for USDA NOP organic certification exclusion, EU import labeling (Regulation EC 1829/2003), and customer COA documentation. True = GMO material; False = non-GMO.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14) for the material. Enables barcode scanning, EDI transactions, and global supply chain traceability. Used in Manhattan Associates WMS for receiving and shipping verification.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT/IATA hazardous materials classification code for transport and storage compliance. Drives storage segregation rules in WMS, TMS routing restrictions, and BOL documentation. Examples: Class 3 (flammable liquids), Class 6 (toxic substances). \'non_hazmat\' for non-regulated materials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|8|9|non_hazmat — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the material master record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the material master record in SAP (MMSTA / MSTAE). Controls whether the material can be procured, used in production, or sold. \'blocked\' prevents new transactions; \'discontinued\' signals end-of-life for inventory drawdown.']
  lot_management_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is managed at the lot/batch level (SAP XCHPF). True = batch-managed (LOT/GRN tracking required for traceability). Critical for FSMA traceability, HACCP lot recall capability, and FEFO rotation in WMS. Seeds, chemicals, and harvested commodities are typically batch-managed.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'SAP material group code (MATKL) used to classify materials for procurement, reporting, and spend analytics. Examples: \'SEED-CORN\', \'FERT-NPK\', \'CHEM-HERB\'. Enables category management and supplier segmentation.']
  material_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short description or name of the material (e.g., \'Urea 46-0-0 Fertilizer\', \'Glyphosate 41% Herbicide\', \'Yellow Corn #2\'). Corresponds to SAP MAKTX (material description). Used in inventory reports, purchase orders, and warehouse labels.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the material aligned with SAP material type (MTART). Drives inventory valuation, procurement, and storage rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|harvested_commodity|packaging|equipment_part|other — promote to reference product]']
  npk_nitrogen_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Nitrogen (N) content percentage in fertilizer materials, expressed as percentage by weight. Part of the NPK (Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium) nutrient analysis required for fertilizer labeling compliance and agronomic application rate calculations. Null for non-fertilizer materials.']
  npk_phosphorus_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Phosphorus (P2O5) content percentage in fertilizer materials, expressed as percentage by weight. Part of the NPK nutrient analysis required for fertilizer labeling and variable rate application (VRA) prescription planning. Null for non-fertilizer materials.']
  npk_potassium_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Potassium (K2O) content percentage in fertilizer materials, expressed as percentage by weight. Part of the NPK nutrient analysis required for fertilizer labeling and soil management planning. Null for non-fertilizer materials.']
  order_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used when ordering this material from suppliers (SAP BESTELLEINHEIT). May differ from base UOM (e.g., ordered in BAG but managed in KG). Critical for purchase order creation and GRN processing in SAP MM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|KG|LB|L|GAL|EA|BAG|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification or equivalent international organic certification. Drives segregation requirements in storage, separate inventory pools, and premium pricing in sales contracts.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of a crop protection chemical and harvest, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Critical for FSMA compliance and food safety scheduling. Null for non-chemical materials.']
  plu_code varchar [note: 'IFPS Price Look-Up (PLU) code assigned to fresh produce and bulk agricultural commodities for retail checkout identification. Distinguishes conventional, organic, and GMO produce at point of sale.']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard price (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'BRL\'). Required for multi-currency operations in international agricultural trade and IFRS financial reporting.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'SAP procurement type (BESKZ) indicating whether the material is externally procured, produced in-house, or both. Drives MRP (Material Requirements Planning) logic in SAP PP and purchasing strategy in SAP MM.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours — the minimum time workers must wait before re-entering a treated field after pesticide application, per EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Required for OSHA and EPA compliance, field scheduling, and worker safety documentation.']
  sap_material_number varchar [note: 'The unique material number assigned in SAP S/4HANA MM module (MATNR field). Serves as the primary business identifier linking this record to the operational ERP system. Used for procurement, inventory management, and production planning transactions.']
  sds_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or URL for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with hazardous materials (chemicals, fertilizers). Required under OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) 29 CFR 1910.1200 and GHS. Enables worker safety compliance and emergency response.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Standard shelf life of the material in days from the date of manufacture or harvest. Used to calculate expiration dates for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation, WMS lot management, and food safety compliance. Drives PHI scheduling for crop protection chemicals.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code used in warehouse management and inventory control systems (Manhattan Associates WMS). Unique identifier for a specific material-packaging-size combination within the distribution network.']
  sscc_prefix varchar [note: 'GS1 company prefix used to generate Serial Shipping Container Codes (SSCC-18) for pallet and container tracking of this material. Enables RFID-based pallet tracking and ASN (Advance Ship Notice) generation in WMS and TMS.']
  standard_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or moving average price per base unit of measure for inventory valuation in SAP MM (STPRS/VERPR). Used for COGS calculation, purchase price variance (PPV) analysis, and financial reporting under IFRS IAS 2 and GAAP ASC 330. Classified confidential as it reflects internal cost strategy.']
  storage_humidity_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage for storage of this material. Critical for grain storage (prevents mold/mycotoxin development), seed viability, and chemical stability. Used in grain bin and warehouse environmental monitoring via IoT sensors.']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this material. Used alongside minimum temperature to define the acceptable storage temperature range for WMS slot assignment, cold chain compliance, and HACCP CCP monitoring.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this material. Drives cold storage assignment in WMS, temperature monitoring alerts via IoT sensors, and HACCP critical control point (CCP) compliance for food safety. Critical for seeds, biologicals, and perishable commodities.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC-A) for retail-facing materials. Used for point-of-sale scanning, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, and retail customer EDI. Aligns with GS1 US standards.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all inventory-managed materials including seeds, fertilizers (NPK), crop protection chemicals (AI/MRL-classified), feed ingredients, harvested commodities, and packaging materials. Captures SAP material number, SKU, GS1 GTIN, SSCC for pallet/container tracking, UPC/PLU codes, unit of measure (BU, CWT, MT, KG), material group, commodity class, GMO indicator, SDS reference, MRL limits per EU Regulation 396/2005 and Codex Alimentarius standards, REI/PHI values per EPA WPS, storage condition requirements (temperature range, humidity, hazmat class), and organic certification reference per USDA NOP. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM material master.'
}

  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Agriculture procurement executes GRNs against outline agreements/contracts. Linking goods_receipt to vendor_contract enables contract volume utilization tracking, contracted price verification at rece']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier from whom goods were received. Populated for purchase receipt types; null for harvest intake or inter-facility transfers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods receipt record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  crop_season varchar [note: 'Agricultural crop season or marketing year associated with the harvested commodity (e.g., \'2024-2025\'). Used for seasonal inventory segregation, WASDE reporting alignment, and crop year cost accounting.']
  crop_variety varchar [note: 'Specific crop variety or cultivar of the harvested commodity received (e.g., \'Pioneer P1197AM\', \'Asgrow AG36X6\'). Supports variety-level traceability, seed performance tracking, and GMO segregation requirements.']
  elevator_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Grain elevator scale ticket number issued at the point of harvest intake. Captures the official weight and grade determination at the elevator. Applicable to harvest receipt types only.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for the received material. Mandatory for seed, chemical, and fertilizer inputs. Drives FEFO lot rotation and shelf-life compliance. Null for bulk grain commodities without defined shelf life.']
  fifo_fefo_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the stock rotation method applied to this receipt for lot assignment and inventory consumption sequencing. FIFO is standard for grain commodities; FEFO is applied for seed, chemical, and fertilizer inputs with expiration dates.']
  grade_determination varchar [note: 'Official USDA grade assigned to the received commodity based on test weight, moisture, damage, and other grade factors per USDA GIPSA Official Grain Standards. Drives pricing, storage assignment, and end-use routing.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the received shipment in kilograms, including packaging and container weight. Used in conjunction with tare weight to calculate net commodity weight.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) identifying the received material at the trade item level. Supports GS1 standards-based supply chain traceability and FSMA Section 204 compliance.']
  harvest_date date [note: 'Date on which the crop was harvested in the field prior to intake. Populated for harvest receipt types only. Supports FSMA Section 204 traceability linking field harvest events to inventory lot creation.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received commodity is a Genetically Modified Organism (GMO) or contains GMO material. Critical for identity preservation, segregation, labeling compliance, and export market eligibility.']
  is_organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received material is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP) or equivalent certification. Drives segregated storage, premium pricing, and organic chain-of-custody documentation.']
  migo_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP goods movement document number generated by the MIGO transaction upon posting the goods receipt. Used for financial posting reconciliation and audit trail back to SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the received commodity expressed as a percentage, measured at intake. Critical for grain quality grading, shrinkage calculation, storage condition assignment, and USDA grade determination.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net commodity weight in kilograms, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight. Represents the actual quantity of commodity received for inventory posting.']
  po_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item number within the originating Purchase Order (PO) against which this receipt is posted. Enables partial receipt tracking and PO line-level quantity reconciliation.']
  quantity_bu decimal(14,4) [note: 'Received quantity expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard unit of measure for grain commodities in North American agricultural markets. Used for commodity trading, WASDE reporting, and inventory valuation.']
  quantity_cwt decimal(14,4) [note: 'Received quantity expressed in Hundredweight (CWT), used for livestock feed, fertilizer, and certain commodity inventory positions. Supports multi-unit inventory management.']
  quantity_mt decimal(14,4) [note: 'Received quantity expressed in Metric Tons (MT), used for international trade, export documentation, and global supply chain reporting.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the goods were physically received at the facility. Used for FIFO/FEFO lot rotation, aging calculations, and regulatory traceability reporting.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt record. Drives downstream inventory posting, quality inspection workflow, and lot assignment. Rejected receipts trigger return-to-vendor or disposition processes.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the goods receipt was recorded in the system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for audit, traceability, and WMS integration.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the receipt event: purchase (supplier delivery against PO), harvest (field intake from crop operations), return (customer return), or transfer (inter-facility stock movement). Determines processing workflow and traceability requirements.']
  sscc varchar [note: 'GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC) for shipment-level tracking of the received pallet or container. Enables end-to-end supply chain traceability from supplier to storage bin.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of the empty container, truck, or packaging in kilograms. Subtracted from gross weight to determine net commodity weight at intake.']
  test_weight_lb_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Test weight of the received grain commodity expressed in pounds per bushel (lb/bu). A key USDA grade factor indicating grain density and quality. Used for grade determination and pricing adjustments.']
  uom_primary varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure in which the received quantity is officially recorded for inventory posting. Determines which quantity field (BU, CWT, MT, KG) is the authoritative inventory position.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the goods receipt record. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all goods received into inventory including supplier deliveries (GRN processing), harvest intake from field operations (capturing crop season, variety, harvest date, field/parcel of origin, elevator ticket number, gross/tare/net weight), customer returns, and inter-facility transfers. Captures GRN number, PO reference, receipt type (purchase, harvest, return, transfer), vendor/supplier, material with GS1 GTIN/SSCC for shipment-level tracking, quantity received in CWT/BU/MT, receiving location, assigned storage bin, lot assignment, quality inspection status, COA reference, moisture reading at intake, test weight, grade determination, and SAP goods movement document (MIGO). Supports FSMA Section 204 traceability, EU General Food Law Regulation 178/2002, FIFO/FEFO lot assignment at receipt, and links field harvest events to inventory stock positions and commodity lot creation.'
}

Table inventory.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each goods issue transaction record in the inventory domain.']

  asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment or vehicle (sprayer, spreader, planter, feed wagon) used to execute the goods issue or field application.']
  certification_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.certification_record. Business justification: FSMA traceability and GlobalG.A.P./organic audit requirements mandate that each goods issue to a customer references the specific certification record authorizing the shipment.   commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory lot (LOT/GRN batch) being consumed, supporting FIFO/FEFO rotation and traceability.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Goods issues are financial transactions posted within a company code. Required for multi-entity agriculture operations to attribute input costs to the correct legal entity for statutory reporting and ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center absorbing the COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) for this goods issue, used for financial allocation and reporting.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Seed, fertilizer, and pesticide goods issues in agriculture are issued to field crews for application operations. Tracking which crew received issued materials is essential for labor cost allocation, ']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Agriculture agronomists and financial controllers use th']

Linking goods_issue to gl_account supports input cost reporting by GL account, crop cost allocation, and P&L ']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice. Business justification: In order-to-cash processing, a goods_issue (outbound inventory movement for a sale) is directly associated with the customer invoice. This link supports AR reconciliation, revenue recognition posting,']

Adding stock_position_id FK links the issue to the stock position being depleted, enabling real-time inventory depletion tracking and FIFO/FEFO complia']
