Linking stock_transfer to weight_ticket supports transfer quant']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number issued for inter-facility or plant-to-plant transfers requiring carrier transport. Serves as the legal shipping document and chain-of-custody record for commodity movements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock transfer record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail creation event. Supports data lineage, compliance auditing, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  destination_storage_bin varchar [note: 'WMS-level bin identifier within the destination storage location where the material will be physically placed. Supports directed putaway logic in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  estimated_transit_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days for the material to travel from source to destination facility. Used for in-transit inventory planning, supply chain continuity, and carrier performance benchmarking.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the goods issue was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM, recording the moment inventory was decremented from the source location. Critical for real-time inventory accuracy and audit trails.']
  goods_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the goods receipt was posted at the destination location in SAP S/4HANA MM or confirmed in Manhattan Associates WMS. Marks the completion of the transfer and inventory increment at destination.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Trade Item Number (14-digit) identifying the product being transferred. Supports GS1 supply chain standards, COOL compliance, and interoperability with trading partners and regulatory systems.']
  is_inter_company boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stock transfer crosses legal entity or company code boundaries, triggering inter-company billing and financial settlement processes in SAP FI/CO. True for transfers between separate legal entities; False for intra-company movements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or instructions associated with the stock transfer, such as special handling requirements, temperature conditions, or regulatory remarks. Captured by warehouse or logistics personnel.']
  qty_transferred decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of material being transferred, expressed in the primary unit of measure (UOM). This is the principal quantitative fact of the transfer transaction.']
  qty_transferred_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Transferred quantity normalized to Metric Tons (MT) for cross-commodity comparison, international reporting, and WASDE-aligned analytics. Derived from qty_transferred and uom at ingestion time.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a quality inspection (QM inspection lot) must be completed upon goods receipt at the destination before the transferred stock is released to unrestricted use. Supports HACCP and food safety compliance.']
  rotation_method varchar [note: 'Stock rotation method applied to select the lot for this transfer. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for grain commodities; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is applied for perishables and seed with expiration dates. Ensures compliance with food safety and quality standards.']
  sap_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM movement type code governing the inventory posting logic for this transfer (e.g., 311 for storage location transfer, 641 for STO goods issue, 101 for goods receipt). Determines accounting entries and stock update behavior.']
  sap_sto_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM stock transport order document number associated with this inter-plant or inter-facility transfer. Enables reconciliation with SAP financial and materials management postings.']
  sscc varchar [note: 'GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code (18-digit) assigned to the pallet or container unit being transferred. Enables unit-level traceability through the supply chain and WMS scanning operations.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the stock transfer was initiated or the goods issue was posted in SAP MM. Used for FIFO/FEFO rotation validation, aging analysis, and supply chain reporting.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the stock transfer, sourced from SAP MM stock transport order (STO) number or Manhattan Associates WMS transfer order number. Used for cross-system traceability and operational communication.']
  transfer_priority varchar [note: 'Operational priority level assigned to the transfer, used by WMS and logistics teams to sequence execution. Critical transfers may relate to expiring lots, quality holds, or supply chain emergencies.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for initiating the stock transfer, such as stock rebalancing, FIFO/FEFO rotation enforcement, quality hold relocation, consolidation, replenishment, or pre-shipment staging. Supports audit and operational reporting.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the stock transfer workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through execution to completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|in_transit|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the stock movement scope: bin-to-bin (within same warehouse), storage location transfer (within same plant), warehouse transfer (between warehouses at same site), plant-to-plant (inter-facility), or cross-dock. Drives routing logic and documentation requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bin_to_bin|plant_to_plant|warehouse_transfer|storage_location_transfer|cross_dock|field_to_bin — promote to reference product]']
  uom varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for the transferred quantity. Agricultural commodities are commonly measured in Bushels (BU), Hundredweight (CWT), or Metric Tons (MT). Chemicals and fertilizers may use gallons (GAL) or liters (L). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CWT|BU|MT|LB|KG|TON|GAL|L — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the stock transfer record. Supports change detection, incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit trail requirements.']
  wms_transfer_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by Manhattan Associates WMS for the warehouse-level transfer order. Links the inventory movement to the WMS execution layer for bin-to-bin and warehouse transfer operations.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inventory movements between storage locations, bins, warehouses, or facilities. Captures transfer order number, source location, destination location, material with GS1 GTIN/SSCC, lot, quantity in CWT/BU/MT, transfer type (bin-to-bin, plant-to-plant, warehouse transfer), WMS transfer order reference, BOL number for inter-facility transfers, carrier, estimated transit time, and transfer reason. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS transfer order management and SAP MM stock transport orders.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cycle count event record in the WMS-driven physical inventory count system.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: USDA licensed warehouse operators must report commodity positions; cycle count scheduling and variance reporting are organized by commodity. Commodity-level count scheduling, variance analysis, and US']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory lot (LOT/GRN batch) being counted. Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation tracking and traceability of harvested commodity or input material batches.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cycle count variances generate financial postings within a company code. Required for period-close physical inventory reconciliation, SOX controls over inventory accuracy, and financial statement asse']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cycle count labor and overhead costs are charged to cost centers. Agriculture operations assign physical inventory counting costs to the responsible cost center for overhead allocation and operational']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP plant (facility/site) where the storage location resides. Supports multi-site inventory accuracy reporting and KPI aggregation.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cycle count variances generate inventory adjustment journal entries (debit/credit inventory variance accounts). Direct FK provides audit trail from physical count discrepancy to financial posting — re']
  primary_cycle_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce employee or contractor assigned to perform the physical count. Links to SAP SuccessFactors workforce record for accountability and audit.']
  primary_cycle_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the inventory item being counted (seed, chemical, fertilizer, or harvested commodity). Links to SAP MM material master.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: A WMS-driven cycle count is performed against a specific stock position — the intersection of material, storage location, and lot that defines an inventory position. cycle_count already has storage_lo']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (grain bin, cold storage, warehouse, field stockpile) where the cycle count was performed. Aligns with SAP MM storage location master and WMS location hierarchy.']
  adjustment_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the confirmed inventory variance from this cycle count has been posted as an inventory adjustment in SAP MM (MI07 Post Differences). True = adjustment posted; False = pending posting. Triggers downstream inventory_adjustment record creation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count results were reviewed and approved by the authorized supervisor. Supports SOX audit trail and FSMA compliance documentation.']
  count_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the physical inventory count was performed. Principal business event date for the cycle count, used for FIFO/FEFO rotation validation and inventory accuracy KPI reporting.']
  count_document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known WMS-generated document number uniquely identifying the cycle count event. Corresponds to the Manhattan Associates WMS count document and SAP MM physical inventory document number used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical count activity was completed at the bin/location level. Combined with count_start_timestamp to calculate count duration for labor and efficiency KPIs.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'The method used to perform the physical count. Indicates whether the counter used manual tally, RF scanner, barcode scanning, RFID technology, or an automated counting system. Supports accuracy benchmarking by method.']
  count_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual physical quantity counted by the counter during the cycle count event. This is the raw observed quantity entered into the WMS or SAP MM physical inventory document.']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical count activity began at the bin/location level. Used for labor productivity analysis and WMS operational reporting.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cycle count event. Tracks workflow progression from open through completion and approval in the WMS and SAP MM inventory management process.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cycle count event indicating whether it was a regularly scheduled count, an ad-hoc count, a recount triggered by variance, an annual physical inventory, or a system-triggered count. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|ad_hoc|recount|annual|triggered — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count record was first created in the system (WMS or SAP MM). Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the lot being counted. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) rotation validation, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance for chemical inputs, and food safety regulatory requirements.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number associated with the lot being counted. Provides traceability back to the original goods receipt event in SAP MM for supply chain continuity.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN-14) for the material being counted. Enables barcode scanning integration with Manhattan Associates WMS and supports GS1 traceability standards.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material being counted by agricultural input or output category. Supports domain-specific inventory accuracy reporting across seed, chemical, fertilizer, harvested commodity, and feed stock categories.']
  original_count_document_number varchar [note: 'For recount records (recount_flag = True), the count document number of the original cycle count that triggered this recount. Enables audit trail linking between original count and recount events.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cycle count record is a recount triggered by an unacceptable variance on a prior count. True = recount; False = original count. Links to the original count via original_count_document_number.']
  sap_physical_inventory_doc varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM physical inventory document number (MI01) corresponding to this cycle count event. Provides the cross-system reference key for reconciliation between Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP MM.']
  scheduled_count_date date [note: 'The originally planned date for the cycle count as defined in the WMS cycle count schedule. Compared against count_date to measure schedule adherence and operational compliance.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'SAP MM stock type category of the inventory being counted. Determines whether the stock is available for use (unrestricted), under quality hold (quality_inspection), blocked, or restricted. Critical for QM integration.']
  system_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The inventory quantity recorded in the WMS/SAP system at the time the cycle count was initiated, before the physical count result is entered. Represents the book inventory position used as the baseline for variance calculation.']
  uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which system_qty, count_qty, and variance_qty are expressed. Agricultural inventory uses CWT (Hundredweight), BU (Bushel), and MT (Metric Ton) for commodities; other units for inputs. Aligns with SAP MM base UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CWT|BU|MT|LB|KG|EA|GAL|TON — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count record was last modified in the system. Tracks updates such as recount entries, variance approvals, and root cause code assignments for audit trail completeness.']
  variance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The variance quantity expressed as a percentage of the system quantity ((variance_qty / system_qty) * 100). Key inventory accuracy KPI metric used to assess count quality and trigger recount thresholds.']
  variance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between the physical count quantity and the system quantity (count_qty minus system_qty). A positive value indicates a surplus; a negative value indicates a shortage. Used to trigger inventory adjustment records.']
  variance_root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized root cause code assigned to explain the inventory variance (e.g., SHRINKAGE, MISCOUNT, MISPLACE, DAMAGE, THEFT, SYSTEM_ERROR, UNIT_CONVERSION). Used for root cause analysis and continuous improvement of inventory accuracy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SHRINKAGE|MISCOUNT|MISPLACE|DAMAGE|THEFT|SYSTEM_ERROR|UNIT_CONVERSION|EXPIRY — promote to reference product]']
  variance_root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description providing additional detail on the root cause of the inventory variance. Supplements the variance_root_cause_code with counter observations and corrective action notes.']
  variance_value_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financial value of the inventory variance in US dollars, calculated as variance_qty multiplied by the standard or moving average unit price. Used for financial impact assessment, P&L reporting, and COGS adjustment authorization.']

  Note: 'WMS-driven cycle count event record capturing scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory counts at the storage location and bin level. Captures count document number, count date, counter assigned, location/bin counted, material with GS1 GTIN, system quantity vs. physical count quantity in CWT/BU/MT, variance quantity, variance percentage, recount flag, count status, and root cause code for variances. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS cycle count module and SAP MM inventory management. Supports inventory accuracy KPI tracking and triggers inventory_adjustment records for confirmed variances.'
}

Table inventory.adjustment {
  adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each inventory adjustment transaction record in the Agriculture lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the inventory_adjustment data product.']
  adjustment_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the commodity, seed, chemical, or fertilizer being adjusted. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM material master.']
  adjustment_material_material_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to inventory.material_master']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the authorized approver who reviewed and approved the inventory adjustment. Required for SOX internal controls and FSMA audit trail compliance. References SAP SuccessFactors employee master.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: Customer claim resolution: inventory adjustments for short shipments, quality rejections, or returns are initiated by customer cases. Linking adjustment to case enables claim-to-adjustment reconciliat']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the commodity or material lot (LOT/batch) being adjusted. Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation tracking and traceability per FSMA requirements.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Inventory adjustments generate financial postings within a company code. Required for multi-entity agriculture operations to correctly attribute shrinkage losses and write-offs to the appropriate lega']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory adjustments for shrinkage, spoilage, and quality losses are charged to cost centers in agriculture. Cost center assignment on adjustments enables crop enterprise loss reporting and variance ']
  cycle_count_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.cycle_count. Business justification: inventory_adjustment has cycle_count_document (STRING) referencing the triggering cycle count document. In WMS-driven operations, cycle count variances automatically trigger inventory adjustments. Add']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory adjustments for shrinkage, spoilage, and write-offs post to specific GL accounts (inventory write-down, shrinkage expense). Direct FK supports period-close reconciliation of inventory value ']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Inventory adjustments generate accounting documents (sap_accounting_doc_number is a denormalized reference). Direct FK to journal_entry supports audit trail for shrinkage/write-off postings, SOX contr']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Inventory adjustments for quality-related losses (mycotoxin-contaminated grain disposal, MRL-failed lot destruction) are triggered by nonconformance events. Direct FK enables P&L impact reporting by n']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Inventory adjustments impact crop enterprise P&L and must be assigned to profit centers for segment reporting. Agriculture management accounting requires tracking shrinkage and spoilage losses by prof']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: A quarantine_hold resolution (disposition decision: destroy, rework, return) triggers an inventory_adjustment to reflect the quantity change. Adding quarantine_hold_id FK (nullable) to inventory_adjus']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: An inventory_adjustment modifies a specific stock_position (shrinkage, moisture loss, quality downgrade). Adding stock_position_id FK directly links the adjustment to the affected position, enabling p']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (grain bin, cold storage, warehouse, field stockpile) where the inventory adjustment occurred. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM storage location master.']
  adjusted_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory quantity after the adjustment has been applied. Expressed in the primary unit of measure (CWT, BU, or MT). Represents the corrected on-hand balance.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the inventory adjustment record. Controls whether the adjustment has been approved, posted to the general ledger, or reversed.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory adjustment event. Shrinkage covers moisture loss, drying shrink, handling loss, and storage deterioration. Cycle count variance corrects WMS discrepancies. Damage write-off records pest damage or physical loss. Quality downgrade reclassifies commodity grade. Reclassification moves stock between categories.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Supports SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who approved the inventory adjustment. Retained as a denormalized audit field to preserve the historical record even if the employee record changes.']
  cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized root cause code identifying the reason for the inventory discrepancy or shrinkage event (e.g., MOIST-LOSS, PEST-DMG, PROC-ERR, CYCLE-VAR). Sourced from SAP MM cause code configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product with full cause code taxonomy]']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action implemented to prevent recurrence of the inventory discrepancy or shrinkage event. Supports continuous improvement and GFSI/SQF audit requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory adjustment record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inventory adjustment document. Generated by SAP S/4HANA MM or Manhattan Associates WMS at time of posting. Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note (GRN) reference number linking the adjusted inventory to its original inbound receipt document. Enables traceability from adjustment back to procurement receipt.']
  is_reversal boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this adjustment record is a reversal of a previously posted adjustment. True when the record cancels a prior adjustment document. Supports audit trail integrity.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM movement type code governing the inventory posting (e.g., 551 for scrapping, 701 for physical inventory difference, 344 for transfer posting). Determines the accounting impact and stock category affected.']
  original_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Inventory quantity recorded before the adjustment event was applied. Expressed in the primary unit of measure (CWT, BU, or MT). Baseline for variance calculation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code identifying the facility (farm, processing plant, warehouse) where the inventory adjustment occurred. Supports multi-site inventory management.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting posting date on which the inventory adjustment was recorded in SAP S/4HANA FI/MM. Determines the fiscal period for COGS and inventory valuation impact. May differ from shrinkage_event_date.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code for the inventory adjustment as defined in SAP S/4HANA MM configuration (e.g., physical inventory difference, goods issue correction, quality rejection). Distinct from cause_code which identifies root cause; reason_code identifies the business justification category.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the specific reason for the inventory adjustment. Provides context beyond the structured reason_code for audit trail and corrective action documentation.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Adjustment document number of the original posting that this record reverses. Populated only when is_reversal is True. Enables bidirectional audit trail linkage between original and reversal documents.']
  shrinkage_event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shrinkage event (moisture loss, pest damage, storage deterioration) physically occurred. May differ from the adjustment posting date. Critical for seasonal shrinkage trend analysis.']
  shrinkage_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of original inventory quantity lost due to the shrinkage event (variance_qty / original_qty * 100). Used for shrinkage trend analysis and benchmarking by storage type, commodity, and season.']
  shrinkage_type varchar [note: 'Specific category of shrinkage event when adjustment_type is shrinkage. Distinguishes moisture loss during drying, physical handling loss, storage deterioration over time, pest damage, or quality-driven downgrade. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: moisture_loss|drying_shrink|handling_loss|storage_deterioration|pest_damage|quality_downgrade|other — promote to reference product]']
  stock_type_after varchar [note: 'SAP stock category of the inventory after the adjustment has been applied. Used for reclassification adjustments to confirm the target stock category.']
  stock_type_before varchar [note: 'SAP stock category of the inventory before the adjustment was applied. Relevant for reclassification adjustments that move stock between unrestricted, quality inspection, blocked, or restricted categories.']
  unit_cost_usd decimal(18,6) [note: 'Standard or moving average cost per unit of measure at the time of the adjustment, in US Dollars. Used to calculate the financial value impact of the inventory adjustment for COGS accuracy.']
  uom_primary varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for all quantity fields (original_qty, adjusted_qty, variance_qty). Agricultural commodities are measured in Hundredweight (CWT), Bushels (BU), or Metric Tons (MT) per USDA standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory adjustment record was last modified in the system. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  value_impact_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the inventory adjustment in US Dollars (variance_qty multiplied by unit_cost_usd). Represents the COGS charge or inventory write-down posted to the general ledger. Negative values indicate inventory value reduction.']
  variance_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net quantity difference between original and adjusted quantities (original_qty minus adjusted_qty). Negative values indicate stock reduction (shrinkage/loss); positive values indicate stock increase (count correction). Expressed in primary UOM.']
  wms_task_number varchar [note: 'Manhattan Associates WMS task or cycle count task number associated with this inventory adjustment. Provides cross-system traceability between the WMS cycle count event and the SAP inventory posting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all inventory quantity and value adjustments including shrinkage events (moisture loss/drying shrink, handling loss, storage deterioration, pest damage, quality downgrade), cycle count variance corrections, damage write-offs, and reclassifications. Captures adjustment document number, adjustment type (shrinkage, variance, damage, downgrade, reclassification), material, lot, location, original quantity, adjusted quantity, variance quantity in CWT/BU/MT, shrinkage type and percentage, cause code, adjustment reason code, corrective action taken, approver, cost impact, estimated value impact, shrinkage event date, and SAP accounting document reference. Serves as the SSOT for all shrinkage tracking, shrinkage trend analysis and benchmarking by storage type, commodity, and season. Supports COGS accuracy for commodity accounting and provides complete audit trail for all inventory corrections.'
}

Table inventory.quarantine_hold {
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each quarantine hold record. Primary key for the quarantine_hold data product in the inventory domain.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: MRL exceedance quarantine holds require tracing back to the specific EPA-registered agrochemical product for regulatory notification, label compliance review, and pesticide use investigation. quaranti']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: Customer complaint cases (MRL exceedance, quality nonconformance, recall) directly trigger quarantine holds in agriculture operations. This link enables bidirectional traceability between the customer']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific inventory lot or batch under quarantine hold. Supports FIFO/FEFO rotation and lot-level traceability per FSMA requirements.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Quarantine holds must be scoped to company code for financial reporting and regulatory compliance. Multi-entity agriculture operations require company code on quarantine holds for consolidated financi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quarantine holds incur testing, extended storage, and potential disposal costs charged to cost centers. Agriculture quality and finance teams track quarantine-related costs by cost center for crop ent']
  crop_protection_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.protection_event. Business justification: A quarantine hold on a commodity lot may be triggered by a crop protection event with PHI violation or MRL exceedance. Linking quarantine_hold to protection_event supports PHI compliance tracking, MRL']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID or employee identifier of the authorized quality manager or regulatory officer who approved the final disposition decision. Required for GFSI, SQF, BRC, and FSMA preventive controls documentation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Quarantine holds that result in write-offs or provisions require GL account assignment for financial posting. Agriculture finance teams post inventory write-down provisions to specific GL accounts whe']
  initiating_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer-initiated recalls and MRL exceedance complaints are a primary trigger for quarantine holds in agriculture. Linking the hold to the initiating customer account supports FSMA recall traceabilit']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Quarantine hold release in agriculture requires a formal inspection outcome (re-grading, MRL re-test clearance). Linking the hold to the governing inspection supports disposition decisions, regulatory']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Quarantine holds triggering inventory write-downs or provisions generate journal entries. Direct FK provides audit trail from food safety/quality event to financial impact — required for GAAP lower-of']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Quarantine holds for MRL exceedance or mycotoxin suspicion require lab sampling before disposition. quarantine_hold carries mrl_detected_level and mrl_exceedance_substance as plain attributes derived ']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: quarantine_hold manages inventory holds for specific materials pending quality inspection, MRL exceedance review, or regulatory compliance. The product currently stores material_number as a free-text ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: MRL exceedance is a primary cause of quarantine holds in agriculture. Linking to mrl_threshold enables regulatory disposition decisions, FDA/USDA audit trails, and dynamic compliance checking as toler']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Inventory quarantine holds are initiated by quality nonconformance events (MRL exceedance, failed inspection). quarantine_hold has initiating_event_reference (plain text) but no typed FK. Direct nonco']
  origin_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: MRL exceedance quarantine holds require tracing back to the originating field for FDA/USDA recall scope determination, PHI compliance investigation, and re-entry interval audits. commodity_lot.origin_']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Receiving quarantine process: the procurement GR is the triggering document for holds placed at receiving (MRL testing required, temperature exceedance, quality rejection flags on procurement_goods_re']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Vendor dispute resolution and regulatory traceability: when received goods are quarantined (MRL exceedance, quality failure), the originating PO is required for vendor chargeback, penalty clause enfor']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Customer quality complaints on a delivered sales order trigger quarantine holds on remaining stock from the same lot — a real FSMA/food safety traceability requirement. This link connects the hold to ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Quarantine holds are frequently initiated based on shipment-level events (temperature excursion during transit, customs inspection hold, MRL exceedance detected at port). Linking quarantine_hold to sh']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent stock position record in the inventory SSOT from which this quarantine hold was initiated.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (grain bin, cold storage, warehouse, field stockpile) where the quarantined inventory is physically held.']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the quarantine hold was resolved and the inventory was either released for use, reworked, destroyed, or returned. Null until disposition is executed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quarantine hold record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, SOX compliance, and regulatory investigation timelines.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision made by the quality or regulatory authority for the quarantined inventory. Release: cleared for normal use; Rework: requires reprocessing before use; Destroy: condemned and disposed; Return: returned to supplier; Donate: donated to approved channel; Regrade: reclassified to lower grade.']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the rationale for the disposition decision, corrective actions taken, and any conditions attached to the release or destruction of the quarantined inventory.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'Anticipated date by which the quarantine hold investigation is expected to be completed and the inventory released or dispositioned. Used for supply chain continuity planning and customer commitment management.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note number from SAP MM goods receipt posting that originated the inventory lot now under quarantine hold. Enables traceability back to the inbound supply chain event.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the quarantine hold record. Used in SAP QM usage decisions, WMS hold tags, and regulatory correspondence. Format: QH-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific reason the quarantine hold was initiated. Captures the triggering event such as COA failure, HACCP critical limit deviation, MRL exceedance, foreign material detection, or customer complaint details.']
  hold_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the quarantine hold was officially initiated and the inventory was blocked in SAP QM and WMS. Principal business event date for the hold lifecycle.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quarantine hold record. Drives SAP QM usage decision workflow and WMS inventory blocking. Open: hold initiated; Under_review: quality/regulatory investigation in progress; Pending_disposition: investigation complete, awaiting disposition approval; Released: stock cleared for use; Closed: disposition executed; Escalated: elevated to regulatory authority.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quarantine hold by its initiating business reason. Drives workflow routing and regulatory notification requirements. Values: quality (COA failure, specification deviation), regulatory (USDA/FDA/EPA review), safety (HACCP deviation, food safety risk), recall (voluntary or mandatory recall), customer_complaint (trade partner complaint), environmental (EPA/ISO 14001 contamination concern).']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'SAP user ID or employee identifier of the person who initiated the quarantine hold. Required for audit trail, SOX compliance, and regulatory investigation documentation.']
  initiating_event_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number of the document or event that triggered the hold, such as a COA (Certificate of Analysis) number, HACCP deviation report number, EU RASFF notification number, FDA recall number, or internal audit finding ID.']
  initiating_event_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the specific triggering event that caused the quarantine hold to be raised. Supports root cause analysis and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coa_failure|haccp_deviation|mrl_exceedance|foreign_material|microbiological|allergen|labeling|supplier_notification|regulatory_alert|customer_complaint|internal_audit — promote to reference product]']
  is_recall_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quarantine hold is associated with a formal product recall (voluntary or mandatory). True triggers recall response procedures per FDA FSMA and USDA FSIS recall protocols.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number assigned at goods receipt (GRN) or production. Supports lot-level traceability for recall response and HACCP deviation investigation.']
  mrl_detected_level decimal(10,6) [note: 'Measured concentration of the residue substance detected in the sample, expressed in mg/kg (ppm). Compared against the applicable MRL threshold to determine exceedance. Null if hold is not MRL-related.']
  phi_days_remaining varchar [note: 'Number of days remaining in the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) at the time the hold was initiated. A positive value indicates the crop was harvested before the PHI elapsed, which may be the root cause of an MRL exceedance hold.']
  qty_on_hold_bu decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of inventory placed under quarantine hold expressed in bushels (BU). Standard unit for grain commodities (corn, soybeans, wheat) in US agricultural trade and USDA WASDE reporting.']
  qty_on_hold_cwt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of inventory placed under quarantine hold expressed in hundredweight (CWT). Primary unit for grain and commodity inventory valuation and trading in US agricultural markets.']
  qty_on_hold_mt decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of inventory placed under quarantine hold expressed in metric tons (MT). Required for international trade documentation, EU regulatory reporting, and Codex Alimentarius compliance.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority (FDA, USDA, EPA, EU RASFF) was formally notified of the quarantine hold or food safety event. Required for FSMA compliance documentation and EU RASFF reporting timelines.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quarantine hold requires mandatory notification to a regulatory authority (FDA, USDA FSIS, EPA, EU RASFF). True when the hold involves a food safety risk, MRL exceedance, or mandatory recall trigger.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the regulatory authority (FDA recall number, USDA FSIS case number, EU RASFF notification number, EPA enforcement action number) for tracking the regulatory response to this hold.']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'SAP QM inspection lot number associated with this quarantine hold. Links the hold to the formal quality inspection process and usage decision workflow in SAP S/4HANA QM module.']
  sap_qm_usage_decision varchar [note: 'SAP QM usage decision code recorded at inspection lot closure. A=Accept (release to unrestricted stock), R=Reject (block/destroy), B=Return to vendor, S=Sort/rework. Drives automatic stock transfer posting in SAP MM.']
  uom_primary varchar [note: 'The primary unit of measure used for inventory valuation and SAP MM stock management for this quarantine hold record. Determines which quantity field is the authoritative measure for disposition and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CWT|BU|MT|KG|LB|EA|CS — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the quarantine hold record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and Databricks Silver Layer incremental processing.']
  wms_hold_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical hold tag or WMS blocking record number assigned by Manhattan Associates WMS to identify and segregate the quarantined inventory in the warehouse or storage facility.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing inventory holds and quarantine status for lots or stock positions pending quality inspection, regulatory review, or food safety investigation. Captures hold number, material, lot, location, quantity on hold in CWT/BU/MT, hold type (quality, regulatory, safety, recall, customer_complaint), hold reason, initiating event reference (COA failure, HACCP deviation, MRL exceedance per EPA/EU Regulation 396/2005), hold start date, expected release date, actual release date, disposition decision (release, rework, destroy, return), and approver. Integrates with SAP QM usage decision and supports FDA/USDA recall response procedures, EU RASFF (Rapid Alert System for Food and Feed) notifications, and Codex Alimentarius food safety standards.'
}

Table quality.inspection {
  inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each inspection event. Primary key for the quality inspection master record.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Inspections are frequently conducted as part of a formal quality audit program (e.g., a GFSI audit includes physical facility inspections, a GLOBALG.A.P. audit includes field inspections). Linking ins']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: GAP and food safety inspections are scoped to specific commodities (e.g., leafy greens vs. tomatoes). Regulatory reporting (FSMA, USDA GAP) and inspection scheduling require commodity-level inspection']
  conservation_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.conservation_practice. Business justification: GAP audits evaluate specific BMPs in place at time of inspection (gap_module_soil_amendments_score, gap_module_field_sanitation_score). Auditors must reference which conservation_practice record was a']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Field and facility inspections (GAP audits, food safety, third-party) incur direct costs (inspector fees, travel) allocated to cost centers. Agriculture operations budget and track inspection costs by']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: GAP and food safety inspections in agriculture are frequently conducted at the crew level — inspecting a specific harvest or packing crew\'s GMP/food safety practices. Linking inspection to the crew be']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual (internal auditor, third-party inspector, or regulatory officer) who conducted the inspection.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, storage location, processing plant, or field site where the inspection was conducted.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: USDA Organic and GlobalG.A.P. certification inspections are scoped to the entire farm operation (farm_unit), not individual fields or facilities. This link enables farm-level inspection history requir']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: GAP field inspections and FSMA Produce Safety Rule inspections are conducted at the field level. Linking inspection to field enables traceability of which fields passed/failed GAP audits — a core requ']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: USDA AMS grade inspections and GAP inspections evaluate compliance against specific grading standards. The inspection stores grade as denormalized text. Linking to grading_standard enables grade certi']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Inspections in food safety contexts are frequently conducted against a specific HACCP plan — verifying CCP monitoring, critical limits, and corrective action procedures. inspection has haccp_ccp_compl']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Storage location inspections (bin audits, warehouse food safety inspections, USDA/FDA facility checks) are a distinct inspection type in agriculture. inspection already has facility_id but storage_loc']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Vendor facility/site inspections (second-party audits) are a core agricultural procurement process for supplier approval and requalification. This is distinct from goods-receipt inspections already co']
  actual_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the inspection was actually performed. May differ from scheduled_date due to rescheduling or operational delays.']
  certification_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection outcome qualifies the facility or operation for certification or recertification under the applicable standard. Derived from score thresholds and major non-conformance counts.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The food safety or agricultural certification standard against which this inspection was conducted (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P., SQF Level 2, BRC Global Standard). Drives checklist selection and certification eligibility assessment.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which all mandated corrective actions from this inspection must be completed and verified. Drives compliance follow-up scheduling and escalation workflows.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more corrective actions have been mandated as a result of this inspection. Triggers downstream corrective action tracking workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection activity was concluded, enabling duration calculation and scheduling analytics.']
  gap_module_ag_water_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Module-level compliance score for the Agricultural Water section of a GAP compliance assessment. Evaluates water source testing, microbial standards, irrigation method risk, and water quality records per FSMA and USDA GAP requirements.']
  gap_module_field_sanitation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Module-level compliance score for the Field Sanitation and Hygiene section of a GAP compliance assessment. Evaluates toilet facilities, handwashing stations, field equipment sanitation, and harvest container cleanliness.']
  gap_module_soil_amendments_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Module-level compliance score for the Soil Amendments section of a GAP compliance assessment. Evaluates manure application intervals, compost treatment standards, and biological soil amendment risk management.']
  gap_module_traceability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Module-level compliance score for the Traceability and Recordkeeping section of a GAP compliance assessment. Evaluates lot identification, harvest records, chain-of-custody documentation, and recall readiness.']
  gap_module_worker_health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Module-level compliance score for the Worker Health, Hygiene, and Training section of a GAP compliance assessment. Evaluates handwashing facilities, illness policies, training records, and personal hygiene practices per FSMA Produce Safety Rule requirements.']
  gis_coordinates varchar [note: 'GPS latitude and longitude coordinates of the inspection site in decimal degrees format, enabling geospatial analysis and field mapping via Esri ArcGIS integration.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inspection event, used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-system references (e.g., SAP QM inspection lot number).']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Operational area or stage of the agricultural supply chain covered by this inspection event (e.g., field operations, post-harvest handling, cold storage, processing facility, inbound receiving). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field|post_harvest|storage|processing|supply_chain|receiving|pre_operational — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record, tracking progression from scheduling through completion and review.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by its regulatory or operational framework. Determines the checklist, scoring methodology, and applicable standards applied during the inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GAP|GMP|HACCP|FSMA|GLOBALG.A.P.|pre_operational|receiving|internal_audit|FSIS|APHIS — promote to reference product]']
  inspector_credential varchar [note: 'Certification or credential held by the inspector (e.g., PCQI - Preventive Controls Qualified Individual, Lead Auditor ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P. approved CB auditor). Validates inspector qualification for the inspection type.']
  inspector_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or agency the inspector represents (e.g., USDA AMS, FDA district office, third-party certification body such as NSF, SGS, or Bureau Veritas). Distinguishes internal from external inspections.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific location inspected, such as field block identifier, warehouse bay, processing line number, or cold storage unit. Complements the structured facility_id reference.']
  major_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances (critical findings) identified during the inspection. Major non-conformances typically result in automatic certification failure or mandatory corrective action before re-inspection.']
  minor_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances identified during the inspection. Minor findings require corrective action within a defined timeframe but do not automatically disqualify certification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for inspector observations, contextual remarks, environmental conditions at time of inspection, or other qualitative information not captured in structured fields.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations or recommendations noted during the inspection that do not constitute formal non-conformances but indicate areas for improvement.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall pass/fail determination for the inspection event. Conditional pass indicates the operation may continue subject to corrective actions within a defined timeframe.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or percentage representing the aggregate compliance performance across all inspection criteria evaluated. Scoring methodology varies by inspection type (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P. percentage, SQF numeric grade).']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up re-inspection when reinspection_required is true. Used for compliance calendar management and audit scheduling.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up re-inspection is required to verify corrective action completion or to re-evaluate a failed inspection outcome.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal inspection report was issued to the inspected party. Starts the clock for corrective action response deadlines and certification validity periods.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Official report number assigned by the regulatory body or certification organization to the formal inspection report document. Used for regulatory submission tracking and document management.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the inspection was originally scheduled to occur, used for compliance planning and audit scheduling.']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'The specific version or edition of the certification standard applied during the inspection (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P. v5.4, SQF Edition 9). Critical for audit traceability as standards evolve.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the inspection activity commenced on-site or in the facility, used for duration tracking and regulatory audit trails.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for all quality inspections across field, post-harvest, storage, processing, and supply chain operations. Captures inspection type (GAP, GMP, HACCP verification, FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., pre-operational, receiving, GAP compliance assessment), scope, facility or field location, scheduled and actual dates, inspector identity, overall pass/fail outcome, and score or grade. For GAP compliance assessments: standard version, module-level scoring (worker health, agricultural water, soil amendments, field sanitation, traceability), and certification eligibility. Regulatory body association (USDA, FDA, FSIS, APHIS). SSOT for all inspection events in the quality domain.'
}

Table quality.inspection_finding {
  inspection_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual inspection or audit finding record. Primary key for the inspection_finding data product in the quality domain SSOT.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: MRL exceedance findings (mrl_exceedance_flag=true) must trace back to the specific EPA-registered agrochemical product. Regulatory reportable findings and corrective action prioritization require know']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Inspection findings can be discovered during formal quality audits (GFSI, GAP, etc.). The inspection_finding table has audit_type and standard_scheme fields that duplicate quality_audit master data. T']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Inspection findings (MRL exceedances, GAP violations, food safety risks) are commodity-specific. Regulatory agencies (FDA, USDA) require findings to be reported by commodity. A compliance officer trac']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific commodity lot or product batch associated with this finding, if applicable. Enables traceability from finding to affected product for recall management, COA linkage, and MRL compliance tracking.']
  conservation_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.conservation_practice. Business justification: GAP and NRCS audit findings cite specific conservation practices that are non-compliant or absent (e.g., buffer strip not maintained, cover crop not established). inspection_finding has gap_gmp_catego']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, farm site, processing plant, or storage location where this finding was identified. Supports facility-level compliance reporting and FSMA Preventive Controls facility registration linkage.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: GAP inspection findings (field sanitation, agricultural water, soil amendments) are scored per field. Linking inspection_finding to field enables field-level risk scoring and finding trend analysis — ']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Inspection findings are discovered during quality inspections. The inspection_finding table has inspection_area and audit_type fields suggesting findings can come from inspections. This FK links findi']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: When mrl_exceedance_flag is set on an inspection finding, the specific regulatory tolerance that was exceeded must be referenced for regulatory_reportable_flag determination, agency notification, and ']
  primary_inspection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (internal auditor, second-party customer auditor, or third-party certification body inspector) who identified and recorded this finding. Supports auditor qualification tracking and finding pattern analysis.']
  prior_finding_inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the earlier finding record that this finding is a recurrence of. Populated when repeat_finding_flag is True. Enables trend analysis and systemic non-conformance identification across audit cycles.']
  safety_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_event. Business justification: Inspection findings related to WPS violations, pesticide handler exposure, or worker safety GMP failures directly correspond to safety incidents. Agriculture regulators (EPA WPS, OSHA) require cross-r']
  ccp_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) associated with this finding, if applicable (e.g., CCP-01 Thermal Processing, CCP-02 Metal Detection). Links the finding to the HACCP plan for hazard analysis and corrective action tracking.']
  certification_impact varchar [note: 'Assessed impact of this finding on the facility\'s or operation\'s current certification status under the applicable GFSI scheme or GLOBALG.A.P. certification. Populated by the lead auditor or certification body. Drives certification status update workflows.']
  closure_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the corrective action must be fully implemented and the finding verified as closed. Distinct from response_due_date (plan submission) — this is the implementation completion deadline. Used for certification readiness tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp when this finding record was first captured in the system. Used for data lineage, SLA tracking, and regulatory audit trails. Populated automatically on insert.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp when this finding was identified or observed during the inspection or audit. Distinct from record creation audit timestamp. Critical for PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) and REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance timelines.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'The disposition decision for any product or material implicated by this finding. Applicable when the finding is associated with a specific commodity lot. Aligns with SAP QM usage decision codes and FSMA preventive controls disposition requirements.']
  evidence_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to supporting evidence documents (photographs, laboratory test results, COA, temperature logs, pesticide application records) stored in the document management system. Supports audit trail completeness and regulatory submission packages.']
  finding_classification varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the finding per food safety and quality management standards. Critical = immediate food safety risk or regulatory violation; Major = significant non-conformance likely to result in product failure; Minor = partial non-conformance; Observation = noted deviation not yet non-conforming; Opportunity for Improvement (OFI) = best-practice recommendation. Drives corrective action urgency and certification impact.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance, observation, or opportunity for improvement. Documents what was found, where, and under what conditions. Supports root cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this finding (e.g., QF-2024-000123). Used in corrective action requests, audit reports, and regulatory submissions. Sourced from SAP QM notification numbering.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding from identification through closure. Drives corrective action workflows and certification readiness dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|under_review|corrective_action_pending|corrective_action_submitted|verified_closed|accepted|waived — promote to reference product]']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the nature of the non-conformance or observation (e.g., \'Inadequate pest control records\', \'CCP temperature log gap\'). Used in dashboards, corrective action requests, and management reports.']
  food_safety_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding poses a direct food safety risk requiring immediate escalation under HACCP or FSMA protocols. When True, triggers mandatory notification workflows to the food safety team and may require product hold or recall assessment.']
  gap_gmp_category varchar [note: 'Categorizes the finding within the GAP/GMP compliance framework. Supports structured reporting to USDA, FDA, and GLOBALG.A.P. certification bodies and enables category-level trend analysis for continuous improvement programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GAP|GMP|HACCP|sanitation|traceability|pest_control|water_quality|worker_health|equipment|documentation — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_area varchar [note: 'The physical area, zone, process step, or operational unit where the finding was identified (e.g., receiving dock, cold storage, processing line 3, field block A, irrigation system). Supports spatial analysis of non-conformance patterns.']
  inspector_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual notes, observations, or recommendations recorded by the inspector or auditor at the time of the finding. Supplements the structured finding_description with qualitative context for root cause analysis.']
  ipm_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is related to Integrated Pest Management (IPM) program compliance, including pest scouting records, pesticide application logs, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) adherence, or REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance.']
  mrl_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding involves a pesticide or chemical residue exceeding the Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) as defined by EPA, Codex Alimentarius, or importing country regulations. Triggers mandatory product hold and regulatory notification.']
  objective_evidence varchar [note: 'Factual, verifiable evidence supporting the finding — records reviewed, measurements taken, observations made, or interviews conducted. Required by ISO 19011 and all GFSI scheme auditing protocols to substantiate the finding classification.']
  parent_event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether the parent event was a quality inspection (routine operational check) or a formal audit (internal, second-party, or third-party certification audit). Drives workflow routing and reporting.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which this finding must be reported, if regulatory_reportable_flag is True. Determines the specific reporting format, timeline, and submission channel required. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USDA|FDA|FSIS|EPA|APHIS|OSHA|state_agency|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding must be reported to a regulatory authority (USDA, FDA, FSIS, EPA, APHIS). When True, triggers mandatory regulatory notification workflows and documentation requirements under FSMA, FSIS directives, or EPA regulations.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified non-conformance at the same location or process. Repeat findings trigger escalated corrective action requirements under GFSI schemes and may impact certification grade.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Required deadline by which a corrective action plan or response must be submitted for this finding. Determined by finding classification and standard scheme requirements (e.g., critical findings typically require 24-hour response; major findings 30 days). Drives SLA monitoring.']
  risk_severity_rating varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity assessment of the finding\'s potential impact on food safety, product quality, regulatory compliance, or business continuity. Distinct from finding_classification (which is scheme-defined); this is the internal risk management rating used for prioritization and HACCP hazard analysis alignment.']
  sap_qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA QM module notification number corresponding to this finding. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer record and the operational system of record for corrective action management and ERP integration.']
  standard_clause_reference varchar [note: 'The specific clause, control point, element, or requirement number within the applicable standard scheme against which this finding was raised (e.g., FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.135, GLOBALG.A.P. CB.7.1, SQF Element 2.4.3, BRC Clause 4.11.2, IFS Requirement 4.18.1). Essential for certification body reporting and corrective action traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp when this finding record was last modified. Tracks changes to status, corrective action assignments, or evidence updates for compliance audit trail purposes.']
  verified_closed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the finding was verified as effectively closed following corrective action implementation. Populated by the verifying auditor or quality manager. Used to calculate closure cycle time and assess corrective action effectiveness.']

  Note: 'Individual findings, observations, and deficiencies identified during quality inspections or formal audits (internal, second-party, third-party certification). Records parent event reference and type (inspection or audit), finding classification (critical, major, minor, observation, opportunity for improvement), applicable standard clause reference (FSMA 21 CFR Part 117, GLOBALG.A.P. control point, SQF Element, BRC Clause, IFS requirement), description of non-conformance or observation, objective evidence, risk severity rating, required response timeline, and disposition. Unified SSOT for ALL quality findings regardless of detection context — replaces separate audit finding tracking. Drives corrective action generation and certification status updates.'
}

Table quality.corrective_action {
  corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record. Primary key for the corrective_action data product in the quality domain.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by agrochemical misuse (wrong product applied, PHI violation, restricted-use violation) must reference the specific agrochemical product. An agrochemist or compliance officer managing ']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.audit. Business justification: corrective_action has source_type (STRING) and source_reference_id (BIGINT) as a polymorphic reference. When source_type = \'audit\', adding a typed audit_id FK provides a direct reference to the audit ']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: Customer complaint-driven CAPA management: when a customer case (e.g., MRL exceedance complaint, contamination report) triggers a corrective action, the CAPA must reference the originating customer ca']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: CAPAs in agriculture are commodity-specific — an MRL exceedance CAPA for strawberries differs from one for spinach. Commodity-level CAPA trend analysis is required for GFSI scheme compliance and buyer']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: CAPAs in agriculture are frequently triggered by commodity lot failures (MRL exceedance, mycotoxin, grade rejection). Linking CAPA to the affected lot enables effectiveness tracking, lot disposition f']
  conservation_practice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.conservation_practice. Business justification: NRCS EQIP and GAP audit corrective actions frequently mandate installation or modification of specific conservation practices (buffer strips, cover crops). Linking CAPA to conservation_practice enable']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPA execution in agriculture incurs labor, materials, and process-change costs charged to cost centers. Cost-of-quality management accounting and CAPA ROI analysis require tracking CAPA expenditures ']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, processing plant, farm site, or warehouse where the nonconformance or finding originated and where the corrective action is to be implemented. Links to the facility master record.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: GAP and FSMA corrective actions are frequently field-specific (e.g., cease irrigation from contaminated source on field X, implement buffer zone on field Y). Linking corrective_action to field enables']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: CAPA remediation expenditures (equipment cleaning, retesting, process upgrades) are posted to GL accounts. Financial period-close and cost-of-quality P&L reporting in agriculture require CAPA costs ma']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Corrective actions in food safety are frequently generated in response to HACCP CCP deviations. The corrective_action table currently has a denormalized haccp_ccp_reference STRING field that stores a ']
  inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_finding. Business justification: Corrective actions (CAPAs) are generated in response to inspection findings. The corrective_action table has source_type and source_reference_number (string-based) but lacks structured FK to findings.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: corrective_action has source_type (STRING) and source_reference_id (BIGINT) as a polymorphic reference. When source_type = \'inspection\', adding a typed inspection_id FK provides a direct reference to ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by MRL exceedances must reference the specific regulatory tolerance to document the preventive action scope, verify effectiveness against the threshold, and satisfy FSMA Preventive Con']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Corrective actions are initiated in response to nonconformances. The corrective_action table has source_type and source_reference_number (string-based) but lacks structured FK to nonconformance. This ']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by organic certification violations (prohibited substance use, audit nonconformances) must reference the specific organic certification at risk. An organic program manager tracking CAP']
  parent_capa_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent CAPA record when this CAPA was generated as a follow-up due to ineffective closure of a prior action. Enables CAPA lineage tracking and recurrence chain analysis.']
  primary_corrective_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or team lead assigned as the primary owner accountable for implementing the corrective and preventive action. Links to the workforce/employee master record in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  required_workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: Agriculture CAPAs triggered by pesticide misuse or food safety violations frequently mandate that responsible employees obtain or renew a specific certification (pesticide applicator license, HACCP ce']
  safety_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_event. Business justification: Under FSMA preventive controls and GFSI schemes, corrective actions frequently mandate a specific training event as the preventive measure.
