  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: CAPAs triggered by vendor non-performance must reference the specific vendor contract to enforce penalty clauses and contractual corrective action requirements. corrective_action has vendor_id but not']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Supplier Corrective Action Requests (SCARs) are a named agriculture procurement process where CAPAs are formally issued to vendors. Quality teams must link a CAPA to the responsible vendor for SCAR tr']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date on which all corrective and preventive actions were fully implemented and the CAPA record was formally closed following successful verification. Compared against target_completion_date to measure on-time closure performance.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the CAPA record, formatted as CAPA-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}. Used for cross-referencing in audit reports, regulatory submissions, and SAP QM notification workflows.']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record, tracking progression from initial opening through root cause analysis, implementation, verification, and closure. Drives workflow routing in SAP S/4HANA QM.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the action is purely corrective (addressing an existing nonconformance), purely preventive (addressing a potential risk), or both. Determines reporting category for FSMA, SQF, BRC, and IFS compliance.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The food safety or agricultural certification standard under which this CAPA was generated or is required to be documented. Determines the specific documentation and closure requirements applicable to the CAPA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|IFS|GLOBALG.A.P.|FSMA|HACCP|ISO_22000|USDA_NOP|other — promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific corrective action(s) taken to eliminate the identified root cause and prevent recurrence of the nonconformance. Documents what was done, how it was done, and the expected outcome.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when the CAPA record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  department varchar [note: 'Name of the department or operational area responsible for implementing the corrective and preventive action (e.g., Quality Assurance, Production, Procurement, Logistics). Used for workload assignment and KPI reporting.']
  effectiveness_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the effectiveness review was conducted to assess whether the implemented corrective and preventive actions successfully eliminated the root cause and prevented recurrence of the nonconformance.']
  effectiveness_review_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative notes from the effectiveness review, documenting evidence reviewed, observations made, and rationale for the effectiveness determination. Supports audit trail and continuous improvement documentation.']
  effectiveness_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the post-implementation effectiveness review, assessing whether the corrective and preventive actions successfully eliminated the root cause and prevented recurrence. A \'not_effective\' outcome triggers a new CAPA cycle.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the nonconformance, inspection finding, audit observation, or food safety incident that triggered this CAPA. Provides the factual basis for root cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  fsma_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA is associated with a food safety event that requires reporting or documentation under the Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA), including Preventive Controls, Produce Safety, or Foreign Supplier Verification Program requirements.']
  gap_gmp_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nonconformance or finding that triggered this CAPA constitutes a violation of Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) or Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). Flags records requiring mandatory regulatory reporting.']
  immediate_containment_action varchar [note: 'Description of the immediate containment or disposition action taken at the time of the finding to prevent affected product from reaching the market or causing further harm (e.g., product quarantine, lot hold, process stoppage). Distinct from the root-cause-based corrective action.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the CAPA record was formally opened and entered into the quality management system. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the CAPA lifecycle. Used for aging analysis and regulatory timeliness reporting.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the systemic preventive action(s) implemented to eliminate the potential cause of a nonconformance or to prevent recurrence across similar processes, products, or facilities. Addresses systemic risk beyond the immediate corrective fix.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the CAPA based on the severity of the finding, food safety risk, regulatory impact, and potential for recurrence. Drives escalation thresholds and target completion timelines.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA addresses a recurring nonconformance or finding that has been previously documented. Recurring issues trigger escalated review and systemic corrective action requirements under GFSI standards.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory clause, standard requirement, or certification criterion that the CAPA addresses (e.g., FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.150, GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6 AF 4.1, SQF Element 2.5.3). Supports regulatory reporting and audit readiness.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee or team lead assigned as the primary owner of the CAPA. Stored for audit trail and regulatory reporting purposes where the named individual must be identified in compliance documentation.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause to enable trend analysis and systemic issue identification across the quality management system. Categories align with standard fishbone/Ishikawa diagram branches used in agricultural food safety programs.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the identified root cause(s) of the nonconformance or finding, as determined through the selected root cause analysis method. Forms the foundation for corrective and preventive action planning.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Structured methodology used to identify the root cause of the nonconformance or finding. Common methods include 5-Why analysis, fishbone (Ishikawa) diagram, fault tree analysis, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA), Pareto analysis, or brainstorming. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 5_why|fishbone|fault_tree|failure_mode_effects_analysis|pareto|brainstorming|other — promote to reference product]']
  sap_qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA QM module notification number associated with this CAPA record. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational SAP QM workflow system.']
  source_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier of the originating record (e.g., nonconformance report number, audit finding reference, inspection report number). Supports cross-document traceability in regulatory submissions and audit packages.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the originating trigger for the CAPA record. Indicates whether the action was initiated from an inspection finding, nonconformance report, audit finding, environmental monitoring positive, customer complaint, Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) exceedance, food safety incident, or internal observation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection_finding|nonconformance|audit_finding|environmental_monitoring|customer_complaint|mrl_exceedance|food_safety_incident|internal_observation — promote to reference product]']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which all corrective and preventive actions must be fully implemented and ready for verification. Used for overdue tracking, escalation management, and regulatory audit readiness reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when the CAPA record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification activity was performed to confirm that the corrective and preventive actions were implemented as planned and are effective.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Description of the method used to verify that the corrective and preventive actions were effectively implemented (e.g., re-inspection, laboratory testing, process audit, document review, observation). Required for FSMA, SQF, BRC, and IFS closure documentation.']

  Note: 'CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) records generated in response to inspection findings, nonconformances (including MRL exceedances and food safety incidents), audit findings, or environmental monitoring positives. Tracks root cause analysis method (5-Why, fishbone, fault tree), corrective action description, preventive action description, responsible party, target completion date, actual closure date, verification method and date, effectiveness review outcome and review date. Supports FSMA, SQF, BRC, and IFS CAPA requirements. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA QM notification workflows. Links to originating finding or nonconformance for full traceability.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the nonconformance record was first created in the quality management system. Supports audit trail, regulatory reporting timelines, and data lineage.']
  detected_residue_level decimal(12,6) [note: 'Measured pesticide residue concentration in mg/kg (ppm) as determined by laboratory analysis. Populated for nonconformance_type = \'mrl_exceedance\'. Compared against applicable MRL standard.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the nonconformance was first identified or detected. Used for regulatory reporting timelines and corrective action due date calculations.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin or mechanism by which the nonconformance was identified. Distinguishes proactive internal detection from reactive external discovery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_audit|external_audit|laboratory_test|operator_observation|customer_complaint|regulatory_inspection|supplier_notification|automated_sensor — promote to reference product]']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date on which the disposition decision was formally approved and recorded. Required for regulatory audit trails and FSMA compliance documentation.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final quality disposition decision for the nonconforming product or material. Determines the fate of affected inventory and drives downstream supply chain and financial actions.']
  food_safety_incident_subtype varchar [note: 'Specific category of food safety incident. Populated only when nonconformance_type = \'food_safety_incident\'. Drives HACCP critical control point review and regulatory reporting requirements.']
  gap_audit_finding_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number linking this nonconformance to a specific GAP or GMP audit finding. Enables traceability between audit programs and resulting nonconformance records.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme under which this nonconformance was identified or is being managed. Determines applicable audit standard requirements for CAPA and closure.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'HACCP hazard classification for food safety incidents. Categorizes the nature of the food safety hazard to guide risk assessment and control measure selection.']
  incident_closure_status varchar [note: 'Detailed closure workflow status for food safety incidents and major nonconformances. Tracks progression from initial detection through CAPA verification and regulatory sign-off.']
  mock_recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a mock/simulated recall exercise rather than an actual recall event. Required for GFSI, SQF, BRC, and GLOBALG.A.P. certification audit readiness.']
  nc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nonconformance record within the quality management workflow.']
  nonconformance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nonconformance category. \'product\' = physical product defect; \'process\' = deviation from SOP/GMP; \'system\' = management system gap; \'mrl_exceedance\' = Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) exceedance; \'food_safety_incident\' = HACCP/FSMA food safety event.']
  qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'External quality notification number from SAP S/4HANA QM module (Q-notification). Serves as the cross-system business identifier for this nonconformance record.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity. Supports MT (Metric Ton), BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), KG, LB, L, GAL, UNIT, CASE per agricultural industry standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MT|BU|CWT|KG|LB|L|GAL|UNIT|CASE — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA/USDA recall classification. Class I = serious adverse health consequences or death; Class II = temporary adverse health consequences; Class III = unlikely to cause adverse health consequences. Populated for food safety incidents requiring recall.']
  regulatory_agencies_notified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory agencies formally notified of this nonconformance (e.g., FDA, USDA, FSIS, EPA, APHIS). Populated when regulatory_notification_required is True.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this nonconformance triggers a mandatory regulatory notification obligation to USDA, FDA, FSIS, EPA, or APHIS. Drives compliance workflow escalation.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the identified root cause of the nonconformance. Supports trend analysis, corrective action prioritization, and CAPA program management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_material|process_deviation|equipment_failure|human_error|supplier_issue|environmental|pest_contamination|storage_condition — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause following investigation. Supports 5-Why or fishbone analysis documentation for CAPA and audit evidence.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity classification of the nonconformance. \'minor\' = low risk, no regulatory impact; \'major\' = significant deviation requiring corrective action; \'critical\' = immediate risk to product integrity or compliance; \'food_safety_crisis\' = imminent public health risk requiring recall or regulatory notification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the nonconformance record. Supports change tracking, audit trail integrity, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Unified master record for all quality failures, deviations, and food safety incidents across crop production, livestock operations, processing, and supply chain. Captures nonconformance type (product, process, system, MRL_exceedance, food_safety_incident), severity level (minor, major, critical, food_safety_crisis), affected lot, commodity or product SKU, detection point and source. For standard nonconformances: disposition decision, financial impact estimate, regulatory notification flag. For MRL-type: pesticide active ingredient, detected residue level, applicable MRL standard (EPA, EU, Codex), exceedance magnitude, affected export market. For food safety incidents: incident subtype (contamination, foreign material, allergen, pathogen, chemical), hazard classification, recall class (I, II, III), mock recall flag, affected quantity and value, regulatory agencies notified, root cause category, and incident closure status. SSOT for ALL quality failures from minor process deviations through food safety crises, recalls, and market withdrawals.'
}

Table quality.certificate_of_analysis {
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for certificate_of_analysis']
  authorized_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FSMA and GFSI require traceable employee sign-off on COA authorization. The existing \'authorized_by\' plain-text field is a denormalized employee reference; replacing it with a proper FK enables compli']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: COAs are issued per commodity for export documentation, buyer requirements, and food safety compliance. The existing commodity_type is a denormalized plain attribute. A proper FK to product.commodity ']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: A COA is issued for a specific commodity lot in agriculture (grain, produce). This FK is the foundational food safety traceability link enabling lot-level COA retrieval for buyer acceptance, export ce']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: COA issuance incurs lab testing and certification costs that must be allocated to a cost center. Agriculture operations track quality testing costs by cost center for operational budgeting and varianc']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: COAs in agriculture are issued to specific buyers for commodity lots they are purchasing. The \'COA issuance to buyer\' process is fundamental — customers require COAs before accepting delivery. Linking']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: COA issuance per delivery destination: in agriculture, COAs accompany shipments to specific customer receiving locations. Linking COA to delivery_location enables FSMA traceability, cold-chain complia']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Retailer and export market COA requirements mandate field-level origin traceability for fresh produce. certificate_of_analysis has country_of_origin and commodity_type. Linking COA to field enables fa']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: COAs issue a USDA grade determination (usda_grade stored as denormalized text). Linking to grading_standard normalizes the grade reference, enabling buyer contract compliance verification, USDA AMS gr']
  laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.laboratory. Business justification: COAs are issued by specific laboratories. The COA table has issuing_lab_name and lab_accreditation_number (strings) but lacks structured FK. This FK enables joining to get complete laboratory details ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: COA mrl_compliant flag and pesticide_residue_result_ppm require reference to the specific regulatory tolerance for the active ingredient/commodity/jurisdiction combination. Export market compliance (E']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable identifier for the COA document as issued by the laboratory or quality department. Used on customer-facing documentation and GFSI audit packages.']
  coa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the COA document. \'released\' indicates the COA is approved for customer-facing distribution. \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced this record. Drives SAP QM usage decision workflow.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the commodity was produced or harvested. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance under USDA AMS regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this COA record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting FSMA record-keeping requirements and GFSI documentation retention policies.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the COA is no longer considered valid for commercial or regulatory purposes. Typically tied to product shelf life or certification validity period.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'GFSI-recognized food safety certification scheme under which this COA was generated or which governs the testing requirements (SQF, BRC, IFS, FSSC 22000, GLOBALG.A.P.). Determines applicable testing protocols and documentation standards.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'GMO testing result for the lot. \'identity_preserved\' indicates the lot has been maintained under IP (Identity Preserved) protocols from seed to shipment. Required for EU export documentation and non-GMO customer specifications.']
  haccp_ccp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) parameters tested in this COA are within established critical limits. False triggers mandatory corrective action under FSMA Preventive Controls and FSIS HACCP regulations.']
  heavy_metal_type varchar [note: 'Specific heavy metal analyte tested. Each metal has distinct regulatory maximum levels under FDA, Codex Alimentarius, and EU regulations requiring separate tracking.']
  heavy_metals_result_ppm decimal(10,6) [note: 'Measured concentration of heavy metal contaminants (lead, cadmium, arsenic, mercury) in parts per million. Compared against FDA and Codex Alimentarius maximum levels for food safety compliance.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Analysis was formally issued and signed off by the authorized laboratory or quality manager. This is the principal business event date for the COA document lifecycle.']
  microbial_result decimal(12,2) [note: 'Measured microbial count expressed as colony forming units per gram (CFU/g). Covers total plate count, E. coli, Salmonella, Listeria, and other pathogens per HACCP critical control point specifications.']
  microbial_test_type varchar [note: 'Specific microbiological analyte or pathogen tested. Salmonella and Listeria are HACCP critical control point parameters under FSMA Preventive Controls and FSIS regulations.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured moisture content of the commodity expressed as a percentage of total weight. Critical quality parameter for grain storage, mold prevention, and commodity grading under USDA AMS standards.']
  mrl_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all pesticide residue results are within the applicable Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for the target market. True = compliant; False = MRL exceedance detected. Critical for export certification and GLOBALG.A.P. audit readiness.']
  mycotoxin_result_ppb decimal(10,4) [note: 'Measured concentration of mycotoxins (aflatoxin, deoxynivalenol, fumonisin, ochratoxin, zearalenone) in parts per billion (ppb). Compared against FDA action levels and EU MRL limits for regulatory compliance.']
  mycotoxin_type varchar [note: 'Specific mycotoxin analyte tested in this COA record. Different mycotoxins have distinct FDA action levels and MRL thresholds under Codex Alimentarius and EU regulations.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Aggregate pass/fail determination across all analytical parameters tested. A single \'fail\' on any critical parameter (e.g., MRL exceedance, mycotoxin above regulatory limit) results in an overall \'fail\'. Drives the release_status decision.']
  pesticide_residue_result_ppm decimal(10,6) [note: 'Measured pesticide residue concentration in parts per million (ppm). Compared against EPA-established Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) and Codex Alimentarius MRL tables for regulatory compliance and export eligibility.']
  phi_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) was observed between the last pesticide application and harvest date. True = PHI observed; False = potential PHI violation. Verified against protection_event records.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the commodity or product as it appears on the COA document, including variety, grade, or specification designation where applicable.']
  protein_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured protein content of the commodity expressed as a percentage of dry weight. Key nutritional and commercial quality parameter for grain, feed, and food ingredient specifications.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lot covered by this COA has been approved for customer shipment and commercial release. \'blocked\' prevents order fulfillment in SAP SD. Distinct from coa_status which tracks the document lifecycle.']
  test_date date [note: 'Date on which laboratory analysis was performed on the sample. Used to determine result validity windows and compliance with regulatory testing timelines.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this COA record. Supports audit trail requirements and change management tracking for regulatory documentation integrity.']

  Note: 'Certificate of Analysis (COA) master records for agricultural commodities, processed products, and inputs. Captures issuing laboratory, sample lot reference (LOT), commodity type, test date, analytical parameters tested (moisture, protein, mycotoxin, pesticide residue, heavy metals, microbial counts), pass/fail status per parameter, MRL compliance flags, and customer-facing release status. Integrates with SAP QM usage decision and supports GFSI certification documentation.'
}

Provides granular traceability beyond the collection point type classification.']
  collection_point_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical location or process stage where the sample was collected. Supports field-to-COA traceability and HACCP critical control point mapping. Values: field (in-crop collection), bin (grain storage), processing_line (in-process), receiving_dock (incoming goods), storage (warehouse), transport (in-transit).']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was physically collected from the collection point. The principal real-world event time for this transaction. Critical for chain-of-custody integrity, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance verification, and FSMA traceability records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the lab sample record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the physical sample material after testing is complete. Released: sample returned or discarded per protocol after passing; Quarantined: held pending further investigation; Destroyed: sample destroyed per biohazard or regulatory protocol; Returned: sample returned to submitter; Resampled: additional sampling required.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date on which the final disposition action was executed for the physical sample material. Required for sample retention compliance and laboratory space management.']
  fsma_sampling_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the FSMA-compliant sampling plan under which this sample was collected. Supports FDA inspection readiness and FSMA Subpart O environmental monitoring and food safety plan documentation. Aligns with FSMA business jargon.']
  gfsi_scheme_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme under which this sample was collected or is being used to demonstrate compliance. Supports certification audit readiness for SQF, BRC, IFS, and GLOBALG.A.P. schemes. Aligns with GFSI, SQF, BRC, IFS business jargons.']
  haccp_ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample was collected as part of HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) critical control point monitoring or verification activities. True requires enhanced documentation and regulatory retention per FSMA Preventive Controls rule. Aligns with HACCP business jargon.']
  mrl_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any test parameter in this sample exceeded the applicable Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) as defined by EPA, Codex Alimentarius, or customer specification. True triggers non-conformance workflow and regulatory notification procedures. Aligns with MRL business jargon.']
  overall_result varchar [note: 'Consolidated pass/fail determination for the sample based on all test parameters in the requested panel against defined acceptance criteria. Pass: all parameters within specification; Fail: one or more parameters exceeded MRL or acceptance threshold; Inconclusive: results require further review; Pending: testing not yet complete.']
  phi_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample collection date was verified to be after the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for any applied crop protection products. True confirms PHI compliance at time of sampling. Critical for pesticide residue MRL compliance. Aligns with PHI business jargon.']
  received_at_lab_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sample was officially received and logged at the testing laboratory. Used to calculate sample transit time, assess sample integrity, and enforce holding time limits per analytical method requirements.']
  required_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which laboratory testing and result reporting must be completed. Drives laboratory scheduling, SLA monitoring, and release-hold decisions for production lots awaiting quality clearance.']
  results_released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory officially released the test results and COA. Marks the completion of the testing lifecycle and triggers downstream quality disposition workflows in SAP QM.']
  sample_condition varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the sample upon receipt at the laboratory. Acceptable: sample integrity maintained; Compromised: minor integrity issues noted but testing may proceed with caveats; Rejected: sample integrity failed, testing cannot proceed. Drives chain-of-custody decisions.']
  sample_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, anomalies, or contextual information recorded at the time of sample collection or receipt. Captures field conditions, equipment calibration notes, or deviations from standard sampling protocol that may affect result interpretation.']
  sample_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the sample at collection or submission. Used for chain-of-custody documentation, COA traceability, and communication with external laboratories. Follows enterprise sample numbering convention (e.g., LAB-2024-000123).']
  sample_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Measured quantity of material collected for the sample (e.g., weight in grams, volume in milliliters). Used to verify adequacy of sample size per analytical method requirements and sampling plan specifications.']
  sample_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample quantity field. Standard units include grams (g), kilograms (kg), milliliters (mL), liters (L), milligrams (mg), and ounces (oz). Must align with the unit specified in the analytical method.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the laboratory sample from submission through disposition. Drives workflow routing in SAP QM and laboratory tracking systems. Values: submitted (collected and logged), received (confirmed at lab), in_testing (analysis underway), completed (results available), rejected (sample failed receipt criteria), disposed (sample destroyed or returned per protocol).']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical material collected for laboratory analysis. Determines applicable analytical methods, acceptance criteria, and regulatory reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|soil|water|tissue|feed|environmental_swab|air|product — promote to reference product]']
  sampling_frequency_code varchar [note: 'Classification of why this sample was collected relative to the sampling plan schedule. Routine: scheduled periodic sampling; Triggered: collected in response to an event or non-conformance; Regulatory: mandated by USDA/FDA/EPA inspection; Verification: HACCP verification sampling; Validation: process or method validation sampling.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the sample was stored during transit and prior to analysis. Critical for maintaining sample integrity for microbiological and chemical analyses. Deviations may invalidate results per ISO/IEC 17025 requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the lab sample record. Supports incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Laboratory sample submission and tracking records for all quality testing activities. Captures sample collection point (field, bin, processing line, receiving dock), sample type (grain, soil, water, tissue, feed, environmental swab), collection date/time, chain of custody, assigned laboratory, requested test panel, sample condition at receipt, and disposition after testing. Includes testing protocol metadata: sampling plan reference, sampling frequency, approved analytical methods, test parameters, and acceptance criteria. Supports field-to-COA traceability, FSMA sampling plan compliance, and HACCP verification sampling.'
}

Table quality.test_result {
  test_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual analytical test result record. Primary key for the test_result data product in the quality domain.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Pesticide residue test results must trace to the specific EPA-registered agrochemical product for MRL compliance reporting, regulatory submission, and resistance management tracking. Agriculture labs ']
  asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory instrument or equipment used to perform the analysis (e.g., HPLC, GC-MS, PCR thermocycler). Supports instrument calibration traceability and ISO/IEC 17025 audit requirements.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: test_result has coa_eligible (BOOLEAN) indicating the result qualifies for inclusion in a Certificate of Analysis. Adding coa_id FK links the individual test result to the specific COA it was included']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific commodity lot (batch) from which the sample was drawn. Enables lot-level traceability for COA generation, regulatory reporting, and potential recall management under FSMA.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory analyst who performed the test and is responsible for sign-off on the result. Required for COA generation and regulatory audit trails.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory (internal or third-party accredited) that performed the analytical test. Supports COA generation and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Test results for moisture, protein, test weight, and defects are evaluated against specific grading standards. Linking test_result to grading_standard enables automated pass/fail against official USDA']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent laboratory sample from which this test result was derived. Links the individual test result to its originating sample submission record.']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: test_result stores mrl_threshold as a denormalized numeric column. Linking to mrl_threshold normalizes the regulatory tolerance reference, enabling pass/fail determination against jurisdiction-specifi']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the non-conformance record raised as a result of a failed test result. Populated when pass_fail_status is \'fail\', linking the test result to the corrective action workflow in SAP QM.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Incoming quality test results (MRL, mycotoxin, germination) must be linked to the specific GRN for lot acceptance/rejection decisions, return claim evidence, and FSMA supplier verification records. te']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Test results drive stock release decisions in SAP QM — a pass result releases blocked stock, a fail triggers quarantine. Linking test_result to stock_position enables automated QM usage decisions, sto']
  action_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Internal or regulatory action threshold below the MRL that triggers corrective action or investigation before the regulatory limit is breached. Supports HACCP CCP monitoring and FSMA preventive controls.']
  analyst_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the laboratory analyst formally reviewed and signed off on the test result. Required for COA issuance and ISO/IEC 17025 quality system compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test result record was first created in the quality management system. Supports audit trail requirements under FSMA, ISO 22000, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  detection_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lowest concentration or quantity of the analyte that can be reliably detected by the test method. Critical for interpreting non-detect results and MRL compliance reporting to FDA and USDA.']
  dilution_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'The dilution factor applied to the sample prior to analysis. Used to back-calculate the actual concentration in the original sample matrix and ensure accurate MRL compliance determination.']
  haccp_ccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test result is associated with a HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) monitoring activity. CCP-flagged results require immediate corrective action upon failure and are subject to FSIS and FDA regulatory review.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'The ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent accreditation number of the laboratory that performed the test. Printed on COAs and required for regulatory submissions to USDA, FDA, and FSIS to validate laboratory competency.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantitative result of the analytical test as reported by the laboratory instrument or analyst. For qualitative tests (e.g., Salmonella presence/absence), this field is null and qualitative_result is used instead.']
  parameter_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the test parameter type. Supports regulatory reporting segmentation and HACCP critical control point (CCP) categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mycotoxin|pesticide_residue|microbiological|physical|chemical|nutritional|heavy_metal|sensory — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]']
  parameter_name varchar [note: 'Name of the analytical parameter being tested (e.g., aflatoxin B1, glyphosate residue, Salmonella spp., moisture content, pH, Electrical Conductivity (EC), nitrate). Drives COA line-item labeling and MRL threshold comparison.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'The compliance determination for this individual test result based on comparison of the measured value against the MRL threshold and action limit. Drives COA issuance, lot release decisions, and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.']
  qualitative_result varchar [note: 'Qualitative outcome for presence/absence or positive/negative tests (e.g., Salmonella not detected, GMO absent). Used when a numeric measured_value is not applicable. Supports FSIS microbiological reporting.']
  quantitation_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lowest concentration at which the analyte can be quantitatively measured with acceptable precision and accuracy. Distinct from LOD; used for MRL compliance determination and COA reporting.']
  recovery_percent decimal(6,2) [note: 'The percentage recovery of a spiked control sample used to validate the analytical method performance for this test run. Acceptable recovery ranges are defined by AOAC and Codex Alimentarius guidelines (typically 70–120%).']
  regulatory_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulatory monitoring or surveillance program under which this test was conducted (e.g., USDA Pesticide Data Program (PDP), FDA Pesticide Residue Monitoring Program, FSIS National Residue Program (NRP), USDA AMS Microbiological Data Program).']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test result must be reported to a regulatory authority (USDA, FDA, FSIS, EPA, or APHIS). Triggered by failed MRL results, CCP deviations, or mandatory surveillance program requirements.']
  result_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the test result was formally reported and made available for COA generation and lot release decisions. Used to calculate laboratory turnaround time KPIs.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the test result record within the quality management system. Controls whether the result is eligible for COA inclusion and lot release authorization.']
  result_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this result is an original first-run test, a repeat test, a confirmatory test following a suspect result, or a retest after corrective action. Supports regulatory investigation workflows.']
  sample_matrix varchar [note: 'The physical material or commodity type being tested (e.g., grain, soil, water, feed, fresh produce, processed food, animal tissue). Determines applicable MRL thresholds and regulatory reporting pathways under USDA, FDA, or FSIS jurisdiction.']
  test_method_code varchar [note: 'The standardized analytical method reference code used to perform the test (e.g., AOAC 2007.01, ISO 6579, EPA Method 8270D, USDA FSIS MLG 4.10). Required for COA documentation and regulatory method validation compliance.']
  test_method_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the analytical method used (e.g., \'AOAC QuEChERS Multi-Residue Pesticide Method\', \'ISO Horizontal Method for Salmonella Detection\'). Complements the method code for COA readability.']
  test_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory analyst observations, anomalies, interferences, or qualifications associated with this test result. Supports audit trail documentation and COA footnotes.']
  test_performed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the analytical test was physically performed in the laboratory. Distinct from sample receipt and result sign-off timestamps. Critical for FSMA traceability and COA dating.']
  test_result_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this test result, used on Certificates of Analysis (COA) and regulatory submissions. Typically sourced from SAP QM or the laboratory information management system (LIMS).']
  uncertainty_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The expanded measurement uncertainty associated with the test result, expressed in the same unit as the measured value. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 accredited laboratory reporting and MRL compliance boundary decisions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., ppb, ppm, CFU/g, %, mg/kg, pH units, mS/cm for EC). Aligns with Codex Alimentarius and AOAC reporting conventions for regulatory submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this test result record was last modified in the quality management system. Required for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and regulatory audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual analytical test results associated with a lab sample. Records test parameter name (e.g., aflatoxin, glyphosate residue, Salmonella, moisture content, pH, EC), measured value, unit of measure (ppb, ppm, CFU/g, %), method reference (AOAC, ISO, EPA method), detection limit, MRL threshold, pass/fail determination, and laboratory analyst sign-off. Provides granular traceability for COA generation and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.'
}

Table quality.haccp_plan {
  haccp_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the HACCP plan record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running regulatory compliance plan binding a facility/process to documented food safety controls.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural commodity or food product category covered by this HACCP plan (e.g., fresh produce, poultry, dairy). Aligns with USDA/FDA commodity classification for regulatory reporting.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production facility, processing plant, or packing house where this HACCP plan is applied. Links to the facility master record for location, registration, and regulatory context.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: FSMA Produce Safety Rule requires on-farm food safety/HACCP plans scoped to specific farm operations. haccp_plan has fsma_preventive_controls_applicable and plan_scope. Farm unit provides the operatio']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: HACCP plans for chemical CCPs (pesticide residue control points) define critical limits based on MRL thresholds. A food safety manager designing a HACCP plan would reference the applicable MRL thresho']
  primary_haccp_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified individual designated as the HACCP team leader responsible for plan development, maintenance, and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Step 1 (assemble HACCP team).']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: HACCP plans in grain storage and cold chain agriculture govern specific storage locations (CCPs for temperature, fumigation, moisture). haccp_plan has facility_id but not storage_location granularity.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the current version of the HACCP plan was formally approved by authorized management. Distinct from effective_date; approval may precede implementation.']
  biological_hazards_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether biological hazards (e.g., Salmonella, E. coli O157:H7, Listeria monocytogenes) were identified as significant hazards requiring a Critical Control Point (CCP) in this HACCP plan.']
  ccp_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Critical Control Points (CCPs) identified and documented within this HACCP plan. Used for plan completeness validation and audit readiness checks.']
  ccp_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the CCP process step where control is applied (e.g., \'Pasteurization heat treatment\', \'Metal detection at packaging line exit\', \'Chlorine wash for fresh produce\').']
  ccp_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for a specific Critical Control Point within the HACCP plan (e.g., CCP-1B for biological, CCP-2C for chemical, CCP-3P for physical). Used to cross-reference monitoring logs and corrective action records.']
  chemical_hazards_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether chemical hazards (e.g., pesticide residues exceeding MRL, allergens, mycotoxins, heavy metals, cleaning chemical residues) were identified as significant hazards in this HACCP plan.']
  corrective_action_procedure varchar [note: 'Documented procedure to be followed when a deviation from the critical limit is detected at the CCP, including product disposition, process correction, and root cause investigation steps. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 5.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this HACCP plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.']
  critical_limit_description varchar [note: 'Documented critical limit(s) for each CCP that distinguish safe from unsafe product (e.g., \'Internal temperature ≥ 72°C for ≥ 15 seconds\', \'Chlorine concentration 50–200 ppm\', \'Metal detector sensitivity: 1.5mm ferrous\'). Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 3.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any active deviation from a critical limit has been recorded against this HACCP plan in the current monitoring period. True signals that a corrective action is open or pending closure.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the HACCP plan became operationally effective and was implemented in production. Marks the start of the plan\'s binding period for compliance purposes.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this HACCP plan version expires or is scheduled for mandatory reassessment review. Nullable for open-ended plans; populated when a defined review cycle is mandated by the regulatory framework or certification body.']
  fsma_preventive_controls_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this HACCP plan is subject to the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Preventive Controls for Human Food rule (21 CFR Part 117). False may apply to exempt facilities or animal food operations governed by a separate rule.']
  gfsi_scheme varchar [note: 'GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme under which this HACCP plan is audited (Safe Quality Food (SQF), British Retail Consortium (BRC), International Featured Standards (IFS), FSSC 22000, or GLOBALG.A.P.). Drives audit scope and documentation requirements.']
  hazard_analysis_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the hazard analysis conducted for this plan, documenting the methodology used to identify and evaluate biological, chemical, and physical hazards at each process step. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 1.']
  intended_use varchar [note: 'Documented intended use and consumer population for the product covered by this plan, including whether the product is intended for vulnerable populations (e.g., infants, immunocompromised). Informs hazard severity assessment.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal reassessment or revalidation of the HACCP plan, triggered by process changes, new hazard information, regulatory updates, or scheduled periodic review. Required by FDA FSMA and GFSI schemes.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the monitoring parameter is measured at the CCP (e.g., continuous, every batch, hourly, per shift). Defined in the HACCP plan and enforced during production operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|every_batch|hourly|every_2_hours|daily|per_shift|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Description of the procedure, instrument, or technique used to monitor the CCP parameter (e.g., \'Calibrated digital thermometer\', \'Inline pH meter\', \'Metal detector test piece challenge\'). Supports equipment calibration traceability.']
  monitoring_parameter varchar [note: 'The physical or chemical parameter measured at the CCP to verify the critical limit is being met (e.g., temperature in °C, pH, chlorine concentration in ppm, metal detector sensitivity in mm). Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 4. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temperature|pH|chlorine_concentration|metal_detector_sensitivity|water_activity|time|pressure|flow_rate|turbidity|conductivity|other — promote to reference product]']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date by which the next formal reassessment of the HACCP plan must be completed. Used for compliance calendar management and audit readiness tracking.']
  open_corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Count of corrective action records currently open and unresolved against this HACCP plan. Used for compliance dashboard monitoring and audit readiness assessment. Zero indicates full compliance.']
  physical_hazards_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical hazards (e.g., metal fragments, glass, bone, hard plastic, stones) were identified as significant hazards requiring a CCP such as metal detection or X-ray inspection in this HACCP plan.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name of the HACCP plan, typically referencing the commodity, process line, or facility scope (e.g., \'Fresh-Cut Leafy Greens Processing — Facility A\').']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier for the HACCP plan, used in regulatory submissions, audit documentation, and cross-system references (e.g., SAP QM inspection lot linkage). Format: HACCP-{FACILITY_CODE}-{YEAR}-{SEQ}.']
  plan_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the operational scope of the HACCP plan — whether it covers an entire facility, a specific process line, a commodity category, or a specific finished product. Determines applicability during audits.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the HACCP plan. \'active\' indicates the plan is in operational use; \'under_review\' indicates a scheduled reassessment is in progress; \'suspended\' indicates temporary halt pending corrective action; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the HACCP plan document, incremented upon each formal review or revision cycle. Supports document control and audit trail requirements (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\').']
  prerequisite_programs varchar [note: 'List of prerequisite programs (PRPs) supporting this HACCP plan, such as Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), sanitation standard operating procedures (SSOPs), pest control, and allergen management programs. PRPs are foundational controls that underpin the HACCP system.']
  process_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the production or processing scope covered by this HACCP plan, including process flow steps from raw material receipt through finished product dispatch. Required by Codex Alimentarius Step 4 (process flow diagram description).']
  recordkeeping_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of the records that must be maintained to document HACCP plan implementation, including monitoring logs, corrective action records, verification records, and retention periods. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 7.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory or certification framework under which this HACCP plan was developed and is maintained (e.g., FDA FSMA Preventive Controls, FSIS HACCP, GFSI scheme such as SQF or BRC). Determines audit requirements and record retention obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA_FSMA|FSIS_HACCP|USDA_NOP|GLOBALG.A.P.|SQF|BRC|IFS|ISO_22000|Codex_Alimentarius|EU_852_2004|other — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this HACCP plan record in the system. Used for change tracking, version control, and data synchronization with SAP QM module.']
  verification_procedure varchar [note: 'Documented activities used to confirm the HACCP plan is functioning effectively, including calibration schedules, record review, periodic product testing, and internal audits. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 6.']

  Note: 'HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) plan master records with embedded CCP monitoring logs for each production process, commodity, or facility. Plan-level: defines process scope, hazard analysis summary, identified biological/chemical/physical hazards, critical control points (CCPs), critical limits, monitoring procedures, corrective action procedures, verification activities, and record-keeping requirements. CCP monitoring log detail: captures real-time or periodic measurements at each CCP including monitoring parameter (temperature, pH, chlorine concentration, metal detector sensitivity), measured value, critical limit, monitoring frequency, operator identity, timestamp, deviation flag, and immediate corrective action taken. Provides both the plan framework and continuous compliance evidence required by FDA FSMA Preventive Controls rule and GFSI scheme auditors (SQF, BRC, IFS).'
}

Table quality.quality_certification {
  quality_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each quality and food safety certification record held by the enterprise. Primary key for the quality_certification data product.']
  certification_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.certification_record. Business justification: Supplier certification satisfying customer requirement: food manufacturer customers require specific certifications (SQF, GlobalG.A.P.). Linking the producer\'s quality_certification to the customer\'s ']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Quality certifications (GAP, GFSI, GlobalG.A.P.) are scoped to specific commodities — a GlobalG.A.P. certificate for tomatoes is distinct from one for peppers. Certification bodies issue certificates ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Annual certification fees (organic, GFSI, GlobalG.A.P.) are charged to cost centers. Certification cost tracking for management accounting and budget planning in agriculture requires this link to allo']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal facility or site master record covered by this certification. Links the certificate to the enterprise\'s facility hierarchy for operational and regulatory reporting.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Organic certificates, GlobalG.A.P. certificates, and GAP certifications in agriculture are issued to farm operations. Linking quality_certification to farm_unit enables direct lookup of which certific']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Certification fees and renewal costs are posted to GL accounts as licensing/certification expense. Financial reporting and cost-of-quality analysis require certification costs mapped to a GL account f']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: GFSI certifications (SQF, BRC, IFS) and FSMA compliance certifications are directly tied to the HACCP plans that govern food safety at a facility. A quality certification should reference the HACCP pl']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Facility-level quality certifications for organic operations (USDA NOP) must reference the product-level organic certificate. This link enables NOP compliance verification, certificate renewal coordin']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: USDA NOP organic certifications are issued against specific parcels (parcel has organic_certification_status, organic_transition_start_date). quality_certification has usda_organic_op_number. Certifyi']
  product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_certification. Business justification: Quality certification audits validate that product-level certifications (Non-GMO Project, Kosher, Halal, SQF) are current and compliant. Linking quality_certification to product_certification enables ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Quality certifications (organic, GlobalG.A.P., GFSI) are tied to profit centers for revenue premium attribution and certification cost P&L reporting. Agriculture operations track certified vs. convent']
  responsible_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: GlobalG.A.P., USDA Organic, and FSMA certifications require a named responsible manager as an accountable employee of record. The existing \'responsible_manager\' text field is denormalized; a proper FK']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Organic and food safety certifications (USDA Organic, SQF, BRC) apply to specific storage locations (certified organic bins, temperature-controlled stores). quality_certification has facility_id but n']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The national or international accreditation body that accredits the certification body (e.g., ANAB - ANSI National Accreditation Board, UKAS - United Kingdom Accreditation Service, DAkkS). Validates the legitimacy of the certification body and is required for GFSI scheme recognition.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'The type of audit that resulted in or is associated with this certification record: initial (first-time certification), recertification (renewal at end of cycle), surveillance (periodic interim audit), unannounced (surprise audit required by some GFSI schemes), or extension (scope extension audit).']
  body varchar [note: 'The name of the accredited third-party certification body or registrar that issued the certificate (e.g., NSF International, SGS, Bureau Veritas, DNV, Intertek). Required for regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS and for GFSI scheme compliance.']
  certificate_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference path or document management system identifier pointing to the scanned or digital copy of the issued certificate (e.g., SharePoint URL, SAP DMS document number). Enables direct access to the certificate document for audits and customer requests.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The externally-issued, unique certificate number assigned by the certification body. Used for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and customer-facing documentation (e.g., COA, supplier declarations). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA QM module certificate tracking.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification. Active: certificate is valid and in good standing. Suspended: temporarily halted by the certification body pending corrective action. Withdrawn: permanently revoked. Expired: past the expiry date without renewal. Pending: initial application or renewal in progress. Under_review: surveillance audit or non-conformance review in progress. Drives certification readiness dashboards and renewal workflows.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the certification by its primary purpose: food_safety (SQF, BRC, IFS, HACCP-based), environmental (ISO 14001, ISCC), organic (USDA NOP), sustainability (ISCC, Fair Trade), social (Fair Trade), or quality_management (ISO 22000). Supports portfolio-level reporting and gap analysis.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all corrective actions arising from audit non-conformances must be submitted to the certification body. Null if no open non-conformances exist. Drives corrective action workflow and escalation alerts.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the certified facility or operation is located (e.g., USA, BRA, DEU). Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance and international regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the certificate expires and must be renewed to maintain certified status. Null for open-ended certifications. Triggers renewal workflow alerts and drives certification readiness dashboards.']
  gfsi_scheme_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is recognized under the Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) benchmarking framework (True) or is a non-GFSI scheme (False). GFSI-recognized schemes include SQF, BRC, IFS, FSSC 22000, and GLOBALG.A.P. Critical for customer and retailer compliance requirements.']
  globalg_ap_producer_number varchar [note: 'The unique GLOBALG.A.P. producer or certificate number assigned by the GLOBALG.A.P. secretariat and published in the GLOBALG.A.P. database. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. certification verification by buyers and retailers.']
  grade_or_level varchar [note: 'The grade, level, or rating awarded by the certification body upon successful audit (e.g., SQF Level 2, SQF Level 3, BRC Grade AA/A/B/C/D, IFS Higher Level/Foundation Level). Null for schemes that do not use graded outcomes. Used for benchmarking and customer qualification.']
  initial_certification_date date [note: 'The date on which the facility or operation first achieved certification under this scheme, regardless of subsequent renewals. Used to calculate certification tenure and demonstrate long-standing compliance to customers and regulators.']
  iscc_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number issued under the International Sustainability and Carbon Certification (ISCC) scheme for bioenergy and biomass sustainability compliance. Null for non-ISCC certifications.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification body formally issued the certificate. Marks the start of the certification validity period. Used for compliance tracking and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.']
  multi_site_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certificate covers multiple sites or facilities under a single certificate number (True) or a single site (False). Multi-site certifications are common for GLOBALG.A.P. Option 2 and BRC multi-site schemes.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The planned date for the next certification or surveillance audit as agreed with the certification body. Drives the surveillance audit schedule and renewal workflow alerts in the certification readiness dashboard.']
  nonconformance_count_major varchar [note: 'The number of major non-conformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Major non-conformances require corrective action within a defined timeframe and may result in certification suspension if unresolved. Tracked per SAP S/4HANA QM module non-conformance records.']
  nonconformance_count_minor varchar [note: 'The number of minor non-conformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Minor non-conformances require corrective action but do not immediately threaten certification status. Tracked per SAP S/4HANA QM module.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, conditions, or qualifications associated with the certification (e.g., scope limitations, special conditions imposed by the certification body, internal observations). Supports operational context for quality managers.']
  product_category_scope varchar [note: 'The product category or commodity type covered by the certification scope as defined by the scheme (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P. crop category: Fruit and Vegetables; BRC product category: Fresh Produce; SQF food sector category). Supports product-level compliance mapping.']
  renewal_reminder_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days before the expiry date at which automated renewal reminder notifications should be triggered. Configurable per scheme to align with certification body lead times and internal preparation requirements.']
  sap_qm_inspection_lot varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA QM module inspection lot number associated with the certification audit or quality inspection event. Enables traceability between the certification record and the source system inspection record for audit and compliance purposes.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'The name of the food safety or quality certification scheme under which the certificate was issued (e.g., Safe Quality Food (SQF), British Retail Consortium (BRC), International Featured Standards (IFS), GLOBALG.A.P., ISO 22000, USDA Organic/National Organic Program (NOP), International Sustainability and Carbon Certification (ISCC), Fair Trade). Drives certification readiness dashboards and renewal workflows.']
  scheme_version varchar [note: 'The edition or version number of the certification scheme standard under which the audit was conducted (e.g., SQF Edition 9, BRC Issue 9, IFS Food Version 8). Critical for tracking standard upgrades and ensuring compliance with the current scheme revision.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the products, processes, activities, or operations covered by the certificate as defined by the certification body (e.g., \'Production and packing of fresh vegetables\', \'Manufacture of animal feed\'). Directly reflects the scope statement printed on the certificate.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification body formally suspended the certificate, if applicable. Null for active or non-suspended certifications. Required for regulatory reporting and customer notification obligations.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for certification suspension as communicated by the certification body (e.g., unresolved major non-conformances, product recall, failure to submit corrective actions). Null if not suspended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this certification record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.']
  usda_organic_op_number varchar [note: 'The USDA National Organic Program (NOP) operation number assigned to the certified organic operation. Required for USDA organic labeling compliance and traceability under the National Organic Program regulations.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the certificate was permanently withdrawn or voluntarily surrendered. Null for active certifications. Required for regulatory reporting and audit trail completeness.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all quality and food safety certifications held by the enterprise, including GFSI scheme certifications (SQF, BRC, IFS), GLOBALG.A.P., USDA Organic (NOP), ISCC, Fair Trade, and ISO 22000/14001. Captures certification body, scheme name, scope of certification, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, surveillance audit schedule, current status (active, suspended, withdrawn), and facility or operation covered. Drives certification readiness dashboards and renewal workflows.'
}

Table quality.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each formal quality or food safety audit record. Primary key for the audit data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Quality audits (GAP, GFSI, organic) are scoped to specific commodities. Audit scheduling, scope definition, and certification outcome reporting all require commodity-level audit tracking. A certificat']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Third-party GFSI and GlobalG.A.P. audits incur certification body fees and travel costs charged to cost centers. Certification cost tracking and cost-of-quality management accounting in agriculture re']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Customer-mandated audits (GFSI, GlobalG.A.P., SQF) are a standard agriculture commercial requirement — buyers require certified suppliers. Linking audit to the requesting customer account enables cust']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead auditor or auditing body representative responsible for conducting this audit.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, farm, processing plant, or operational site that was the subject of this audit.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: USDA Organic, GlobalG.A.P., and GFSI scheme audits are issued to farm operations. Linking audit to farm_unit enables the farm-level audit history required for certification renewal, buyer qualificatio']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: GAP audits and organic certification audits are conducted at the field level. Linking audit to field enables field-level audit history and traceability of which fields are GAP-certified — a named requ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Audit fees (certification body, third-party auditor) are posted to GL accounts as certification/compliance expense. Financial period-close and cost-of-quality P&L reporting require audit costs mapped ']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Quality audits (GFSI, GLOBALG.A.P., internal HACCP verification audits) are conducted to verify the adequacy and implementation of HACCP plans. A quality audit should reference the specific HACCP plan']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: Organic certification audits (USDA NOP) and GlobalG.A.P. audits require parcel-level legal land traceability. Parcel holds organic_certification_status and organic_transition_start_date. Auditors must']
  previous_audit_quality_audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding audit record for the same facility and standard, enabling trend analysis and certification history tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Quality audits are conducted for specific business units whose P&L is tracked by profit center. Audit costs and certification outcomes (premium market access, suspension) directly affect profit center']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Vendor qualification audits (second-party audits) are a named procurement process in agriculture — quality_audit records vendor facility assessments for GlobalG.A.P., GFSI, and FSMA compliance. qualit']
  workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific worker certification or license that was reviewed during this audit.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the audit concluded, including closing meeting. Used to calculate audit duration and track completion.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the audit commenced on-site or remotely. May differ from scheduled_date due to rescheduling.']
  announced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the auditee was given advance notice of the audit (True = announced, False = unannounced). Unannounced audits are required by certain standards (e.g., BRC unannounced option) and carry higher credibility with retail customers.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the audit was conducted: on_site (physical presence at the facility), remote (conducted via video/document sharing), hybrid (combination of on-site and remote), or document_review (desk-based review only).']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to this audit, used in correspondence, reports, and regulatory submissions. Follows the format AUD-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the operations, processes, product categories, or geographic areas covered by this audit (e.g., \'Fresh produce packing and cold storage operations\', \'Grain receiving and storage — Facility A\').']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit record, tracking progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit by party relationship: internal (first-party, conducted by own staff), second_party (customer or buyer-initiated), third_party (independent certification body), regulatory (government agency), or surveillance (periodic check between full certification cycles).']
  auditing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the certification body, regulatory agency, or organization that conducted or commissioned the audit (e.g., NSF International, SGS, Bureau Veritas, USDA AMS, FDA).']
  auditor_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the auditor regarding this specific credential during this specific audit. May include observations about renewal status, scope limitations, or corrective action recommendations specific to this credential review.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current certificate expires and recertification is required. Critical for audit program scheduling and maintaining uninterrupted certification status.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification body formally issued the certificate following a successful audit. Marks the start of the certification validity period.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number issued by the certification body upon successful audit completion. Used for regulatory submissions, customer verification, and GFSI database registration.']
  certification_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal certification decision resulting from the audit: certified (new certification granted), recertified (existing certification renewed), conditional (certification granted with conditions), suspended (certification temporarily withdrawn), withdrawn (certification permanently revoked), or not_certified (audit failed).']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The formal quality or food safety standard against which this audit was conducted (e.g., SQF, BRC, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P., USDA Organic, ISO 22000, HACCP, GAP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|IFS|GLOBALG.A.P.|USDA_Organic|ISO_22000|HACCP|GAP|GMP|FSMA — promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action_closed_date date [note: 'The date on which all corrective actions from this audit were formally accepted and closed by the auditing body or internal quality team. Null if corrective actions are still open.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all required corrective actions from this audit must be submitted to the auditing body or documented as completed. Null if no corrective actions are required.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more corrective actions are required as a result of this audit. True when any major or minor non-conformances were identified that mandate a formal corrective action response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days the audit spanned from actual start to actual end. Used for audit program benchmarking and resource planning.']
