  finding_if_expired varchar [note: 'The formal audit finding recorded if the credential was expired, lapsed, or otherwise non-compliant at the time of the audit review. Populated only when the credential failed verification; null if the credential was current and valid.']
  fsma_rule varchar [note: 'The specific FSMA rule or subpart applicable to this audit, if the audit is FSMA-related (e.g., Produce Safety Rule, Preventive Controls for Human Food, Foreign Supplier Verification Program). Null for non-FSMA audits.']
  gap_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit resulted in or maintained a Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) certification. Relevant for USDA GAP/GHP audits and GLOBALG.A.P. assessments.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade or categorical rating assigned as the audit outcome per the applicable standard (e.g., BRC AA/A/B/C/D, SQF Excellent/Good/Satisfactory/Unsatisfactory). Null if the standard uses numeric score only. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AA|A|B|C|D|Excellent|Good|Satisfactory|Unsatisfactory — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_auditor_credential varchar [note: 'Professional certification or credential held by the lead auditor (e.g., SQF Practitioner, PCQI, REHS, ISO 22000 Lead Auditor). Validates auditor competency for the standard being assessed.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor who conducted the audit. Captured for traceability and credential verification purposes.']
  major_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Total number of major non-conformances (critical findings) identified during the audit. Major non-conformances typically jeopardize certification and require immediate corrective action.']
  minor_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Total number of minor non-conformances identified during the audit. Minor non-conformances require corrective action within a defined timeframe but do not immediately jeopardize certification.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next audit (recertification or surveillance) must be completed to maintain continuous certification status. Derived from certificate expiry and standard-specific surveillance requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, auditor remarks, or internal quality team commentary not captured in structured fields.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit. Observations are not non-conformances but indicate areas where practices could be strengthened.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score or percentage achieved in the audit against the applicable standard. Scoring methodology varies by standard (e.g., SQF uses percentage, BRC uses weighted scoring). Null if the standard uses grade-only outcomes.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal audit report was issued by the auditing body to the auditee. Marks the start of the corrective action response window.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number of the formal audit report document issued by the auditing body. Used for document management, regulatory submissions, and customer sharing.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the audit was originally scheduled to begin. Used for audit program planning and resource coordination.']
  site_audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the operations, processes, or product categories audited at this specific trial site within the broader audit engagement. May differ from the overall audit scope when a multi-site audit covers different activities at each site.']
  site_certification_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal certification decision for this specific trial site resulting from the audit. In multi-site campaigns, individual sites may receive different outcomes (e.g., one site certified, another conditionally certified). Cannot reside on audit alone as it varies per site.']
  standard_version varchar [note: 'The specific version or edition of the certification standard used for this audit (e.g., SQF Edition 9, BRC Issue 9, IFS Food Version 8). Critical for tracking compliance against evolving standard requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this audit record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP S/4HANA QM module, and Silver layer delta processing.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The specific date on which the auditor verified this credential during the audit. Distinct from workforce_certification.verification_date, which records the general HR verification date independent of any audit.']
  verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification or license was confirmed as current, valid, and compliant at the time of this audit review. True means the auditor verified the credential was in good standing during the audit; false indicates it could not be verified or was found non-compliant.']

  Note: 'Master record for all formal quality and food safety audits including internal audits, second-party customer audits, and third-party certification audits (SQF, BRC, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P., USDA Organic). Captures audit type, auditing body or auditor name, audit scope, facility or operation audited, scheduled and actual audit dates, audit standard and version, overall grade or score, and certification outcome. Distinct from operational inspections — audits are formal system-level assessments against a defined standard.'
}

Table quality.laboratory {
  laboratory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for laboratory']
  parent_laboratory_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on laboratory (parent_laboratory_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accreditation body that granted laboratory certification (e.g., A2LA, ANAB, UKAS).']
  accreditation_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory accreditation became effective.']
  accreditation_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current laboratory accreditation expires and requires renewal.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Unique accreditation certificate number issued by the accreditation body.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the laboratory under ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent standards.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the laboratory facility.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as suite, building, or floor number.']
  biosafety_level varchar [note: 'Biosafety level classification for laboratories handling biological agents.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the laboratory is located.']
  clia_certificate_number varchar [note: 'CLIA certificate number if laboratory performs clinical testing on agricultural workers or food safety personnel.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the laboratory is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory record was first created in the system.']
  data_integrity_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory meets data integrity requirements under ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available).']
  equipment_calibration_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard frequency in days for calibration of laboratory testing equipment.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date when the laboratory was established and began operations.']
  fda_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA registration number for laboratories conducting food safety testing under FSMA requirements.']
  gfsi_certification_scheme varchar [note: 'GFSI-recognized certification scheme the laboratory operates under (e.g., SQF, BRC, IFS, FSSC 22000). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|IFS|FSSC_22000|GLOBALG.A.P.|PrimusGFS|CanadaGAP — promote to reference product]']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique code for the laboratory used in regulatory filings and certifications.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the laboratory facility.']
  laboratory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of laboratory based on operational model and ownership structure.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent external audit or assessment of the laboratory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this laboratory record was last updated.']
  lims_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Laboratory Information Management System used for sample tracking and data management.']
  next_audit_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next external audit or surveillance assessment.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the laboratory facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the laboratory location.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary laboratory contact for quality and compliance communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person or laboratory director.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the laboratory contact.']
  proficiency_testing_participation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory participates in proficiency testing programs to validate testing competency.']
  quality_management_system varchar [note: 'Quality management system framework the laboratory operates under.']
  specialization_area varchar [note: 'Primary area of laboratory specialization (e.g., microbiology, chemistry, pesticide residue, mycotoxin, allergen, GMO, nutritional analysis).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the laboratory is located.']
  testing_capacity_samples_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of samples the laboratory can process per day under normal operating conditions.']
  testing_scope varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of testing capabilities and scope of accreditation including test methods and matrices.']
  turnaround_time_standard_hours varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time in hours for routine test results from sample receipt to report delivery.']
  usda_establishment_number varchar [note: 'USDA establishment number for laboratories testing meat, poultry, and egg products.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for laboratory. Referenced by laboratory_id.'
}

Table customer.account {
  account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the agricultural enterprise data platform. Primary key for the account master.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In SAP-based Agriculture ERP, every customer account is assigned to a company code for AR posting, revenue recognition, and credit control area assignment. Agriculture domain experts expect this link ']
  distribution_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.distribution_channel. Business justification: Customer accounts in agriculture are served through specific distribution channels (direct, broker, cooperative, export) that govern pricing strategy and order processing. The existing plain-text \'dis']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Every Agriculture account requires an assigned account manager employee for territory management, commission calculations, renewal tracking, and customer relationship reporting. This fundamental CRM-H']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: In agriculture ERP (SAP SD), customer accounts are assigned to a sales organization governing pricing procedures, order processing rules, and revenue recognition. The existing plain-text \'sales_org_co']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent account record for B2B enterprise account hierarchy management. Enables roll-up reporting across subsidiary accounts, divisions, or buying groups within a corporate family.']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: Account-level payment terms drive all invoicing and credit decisions in agriculture. Replacing denormalized payment_terms_code with a FK to payment_term enforces referential integrity, supports credit']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: In agriculture ERP (SAP), every customer account is assigned to a profit center for revenue attribution, P&L reporting by crop/commodity segment, and customer profitability analysis. A domain expert w']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: FK linking account to its primary customer segment classification. Accounts are classified by segment for pricing, service tier, and sales strategy. The segment product defines all classification sche']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Customer accounts in agriculture are assigned to sales territories for rep coverage, quota allocation, and market penetration reporting. Territory-level account portfolio management and sales rep perf']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customer account. Controls whether the account is eligible for order processing, credit extension, and CRM engagement.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier assigned to the customer account based on revenue contribution, volume, and relationship importance. Drives service level agreements, pricing tiers, and sales resource allocation.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer account by business model and relationship type. Drives pricing, credit, and service tier logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: B2B_enterprise|B2C_individual|cooperative|distributor|wholesaler|retailer|food_manufacturer|direct_consumer — promote to reference product]']
  annual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or reported annual revenue of the customer organization, expressed in the account\'s billing currency. Used for account tiering, credit risk assessment, and strategic segmentation.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the customer\'s official billing address (street number and name). Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and regulatory reporting.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the customer\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction assignment, regional analytics, and logistics planning.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the customer\'s billing address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, export controls, and international tax reporting.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the customer\'s billing address. Used for tax rate lookup, geographic segmentation, and delivery zone assignment.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the customer\'s billing address. Used for sales tax determination, USDA/FDA jurisdictional compliance, and regional reporting.']
  cool_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer account requires Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance on all product shipments and documentation, per USDA Agricultural Marketing Act requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR compliance tracking.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance approved for this customer account, expressed in the account\'s billing currency. Enforced during sales order processing in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or third-party credit rating assigned to the customer account, reflecting creditworthiness and payment risk. Used by finance to set credit limits and payment terms. Examples: AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB, B, CCC.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the default billing currency for this customer account (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Used in sales order and invoice processing.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number uniquely identifying the customer\'s legal business entity. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier/buyer verification, and B2B trade compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account became active and eligible for business transactions. Marks the start of the commercial relationship.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Approximate number of employees at the customer organization. Used for account sizing, market segmentation, and sales resource prioritization.']
  fsma_registered_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s receiving facility is registered under the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA), which is required for certain food product shipments and traceability compliance.']
  gmo_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s stated preference regarding Genetically Modified Organism (GMO) content in purchased agricultural products. Drives product eligibility filtering and supply chain segregation requirements.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this account within the B2B enterprise account hierarchy (e.g., 1 = ultimate parent, 2 = regional subsidiary, 3 = local buying unit). Supports hierarchical roll-up analytics.']
  industry_code varchar [note: 'North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) or Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code describing the customer\'s primary industry segment (e.g., food manufacturing, wholesale trade, retail grocery). Supports market segmentation and analytics.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the customer account as filed with the relevant government authority (e.g., food manufacturer, distributor, cooperative, or individual consumer). Used for contracts, invoicing, and regulatory compliance.']
  organic_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer account requires USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified products for all or part of their purchases. Triggers organic supply chain routing and COA documentation requirements.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the customer account, used for order confirmations, invoices, compliance notifications, and CRM communications.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the customer account, including country code. Used for order management, logistics coordination, and customer service.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'Unique account identifier from Salesforce CRM, used to synchronize customer relationship data, sales pipeline, and partner management activities between the CRM and the enterprise data platform.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'Customer master record number assigned in SAP S/4HANA SD module. Used as the cross-reference key for order management, billing, and accounts receivable reconciliation.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal or state tax identification number (EIN, TIN, or VAT number) for the customer entity. Required for tax reporting, invoicing compliance, and USDA/FDA regulatory submissions.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for lifecycle reporting, churn analysis, and regulatory record retention.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) name used commercially by the customer, which may differ from the registered legal name. Supports sales and marketing operations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record. Used for change data capture, audit trail, and CRM synchronization.']

  Note: 'Master record for all customer accounts served by the agricultural enterprise — including food manufacturers, distributors, wholesalers, retailers, cooperatives, and direct consumers. Captures account type (B2B enterprise, B2C individual), legal entity name, tax identification, DUNS number, credit rating, account status, assigned sales territory, Salesforce CRM account ID, SAP customer master number, GLOBALG.A.P. buyer certification status, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance flag, preferred commodity categories, and account tier classification. This is the SSOT for WHO the business serves.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the individual contact person record within the customer domain. Serves as the primary key for all contact-related data.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent customer account with which this contact is associated (e.g., food manufacturer, distributor, retailer, cooperative).']
  reports_to_contact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the contact\'s manager or superior within the same customer organization. Enables organizational hierarchy mapping for enterprise B2B accounts (e.g., a buying agent reporting to a procurement director).']
  account_role varchar [note: 'The contact\'s functional role within the sales and account management process. Supports Salesforce CRM opportunity management and stakeholder mapping for complex B2B agricultural supply chain deals.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. \'active\' indicates an engaged contact; \'inactive\' indicates a former contact no longer at the organization; \'pending\' indicates awaiting verification; \'do_not_contact\' indicates opt-out or legal restriction.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact\'s functional role in the business relationship. \'primary\' is the main account contact; \'technical\' covers agronomists and food safety officers; \'billing\' covers accounts payable; \'regulatory\' covers FSMA/HACCP compliance officers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|technical|billing|regulatory|emergency — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the contact is based. Used for GDPR jurisdiction determination, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) preference alignment, and regional sales territory assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail anchor for record lineage, GDPR data retention calculations, and data governance compliance.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area of the contact within their company (e.g., Procurement, Food Safety, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Agronomy, Finance). Supports targeted communication and segmentation.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has requested to not be contacted by phone. Enforces compliance with telemarketing regulations and respects contact communication preferences. True = do not call.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact. Used for order confirmations, compliance notifications, FSMA correspondence, and marketing communications subject to GDPR consent.']
  email_bounced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact\'s email address has produced a hard bounce, rendering it undeliverable. Used to suppress invalid email addresses from marketing and transactional communications and trigger data quality remediation.']
  email_bounced_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact\'s email address first produced a hard bounce. Used to track data quality degradation over time and prioritize contact record remediation efforts.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given (first) name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communication and CRM correspondence.']
  gdpr_consent_basis varchar [note: 'The GDPR lawful basis under which this contact\'s personal data is processed. Required for all contacts associated with EU/international operations. \'consent\' requires explicit opt-in; \'contract\' applies to contacts involved in active supply agreements; \'legitimate_interest\' applies to B2B prospecting.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact holds purchasing or contractual decision-making authority at the customer organization. Used by sales teams to prioritize engagement and route proposals through Salesforce CRM opportunity management.']
  is_fsma_point_of_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated FSMA point of contact at the customer organization. FSMA Section 204 requires traceability recordkeeping contacts to be identified for food safety compliance and regulatory communications with the FDA.']
  is_haccp_coordinator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact serves as the HACCP coordinator at the customer organization. HACCP coordinators are responsible for food safety plan oversight and are key contacts for quality assurance, audit communications, and corrective action notifications.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary/main point of contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per customer account should be flagged as primary. Used for default routing of account communications and escalations.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact at their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Food Safety Officer, Agronomist, Buying Agent, Quality Assurance Director). Used for routing communications and identifying decision-making authority.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region subtag) representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., en-US, es-MX, fr-FR, pt-BR). Supports multilingual correspondence for global food manufacturer and distributor contacts.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to identify dormant contacts, measure engagement health, and trigger re-engagement workflows.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family (last) name of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for reporting and correspondence.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'The originating channel or event through which this contact was first acquired or identified. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis of lead generation programs, and sales pipeline reporting in Salesforce CRM.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'URL to the contact\'s LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and account management teams for relationship intelligence, prospecting, and partner engagement via Salesforce CRM integration.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications (newsletters, product promotions, agricultural insights). Required under GDPR for international operations and CAN-SPAM for US contacts. True = opted in; False = opted out or no consent given.']
  marketing_opt_in_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact provided explicit marketing consent. Required for GDPR audit trail demonstrating lawful basis for processing personal data for marketing purposes.']
  marketing_opt_out_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact withdrew marketing consent or requested opt-out. Triggers suppression from all marketing campaigns. Required for GDPR compliance and CAN-SPAM unsubscribe processing.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile/cell phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for SMS alerts, two-factor authentication, and field-level communications with agronomists or logistics coordinators.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for account managers and sales representatives to capture qualitative context about the contact, including relationship history, communication preferences, and strategic account notes. Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive business relationship details.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent supply chain communications, food safety alerts, and account management calls.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred channel for receiving business communications. Drives outreach strategy for sales, compliance notifications, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with GDPR consent management.']
  salesforce_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM, used for bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for sales pipeline and partner management.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal correspondence, contract documents, and regulatory communications.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact\'s location (e.g., America/Chicago, Europe/London). Used to schedule communications, meetings, and automated alerts at appropriate local times for global agricultural supply chain partners.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contact record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer pipeline, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a customer account — including procurement managers, food safety officers, agronomists, and buying agents at food manufacturers, distributors, and retail chains. Stores full name, job title, department, primary phone, email, preferred communication channel, LinkedIn profile, Salesforce CRM contact ID, FSMA point-of-contact flag, HACCP coordinator flag, and opt-in/opt-out marketing consent status per GDPR requirements for international operations.'
}

Table customer.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each address record associated with a customer account or contact. Serves as the primary key for the address entity in the customer domain Silver layer.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account this address belongs to. Links the address record to the parent customer entity in the customer domain.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual contact person this address is associated with, when the address belongs to a specific contact rather than the overall account. Nullable for account-level addresses.']
  parent_address_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on address (parent_address_id)']
  address_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this address was originally sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during master data management synchronization across Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, Oracle NetSuite, and Granular Farm Management.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the functional purpose of the address. \'billing\' is the invoicing address; \'shipping\' is the delivery destination; \'registered\' is the legal business registration address; \'farm_gate\' is a pickup point at the farm; \'cold_storage\' is a refrigerated facility address; \'mailing\' is for general correspondence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|registered|farm_gate|cold_storage|mailing|warehouse|processing_plant — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  city varchar [note: 'City, town, municipality, or locality name of the address. For rural agricultural addresses, may reference the nearest incorporated municipality.']
  cool_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code designating the country of origin for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance purposes. May differ from the physical address country for re-export or processing facility scenarios. Mandatory for USDA COOL compliance on covered commodities including beef, pork, lamb, chicken, fish, shellfish, perishable agricultural commodities, peanuts, pecans, ginseng, and macadamia nuts.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the address (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'MEX\', \'BRA\'). Used for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, international shipping documentation (BOL, AWB), and FDA/USDA import/export regulatory reporting.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or parish name of the address. Critical for USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) county-level agricultural program eligibility, crop insurance administration, and state agricultural reporting. Also used for EPA and APHIS county-level regulatory compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was first created in the system. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under FSMA, GDPR, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special delivery instructions specific to this address. Includes gate access codes, dock numbers, receiving hours, refrigeration requirements for cold chain deliveries, and farm-specific access directions not captured in structured address fields. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS and TMS delivery notes.']
  do_not_ship boolean [note: 'Indicates that shipments must not be dispatched to this address. May be set due to regulatory restrictions, customer request, failed delivery history, or compliance holds. Enforced during order fulfillment in Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet DUNS (Data Universal Numbering System) nine-digit identifier for the business entity at this address. Used for supplier qualification, credit risk assessment, USDA program eligibility verification, and federal procurement compliance.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this address record is valid and effective for business use. Supports time-bounded address management for seasonal farm gate locations, temporary cold storage facilities, and lease-based distribution points.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this address record is no longer valid for business use. Nullable for indefinitely active addresses. Used to manage seasonal farm gate closures, facility relocations, and lease expirations for distribution network addresses.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of physical facility at this address. Supports logistics planning, cold chain management, and regulatory compliance routing. \'farm\' indicates a production site; \'cold_storage\' indicates temperature-controlled facility relevant to food safety (FSMA, HACCP); \'cooperative\' indicates an agricultural cooperative location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: farm|warehouse|distribution_center|cold_storage|processing_plant|retail_store|office|cooperative|grain_elevator|packing_house — promote to reference product]']
  fips_code varchar [note: 'Five-digit FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) county code assigned by the U.S. Census Bureau. Used for USDA program administration, FSA county office routing, crop insurance mapping, and federal agricultural subsidy eligibility determination.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Precision level of the latitude/longitude geocoding result from Esri ArcGIS. \'rooftop\' indicates exact parcel-level match; \'range_interpolated\' is street-level interpolation; \'geometric_center\' is centroid of a polygon; \'approximate\' is postal code or city centroid; \'unverified\' means coordinates have not been validated.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) — a 13-digit identifier uniquely identifying this physical location in the global supply chain. Used for EDI transactions, traceability in food supply chains, GLOBALG.A.P. location registration, and GS1-compliant logistics documentation including BOL and ASN.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address record is currently active and usable for business operations. Inactive addresses are retained for historical audit and traceability but excluded from operational address selection for orders, invoices, and shipments.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary address for the customer account or contact. Only one address per customer per address type should be flagged as primary. Used to determine the default address for billing, shipping, or correspondence when multiple addresses exist.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a residential address as opposed to a commercial or agricultural business address. Affects carrier surcharge calculations, delivery scheduling windows, and B2C vs B2B routing logic for direct-to-consumer agricultural product deliveries.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), sourced from Esri ArcGIS geocoding. Supports GIS-based spatial analysis, delivery route optimization via TMS, farm gate proximity calculations, and precision agriculture field mapping. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including street number and street name. For farm gate addresses, may include rural route or farm road designation.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, unit, building, floor, dock number, or additional location detail. Commonly used for distribution center dock numbers or warehouse unit identifiers.']
  line3 varchar [note: 'Tertiary address line for additional location detail such as industrial park name, complex name, or rural delivery instructions. Used primarily for international addresses requiring additional lines.']
  loading_dock_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a loading dock is available at this address for freight deliveries. Critical for logistics planning of bulk agricultural inputs (fertilizers, seeds, pesticides) and large-volume produce shipments requiring dock-height unloading.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), sourced from Esri ArcGIS geocoding. Supports GIS-based spatial analysis, delivery route optimization, and proximity-based territory management. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP code or postal code for the address. Supports USPS ZIP+4 format for domestic addresses and international postal code formats. Used for delivery routing, sales territory assignment, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance zone mapping.']
  refrigeration_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether refrigerated or cold storage receiving capability is available at this address. Essential for cold chain compliance when delivering perishable agricultural commodities, fresh produce, dairy, and meat products per FSMA and HACCP requirements.']
  salesforce_address_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this address record in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Databricks Silver layer and Salesforce for sales pipeline management, partner relationship tracking, and customer account maintenance.']
  sap_address_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this address record in SAP S/4HANA Business Partner (BP) address management. Used for reconciliation between the enterprise ERP and the lakehouse Silver layer for billing, shipping, and procurement address data.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, territory, or administrative region of the address. Stored as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (e.g., \'US-IA\' for Iowa, \'CA-ON\' for Ontario). Critical for USDA state-level agricultural reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., \'America/Chicago\', \'America/Los_Angeles\'). Used for scheduling delivery windows, coordinating harvest pickup times, and aligning order cutoff times across multi-timezone distribution networks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this address record was last modified. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and audit compliance under GDPR and SOX.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the address was last validated against postal authority databases or geocoding services. Used to schedule re-validation cycles and assess address data freshness for logistics and compliance purposes.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the address record against postal authority databases. \'validated\' confirms the address is deliverable per USPS CASS certification or international equivalent; \'unvalidated\' has not been checked; \'invalid\' failed validation; \'pending_review\' requires manual verification. Critical for ensuring delivery accuracy for agricultural inputs and produce shipments.']

  Note: 'Physical and mailing addresses associated with customer accounts and contacts — including billing addresses, shipping/delivery locations, farm gate pickup points, cold storage facility addresses, and registered business addresses. Stores address type (billing, shipping, registered, farm gate), street lines, city, state/province, postal code, country, GIS coordinates (latitude/longitude from Esri ArcGIS), COOL country of origin code, address validation status, and primary address flag. Supports multi-location customers such as retail chains and distribution networks.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer market segment classification record in the Agriculture data platform. Primary key for the customer_segment product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agriculture customer segments are defined by commodity focus (grain buyers, produce buyers, livestock processors). Segment-based pricing tiers, campaign targeting, and agronomic service tier assignmen']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Customer segments in agriculture define required grading standards for pricing tiers and compliance (e.g., premium retail segment mandates USDA Grade A). Segment-level grading requirements drive contr']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: Agriculture customer segments are scoped to product hierarchy categories (grain segment, produce segment, specialty crops). Linking segment to product hierarchy enables segment-based pricing, complian']
  parent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on segment (parent_segment_id)']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: Customer segments in agriculture define tier-level payment terms (e.g., cooperative members get harvest deferral). Replacing denormalized payment_terms_code with a FK to payment_term enables segment-d']
  account_manager_pool varchar [note: 'Name or code of the designated account manager team or pool responsible for accounts in this segment (e.g., Enterprise Ag Sales Team, Export Markets Group, Cooperative Relations Team). Used for Salesforce CRM territory assignment and SAP SuccessFactors workforce planning.']
  agronomic_service_tier varchar [note: 'Level of agronomic advisory and technical support services allocated to accounts in this segment. PREMIUM = dedicated agronomist, field visits, VRA prescriptions; STANDARD = shared agronomist pool, periodic consultations; BASIC = self-service digital tools only; NONE = no agronomic services provided.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which customer accounts are assigned to this segment. MANUAL = assigned by account manager or sales operations; RULE_BASED = automatically assigned based on defined criteria (revenue band, geography, certification); ML_SCORED = assigned via machine learning scoring model based on behavioral and transactional data.']
  campaign_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accounts assigned to this segment are eligible for Salesforce CRM marketing campaigns and targeted outreach programs. True = eligible for campaign inclusion; False = excluded from campaign targeting (e.g., inactive or archived segments).']
  cool_labeling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) documentation and labeling compliance is required for products supplied to accounts in this segment. Mandatory for retail and export segments under USDA COOL regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this customer segment record was first created in the Agriculture data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Default credit limit in USD applied to accounts assigned to this segment. Used as the baseline credit exposure threshold in SAP S/4HANA FI credit management (FD32) and Oracle NetSuite AR. Individual account credit limits may override this segment-level default.']
  crm_segment_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this segment as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Agriculture data platform (Silver Layer) and Salesforce CRM for pipeline targeting and account management. Null if not yet synchronized.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this segment definition becomes active and available for account assignment. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical segment migration analysis. Aligns with SAP SD validity period management.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this segment definition expires and is no longer available for new account assignments. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Supports segment lifecycle management and historical migration tracking.']
  fsma_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) compliance documentation is required from customer accounts in this segment. Relevant for food manufacturer and distributor segments subject to FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule and Preventive Controls.']
  geography_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic market scope defining where this segment applies (e.g., domestic US, North America, LATAM Export, EU Export, Global). Supports Salesforce CRM regional campaign targeting and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance tracking for export segments.']
  globalg_ap_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification is a mandatory requirement for customer accounts assigned to this segment. True = certification required for onboarding and retention; False = certification not mandated.']
  gmo_preference varchar [note: 'Segment-level GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) supply preference governing product catalog eligibility and sourcing requirements. GMO_ACCEPTED = standard supply accepted; NON_GMO_REQUIRED = non-GMO verified supply mandatory; NO_PREFERENCE = no restriction applied.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) applicable to sales transactions for accounts in this segment. Governs risk transfer and freight responsibility. Particularly relevant for export distributor segments. Drives SAP SD delivery and billing document configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this segment definition was most recently reviewed and validated by the sales operations or data governance team. Used to track review cadence compliance and identify stale segment definitions requiring refresh.']
  max_segment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum ML-derived or rule-based score threshold (0.00–100.00) defining the upper bound for account assignment to this segment. Null indicates no upper bound (e.g., top-tier strategic segment). Used with min_segment_score to define the qualifying score range.']
  min_segment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum ML-derived or rule-based score threshold (0.00–100.00) required for a customer account to qualify for assignment to this segment. Used in conjunction with max_segment_score to define the score band for this segment.']
  ml_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the ML scoring model used when assignment_method is ML_SCORED. Enables reproducibility, model governance, and audit traceability for AI-driven segment assignments. Null when assignment_method is MANUAL or RULE_BASED.']
  organic_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification is required for accounts in this segment. Applies to organic produce buyers and specialty food manufacturers requiring certified organic supply.']
  pricing_tier_code varchar [note: 'SAP SD pricing condition tier code applicable to all accounts assigned to this segment (e.g., PT-GOLD, PT-SILVER, PT-STANDARD). Drives price list determination, volume discount schedules, and contract pricing in SAP S/4HANA SD module.']
  revenue_band_max_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annual revenue threshold (in USD) for a customer account to qualify for this segment. Null indicates no upper bound (e.g., for the highest-tier strategic segment). Used in conjunction with revenue_band_min_usd for rule-based segment assignment.']
  revenue_band_min_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annual revenue threshold (in USD) for a customer account to qualify for this segment. Used as a rule-based assignment criterion alongside revenue_band_max_usd. Supports SAP SD pricing tier determination and Salesforce CRM pipeline segmentation.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which segment criteria, account assignments, and scoring thresholds are reviewed and recalibrated by the sales operations or data governance team. Supports proactive segment management and CRM pipeline accuracy.']
  sap_customer_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA SD module customer group code (field: KDGRP) mapped to this segment. Drives pricing condition determination, output determination, and account assignment in SAP SD. Aligns segment definitions with SAP master data for seamless ERP integration.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the segment (e.g., LFM-001, REG-COOP, EXP-DIST). Used as the business key in Salesforce CRM campaign targeting and SAP SD pricing condition determination.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the business characteristics, target profile, and strategic purpose of this customer segment. Supports sales team onboarding and CRM campaign briefing.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the market segment (e.g., Large Food Manufacturer, Regional Cooperative, Direct Farm Consumer, Export Distributor). Used in Salesforce CRM pipeline targeting and reporting dashboards.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are available for account assignment and CRM campaign targeting. Draft segments are under review. Archived segments are retained for historical analysis only.']
  segment_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic importance tier assigned to the segment, driving service level, account manager allocation, and agronomic support intensity. STRATEGIC = top-tier enterprise accounts; PREFERRED = high-value recurring buyers; STANDARD = general commercial accounts; EMERGING = growth-stage prospects; INACTIVE = dormant segments.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the segment by commercial relationship model: B2B (business-to-business food manufacturers, distributors, wholesalers), B2C (direct farm consumers), B2G (government/institutional buyers), COOPERATIVE (agricultural cooperatives), EXPORT (international export distributors).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this segment definition originated. SALESFORCE_CRM = Salesforce CRM segment object; SAP_SD = SAP S/4HANA SD customer group; GRANULAR = Granular Farm Management financial planning; MANUAL = manually created in the data platform.']
  sqf_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SQF (Safe Quality Food) certification is a mandatory requirement for customer accounts in this segment. Applies primarily to food manufacturer and distributor segments. True = SQF certification required; False = not required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this customer segment record. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data loads, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  volume_commitment_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum annual purchase volume commitment in Metric Tons (MT) expected from accounts in this segment. Used to qualify accounts for segment-specific pricing tiers and contract terms. Null if no volume commitment is required for this segment.']

  Note: 'Market segmentation classifications applied to customer accounts for targeted sales, pricing, and agronomic support strategies. Defines segment name (e.g., Large Food Manufacturer, Regional Cooperative, Direct Farm Consumer, Export Distributor), segment criteria (revenue band, commodity focus, geography, certification tier), applicable pricing tier, assigned account manager pool, GLOBALG.A.P. or SQF certification requirement flag, segment-to-account assignment tracking (assigned segment, assignment date, expiry date, assignment method — manual, rule-based, or ML-scored — segment score, and assignment history). Used by Salesforce CRM for pipeline targeting and SAP SD for pricing condition determination. Enables historical tracking of segment migrations and supports CRM-driven campaign targeting and tiered agronomic service allocation.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service and support case record. Primary key for the case data product within the customer domain.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (food manufacturer, distributor, wholesaler, retailer, cooperative, or direct consumer) that raised this case. Links to the customer account master.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: MRL exceedance and pesticide residue cases in agriculture must reference the specific agrochemical product (active ingredient, EPA registration) for regulatory notification to EPA/USDA, corrective act']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice. Business justification: Quality complaint and FSMA traceability cases in agriculture must be tied to the originating invoice for credit memo issuance, regulatory audit, and SLA tracking. Agriculture domain experts expect eve']
  assigned_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Case management in Agriculture (food safety complaints, MRL exceedances, FSMA traceability) requires tracking which employee owns each case for SLA reporting, workload management, and regulatory audit']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Quality and food safety cases (MRL exceedances, FSMA traceability events, grade disputes) are reported and analyzed by commodity in agriculture. Regulatory notifications and corrective actions require']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific commodity lot (LOT) associated with this case, enabling traceability for quality disputes, MRL exceedance claims, and FSMA traceability requests.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer contact person who submitted or is the primary point of contact for this case.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Cases related to contract non-performance, volume shortfalls, or quality breaches in agriculture are raised against specific contracts. Contract-level case tracking and dispute resolution reporting re']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Customer cases (MRL exceedances, FSMA recalls, quality complaints) in agriculture generate direct costs — lab testing, corrective actions, regulatory notifications — that must be charged to a cost cen']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Customer service cases in agriculture frequently involve delivery issues, quality disputes at receiving facilities, and FSMA traceability events tied to specific delivery locations (grain elevators, c']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: Quality complaints, short-delivery claims, and FSMA traceability events in agriculture are raised against specific delivery orders. A direct FK from case to delivery_order enables delivery-level case ']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: BE Act disclosure violation cases and GMO mislabeling complaints reference specific GMO declarations. case already has mrl_exceedance_flag pattern; gmo_declaration_id enables tracking BE disclosure co']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Grading disputes and quality non-conformance cases in agriculture reference the specific grading standard the product failed to meet. Linking case to grading_standard enables dispute resolution tracki']
  implicated_crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: FSMA Section 204 traceability and MRL exceedance cases require identifying the specific harvest or application crew implicated in a quality or food safety incident. Linking a customer complaint case t']
  label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.label. Business justification: Labeling complaints, COOL violations, allergen mislabeling, and organic seal misuse cases in agriculture reference specific label versions. Linking case to label enables label-version-level regulatory']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Food safety cases, quality complaints, and FSMA traceability events in agriculture are tied to specific product master SKUs. Linking case to product master enables SKU-level quality reporting, recall ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: When case.mrl_exceedance_flag is set, the specific MRL threshold exceeded must be referenced for EPA/USDA regulatory notification, corrective action documentation, and FSMA traceability reporting. Agr']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: USDA NOP organic fraud complaints and organic certification suspension cases reference specific product organic certifications. Linking case to organic_certification enables regulatory notification tr']
  parent_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on case (parent_case_id)']
  product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_certification. Business justification: GFSI audit non-conformances, GlobalG.A.P. suspension cases, and SQF corrective action cases reference specific product certifications. case has gfsi_audit_relevant_flag and haccp_corrective_action_fla']
  safety_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.safety_event. Business justification: Pesticide application incidents that cause worker exposure (OSHA recordable safety_event) simultaneously generate customer MRL exceedance cases. Linking these enables joint EPA/OSHA/FSMA regulatory re']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Customer service cases in agriculture (quality complaints, delivery disputes, MRL exceedances) are raised against specific sales orders. Order-level case management, SLA tracking, and quality dispute ']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed performance complaints, germination failures, and warranty claims in agriculture are filed against specific seed varieties. Linking case to seed_variety enables variety-level quality tracking, re']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Customer quality cases in agriculture (MRL exceedances, crop damage, contamination) frequently trace back to a specific input vendor. Linking case to vendor enables Supplier Corrective Action Requests']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of product or commodity affected by the issue described in this case, expressed in the unit of measure specified in affected_quantity_uom. Used for impact assessment, credit calculation, and regulatory reporting.']
  affected_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected_quantity field. Uses standard agricultural units including Metric Ton (MT), Bushel (BU), Hundredweight (CWT), Kilogram (KG), Pound (LB), Case (CS), or Each (EA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MT|BU|CWT|KG|LB|CS|EA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading (BOL) number for the shipment referenced in this case, used for delivery issue cases and FSMA traceability requests to trace the supply chain movement of the affected commodity lot.']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the customer issue, complaint, or inquiry as reported by the customer. Captures the full context of the case including symptoms, affected products, and customer impact.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the case, used in customer communications, audit trails, and GFSI audit readiness documentation. Typically formatted as CASE- followed by a numeric sequence.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer service case, tracking progression from initial submission through resolution and closure. Supports SLA monitoring and GFSI audit readiness.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of the customer service case. Distinguishes quality disputes, delivery issues, Certificate of Analysis (COA) discrepancies, Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) exceedance claims, Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) traceability requests, complaints, and general inquiries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_dispute|delivery_issue|coa_discrepancy|mrl_exceedance|fsma_traceability|complaint|general_inquiry — promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer case was formally closed or resolved. Used to calculate total case duration and SLA compliance metrics.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with this case, used when the case involves COA discrepancies or quality disputes against a specific analytical certificate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this case record was first created in the system. Distinct from opened_timestamp which reflects the business event time.']
  customer_reference_number varchar [note: 'The customer\'s own internal reference number or complaint ID for this case, as provided by the customer (food manufacturer, distributor, or retailer). Enables cross-referencing with the customer\'s own complaint management system.']
  escalated_to varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager, specialist team, or regulatory body to whom the case has been escalated, if applicable. Relevant for HACCP corrective action escalations and MRL regulatory notifications.']
  fsma_traceability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case involves an FSMA traceability request requiring lot-level supply chain tracing per the FSMA Food Traceability Rule (Section 204). Triggers enhanced documentation and response protocols.']
  gfsi_audit_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case is relevant for inclusion in GFSI audit documentation and customer complaint trend analysis. True for cases involving food safety, quality, or regulatory compliance issues that must be reported during GFSI certification audits.']
  haccp_corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case has triggered a formal HACCP corrective action record in the quality management system. True when the case involves a food safety hazard requiring documented corrective action per HACCP protocols.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'The human-readable batch or lot number (LOT) of the commodity or product referenced in this case, as printed on packaging or shipping documents. Supports FSMA traceability and HACCP corrective action tracking.']
  mrl_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case involves a claim or confirmed finding of pesticide or chemical residue exceeding the Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) as regulated by the EPA and FDA. Triggers regulatory notification workflows.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer case was officially opened and entered into the system. This is the principal business event timestamp for SLA calculation and regulatory traceability.']
  origin_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the customer submitted the case (e.g., email, phone, web portal, EDI). Supports channel analytics and customer experience reporting.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the case, determining SLA targets and escalation thresholds. Critical cases typically involve food safety risks such as MRL exceedances or HACCP corrective actions.']
  regulatory_body_notified varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific regulatory authority notified in connection with this case when regulatory_notification_flag is True. Supports compliance audit trails and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|USDA|EPA|FSIS|APHIS|OSHA|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_notification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory authority (FDA, USDA, EPA, FSIS, or APHIS) has been notified about this case. Required for food safety incidents, MRL exceedances, and FSMA mandatory reporting events.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action taken and resolution provided to the customer, including any product replacements, credits, process changes, or regulatory notifications made.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of how the case was resolved (e.g., product replacement, credit memo issued, corrective action implemented, regulatory escalation). Supports resolution trend analytics and GFSI audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: replacement|credit_memo|refund|corrective_action|information_provided|no_action|escalated_to_regulator — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorized root cause of the customer issue as determined during case investigation. Supports HACCP corrective action tracking, trend analysis, and GFSI audit readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: packaging_defect|contamination|mrl_exceedance|labeling_error|transport_damage|storage_failure|documentation_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing detailed explanation of the identified root cause, including contributing factors, investigation findings, and supporting evidence. Complements the root_cause_category with granular context.']
  salesforce_case_code varchar [note: 'The native Salesforce CRM case record identifier (15 or 18 character Salesforce ID) for this case. Enables bidirectional integration between the lakehouse silver layer and Salesforce CRM for sales pipeline and partner management.']
  sap_qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA Quality Management (QM) module notification number linked to this case when a formal quality notification has been raised. Supports cross-system traceability between customer-facing cases and internal QM processes.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'The actual number of hours taken to resolve this case from opening to closure. Compared against sla_target_hours to determine SLA compliance and populate the sla_breach_flag.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this case breached the agreed Service Level Agreement (SLA) response or resolution time. True when the case was not resolved within the contracted SLA window. Supports KPI reporting and customer satisfaction management.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted SLA target resolution time in hours for this case, determined by case priority and customer account tier. Used to calculate SLA compliance and breach status.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief, human-readable title or subject line summarizing the nature of the customer case, used for quick identification in case lists and dashboards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this case record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Customer service and support case records capturing complaints, quality disputes, delivery issues, COA (Certificate of Analysis) discrepancies, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) exceedance claims, FSMA traceability requests, and general inquiries raised by food manufacturers, distributors, and retail customers. Stores case number, case type, priority, status, associated account and contact, commodity lot (LOT) reference, COA reference, root cause category, resolution description, SLA breach flag, and Salesforce CRM case ID. Supports HACCP corrective action tracking and GFSI audit readiness. Owned by the customer domain as the SSOT for customer-facing service interactions — distinct from the quality domain\'s non-conformance records which track internal production quality events, and distinct from the sales domain\'s order dispute records which track commercial transaction disputes.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Agriculture lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account this credit profile governs. Links to the customer.account master record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Credit profiles in Agriculture are managed per legal entity for consolidated credit risk reporting and SAP credit management (credit control area maps to company code). The sap_credit_account_number i']
  credit_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: A credit profile should reference the customer-side contact responsible for credit matters (e.g., the accounts payable manager or finance director at the customer organization). This is a standard CRM']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal credit analyst or credit manager responsible for managing and reviewing this customer credit profile. Links to the workforce/employee master. Supports accountability and workload management reporting.']
  parent_credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on credit_profile (parent_credit_profile_id)']
  reconciliation_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: In SAP AR subledger design, each customer credit profile is linked to a reconciliation GL account (AR trade receivables). Agriculture finance teams require this link for AR aging reports, period-close']
  available_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining credit capacity available to the customer, calculated as credit_limit minus outstanding_balance minus any open sales order exposure. Stored as a snapshot value refreshed by the AR integration pipeline. Negative values indicate the customer has exceeded their credit limit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Audit trail field for data governance and SOX compliance.']
  credit_analyst_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the credit analyst documenting the rationale for credit decisions, observations from customer financial reviews, special conditions, or escalation context. Supports audit trail and credit committee review processes.']
  credit_hold_date date [note: 'Date on which the credit hold was placed on the customer account. Populated when credit_hold_flag is True. Used to track hold duration and escalation timelines.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is currently placed on credit hold, blocking new sales order processing and shipment release. When True, all outbound order fulfillment is suspended pending credit resolution. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA credit block indicator (VBAK-CMGST / KNKK-CTLPC).']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the customer account is on credit hold. Populated when credit_hold_flag is True. \'compliance_hold\' covers FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., or regulatory non-compliance scenarios that trigger a commercial hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overdue_balance|credit_limit_exceeded|payment_default|legal_dispute|manual_hold|compliance_hold — promote to reference product]']
  credit_hold_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent credit hold was released and normal trading resumed. Null if the account is currently on hold or has never been held. Used for hold duration analysis and collections performance reporting.']
  credit_insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is covered by a trade credit insurance policy, which mitigates the company\'s exposure to non-payment risk. When True, the insured credit limit and policy details should be referenced in the credit analyst notes.']
  credit_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer account. Populated only when credit_insurance_flag is True. Used for claims processing and insurer reporting.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved trade credit exposure extended to the customer account, expressed in the account base currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI credit segment (FD32 / KNKK-KLIMK). Governs order release and credit hold logic.']
  credit_limit_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current credit limit became effective. Enables historical tracking of credit limit changes and supports audit trail requirements for credit management governance.']
  credit_limit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current approved credit limit expires and requires renewal or re-approval. Null indicates an open-ended credit limit with no scheduled expiry. Used to trigger credit renewal workflows.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or next-due date for the periodic credit review of this customer account. Credit analysts use this date to trigger reassessment of credit limits, ratings, and payment terms. Typically set annually or triggered by payment events.']
  credit_risk_category varchar [note: 'Aggregated credit risk classification for the customer account, derived from internal rating, DSO, payment history, and external rating inputs. Used for portfolio-level credit risk reporting, provisioning under IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss, and prioritization of credit analyst workload.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the credit limit, outstanding balance, and available credit are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Aligns with the customer account base currency in SAP S/4HANA.']
  dispute_count_open varchar [note: 'Number of currently open invoice disputes raised by or against the customer. Elevated dispute counts may indicate billing issues, quality disputes (e.g., COA failures, MRL exceedances), or credit risk signals. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR dispute management or Oracle NetSuite AR.']
  dso_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Days Sales Outstanding — the average number of days the customer takes to pay invoices, calculated over the trailing measurement period. A key accounts receivable efficiency and credit risk KPI. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR aging analysis or Oracle NetSuite AR reports. High DSO values may trigger credit review.']
  dso_measurement_period_days varchar [note: 'The rolling lookback period in calendar days over which the DSO metric was calculated (e.g., 90, 180, 365). Provides context for interpreting the dso_days value and ensures consistent comparison across customers.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this credit profile record became the active governing credit profile for the customer account. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical credit profile versioning in the lakehouse.']
  external_credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating or score sourced from an external credit bureau or agency such as Dun & Bradstreet (D&B PAYDEX score), Experian Business, or Equifax Commercial. Stored as a free-form string to accommodate varying agency rating scales. Supplements the internal rating for credit limit decisions and risk classification.']
  external_rating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party credit bureau or rating agency that provided the external_credit_rating. Enables traceability and comparison across multiple agency sources when multiple ratings are maintained. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Dun & Bradstreet|Experian|Equifax|Moody\'s|S&P|Fitch|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  internal_credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internally assigned credit risk rating for the customer, reflecting the company\'s own assessment of payment reliability and financial health. Rating scale: A1 (lowest risk) through D (highest risk / default). Assigned by the credit analyst and reviewed periodically. Used for credit limit setting and risk-based pricing decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A1|A2|A3|B1|B2|B3|C1|C2|D — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the most recent payment received from the customer, in the account base currency. Provides context alongside last_payment_date for assessing payment behavior and partial payment patterns.']
