  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received from the customer against an outstanding invoice or account balance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR clearing records or Oracle NetSuite AR payment history. Used in DSO calculation and payment behavior analysis.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed for this customer account. Used to track review frequency compliance and to calculate time elapsed since last assessment.']
  netsuite_customer_internal_code varchar [note: 'The internal customer ID as maintained in Oracle NetSuite AR, used for cross-system reconciliation of credit exposure data between SAP S/4HANA and NetSuite for customers managed in both systems.']
  open_sales_order_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of open, unshipped, or unbilled sales orders currently outstanding for the customer. Included in credit exposure calculations alongside the AR balance to determine true credit utilization. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD open order value (VBAK/VBAP).']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total open accounts receivable balance for the customer, including all unpaid invoices and debit memos. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR open item management or Oracle NetSuite AR. Used to calculate available credit and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of invoices that have passed their due date and remain unpaid, in the account base currency. A key indicator of collection risk. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR aging buckets or Oracle NetSuite AR aging report.']
  overdue_days_bucket varchar [note: 'Aging bucket classification for the customer\'s overdue balance, aligned with standard AR aging analysis periods: current (not yet due), 1–30 days, 31–60 days, 61–90 days, 91–120 days, over 120 days. Drives collections prioritization and IFRS 9 provisioning stage assignment.']
  payment_behavior_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the customer\'s historical payment behavior, derived from on-time payment rate, average days late, and dispute frequency. Higher scores indicate better payment discipline. Used to calibrate internal credit ratings and credit limit decisions.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code assigned to this customer credit profile, governing invoice due dates and early payment discount eligibility (e.g., NET30, NET60, NET90, COD, CIA, 2/10NET30). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration (T052). Drives accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit profile. \'active\' indicates normal trading; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary hold; \'under_review\' indicates a scheduled or triggered credit review is in progress; \'pending_approval\' indicates a new or revised profile awaiting credit committee sign-off; \'closed\' indicates the profile has been deactivated.']
  sap_credit_account_number varchar [note: 'The customer credit account number as maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI/AR credit management (KNKK table / FD32 transaction). Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and SAP.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this credit profile record was superseded, closed, or terminated. Null for the currently active profile. Enables slowly changing dimension (SCD Type 2) history management in the silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this credit profile record in the lakehouse silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Credit and financial risk profile for each customer account governing trade credit terms, credit limits, and payment risk classification. Stores credit limit (in base currency), current outstanding balance, available credit, credit rating (internal and external — e.g., Dun & Bradstreet), payment terms (Net 30, Net 60, COD), credit hold flag, credit review date, last payment date, days sales outstanding (DSO), and credit analyst notes. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA FI/AR and Oracle NetSuite AR for real-time credit exposure management.'
}

Table customer.certification_record {
  certification_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each customer certification record in the Agriculture data lakehouse. Primary key for the certification_record product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that holds this certification. Links the certification record to the account master in the customer domain.']
  certifying_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: In agriculture, third-party certification audits (GlobalG.A.P., organic, FSMA) are conducted by accredited certifying bodies registered as vendors. Linking certification_record to the certifying vendo']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Customer certifications (GlobalG.A.P. buyer, organic buyer, FSMA facility) are scoped to specific commodities. Supplier qualification and order fulfillment processes verify that buyer certifications c']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Food safety and agricultural certifications (GlobalG.A.P., SQF, FSMA, organic) require a designated responsible contact — typically an FSMA point of contact, HACCP coordinator, or quality manager. The']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Certification audits (GlobalG.A.P., organic, FSMA, SQF) incur audit fees, corrective action costs, and internal review labor that must be tracked against a cost center for agricultural compliance cost']
  delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.delivery_location. Business justification: Certifications in agriculture are site-specific — a GlobalG.A.P., SQF, or FSMA certification applies to a specific facility or delivery location (grain elevator, cold storage, processing plant). The c']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Customer facility certifications (packing houses, grading stations) are issued against specific USDA or industry grading standards. Linking certification_record to grading_standard enables tracking wh']
  internal_reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Internal review of buyer certification records (GlobalG.A.P., organic, FSMA) in Agriculture requires a traceable link to the reviewing employee for audit trails, regulatory compliance documentation, a']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Organic handler certifications and food safety certifications in agriculture are scoped to specific product masters (SKUs). Linking certification_record to product master enables product-level certifi']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Customer certifications that include MRL compliance verification (GlobalG.A.P., FSMA verified supplier) must reference the specific MRL threshold verified for audit trail, regulatory compliance report']
  parent_certification_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on certification_record (parent_certification_record_id)']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent certification audit or inspection was conducted by the certifying body. Supports audit scheduling, compliance monitoring, and GFSI-aligned supplier qualification reviews.']
  audit_grade varchar [note: 'Grade or rating assigned by the certifying body following the most recent audit (e.g., A, B, C for SQF; Excellent, Good, Acceptable for BRC). Determines preferential pricing tiers and GFSI supply chain qualification levels.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved during the most recent certification audit, expressed as a percentage or points total per the certifying body\'s scoring methodology. Supports quantitative supplier performance benchmarking.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit that resulted in this certification record (initial certification, surveillance, renewal, unannounced inspection, or follow-up). Supports compliance tracking and audit history analysis.']
  certificate_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference path or URI to the scanned certificate document stored in the enterprise document management system or data lake. Enables direct access to the original certificate for audit, trade, and compliance verification purposes.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally issued unique certificate number assigned by the certifying body. Used for trade eligibility verification, COOL compliance checks, and GFSI supply chain qualification of buyers.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Textual description of the products, processes, sites, or activities covered by the certification (e.g., \'Fresh fruit and vegetable packing and storage\', \'Poultry processing and cold chain\'). Critical for MRL verification and COOL compliance scoping.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification record. Drives trade eligibility decisions, MRL verification workflows, and GFSI-aligned supply chain qualification gates. Active certifications are required for preferential pricing and COOL compliance.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the food safety, sustainability, or agricultural certification held by the customer account. Determines trade eligibility and preferential pricing applicability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GLOBALG.A.P.|SQF|BRC|IFS|USDA_ORGANIC_NOP|FSMA|ISO_22000|ISCC|FAIR_TRADE_USA|HACCP|ISO_14001|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  certified_site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the certified site\'s location. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, import/export eligibility, and MRL jurisdiction determination.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document path for the Certificate of Analysis (COA) associated with this certification record. Links product quality test results to the certification for GFSI and MRL compliance verification.']
  cool_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record satisfies COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) requirements for the certified commodity and site. True = COOL eligible; False = not eligible. Drives COOL compliance reporting and trade documentation.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the certified entity must submit evidence of corrective actions for non-conformances identified during the audit. Supports compliance monitoring and certification reinstatement workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the Agriculture data lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification expires and must be renewed to maintain trade eligibility. Used to trigger renewal workflows, preferential pricing reviews, and GFSI supply chain disqualification alerts.']
  fsma_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA facility registration number issued under the Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA). Required for FSMA-registered food facilities and used to verify buyer compliance with FDA import and supply chain requirements.']
  globalg_ap_option varchar [note: 'GLOBALG.A.P. certification option applicable to this record: Option 1 (individual producer), Option 2 (producer group), Group, or Multisite. Relevant only for GLOBALG.A.P. certification type; null for other certification types.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) status declared within the scope of this certification. Supports buyer GMO preference matching and COOL/labeling compliance for food manufacturers and retailers.']
  internal_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the internal quality or compliance team completed their review of this certification record. Part of the internal audit trail for GFSI, FSMA, and SOX compliance.']
  internal_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification record has been reviewed and approved by the internal quality or compliance team. True = internally reviewed and approved; False = pending internal review. Supports internal QA workflow management.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certifying body formally issued the certificate. Marks the start of the certification\'s validity period and is used for trade eligibility determination and COOL compliance verification.']
  major_nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Major non-conformances may result in certification suspension and are a critical input to buyer risk management and GFSI qualification decisions.']
  mrl_compliance_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s certification scope has been verified as compliant with applicable Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for the relevant commodity and destination market. True = MRL compliance confirmed; False = not yet verified or non-compliant.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next certification audit or surveillance visit. Used for proactive compliance management and supply chain qualification planning.']
  nonconformance_count varchar [note: 'Total number of non-conformances (major and minor) identified during the most recent certification audit. Used for supplier risk scoring and GFSI-aligned supply chain qualification assessments.']
  organic_operation_type varchar [note: 'Type of organic operation certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP): producer, handler, processor, retailer, or broker. Applicable only for USDA Organic NOP certification type.']
  preferential_pricing_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification qualifies the customer account for preferential pricing tiers in the sales and distribution process. True = eligible for preferential pricing; False = standard pricing applies. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD pricing conditions.']
  salesforce_cert_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the corresponding certification record in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Agriculture data lakehouse and Salesforce for sales pipeline and partner management workflows.']
  sap_vendor_cert_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA QM module vendor certification code associated with this certification record. Used for quality management integration, goods receipt inspection, and procurement qualification in SAP.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Textual description of the reason for certification suspension or revocation, as communicated by the certifying body. Populated only when certification_status is \'suspended\', \'withdrawn\', or \'revoked\'. Supports corrective action tracking.']
  trade_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is currently eligible to trade based on the active status of this certification. True = trade eligible; False = trade blocked pending certification renewal or remediation. Used in order management and supply chain qualification gates.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was last modified in the Agriculture data lakehouse. Supports change data capture, audit trail, and Silver layer delta processing.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the validity of this certification: third-party audit, self-declaration, document review, or regulatory inspection. Supports risk-based supplier qualification and GFSI compliance tiering.']

  Note: 'Tracks food safety, sustainability, and agricultural certifications held by customer accounts that are required for trade eligibility or preferential pricing. Captures certification type (GLOBALG.A.P., SQF, BRC, IFS, USDA Organic NOP, FSMA compliance, ISO 22000, ISCC, Fair Trade USA, HACCP), certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, scope of certification, audit grade, and certificate document reference. Critical for COOL compliance, MRL verification, and GFSI-aligned supply chain qualification of buyers.'
}

Table customer.account_relationship {
  account_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account-to-account relationship record in the customer domain. Primary key for the account_relationship data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Business relationships between agriculture accounts (broker-buyer, co-packer, cooperative member) are scoped to specific commodities. Volume commitment tracking, exclusivity agreements, and toll proce']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Inter-account relationships in agriculture (broker-buyer, co-packer, cooperative membership) are formalized through sales contracts. The existing plain-text \'contract_reference_number\' is denormalized']
  primary_account_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee or account manager responsible for managing and maintaining this account relationship. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors workforce records and aligned with Salesforce CRM owner assignments.']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed licensing and dealer agreements between breeders and distributors are tied to specific seed varieties. account_relationship has broker_license_number and contract_reference_number; linking to see']
  source_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating or initiating account party in the relationship (e.g., the cooperative, buying group, or broker initiating the affiliation). Represents side A of the lateral relationship.']
  target_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving or counterpart account party in the relationship (e.g., the grain elevator, food manufacturer, or retailer on the receiving end). Represents side B of the lateral relationship.']
  annual_volume_commitment_mt decimal(18,3) [note: 'Contractually committed annual commodity volume in Metric Tons (MT) that the source account agrees to supply or the target account agrees to purchase within this relationship. Supports WASDE-aligned supply planning and cooperative grain pool management.']
  broker_license_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory license number of the agricultural commodity broker when relationship_type is broker_buyer. Required for compliance with USDA Packers and Stockyards Act and grain broker licensing regulations.']
  buying_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the buying group or purchasing alliance to which the target account belongs when relationship_type is buying_group. Supports volume-based pricing and consolidated procurement negotiations in SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  co_packer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target account acts as a co-packer (True) for the source account, manufacturing or packaging agricultural products under the source accounts brand or specifications. Supports FSMA supplier verification and GFSI audit tracking.']
  contractual_basis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account relationship is underpinned by a formal written contract (True) or is an informal/operational arrangement (False). Supports legal and compliance review processes under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  cool_compliance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance documentation is required for commodity transactions within this relationship. Applicable to covered commodities under USDA AMS COOL regulations.']
  cooperative_membership_number varchar [note: 'Formal membership identifier assigned by the agricultural cooperative to the member account. Applicable when relationship_type is cooperative_member. Used for grain marketing pool allocations and patronage dividend calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account relationship record was first created in the system. Provides full audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance reporting.']
  credit_exposure_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate credit exposure permitted across both accounts within this relationship, expressed in the base currency. Used for combined credit risk management in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Oracle NetSuite AR modules.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial terms within this relationship (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Governs credit exposure limit and any contractual monetary thresholds.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the account relationship becomes or became legally and operationally binding. Used for time-bounded network analysis and contract compliance tracking.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship carries an exclusivity clause (True), preventing either party from entering into equivalent relationships with competing accounts within the defined commodity and geographic scope.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the account relationship expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null indicates an open-ended relationship with no defined end. Supports proactive renewal management in Salesforce CRM.']
  fsma_verified_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target account has been verified as a qualified supplier under FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) or Preventive Controls supplier verification requirements applicable to this relationship.']
  geographic_scope_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or region code defining the geographic territory within which this account relationship is operative. Supports COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance and territorial sales analysis.']
  globalg_ap_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target account holds a valid GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification relevant to this relationship. Supports buyer certification requirements and supply chain compliance audits.']
  gmo_preference varchar [note: 'GMO handling preference governing commodity transactions within this relationship. Drives identity preservation protocols, segregation requirements, and labeling compliance under USDA BE Disclosure Standard.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the account relationship was most recently formally reviewed by the relationship manager or account team. Supports periodic relationship health checks and CRM activity tracking in Salesforce.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal review of this account relationship. Enables proactive relationship management workflows and CRM task generation in Salesforce for the assigned relationship manager.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship involves certified organic commodity transactions (True), requiring both parties to maintain USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification. Drives organic supply chain segregation and audit trail requirements.']
  preferred_partner_flag boolean [note: 'Designates the target account as a preferred or strategic partner (True) for the source account. Influences pricing tiers, allocation priority during commodity shortages, and CRM opportunity scoring in Salesforce.']
  pricing_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of pricing mechanism governing commodity transactions within this relationship. Fixed pricing locks a rate; basis pricing ties to a futures market spread; spot uses current market price; formula uses a defined calculation; negotiated is case-by-case.']
  relationship_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the relationship is directional (source acts upon target only) or mutual (both parties share equal standing). Critical for cooperative membership networks where obligations may flow one way.']
  relationship_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing qualitative context, special terms, operational nuances, or historical background relevant to this account relationship. Used by relationship managers for CRM narrative and account planning in Salesforce.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account relationship. Drives CRM pipeline visibility and supply chain partner eligibility checks in Salesforce and SAP S/4HANA SD.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the lateral relationship between the two account parties. Drives network analysis for agricultural commodity trading and cooperative grain marketing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cooperative_member|buying_group|co_packer|toll_processor|broker_buyer|distributor_retailer|preferred_supplier — promote to reference product]']
  salesforce_relationship_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this account relationship record as stored in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for sales pipeline and partner management.']
  toll_processing_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this relationship involves a toll processing arrangement (True), where one party processes agricultural commodities owned by the other party for a fee. Relevant for grain milling, oilseed crushing, and feed manufacturing partnerships.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this account relationship record. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit trail requirements in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the annual_volume_commitment field. Standard agricultural units include Metric Ton (MT), Bushel (BU), Hundredweight (CWT), Pound (LB), and Kilogram (KG). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM unit of measure configuration.']

  Note: 'Captures lateral and cross-account relationships between customer entities beyond parent-child hierarchy — including cooperative membership networks, buying group affiliations, co-packer relationships, toll processing partnerships, and broker-buyer relationships. Stores relationship type, both account parties, relationship direction, effective and expiry dates, contractual basis flag, and relationship manager. Enables network-level analysis of customer interdependencies critical for agricultural commodity trading and cooperative grain marketing.'
}

Table customer.delivery_location {
  delivery_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each authorized delivery and receiving location registered to a customer account. Primary key for the delivery_location entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that owns or is associated with this delivery location. Links the receiving point to the customer master record.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: delivery_location has fully embedded address fields (address_line1, address_line2, city, state_province, postal_code, country_code) that duplicate the normalized address table. The address table is th']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In Agriculture commodity sales, ship-to delivery locations (sap_ship_to_party_number) are assigned to company codes for sales tax jurisdiction determination, revenue recognition by legal entity, and C']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Agricultural delivery locations (grain elevators, cold storage, processing facilities) incur operating costs — receiving labor, refrigeration, dock management — tracked by cost center for logistics co']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: USDA-approved grading facilities and packing houses are certified to specific grading standards. Linking delivery_location to grading_standard enables routing decisions (only ship to locations certifi']
  parent_delivery_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on delivery_location (parent_delivery_location_id)']
  receiving_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Each delivery location in agriculture (grain elevator, cold storage, processing facility) has a designated receiving contact responsible for coordinating deliveries, signing bills of lading, and manag']
  site_coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Agriculture supply chain operations assign a responsible employee as site coordinator for each customer delivery location to manage FSMA receiving compliance, temperature-controlled receiving, and USD']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Delivery locations (grain elevators, processing plants, distribution centers) in agriculture are assigned to sales territories for coverage planning, logistics routing, and territory-level delivery vo']
  appointment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a scheduled delivery appointment is required before a carrier can deliver to this location. When true, the TMS must generate an appointment request prior to dispatch.']
  bol_delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text delivery instructions printed on the Bill of Lading (BOL) for this location, such as gate access codes, dock assignment rules, required check-in procedures, or special unloading requirements. Critical for carrier compliance at receiving.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery location record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.']
  dock_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading and unloading dock doors available at this receiving location. Used for capacity planning, appointment scheduling, and throughput analysis in the WMS.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this delivery location is authorized and active for use in shipment routing and order fulfillment. Locations cannot be used for deliveries prior to this date.']
  fsma_facility_registration_number varchar [note: 'FDA-issued facility registration number under the Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) for food facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food for human or animal consumption. Required for FSMA traceability compliance and Prior Notice submissions.']
  hazmat_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery location is authorized and equipped to receive hazardous materials such as agricultural chemicals, pesticides, or fertilizers regulated under EPA and DOT standards.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Captured via GPS or GIS geocoding for precision routing, geofencing, and spatial analytics in Esri ArcGIS.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric business identifier for the delivery location used in ERP, WMS, and TMS systems for routing and order fulfillment. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA SD ship-to party code.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the delivery and receiving location, such as \'Chicago Cold Storage Dock 3\', \'Midwest Grain Elevator — Receiving\', or \'Plant A Food Manufacturing Dock\'. Used in shipping documents and BOL instructions.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the delivery location. Inactive or suspended locations must not be used for new shipment routing. Pending approval locations are awaiting compliance verification.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the receiving facility. Determines applicable compliance requirements, commodity acceptance rules, and logistics handling procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain_elevator|cold_storage|food_manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|retail_hub|cooperative_facility|farm_direct — promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used alongside latitude for GIS-based route optimization, proximity analysis, and carrier dispatch.']
  max_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that this location can maintain for cold chain storage. Used alongside min_temperature_c to define the acceptable temperature range for perishable commodity receiving.']
  max_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum gross vehicle weight in kilograms that the facility\'s access roads, docks, or receiving infrastructure can accommodate. Used by TMS to validate carrier vehicle selection and prevent infrastructure damage.']
  min_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that this location can maintain for cold chain storage. Applicable only when temperature_controlled_flag is true. Used to validate commodity compatibility for perishable shipments.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this receiving location holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification, enabling it to receive and handle certified organic commodities without compromising organic integrity.']
  receiving_days varchar [note: 'Days of the week on which this location accepts deliveries, expressed as a comma-separated list (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI\'). Used for carrier scheduling and delivery appointment planning.']
  receiving_hours_close varchar [note: 'Scheduled closing time for receiving operations at this location in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Deliveries arriving after this time may be refused or require special arrangement.']
  receiving_hours_open varchar [note: 'Scheduled opening time for receiving operations at this location in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used by TMS and carrier scheduling to ensure deliveries are dispatched within the facility\'s operating window.']
  salesforce_location_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM record ID for this delivery location as stored in the Salesforce account or contact hierarchy. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the CRM sales pipeline and the operational delivery location master.']
  sap_ship_to_party_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA SD module ship-to party number corresponding to this delivery location. Used for ERP order-to-cash integration, delivery document creation, and outbound shipment processing.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery location has temperature-controlled receiving and storage capability for perishable commodities such as fresh produce, dairy, or meat. Required for cold chain compliance and HACCP documentation.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this delivery location is decommissioned or no longer authorized for use. Null indicates the location is open-ended and currently active. Used to enforce routing validity in TMS and WMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery location record. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  usda_establishment_number varchar [note: 'USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) establishment number assigned to meat, poultry, or egg product processing facilities. Required for FSIS-regulated shipments and must appear on shipping labels and BOL documents.']

  Note: 'Authorized delivery and receiving locations registered for customer accounts — including grain elevator receiving points, cold storage facilities, food manufacturing plant docks, distribution center addresses, and retail distribution hubs. Captures location name, location type, physical address, GIS coordinates, receiving hours, dock capacity, commodity types accepted, temperature control capability (for perishables), FSMA facility registration number, USDA establishment number (for meat/poultry), preferred carrier, and BOL delivery instructions. Distinct from the address entity — this is an operational receiving point with logistics and compliance attributes.'
}

Table product.agrochemical_product {
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each agrochemical product record in the master catalog. Primary key for the agrochemical_product data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_master. Business justification: agrochemical_product is a specialized subtype of the product master catalog. Every registered agrochemical (pesticide, herbicide, fungicide) must have a corresponding master product record for SKU/UPC']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: agrochemical_product.mrl_threshold_ppm is a denormalized copy of the authoritative MRL value stored in mrl_threshold. Adding mrl_threshold_id FK normalizes this reference, ensuring MRL values are main']
  active_ingredient_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'The concentration of the active ingredient (AI) expressed as a percentage by weight (% w/w) or grams per liter (g/L) as stated on the EPA-registered label. Used to calculate application rates and verify MRL compliance.']
  active_ingredient_name varchar [note: 'The common chemical name of the primary active ingredient (AI) in the agrochemical product as defined by the EPA and IUPAC nomenclature (e.g., \'glyphosate\', \'azoxystrobin\', \'chlorpyrifos\'). Critical for MRL compliance, PHI determination, and resistance management programs.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'The EPA-label approved method(s) for applying the agrochemical product to crops or soil (e.g., foliar spray, soil incorporation, seed treatment, aerial application). Determines equipment requirements and VRA prescription compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: foliar_spray|soil_incorporation|seed_treatment|aerial_application|drip_irrigation|broadcast|banding|fumigation|chemigation — promote to reference product]']
  application_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maximum application rate (e.g., oz/acre, lb/acre, L/ha). Required to correctly interpret and enforce the max_application_rate field for VRA prescriptions and compliance audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oz/acre|fl_oz/acre|lb/acre|pt/acre|qt/acre|gal/acre|kg/ha|L/ha — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the active ingredient concentration value (e.g., percent by weight, grams per liter). Ensures correct interpretation of the concentration field for application rate calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the agrochemical product record was first created in the master catalog system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial reporting controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'The United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) registration number assigned to the pesticide product under FIFRA. Format: registrant-number-product-number (e.g., \'524-308\'). Mandatory for all pesticide products sold or distributed in the US. Required for FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance documentation.']
  epa_toxicity_category varchar [note: 'The EPA acute toxicity category (I through IV) assigned to the agrochemical product based on oral, dermal, inhalation, and eye/skin irritation studies. Category I = most toxic (DANGER label); Category IV = least toxic. Drives PPE requirements, worker safety training, and medical surveillance programs.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'The physical and chemical formulation classification of the agrochemical product (e.g., emulsifiable concentrate, wettable powder, suspension concentrate, granule). Determines mixing, handling, and application equipment requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emulsifiable_concentrate|wettable_powder|suspension_concentrate|granule|soluble_liquid|dry_flowable|water_dispersible_granule|microencapsulated|ultra_low_volume — promote to reference product]']
  groundwater_advisory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EPA-registered label includes a groundwater advisory statement, signaling potential for leaching into groundwater under certain soil and climatic conditions. True = groundwater advisory present; False = no advisory. Drives BMP (Best Management Practices) and buffer zone requirements.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'The US DOT hazardous materials classification for the agrochemical product as defined in 49 CFR. Determines transportation, labeling, placarding, and emergency response requirements. Used in TMS (Transportation Management System) for shipment compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Class_1|Class_2|Class_3|Class_4|Class_5|Class_6|Class_8|Class_9|Not_Regulated — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  label_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the current version of the EPA-registered product label was approved. The label is the law under FIFRA — this date establishes the authoritative version for compliance purposes and triggers label update workflows when amendments are approved.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the agrochemical product record was most recently modified in the master catalog. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source systems (SAP S/4HANA), and regulatory audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the company that physically manufactures or formulates the agrochemical product. May differ from the EPA registrant for generic or private-label products. Used for supplier management and procurement in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  max_application_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable application rate of the agrochemical product per application event as specified on the EPA-registered label. Used in conjunction with application_rate_unit to enforce label compliance and prevent over-application.']
  max_seasonal_applications varchar [note: 'The maximum number of times the agrochemical product may be applied per growing season per crop as specified on the EPA-registered label. Enforces resistance management and prevents MRL exceedances from cumulative applications.']
  mode_of_action_code varchar [note: 'The IRAC (Insecticide Resistance Action Committee), HRAC (Herbicide Resistance Action Committee), or FRAC (Fungicide Resistance Action Committee) mode of action classification code for the active ingredient. Critical for IPM resistance management rotation planning (e.g., HRAC Group 9 for glyphosate, FRAC Group 11 for strobilurins).']
  omri_listed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agrochemical product appears on the Organic Materials Review Institute (OMRI) Products List, confirming suitability for use in certified organic operations. True = OMRI listed; False = not listed. Complements organic_approved_flag with third-party verification.']
  organic_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agrochemical product is approved for use in certified organic production systems under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = approved for organic use; False = not approved. Drives product selection for organic-certified fields and NOP compliance documentation.']
  package_size decimal(10,4) [note: 'The quantity of agrochemical product contained in the standard commercial packaging unit (e.g., 2.5, 1.0, 5.0). Used in conjunction with package_unit for procurement, inventory management, and application rate calculations in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  package_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the package size (e.g., gallon, liter, kg, lb). Combined with package_size to define the standard commercial unit for procurement, inventory, and cost tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gallon|quart|pint|liter|kg|lb|oz|fl_oz|ton — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pre_harvest_interval_days varchar [note: 'The minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of the agrochemical product and the harvest of the treated crop, as specified on the EPA-registered label. Critical for MRL compliance and food safety under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Internal SKU or material number assigned in SAP S/4HANA MM to uniquely identify the agrochemical product within the enterprise ERP system. Used for procurement, inventory, and cost tracking.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The registered commercial or trade name of the agrochemical product as it appears on the EPA-registered label and product packaging (e.g., \'Roundup PowerMAX\', \'Headline AMP Fungicide\').']
  product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agrochemical product in the master catalog. \'active\' = currently registered and available for use; \'discontinued\' = no longer manufactured or sold; \'suspended\' = EPA registration suspended pending review; \'pending_registration\' = awaiting EPA approval; \'cancelled\' = EPA registration voluntarily cancelled.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agrochemical product by its primary mode of action or functional category. Drives regulatory compliance requirements, application protocols, and PHI/REI enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pesticide|herbicide|fungicide|insecticide|fertilizer|adjuvant|plant_growth_regulator|rodenticide|nematicide|fumigant — promote to reference product]']
  registrant_name varchar [note: 'The name of the company or individual holding the EPA registration for the agrochemical product (e.g., \'Bayer CropScience\', \'Syngenta AG\', \'BASF Corporation\'). Used for regulatory correspondence, label verification, and supplier qualification.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the EPA registration for the agrochemical product expires and must be renewed. Used to trigger re-registration workflows and prevent use of products with lapsed registrations. Critical for FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance audits.']
  resistance_group varchar [note: 'The resistance management group designation (e.g., \'HRAC Group 9\', \'IRAC Group 4A\', \'FRAC Group 3\') used to plan chemical rotation programs and prevent development of resistant pest populations. Supports IPM compliance and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  restricted_entry_interval_hours varchar [note: 'The minimum number of hours that must pass after a pesticide application before workers may re-enter the treated area without personal protective equipment (PPE), as mandated by the EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Required for OSHA and EPA compliance.']
  restricted_use_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agrochemical product is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring purchase and application only by certified pesticide applicators. True = restricted use; False = general use. Drives applicator certification verification workflows.']
  sds_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the agrochemical product, compliant with OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom 2012) and GHS format. Required for worker safety, emergency response, and regulatory compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA QM document management.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'The guaranteed shelf life of the agrochemical product in months from the date of manufacture, as specified by the manufacturer. Used for inventory rotation (FIFO), expiry date tracking, and procurement planning in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'The EPA-mandated signal word appearing on the pesticide label indicating the acute toxicity category of the product: DANGER (Category I - most toxic), WARNING (Category II), CAUTION (Category III - least toxic), or EXEMPT. Drives PPE requirements and worker safety protocols.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius permitted for safe storage of the agrochemical product, as specified in the SDS and product label. Exceeding this threshold may cause product degradation, pressure buildup, or hazardous reactions. Used for WMS storage compliance.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required to maintain product integrity and prevent degradation, as specified in the SDS and product label. Used for warehouse storage condition compliance in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The United Nations (UN) four-digit identification number assigned to hazardous materials for international transport (e.g., \'UN1993\' for flammable liquids, \'UN2902\' for pesticides). Required on shipping documents, BOL, and placards for DOT and IATA compliance.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all registered agrochemical products (pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, insecticides, fertilizers) used in crop protection and nutrition programs. Captures EPA registration number, active ingredient (AI), formulation type, MRL limits by crop, PHI, REI, SDS reference, restricted use status, and approved application methods. Serves as the SSOT for agrochemical compliance data supporting FSMA, EPA, and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.'
}

Table product.commodity {
  commodity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each agricultural commodity record in the enterprise catalog. Serves as the primary key for the commodity reference table. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) threshold record for this commodity. MRL defines the maximum concentration of pesticide residues legally permitted in or on food and feed commodities per EPA and Codex Alimentarius standards.']
  allergen_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity is classified as a major food allergen under FDA regulations (e.g., wheat, soybeans, peanuts, tree nuts, milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, sesame). Triggers allergen labeling and cross-contact prevention requirements.']
  allergen_type varchar [note: 'Specific allergen classification when allergen_flag is True (e.g., \'gluten/wheat\', \'soy\', \'peanut\', \'tree nut\', \'milk\', \'egg\', \'fish\', \'shellfish\', \'sesame\'). Required for FDA-compliant allergen labeling and HACCP allergen control plans.']
  alternate_uom varchar [note: 'Secondary unit of measure used for conversion, international trade, or customer-specific reporting (e.g., MT as alternate to BU for export contracts). Supports dual-unit inventory management in SAP MM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|KG|TON|BBL|GAL|DOZ|HEAD|OTHER — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  benchmark_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Reference benchmark price per standard unit of measure for this commodity, typically derived from the nearest futures contract or USDA AMS market report. Used for budget planning, contract pricing, and financial reporting. Expressed in USD.']
  commodity_class varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the commodity by agricultural category. Drives domain-specific processing rules, storage requirements, and regulatory compliance workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|oilseed|fiber|produce|livestock|dairy|poultry|aquaculture|other — promote to reference product]']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known enterprise code uniquely identifying the commodity across operational systems (SAP MM material number, Granular Farm Management commodity code). Used for cross-system reconciliation and procurement referencing.']
  commodity_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the agricultural commodity (e.g., \'Yellow Corn No. 2\', \'Hard Red Winter Wheat\', \'Soybeans\'). Used in reporting, labeling, and trade documentation.']
  commodity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity record in the enterprise catalog. Active = currently traded/produced; Inactive = temporarily suspended; Discontinued = no longer produced or traded; Pending Approval = awaiting regulatory or internal approval for new commodity onboarding.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Sub-classification within the commodity class providing finer granularity (e.g., \'cereal grain\', \'legume\', \'root vegetable\', \'red meat\', \'whole milk\'). Supports product hierarchy analytics and USDA reporting segmentation.']
  cool_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) is mandatory for this commodity under USDA AMS regulations. True = COOL labeling required at retail (e.g., beef, pork, lamb, chicken, fish, fresh/frozen produce); False = exempt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity record was first created in the enterprise data catalog. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX financial reporting controls for master data governance.']
  crop_year varchar [note: 'The marketing crop year associated with this commodity definition (e.g., 2024 for the 2024/25 marketing year). Used for WASDE alignment, USDA program eligibility, and seasonal price benchmarking. Follows USDA marketing year conventions by commodity.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this commodity record is valid and active in the enterprise catalog. Used for temporal data management, crop year transitions, and regulatory compliance period tracking.']
  exchange_market varchar [note: 'Primary futures exchange where this commodity is traded (e.g., CBOT for grains, ICE for cotton and sugar). Determines which price feed and contract specifications apply for hedging and mark-to-market valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CBOT|CME|ICE|NYMEX|KCBT|MGE|EURONEXT|OTHER — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exchange_ticker varchar [note: 'Exchange ticker symbol for the commodity on futures markets (e.g., \'ZC\' for CBOT Corn, \'ZS\' for CBOT Soybeans, \'ZW\' for CBOT Wheat, \'CT\' for ICE Cotton). Used for market price benchmarking, hedging, and DTN/WASDE price feed integration.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this commodity record expires or is superseded in the enterprise catalog. Null indicates an open-ended active record. Used for catalog version management and regulatory compliance period end dates.']
  fsma_covered_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity is subject to FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) requirements, including the Produce Safety Rule (21 CFR Part 112) or Preventive Controls for Human Food rule. Drives food safety plan and traceability requirements.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO), is non-GMO, or holds a certified GMO-free designation. Critical for labeling compliance (USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard), export market access, and customer segregation requirements.']
  haccp_category varchar [note: 'HACCP food safety category classification for this commodity. Determines the applicable HACCP plan type and critical control point requirements for processing and handling. Aligns with GFSI, SQF, and BRC food safety management standards.']
  hs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification code used for international trade documentation, customs declarations, import/export compliance, and duty rate determination. Typically 6–10 digits per WCO standard.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this commodity record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail maintenance, and master data synchronization across SAP S/4HANA, Granular, and Climate FieldView.']
  moisture_content_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content percentage for this commodity at point of acceptance or storage. Exceeding this threshold triggers drying requirements or grade dockage. Critical for grain quality management per USDA GIPSA grading standards (e.g., corn ≤15.5%, soybeans ≤13%).']
  ndvi_baseline decimal(5,4) [note: 'Reference Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) baseline value for this commodity crop type at optimal growth stage, used for remote sensing yield estimation and crop health benchmarking in Climate FieldView and Esri ArcGIS analytics.']
  nop_category varchar [note: 'USDA National Organic Program (NOP) classification for this commodity. Certified organic = fully compliant with 7 CFR Part 205; Transitional = in 3-year transition period; Conventional = standard production; Not applicable = non-crop commodity.']
  organic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commodity is eligible for organic certification under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = eligible for organic production and labeling; False = not eligible (e.g., synthetic inputs required). Drives segregation requirements in storage and logistics.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of origin for this commodity (e.g., USA, BRA, ARG). Required for COOL compliance, import/export documentation, and country-of-origin risk assessment.']
  perishable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity is perishable and requires temperature-controlled storage or expedited handling. True = perishable (e.g., fresh produce, dairy, eggs); False = non-perishable (e.g., dry grains, cotton). Drives warehouse task prioritization in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  price_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the benchmark_price (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency operations for international commodity trading and export contracts.']
  standard_uom varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure used for trading, pricing, and inventory tracking of this commodity (e.g., BU = Bushel for grains, CWT = Hundredweight for livestock, MT = Metric Ton for export). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure and USDA reporting standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|KG|TON|BBL|GAL|DOZ|HEAD|OTHER — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this commodity. Exceeding this threshold triggers quality alerts and may result in grade downgrade or rejection per USDA grading standards.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this commodity to maintain quality and safety. Used to configure cold chain requirements in warehouse management and transportation planning.']
  test_weight_min_lbs_bu decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable test weight in pounds per bushel for grade determination of this grain commodity (e.g., US No. 2 Yellow Corn = 54 lbs/bu). Used in USDA GIPSA official grading and price dockage calculations.']
  typical_yield_per_acre decimal(10,4) [note: 'Reference average yield per acre for this commodity under standard production conditions, expressed in the standard_uom (e.g., 180 BU/acre for corn, 50 BU/acre for soybeans). Used as baseline for yield variance analysis, crop insurance, and USDA APH (Actual Production History) calculations.']
  uom_conversion_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric factor to convert from standard_uom to alternate_uom (e.g., 1 BU corn = 0.0254 MT; factor = 0.0254). Essential for export documentation, international invoicing, and cross-system inventory reconciliation.']
  usda_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Official USDA commodity code assigned by the Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) or Farm Service Agency (FSA). Required for USDA program participation, federal reporting, and commodity loan eligibility determination.']
  usda_grade_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable USDA grade standard designation for this commodity (e.g., \'US No. 1\', \'US No. 2\', \'US Extra Fancy\', \'USDA Choice\'). Determines quality thresholds, price premiums/discounts, and market eligibility. Linked to grading_standard reference product.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all agricultural commodities traded and produced by the enterprise — the raw, unprocessed agricultural outputs before they become sellable products. Includes grains (corn, wheat, soybeans, rice, barley), oilseeds (canola, sunflower), fiber crops (cotton), produce categories, and livestock commodity classes. Captures USDA commodity codes, HS (Harmonized System) tariff codes for international trade classification, commodity class, unit of measure (BU, CWT, MT), GMO status flag, organic eligibility, and commodity exchange ticker symbols (CBOT, CME). Linked to grading_standard and mrl_threshold for quality and compliance reference. Distinct from product_master: commodity defines WHAT is grown/raised; product_master defines HOW it is packaged and sold.'
}

Table product.master {
  master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for master']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: product_master represents sellable agricultural products that are often based on specific commodities (e.g., a corn product references the corn commodity). Adding commodity_id FK links the product to ']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.grading_standard']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_hierarchy. Business justification: product_master is the central product catalog and must reference product_hierarchy for multi-level classification (division → category → subcategory → product line). This FK enables hierarchical repor']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Commercial brand or trade name under which the product is marketed and sold. Relevant for value-added, processed, and seed products. Used in Salesforce CRM for customer-facing product catalog and sales pipeline management.']
  coa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis (COA) must accompany each shipment of this product. True = COA required for quality verification, regulatory compliance, or customer contract requirements. Drives quality management workflows in SAP QM module.']
  cool_country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance. Mandatory for covered commodities under USDA COOL regulations (7 CFR Part 60 and 65). Used on retail labels and customer documentation.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost value (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Ensures multi-currency financial reporting accuracy for international agricultural operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product master record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data governance and compliance with SOX financial reporting controls. Aligned with SAP MM material creation timestamp.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which this product was or is scheduled to be discontinued and removed from active use in transactions. Null for active products. Used for lifecycle management, inventory liquidation planning, and customer notification workflows.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this product record becomes active and available for use in transactions (purchasing, sales, production). Corresponds to SAP MM material creation date and marks the start of the product lifecycle in the enterprise catalog.']
  food_safety_cert varchar [note: 'Primary food safety certification standard applicable to this product (e.g., SQF - Safe Quality Food, BRC - British Retail Consortium, IFS - International Featured Standards, GLOBALG.A.P., HACCP, ISO 22000). Drives audit requirements and customer qualification criteria.']
  fsma_regulated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is subject to FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) regulations, including Preventive Controls for Human Food (21 CFR Part 117) or Produce Safety Rule (21 CFR Part 112). Drives food safety plan requirements and traceability obligations.']
  gap_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is produced under verified Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) standards. True = GAP-certified production. Required for certain retail customer contracts and export market access. Aligned with USDA GAP/GHP audit program.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Detailed GMO classification of the product per FDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard. \'conventional_gmo\' = contains bioengineered ingredients; \'non_gmo_verified\' = third-party verified non-GMO; \'bioengineered\' = must carry USDA BE disclosure; \'not_applicable\' = non-food or non-crop product.']
  gtin varchar [note: '14-digit Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) used for global supply chain identification, EDI transactions, and international trade. Encompasses UPC, EAN, and ITF-14 formats under the GS1 standard.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous materials classification code for products that are regulated as dangerous goods during transport (e.g., certain agricultural chemicals, fumigants, compressed gases). Null for non-hazardous products. Required for BOL (Bill of Lading) and shipping documentation.']
  humidity_sensitivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product requires controlled humidity storage conditions. True = humidity-sensitive product requiring specific relative humidity (RH) range in storage. Drives warehouse zone assignment and storage condition monitoring.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this product master record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance. Aligned with SAP MM last change timestamp.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the product in the enterprise catalog. Controls whether the product can be ordered, produced, or sold. Aligned with SAP MM material status (MMSTATUS) field. \'seasonal\' indicates availability limited to specific growing or harvest periods.']
  light_sensitive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product requires protection from light exposure during storage and transport. True = light-sensitive product (e.g., certain produce, seed varieties, specialty crops). Drives packaging and storage specification requirements.']
  mrl_threshold_ppm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) threshold in parts per million (PPM) for pesticide or chemical residues permitted on this product per EPA and Codex Alimentarius standards. Used in quality control, COA validation, and export compliance. Null for non-food products.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Standard net weight of one sellable unit of this product in kilograms (KG). Used for freight calculations, inventory valuation, customs declarations, and compliance with USDA and FDA labeling requirements. For bulk commodities, represents the standard unit weight (e.g., per bushel, per CWT).']
  non_gmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is verified as non-GMO (Genetically Modified Organism). True = non-GMO verified product. Relevant for labeling compliance, customer preference management, and Non-GMO Project verification programs.']
  organic_cert_number varchar [note: 'Official USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification number issued by the accredited certifying agent. Required on organic product labels and COA (Certificate of Analysis) documentation. Null for non-organic products.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = NOP-certified organic product. Drives labeling requirements, premium pricing, and supply chain segregation from conventional products.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for this product at the sellable unit level (e.g., bulk, bag, box, crate, pallet, drum). Drives warehouse storage configuration, shipping requirements, and labeling compliance. Aligned with SAP MM packaging data and Manhattan Associates WMS container types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk|bag|box|crate|pallet|drum|tote|carton|bottle|pouch — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plu_code varchar [note: '4- or 5-digit Price Look-Up (PLU) code assigned to fresh produce and bulk agricultural commodities for retail checkout identification. Managed by the International Federation for Produce Standards (IFPS).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Second-level product hierarchy classification (e.g., Grains & Oilseeds, Fresh Produce, Dairy, Poultry, Beef, Pork, Fiber Crops, Horticultural Products, Seeds, Fertilizers). Used for reporting, pricing, and supply chain segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grains_oilseeds|fresh_produce|dairy|poultry|beef|pork|fiber_crops|horticultural|seeds|fertilizers — promote to reference product]']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Named product line or portfolio grouping to which this product belongs (e.g., Organic Harvest Line, Premium Grain Series, Non-GMO Select). Supports marketing segmentation, pricing strategy, and customer account management.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The full commercial or trade name of the agricultural product as it appears on labeling, sales orders, and customer-facing documents. Aligned with SAP MM material description field.']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'Third-level product hierarchy classification providing granular segmentation within a product category (e.g., within Grains: Corn, Wheat, Soybeans, Rice; within Fresh Produce: Leafy Greens, Root Vegetables, Citrus). Supports detailed analytics and procurement planning.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the agricultural product type distinguishing between raw commodities (grains, produce, fiber), processed goods, seed/input products, livestock-derived items (meat, dairy, eggs), value-added products, and agricultural inputs (fertilizers, chemicals). Drives linkage to type-specific detail tables.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum shelf life of the product in days from the date of production or harvest under standard storage conditions. Used for inventory rotation (FEFO - First Expired First Out), expiry date calculation, and food safety compliance per FSMA requirements.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) code uniquely identifying a specific product variant for inventory tracking, order management, and warehouse operations. Corresponds to SAP MM material number used in WMS and ERP transactions.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per base unit of measure used for inventory valuation, variance analysis, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation. Set during annual cost roll-up in SAP CO module. Confidential as it reflects internal pricing strategy and margin structure.']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted storage temperature in degrees Celsius for maintaining product quality and safety. Exceeding this threshold triggers quarantine hold procedures in the WMS and quality management system.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for maintaining product quality and safety. Used by Manhattan Associates WMS for cold chain storage zone assignment and temperature monitoring compliance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure (UOM) for this product used in inventory management, purchasing, and sales transactions. Aligned with SAP MM base unit of measure (MEINS). Common agricultural units include BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), KG (Kilogram), LB (Pound). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|LB|MT|BU|CWT|EA|CS|BAG|TON|L|GAL — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier used for retail point-of-sale scanning, consumer packaging, and retail distribution. Required for products sold through retail channels.']
  usda_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Official USDA commodity classification code used for federal reporting, subsidy programs, crop insurance, and WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates) alignment. Maps to USDA Agricultural Marketing Service commodity codes.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'SAP MM valuation class code that determines the G/L (General Ledger) account assignment for inventory postings. Controls how inventory value is recorded in financial accounting (FI module) and cost accounting (CO module). Critical for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) and inventory valuation reporting.']

  Note: 'Central master catalog and SSOT for all sellable, transferable, and internally consumed agricultural products. Every product in the enterprise — whether a raw commodity packaged for sale, a processed good, a seed variety, a livestock-derived item, or an agricultural input — has exactly one product_master record. Captures SKU, PLU, UPC, GTIN codes, product hierarchy classification, brand, product line, net weight, packaging type, shelf life, storage requirements (temperature range, humidity, light sensitivity), and product lifecycle status (active, discontinued, seasonal). Distinguishes between conventional, organic (NOP-certified), and non-GMO product lines. Type-specific detail is held in linked detail tables (seed_variety, livestock_product, processed_good, input_product). Aligned with SAP S/4HANA Material Master (MM module).'
}

Table product.hierarchy {
  hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the product hierarchy. Primary key for the product_hierarchy data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: hierarchy stores grading_standard_code (STRING) as a denormalized reference to the applicable grading standard for a product classification level. The grading_standard table is the authoritative SSOT ']
  parent_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the product hierarchy tree. Null for root-level Division nodes. Enables recursive traversal for roll-up reporting across crop commodities, livestock products, and processed goods.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'Broad commodity grouping for this hierarchy node aligned with USDA and market reporting conventions. Examples: Grains & Oilseeds, Fruits & Vegetables, Dairy, Poultry & Eggs, Meat & Livestock, Fiber Crops, Specialty Crops, Crop Inputs. Used in WASDE reporting and market price benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Grains & Oilseeds|Fruits & Vegetables|Dairy|Poultry & Eggs|Meat & Livestock|Fiber Crops|Specialty Crops|Crop Inputs — promote to reference product]']
  cool_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) is mandatory for products in this hierarchy node under USDA AMS regulations. True = COOL labeling required on all products in this node; False = COOL not required. Drives labeling compliance workflows and retail packaging requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this product hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_steward varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business data steward responsible for maintaining the accuracy and completeness of this hierarchy node. Supports data governance accountability, change management workflows, and master data management (MDM) ownership tracking.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Sales Division code associated with this hierarchy node. Drives pricing, sales organization assignment, and profit center determination. Examples: CROP (Crop Commodities), LVST (Livestock Products), PROC (Processed Goods), SEED (Seed & Inputs), VADD (Value-Added Products).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this product hierarchy node is valid and active for product classification. Supports temporal hierarchy management and ensures historical accuracy when hierarchy structures change. Used in SAP S/4HANA time-dependent product hierarchy configurations.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this product hierarchy node is no longer valid for new product assignments. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active node. Supports hierarchy versioning and ensures products are not assigned to expired classification nodes.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference code mapping this hierarchy node to an external classification system such as UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code), HS (Harmonized System) tariff code, or trading partner category code. Supports EDI, customs documentation, and global trade compliance.']
  food_safety_category varchar [note: 'FDA/USDA food safety classification for products in this hierarchy node. Determines applicable food safety regulations, inspection requirements, and labeling rules. Ready-to-Eat products require stricter controls than Raw Agricultural Commodities. Non-Food and Crop Input nodes are exempt from food safety rules.']
  fsma_category varchar [note: 'FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) regulatory category applicable to products in this hierarchy node. Determines which FSMA rules govern production, handling, and supply chain requirements. Drives compliance program assignment and audit scope.']
  gfsi_scheme_applicable varchar [note: 'Primary Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI)-recognized certification scheme applicable to products in this hierarchy node. Drives supplier qualification, audit requirements, and customer contract compliance. Values: SQF (Safe Quality Food), BRC (British Retail Consortium), IFS (International Featured Standards), GLOBALG.A.P., Not Applicable.']
  gmo_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates the Genetically Modified Organism (GMO) status applicable to products in this hierarchy node. Values: GMO (contains genetically modified material), Non-GMO (no GMO material, not certified), GMO-Free Certified (third-party verified non-GMO), Not Applicable (e.g., agrochemicals). Required for COOL labeling, USDA BE (Bioengineered) disclosure, and export documentation.']
  gpc_brick_code varchar [note: 'GS1 Global Product Classification (GPC) Brick-level code for this hierarchy node. Enables standardized product categorization for retail trading partners, EDI transactions, and global supply chain interoperability. Particularly relevant for produce, dairy, and packaged goods nodes.']
  haccp_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) plans are required for products in this hierarchy node. True = HACCP plan mandatory (e.g., meat, poultry, juice, seafood); False = HACCP not required. Drives food safety program assignment and FSIS/FDA audit requirements.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this hierarchy node are classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and transportation documentation (e.g., certain agrochemicals, fertilizers).
