True = HAZMAT classification applies; False = standard handling. Drives SDS (Safety Data Sheet) requirements and DOT/EPA compliance.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy node. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA PRODH (Product Hierarchy) field used in SD pricing and MM material classification. Used for cross-system integration with SAP, Oracle NetSuite, and Granular Farm Management.']
  hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of this hierarchy node, including the scope of products it encompasses, applicable production methods, and any relevant regulatory or quality notes. Used in training materials, data catalogs, and business user documentation.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this node within the hierarchy tree. Level 1 = Division (e.g., Crop Commodities), Level 2 = Category (e.g., Grains), Level 3 = Sub-Category (e.g., Cereal Grains), Level 4 = Product Family (e.g., Corn), Level 5 = SKU/Item. Supports roll-up reporting in SAP S/4HANA SD and analytics.']
  hierarchy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of this hierarchy node (e.g., \'Grains & Oilseeds\', \'Fresh Produce\', \'Dairy Products\', \'Crop Inputs - Seed\'). Used in reporting, category management, and retail planogram structures.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this product hierarchy node. Active = in use for product classification and reporting; Inactive = temporarily disabled; Deprecated = replaced by a new node, retained for historical data; Pending Approval = awaiting business owner sign-off before activation. Controls whether new products can be assigned to this node.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this product hierarchy node record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  level_name varchar [note: 'Business label for the hierarchy depth at this node. Standardized level names: Division, Category, Sub-Category, Product Family, SKU. Enables consistent labeling in reports and dashboards without requiring numeric level interpretation.']
  mrl_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EPA/Codex Alimentarius Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for pesticide residues apply to products in this hierarchy node. True = MRL testing and compliance required; False = MRL not applicable (e.g., non-food inputs). Drives residue testing protocols and COA requirements.']
  organic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products classified under this hierarchy node are eligible for USDA National Organic Program (NOP) organic certification. True = organic product line; False = conventional or non-organic. Drives organic certification tracking, NOP compliance workflows, and organic premium pricing.']
  path varchar [note: 'Materialized full path string from root to this node using delimiter notation (e.g., \'Crop Commodities / Grains / Cereal Grains / Corn\'). Supports efficient hierarchy traversal queries in Databricks without recursive CTEs. Maintained as a denormalized convenience field.']
  phi_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) tracking is required for products in this hierarchy node. True = PHI compliance mandatory (food/feed crops subject to pesticide application); False = PHI not applicable. Drives crop protection event tracking and harvest scheduling compliance.']
  plu_category_code varchar [note: 'International Federation for Produce Standards (IFPS) Price Look-Up (PLU) category code for fresh produce hierarchy nodes. Used at retail point-of-sale for produce identification and pricing. Applicable primarily to fresh fruit, vegetable, and herb product hierarchy nodes.']
  pricing_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for pricing and invoicing products in this hierarchy node. May differ from reporting UOM (e.g., corn priced per BU but reported in MT). Drives SAP S/4HANA SD condition record pricing and Oracle NetSuite invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|KG|TON|DOZ|EA|GAL|L — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  product_line_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the product line represented by this hierarchy node. Distinguishes between Crop Commodity (grains, produce, fiber), Livestock Product (meat, dairy, eggs), Processed Good, Seed Input, Value-Added, and Agrochemical product lines. Drives business rules, compliance requirements, and reporting segmentation.']
  reporting_uom varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure used for volume and yield reporting for products in this hierarchy node. Aligned with USDA NASS and WASDE reporting conventions. BU = Bushel, CWT = Hundredweight, MT = Metric Ton, LB = Pound, KG = Kilogram, TON = Short Ton, DOZ = Dozen, EA = Each, GAL = Gallon, L = Liter. Used in SAP S/4HANA MM base unit of measure configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|KG|TON|DOZ|EA|GAL|L — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retail_category_code varchar [note: 'Internal retail category management code used for merchandising, planogram assignment, and retail buyer reporting. Aligns with PLU (Price Look-Up) category structures and retail trading partner category definitions. Used in Salesforce CRM for customer-facing category reporting.']
  sap_material_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Material Group (MATKL field) associated with this hierarchy node. Used in SAP MM for procurement categorization, account determination, and purchasing info records. Enables cross-reference between the product hierarchy and SAP material master classification.']
  sap_product_hierarchy varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA native Product Hierarchy (PRODH) code for this node, up to 18 characters. Used directly in SAP SD condition records for pricing, rebate agreements, and sales reporting. Serves as the integration key between this data product and SAP S/4HANA SD/MM modules.']
  sort_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying this hierarchy node among its siblings at the same parent level. Controls the display sequence in SAP S/4HANA product hierarchy browsers, reporting dashboards, and retail category management tools. Lower numbers appear first.']
  temperature_class varchar [note: 'Required storage and handling temperature classification for products in this hierarchy node. Drives cold chain logistics requirements, warehouse slotting in Manhattan Associates WMS, and transportation mode selection in TMS. Values: Ambient, Refrigerated, Frozen, Controlled Atmosphere.']
  usda_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Official USDA commodity classification code assigned to this hierarchy node. Aligns with USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) and National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS) commodity groupings. Required for USDA regulatory reporting, WASDE alignment, and federal program compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines the multi-level classification hierarchy for all agricultural products — from enterprise product division down through category, sub-category, product family, and individual SKU. Supports USDA commodity groupings, internal merchandising hierarchies, and retail category management structures. Enables roll-up reporting across crop commodities, livestock products, processed goods, seed/input products, and value-added lines. Used in SAP S/4HANA SD and MM modules for pricing and reporting hierarchies.'
}

Table product.seed_variety {
  seed_variety_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each seed variety record in the master catalog. Primary key for the seed_variety data product.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: seed_variety represents cultivars of specific crop species (corn, soybean, wheat) that correspond directly to commodity types in the commodity catalog. Linking seed_variety to commodity via commodity_']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: seed_variety carries three denormalized GMO attributes: aphis_petition_number (the APHIS regulatory petition number for the GMO event), gmo_status (STRING), and non_gmo_verified_flag (BOOLEAN). The gm']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_master. Business justification: seed_variety is a specialized product subtype representing cultivars sold as input products. It must reference product_master for unified SKU/UPC management, lifecycle status, and commercial catalog i']
  breeder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the seed company, research institution, or individual breeder that developed and holds intellectual property rights over this variety (e.g., \'Bayer Crop Science\', \'Corteva Agriscience\', \'Syngenta\'). Required for licensing compliance and royalty tracking.']
  catalog_year varchar [note: 'The crop year (4-digit year) for which this seed variety is listed in the commercial product catalog. Used for annual catalog management, variety retirement tracking, and historical performance analysis in Granular Farm Management and Climate FieldView.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seed variety record was first created in the master catalog system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  crop_species varchar [note: 'Botanical species name of the crop (e.g., \'Zea mays\' for corn, \'Glycine max\' for soybean, \'Triticum aestivum\' for wheat). Aligns with USDA PLANTS Database taxonomy and supports regulatory reporting to APHIS and EPA.']
  crop_type varchar [note: 'High-level agronomic classification of the crop category. Used for product hierarchy segmentation, supply chain routing, and USDA commodity code alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|oilseed|fiber|forage|vegetable|fruit|pulse|tuber|specialty|cover_crop — promote to reference product]']
  days_to_maturity varchar [note: 'Average number of calendar days from planting to physiological maturity under typical growing conditions for the primary production region. Used for crop scheduling, harvest planning, and frost risk assessment in Climate FieldView and Granular Farm Management.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which this seed variety was officially discontinued from commercial sale or production. Null if the variety is still active. Used for catalog management, inventory clearance planning, and historical crop planning records.']
  disease_resistance_profile varchar [note: 'Structured description of the variety\'s resistance or tolerance ratings to key crop diseases (e.g., \'Gray Leaf Spot: R, Northern Corn Leaf Blight: MR, Goss\'s Wilt: S\'). Ratings typically follow industry conventions: R=Resistant, MR=Moderately Resistant, MS=Moderately Susceptible, S=Susceptible. Informs crop protection planning and IPM programs.']
  drought_tolerance_rating varchar [note: 'Categorical rating of the variety\'s ability to maintain yield under water-deficit stress conditions. Used for irrigation planning, water resource management, and ET (Evapotranspiration)-based irrigation scheduling in precision agriculture systems.']
  germination_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum guaranteed germination rate percentage for this seed variety as tested under AOSA (Association of Official Seed Analysts) standard germination test conditions. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Required for Federal Seed Act label compliance and quality assurance.']
  herbicide_tolerance_traits varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of herbicide tolerance traits present in this variety (e.g., \'glyphosate, glufosinate, dicamba\'). Used for agrochemical selection, VRA prescription compatibility, and EPA resistance management compliance. Supports IPM resistance group tracking.']
  insect_resistance_traits varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of insect resistance traits (e.g., \'Bt Cry1Ab, Cry3Bb1, VIP3A\') present in this variety. Used for EPA insect resistance management (IRM) compliance, refuge planning, and IPM program design. Required for APHIS regulatory documentation on Bt crops.']
  introduction_date date [note: 'Date on which this seed variety was first commercially introduced to the market or added to the internal product catalog. Used for variety lifecycle management, performance trend analysis, and regulatory documentation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this seed variety record in the master catalog. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  licensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the seed licensor or technology provider as registered in the ERP procurement master. Used to link seed variety to licensing agreements, royalty schedules, and supplier records in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  nop_organic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this seed variety is eligible for use in USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified organic production. True = organically produced seed available and eligible; False = not eligible (e.g., treated with prohibited substances or GMO). Drives organic crop planning and NOP compliance documentation.']
  package_weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Net weight of the standard commercial seed package in kilograms. Used for freight calculation, warehouse storage planning, and logistics management in Manhattan Associates WMS and TMS (Transportation Management System).']
  plant_height_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the variety\'s typical plant height at maturity. Used for equipment calibration (combine header height), lodging risk assessment, and field operation planning in John Deere Operations Center.']
  plant_variety_protection_number varchar [note: 'USDA Plant Variety Protection Office certificate number assigned to this variety, granting intellectual property protection to the breeder. Null if the variety is not PVP-protected or is in the public domain. Required for licensing compliance and royalty management.']
  purity_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of pure seed (by weight) of the labeled variety in the seed lot, as determined by official seed analysis. Required for Federal Seed Act label compliance. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.50 = 99.5% pure seed).']
  recommended_planting_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions, states, or USDA Plant Hardiness Zones where this variety is agronomically recommended for commercial production (e.g., \'Corn Belt, Northern Plains, Zone 5-7\'). Supports geographic segmentation in Esri ArcGIS field mapping and regional sales planning.']
  relative_maturity_rating decimal(4,1) [note: 'Numeric relative maturity rating indicating the number of days or heat units required for the variety to reach physiological maturity relative to a standard check variety (e.g., 108.5 for a 108.5-day corn hybrid, 3.8 for a 3.8 maturity group soybean). Critical for crop planning, planting date optimization, and frost risk management in Climate FieldView and Trimble Ag Software.']
  seed_class varchar [note: 'Official seed certification class indicating the generation and purity level of the seed as defined by the Association of Official Seed Certifying Agencies (AOSCA). Classes: \'breeder\' (source seed from originating breeder), \'foundation\' (first generation from breeder seed), \'registered\' (second generation), \'certified\' (commercially distributed certified seed), \'commercial\' (uncertified commercial seed).']
  seed_size_category varchar [note: 'Physical size classification of the seed kernel used for planter plate selection, seeding equipment calibration, and seed unit weight calculations. Affects planter population accuracy and is required for John Deere Operations Center equipment setup.']
  seed_treatment_active_ingredient varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of active ingredients (AI) in the seed treatment formulation (e.g., \'clothianidin, metalaxyl, fludioxonil\'). Required for MRL compliance, EPA registration cross-reference, and FSMA produce safety rule documentation.']
  seed_treatment_applied varchar [note: 'Name or code of the seed treatment product(s) applied to the seed lot prior to sale or planting (e.g., \'Poncho 1250 + Acceleron\', \'Cruiser Maxx Vibrance\'). Includes fungicide, insecticide, and biological seed treatments. Required for EPA label compliance, PHI/REI tracking, and NOP organic eligibility determination.']
  seeding_rate_per_acre decimal(10,2) [note: 'Manufacturer or agronomist-recommended seeding rate expressed as seeds per acre for optimal stand establishment. Used for VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation in Trimble Ag Software and seed quantity planning in Granular Farm Management.']
  seeds_per_unit varchar [note: 'Number of seeds per standard commercial unit (e.g., seeds per 80,000-count bag for corn, seeds per 140,000-count bag for soybean). Used for inventory management, seeding rate calculations, and order quantity planning in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  trait_package varchar [note: 'Descriptive label of the bundled biotechnology or conventionally bred trait stack applied to this variety (e.g., \'RoundupReady2Xtend + Bollgard3\', \'Enlist E3\', \'LibertyLink GT27\'). Captures herbicide tolerance, insect resistance, drought tolerance, and other engineered or selected traits. Used for IPM planning, agrochemical compatibility, and regulatory reporting.']
  variety_code varchar [note: 'Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the seed variety in ERP and FMIS systems (e.g., SAP S/4HANA material master number or Trimble Ag Software variety code). Used as the business key for cross-system integration and SKU linkage.']
  variety_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or proprietary name of the seed variety as registered by the breeder or licensor (e.g., \'DeKalb DKC62-08\', \'Pioneer P1197AM\'). This is the primary human-readable identifier used in field operations, procurement, and sales.']
  variety_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the seed variety in the product catalog. \'active\' indicates commercially available; \'discontinued\' indicates no longer sold or planted; \'under_review\' indicates pending regulatory or internal evaluation; \'pending_approval\' indicates awaiting APHIS or NOP certification; \'withdrawn\' indicates removed due to regulatory or quality issues.']
  yield_potential_rating varchar [note: 'Categorical rating of the variety\'s yield potential relative to comparable varieties in the same crop class and maturity group, as determined by multi-environment trial (MET) data. Used for variety selection, agronomic planning, and YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) benchmarking in Granular Farm Management.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all seed varieties and cultivars used in crop production and sold as input products. Captures variety name, crop species, breeder/licensor, trait package (herbicide tolerance, insect resistance), GMO status, non-GMO verification status, relative maturity rating, yield potential rating, disease resistance profile, and seed treatment applied. Tracks NOP organic seed eligibility and APHIS biotech regulatory status. Sourced from Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView variety databases.'
}

Table product.organic_certification {
  organic_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each organic certification record. Primary key for the organic_certification data product.']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural product or commodity in the product catalog that is covered under this organic certification. Links the certification record to the specific sellable or transferable item for organic labeling and premium pricing eligibility.']
  allowed_materials_list_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference or identifier for the approved materials list associated with this certification. Lists all inputs (fertilizers, pesticides, processing aids) approved for use under the certified organic system plan, cross-referenced against the USDA National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances.']
  annual_renewal_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the current annual renewal status of the organic certification. Renewed: successfully renewed for the current certification year. Pending_renewal: renewal application submitted and under review. Not_renewed: operation chose not to renew. Overdue: renewal deadline passed without submission.']
  certificate_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the scanned or digital copy of the official organic certificate issued by the certifying agent. Supports audit trail, labeling compliance verification, and customer COA (Certificate of Analysis) requests.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-issued certificate number assigned by the accredited certifying agent. This is the official document reference used in regulatory filings, labeling claims, and audit trails under USDA NOP requirements.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'The operational scope covered by this organic certificate as defined by USDA NOP. Valid values: crops (field and greenhouse crop production), livestock (animal husbandry), handling_processing (post-harvest handling and processing operations), wild_crops (wild-harvested plant materials), aquatic_animals (aquatic animal production). Determines which products and operations are covered under this certificate.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organic certification. Active: certificate is valid and in good standing. Suspended: temporarily halted pending investigation or corrective action. Revoked: permanently cancelled due to violation. Pending: application submitted, awaiting certifying agent approval. Expired: past expiration date without renewal. Withdrawn: voluntarily relinquished by the operation.']
  certified_acreage decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total land area in acres covered under this organic certification. Represents the certified organic production footprint and is used for yield planning, premium pricing calculations, and regulatory reporting to USDA.']
  certifying_agent_code varchar [note: 'The official USDA-assigned accreditation code or identifier for the certifying agent. Used for regulatory cross-referencing with USDA AMS accreditation records and electronic filing systems.']
  certifying_agent_name varchar [note: 'The name of the USDA-accredited certifying agent (ACA) that issued and administers this organic certificate. Certifying agents are third-party organizations accredited by the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) to certify organic operations.']
  cool_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the organic certification record supports Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance requirements. COOL mandates disclosure of the country of origin for covered commodities at retail, and organic products must meet both NOP and COOL labeling requirements.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the certified organic product was produced or primarily grown. Required for COOL compliance and international organic equivalency agreements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this organic certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance record-keeping in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the organic certification becomes legally valid and binding. Marks the start of the certified period during which organic labeling claims and premium pricing are authorized.']
  equivalency_standard varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific international organic standard or equivalency arrangement recognized by this certification (e.g., EU Organic, Canada Organic, Japan JAS Organic, IFOAM). Populated only when international_equivalency_flag is True.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the organic certification expires and must be renewed to maintain organic status. Organic certificates are typically issued on an annual basis. Expiration triggers renewal workflow and may affect organic premium pricing eligibility.']
  fsma_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certified organic operation is also compliant with applicable Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) rules, including the Produce Safety Rule and Preventive Controls for Human Food. Organic certification does not automatically confer FSMA compliance.']
  globalg_ap_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the operation holds concurrent GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification in addition to USDA NOP organic certification. GLOBALG.A.P. certification is often required by major retail buyers and export markets.']
  gmo_free_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certified operation and its products are verified as free from Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs). USDA NOP prohibits the use of excluded methods including genetic engineering; this flag confirms GMO-free status as part of organic compliance.']
  international_equivalency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is recognized under an international organic equivalency arrangement (e.g., USDA-EU Organic Equivalency Agreement, USDA-Canada Organic Equivalency Arrangement). Enables certified organic products to be sold as organic in partner countries without additional certification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent on-site inspection conducted by the certifying agent at the certified operation. Annual on-site inspections are required under USDA NOP to verify compliance with organic standards.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this organic certification record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to certification status, renewal events, suspension/revocation actions, and document updates for audit and compliance purposes.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next on-site inspection by the certifying agent. Used for compliance planning and operational readiness to ensure the operation is prepared for the annual NOP inspection.']
  nop_system_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the Organic System Plan (OSP) submitted to and approved by the certifying agent. The OSP is the foundational compliance document describing all practices, materials, and monitoring activities for the certified operation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, conditions, or special provisions associated with this organic certification record. May include certifying agent conditions, corrective action requirements, or operational notes relevant to compliance management.']
  operation_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the certified organic operation (farm, ranch, or handling facility) as it appears on the organic certificate. This is the entity name recognized by the certifying agent and USDA NOP for compliance purposes.']
  operation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the certified organic operation type. Producer: farms and ranches growing organic crops or raising organic livestock. Handler: facilities processing or packaging organic products. Broker: entities buying and selling organic products. Importer: entities importing certified organic products. Distributor: entities distributing certified organic products.']
  organic_label_type varchar [note: 'The USDA NOP-defined organic labeling category authorized for products under this certification. 100_percent_organic: all ingredients are certified organic. Organic: at least 95% certified organic ingredients. Made_with_organic: 70-95% certified organic ingredients. Organic_ingredients: less than 70% organic, may list specific organic ingredients. Determines permissible label claims and USDA Organic Seal usage.']
  premium_pricing_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products covered by this certification are currently eligible for organic premium pricing in sales and distribution channels. Eligibility requires active certification status and valid organic label type authorization.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the annual renewal application and supporting documentation must be submitted to the certifying agent to maintain uninterrupted organic certification status.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The date on which the organic certification was permanently revoked by the certifying agent. Populated only when certification_status is \'revoked\'. Revocation is the most severe enforcement action and prohibits the operation from making organic claims.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for permanent revocation of the organic certification. Documents the specific violation or fraudulent activity that resulted in the certifying agent revoking the certificate.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the organic certification was suspended by the certifying agent. Populated only when certification_status is \'suspended\'. Suspension may result from non-compliance findings, use of prohibited substances, or failure to submit required documentation.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for suspension of the organic certification. Documents the specific non-compliance finding or violation that triggered the suspension action by the certifying agent.']
  transition_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the mandatory 36-month organic transition period has been completed for the land or operation covered by this certification. True indicates the operation has fulfilled the transition requirement and is eligible for full organic certification.']
  transition_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the operation began the mandatory 36-month transition period required before land can be certified organic under USDA NOP. During transition, no prohibited substances may be applied to the land.']

  Note: 'Tracks USDA National Organic Program (NOP) organic certification records for products, product lines, and production operations. Captures certifying agent name, certificate number, certification scope (crops, livestock, handling/processing), certification effective date, expiration date, annual renewal status, allowed materials list, and any suspension or revocation events. Links products to their certified organic status and supports COOL and organic labeling compliance. Required for organic premium pricing and retail organic claims.'
}

Table product.gmo_declaration {
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each GMO declaration record. Primary key for the gmo_declaration data product.']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural product or commodity for which this GMO declaration is recorded. Links to the product catalog master.']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: gmo_declaration.organic_certified_flag and organic_certificate_number indicate that a GMO declaration may reference an associated organic certification (non-GMO products are often also organically cer']
  aphis_petition_number varchar [note: 'APHIS petition number associated with the deregulation or regulatory approval of the specific bioengineered trait or transformation event. Used to verify US market approval status and cross-reference APHIS regulatory records.']
  be_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product requires Bioengineered (BE) food disclosure labeling under the USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS). Drives label generation and compliance workflows.']
  be_disclosure_type varchar [note: 'Method of BE food disclosure used on product labeling as required by USDA NBFDS. Options include on-package text, USDA BE symbol, electronic/digital link (QR code), or phone number. \'not_required\' when product is exempt.']
  certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the non-GMO certificate or GMO verification certificate expires and must be renewed. Drives compliance monitoring and re-certification workflows.']
  china_market_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for import or commercial use in China. Required for export documentation to Chinese buyers and compliance with MARA (Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs) biosafety certificates.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the Certificate of Analysis (COA) associated with this GMO declaration. The COA contains the full laboratory test results and is provided to customers and regulatory bodies.']
  cool_country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the product as required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance. Relevant for GMO declarations as country of origin may affect applicable GMO regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this GMO declaration record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  declaration_date date [note: 'Date on which the GMO declaration was formally submitted or issued. Represents the principal business event date for this record, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  declaration_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this GMO declaration record, used in regulatory submissions, export documentation, and audit trails. Follows internal numbering convention (e.g., GMO-2024-00123).']
  declaration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GMO declaration record. Tracks the workflow from initial draft through regulatory review to approval, expiry, or withdrawal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|rejected|expired|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  declared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or organization responsible for submitting this GMO declaration. Used for accountability, audit trail, and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  donor_organism varchar [note: 'Scientific name of the organism from which the introduced genetic material was derived (e.g., \'Agrobacterium tumefaciens\', \'Bacillus thuringiensis\'). Required for regulatory submissions and export documentation.']
  eu_market_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for commercial use or import in the European Union. Critical for export compliance and trade documentation.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Declared GMO classification of the product. \'bioengineered\' aligns with USDA BE disclosure requirements. \'non_gmo\' indicates verified absence of bioengineered material. \'conventional\' indicates no GMO testing performed. \'under_assessment\' indicates testing in progress.']
  gmo_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable percentage of GMO material for the product to be classified as non-GMO or to meet the applicable regulatory threshold for the target market (e.g., 0.9% for EU, 5.0% for Japan). Drives compliance determination logic.']
  introduced_gene varchar [note: 'Name of the gene or genetic construct introduced into the organism (e.g., \'cp4 epsps\', \'cry1Ab\', \'bar\'). Used for scientific documentation, regulatory submissions, and export market approval cross-referencing.']
  ip_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the Identity Preservation (IP) protocol applied to maintain the segregation and traceability of non-GMO or specific GMO product streams throughout the supply chain. Supports USDA BE disclosure and export market requirements.']
  japan_market_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for import or commercial use in Japan. Required for export documentation to Japanese buyers under Japan\'s Food Safety Commission GMO approval framework.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this GMO declaration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.']
  non_gmo_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate or verification number issued by the non-GMO verification body. Used for audit trail, customer documentation, and regulatory compliance. Null when no third-party verification exists.']
  non_gmo_verification_body varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party organization that verified and certified the non-GMO status of the product (e.g., Non-GMO Project, USDA Organic NOP). Applicable only when gmo_status is \'non_gmo\'. \'none\' indicates no third-party verification.']
  organic_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification. Organic certification inherently prohibits GMO use; this flag supports cross-validation of GMO status against organic certification status.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, qualifications, or explanations related to the GMO declaration. May include details on testing anomalies, conditional approvals, or market-specific exceptions.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the GMO declaration was reviewed and approved or rejected by the responsible quality or regulatory team. Supports workflow tracking and audit compliance.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality assurance or regulatory compliance officer who reviewed and approved or rejected this GMO declaration. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  sample_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number of the product sample submitted for GMO testing. Enables traceability from the GMO declaration back to the specific production lot for recall management and audit purposes.']
  test_result_percent decimal(6,4) [note: 'Quantitative result of GMO testing expressed as a percentage of GMO material detected in the sample (e.g., 0.0500 = 0.05%). Used to assess compliance against regulatory thresholds (e.g., EU 0.9% labeling threshold, Japan 5% threshold).']
  testing_laboratory varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited laboratory that performed GMO testing on the product. Must be an ISO 17025-accredited laboratory for regulatory acceptance of results.']
  testing_method varchar [note: 'Laboratory method used to test for the presence or absence of GMO material in the product (e.g., PCR - Polymerase Chain Reaction, ELISA - Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay). \'not_tested\' when no laboratory testing was performed.']
  threshold_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Jurisdiction or market for which the gmo_threshold_percent applies. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or \'global\' for internationally harmonized thresholds. Enables multi-market compliance management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|EU|CHN|JPN|BRA|CAN|AUS|global — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trait_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive name and functional summary of the introduced genetic trait (e.g., \'Herbicide tolerance - glyphosate\', \'Insect resistance - Bt Cry1Ab protein\', \'Drought tolerance - DREB transcription factor\'). Supports product labeling, COA documentation, and export market compliance.']
  us_market_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for commercial use in the United States market. Verified against APHIS deregulation status and FDA/EPA approvals.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which GMO status was verified through testing, certification, or third-party audit. Used to determine currency of the declaration and trigger re-verification workflows.']

  Note: 'Records GMO status declarations and non-GMO verification records for agricultural products and commodities. Captures GMO trait description, transformation event identifier (APHIS petition number), approved market status by jurisdiction (US, EU, China), non-GMO verification body (Non-GMO Project, USDA Organic), identity preservation (IP) protocol reference, and testing/verification date. Supports USDA BE (Bioengineered Food Disclosure) labeling compliance and export market GMO documentation requirements.'
}

Table product.grading_standard {
  grading_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each grading standard record in the agricultural product catalog. Primary key for the grading_standard reference entity.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: grading_standard is defined for specific commodity types (e.g., USDA Grade No. 1 Hard Red Winter Wheat). grading_standard.commodity_name is a denormalized string reference to the commodity catalog. Ad']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: grading_standard currently stores mrl_threshold_ppm as a denormalized DECIMAL scalar. The mrl_threshold table is the authoritative SSOT for Maximum Residue Limits. A grading standard for an agricultur']
  administering_agency varchar [note: 'Federal agency responsible for administering and enforcing this grading standard (e.g., AMS - Agricultural Marketing Service, GIPSA - Grain Inspection Packers and Stockyards Administration, FSIS - Food Safety and Inspection Service). Determines inspection authority and regulatory citation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AMS|GIPSA|FSIS|APHIS|FDA|EPA|NOP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  applicable_market_class varchar [note: 'Market class or sub-class of the commodity to which this grading standard applies (e.g., \'Hard Red Winter\', \'Soft White\', \'Yellow Dent Corn\', \'Angus Beef\'). Provides finer commodity segmentation within the commodity type for accurate grade assignment.']
  coa_template_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the COA document template associated with this grading standard. Used by quality inspection workflows in SAP QM to auto-populate COA documents upon grade assignment.']
  color_requirement varchar [note: 'Descriptive color specification or color score range required for this grade designation (e.g., \'minimum 50% red color for US Extra Fancy apples\', \'bright yellow for US No. 1 corn\'). Applies primarily to produce and livestock grading per USDA AMS standards.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Broad commodity category to which this grading standard applies (e.g., grain, oilseed, produce, fiber, livestock, dairy, poultry, egg, processed). Aligns with USDA commodity classification for routing to the correct inspection workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|oilseed|produce|fiber|livestock|dairy|poultry|egg|processed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cool_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) is required for products graded under this standard. When True, COA and product labeling must include country of origin per USDA AMS COOL regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grading standard record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per SOX and FSMA compliance frameworks.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this grading standard became or becomes officially effective and enforceable. Used to determine applicability during quality inspection and COA generation workflows.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this grading standard expires or is superseded. Null indicates the standard is open-ended with no scheduled expiration. Used to enforce version control in quality inspection workflows.']
  foreign_material_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable percentage of foreign material (non-commodity matter) permitted for this grade designation. A standard GIPSA grading factor for grains, oilseeds, and other bulk commodities.']
  gfsi_scheme_alignment varchar [note: 'Identifies the GFSI-recognized food safety scheme (e.g., SQF, BRC, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P., FSSC 22000) with which this grading standard is aligned or compatible. Supports customer audit and certification requirements.']
  gmo_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this grading standard applies to GMO products, non-GMO products, or both. Supports product line segregation for non-GMO and conventional product lines per USDA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  grade_designation varchar [note: 'Official grade label assigned by the administering agency (e.g., \'US No. 1\', \'US No. 2\', \'US Extra Fancy\', \'USDA Prime\', \'USDA Choice\', \'US Grade A\'). Represents the quality tier within the standard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US No. 1|US No. 2|US No. 3|US Extra Fancy|USDA Prime|USDA Choice|USDA Select|US Grade A|US Grade B|US Grade C — promote to reference product]']
  grading_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to assess and assign this grade (e.g., visual inspection, mechanical sizing, laboratory analysis, electronic/NIR sensing, or a combination). Determines inspection workflow and equipment requirements in SAP QM and field operations.']
  inspection_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of grading inspections for commodities subject to this standard (e.g., per lot, per shipment, per batch, periodic, continuous). Drives quality inspection scheduling in SAP QM and Manhattan WMS workflows.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this grading standard record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP QM, and regulatory audit trail maintenance.']
  moisture_content_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable moisture content (%) for a commodity to qualify for this grade designation. A critical grading criterion for grains and oilseeds per GIPSA standards. Exceeding this threshold results in grade reduction or rejection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or clarifications related to this grading standard (e.g., seasonal applicability, regional exceptions, special handling requirements). Used by quality and compliance teams.']
  organic_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this grading standard is applicable to USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified organic products. When True, the standard supports organic product line quality certification and COA generation.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date on which this grading standard was officially published in the Federal Register or by the administering agency. Distinct from the effective date; used for regulatory audit trails and compliance documentation.']
  regulation_citation varchar [note: 'Official regulatory citation for the grading standard, including CFR title, part, and section (e.g., \'7 CFR Part 26\', \'7 CFR Part 868\', \'9 CFR Part 319\'). Required for COA generation and FSMA compliance documentation.']
  serious_defects_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable percentage of serious defects (e.g., heat-damaged kernels, mold, insect damage) permitted for this grade. Serious defects are a distinct sub-category of total defects with stricter tolerance limits per GIPSA standards.']
  size_requirement varchar [note: 'Size specification or size range required for this grade designation (e.g., minimum diameter in mm, weight per unit, count per carton). Applies to produce, eggs, and livestock products per USDA AMS grading standards.']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the grading standard (e.g., \'USDA-GR1-CORN\', \'AMS-EF-APPLE\'). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and COA generation.']
  standard_document_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official published grading standard document on the administering agency\'s website (e.g., USDA AMS, GIPSA). Provides direct access to the authoritative source document for quality inspectors and compliance teams.']
  standard_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the grading standard as published by the administering agency (e.g., \'United States Standards for Grades of Corn\', \'USDA Extra Fancy Grade for Apples\'). Used in COA documentation and quality inspection workflows.']
  standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the grading standard. \'active\' indicates the standard is currently in force; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'withdrawn\' indicates no longer applicable; \'draft\' or \'pending\' indicates under review or awaiting official publication.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'Standard code of the newer grading standard that replaces this record when status is \'superseded\'. Enables forward navigation in the version chain for compliance and historical reporting.']
  test_weight_min_lbs_bu decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum test weight in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu) required for a grain or oilseed commodity to qualify for this grade. Test weight is a key GIPSA grading factor reflecting grain density and quality.']
  total_defects_max_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable percentage of total defects (damaged kernels, heat damage, broken kernels, etc.) permitted for this grade designation. Applies to grain, oilseed, and produce grading per GIPSA and AMS standards.']
  usda_commodity_code varchar [note: 'Official USDA commodity code associated with this grading standard, enabling alignment with USDA AMS, GIPSA, and WASDE reporting systems. Used for regulatory reporting and supply chain traceability.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision number of the grading standard as published by the administering agency (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2023\', \'3\'). Supports audit trails and historical compliance verification.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all applicable grading standards and grade specifications for agricultural commodities and products. Captures USDA grade designation (e.g., US No. 1, US Extra Fancy, USDA Prime), commodity type, grading criteria (moisture content, test weight, foreign material tolerance, defect limits), applicable regulation citation, effective date, and administering agency (AMS, FSIS, GIPSA). Supports quality inspection workflows and COA generation. Aligned with USDA AMS and GIPSA official grade standards.'
}

Table product.mrl_threshold {
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each MRL threshold record in the regulatory reference catalog. Primary key for this table.']
  active_ingredient_cas_number varchar [note: 'CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the pesticide active ingredient chemical substance. Enables unambiguous cross-referencing across regulatory databases and SDS documentation.']
  active_ingredient_name varchar [note: 'Common or IUPAC chemical name of the pesticide active ingredient (AI) for which the MRL is established. Used to cross-reference against agrochemical product labels and COA verification.']
  analytical_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated analytical method (e.g., AOAC, EN, EPA method number) used for laboratory detection and quantification of the residue to verify compliance with this MRL. Supports COA and laboratory accreditation alignment.']
  application_method varchar [note: 'Method of pesticide application for which this MRL tolerance is established (e.g., foliar spray, soil application, seed treatment, post-harvest treatment, fumigation). Affects residue behavior and PHI calculation.']
  codex_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official Codex Alimentarius MRL reference number (CXL number) for this tolerance, enabling direct cross-reference to the Codex MRL database. Used for international trade documentation and export COA verification.']
  commodity_group varchar [note: 'Broader commodity group classification under which the specific commodity falls (e.g., cereal grains, fruiting vegetables, root and tuber vegetables, meat and poultry). Supports group tolerance application per EPA and Codex rules.']
  commodity_part varchar [note: 'Specific part of the commodity to which the MRL applies (e.g., whole commodity, edible portion only, peel, grain, muscle tissue, fat, liver, milk, eggs). Relevant for processed commodity tolerances and animal product MRLs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_commodity|edible_portion|peel|seed|grain|muscle|fat|liver|kidney|milk|eggs — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MRL threshold record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking per Databricks Lakehouse governance standards.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Originating regulatory database or system from which this MRL record was sourced (e.g., EPA Office of Pesticide Programs tolerance database, Codex MRL database, EU Pesticides database). Supports data lineage and refresh cycle management.']
  default_mrl_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MRL is a default/general tolerance applied in the absence of a specific established tolerance (e.g., Codex default of 0.01 ppm, EU default of 0.01 ppm under Regulation EC 396/2005). True = default MRL applies; False = specific established tolerance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this MRL tolerance became legally effective and enforceable by the regulatory jurisdiction. Used to determine applicability for current crop season compliance and PHI scheduling.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'EPA pesticide registration number associated with the active ingredient or product formulation. Links MRL tolerance to the registered pesticide product for PHI compliance and export documentation.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Regulatory justification for a tolerance exemption, applicable when tolerance_type is \'exemption\'. Describes why the active ingredient is exempt from MRL requirements (e.g., naturally occurring substance, minimal risk, biochemical pesticide). Null for non-exemption tolerance types.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this MRL tolerance expires or is scheduled for revocation. Null for indefinite tolerances. Critical for time-limited emergency exemptions (Section 18) and temporary import tolerances.']
  federal_register_citation varchar [note: 'U.S. Federal Register citation (volume, page number, and date) for the EPA final rule establishing or amending this tolerance. Provides regulatory traceability for audit and legal compliance purposes.']
  gmo_applicability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MRL tolerance specifically applies to or was established in the context of GMO crop varieties (e.g., herbicide-tolerant GMO crops with higher herbicide residue tolerances). True = GMO-specific tolerance; False = applies to conventional and GMO equally.']
  import_tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this MRL was established specifically as an import tolerance to facilitate trade for commodities treated with a pesticide not registered domestically. True = import tolerance; False = domestic tolerance. Relevant for FOB/CIF export documentation.']
  jurisdiction_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the importing or regulating country associated with this MRL. Used for export eligibility checks and Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance. Null for multi-country jurisdictions (e.g., EU, Codex).']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this MRL record was last reviewed and confirmed against the authoritative regulatory source database. Supports data quality management and ensures currency of compliance reference data.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MRL threshold record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data refresh, change detection, and regulatory data currency validation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  limit_of_quantification_ppm decimal(10,6) [note: 'Lowest concentration of the active ingredient residue that can be reliably quantified by the validated analytical method. Used to determine whether a non-detect result constitutes MRL compliance, particularly for default MRL tolerances.']
  mode_of_action_code varchar [note: 'IRAC (Insecticide Resistance Action Committee), FRAC (Fungicide Resistance Action Committee), or HRAC (Herbicide Resistance Action Committee) mode of action classification code for the active ingredient. Supports IPM resistance management planning.']
  mrl_value_ppm decimal(10,4) [note: 'The established maximum residue limit expressed in parts per million (ppm, equivalent to mg/kg) for the active ingredient on the specified commodity. Core compliance threshold used for COA verification, pre-harvest interval enforcement, and export eligibility determination.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory notes, conditions, or qualifications associated with this MRL tolerance (e.g., specific use restrictions, footnotes from regulatory publications, pending review status, or special conditions for application).']
  organic_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the active ingredient is approved for use in certified organic production under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. True = approved for organic use; False = not approved. Supports organic product line compliance and NOP certification maintenance.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days required between the last pesticide application and harvest to ensure residues do not exceed the MRL at time of harvest. Critical for crop protection scheduling and FSMA compliance. Sourced from EPA label requirements.']
  processing_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'Ratio of residue concentration in the processed commodity to residue concentration in the raw agricultural commodity (RAC). Used to calculate MRL compliance for processed food products derived from the raw commodity. Values >1 indicate concentration; values <1 indicate reduction.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or jurisdiction that established this MRL (e.g., US_EPA for United States Environmental Protection Agency, EU for European Union Regulation EC 396/2005, CODEX for Codex Alimentarius Commission). Critical for export documentation and multi-market compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US_EPA|EU|CODEX|CANADA|JAPAN|AUSTRALIA|CHINA|BRAZIL|MEXICO|KOREA|INDIA|TAIWAN — promote to reference product]']
  residue_definition varchar [note: 'Regulatory definition of what constitutes the measurable residue for enforcement purposes, including parent compound and relevant metabolites or degradation products. Critical for laboratory COA analysis method alignment.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously established MRL tolerance was formally revoked or cancelled by the regulatory authority. Null for active tolerances. Supports historical compliance audit trails and COA re-verification.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating regulatory database (e.g., EPA tolerance petition number, Codex CXL reference, EU database entry ID). Enables traceability back to the authoritative source system for audit and refresh purposes.']
  tolerance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MRL tolerance record: \'active\' for currently enforceable tolerances, \'revoked\' for cancelled tolerances, \'expired\' for time-limited tolerances past their expiry date, \'pending\' for proposed tolerances under regulatory review, \'suspended\' for temporarily suspended tolerances.']
  tolerance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MRL tolerance type: \'established\' for standard EPA/Codex tolerances, \'exemption\' for active ingredients exempt from tolerance requirements, \'import_tolerance\' for tolerances set specifically for imported commodities, \'emergency_exemption\' for Section 18 emergency use tolerances, \'group_tolerance\' for tolerances covering a commodity group.']

  Note: 'Reference table of Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for pesticide active ingredients (AI) on specific agricultural commodities by regulatory jurisdiction. Captures active ingredient name, EPA registration number, commodity or commodity group, MRL value (ppm), regulatory jurisdiction (US EPA, EU, Codex Alimentarius, importing country), effective date, and tolerance type (established, exemption, import tolerance). Critical for pre-harvest interval (PHI) compliance, export documentation, and COA verification. Sourced from EPA and Codex Alimentarius databases.'
}

Table product.label {
  label_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each label record in the agricultural product labeling catalog. Primary key for the label data product.']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: label currently has a non_gmo_claim_flag (BOOLEAN) that indicates a non-GMO claim on the label but provides no traceability to the supporting declaration. Under USDA Bioengineered (BE) Disclosure regu']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: label.usda_grade_designation is a denormalized string reference to the applicable USDA grade standard displayed on the label. Adding grading_standard_id FK normalizes this to the authoritative grading']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural product or commodity this label is associated with in the product catalog (crop commodity, livestock product, processed good, seed/input, or value-added product).']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: label.mrl_compliance_flag indicates whether the label is MRL-compliant, but there is no reference to the specific MRL threshold being complied with. Adding mrl_threshold_id FK links the label to the a']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: label.organic_certification_code is a denormalized string reference to the NOP organic certification that authorizes the organic seal on the label. Adding organic_certification_id FK normalizes this t']
  allergen_cross_contact_risk varchar [note: 'Assessed risk level for unintentional allergen cross-contact during production, handling, or packaging. Drives \'may contain\' advisory statement decisions and HACCP allergen control plan requirements.']
  allergen_milk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether milk (FDA Big 9 allergen) is intentionally included as an ingredient in this product. When True, milk must be declared in the ingredient list and allergen statement per FALCPA.']
  allergen_soy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether soybeans (FDA Big 9 allergen) are intentionally included as an ingredient. Particularly relevant in agricultural products given soy\'s prevalence in processed and feed products.']
  allergen_wheat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether wheat (FDA Big 9 allergen) is intentionally included as an ingredient. When True, wheat must be declared in the ingredient list and allergen statement per FALCPA.']
  applicable_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or market identifiers for which this label version is approved and valid (e.g., \'USA,CAN,MEX\'). Supports multi-market label management and export compliance.']
  artwork_file_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URI pointing to the approved label artwork file (e.g., PDF, AI, EPS). Supports label artwork version control and print production workflows.']
  calories_per_serving varchar [note: 'Total caloric value per serving as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel, expressed in kilocalories (kcal). Calculated from macronutrient composition per FDA rounding rules.']
  cool_designation varchar [note: 'Country of origin declaration required under COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) regulations. Specifies the country or countries where the product was produced, grown, or processed. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., \'USA\', \'MEX\', \'CAN\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this label record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this label version becomes commercially active and legally binding for product packaging and distribution. Aligns with regulatory approval and market launch timelines.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this label version is no longer valid for use on new production runs. Null for open-ended approvals. Triggers label review and renewal workflows.']
  haccp_allergen_control_plan_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the HACCP allergen control plan document governing allergen management for this product label. Links label allergen declarations to the facility\'s preventive controls documentation.']
  ingredient_statement varchar [note: 'Full ingredient list as it appears on the label, listed in descending order of predominance by weight. Must comply with FDA 21 CFR Part 101.4 ingredient declaration requirements.']
  label_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to this label version, used for cross-referencing with SAP QM label management and regulatory submission tracking.']
  label_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the label, typically reflecting the product name, market, and version (e.g., \'Organic Whole Wheat Flour — US Retail v3\').']
  label_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the label record. Tracks progression from draft through regulatory approval to active use, supersession by a newer version, or withdrawal from market.']
  label_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the label by its intended commercial use channel: retail consumer packaging, foodservice bulk, export market, private label, or ingredient/intermediate product.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this label record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream data synchronization.']
  mrl_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label\'s associated product has been verified to comply with applicable Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) thresholds for pesticide and agrochemical residues as set by EPA, Codex Alimentarius, or destination market regulators.']
  net_weight_declaration varchar [note: 'Mandatory net weight or net quantity statement as it appears on the label (e.g., \'5 LB (2.27 kg)\', \'16 FL OZ (473 mL)\'). Must comply with NIST Handbook 133 and FDA net quantity of contents regulations.']
  nutrition_analysis_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the nutrition facts panel values: laboratory analysis (wet chemistry), nutrient database calculation (USDA FoodData Central), recipe calculation, or a combination. Affects accuracy and regulatory defensibility.']
  organic_seal_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label is authorized to display the USDA Organic seal under the National Organic Program (NOP). True only when the product and operation hold valid NOP certification.']
  plu_code varchar [note: '4 or 5-digit Price Look-Up (PLU) code used for fresh produce identification at retail point-of-sale. Assigned by the International Federation for Produce Standards (IFPS). Applicable to fresh fruit, vegetable, and herb products.']
  protein_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Protein content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Mandatory declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts label.']
  regulatory_approval_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or tracking code issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., USDA AMS label approval number, FDA submission reference) confirming this label has been reviewed and authorized.']
  serving_size_description varchar [note: 'Serving size as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel (e.g., \'1 cup (240g)\', \'2 tablespoons (32g)\'). Must align with FDA Reference Amounts Customarily Consumed (RACC) for the product category.']
  servings_per_container decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of servings per container as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. May be a whole number or decimal (e.g., 2.5 servings). Required for FDA-compliant Nutrition Facts labeling.']
  sodium_mg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sodium content per serving in milligrams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Key nutrient for dietary guidance and FDA mandatory declaration.']
  total_carbohydrate_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total carbohydrate content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel, including dietary fiber and total sugars.']
  total_fat_g decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total fat content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Includes saturated fat, trans fat, polyunsaturated fat, and monounsaturated fat.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode number printed on the label for retail point-of-sale scanning and supply chain identification. Assigned by GS1 and linked to the product SKU.']
  usda_ams_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the label submission and approval with the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), required for meat, poultry, and egg products. Tracks submission through final approval or rejection.']
  usda_grade_shield_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this label is authorized to display a USDA grade shield (e.g., USDA Grade A, USDA Choice). Requires grading certificate from USDA AMS grading service.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this label iteration (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.3\'). Incremented whenever label content, claims, or regulatory data changes. Supports label version history and change control.']

  Note: 'Manages all regulatory and commercial labeling content and compliance records for agricultural products. Covers COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) designations, USDA organic seal authorization, non-GMO label claims, USDA grade shield usage, allergen declarations (FDA Big 9: milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame — including intentional inclusion flags, cross-contact risk levels, and allergen control measure references), nutrition facts panel data (macronutrients, micronutrients, caloric value, serving size, analysis method), net weight declarations, and label artwork file references. Captures label version, effective date, regulatory approval reference, applicable markets/jurisdictions, and HACCP allergen control plan linkage. Tracks FDA FSMA labeling and Preventive Controls allergen management compliance, USDA AMS label approval status, and supports SAP S/4HANA QM module label management.'
}

Table product.product_certification {
  product_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each product certification record in the agricultural certification catalog.']
  gmo_declaration_id varchar [note: 'APHIS petition number or unique transformation event identifier assigned to a specific genetically modified organism event (e.g., MON810, GTS 40-3-2). Required for USDA Bioengineered (BE) food disclosure and export market approval documentation.']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural product or commodity in the product master catalog to which this certification applies.']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: certification is the consolidated SSOT for all third-party and regulatory certifications. When a certification record represents an NOP organic certification, it should reference the detailed organic_']
  allowed_materials_list_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document URL for the USDA NOP National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances applicable to this organic certification scope. Ensures that only approved inputs are used in certified organic production.']
  approved_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes representing jurisdictions where this GMO transformation event or certification has received regulatory approval for commercial use or import (e.g., USA, CAN, BRA, CHN, EU member states). Critical for export documentation and market access decisions.']
  audit_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Frequency of the required audit or inspection cycle expressed in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 6 for semi-annual). Determines the recurrence interval for compliance audits under the certification program.']
  be_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is subject to USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS) labeling requirements. True if the product contains detectable modified genetic material from the USDA BE disclosure list.']
  brc_grade varchar [note: 'BRC Global Standard audit grade awarded at the most recent certification audit (AA, A, B, C, or D), where AA is the highest achievable grade for unannounced audits. Null if certification_type is not BRC.']
  certificate_document_ref varchar [note: 'File path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the official certificate document. Supports audit readiness, export documentation, and retail label claim substantiation.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-issued unique certificate number assigned by the certifying body (e.g., USDA NOP organic certificate number, GLOBALG.A.P. GGN, SQF certificate number). Used for audit trails, export documentation, and retail label claims.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'Operational scope covered by the certification as defined by the certifying body. For USDA NOP organic, scope is one of: crops, livestock, handling (processing), or wild crop. For food safety certifications, scope may reference specific product categories or processing operations.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the certification. Active indicates the certificate is valid and in force; suspended indicates a temporary hold pending investigation; revoked indicates permanent cancellation by the certifying body; expired indicates the certificate has passed its expiration date without renewal.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification held by the product. Covers USDA NOP organic, USDA Bioengineered (BE) food declaration, Non-GMO Project Verified, GLOBALG.A.P., SQF, BRC, and others. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic_nop|gmo_be_declaration|non_gmo_verified|globalg_ap|sqf|brc|ifs|fair_trade|rainforest_alliance|kosher|halal|iscc — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_body_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number assigned to the certifying body by the relevant oversight authority (e.g., USDA NOP accreditation number for organic certifying agents, GFSI accreditation reference). Validates the legitimacy of the certifying organization.']
  certifying_body_name varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited third-party organization or regulatory agency that issued the certification (e.g., Oregon Tilth, CCOF, NSF International, SGS, Bureau Veritas, Non-GMO Project, SQF Institute). Required for audit and export documentation.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with this certification, containing laboratory test results for residues, contaminants, or identity preservation verification.']
  cool_country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3-letter country code indicating the country of origin of the certified product as required under USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) regulations. Used for retail label compliance and export documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the certification is operationally effective and the product may be marketed under the certified claim. May differ from issue_date if there is a transition or grace period.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification expires and must be renewed to maintain certified status. Critical for organic premium pricing, non-GMO market access, and retail label claims. Triggers renewal workflow when approaching.']
  fair_trade_producer_code varchar [note: 'Unique producer or cooperative code assigned by Fair Trade USA or Fairtrade International to identify the certified producer organization associated with this product\'s fair trade certification.']
  globalg_ap_number varchar [note: '13-digit GLOBALG.A.P. Number (GGN) assigned to the certified producer or production site under the GLOBALG.A.P. certification scheme. Used for supply chain traceability and retail buyer verification via the GLOBALG.A.P. database.']
  halal_certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the recognized halal certifying organization (e.g., IFANCA, ISWA, HFA) that issued the halal certification for this product. Null if certification_type is not halal.']
  ip_protocol_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code or document identifier for the Identity Preservation (IP) protocol applied to maintain non-GMO or specialty crop segregation integrity throughout the supply chain. Required for Non-GMO Project Verified and identity-preserved commodity programs.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certifying body formally issued the certificate. Marks the start of the certification\'s legal validity and is printed on the certificate document.']
  kosher_certifying_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the recognized kosher certifying agency (e.g., OU, OK, KOF-K, Star-K) that issued the kosher certification for this product. Null if certification_type is not kosher.']
  label_claim_text varchar [note: 'Approved marketing claim text authorized by this certification for use on product packaging and retail labels (e.g., \'USDA Organic\', \'Non-GMO Project Verified\', \'Certified Kosher\', \'Fair Trade Certified\'). Must align with certifying body approved language.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent on-site inspection or audit conducted by the certifying body or accredited auditor. Used to track audit cycle compliance and readiness for renewal.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record. Used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.']
  last_verification_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent laboratory testing, residue verification, or document review performed to confirm ongoing compliance with certification requirements (e.g., MRL residue testing for organic, identity preservation testing for non-GMO).']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the next scheduled inspection or audit by the certifying body is planned. Supports audit cycle planning and operational readiness.']
  non_gmo_project_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product has received Non-GMO Project Verified status, entitling it to display the Non-GMO Project butterfly seal on retail packaging. Supports non-GMO market access and premium pricing.']
