The existing is_organic_certified boolean is a denormal']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Invoice line-level profit center assignment enables segment-level revenue reporting by commodity or service type (grain, seed, agronomy). Agriculture enterprises require line-level profit center for a']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed sales invoice lines require variety-level reference for trait royalty billing, Plant Variety Protection (PVP) compliance, and phytosanitary certificate documentation. Seed companies invoice at va']
  shipment_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment_line. Business justification: Invoice lines in agriculture map to shipment lines for revenue recognition, FSMA lot-level traceability billing, and quality-adjusted pricing. Direct link supports invoice-to-shipment-line matching fo']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Storage fee invoice lines must reference the specific storage location being billed. Grain elevator operators bill producers per storage location (bin/silo). This link supports storage billing reconci']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: invoice.line.weight_ticket_ref is a denormalized string reference to a weight ticket. Each invoice line item for a commodity lot should reference the specific weight ticket that certified the delivery']
  basis_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'The local cash price differential (basis) relative to the futures contract reference price, expressed in the invoice currency per UOM. Positive basis indicates premium over futures; negative indicates discount. Critical for agricultural commodity pricing reconciliation.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with the shipment of the commodity billed on this line. Provides logistics traceability and supports freight reconciliation and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, SOX compliance, and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD, BRL). Completes the MONETARY_TRIPLET currency dimension. Typically USD for domestic US agricultural transactions.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the commodity, livestock, or service was delivered or performed, establishing the revenue recognition trigger date per GAAP ASC 606 / IFRS 15 performance obligation satisfaction.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount or price reduction applied to this line item, expressed in the invoice currency. Includes trade discounts, volume rebates, quality deductions, and dockage adjustments for commodity grading deficiencies.']
  dockage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deduction applied to the billed quantity for foreign material, moisture excess, or quality deficiencies per USDA grain inspection standards. Drives quantity and price adjustments on the invoice line.']
  futures_contract_ref varchar [note: 'CME/CBOT futures contract month and symbol used as the pricing reference for this line when pricing_basis is \'futures\' or \'formula\' (e.g., \'ZC-DEC24\' for December 2024 Corn futures). Null when pricing_basis is spot or contract.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross billed amount for this line before discounts and adjustments, calculated as quantity × unit_price. Expressed in the invoice currency. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for revenue recognition.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity billed on this line contains or is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO). Required for labeling compliance under USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard and export documentation.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice line item is subject to sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes. Drives tax calculation logic in SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle NetSuite Tax modules.']
  line_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the commodity, crop product, livestock, or service being billed on this line (e.g., \'Yellow Corn No. 2 - Harvest 2024\', \'Feeder Cattle - 500-600 lb\', \'Custom Application Service\').']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this line item within the parent invoice. Used for ordering, display, and reconciliation of multi-line invoices. Satisfies LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this invoice line item within the AR lifecycle. Drives workflow routing, dispute management, and revenue recognition timing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|billed|disputed|cancelled|credited|settled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice line item indicating whether it represents a commodity sale, livestock transaction, service charge, freight, storage, handling fee, penalty, or credit memo. Drives revenue recognition category assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commodity|livestock|service|freight|storage|handling|penalty|credit_memo — promote to reference product]']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content percentage of the grain or commodity at time of delivery/billing. Used to calculate moisture dockage adjustments and shrinkage deductions applied to the billed quantity and price.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net billed amount for this line after discounts and before tax, calculated as gross_amount minus discount_amount. Represents the taxable base for this line. Satisfies LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT and MONETARY_TRIPLET categories.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the commodity billed, required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance under USDA Agricultural Marketing Act.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order number referencing this invoice line. Required for three-way match (PO-GRN-Invoice) reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA MM and Oracle NetSuite procurement workflows.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the commodity, livestock, or service units billed on this line. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in uom. Satisfies LINE_QUANTITY category. For grain: bushels (BU); for livestock: head; for bulk commodities: metric tons (MT) or hundredweight (CWT).']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Revenue recognition category assigned to this line for GL posting and financial reporting under GAAP ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Drives the revenue account mapping in SAP S/4HANA CO module and Oracle NetSuite GL.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount assessed on this line item (sales tax, VAT, excise tax, or agricultural levy), expressed in the invoice currency. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET adjustment component.']
  test_weight decimal(7,2) [note: 'Test weight of the grain commodity in pounds per bushel (lb/BU) as measured at delivery. A key quality parameter affecting grade assignment and price adjustments for corn, soybeans, wheat, and other grains.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Price per unit of measure for this line item. Expressed in the invoice currency. May reflect spot market price, futures-based contract price, or negotiated contract price depending on pricing_basis.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the billed quantity. Agricultural-standard units include BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), LB (Pound), KG (Kilogram), HEAD (livestock count), TON (short ton), GAL (gallon), L (liter), ACRE, EA (each for services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|KG|HEAD|TON|GAL|L|ACRE|EA — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this invoice line record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, dispute resolution, and SOX audit requirements.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each AR invoice, representing individual commodity lots, crop products, livestock, or service charges billed. Captures line number, commodity code, crop/livestock description, quantity (BU, CWT, MT, head), unit of measure, unit price, pricing basis (spot, futures, contract), gross amount, discount, net amount, tax amount, GLN/lot reference, weight-ticket reference, and revenue recognition category. Supports commodity-based pricing and weight-ticket reconciliation per agricultural billing standards.'
}

Table invoice.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each payment transaction record in the accounts receivable (AR) ledger.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the paying customer (food manufacturer, cooperative, distributor, or agricultural buyer) who submitted this payment.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Customer payments (grain settlement, cooperative payments) are deposited into specific company bank accounts. Cash management, bank reconciliation, and daily cash position reporting in agriculture req']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_account. Business justification: Payments received from agricultural customers must be linked to the billing_account AR master for AR balance updates, last_payment_date/amount tracking, and avg_days_to_pay calculation. payment alread']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payments are received by a specific legal entity. In multi-entity agriculture operations (multiple grain elevators, cooperative subsidiaries), cash receipts must be attributed to the correct legal ent']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying sales or commodity purchase contract linked to this payment, particularly relevant for advance payments, crop pre-purchase agreements, and seasonal payment programs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR payment processing costs (bank fees, remittance processing) are allocated to cost centers. payment.cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute; replacing with proper FK enforces referential ']
  gl_posting_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cash receipt payments generate GL postings (debit bank/cash, credit AR). In agriculture AR operations, linking payment to its GL journal entry supports cash application reconciliation, period close AR']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary invoice against which this payment is applied. Supports invoice-to-payment reconciliation in accounts receivable (AR).']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Lease rent payments are a primary cash outflow in agriculture operations. Linking payment to the governing lease enables lease payment reconciliation, cash-rent payment history reporting, and verifica']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Agriculture grain elevators process bulk producer settlement payment runs (weekly/monthly). Linking AR payments to payment_run enables reconciliation of batch settlement disbursements, audit trails fo']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: payment.payment_terms_code is a denormalized string reference to the payment_term reference table. Payments must reference the authoritative payment term to validate early payment discount eligibility']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AR cash receipts must be attributed to profit centers for segment-level cash flow and AR aging reporting. Agriculture businesses track producer payments and customer receipts by division (grain, lives']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: payment has sales_order_reference as a plain denormalized text field. A proper FK to sales.order supports cash application workflows, order-level payment status reporting, and AR clearing — standard o']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: payment.weight_ticket_reference is a denormalized string reference to a weight ticket. In agricultural commodity payments (especially grain elevator settlements and producer payments), payments are of']
  advance_purpose varchar [note: 'Specific business purpose for advance or prepayment transactions. Applicable only when payment_type is \'advance\' or \'deposit\'. Supports agricultural-specific advance payment programs including crop pre-purchase financing, seed and input supply advances, and grain storage deposits.']
  amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross amount of the payment as submitted by the payer in the transaction currency. Represents the total funds received before any deductions, discounts, or adjustments are applied.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been successfully applied against one or more AR invoices. The difference between payment_amount and applied_amount yields the unapplied balance.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the bank for the payment transaction (e.g., ACH trace number, wire confirmation number, check clearing reference). Used for bank reconciliation and payment tracing.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Interface or channel through which the payment was submitted. \'portal\' = customer self-service web portal; \'edi\' = EDI 820 electronic remittance; \'manual\' = manually entered by AR staff; \'bank_direct\' = bank-initiated lockbox or direct debit; \'mobile\' = mobile payment application.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Paper check number when payment_method is \'check\'. Required for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail in agricultural cooperative and distributor payments.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was cleared against the AR invoice in the accounting system. Marks the completion of the cash application process and AR balance reduction.']
  commodity_lot_reference varchar [note: 'Lot number (LOT) or batch identifier of the agricultural commodity (grain, produce, livestock) associated with this payment. Enables traceability from payment back to specific commodity lot for FSMA compliance and quality reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency agricultural commodity sales and international trade settlements.']
  discount_taken_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Early payment discount amount deducted by the payer per agreed payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Recorded separately for cash discount accounting and terms compliance tracking.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment was contractually due per the invoice payment terms. Used for early/late payment analysis, discount eligibility determination, and AR aging reporting.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the company\'s functional currency (USD) at the time of payment. Required for multi-currency AR reconciliation and IFRS 21 translation.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Payment amount converted to the company\'s functional currency (USD) using the exchange_rate at the time of payment. Used for consolidated financial reporting and GL posting.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) account code to which this payment is posted in the financial accounting system. Supports revenue recognition, AR clearing, and advance payment liability accounting per GAAP/IFRS.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is an intercompany transaction between related legal entities within the agricultural enterprise group. Required for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements per GAAP/IFRS.']
  is_partial_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment covers only a portion of the total invoice amount. True when applied_amount is less than the full invoice balance. Supports installment and partial payment tracking for agricultural seasonal payment programs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by AR staff regarding the payment, such as dispute context, special handling instructions, customer communication details, or reconciliation remarks.']
  payer_bank_account varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized bank account identifier of the payer used for ACH, EFT, or wire payments. Stored in masked format for PCI compliance. Used for payment routing validation and fraud detection.']
  payer_bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number (9 digits) of the payer\'s bank for ACH and wire payment processing. Used for payment routing validation and bank reconciliation.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The business event date on which the payment was received or initiated by the payer. Used for aging analysis, cash application, and revenue recognition timing per GAAP/IFRS.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Financial instrument or mechanism used to submit the payment. \'ACH\' = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; \'wire\' = bank wire transfer; \'check\' = paper check; \'EFT\' = Electronic Funds Transfer; \'commodity_offset\' = payment settled via commodity credit or offset against grain/livestock delivery — common in agricultural cooperative and elevator transactions.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the payment transaction, typically matching the payment document number in SAP S/4HANA FI or Oracle NetSuite. Used for cross-system reconciliation and customer remittance matching.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction. \'pending\' = received but not yet bank-confirmed; \'cleared\' = bank-confirmed and applied to AR; \'returned\' = rejected by bank (e.g., NSF check, ACH return); \'voided\' = cancelled before clearing; \'on_hold\' = suspended pending investigation or dispute resolution.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment by its business purpose and timing relative to the invoice. \'standard\' = payment against an existing invoice; \'advance\' = prepayment before invoice issuance (crop pre-purchase, seed advance, input supply advance); \'deposit\' = security or storage deposit; \'installment\' = partial payment under a deferred or seasonal payment plan.']
  remittance_advice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from the payer\'s remittance advice document accompanying the payment. Used to match payment to specific invoices and deductions in the cash application process.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized bank return reason code when payment_status is \'returned\' (e.g., NACHA ACH return codes R01=Insufficient Funds, R02=Account Closed, R03=No Account). Required for returned payment processing and customer follow-up.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Remaining payment balance not yet applied to any invoice. Represents funds held in suspense or advance payment accounts pending invoice matching. Common in agricultural commodity sales with prepayment programs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage for SOX compliance and financial controls.']
  value_date date [note: 'The bank value date on which funds are credited to the company\'s account. May differ from payment_date for wire transfers and ACH batches. Critical for accurate cash position reporting and bank reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Records all inbound payment transactions applied against AR invoices from agricultural customers including food manufacturers, cooperatives, and distributors. Captures payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, EFT, commodity offset), payment amount, currency, bank reference, remittance advice number, payer account, applied invoice references, unapplied balance, payment type (standard, advance/prepayment, deposit, installment), payment purpose for advances (crop pre-purchase, seed advance, input supply advance, storage deposit), linked contract reference for advance payments, and payment status (pending/cleared/returned/voided). Supports seasonal payment terms, deferred payment arrangements, and advance payment programs common in agricultural commodity sales.'
}

Table invoice.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each payment allocation record linking a payment to a specific invoice or invoice line. Serves as the primary key for this association entity.']
  clearing_journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Payment allocations (clearing open AR items) generate GL clearing journal entries. In agriculture AR operations, matching payments to invoices triggers GL clearing postings. clearing_document_number i']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payment allocation cost center assignment is required for AR clearing cost tracking and overhead allocation. payment_allocation.cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute; proper FK enforces r']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice header being settled by this allocation. Supports multi-invoice settlement scenarios common in commodity contract billing and cooperative patronage distribution.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific invoice line item being settled. Enables partial payment tracking at the line level, critical for weight-ticket reconciliation and commodity-based billing where individual line items may be settled independently.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment record being allocated. Links the allocation to the source payment transaction in the accounts receivable (AR) ledger.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Payment allocations in agriculture AR clearing must be assigned to profit centers for segment-level AR reconciliation and intercompany clearing. Enables profit-center-level open item management and cl']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: payment_allocation.weight_ticket_number is a denormalized string reference to a weight ticket. Payment allocations in commodity billing frequently reference specific weight tickets to reconcile which ']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The gross monetary amount of the payment applied to this invoice or invoice line in the transaction currency. Core financial field for AR settlement, revenue recognition, and cash application reporting per GAAP/IFRS standards.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business date on which the payment was applied to the invoice. Used for aging analysis, cash application reporting, and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation. May differ from the payment receipt date in seasonal agricultural billing cycles.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for this payment allocation record, used in AR clearing documents, remittance advice matching, and audit trails. Corresponds to the SAP FI clearing document number or Oracle NetSuite application number.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment allocation: pending (awaiting posting), posted (applied to AR ledger), reversed (allocation reversed/cancelled), reconciled (matched and confirmed in bank reconciliation), disputed (under investigation for discrepancy). Drives AR aging and cash application workflows.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the payment application: full (complete invoice settlement), partial (portion of invoice settled), advance (prepayment applied against future invoice), credit_offset (credit memo applied), netting (offsetting payable against receivable), write_off (uncollectable balance cleared). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full|partial|advance|credit_offset|netting|write_off — promote to reference product]']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the finance manager or controller who approved this payment allocation, particularly for write-offs, large variance clearances, or deduction approvals. Supports segregation of duties and SOX internal control requirements.']
  base_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated amount converted to the company\'s functional (base) currency for general ledger (GL) posting and financial reporting. Required for multi-currency operations in international agricultural commodity trading per IFRS 21.']
  commodity_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural commodity purchase or sales contract against which this payment is being applied. Supports commodity contract payment matching for grain, livestock, and produce contracts. Links to forward contracts, basis contracts, and hedge-to-arrive (HTA) arrangements.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or username of the AR specialist or system process that created this payment allocation record. Required for SOX internal controls, audit trail, and segregation of duties compliance in financial operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment allocation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting per SOX financial controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency agricultural commodity trading and international supply chain billing.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of deductions taken by the payer against the invoice, such as quality dockage, moisture penalties, freight charges, or promotional allowances. Distinct from discount_taken_amount as deductions are typically disputed or contractually defined adjustments in commodity billing.']
  deduction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the reason for any deduction taken by the payer against the invoice amount (e.g., quality deduction, moisture dockage, freight deduction, promotional allowance). Common in agricultural commodity billing where buyers deduct for grade, moisture, or foreign material.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The last date by which payment must be received to qualify for the early payment discount. Used to validate whether the early_payment_discount_amount was legitimately earned. Derived from invoice date and payment terms.']
  discount_taken_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the trade or cash discount applied at the time of payment allocation. Represents the discount actually utilized by the payer, which may differ from the discount offered. Used in AR reconciliation and discount expense reporting.']
  early_payment_discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the early payment discount earned by the payer for settling the invoice before the discount due date. Distinct from standard trade discounts; tied to payment terms such as 2/10 Net 30 common in agricultural supply chain billing.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the base/functional currency at the time of allocation. Sourced from the ERP system\'s daily exchange rate table. Required for IFRS 21 and GAAP ASC 830 compliance.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger (GL) account code to which this payment allocation is posted for revenue recognition and AR clearing. Ensures proper accounting treatment per GAAP/IFRS chart of accounts. Sourced from SAP FI GL account determination or Oracle NetSuite account mapping.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment allocation is between entities within the same corporate group (intercompany transaction). Required for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements per GAAP ASC 810 and IFRS 10.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payment allocation record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to allocation status, amounts, or reconciliation state. Required for data lineage and SOX audit trail.']
  netting_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the intercompany or counterparty netting arrangement when allocation_type is \'netting\'. Used in agricultural supply chain scenarios where a company is both buyer and seller to the same counterparty (e.g., grain merchandiser netting payables against receivables).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the AR specialist regarding this payment allocation. May include details about partial payment agreements, dispute resolutions, weight-ticket discrepancies, or special handling instructions for seasonal payment arrangements.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting period posting date for this allocation in the general ledger (GL). May differ from the allocation date when period-end adjustments are made. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and AR aging per GAAP/IFRS.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the bank reconciliation matching for this allocation: unreconciled (not yet matched to bank statement), matched (confirmed against bank statement), partially_matched (partial bank confirmation), exception (discrepancy requiring investigation), cleared (fully reconciled and closed). Essential for month-end close and cash management in agricultural operations.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment allocation was reversed. Populated only when allocation_status is \'reversed\'. Used for AR aging recalculation and audit trail documentation.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for reversing this payment allocation (e.g., returned check, bank rejection, incorrect application, duplicate payment). Populated only when allocation_status is \'reversed\'. Required for audit trail and AR dispute management.']
  value_date date [note: 'The bank value date on which funds were credited or debited, used for cash flow reporting and bank reconciliation. May differ from the posting date in agricultural cooperative payment cycles and commodity settlement workflows.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between the invoice amount and the payment allocated, representing unresolved discrepancies such as pricing disputes, weight-ticket measurement differences, or deduction claims. Positive value indicates underpayment; negative indicates overpayment. Critical for weight-ticket settlement reconciliation in grain and commodity billing.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount of the invoice balance written off as uncollectable or as an approved variance adjustment. Used in bad debt management, small balance write-offs, and weight-ticket variance clearance in commodity billing workflows.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking payments to specific invoices and invoice lines, enabling partial payment tracking, multi-invoice settlement, and split-payment reconciliation. Captures allocation date, allocated amount, discount taken, early payment discount, write-off amount, allocation type (full/partial/advance/credit_offset/netting), reconciliation status, and variance amount. Critical for weight-ticket settlement reconciliation, commodity contract payment matching, and cooperative patronage distribution application in agricultural billing workflows.'
}

Table invoice.weight_ticket {
  weight_ticket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the weight ticket record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.bill_of_lading. Business justification: BOL-to-weight-ticket reconciliation is a core grain elevator settlement process — the BOL weight is matched against scale weight for settlement calculation. Direct link supports this reconciliation, s']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Weight tickets at grain elevators are always commodity-specific — the commodity determines grading standards, moisture adjustment tables, and settlement price basis. A normalized commodity_id replaces']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: FSMA traceability and grain settlement: weight tickets are the source document for commodity lot creation at grain elevators. Direct FK enables lot-level settlement reporting, quality-to-payment trace']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Weight ticket processing costs (scale operations, grading labor) are tracked by cost center at the elevator/facility level. Agriculture operations require cost center assignment on weight tickets for ']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: Weight tickets are generated at the scale when a delivery arrives at the elevator or processing facility. Direct FK to delivery_order is a fundamental agriculture operations link — every weight ticket']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Certified scale operator license or employee ID of the individual who performed the weighing. Required for legal defensibility of certified weights under state weights-and-measures law and USDA FGIS standards.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving facility, grain elevator, or livestock scale facility where the commodity was weighed. Links to the facility master for location, license, and operator certification details.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Weight tickets record grain deliveries from specific farm operations. Linking weight_ticket to farm_unit enables producer-level delivery aggregation for settlement statements, FSA reporting, crop insu']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating field or farm unit from which the commodity was harvested. Enables field-level yield reconciliation, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, and precision agriculture analytics linking weight tickets to yield records.']
  field_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Harvest field operations produce grain loads weighed at the elevator. Linking weight tickets to the originating harvest field operation enables farm-to-elevator traceability, yield reconciliation (GPS']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Weight tickets record the USDA grade assigned at commodity intake — this grade directly determines price adjustments and settlement amounts. A grading_standard_id FK normalizes the denormalized usda_g']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Weight tickets in grain receiving are paired with USDA or third-party quality inspections. weight_ticket.inspection_certificate_number is a denormalized text field; a FK to inspection_id enables prope']
  inventory_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Grain settlement reconciliation: weight tickets are the scale document for commodity delivery; linking to goods_receipt enables FSMA lot traceability, GRN-to-ticket matching for AP settlement, and inv']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Grain intake process: every weight ticket at delivery has a simultaneous lab sample taken from the load. This direct link enables traceability from delivery ticket to sample results for settlement cal']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: FSMA requires lot-level traceability from field to scale to invoice. Linking weight_ticket to lot_trace connects the physical weighing event to the traceable lot record, enabling lot-level settlement ']
  primary_weight_asset_id varchar [note: 'License plate number or fleet identifier of the truck or vehicle delivering the commodity. Used for tare weight lookup, driver verification, and logistics traceability per FSMA Traceability Rule.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Grain elevator weight tickets are the primary revenue recognition event; assigning them to profit centers enables facility/division-level revenue reporting. Agriculture domain experts expect weight ti']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Weight tickets in grain procurement are generated against a specific PO governing delivery terms, price, and quantity. The plain `purchase_order_number` text column is a denormalization. This FK enabl']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Bulk seed delivery weight tickets require variety reference for seed certification compliance, variety purity verification at point of delivery, and trait royalty calculation at receipt. Seed warehous']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: In grain elevator operations, weight tickets are processed during specific shifts. Linking weight_ticket to shift supports shift-based throughput reporting, labor cost allocation per delivery, and aud']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: In ag grain operations, weight tickets are generated for inbound truck deliveries that correspond to shipment records. Linking weight_ticket to shipment supports shipment-to-settlement reconciliation,']
  transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.transport_order. Business justification: Weight tickets at grain elevators are generated when a transport order delivers grain. Direct link supports transport order reconciliation against weight tickets for freight billing, load confirmation']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Weight tickets at grain elevators identify the delivering vendor/producer. This link enables vendor delivery tracking, producer settlement reconciliation, and on-time delivery reporting. Agriculture d']
  broken_kernels_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of broken corn kernels and foreign material (BCFM) in a corn sample, as defined by USDA FGIS grading standards. A key grade factor for corn that drives price discounts and grade assignment.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the commodity category. Drives applicable grading standards, inspection protocols, and settlement calculation rules (e.g., grain uses bushel-based pricing; livestock uses CWT-based pricing).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the weight ticket record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA MM or Granular Farm Management). Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Required for multi-currency operations and IFRS financial reporting.']
  damage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of grain kernels that are heat-damaged, mold-damaged, sprouted, or otherwise damaged as determined by USDA FGIS inspection. A primary grade factor affecting commodity grade assignment and price discounts.']
  dockage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the gross sample weight consisting of material other than the grain (weed seeds, chaff, straw, sand) that passes through a prescribed sieve. Deducted from net weight before pricing per USDA FGIS grading standards.']
  driver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the truck driver delivering the commodity. Required for chain-of-custody documentation, FSMA traceability, and facility access records.']
  foreign_material_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the grain sample consisting of foreign material (non-grain matter that does not pass through the dockage sieve). A grade-determining factor per USDA FGIS standards that affects commodity grade and price discounts.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total certified scale weight of the loaded vehicle (truck/trailer plus commodity) in kilograms as recorded on the certified scale. Foundational quantity for net weight calculation per state weights-and-measures law.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity delivered on this weight ticket is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO). Required for identity preservation programs, non-GMO certification, and international trade compliance (EU GMO labeling regulations).']
  is_organic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). Drives premium pricing, segregated storage requirements, and organic certificate documentation requirements.']
  is_split_load boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this weight ticket represents a split-load delivery where a single truck carries commodity from multiple fields, farms, or ownership lots. When True, multiple weight tickets may share the same truck/trailer ID and require proportional allocation.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Moisture content of the grain or oilseed sample expressed as a percentage of total weight, measured at time of delivery. Used to calculate moisture dockage/shrink adjustments to net weight and price per USDA FGIS grading standards. Null for livestock.']
  net_weight_bu decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net commodity weight converted to bushels (BU) using USDA standard test weight per bushel for the specific commodity (e.g., 56 lbs/bu for corn, 60 lbs/bu for wheat). Used for grain pricing, settlement, and WASDE reporting. Null for livestock and cotton.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Certified net commodity weight in kilograms, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight. This is the authoritative quantity used for invoice line reconciliation, settlement calculations, and USDA FGIS reporting. Stored as a certified value from the scale system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for scale operator or receiving clerk notes regarding special conditions, quality observations, load discrepancies, or dispute details associated with this weight ticket. Supports exception handling and dispute resolution.']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Contracted or market price per unit of measure (per bushel for grain, per CWT for livestock, per pound for cotton) applied to this weight ticket for settlement calculation. Sourced from the applicable sales order or commodity contract.']
  price_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure to which the price per unit applies. BU = Bushel (grain); CWT = Hundredweight (livestock); MT = Metric Ton; LB = Pound (cotton); KG = Kilogram. Drives settlement amount calculation.']
  scale_datetime timestamp [note: 'The certified date and time the commodity was weighed on the certified scale. This is the principal business event timestamp and the authoritative quantity determination moment per state weights-and-measures law. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  settlement_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross settlement amount payable to the vendor/grower for this weight ticket, calculated as adjusted net weight multiplied by price per unit before deductions. Represents the principal monetary value of this transaction for AR/AP reconciliation.']
  tare_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Certified scale weight of the empty vehicle (truck/trailer without commodity) in kilograms. Subtracted from gross weight to determine net commodity weight. May be captured on inbound or outbound weigh.']
  test_weight_lbs_per_bu decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured weight per bushel of the grain sample in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu), indicating grain density and quality. Used as a grade factor per USDA FGIS standards (e.g., minimum 56 lbs/bu for No. 2 Yellow Corn). Null for non-grain commodities.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable certified scale ticket number assigned by the elevator or receiving facility. Used for reconciliation against invoice lines, grain settlement documents, and USDA FGIS inspection records. Unique within a facility.']
  ticket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the weight ticket. \'draft\' = captured but not yet certified; \'open\' = certified and awaiting settlement; \'settled\' = reconciled to an invoice line and payment; \'voided\' = cancelled/corrected; \'disputed\' = under quality or quantity dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the weight ticket record. Tracks corrections, grade adjustments, or settlement status changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Certified scale weight records for commodity deliveries (grain, oilseed, cotton, livestock) serving as the authoritative quantity source for invoice line reconciliation. Captures ticket number, scale date/time, commodity type, gross weight, tare weight, net weight, moisture content, test weight, dockage percentage, grade factor adjustments (broken kernels, foreign material, damage), scale operator certification, facility/elevator location, truck/trailer ID, driver, and settlement status. Must reconcile to invoice lines per USDA FGIS grain inspection standards and state weights-and-measures regulations. Supports split-load tickets and multi-commodity loads.'
}

Table invoice.billing_account {
  billing_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_account']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master customer entity in the CRM or ERP customer master. Links the AR billing account to the corresponding buyer record in Salesforce CRM or SAP SD customer master.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Producer billing accounts are linked to specific bank accounts for ACH/direct deposit of settlement payments. billing_account.bank_account_ref is a denormalized plain attribute; proper FK to finance.b']
  billing_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.account']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Billing accounts (AR accounts) are maintained by a specific legal entity. In multi-entity agriculture operations, AR aging reports by legal entity, credit limit management by entity, and statutory AR ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or sales representative responsible for managing this AR billing account relationship. Used for collections escalation routing and customer relationship management in Salesforce CRM.']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: billing_account.payment_terms_code is a denormalized string reference to the payment_term reference table. The billing account master should FK to the authoritative payment_term record to enforce cons']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Billing accounts in agriculture are segmented by profit center (grain merchandising, agronomy services, livestock) for AR aging and credit management reporting by division. Profit center on billing ac']
  account_close_date date [note: 'Date on which the AR billing account was closed or deactivated. Null for active accounts. Used for account lifecycle reporting and historical AR analysis.']
  account_open_date date [note: 'Date on which the AR billing account was formally opened and activated in the AR subledger. Represents the effective start of the financial relationship between the agricultural enterprise and the buyer.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the AR billing account. \'active\' indicates normal billing operations; \'on_hold\' indicates credit hold applied; \'pending_review\' indicates credit review in progress; \'closed\' indicates account terminated.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing account by customer category relevant to agricultural commerce. Drives applicable payment terms, tax treatment, and regulatory reporting (e.g., COOL, USDA export documentation).']
  aging_1_30_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices 1 to 30 days past due, expressed in the account currency. Used in AR aging reports for collections prioritization and expected credit loss provisioning.']
  aging_31_60_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices 31 to 60 days past due, expressed in the account currency. Elevated aging in this bucket may trigger credit review or collection escalation for agricultural buyers.']
  aging_61_90_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices 61 to 90 days past due, expressed in the account currency. Accounts in this bucket are typically escalated to senior credit management and may trigger collateral enforcement actions.']
  aging_91_120_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices 91 to 120 days past due, expressed in the account currency. Balances in this bucket are typically subject to formal demand letters and potential write-off evaluation.']
  aging_current_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices not yet past due (within current payment terms), expressed in the account currency. Part of the standard AR aging bucket analysis used for cash flow forecasting and credit management.']
  aging_over_120_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding AR balance for invoices more than 120 days past due, expressed in the account currency. Represents the highest-risk aging bucket; balances here are evaluated for bad debt write-off per GAAP ASC 310 and IFRS 9 ECL provisioning.']
  ar_account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric AR account number assigned to the billing customer in the SAP FI-AR subledger or Oracle NetSuite AR module. Used on invoices, statements, and remittance advice for buyer identification.']
  avg_days_to_pay decimal(6,2) [note: 'Rolling average number of days from invoice date to payment receipt for this AR account, calculated over the trailing 12-month period. Key KPI for credit risk assessment and payment behavior benchmarking in agricultural commodity sales.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the billing customer\'s invoicing address. Used on invoice documents, statements, and regulatory filings including USDA and FDA traceability records.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing customer\'s invoicing address. Required for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance reporting.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing customer\'s invoicing address (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'BRA\'). Drives export documentation requirements, COOL labeling, and applicable tax/VAT treatment for international agricultural trade.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing customer\'s invoicing address. Used for tax rate determination, geographic sales analysis, and invoice document generation.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing customer\'s invoicing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, state-level agricultural regulatory reporting, and USDA program eligibility.']
  collateral_type varchar [note: 'Type of collateral securing the AR account credit exposure. Agricultural-specific collateral types include crop liens (UCC Article 9 security interest in standing or harvested crops) and equipment liens. Drives credit limit and risk classification decisions.']
  collateral_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Appraised or estimated monetary value of the collateral securing the AR account, expressed in the account currency. Used in credit exposure calculations and expected credit loss (ECL) modeling per IFRS 9.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AR billing account record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX internal control requirements.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR account is currently under a credit hold, blocking new sales orders from being processed. When True, SAP SD order creation is blocked pending credit resolution.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the AR account is placed on credit hold. Supports credit management workflow and audit trail for SOX compliance. \'collateral_expired\' is specific to agricultural crop lien or letter of credit expiry.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding AR balance authorized for this billing account, expressed in the account currency. Enforced during sales order creation in SAP SD credit management. Reviewed periodically based on crop cycle seasonality and buyer financial health.']
  credit_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled or last-completed date for periodic credit review of this AR account. Agricultural accounts are typically reviewed annually or after significant crop season events, commodity price swings, or weather-related losses.']
  credit_risk_category varchar [note: 'Internal credit risk classification assigned to the billing account based on financial analysis, payment history, and market conditions. \'watch\' indicates accounts under heightened monitoring due to commodity price volatility or crop failure risk.']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numeric credit score assigned to the billing account based on payment history, financial statements, and agricultural market exposure. Used to determine credit limit and payment terms. Typically sourced from internal scoring model or third-party credit bureau.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the AR account (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'BRL\'). All monetary amounts on this account are denominated in this currency. Supports multi-currency agricultural export transactions.']
  current_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding accounts receivable balance for this billing account as of the last subledger posting date, expressed in the account currency. Represents the sum of all open invoice amounts net of payments and credit memos.']
  dispute_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding AR balance currently under formal dispute by the customer, expressed in the account currency. Disputed amounts are excluded from collections activity and tracked separately per GAAP revenue recognition guidance. Common in agricultural weight-ticket reconciliation disputes.']
  gl_reconciliation_account varchar [note: 'SAP GL reconciliation account number to which this AR subledger account posts. Ensures the AR subledger balance reconciles to the GL trade receivables account per GAAP ASC 310 and SOX internal controls.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the most recent payment received and posted against this AR account, in the account currency. Supports cash application reconciliation and payment pattern analysis.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received and posted against this AR account. Used in collections analysis, average days to pay calculation, and credit risk monitoring.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred or contracted payment method for this AR account. \'commodity_offset\' is an agricultural-specific method where payment is settled via commodity delivery or grain bank offset rather than cash.']
  seasonal_terms_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this AR account is subject to seasonal payment terms aligned to agricultural harvest cycles (e.g., deferred payment until post-harvest). When True, standard payment terms are overridden by the seasonal schedule defined in the payment terms code.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Federal or state tax identification number (EIN, TIN, or VAT number) for the billing customer entity. Required for 1099 reporting, USDA program payments, and international trade documentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AR billing account record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable account master for each billing customer entity, representing the financial relationship between the agricultural enterprise and its buyers. Captures AR account number, customer reference, credit limit amount, credit risk category (low/medium/high/watch), credit scoring basis, collateral type (crop lien, equipment lien, letter of credit, surety bond), collateral value, credit review date, credit hold status, credit hold reason, current balance, aging buckets (current/30/60/90/120+ days), payment terms code, currency, last payment date, average days to pay, dispute balance, and account manager. Integrates with SAP FI-AR subledger and Oracle NetSuite AR module.'
}

Table invoice.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each payment term configuration record in the agricultural billing reference master.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payment terms define early payment discount GL accounts for posting cash discounts taken. payment_term.gl_account_code is a denormalized plain attribute; proper FK to finance.gl_account enforces refer']
  applicable_commodity_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of commodity categories to which this payment term is applicable (e.g., \'grain,oilseed\', \'livestock\', \'produce\', \'all\'). Supports commodity-specific settlement schedules such as deferred grain payment programs and livestock settlement windows. Null indicates applicability to all commodity types.']
  applicable_company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code(s) for which this payment term is valid. Comma-separated if applicable to multiple company codes. Null indicates applicability across all company codes. Supports multi-entity agricultural enterprise structures.']
  applicable_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment classification for which this payment term is designed. Allows differentiated term offerings for growers, cooperatives, distributors, and processors within the agricultural supply chain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|grower|cooperative|distributor|processor|retailer|export — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_sales_org varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sales organization code(s) for which this payment term is valid. Comma-separated if applicable to multiple sales organizations. Null indicates global applicability across all sales organizations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the finance or credit manager who approved this payment term configuration for use. Supports SOX internal controls over financial reporting and segregation of duties requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this payment term configuration was formally approved by the authorized finance or credit manager. Supports SOX audit trail and change management documentation.']
  baseline_date_type varchar [note: 'Defines the reference date from which net due days and discount days are calculated. Agricultural-specific options include weight_ticket_date (grain elevator settlements) and harvest_completion_date (post-harvest deferred programs). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment term baseline date configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this payment term configuration record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  credit_limit_required decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum approved credit limit (in functional currency) that a customer must hold to be eligible for this payment term. Supports credit risk management for extended or deferred agricultural payment programs. Null if no minimum credit limit is required.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this payment term\'s monetary thresholds and discount calculations are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency agricultural export transactions.']
  deferral_max_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days that payment may be deferred from the invoice date under a seasonal deferral program, regardless of when the trigger event occurs. Caps the deferral window to manage credit exposure. Null when is_seasonal_deferral is false.']
  deferral_trigger_event varchar [note: 'The business event that triggers the start of the payment window for seasonally deferred terms. For example, \'harvest_completion\' starts the net due days clock after crop harvest is recorded. Null when is_seasonal_deferral is false.']
  discount_days_1 varchar [note: 'Number of days from the baseline date within which payment must be received to qualify for the first-tier early payment discount percentage. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD1T field. Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  discount_days_2 varchar [note: 'Number of days from the baseline date within which payment must be received to qualify for the second-tier early payment discount. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD2T field. Null if no second discount tier applies.']
  discount_percent_1 decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the invoice amount if payment is received within discount_days_1. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = 2%). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD1P field. Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  discount_percent_2 decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the invoice amount if payment is received within discount_days_2 but after discount_days_1. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD2P field. Null if no second discount tier applies.']
  dunning_procedure_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA dunning procedure code assigned to this payment term, controlling the escalation schedule for overdue AR notices. Seasonal agricultural terms may use specialized dunning procedures that account for harvest timing before escalating collection actions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this payment term configuration becomes valid and may be applied to new invoices, sales orders, or purchase orders. Supports seasonal term activation aligned with crop or livestock cycles.']
  erp_term_key varchar [note: 'Native payment term key as stored in the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA ZTERM field, Oracle NetSuite term ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this payment term configuration is no longer valid for assignment to new transactions. Null indicates an open-ended term with no planned expiration. Supports seasonal program sunset dates.']
  fsma_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment term configuration has been reviewed and confirmed compliant with FSMA supplier payment provisions, particularly for produce safety rule supplier agreements and preventive controls.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of installment payments required under this term when is_installment is true. For example, 3 for a three-payment seasonal schedule. Null when is_installment is false.']
  installment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines how installment amounts are distributed across the payment schedule. \'harvest_triggered\' supports agricultural deferred programs where installment timing is tied to crop harvest completion events. Null when is_installment is false.']
  is_installment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment term requires payment in multiple installments rather than a single lump sum. When true, installment_count and installment_schedule_type define the installment structure. Common for large agricultural equipment purchases and seasonal input financing.']
  is_seasonal_deferral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment term includes a seasonal deferral provision, allowing payment to be deferred to a post-harvest or post-season window. Characteristic of agricultural deferred grain payment programs and spring input financing.']
  late_payment_grace_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days beyond the due date before late payment penalties begin to accrue. Provides a grace window before penalty charges are triggered. Null if no grace period applies.']
  late_payment_penalty_percent decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual percentage rate applied to overdue invoice balances as a late payment penalty or interest charge. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0150 = 1.5% per annum). Null if no late payment penalty applies under this term.']
  net_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from the invoice date (or baseline date) by which full payment is due. For example, 30 for Net 30 terms. Core field in SAP S/4HANA payment term configuration (ZBD3T). Drives AR due date calculation.']
  netsuite_term_code varchar [note: 'Payment term identifier as defined in Oracle NetSuite GL/AR module. Populated when the term originates from or is synchronized with NetSuite. Supports dual-ERP environments where SAP S/4HANA and Oracle NetSuite coexist.']
  payment_method_restriction varchar [note: 'Restricts the allowable payment methods under this term. For example, large grain settlement programs may require ACH or wire transfer only. \'none\' indicates no restriction. Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment method configuration.']
  price_fixation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity price must be fixed (priced) before the payment due date clock starts. Relevant for deferred pricing grain contracts (basis contracts, hedge-to-arrive) where the final invoice amount is not determined at delivery.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the payment term configuration, as defined in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD module (e.g., NET30, DEFGRAIN90, LSTK45). Used on invoices, sales orders, and purchase orders for term lookup.']
  term_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the payment term, including applicable conditions, seasonal rules, commodity restrictions, and any special settlement provisions relevant to agricultural billing scenarios.']
  term_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the payment term configuration (e.g., \'Net 30 Days\', \'Deferred Grain Payment 90 Days Post-Harvest\', \'Livestock Settlement 14 Days\'). Displayed on customer-facing documents and AR aging reports.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment term configuration. Inactive or deprecated terms are retained for historical invoice reference but cannot be assigned to new transactions.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment term by its structural type. Supports agricultural-specific types including deferred seasonal programs (post-harvest grain payment windows), commodity-price-based settlement, and livestock consignment settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_net|early_payment_discount|deferred_seasonal|installment|consignment_settlement|commodity_price_based|prepayment — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this payment term configuration record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  weight_ticket_reconciliation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment under this term requires reconciliation against a weight ticket (scale ticket) before the invoice due date is triggered. Critical for grain elevator and commodity settlement billing scenarios where final payment amount depends on certified weight.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining payment term configurations applicable to agricultural billing including standard net terms, seasonal deferred payment programs, and commodity-specific settlement schedules. Captures term code, term description, net days, discount days, early payment discount percentage, seasonal deferral rules (e.g., post-harvest payment windows), installment schedule flag, installment count, and applicable commodity types. Supports agricultural-specific terms such as deferred grain payment programs and livestock settlement windows.'
}

Table invoice.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for dispute']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural customer (buyer, distributor, food manufacturer, or retailer) who raised the dispute against the invoice.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.bill_of_lading. Business justification: BOL is the primary shipping document referenced in agriculture billing disputes (seal discrepancies, weight variances, delivery condition). Direct dispute→BOL link supports BOL-level dispute resolutio']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_account. Business justification: Disputes are raised against invoices by agricultural customers and must be linked to the billing_account AR master for dispute balance tracking (billing_account.dispute_balance field). dispute already']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Invoice disputes in commodity agriculture are frequently triggered by COA discrepancies (grade claimed vs invoiced, MRL exceedance). dispute.coa_reference is a denormalized text field; a FK to coa_id ']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Disputes in ag operations frequently involve commodity-specific issues (MRL exceedances, grade disputes, quality rejections). A commodity_id FK enables commodity-level dispute analytics, supports FSMA']
  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Direct FK replaces denormalized commodity_lot_reference, enabling structured d']

In multi-entity agriculture operations, dispute reporting by legal entity supports credit risk management, collections performance reporting, and finan']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Agriculture disputes (MRL exceedances, short-weight claims, quality rejections) require direct linkage to the customer\'s FSMA point of contact or HACCP coordinator for regulatory notification and SLA ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying sales or commodity purchase contract governing the disputed transaction, used for contract terms verification during dispute resolution.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Dispute resolution costs (labor, testing, logistics) are allocated to cost centers. dispute.cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute; proper FK to finance.cost_center enforces referential in']
  delivery_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.delivery_order. Business justification: Agriculture disputes commonly originate from delivery discrepancies — short weight, rejected loads, quality failures at delivery point. Direct FK to delivery_order links the dispute to the triggering ']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal employee or team responsible for investigating and resolving the dispute. Typically an AR specialist, commodity quality analyst, or compliance officer depending on dispute type.']

  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. A grading_standard_id FK provides the formal standard reference for dispute reso']

Business justification: Invoice disputes in commodity agriculture are directly triggered by specific inspection findings (MRL exceedance, grade failure, foreign material). A FK from dispute to inspection_finding_id enables f']
  inventory_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Dispute origination traceability: quality disputes in grain agriculture originate from quality determinations made at goods receipt. Direct FK enables dispute resolution teams to access original recei']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice against which this dispute has been raised. Links to the invoice header record in the accounts receivable (AR) ledger.']

Business justification: Dispute resolution write-offs and settlement adjustments generate journal entries for GL posting. Agriculture domain experts expect disputes to link to their resolution journal entry for audit trail, ']
  lab_sample_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Quality dispute resolution process: when a buyer disputes commodity quality (moisture, residues, mycotoxins), the original lab sample is referenced for re-testing or chain-of-custody verification. Dis']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Ag invoice disputes most commonly arise from lot-level quality issues: grade disputes, moisture discrepancies, MRL exceedances, or organic certification failures. Linking dispute to lot_trace enables ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold.
