  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Agriculture disputes (quality, weight, grade) must be tracked by profit center for segment-level dispute reporting and financial exposure analysis. Management reporting requires profit-center-level di']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: Quarantine holds on commodity lots (MRL exceedances, quality failures) directly trigger invoice disputes in grain trading. Linking dispute to quarantine_hold supports dispute resolution workflows and ']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: dispute has sales_order_reference as a plain denormalized text field. A proper FK to sales.order enables order-level dispute tracking, SLA reporting by order, and resolution workflows — essential for ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Disputes in agriculture arise from shipment-level issues (short weight, temperature excursion, damaged goods). Direct dispute→shipment link supports shipment dispute resolution workflows, carrier clai']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: dispute.weight_ticket_reference is a denormalized string reference to a weight ticket. Agricultural billing disputes frequently originate from disagreements over certified scale weights, moisture read']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the audit trail creation marker for SOX compliance and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this dispute record (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Supports multi-currency agricultural commodity trading.']
  customer_claim_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The specific monetary amount the customer is claiming as a credit, refund, or price adjustment. May differ from the disputed_amount if the customer is requesting a specific settlement figure based on their own calculations (e.g., weight-ticket reconciliation or commodity grade re-pricing).']
  days_to_resolve varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed between dispute_date and resolution_date. Populated upon resolution. Used for KPI reporting on dispute resolution cycle time and SLA compliance tracking.']
  dispute_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the customer formally raised the dispute. This is the principal business event date used for aging calculations, SLA tracking, and regulatory reporting timelines.']
  dispute_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text narrative provided by the customer or AR team describing the nature of the dispute, including specific claims, supporting evidence references, and any relevant field or delivery observations.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable unique identifier for the dispute, used in customer communications, USDA grain grading arbitration filings, and audit trails. Format: DSP-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the dispute. \'open\' = newly filed; \'under_review\' = assigned and being investigated; \'resolved\' = final determination reached; \'escalated\' = elevated to senior arbitration or regulatory body; \'withdrawn\' = customer retracted the claim.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for the dispute. Common agricultural billing dispute types include: price_discrepancy, weight_variance, quality_rejection, grade_dispute, mrl_exceedance_deduction, duplicate_billing, contract_terms_disagreement, short_shipment, packaging_defect. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price_discrepancy|weight_variance|quality_rejection|grade_dispute|mrl_exceedance_deduction|duplicate_billing|contract_terms_disagreement|short_shipment|packaging_defect — promote to reference product]']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the invoice amount that the customer is formally contesting. May equal the full invoice amount (full dispute) or a subset (partial dispute) such as a weight variance deduction or MRL exceedance penalty.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent escalation action was taken. Null if the dispute has never been escalated. Used for escalation aging and regulatory timeline compliance.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of how many times the dispute has been escalated to a higher authority. Level 0 = not escalated; Level 1 = escalated to senior AR manager; Level 2 = escalated to legal/compliance; Level 3 = escalated to external arbitration (e.g., USDA GIPSA commodity arbitration).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the dispute was escalated, such as failure to reach agreement within SLA, regulatory involvement required, or high-value commodity arbitration triggered.']
  invoice_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total billed amount on the original invoice as issued to the customer, captured at the time of dispute initiation for reference and variance calculation.']
  is_partial_dispute boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute covers only a portion of the invoice amount (True) or the full invoice amount (False). Drives AR aging treatment and partial payment clearing logic in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.']
  is_regulatory_dispute boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the dispute involves a regulatory compliance dimension (e.g., FSMA traceability violation, EPA MRL exceedance, USDA grade certification challenge, COOL labeling dispute). Regulatory disputes trigger mandatory escalation and compliance reporting workflows.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute, influencing reviewer assignment and resolution SLA targets. \'critical\' is typically reserved for disputes involving regulatory violations (e.g., MRL exceedance, FSMA non-compliance) or high-value commodity contracts.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the dispute was formally closed and a resolution determination was recorded. Null if the dispute is still open or under review. Used for SLA compliance measurement and AR aging reports.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the assigned reviewer documenting the investigation findings, evidence reviewed (weight tickets, COA, grade certificates), and the rationale for the resolution determination.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'The method by which the dispute was resolved. \'credit_issued\' = credit memo applied to AR; \'price_adjusted\' = invoice repriced per commodity grade or weight; \'claim_denied\' = dispute rejected after review; \'partial_settlement\' = negotiated partial credit; \'replacement_shipment\' = goods replaced; \'contract_amended\' = contract terms updated.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final agreed monetary settlement amount upon resolution of the dispute. Populated only when dispute_status = \'resolved\'. May reflect a full credit, partial credit, price adjustment, or zero (claim denied).']
  sla_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which the dispute must be resolved per internal Service Level Agreement (SLA) or contractual dispute resolution terms. Calculated from dispute_date plus the applicable SLA window based on dispute_type and priority_level.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this dispute record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks formal disputes raised by agricultural customers against issued invoices, managing the dispute lifecycle from initiation through resolution. Captures dispute number, disputed invoice reference, dispute date, dispute type (price discrepancy, weight variance, quality rejection, grade dispute, MRL exceedance deduction, duplicate billing, contract terms disagreement), disputed amount, customer claim amount, dispute status (open/under_review/resolved/escalated/withdrawn), resolution date, resolution type (credit issued, price adjusted, claim denied, partial settlement), adjusted settlement amount, assigned reviewer, and escalation history. Supports USDA grain grading dispute workflows and commodity quality arbitration processes.'
}

Table invoice.billing_schedule {
  billing_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the billing schedule record. Primary key for the billing_schedule data product.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or counterparty to whom recurring or milestone-based invoices will be issued under this billing schedule.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_account. Business justification: billing_schedule defines recurring billing for agricultural contracts but currently only references customer.account (cross-domain) via account_id. It is missing a direct FK to the in-domain billing_a']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural commodity (e.g., grain, livestock, crop inputs) associated with this billing schedule, enabling commodity-based pricing and weight-ticket reconciliation.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Billing schedules for recurring storage, handling, or service contracts in agriculture are configured for a specific legal entity. Company code linkage supports entity-specific billing configuration, ']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the long-term agricultural contract (grain storage agreement, crop input supply contract, livestock management service agreement) that this billing schedule governs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Recurring billing schedules for storage fees, handling charges, and drying fees in agriculture are tied to specific facilities. Direct link supports facility-based recurring billing automation, storag']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Farm-unit-level billing schedules drive irrigation district assessments, drainage fees, and FSA program payment cycles. Linking billing_schedule to farm_unit enables farm-unit profitability reporting ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: invoice.billing_schedule carries a gl_account_code text field that is a denormalized reference to finance.gl_account. Billing schedules drive revenue recognition postings to specific GL accounts; a pr']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.lease. Business justification: Agricultural lease payments follow defined schedules (annual, semi-annual cash rent). Linking billing_schedule to the governing lease enables automated lease payment generation, renewal notice trigger']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: billing_schedule.payment_terms_code is a denormalized string reference to the payment_term reference table. Billing schedules for long-term agricultural contracts must reference the authoritative paym']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed trait licensing and Plant Variety Protection (PVP) royalty billing schedules are structured per variety. Licensor reporting and IP compliance tracking require variety-level billing schedule refer']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Grain elevator storage fee billing schedules are explicitly tied to the physical storage location (bin, silo, warehouse). Linking billing_schedule to storage_location enables automated storage fee inv']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this billing schedule was formally approved and authorized for use. Supports audit trail and financial controls compliance.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether each billing run under this schedule requires explicit management approval before the invoice is released to the customer. Supports internal controls and SOX compliance for material agricultural contracts.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who authorized this billing schedule for activation. Required for audit trail and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  auto_invoice_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether invoices are automatically generated and posted when the billing date or milestone is reached (True), or whether manual review and approval is required before invoice creation (False).']
  billing_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount to be invoiced per billing cycle. Used when the billing amount is predetermined (e.g., fixed storage fee per month). Null when billing_formula is used instead for variable or commodity-based pricing.']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Day of the month (1–31) on which recurring invoices are generated for monthly or quarterly schedules. For example, a value of 1 means invoices are generated on the first of each billing period month.']
  billing_formula varchar [note: 'Expression or formula used to calculate the invoice amount dynamically, supporting commodity-based pricing (e.g., \'quantity_mt * market_price_per_mt\', \'weight_cwt * rate_per_cwt\'). Used when billing_amount is not fixed. Supports NPK input pricing, weight-ticket reconciliation, and variable rate billing.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated under this schedule. Supports agricultural-specific cycles including seasonal (aligned with crop harvest or livestock production calendars) and milestone-based (triggered by specific agricultural events). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weekly|monthly|quarterly|semi_annual|annual|seasonal|milestone — promote to reference product]']
  billing_month_of_year varchar [note: 'Month of the year (1–12) on which annual or seasonal invoices are generated. Supports crop harvest calendar alignment (e.g., month 10 for fall harvest billing) and livestock production cycle billing.']
  billing_tolerance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before or after the scheduled billing date within which invoice generation is still considered on-schedule. Accommodates agricultural operational variability such as harvest delays or weather-related disruptions.']
  completed_occurrences varchar [note: 'Count of billing events that have been successfully executed (invoices generated and posted) under this schedule to date. Used to track schedule progress and remaining billing obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing schedule record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the billing amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency agricultural trade and international commodity contracts.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the billing amount for each invoice generated under this schedule. Supports volume-based, seasonal, or contractual discount arrangements common in agricultural supply chain agreements.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this billing schedule expires and no further invoices will be generated. Nullable for open-ended schedules. Aligns with contract expiry or crop season end.']
  last_billed_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent invoice was generated under this billing schedule. Used to track billing history and validate schedule adherence.']
  milestone_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the triggering milestone was confirmed as completed, authorizing invoice generation for milestone-based billing schedules.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Description of the agricultural business event or milestone that triggers invoice generation for milestone-type schedules (e.g., \'Harvest completion\', \'Grain delivery to elevator\', \'Livestock weaning\', \'Crop input delivery confirmed\').']
  next_billing_date date [note: 'The date on which the next invoice will be automatically generated or triggered under this schedule. Updated after each successful billing run.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or exceptions related to this billing schedule (e.g., \'Billing deferred pending USDA crop report\', \'Weight-ticket reconciliation required before invoicing\').']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue from invoices generated by this schedule per IFRS 15 / ASC 606 standards. \'point_in_time\' for delivery-based recognition; \'over_time\' for service contracts; \'milestone_based\' for performance obligation completion; \'proportional\' for percentage-of-completion.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this billing schedule, used for cross-system reference and communication with customers and partners (e.g., BSCHED-2024-001).']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the billing schedule (e.g., \'Annual Grain Storage Q1 Billing\', \'Seasonal Crop Input Monthly Installment\').']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the billing schedule. \'active\' indicates invoices are being generated; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily paused; \'completed\' indicates all billing milestones fulfilled; \'cancelled\' indicates terminated before completion; \'draft\' indicates not yet activated.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the billing schedule by its triggering mechanism. \'recurring\' for time-based periodic billing; \'milestone\' for event-triggered billing (e.g., harvest completion); \'one_time\' for single-occurrence billing; \'seasonal\' for crop or livestock production calendar-aligned billing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this billing schedule originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Oracle NetSuite, Granular Farm Management). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which this billing schedule becomes effective and the first billing cycle begins. Aligns with contract commencement or crop season start.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to invoices generated by this schedule, determining applicable tax rates and exemptions (e.g., agricultural tax exemptions, state sales tax codes). Integrates with SAP S/4HANA tax determination.']
  total_billing_occurrences varchar [note: 'Total number of billing events planned for this schedule over its full lifecycle. For example, a 12-month monthly schedule has 12 occurrences. Null for open-ended or milestone-only schedules.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this billing schedule record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Defines recurring or milestone-based billing schedules for long-term agricultural contracts including grain storage agreements, crop input supply contracts, and livestock management service agreements. Captures schedule code, billing frequency (weekly/monthly/quarterly/seasonal/milestone), next billing date, billing amount or formula, contract reference, commodity reference, billing start date, billing end date, auto-invoice flag, and schedule status. Supports seasonal billing cycles aligned with crop harvest and livestock production calendars.'
}

Table invoice.price_basis {
  price_basis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_basis']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Price basis records (futures exchange, basis amount, delivery specs) are inherently commodity-specific in ag trading. Linking to commodity_id enables basis-by-commodity reporting, forward contract pri']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Price basis in ag commodity trading is grade-specific — No. 2 Yellow Corn commands a different basis than No. 3. A grading_standard_id FK normalizes the denormalized grade_standard field and enables g']
  market_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.market_price. Business justification: A price basis record in grain trading references a specific market price source (exchange settlement, WASDE reference). Linking price_basis to market_price enables automated basis calculation, price f']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Commodity pricing configuration: price basis records define futures-based pricing for specific commodities; material_master is the authoritative commodity specification record (grade standards, moistu']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price basis records in grain trading are managed by profit center (trading desk, merchandising division). Linking price_basis to profit_center enables basis trading P&L reporting by division and suppo']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Commodity price basis (futures differential) is location-specific in grain trading — each elevator/storage location has its own basis. Linking price_basis to storage_location replaces denormalized del']
  applicable_contract_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of contract types for which this price basis is valid (e.g., \'basis_contract,hedge_to_arrive,forward_contract,spot_purchase\'). Restricts the use of this basis record to appropriate contract contexts in invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: basis_contract|hedge_to_arrive|forward_contract|spot_purchase|deferred_payment|minimum_price — promote to reference product]']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved this price basis record for use in invoice calculations. Required for SOX internal controls over financial reporting and GAAP revenue recognition governance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this price basis record was formally approved for use. Required for SOX audit trail and financial reporting controls. Null if the record is in pending or draft status.']
  basis_amount decimal(10,4) [note: 'The premium or discount applied over or under the futures contract price to arrive at the cash price, expressed in the currency unit per unit of measure (e.g., -0.15 USD/bushel represents a 15-cent under basis for corn). Negative values indicate a discount below futures; positive values indicate a premium above futures.']
  basis_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this price basis configuration (e.g., CORN-CBOT-DEC, SOY-CME-MAR). Used in contract references, invoice line items, and grain elevator systems to reference the applicable pricing basis.']
  basis_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the basis amount is denominated (e.g., USD for US domestic grain markets, CAD for Canadian operations). Ensures correct currency handling in multi-currency agricultural operations.']
  basis_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the price basis (e.g., \'December Corn CBOT Basis - Central Illinois\'). Used in reports, invoices, and contract documents for clear identification of the pricing basis applied.']
  basis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price basis record. Active records are eligible for invoice pricing; inactive/expired records are retained for historical reference and audit; superseded records have been replaced by a newer basis version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price basis record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record creation per SOX and GAAP financial reporting controls.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date from which this price basis record becomes active and eligible for use in invoice calculations. Basis records are date-sensitive as commodity basis levels change with market conditions and seasonal patterns.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The calendar date after which this price basis record is no longer valid for new invoice calculations. Null indicates an open-ended basis with no defined expiration. Aligns with futures contract expiration cycles and seasonal marketing periods.']
  formula_expression varchar [note: 'For formula-based pricing methods, the mathematical expression or rule used to calculate the final price (e.g., \'CBOT_CLOSE + BASIS + FREIGHT_ALLOWANCE - MOISTURE_DISCOUNT\'). Null for non-formula pricing methods. Stored as a string expression interpreted by the pricing engine.']
  freight_allowance decimal(10,4) [note: 'The freight cost component embedded in or deducted from the basis amount, expressed per unit of measure. Represents the transportation cost from the delivery location to the terminal market or export elevator. Used in FOB vs. CIF pricing reconciliation.']
  futures_contract_month varchar [note: 'The specific futures contract delivery month and year used as the pricing reference (e.g., DEC-2025, MAR-2026). Critical for grain and livestock invoice pricing to identify which futures contract price is applied in basis calculations.']
  futures_exchange varchar [note: 'The futures exchange whose contract price is used as the reference price for this basis (e.g., CBOT for corn/soybeans/wheat, CME for cattle/hogs, ICE for cotton). Null for spot_market or flat_price methods.']
  gmo_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price basis applies to GMO commodity (True) or non-GMO/identity-preserved commodity (False). Non-GMO commodities typically command a premium basis over standard GMO commodity prices, requiring separate basis records.']
  handling_charge decimal(10,4) [note: 'The per-unit elevator or handling fee included in or deducted from the basis calculation (e.g., 0.02 USD/BU for grain elevator handling). Represents the cost of receiving, storing, and loading grain at the delivery location.']
  max_quantity decimal(14,4) [note: 'The maximum commodity quantity (in the specified unit of measure) for which this basis applies. Basis records may be quantity-banded for volume pricing tiers. Null indicates no maximum quantity restriction.']
  min_quantity decimal(14,4) [note: 'The minimum commodity quantity (in the specified unit of measure) required for this basis to apply. Basis levels may differ for small lots vs. full truckload or barge quantities. Null indicates no minimum quantity restriction.']
  moisture_adjustment_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The per-unit price adjustment applied per percentage point of moisture above or below the standard moisture level (e.g., -0.0035 USD/BU per 1% moisture above 15.5% for corn). Used in weight-ticket reconciliation and invoice adjustments for grain quality discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this price basis record (e.g., \'Applies only to identity-preserved white corn\', \'Subject to CBOT limit move provisions\'). Supports operational transparency and audit documentation.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price basis applies to USDA-certified organic commodity (True) or conventional commodity (False). Organic commodities command significant basis premiums and require separate pricing basis records for accurate invoice generation.']
  price_ceiling decimal(10,4) [note: 'The maximum cash price per unit of measure above which this basis will not result in an invoice price, regardless of futures movement. Null if no ceiling applies. Used in capped-price contract billing scenarios common in livestock and specialty crop markets.']
  price_floor decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum cash price per unit of measure below which this basis will not result in an invoice price, regardless of futures movement (e.g., minimum price guarantee in minimum-price contracts). Null if no floor applies. Used in minimum-price contract billing scenarios.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'The method used to determine the commodity price for invoice calculations. Options include: futures_basis (price = futures + basis), spot_market (current cash price), formula (custom formula-driven), flat_price (fixed agreed price), basis_contract (basis locked, futures open), hedge_to_arrive (futures locked, basis open).']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price basis is seasonal in nature (True) or applies year-round (False). Seasonal bases are common in grain marketing where harvest-time basis levels differ significantly from pre-harvest or post-harvest periods.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating operational system from which this price basis record was sourced (e.g., SAP_SD for SAP S/4HANA Sales & Distribution pricing conditions, GRANULAR for Granular Farm Management, DTN for DTN market data feed). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  standard_moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The reference moisture percentage at which no moisture adjustment is applied (e.g., 15.5% for corn, 13.0% for soybeans). Moisture readings from weight tickets above this level trigger discounts; below may trigger premiums per the moisture_adjustment_rate.']
  standard_test_weight_lbs decimal(5,2) [note: 'The reference test weight in pounds per bushel at which no test weight adjustment is applied (e.g., 56 lbs/BU for corn, 60 lbs/BU for soybeans, 60 lbs/BU for wheat). Deviations from this standard trigger price adjustments per test_weight_adjustment_rate.']
  test_weight_adjustment_rate decimal(6,4) [note: 'The per-unit price adjustment applied per pound below the standard test weight (e.g., -0.01 USD/BU per pound below 56 lbs/BU for corn). Test weight is a key quality factor in grain pricing that affects invoice amounts.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure against which the basis amount is applied (e.g., BU for bushel in grain, CWT for hundredweight in livestock, MT for metric ton in export contracts). Aligns with USDA and exchange standard units for the commodity type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|CWT|MT|LB|TON|KG|OZ — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price basis record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and incremental load processing in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Reference table defining commodity pricing bases used in agricultural invoice calculations including futures-based pricing, basis contracts, spot pricing, and formula pricing. Captures basis code, commodity type (corn, soybeans, wheat, cotton, cattle, hogs), pricing method (CBOT futures, CME futures, spot market, DTN market data, WASDE-referenced), basis amount (premium/discount over futures), effective date, expiry date, delivery location, and applicable contract types. Essential for accurate commodity invoice pricing in grain and livestock billing.'
}

  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the net settlement amount must be paid or remitted, calculated from the statement date and applicable payment terms. Critical for AR aging and dunning management.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the settlement statement transactions were posted to the General Ledger (GL) for revenue recognition and financial reporting purposes. May differ from statement_date for period-end accruals.']
  handling_deduction_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total grain handling and elevation fees deducted from the producer\'s settlement for receiving, conditioning, and loading-out services at the elevator during the settlement period.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross amount of all invoices and charges billed to the producer during the settlement period, before credits, deductions, or payments. Represents the sum of commodity purchases, services, and other charges.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The definitive net amount due to or from the producer after applying opening balance, invoiced amounts, credits, payments, and all deductions. Positive value indicates amount owed by producer; negative value indicates amount payable to producer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, special instructions, or explanatory comments related to this settlement statement, such as price adjustment rationale or seasonal payment arrangement details.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The accounts receivable (AR) balance carried forward from the prior settlement period for this producer or account. Represents the starting point for the current period reconciliation.']
  patronage_dividend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cooperative patronage dividend or equity distribution allocated to the producer-member for the settlement period, based on their proportional volume of business with the cooperative. Applicable only for cooperative settlement types.']
  payments_received_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cash payments, ACH transfers, wire transfers, and check payments received from or applied to the producer\'s account during the settlement period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the billing/settlement period covered by this statement. Together with period_start_date, defines the date range of all transactions summarized herein.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the billing/settlement period covered by this statement. Together with period_end_date, defines the date range of all transactions summarized herein.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement cycle frequency. Daily: grain elevator daily settlements; Weekly: livestock auction settlements; Monthly: standard AR billing; Seasonal: harvest-season settlements; Crop Year: annual cooperative patronage distributions.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total_quantity field. BU: Bushel; CWT: Hundredweight; MT: Metric Ton; LB: Pound; TON: Short Ton; HEAD: individual livestock count. Aligns with USDA commodity reporting standards.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'The date on which revenue associated with this settlement statement is recognized per GAAP ASC 606 or IFRS 15 five-step model. May differ from statement_date for multi-period commodity contracts.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural settlement scenario. Grain Elevator: grain storage and marketing settlements per state grain dealer licensing; Livestock Auction: proceeds from livestock sales; Cooperative Patronage: annual patronage dividend distributions; Commodity Purchase: direct commodity buyer settlements; Custom Farming: contract farming service settlements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain_elevator|livestock_auction|cooperative_patronage|commodity_purchase|custom_farming — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this settlement statement originated. SAP_S4HANA: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR module; GRANULAR: Granular Farm Management; ORACLE_NETSUITE: Oracle NetSuite AR; FMIS: Farm Management Information System; MANUAL: manually created.']
  statement_date date [note: 'The official business date on which the settlement statement was generated and issued to the producer or counterparty. This is the principal real-world event date for the settlement document.']
  statement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable unique identifier for the settlement statement as printed on the document and referenced in producer communications. Follows the format SS-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  statement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the settlement statement. Draft: in preparation; Issued: sent to producer; Disputed: producer has raised a discrepancy; Settled: fully paid and reconciled; Cancelled/Void: reversed or invalidated.']
  storage_deduction_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total grain storage fees deducted from the producer\'s settlement for commodity storage at the elevator or warehouse facility during the settlement period. A key deduction category in grain elevator settlements.']
  total_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of commodity or product covered by this settlement statement, expressed in the unit of measure specified in quantity_uom. Supports weight-ticket reconciliation and bushel/metric ton settlement calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this settlement statement record was last modified in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Tracks revisions, dispute updates, and status changes for audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Periodic settlement statements issued to agricultural producers, cooperatives, grain elevators, and commodity buyers summarizing all billing transactions within a settlement period. Captures statement number, period type (daily/weekly/monthly/seasonal/crop-year), customer/producer reference, opening balance, invoiced amount, credits issued, payments received, deductions (storage, drying, handling fees), net settlement amount, statement date, and delivery method. Serves as the definitive reconciliation document for grain elevator settlements (per state grain dealer licensing requirements), livestock auction settlements, and cooperative patronage distributions.'
}

Direct FK enables traceability from financial ']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice_line. Business justification: Price adjustments in agricultural billing are applied at the commodity line level (e.g., moisture adjustment, grade reclassification). price_adjustment already has ar_invoice_id -> invoice (header lev']
  lot_trace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Ag price adjustments for moisture, dockage, grade, and quality factors are applied at the lot level. Linking price_adjustment to lot_trace enables lot-level quality deduction tracking, supports FSMA-c']
  master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Price adjustments for branded/packaged agricultural products (seeds, agrochemicals) require master product reference for product-level adjustment reporting, P&L attribution, and audit trails. Distinct']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: MRL exceedance-triggered price adjustments (dockage, rejection, price reduction) require FK to the specific MRL threshold breached for EPA/USDA regulatory audit trails and export compliance documentat']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Price adjustments triggered by nonconformances (MRL exceedance, pesticide residue, grade failure) are a standard agriculture billing process. A FK from price_adjustment to nonconformance_id enables qu']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price adjustments (quality deductions, moisture adjustments, basis changes) must be attributed to profit centers for segment-level margin analysis. price_adjustment.profit_center_code is a denormalize']
  quarantine_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: MRL exceedances or quality holds trigger mandatory price adjustments (discounts, rejections) on commodity invoices. Linking price_adjustment to quarantine_hold enables automated price deduction workfl']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed quality price adjustments (germination failure, purity deficiency below label claim) require variety reference for PVP compliance, breeder royalty recalculation, and seed certification audit trai']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Price adjustments in agriculture (moisture deductions, dockage, quality discounts) are applied at the shipment level. Direct link supports shipment-level price adjustment tracking, carrier claim deduc']
  test_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.test_result. Business justification: Individual test results (moisture %, protein %, mycotoxin ppb) directly calculate price adjustments in commodity grain settlement. price_adjustment.quality_factor_code is a denormalized text reference']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the weight ticket record used for final weight reconciliation adjustments. Applicable when the adjustment is triggered by a moisture dockage correction or net weight discrepancy between provisional and final scale readings.']
  yield_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.yield_record. Business justification: Quality-based price adjustment traceability: when grain is re-tested post-delivery and moisture, dockage, or grade differs from invoice, a price adjustment is issued. This FK links the adjustment to t']
  adjusted_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The final settled unit price per unit of measure after applying the price adjustment. Reflects final commodity pricing, quality re-grading outcome, or contract true-up result.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value of the price adjustment (positive for increases, negative for decreases). Calculated as (adjusted_unit_price - original_unit_price) × quantity_affected. Drives AR debit or credit memo issuance and revenue recognition true-up.']
  adjustment_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the adjustment increases or decreases the original invoice amount. Drives debit memo vs. credit memo issuance and AR balance impact.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the price adjustment document. Used in customer communications, remittance advice, and audit trails. Format: PA-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price adjustment record. Controls workflow progression from initial creation through approval, GL posting, and potential reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|posted|reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the business reason driving the price adjustment. Determines the accounting treatment and GL account mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: final_price_true_up|quality_premium|quality_discount|moisture_dockage|futures_settlement|basis_adjustment|freight_differential — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the price adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, SLA monitoring, and period-end close management.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the price adjustment in the internal approval workflow. Price adjustments above a materiality threshold require multi-level authorization before GL posting per SOX internal controls.']
  basis_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The basis differential (in cents per bushel or dollars per metric ton) representing the difference between the local cash price and the relevant futures contract price. Used in basis adjustment and futures settlement price adjustment types.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the price adjustment record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and period-end close monitoring.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Supports multi-currency operations for international commodity trading.']
  dockage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage deduction applied to gross weight for foreign material, weed seeds, or other non-grain matter. Used in moisture dockage correction adjustments to reconcile provisional vs. final net weight and settlement amount.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business date on which the price adjustment becomes effective for pricing and settlement purposes. For futures price settlements, this aligns with the contract delivery period or pricing date specified in the commodity contract.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the adjustment amount from the transaction currency to the functional (reporting) currency at the time of posting. Relevant for cross-border commodity sales.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Adjustment amount expressed in the entity\'s functional (reporting) currency after applying the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  futures_contract_month varchar [note: 'The CBOT/CME futures contract delivery month code used as the pricing reference for basis or futures-plus-basis adjustments (e.g., Z24 for December 2024 corn). Applicable when pricing_basis is \'futures_plus_basis\' or \'basis\'.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the price adjustment is posted to the General Ledger. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition true-up and AR balance adjustment. May differ from effective_date for period-end accruals.']
  moisture_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Final measured moisture content (%) of the commodity used to calculate moisture dockage corrections. Deviations from standard moisture (e.g., 15.5% for corn) trigger dockage adjustments that reduce the net settlement weight and price.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, business justification, or operational notes related to the price adjustment. May include market conditions, counterparty communications, or inspector remarks supporting the adjustment.']
  original_unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The provisional unit price per unit of measure (e.g., per bushel, per metric ton, per hundredweight) stated on the originating invoice before any adjustment. Basis for calculating the adjustment delta.']
  price_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure against which the original and adjusted unit prices are expressed. Common agricultural units include BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), LB (Pound), KG (Kilogram), TON (Short Ton).']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer or counterparty purchase order number associated with the original commodity transaction. Included on adjustment documents for customer reconciliation and three-way matching in accounts payable.']
  quality_factor_code varchar [note: 'Specific quality attribute code (e.g., test weight, heat damage, total damage, broken kernels, foreign material) that triggered a quality premium or discount adjustment. Aligns with USDA GIPSA grading factor codes.']
  quantity_affected decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of commodity (expressed in price_unit_of_measure) subject to the price adjustment. Used to calculate the total adjustment amount as (adjusted_unit_price - original_unit_price) × quantity_affected.']
  reversal_reference varchar [note: 'Adjustment number of the reversing price adjustment document if this record has been reversed. Populated when adjustment_status is \'reversed\'. Supports audit trail and double-entry accounting integrity.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this price adjustment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across integrated systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the price adjustment record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Records post-invoice price adjustments applied to commodity billing due to market price changes, quality re-grading, final weight reconciliation, or contract price true-up events. Captures adjustment number, originating invoice reference, adjustment type (final price true-up, quality premium/discount, moisture dockage correction, futures price settlement, basis adjustment, freight differential), original unit price, adjusted unit price, quantity affected, adjustment amount, effective date, and approval status. Common in grain and oilseed billing where provisional invoices are issued pending final commodity pricing.'
}

Table procurement.vendor {
  vendor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the vendor master record in the procurement domain. Primary key for all vendor-related transactions and qualifications.']
  relationship_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ag procurement teams assign an internal relationship manager to each vendor for scorecard management, audit coordination, and corrective action follow-up. This FK drives vendor performance reporting b']
  aphis_permit_number varchar [note: 'USDA APHIS permit number for vendors supplying regulated biological materials, plant propagation materials, or animal health products subject to APHIS import/movement controls.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s bank account number used for electronic funds transfer (EFT) and ACH payment processing. Classified restricted due to PCI/financial data sensitivity.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'ABA routing transit number identifying the vendor\'s financial institution for ACH and wire transfer payments. Classified restricted due to financial data sensitivity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the vendor\'s primary registered business address. Used for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, import/export documentation, and FOB/CIF contract defaults.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the vendor\'s invoicing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Used for AP invoice processing, FX conversion, and spend analytics.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number uniquely identifying the vendor\'s legal entity globally. Used for supplier risk assessment, credit checks, and USDA/FDA supplier verification.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'EPA registration number for vendors supplying pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or other regulated agrochemicals. Required under FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act) for legal sale and distribution of crop protection products.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of ordered quantity actually delivered by the vendor within the scorecard period. Measures supply reliability for critical agricultural inputs such as seeds, NPK fertilizers, and crop protection chemicals.']
  fsma_svcp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has been verified as compliant with FSMA Supplier Verification Control Program (SVCP) requirements under 21 CFR Part 1, Subpart L. Required for vendors supplying food-grade or food-contact agricultural inputs.']
  incoterm_default varchar [note: 'Default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) agreed with this vendor for shipment of agricultural inputs. Determines risk transfer point and freight cost responsibility. Free On Board (FOB) and Cost Insurance and Freight (CIF) are most common for bulk commodity inputs.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of vendor invoices that matched the corresponding PO and GRN without discrepancy within the scorecard period. Drives AP processing efficiency and three-way match success rate.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed vendor qualification or food safety audit. Used to determine audit currency and trigger re-audit scheduling.']
  last_audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) from the most recent vendor qualification audit. Reflects compliance with GAP, GMP, HACCP, and food safety standards. Scores below threshold trigger conditional status or disqualification.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the vendor organization as it appears on official government and tax documents. Used for contract execution, invoice matching, and regulatory filings.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of purchase orders delivered by the vendor on or before the confirmed delivery date within the current scorecard period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for vendor scorecard and contract renewal decisions.']
  open_corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Count of open corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the vendor for quality, compliance, or delivery failures. Non-zero values may trigger conditional status or procurement holds pending resolution.']
  overall_scorecard_rating varchar [note: 'Composite vendor performance rating derived from on-time delivery, fill rate, quality acceptance, invoice accuracy, and regulatory compliance scores. Drives preferred vendor designation and contract renewal decisions.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP/NetSuite payment terms code defining the agreed payment schedule (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30). Governs AP invoice due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the vendor\'s account manager or procurement liaison. Used for PO transmission, GRN confirmations, and compliance correspondence.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary business telephone number for the vendor\'s procurement or account management contact. Includes country dialing code for international vendors.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor was most recently granted approved or conditional qualification status following audit and documentation review.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor\'s qualification within the approved supplier program. Controls whether purchase orders may be issued. \'Conditional\' indicates approval with restrictions (e.g., limited input categories or volume caps).']
  quality_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0.00–100.00) of received goods that passed quality inspection (GRN quality check) without rejection or return within the scorecard period. Reflects vendor\'s adherence to COA specifications and MRL compliance for agrochemical inputs.']
  regulatory_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0.00–100.00) reflecting the vendor\'s compliance with applicable regulatory requirements including EPA registration currency, APHIS permit validity, SDS documentation completeness, FSMA SVCP status, and MRL adherence.']
  requalification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the vendor must complete re-qualification activities (audit, documentation refresh, certification renewal) to maintain approved status. Triggers procurement alerts when approaching.']
  sap_vendor_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM vendor account group code (e.g., LIEF for standard suppliers, KRED for one-time vendors) controlling field selection, number ranges, and partner functions in the vendor master.']
  scorecard_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance measurement period for which the current scorecard metrics (on-time delivery, fill rate, quality acceptance, invoice accuracy, regulatory compliance) were calculated.']
  sds_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has provided current Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for all supplied chemical inputs (fertilizers, agrochemicals, fuel) in compliance with OSHA HazCom 2012 (GHS). True = all required SDS documents on file and current.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number (TIN) assigned by the IRS. Required for 1099 reporting, AP processing, and regulatory compliance. Classified restricted due to sensitivity of tax identity data.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing-business-as (DBA) or trade name used in day-to-day commercial operations, which may differ from the legal entity name.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the vendor master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and Oracle NetSuite, and audit trail maintenance.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric business identifier assigned to the vendor in SAP S/4HANA MM and Oracle NetSuite for cross-system reference, purchase order creation, and AP processing.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor by the primary category of agricultural inputs or services supplied. Drives procurement routing, compliance requirements, and SDS/EPA documentation obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed_supplier|fertilizer_distributor|agrochemical_supplier|feed_ingredient_vendor|fuel_supplier|equipment_parts_dealer|service_provider|logistics_carrier|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for all approved agricultural input suppliers and service providers including seed companies, fertilizer distributors, agrochemical suppliers, feed ingredient vendors, fuel suppliers, and equipment parts dealers. Captures vendor identity (tax ID, DUNS number), qualification lifecycle (pending, approved, conditional, suspended, disqualified), GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI certification numbers and expiry dates, EPA/APHIS registration numbers, SDS documentation compliance flags, payment terms, FOB/CIF contract defaults, bank details, and SAP S/4HANA vendor master integration keys. Qualification attributes: qualification date, re-qualification due date, SQF/BRC/IFS certification status, FSMA SVCP compliance status, audit score, and approved input categories. Performance metrics (periodic): on-time delivery rate, fill rate, quality acceptance rate, invoice accuracy, regulatory compliance score, overall scorecard rating, and corrective action tracking. SSOT for supplier identity, qualification status, and performance within the procurement domain.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_order {
  purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the purchase_order data product.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Purchase orders for agrochemicals reference the specific EPA-registered product. Required for restricted-use pesticide procurement controls, EPA compliance reporting, and hazmat shipping documentation']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.budget. Business justification: A purchase order commits budget — this is a fundamental procurement control. While purchase_order already references finance.cost_center and finance.crop_enterprise_budget (cross-domain), the procurem']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Purchase orders are created and owned by procurement buyers (employees). Linking buyer to PO supports procurement accountability reporting, spend-by-buyer analysis, and segregation-of-duties controls ']
  capital_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capital_project. Business justification: CAPEX purchase orders in agriculture (irrigation systems, grain storage, farm equipment) are linked to capital projects for project cost tracking, WBS settlement, and asset capitalization. The PO has ']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Purchase orders for commodity inputs reference the specific commodity for inventory planning and price settlement. Commodity-level PO reporting is standard in grain merchandising and feed procurement ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: POs in multi-entity agriculture operations are issued under a specific legal entity for financial posting, tax jurisdiction determination, and intercompany procurement reporting. Agriculture domain ex']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Agricultural input POs (seeds, chemicals, fertilizers) are charged to cost centers for crop production cost allocation. Supports budget vs. actual spend analysis by cost center and COGS tracking per c']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: CAPEX and input POs in agriculture are executed against crop enterprise budgets. Linking POs to crop enterprise budgets enables budget vs. actual input cost tracking at crop/field level — essential fo']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Agricultural input POs (seed, fertilizer, chemicals) must be allocated to a specific farm unit for FSA reporting, crop insurance reconciliation, and farm-level cost accounting. A farm operations exper']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Field-level input procurement is a core precision agriculture process — agronomists allocate seed, fertilizer, and chemical POs to specific fields for input cost-per-acre reporting, agronomic record-k']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: POs are raised per growing season for seasonal inputs. Season-level procurement spend reporting, budget vs. actual analysis, and FSMA compliance tracking all require a proper FK to growing_season. Rep']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Purchase orders for FSMA-regulated agricultural inputs (pesticides, food-contact materials) must reference the HACCP plan governing their use. purchase_order has fsma_compliance_flag but no link to th']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: A purchase order procures a specific agricultural input that should be referenced from the procurement input catalog. This FK links the PO to the authoritative catalog record, enabling catalog-driven ']
  management_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.management_zone. Business justification: Variable rate application (VRA) procurement is a named precision agriculture workflow — POs for zone-specific seed rates and fertilizer prescriptions are tied to management zones. Any precision ag dom']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Purchase orders for certified organic commodities must reference the supplier\'s organic certification to verify NOP compliance at order time. USDA NOP audit trails and organic premium pricing authoriz']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP plant (facility/farm operation) that is the receiving location for the ordered agricultural inputs. Determines storage location assignment, GRN processing, and inventory posting. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.WERKS.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Agricultural POs for crop inputs (seeds, fertilizers, chemicals) are assigned to profit centers (e.g., corn enterprise, soybean enterprise) for enterprise-level P&L and commitment accounting. Standard']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Purchase orders for seed procurement specify the exact variety ordered. Variety-level PO tracking is fundamental to seed supply chain management and drives planting program fulfillment reporting.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master procurement contract or blanket agreement against which this PO is released. Enables contract compliance monitoring, spend-against-contract analytics, and price validation. Null for spot/open-market purchases without a governing contract.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (supplier) master record from whom agricultural inputs are being procured. Links to the vendor/supplier master data product for full vendor profile, qualification status, and performance data.']
  aphis_permit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an APHIS import or movement permit is required for the agricultural inputs on this PO (e.g., regulated seeds, biological control agents, plant materials, or animal feed ingredients subject to APHIS oversight). Triggers permit documentation workflow.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the purchase order received final authorization from the designated approver(s) per the procurement approval matrix. Required for SOX financial controls documentation and audit trail. Null if PO is still pending approval or was cancelled before approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the PO approval workflow. Tracks whether the PO has passed through the required authorization levels per the procurement approval matrix. Distinct from po_status which tracks the overall PO lifecycle. Required for SOX internal controls and procurement governance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who granted final approval for the purchase order per the procurement authorization matrix. Required for SOX audit trail and procurement governance documentation. Corresponds to SAP MM release strategy approver.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Financial classification of the purchase order spend as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) for long-lived assets (e.g., equipment parts, infrastructure) or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) for consumable agricultural inputs (seeds, fertilizers, chemicals, fuel). Drives GL account assignment and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the agricultural inputs being procured. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, USDA import documentation, tariff classification, and supply chain risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the source system (SAP MM or Oracle NetSuite). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement for PO processing cycle time. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.AEDAT (creation date) combined with creation time.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PO transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Determines the currency in which vendor invoices will be processed and AP liabilities recorded. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.WAERS.']
  epa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PO involves EPA-regulated materials such as registered pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or other crop protection chemicals subject to EPA FIFRA registration requirements. Triggers SDS documentation requirements and MRL compliance review.']
  fsma_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PO is subject to FSMA supply chain compliance requirements, including supplier verification, hazard analysis, and corrective action procedures under the Preventive Controls and Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) rules. True for food-contact agricultural inputs and feed ingredients.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the agricultural inputs on this PO contain, are derived from, or are free of Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs). Critical for identity preservation programs, USDA NOP organic certification compliance, COOL labeling, and customer supply chain transparency requirements.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and vendor. FOB and CIF are most common for bulk agricultural commodity inputs. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.INCO1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., \'FOB Houston\' or \'CIF Rotterdam\'). Specifies the exact geographic point of risk and cost transfer. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.INCO2.']
  input_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of agricultural inputs being procured on this PO. Drives spend analytics by input type, regulatory compliance routing (EPA/APHIS for crop protection chemicals, FSMA for feed ingredients), and budget allocation to OPEX vs CAPEX. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seeds|fertilizers|crop_protection|feed_ingredients|fuel|spare_parts|operational_supplies|services — promote to reference product]']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase order record in the source system. Tracks changes to PO terms, quantities, pricing, or status. Essential for incremental data pipeline processing and change data capture from SAP MM and Oracle NetSuite.']
  lot_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lot-level traceability is required for the inputs on this PO. When true, GRN processing must capture LOT numbers for each delivery. Mandatory for seeds (variety purity), crop protection chemicals (MRL compliance), and feed ingredients (FSMA traceability). Drives inventory.commodity_lot creation on receipt.']
  net_po_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net total value of the purchase order after applying header-level discounts but before tax. Represents the actual procurement spend commitment for budget and OPEX/CAPEX reporting. Used in EBITDA and COGS analysis for agricultural input cost management.']
  netsuite_po_ref varchar [note: 'Oracle NetSuite internal PO transaction reference number. Used for AP processing, vendor payment reconciliation, and GL posting in NetSuite. Enables cross-system traceability between SAP MM and NetSuite for dual-system procurement environments.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Vendor payment terms code defining the due date calculation and any early payment discount conditions (e.g., \'NET30\', \'2/10NET30\'). Drives AP payment scheduling in Oracle NetSuite and cash flow forecasting. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.ZTERM.']
  po_date date [note: 'The business date on which the purchase order was formally issued to the vendor. This is the principal real-world event date for the PO transaction. Used for aging analysis, payment term calculation, and regulatory compliance documentation. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.BEDAT.']
  po_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, special handling requirements, or procurement-specific notes communicated to the vendor on this PO. May include PHI/REI compliance reminders for crop protection chemical orders, organic certification requirements, or special packaging instructions.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable PO number issued to the vendor. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.EBELN (purchasing document number) and Oracle NetSuite PO transaction number. Used on all vendor communications, GRN matching, and AP invoice reconciliation.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order. Tracks the PO from initial draft through approval, issuance to vendor, goods receipt, and final closure or cancellation. Drives workflow routing in SAP MM and AP processing in NetSuite. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|sent|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order by procurement mechanism. Standard POs are single-event purchases; blanket POs cover recurring supply agreements; contract releases draw against a master contract; emergency POs bypass standard approval for urgent agricultural inputs. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.BSART (purchasing document type).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the buyer requests delivery of the ordered agricultural inputs at the specified plant or storage location. Used for supply chain planning, crop season alignment, and vendor on-time delivery KPI measurement. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO.EINDT (delivery date at header level).']
  sap_mm_doc_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing document number from the EKKO table (EBELN field). 10-digit numeric identifier used for cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and SAP MM. Enables drill-back to source ERP for AP processing and GRN matching.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is required for the chemical or biological inputs on this PO per OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom). True for pesticides, fertilizers, fuels, and other hazardous agricultural chemicals. Triggers SDS documentation collection workflow.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the purchase order in the PO currency, including VAT, GST, or applicable sales/use tax on agricultural inputs. Required for AP invoice matching and tax liability reporting. Corresponds to SAP MM tax condition values.']
  total_po_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross total value of the purchase order in the PO currency, representing the sum of all line item totals before any header-level discounts or taxes. Used for spend analytics, budget commitment tracking, and AP liability accrual. Corresponds to SAP MM EKKO total value.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a formal PO issued to a vendor for agricultural inputs including seeds, NPK fertilizers, crop protection chemicals, feed ingredients, fuel, spare parts, and operational supplies. Header: PO number, vendor reference, delivery terms (FOB/CIF), incoterms, requested delivery date, total PO value, currency, approval status, SAP MM document number (EKKO), Oracle NetSuite PO reference, and FSMA/EPA compliance flags. Line items: material/SKU code, commodity description, quantity (BU, MT, CWT, or unit), unit of measure, agreed unit price, line total, requested delivery date, plant/storage location, crop season, lot tracking requirement flag, MRL compliance flag, PHI/REI requirements, and GMO status indicator. SSOT for all PO header and line-level data. Maps to SAP MM EKKO (header) and EKPO (item).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_goods_receipt {
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Goods Receipt Note (GRN) record in the procurement domain. Primary key for this Silver Layer data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product.
