  commodity_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Every procurement GRN in agriculture creates or receives into a commodity lot for traceability. Linking procurement_goods_receipt to commodity_lot enables farm-to-table lot traceability, FSMA supplier']
  delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.delivery_schedule. Business justification: A goods receipt (GRN) fulfills a planned delivery schedule line — the GRN is the child (actual receipt) and the delivery_schedule is the parent (planned delivery). This FK enables schedule vs. actual ']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Goods receipts for commodity inputs are inspected against the applicable USDA grading standard. The grading standard determines acceptance/rejection criteria and price settlement at receipt. Core to c']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Goods receipt in agriculture triggers a quality inspection (incoming material inspection). procurement_goods_receipt.quality_inspection_status is a denormalized status flag; a proper FK to quality.ins']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Goods receipts for agricultural inputs (seeds, chemicals, grain) trigger automatic GR/IR journal entries in SAP MIGO. Linking GR to journal_entry enables 3-way match audit trail, FSMA compliance docum']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the agricultural input received (seed, fertilizer, crop protection chemical, feed ingredient, fuel, parts, or operational supply).']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Goods receipt for agrochemicals and commodities with mrl_testing_required flag must reference the specific MRL threshold being tested against. FSMA and food safety compliance reporting require traceab']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Purchase Order (PO) against which this Goods Receipt Note (GRN) is posted. Enables three-way match (PO–GRN–Invoice) in AP processing.']
  receiver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Goods receipts for agricultural inputs are performed by specific receiving employees. Linking to employee supports FSMA traceability requirements, quality inspection accountability, and labor producti']
  receiving_crew_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.crew. Business justification: Bulk commodity and agrochemical receipts in ag operations involve a crew for unloading, grading, and bin placement — not just one individual. Crew-level receipt tracking supports H2A labor cost alloca']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Goods receipts for seed deliveries reference the variety for germination testing, purity verification, and lot tracking. Variety-level receipt quality control is mandatory for seed certification compl']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: GRN creation in agriculture is triggered by physical shipment arrival. Linking procurement_goods_receipt→supply.shipment enables 3-way match automation (PO/shipment/invoice), cold chain breach investi']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm, warehouse, feed mill, or processing facility where the agricultural inputs were physically received.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/supplier who delivered the goods. Links to the vendor master record for supplier performance tracking and AP invoice matching.']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: In grain agriculture, the weight ticket IS the primary receiving document — GRN quantity and quality are determined by the weight ticket. Formalizing this FK enables grain receiving reconciliation, se']
  accepted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the received agricultural input that passed quality inspection and was accepted into inventory. Triggers inventory update and AP invoice matching for the accepted portion.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading (BOL) number from the carrier or freight forwarder accompanying the shipment. Used for logistics traceability, customs documentation, and dispute resolution.']
  coa_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Certificate of Analysis (COA) was received from the supplier with this delivery. Mandatory for fertilizers, crop protection chemicals, feed ingredients, and biologicals per GFSI and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the received agricultural input. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance and import documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GRN record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit price and total receipt value fields (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency procurement for international agricultural input sourcing.']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) declaration status for the received agricultural input, particularly seeds and feed ingredients. Required for USDA NOP organic certification compliance and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) documentation.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code governing the delivery and risk transfer for this shipment (e.g., FOB — Free On Board, CIF — Cost Insurance and Freight). Determines when title and risk pass to the buyer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|DAP|DDP|FCA|CFR|CPT|CIP|DAT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  input_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural input type received. Drives downstream compliance workflows: SDS documentation for crop protection chemicals, COA verification for feed ingredients, cold-chain monitoring for biologicals and vaccines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|fuel|parts|operational_supply|biological|vaccine — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Accounts Payable (AP) three-way match process between the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt Note (GRN), and supplier invoice. Drives payment release or block in Oracle NetSuite and SAP FI/AP.']
  lot_batch_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned to the received agricultural input at time of GRN posting. Critical for forward and backward traceability across the supply chain, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance, and MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) testing.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number or ERP item code for the received agricultural input. Retained on the GRN line for direct reference and cross-system reconciliation.']
  migo_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM goods movement document number generated by the MIGO transaction upon posting of the goods receipt. Used for ERP reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  mrl_testing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) testing is required for this received input before release to use. Applicable to crop protection chemicals and certain feed ingredients per EPA and Codex Alimentarius standards.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the agricultural input ordered on the originating Purchase Order (PO) line. Used for over/under delivery variance analysis and AP three-way match.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable Purchase Order (PO) number from the source ERP system. Retained on the GRN header for direct reference without requiring a join, supporting AP three-way match and vendor communications.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Accounting posting date for the goods receipt in the ERP system. May differ from the physical receipt timestamp when receipts are posted retroactively. Drives AP period allocation.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection for the received agricultural input. Drives release to inventory or quarantine hold. Linked to COA verification and MRL testing workflows.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Goods Receipt Note (GRN). Controls downstream AP invoice matching and inventory update workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|posted|partially_accepted|fully_accepted|rejected|reversed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the agricultural inputs were physically received at the facility. Principal business event timestamp for the GRN. Used for lot traceability, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance, and cold-chain monitoring.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the agricultural input physically received at the facility as recorded on the GRN. May differ from ordered quantity due to short shipments or over-deliveries.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the received agricultural input that failed quality inspection and was rejected. Triggers supplier return process and debit memo in AP. Must be reconciled with accepted_quantity + rejected_quantity = received_quantity.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for rejection of the rejected quantity. Examples: DAMAGED, MRL_EXCEEDANCE, COA_FAIL, WRONG_PRODUCT, TEMP_EXCEEDANCE, LABEL_NONCOMPLIANT, PEST_CONTAMINATION. Populated only when rejected_quantity > 0. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGED|MRL_EXCEEDANCE|COA_FAIL|WRONG_PRODUCT|TEMP_EXCEEDANCE|LABEL_NONCOMPLIANT|PEST_CONTAMINATION|SHORT_DATED — promote to reference product]']
  sds_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is on file for the received chemical or biological input. Mandatory for crop protection chemicals, fertilizers, and fuel per EPA and OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom).']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Specific storage bin, silo, tank, or warehouse location where the accepted quantity was physically placed after receipt. Used for inventory putaway confirmation and WMS task management.']
  temperature_at_receipt_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of receipt for cold-chain sensitive agricultural inputs including biologicals, vaccines, and temperature-sensitive feed ingredients. Null for non-temperature-sensitive materials.']
  temperature_exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recorded temperature at receipt exceeded the specified cold-chain tolerance for the material. When True, triggers quality hold and supplier notification workflow. Applicable to biologicals, vaccines, and cold-chain feed ingredients.']
  total_receipt_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the accepted quantity at the PO unit price (accepted_quantity × unit_price). Used for inventory valuation posting, AP accrual, and spend analytics. Currency is USD unless otherwise specified.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields on this GRN line (ordered, received, accepted, rejected). Standard agricultural units: KG (kilogram), MT (Metric Ton), LB (pound), CWT (Hundredweight), BU (Bushel), L (litre), GAL (gallon), EA (each), BAG, TON. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|LB|CWT|BU|L|GAL|EA|BAG|TON — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Purchase price per unit of measure for the received agricultural input as per the Purchase Order (PO). Used for inventory valuation (moving average or standard cost) and AP invoice matching.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the GRN record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance.']
  vendor_delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the shipment. Used for cross-referencing vendor documentation during GRN processing and dispute resolution.']

  Note: 'Goods Receipt Note (GRN) record capturing the physical receipt and inspection of agricultural inputs at farm, warehouse, feed mill, or processing facility. Header: GRN number, PO reference, vendor delivery note number, receiving location, receipt date/time, overall receipt status, and SAP MM goods movement document number (MIGO). Line items: received quantity vs. ordered quantity, unit of measure, accepted quantity, rejected quantity with rejection reason code, lot/batch number assigned, storage bin/silo assignment, quality inspection status, COA received flag, SDS on file flag, temperature at receipt (for biologicals, vaccines, and cold-chain feed ingredients), and temperature exceedance flag. SSOT for all GRN header and line-level data. Triggers AP invoice three-way match workflow and inventory update. Feeds quality inspection workflow for COA verification and MRL testing.'
}

  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number used in procurement correspondence, PO references, and audit trails. Typically sourced from SAP MM outline agreement document number.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract. Draft: under negotiation; Pending Approval: awaiting authorization; Active: binding and in force; Suspended: temporarily halted; Expired: past end date without renewal; Terminated: ended early; Closed: fully fulfilled and archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract by its commercial structure. Framework agreements cover multiple POs over time; spot contracts are single-event; annual supply agreements govern a full crop year; blanket purchase orders allow call-offs; consignment contracts defer payment until consumption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|spot|annual_supply|blanket_purchase|consignment|index_linked — promote to reference product]']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated monetary value of the contract over its full term, calculated as contracted_volume × base_price. Used for spend analytics, CAPEX/OPEX planning, and financial reporting. For formula-based contracts, this is the estimated value at time of signing.']
  contracted_volume decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total quantity of the agricultural input committed under this contract for the contract period. Expressed in the unit of measure specified in volume_uom (BU, MT, CWT, L, KG, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the vendor contract record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (base_price, volume_tier thresholds, penalty amounts). Typically USD for domestic US agricultural contracts.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms (Incoterms) governing risk transfer and freight responsibility between the enterprise and the vendor. FOB = Free On Board; CIF = Cost Insurance and Freight; EXW = Ex Works; DAP = Delivered at Place; DDP = Delivered Duty Paid; FCA = Free Carrier; CFR = Cost and Freight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|DAP|DDP|FCA|CFR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_terms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port associated with the Incoterms delivery terms (e.g., \'Port of New Orleans\' for FOB, \'Chicago Terminal\' for DAP). Required to complete the Incoterms designation per ICC 2020.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract expires or is scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended evergreen agreements. Aligns with SAP MM validity end date (EKKO-KDATE).']
  epa_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agricultural input covered by this contract (e.g., pesticides, herbicides, fungicides) requires EPA registration under FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act). Mandatory compliance check for chemical input procurement.']
  force_majeure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a force majeure clause that excuses non-performance due to extraordinary events (natural disasters, crop failures, regulatory actions). Critical for agricultural supply chain risk management.']
  globalg_ap_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is contractually required to hold a valid GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification for the duration of this contract. True = certification mandatory.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity per individual purchase order call-off against this contract. Expressed in the same unit of measure as volume_uom. Vendor may refuse orders below this threshold.']
  organic_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must supply inputs certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). Applicable for organic seed, organic fertilizer, and approved organic crop protection inputs.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment terms governing invoice settlement (e.g., \'Net 30\', \'2/10 Net 30\', \'Net 60 EOM\'). Drives AP payment scheduling in SAP FI and Oracle NetSuite AR.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract contains penalty clauses for non-performance, late delivery, or quality failures. True = penalty clauses exist; False = no penalty provisions.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of penalty provisions including trigger conditions, calculation method, and maximum liability cap (e.g., \'2% of PO value per week of delay, capped at 10%\'). Null if penalty_clause_flag is False.']
  price_adjustment_formula varchar [note: 'Text description or algebraic expression defining how the price is adjusted for formula-based or index-linked contracts (e.g., \'WASDE corn price + $0.15/BU basis\'). Null for fixed-price contracts.']
  price_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the contracted pricing terms (base_price, price_type) become effective. May differ from contract start_date if pricing is renegotiated mid-term.']
  price_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the contracted pricing terms expire. After this date, new pricing must be negotiated or the contract reverts to spot pricing. Aligns with SAP MM condition validity end date.']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the contract price is determined. Fixed: locked price for the contract term; Formula-based: price derived from a calculation; WASDE-indexed: price tied to USDA World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates; Spot: prevailing market price at time of order; Cost-plus: vendor cost plus agreed margin.']
  released_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all purchase orders (POs) released against this contract to date. Enables spend tracking against contract ceiling and open commitment reporting.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days prior to the contract end date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives procurement calendar alerts.']
  sap_outline_agreement_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM module outline agreement document number (10-digit) serving as the system-of-record identifier for this contract. Used for integration with AP processing and GRN matching.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must provide a current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the supplied input as a condition of contract. Mandatory for all chemical inputs (fertilizers, crop protection chemicals, fuel) per OSHA HazCom Standard.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the vendor is authorized to supply against it. Aligns with the SAP MM validity start date (EKKO-KDATB).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the vendor contract record. Used for change tracking, delta processing in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit compliance.']
  volume_tier_threshold_1 decimal(18,4) [note: 'First volume breakpoint (in volume_uom) at which a tiered pricing discount or rebate is triggered. Orders exceeding this quantity qualify for the Tier 1 price adjustment.']
  volume_tier_threshold_2 decimal(18,4) [note: 'Second volume breakpoint (in volume_uom) at which a higher tiered pricing discount or rebate is triggered. Null if only one tier exists.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contracted volume. BU = Bushel; MT = Metric Ton; CWT = Hundredweight; KG = Kilogram; L = Litre; GAL = Gallon; TON = Short Ton; LB = Pound. Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|MT|CWT|KG|L|GAL|TON|LB — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Formal procurement contract governing the supply of agricultural inputs between the enterprise and a qualified vendor. Captures contract type (framework, spot, annual supply agreement), commodity or input category covered, contracted volume (BU/MT/CWT), delivery terms (FOB/CIF), contract start and end dates, auto-renewal flag, penalty clauses, force majeure provisions, GLOBALG.A.P. compliance requirement, EPA registration requirement for chemical inputs, and SAP MM outline agreement document number. Pricing terms: price type (fixed, formula-based, WASDE-indexed, spot), base price, price adjustment formula, volume tier thresholds, minimum order quantity, currency, and effective/expiry dates. SSOT for all contractual agreements and negotiated pricing with vendors — no separate price agreement product exists.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_requisition {
  purchase_requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the purchase requisition record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Purchase requisitions for restricted-use pesticides must reference the specific EPA-registered agrochemical product for pre-approval compliance checks and certified applicator authorization verificati']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last actioned (approved or rejected) this requisition in the approval workflow. Supports audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.budget. Business justification: Purchase requisitions must check budget availability before approval — the existing \'budget_available_flag\' boolean captures the result of this check but does not identify WHICH budget was checked. Ad']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Purchase requisitions for commodity inputs (grain, feed, fertilizer) must reference the commodity for budget allocation, spend analysis by commodity class, and FSMA coverage determination. Commodity-l']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity agricultural operations require company code assignment on purchase requisitions for intercompany procurement, financial posting authorization, and legal entity cost allocation. Standard ']

  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Purchase requisitions in agricultural procurement frequently specify a preferred or sole-source vendor (e.g., a specific seed supplier for a proprietary variety, or an EPA-registered chemical supplier']

  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the multi-level approval workflow for this requisition. Tracks whether the requisition is awaiting approval, has been approved, rejected, or escalated to a higher authority based on spend thresholds.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the purchase requisition received final approval in the workflow. Used for procurement cycle time KPI measurement and SOX audit trail.']

  converted_to_po_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approved requisition has been converted into a formal Purchase Order (PO) in SAP MM. True = PO raised; False = PO not yet created. Supports open requisition monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase requisition record was first captured in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated cost values on this requisition (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Supports multi-currency procurement operations for international agricultural supply chains.']
  estimated_total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total estimated procurement cost calculated as requested quantity multiplied by estimated unit cost. Used for budget availability validation and CAPEX/OPEX spend approval thresholds.']
  estimated_unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated cost per unit of the agricultural input at the time of requisition, used for budget availability checks and spend forecasting. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  gmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisitioned seed or biological input is a Genetically Modified Organism (GMO). Required for USDA NOP organic certification compliance, COOL labeling, and supply chain segregation management.']
  input_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the agricultural input being requisitioned. Drives procurement routing, regulatory compliance checks (EPA/APHIS for agrochemicals), and spend category analytics. Values: seed, fertilizer (NPK), agrochemical (AI/MRL), feed, fuel, parts, supplies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|agrochemical|feed|fuel|parts|supplies — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  justification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text business justification provided by the requester explaining the operational need for the agricultural input, including agronomic rationale, IPM program requirements, or livestock nutrition plan references.']
  netsuitepr_ref varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for the corresponding purchase requisition record in Oracle NetSuite (used for AP processing and financial reconciliation where NetSuite is the financial system of record alongside SAP).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP MM plant code identifying the farm site, processing facility, or storage location for which the agricultural inputs are being requisitioned. Supports multi-site procurement management.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requisition by the requesting department, indicating urgency relative to operational needs such as planting windows, PHI compliance deadlines, or livestock feed shortages.']
  regulatory_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisitioned input requires regulatory pre-approval before procurement, such as EPA registration for pesticides, APHIS permits for biological inputs, or FDA compliance for feed additives.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the approver when a requisition is rejected. Captures the business rationale for rejection to support process improvement and requester communication.']
  requested_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of the agricultural input material requested, expressed in the base unit of measure (UOM). Supports volume-based spend estimation and supplier capacity planning.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Name of the farm operations, agronomy, livestock, maintenance, or other internal department that initiated the purchase requisition. Used for spend analytics and budget allocation reporting.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the requested agricultural input must be delivered to the plant or farm site to meet operational needs such as planting windows, PHI compliance, or livestock feeding schedules.']
  requisition_date date [note: 'The business date on which the purchase requisition was formally raised by the requesting department. This is the principal real-world event date for this transaction, distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the purchase requisition, used across procurement workflows and communications. Corresponds to SAP MM BANF number (EBAN-BANFN).']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the purchase requisition within the procurement approval workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and conversion to a formal Purchase Order (PO). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|pending_approval|approved|rejected|cancelled|converted_to_po — promote to reference product]']
  sap_banf_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM native 10-digit purchase requisition document number (BANF/EBAN table key). Used for direct traceability back to the system of record for AP processing and audit.']
  sds_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is required for the requisitioned material, applicable to agrochemicals, fertilizers, and hazardous substances. True = SDS documentation mandatory before PO conversion. Supports EPA and OSHA compliance.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested quantity, aligned with SAP MM base UOM and agricultural industry standards. Examples: KG (kilogram), MT (metric ton), BU (bushel), CWT (hundredweight), L (litre), GAL (gallon), EA (each), BAG. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KG|MT|LT|L|BU|CWT|EA|BAG|TON|GAL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the purchase requisition record. Supports change tracking, approval workflow auditing, and SOX compliance.']

  Note: 'Internal request to procure agricultural inputs initiated by farm operations, agronomy, livestock, or maintenance teams before a formal PO is raised. Captures requisition number, requesting department/cost center, input category (seed, fertilizer, agrochemical, feed, fuel, parts), material description, required quantity and UOM, required delivery date, estimated unit cost, budget availability flag, approval workflow status, approver chain, and SAP MM purchase requisition document number (BANF/EBAN).'
}

Table procurement.procurement_ap_invoice {

Business justification: AP invoice approval is a financial control requiring employee traceability for three-way match and payment authorization. The existing \'approved_by\' plain-text column is a denormalized employee refere']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice. Business justification: In ag commodity back-to-back trades, the AP invoice (buying from farmer) must be reconciled against the AR invoice (selling to processor) for intercompany settlement and margin reporting. Agriculture ']
  commodity_price_basis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.price_basis. Business justification: Grain procurement AP invoices are priced using futures + basis methodology. Linking AP invoices to the price_basis master enables basis pricing reconciliation between buy-side and sell-side, a fundame']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi-entity agricultural operations post AP invoices to specific company codes for intercompany clearing, legal entity financial statements, and tax jurisdiction assignment. No existing company_code ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Procurement AP invoices require cost center assignment for expense allocation to crop production cost centers (e.g., allocating fertilizer invoices to specific field operations). Supports cost center-']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Procurement AP invoices require GL account assignment for financial posting (seed expense, chemical expense, fertilizer expense accounts). Supports automatic account determination and financial coding']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Procurement AP invoice posting generates a journal entry (GR/IR clearing, expense recognition). Linking to finance.journal_entry enables 3-way match reconciliation between procurement and finance subl']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: AP invoices are cleared through payment runs. Linking procurement_ap_invoice to the payment_run that settled it enables payment status tracking, cash flow forecasting, and vendor payment reconciliatio']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Goods Receipt Note (GRN) confirming physical delivery of agricultural inputs. Required for three-way match (PO-GRN-Invoice) validation. Null if invoice precedes goods receipt.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Procurement AP invoices for agricultural inputs must be assigned to profit centers for crop enterprise P&L reporting (e.g., allocating fertilizer costs to corn vs. soybean enterprise). Drives enterpri']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Purchase Order (PO) against which this invoice is matched. Used in three-way match validation (PO-GRN-Invoice). Core procurement linkage per SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing document flow.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: AP invoices must be validated against contracted prices, payment terms, and delivery terms. Linking procurement_ap_invoice directly to vendor_contract enables automated price compliance checking (invo']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (supplier) who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master record for agricultural input suppliers (seed, fertilizer, crop protection chemical, feed, fuel, parts). Satisfies PARTY_REFERENCE category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  weight_ticket_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to invoice.weight_ticket. Business justification: Three-way match in grain procurement (PO + GRN + weight ticket) is the core AP settlement process in agriculture. AP invoices must be matched to weight tickets to verify delivered quantity, moisture, ']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this AP invoice within the procurement domain. Approved invoices are forwarded to the Finance domain for payment disbursement and AP ledger posting. Rejected invoices are returned to the vendor or placed on hold.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was formally approved by the procurement team for payment processing. Used for processing SLA measurement, audit trail, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  coa_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s Certificate of Analysis (COA) has been received for regulated agricultural inputs (crop protection chemicals, fertilizers, feed ingredients). COA is required for FSMA compliance and quality acceptance. Missing COA triggers dispute type \'missing_coa_sds\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this AP invoice record was first created in the system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for processing SLA measurement and audit trail.']
  credit_note_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vendor-issued credit note associated with this invoice, issued following dispute resolution (e.g., quality rejection of crop protection chemicals, quantity shortfall). Null if no credit note has been issued.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the vendor invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency procurement under FOB/CIF contract terms with international agricultural input suppliers. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this invoice is currently under formal dispute with the vendor. True when a dispute has been raised; False when no dispute is active. Triggers dispute management workflow within the procurement domain.']
  dispute_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the dispute resolution process for this invoice. Tracks progression from dispute initiation through vendor negotiation to resolution or escalation. Resolved disputes may result in credit note issuance or invoice adjustment.']
  dispute_root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause classification for the invoice dispute, used for supplier performance analytics and process improvement. Examples include vendor billing error, receiving discrepancy, contract interpretation, quality failure, or documentation non-compliance (missing COA/SDS). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vendor_billing_error|receiving_discrepancy|contract_interpretation|quality_failure|documentation_noncompliance|pricing_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Category of dispute raised against the vendor invoice. Includes quantity discrepancy (invoiced vs received), price variance (invoiced vs PO price), quality rejection (input failed QA/COA), duplicate invoice, and missing Certificate of Analysis (COA) or Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for regulated agricultural chemicals. Null if no dispute.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The portion of the invoice amount currently under dispute, expressed in the invoice currency. May be less than the total invoice amount for partial disputes. Used for AP aging and cash flow forecasting.']
  due_date date [note: 'Calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Net 90). Derived from invoice date plus payment term days but stored as a business field for AP aging and cash flow planning.']
  early_payment_discount_date date [note: 'Last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount (e.g., 2/10 Net 30 — pay within 10 days to receive 2% discount). Null if no early payment discount applies.']
  early_payment_discount_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage discount offered by the vendor for early payment (e.g., 0.02 = 2% discount if paid within the early payment discount window). Used for cash flow optimization and dynamic discounting decisions in agricultural input procurement.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number confirming physical receipt of agricultural inputs at the warehouse or farm location. Used for three-way match documentation and audit trail.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total gross amount of the vendor invoice before tax deductions, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the sum of all invoiced line items. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery and risk transfer point for the agricultural input shipment (e.g., FOB — Free On Board, CIF — Cost Insurance and Freight). Determines freight cost allocation and invoice validation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|DAP|DDP|FCA|CFR|CPT|CIP|DAT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  input_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of agricultural inputs covered by this invoice. Drives compliance requirements: crop protection chemicals require EPA/APHIS compliance and SDS documentation; seeds may require GMO declarations; fertilizers require NPK specification review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seeds|fertilizers|crop_protection|feed_ingredients|fuel|parts|equipment|services|other — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the vendor\'s invoice document, representing the principal real-world business event date. Used for payment term calculation, aging analysis, and period accruals. Satisfies BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the AP invoice within the procurement workflow. Drives routing, escalation, and payment eligibility. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|under_review|matched|approved|disputed|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type. Distinguishes standard vendor invoices from credit notes (issued after quality rejection or dispute resolution), debit notes, prepayments, and intercompany transactions for agricultural input procurement.']
  match_discrepancy_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the discrepancy identified during three-way match validation, expressed in the invoice currency. Positive value indicates over-invoicing; negative indicates under-invoicing. Used to determine dispute escalation thresholds.']
  match_discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Type of discrepancy identified during three-way match validation. Quantity variance indicates invoiced quantity differs from GRN quantity; price variance indicates invoiced unit price differs from PO price. Drives dispute initiation and resolution workflow.']
  net_payable_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net amount payable to the vendor after applying taxes, discounts, and any credit adjustments, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the final obligation amount. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  netsuite_ap_doc_ref varchar [note: 'Oracle NetSuite internal AP document reference number assigned upon invoice entry into the AP sub-ledger. Used for cross-system reconciliation between procurement and finance domains.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by procurement staff during invoice processing, dispute resolution, or match exception handling. Captures context for audit trail, vendor communications, and escalation documentation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Vendor-agreed payment terms code as configured in SAP S/4HANA or Oracle NetSuite (e.g., NET30, NET60, NET90, 2/10NET30). Governs due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility for agricultural input supplier invoices.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable Purchase Order (PO) number as printed on the vendor invoice and procurement documents. Used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing documents and vendor communications.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the vendor invoice was physically or electronically received by the procurement team. Used to calculate processing lead time and determine payment due date under net payment terms.']
  sds_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor\'s Safety Data Sheet (SDS) has been received for hazardous agricultural inputs (crop protection chemicals, pesticides, herbicides). SDS is mandatory under EPA and OSHA regulations for chemical handling and storage compliance.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax), expressed in the invoice currency. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for tax reporting and AP ledger posting by the Finance domain.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match validation comparing the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt Note (GRN), and vendor invoice for quantity and price alignment. Core procurement control for agricultural input invoice processing. Matched invoices are eligible for payment approval.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this AP invoice record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks changes during dispute resolution, match correction, and approval workflows.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned invoice number as printed on the vendor\'s invoice document. Used for duplicate invoice detection, vendor reconciliation, and dispute resolution. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable invoice record received from a vendor following delivery of agricultural inputs. Captures vendor invoice number, invoice date, PO reference, GRN reference, invoiced line items with quantities and amounts, tax amount, currency, payment terms (net 30/60/90, early payment discount percentage), due date, three-way match status (PO-GRN-invoice), match discrepancy details, and Oracle NetSuite AP document reference. Includes dispute management: dispute type (quantity discrepancy, price variance, quality rejection, duplicate invoice, missing COA/SDS), disputed amount, resolution status, credit note reference, and root cause category. Procurement-side SSOT for vendor invoice processing and three-way match validation. BOUNDARY: Procurement owns invoice receipt, three-way match validation, and dispute resolution. Finance domain owns payment disbursement, AP ledger posting, and cash management. Three-way match failures escalate within procurement; payment failures escalate to finance.'
}

Table procurement.input_catalog {
  input_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each agricultural input record in the master catalog. Primary key for the input_catalog data product in the procurement domain.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Input catalog agrochemical entries must reference the agrochemical_product master for EPA registration lookup, pre-harvest interval enforcement, SDS management, and label compliance during procurement']
  chemical_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.chemical_compliance. Business justification: Each crop protection chemical or biological input in the catalog must have an associated regulatory compliance record (EPA registration, SDS, MRL, PHI/REI data). input_catalog currently stores epa_reg']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Input catalog entries for commodity-type inputs reference the specific commodity for grade standards, MRL thresholds, and storage requirements. Commodity-level catalog management drives procurement sp']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: Input catalog has denormalized \'crop_target\' plain attribute. Normalizing to crop_id enforces referential integrity for EPA label compliance (inputs registered for specific crops), MRL reporting by cr']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Input catalog items (seeds, chemicals, fertilizers) carry default GL accounts for automatic account determination during PO creation — standard SAP MM account determination pattern. Agriculture operat']
  grading_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Input catalog entries for commodity inputs must reference the applicable grading standard to define quality acceptance criteria for procurement. Goods receipt inspection and vendor scorecard quality m']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Input catalog entries for agrochemicals carry a denormalized mrl_ppm value. Normalizing via FK to mrl_threshold supports regulatory compliance screening at catalog entry time and ensures procurement u']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Input catalog entries for seed products reference the specific seed variety for trait package, maturity rating, and regulatory attributes (APHIS petition, PVP number). Variety-level catalog management']
  active_ingredient varchar [note: 'Primary active ingredient(s) in crop protection chemicals or veterinary products (e.g., \'glyphosate\', \'chlorpyrifos\', \'azoxystrobin\'). Critical for MRL compliance, PHI determination, and HACCP hazard analysis. Null for non-chemical inputs.']
  active_ingredient_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Concentration of the active ingredient expressed as a percentage of the total formulation (e.g., 41.0 for 41% glyphosate). Used for application rate calculations, MRL compliance verification, and SDS documentation.']
  aphis_permit_category varchar [note: 'USDA APHIS permit classification for biological inputs including biocontrol agents, plant pests, and veterinary biologicals. Required for import, interstate movement, and field release of regulated biological organisms. Null or \'not_applicable\' for non-biological inputs.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this input was approved and added to the active procurement catalog following vendor qualification, regulatory compliance verification, and internal approval workflow. Supports audit trail for GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI certification.']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure for procurement and inventory transactions (e.g., KG, MT, L, GAL, BU, CWT, EA). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM base UOM and drives GRN quantity validation and stock position calculations.']
  catalog_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the input in the master catalog. Controls whether the input is available for purchase requisition and PO creation in SAP S/4HANA MM. \'restricted\' indicates regulatory or internal procurement hold.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this input was manufactured or produced. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, import documentation, and APHIS phytosanitary permit requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost and pricing fields (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'BRL\'). Supports multi-currency procurement operations and IFRS financial reporting.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Pesticide or fertilizer formulation type code (e.g., EC=Emulsifiable Concentrate, WP=Wettable Powder, SC=Suspension Concentrate, WG=Water Dispersible Granule, GR=Granule, SL=Soluble Liquid). Affects application equipment compatibility and handling procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EC|WP|SC|WG|GR|SL|CS|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gmo_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the input (primarily seeds) is genetically modified (GMO), non-GMO, conventional (no GMO claim), or not applicable for non-seed inputs. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and customer supply chain transparency.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'DOT hazardous materials classification code for transport and storage compliance (e.g., \'3\' for flammable liquids/fuels, \'6.1\' for toxic substances, \'8\' for corrosives). Null for non-hazardous inputs. Required for BOL documentation and TMS routing.']
  input_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the agricultural input type. Drives procurement workflows, regulatory compliance checks, and SDS documentation requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|fuel|spare_part|operational_supply|biological — promote to reference product]']
  input_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable commercial or trade name of the agricultural input (e.g., \'Roundup PowerMAX\', \'Pioneer P1197AM\', \'Urea 46-0-0\'). Used for display, reporting, and procurement communications.']
  input_subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular classification within the input category (e.g., for crop_protection: herbicide, insecticide, fungicide, nematicide; for fertilizer: nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, micronutrient, organic). Supports IPM program management and NPK spend analytics.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory and compliance review of this catalog entry, including EPA registration status, MRL updates, SDS currency, and vendor qualification renewal. Supports periodic review cycle management per GLOBALG.A.P. and FSMA requirements.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from PO issuance to GRN for this input from the preferred vendor. Used for reorder point calculation, planting season procurement planning, and supply chain risk management.']
  lot_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this input requires lot/batch number tracking throughout the supply chain for traceability, recall management, and FSMA compliance. True for seeds, crop protection chemicals, feed ingredients, and biologicals.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique material number assigned in SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle NetSuite for this agricultural input. Used as the primary cross-system business identifier for procurement, inventory, and AP processing.']
  minimum_order_qty decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum quantity per purchase order as required by the preferred vendor, expressed in the purchase UOM. Prevents creation of POs below vendor minimums and supports procurement cost optimization.']
  mode_of_action varchar [note: 'Biochemical mode of action classification for crop protection chemicals using HRAC (herbicides), IRAC (insecticides), or FRAC (fungicides) group codes (e.g., \'HRAC Group 9\', \'IRAC Group 4A\'). Critical for IPM resistance management programs.']
  npk_grade varchar [note: 'NPK nutrient analysis expressed as N-P-K percentage (e.g., \'46-0-0\' for urea, \'18-46-0\' for DAP, \'0-0-60\' for potash). Mandatory for all fertilizer inputs. Null for non-fertilizer inputs. Drives soil fertility planning and nutrient management compliance.']
  organic_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this input is certified for use in USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified operations. True only for inputs on the USDA NOP National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances. Critical for organic farm procurement compliance.']
  organic_certifier varchar [note: 'Name of the USDA-accredited certifying agent that issued the organic certification for this input (e.g., \'CCOF\', \'Oregon Tilth\', \'OMRI\'). Required when organic_certified is True. Supports NOP audit trail and supplier qualification.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of a crop protection chemical and harvest, as specified on the EPA-registered label. Enforced during crop protection event scheduling to ensure MRL compliance.']
  purchase_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used on purchase orders and vendor invoices, which may differ from the base UOM (e.g., purchased in MT but stocked in KG). Supports PO creation and AP invoice matching in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated areas after pesticide application, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Critical for workforce safety compliance and OSHA reporting.']
  restricted_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crop protection chemical is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring a certified pesticide applicator license for purchase and application. Drives applicator certification verification in procurement workflow.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is required for this input under OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) and EPA regulations. True for all crop protection chemicals, fertilizers, and fuels. Drives SDS document management workflow.']
  seed_treatment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether seeds are chemically treated (fungicide/insecticide coating), untreated, organically treated, or not applicable for non-seed inputs. Affects handling requirements, PHI/REI applicability, and USDA NOP organic certification eligibility.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days this input remains effective and safe from the date of manufacture or receipt, as specified by the manufacturer. Drives FIFO/FEFO inventory management, reorder policy, and expiry-based quarantine holds in the WMS.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'EPA-mandated signal word on the pesticide label indicating acute toxicity level: DANGER (highest toxicity, Category I/II), WARNING (moderate toxicity, Category III), CAUTION (lowest toxicity, Category IV), or not_applicable for non-pesticide inputs. Required for SDS and worker safety training.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned or internal SKU code for this input, used for order management, barcode scanning, and RFID tracking in the warehouse. Supports Manhattan Associates WMS receiving and inventory control operations.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for this input to maintain efficacy, safety, and regulatory compliance. Drives WMS bin location assignment in Manhattan Associates and warehouse task planning for proper segregation of incompatible materials.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all procurable agricultural inputs including seeds (variety, GMO/non-GMO, treated/untreated), fertilizers (NPK grades, micronutrients, organic), crop protection chemicals (herbicides, insecticides, fungicides with AI and MRL data), feed ingredients, fuel types, spare parts, and operational supplies. Captures material code, input category, unit of measure, EPA registration number (for chemicals), APHIS permit category (for biologicals), SDS required flag, PHI/REI values, MRL limits, and preferred vendor list.'
}

Table procurement.chemical_compliance {
  chemical_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each chemical compliance record in the procurement domain. Primary key for the chemical_compliance data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a regulatory compliance record for a procured crop protection chemical or biological input.']
  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agrochemical product master record in the product domain. Links this compliance record to the specific crop protection chemical or biological input product being regulated.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FSMA Preventive Controls and EPA regulations require a named responsible person for each chemical compliance record. Ag compliance officers are accountable for SDS currency, MRL limits, and registrati']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: Chemical compliance records specify which crop a pesticide is registered for and its MRL. Linking to crop enables EPA label compliance verification, MRL reporting by crop, and FSMA preventive control ']
  mrl_threshold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Chemical compliance records track MRL values (mrl_value, mrl_commodity, mrl_market) that are denormalized from the mrl_threshold product. Normalizing via FK supports FSMA preventive controls documenta']
  active_ingredient_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Percentage concentration of the active ingredient (AI) in the formulated product, expressed as weight/weight (w/w) or weight/volume (w/v) percent. Used for dose calculation, MRL compliance, and SDS documentation.']
  active_ingredient_name varchar [note: 'Common chemical name of the primary active ingredient (AI) responsible for the pesticidal action of the product, as listed on the EPA-approved label. Critical for MRL compliance checks and PHI determination.']
  aphis_import_permit_number varchar [note: 'APHIS-issued permit number authorizing the importation of regulated biological inputs, biopesticides, or plant protection products. Required for cross-border procurement of biological agents under APHIS jurisdiction.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the chemical compliance record. \'active\' indicates the product is currently registered and compliant for use; \'suspended\' indicates temporary regulatory hold; \'cancelled\' indicates EPA registration cancellation; \'pending_review\' indicates under regulatory review; \'expired\' indicates registration or SDS has lapsed.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the active ingredient concentration value (e.g., % w/w, % w/v, g/L). Ensures correct interpretation of concentration for compliance calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this chemical compliance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for OSHA HazCom, EPA regulatory reporting, and FSMA recordkeeping. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA QM or Oracle NetSuite document creation timestamp.']
  disposal_instructions varchar [note: 'Disposal methods and instructions for the chemical product and its container as specified in SDS Section 13. Includes EPA waste classification, disposal facility requirements, and container rinsing procedures. Required for EPA RCRA compliance and environmental management.']
  emergency_contact varchar [note: '24-hour emergency telephone number for chemical emergency response, as listed on SDS Section 1 (e.g., CHEMTREC 1-800-424-9300). Required by OSHA HazCom and DOT hazardous materials regulations for emergency response coordination.']
  epa_registration_number varchar [note: 'EPA-issued registration number for the pesticide product under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA). Required for legal sale and use in the United States. Format: registrant-product[-supplemental].']
  first_aid_measures varchar [note: 'Summary of first aid instructions for exposure routes (inhalation, skin contact, eye contact, ingestion) as specified in SDS Section 4. Required for OSHA HazCom compliance and emergency response planning at field and warehouse locations.']
  formulation_type varchar [note: 'Standardized formulation type code for the crop protection product (e.g., EC=Emulsifiable Concentrate, WP=Wettable Powder, SC=Suspension Concentrate, WG=Water Dispersible Granule, GR=Granule, SL=Soluble Liquid). Used for application equipment selection, mixing instructions, and storage classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EC|WP|SC|WG|GR|SL|EW|CS|DP|UL|SP|ZC — promote to reference product]']
  gap_certification_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical compliance record is subject to GAP certification requirements, including GLOBALG.A.P. IFA or equivalent scheme. When true, the chemical\'s use must be documented in the farm\'s GAP compliance records and subject to third-party audit.']
  ghs_hazard_classification varchar [note: 'GHS hazard classification category for the chemical product, identifying the type and severity of physical, health, and environmental hazards (e.g., \'Acute Toxicity Category 3\', \'Flammable Liquid Category 2\'). Drives PPE requirements, storage segregation, and emergency response planning.']
  haccp_critical_control_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical input is identified as a critical control point (CCP) in the facility\'s HACCP plan. When true, mandatory monitoring, corrective action, and verification procedures apply per Codex Alimentarius HACCP principles and FSMA Preventive Controls.']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Standardized GHS H-code hazard statements describing the nature and degree of hazards posed by the chemical (e.g., \'H301: Toxic if swallowed; H400: Very toxic to aquatic life\'). Stored as a semicolon-delimited list of H-codes and descriptions. Required on SDS Section 2.']
  is_fsma_preventive_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this chemical input is subject to FSMA preventive controls requirements, triggering mandatory hazard analysis, preventive control measures, monitoring, corrective actions, and recordkeeping under the FSMA Preventive Controls for Human Food rule.']
  is_gmo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the biological input contains or is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO). Required for APHIS biotechnology regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and organic certification exclusion under USDA National Organic Program (NOP).']
  is_restricted_use_pesticide boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring purchase and use only by certified applicators or persons under their direct supervision. Critical for procurement authorization and applicator certification tracking.']
  label_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product label has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with all applicable EPA label requirements, including use directions, safety precautions, PHI, REI, and first aid statements. Required for GAP certification and HACCP compliance documentation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this chemical compliance record. Tracks changes to SDS revisions, registration status updates, MRL changes, or label amendments. Required for regulatory audit trail and change management.']
  phi_days varchar [note: 'Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of the chemical and the harvest of the crop. Mandatory for GAP compliance and FSMA preventive controls to ensure MRL compliance at harvest.']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Required personal protective equipment (PPE) for handling, mixing, loading, and applying the chemical product as specified on the EPA label and SDS Section 8. Includes gloves, respirator type, eye protection, and protective clothing specifications. Mandatory for EPA Worker Protection Standard compliance.']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Standardized GHS P-code precautionary statements describing recommended measures to minimize or prevent adverse effects from exposure, storage, or disposal (e.g., \'P260: Do not breathe dust/fume; P501: Dispose of contents in accordance with local regulations\'). Stored as semicolon-delimited P-codes.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the crop protection product by its primary mode of action or target pest category. Drives regulatory pathway, label requirements, and IPM program classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: herbicide|insecticide|fungicide|rodenticide|biopesticide|plant_growth_regulator|adjuvant|nematicide|bactericide — promote to reference product]']
  registration_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the EPA registration for this chemical product became effective. Marks the start of the legally authorized period for sale and use of the product in the United States under FIFRA.']
  registration_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the EPA registration for this chemical product expires or is due for renewal. Null for registrations without a fixed expiry. Triggers procurement hold and re-registration workflow when approaching expiry.']
  rei_hours varchar [note: 'Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours — the minimum time that must pass after a pesticide application before workers may re-enter the treated area without personal protective equipment (PPE). Required for OSHA HazCom and EPA Worker Protection Standard compliance.']
  sds_document_number varchar [note: 'Unique document control number assigned to the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for this chemical product. Used for document management, OSHA HazCom compliance, and audit trail. Corresponds to the SDS document identifier in the supplier\'s document management system.']
  sds_expiry_date date [note: 'Date by which the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) must be reviewed and reissued or confirmed current. Typically set 3-5 years from the revision date per industry practice. Triggers SDS renewal workflow to maintain OSHA HazCom compliance.']
  sds_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) as issued by the chemical manufacturer or supplier. OSHA requires SDS to be updated within 3 months of new hazard information. Critical for ensuring workers have access to current hazard information.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS/EPA signal word appearing on the product label and SDS indicating the relative level of hazard severity: DANGER (most severe), WARNING (moderate), CAUTION (least severe), or none for non-hazardous products. Required on SDS Section 2 and product label.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (USPS abbreviation) for which the state_registration_status applies. Enables state-by-state compliance tracking for pesticide products that require individual state registrations under FIFRA Section 24(c).']
  state_registration_status varchar [note: 'Registration status of the pesticide product in the specific state(s) where it will be used. States may impose additional registration requirements beyond federal EPA registration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: registered|not_registered|pending|cancelled|restricted — promote to reference product if multi-state tracking is required]']
  storage_requirements varchar [note: 'Storage conditions and requirements for the chemical product as specified in SDS Section 7, including temperature range, humidity, segregation from incompatible materials, ventilation, and container requirements. Used for warehouse storage planning and EPA/OSHA compliance.']

  Note: 'Regulatory compliance record for each procured crop protection chemical and biological input, including associated Safety Data Sheet (SDS) documentation. Captures EPA registration number, APHIS import permit number, active ingredient (AI) name and concentration, MRL values by commodity and market, PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) in days, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) in hours, restricted use pesticide (RUP) flag, state-specific registration status, label compliance confirmation, and FSMA preventive controls applicability flag. Includes SDS attributes: SDS document number, revision date, GHS hazard classification, signal word, hazard and precautionary statements, first aid measures, storage requirements, disposal instructions, PPE requirements, emergency contact, and SDS expiry/review date. Required for OSHA HazCom compliance, EPA regulatory reporting, GAP certification, and HACCP compliance.'
}

Table procurement.delivery_schedule {
