  agrochemical_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Delivery schedules for agrochemicals reference the specific product for hazmat transport compliance (UN number, hazmat class) and PHI/REI application timing planning. Product-level delivery scheduling']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Delivery schedules for commodity inputs reference the specific commodity for storage planning and processing scheduling. Commodity-level delivery tracking drives elevator and feed mill capacity planni']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Input delivery schedules (seed, fertilizer, chemicals) are planned to specific farm units for seasonal application timing. Linking delivery_schedule to farm_unit enables logistics coordination, ensure']
  field_crop_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.field_crop_plan. Business justification: Input delivery schedules are planned against specific field crop plans to align delivery timing with planting and application windows. This link enables on-time delivery tracking per field plan, suppo']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Field-level delivery scheduling drives application timing and logistics in precision agriculture — inputs must arrive at the right field before planting or application windows. Currently delivery_sche']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: Input delivery schedules are planned around planting windows within a growing season. A proper FK enables delivery-vs-planting-window compliance reporting and seasonal logistics planning. Replaces den']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural input material (seed, fertilizer NPK, crop protection chemical AI, feed ingredient, fuel, parts) being scheduled for delivery. Links to the material master for specifications, MRL compliance, and SDS documentation.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Purchase Order (PO) or blanket PO under which this delivery schedule line is created. Links to the procurement PO master for contract terms, pricing, and vendor details.']
  receiving_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Agriculture delivery scheduling requires directing inbound shipments to specific grain elevators or warehouses for capacity planning and receiving dock scheduling. The existing delivery_location_id po']
  seed_variety_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Delivery schedules for seed procurement specify the variety being delivered. Seed delivery timing is variety-specific (planting window, maturity). Variety-level delivery scheduling is essential for pl']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: The domain description explicitly states delivery schedules are created \'under a vendor contract or blanket PO.\' While the PO link already exists (delivery_schedule.purchase_order_id → purchase_order)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the qualified vendor or supplier responsible for fulfilling this delivery schedule line. Supports vendor performance tracking and USDA/EPA-compliant supplier qualification records.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the agricultural input was physically received at the delivery location. Populated upon Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing. Used for on-time delivery KPI calculation and vendor performance scoring.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading (BOL) number issued by the carrier as the legal contract of carriage and receipt of goods. Required for FSMA traceability, COOL compliance, and AP invoice matching. Critical for agricultural chemical and biological shipment documentation.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the transportation carrier or logistics provider responsible for delivering the agricultural input. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight audit.']
  carrier_reference varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned reference or tracking number for the shipment. Enables real-time shipment tracking and exception management for in-transit deliveries.']
  coa_number varchar [note: 'Certificate of Analysis (COA) document number provided by the vendor confirming the quality specifications of the delivered agricultural input (e.g., NPK analysis for fertilizers, AI concentration for crop protection chemicals, nutritional profile for feed ingredients).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery schedule record was first created in the system, sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle NetSuite. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial reporting controls and FSMA traceability.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price and transaction value (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency procurement operations and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why a delivery schedule line has been delayed or cancelled. Supports supply chain risk analysis, vendor performance management, and contingency planning for critical agricultural inputs.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery destination type. Determines receiving procedures, storage requirements, and compliance checks (e.g., feed mill requires FDA feed safety compliance; chemical warehouse requires SDS and EPA storage regulations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: farm_gate|grain_elevator|warehouse|feed_mill|fuel_tank|processing_plant — promote to reference product]']
  estimated_arrival_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the estimated arrival window at the delivery location. Together with estimated_arrival_start defines the delivery window for receiving operations and carrier coordination.']
  estimated_arrival_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the estimated arrival window at the delivery location. Supports dock scheduling, receiving staff planning, and cold-chain management for perishable biologicals.']
  grn_number varchar [note: 'Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number generated upon physical receipt and inspection of the agricultural input at the delivery location. Triggers three-way match (PO-GRN-Invoice) in SAP S/4HANA MM and Oracle NetSuite AP.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (INCOTERMS) code defining the point of risk and cost transfer between vendor and buyer. Common agricultural trade terms include FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight). Governs freight cost allocation and insurance responsibility.']
  input_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural input material being delivered. Drives compliance workflows: crop protection chemicals require SDS and EPA/APHIS documentation; fertilizers require NPK specification; seeds require GMO and phytosanitary certificates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|fuel|parts|supplies — promote to reference product]']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agricultural input is a perishable biological product (e.g., live beneficial insects, biostimulants, inoculants, vaccines) requiring cold-chain management and expedited just-in-time delivery. Triggers special handling and temperature monitoring requirements.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery schedule record. Tracks schedule revisions, status changes, and quantity adjustments. Required for data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer and SOX audit compliance.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or Lot Number (LOT) assigned to the agricultural input shipment by the manufacturer or vendor. Enables end-to-end traceability from supplier to field application, supporting FSMA Section 204 traceability, recall management, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) matching.']
  phi_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivered crop protection chemical requires Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) compliance tracking. When True, the receiving system must enforce PHI restrictions to ensure MRL compliance at harvest. Applicable to pesticides and herbicides with EPA-registered PHI requirements.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'The principal planned calendar date on which the agricultural input is scheduled to arrive at the delivery location. Aligned with seasonal crop planting calendars and livestock feeding program schedules to ensure timely availability.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of agricultural input received and confirmed via Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing. May differ from scheduled_quantity due to short shipments, overages, or quality rejections. Triggers AP invoice matching in Oracle NetSuite.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the delivery schedule line, typically sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM scheduling agreement or Purchase Order (PO) schedule line. Used for cross-system reconciliation and vendor communication.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule line. Tracks progression from initial planning through vendor confirmation, transit, receipt, and exception states (delayed, cancelled). Supports just-in-time delivery monitoring and supply continuity planning.']
  scheduled_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The planned quantity of agricultural input material to be delivered on this schedule line, expressed in the base unit of measure (e.g., MT metric tons, BU bushels, CWT hundredweight, liters, units). Drives pre-positioning and inventory planning.']
  sds_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is required for this delivery. Mandatory for crop protection chemicals, fertilizers, and fuel deliveries per EPA and OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom). SDS must accompany shipment documentation.']
  seasonal_program varchar [note: 'Name or code of the seasonal agricultural program this delivery schedule supports (e.g., \'Spring Planting 2025\', \'Fall Fertilizer Pre-Position\', \'Q3 Feed Ingredient Program\'). Enables grouping of schedule lines by crop season or livestock feeding cycle for supply continuity planning.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for delivering the agricultural input. Common modes include truck (road), rail, barge (waterway), and air (for perishable biologicals). Influences BOL documentation requirements and freight cost allocation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the scheduled and received quantities. Common agricultural units include MT (Metric Ton), BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), KG (Kilogram), LT (Liter), GAL (Gallon). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA base UOM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MT|BU|CWT|KG|LT|GAL|TON|BAG|UNIT — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Contracted unit price for the agricultural input as agreed in the Purchase Order (PO) or vendor contract. Used for goods receipt valuation, AP invoice matching, and spend analytics. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity of pricing terms.']
  vendor_confirmed_date date [note: 'The delivery date formally confirmed by the vendor or supplier, which may differ from the planned delivery date. Used for vendor performance measurement and supply risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Planned delivery schedule for agricultural inputs under a vendor contract or blanket PO, aligned with seasonal crop planting calendars and livestock feeding programs. Captures schedule line number, PO or contract reference, input material, planned delivery date, scheduled quantity, delivery location (farm gate, grain elevator, warehouse, feed mill, fuel tank), transport mode (truck, rail, barge), carrier reference, bill of lading (BOL) number, estimated arrival window, and schedule status (planned, vendor-confirmed, in-transit, received, delayed, cancelled). Supports just-in-time delivery of perishable biologicals, seasonal fertilizer pre-positioning, and feed ingredient supply continuity planning.'
}

Table procurement.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for budget']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who formally approved this procurement budget allocation. Distinct from the budget owner; the approver may be a finance controller or senior manager. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  budget_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager formally accountable for this procurement budget. The budget owner is responsible for approving spend requests, monitoring variance, and authorizing budget revisions. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors HR.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Procurement budgets in agriculture are allocated by commodity (e.g., corn seed budget, soybean fertilizer budget). Commodity-level budget tracking enables spend-to-budget reporting by crop and drives ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Procurement budgets in multi-entity agriculture operations are scoped to a legal entity for consolidated financial reporting, intercompany budget allocation, and USDA program payment tracking by entit']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which this procurement budget is allocated. Enables OPEX/CAPEX tracking and spend analytics by organizational unit.']
  crop_enterprise_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: The procurement operational budget references the finance-side crop enterprise budget to ensure input procurement spending aligns with the crop-level financial plan. Supports integrated budget managem']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm enterprise or business unit to which this budget is assigned. Supports spend analytics segmented by farm operation within the agricultural enterprise.']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Field-level budget allocation is a standard precision agriculture financial management process — input cost budgets, yield revenue projections, and profitability analysis are tracked per field. Curren']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Procurement budgets for agricultural inputs map to GL accounts (seed expense, fertilizer expense, pesticide expense) for budget vs. actual variance reporting. The existing gl_account_code is a denorma']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Agricultural farm budgets are built at position level for headcount and labor cost planning (H2A positions, seasonal labor). Linking budget to position enables labor budget vs. actual payroll variance']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of procurement spend actually incurred to date against this budget, based on posted Goods Receipt Notes (GRNs) and approved vendor invoices processed through SAP MM/FI and Oracle NetSuite AP. Represents confirmed expenditure.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount formally allocated and approved for procurement spend within this budget record for the defined period and input category. Represents the authorized spending ceiling before any commitments or actuals are recorded.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this procurement budget was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from draft to approved status and establishes the authorization date for audit and SOX compliance purposes.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this procurement budget record within the ERP and farm management systems. Used for cross-system reconciliation between SAP CO and Granular Farm Management.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the procurement budget record (e.g., \'FY2025 Q2 Seed & Fertilizer - North Farm\'). Used in reporting dashboards and budget owner communications.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the procurement budget record. Draft budgets are under preparation; approved budgets are authorized for spend; active budgets have open commitments; frozen budgets are temporarily suspended; closed budgets have completed their period; cancelled budgets are voided.']
  capex_opex_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this procurement budget is classified as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) for long-term asset acquisition (e.g., irrigation infrastructure, equipment) or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) for recurring inputs (e.g., seeds, fertilizers, agrochemicals, fuel). Critical for financial reporting under GAAP and IFRS.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of open and confirmed Purchase Orders (POs) that have been raised against this budget but not yet invoiced or goods-receipted. Derived from open PO commitments in SAP MM. Represents encumbered budget that is no longer freely available.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this procurement budget record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation event required for SOX financial controls and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this budget record are denominated (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency agricultural operations and IFRS reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period granularity of this budget record — annual for full-year budgets or quarterly (Q1–Q4) for sub-annual allocations. Supports both annual OPEX planning and quarterly reforecast cycles.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (e.g., 2025) to which this procurement budget allocation applies. Aligns with the agricultural enterprise\'s financial reporting calendar in SAP FI/CO.']
  input_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural input type covered by this budget record. Drives spend analytics by input category and supports NPK fertilizer, AI (Active Ingredient) agrochemical, and feed ingredient cost tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|agrochemical|feed|fuel|parts|supplies|other — promote to reference product]']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procurement budget record is locked against further modifications (True) or remains open for amendment (False). Budgets are typically locked at period close or upon reaching a frozen status to preserve financial integrity.']
  last_revision_date date [note: 'The date on which this procurement budget record was most recently revised or amended. Tracks the most recent budget adjustment event, supporting audit trails and reforecast cycle management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for budget owner or finance controller annotations, including justification for budget revisions, seasonal adjustments, or compliance notes related to EPA/APHIS regulated input categories such as agrochemicals.']
  original_allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial budget amount as approved at the start of the budget period, before any revisions or amendments. Preserved for variance analysis comparing original plan versus revised plan versus actuals — a key OPEX control metric.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this budget period closes. Defines the boundary for spend commitment and actuals accumulation within this budget record.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this budget period becomes effective. For annual budgets this is typically the first day of the fiscal year; for quarterly budgets it is the first day of the quarter.']
  remaining_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The unencumbered budget balance remaining for new procurement commitments, calculated as allocated_amount minus committed_amount minus actual_spend_amount. Provides real-time budget availability for procurement controllers and farm managers.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer counter tracking how many times this procurement budget has been revised since initial approval. Version 0 represents the original approved budget; each subsequent amendment increments this counter. Supports budget reforecast audit trails.']
  spend_to_budget_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the allocated budget that has been consumed by actual spend to date (actual_spend_amount / allocated_amount × 100). A key KPI for procurement controllers to monitor budget utilization rate and flag potential overspend risk.']
  transfer_in_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount transferred into this budget record from other budget lines or cost centers during the period. Supports budget reallocation tracking between input categories or farm enterprises.']
  transfer_out_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount transferred out of this budget record to other budget lines or cost centers during the period. Together with transfer_in_amount, enables full reconciliation of budget movements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this procurement budget record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and provides the audit trail update event for SOX compliance.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary difference between the allocated budget amount and the total of committed plus actual spend. A positive value indicates underspend; a negative value indicates overspend. Used for OPEX control reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  variance_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the allocated budget amount (variance_amount / allocated_amount × 100). Enables proportional comparison of budget performance across cost centers, input categories, and farm enterprises of different sizes.']

  Note: 'Procurement budget allocation record by input category, cost center, farm enterprise, and fiscal period. Captures budget period (annual/quarterly), input category (seed, fertilizer, agrochemical, feed, fuel, parts), allocated budget amount, committed spend (derived from open POs), actual spend to date, remaining budget, variance amount and percentage, budget owner, and last revision date. Enables OPEX control, CAPEX vs. OPEX classification for agricultural inputs, and spend analytics by category, season, and farm enterprise. Sourced from Granular Farm Management and SAP CO.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each employee record in the agricultural enterprise. Primary key for all workforce identity data across farm operations and processing facilities.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each employee is employed by a specific legal entity (company code) for W-2 issuance, payroll tax filing, ACA reporting, and workers\' comp coverage. Multi-entity ag operations (farm LLCs under a holdi']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct supervisor or manager. Enables organizational hierarchy traversal for reporting structures, approval workflows, and labor cost roll-up. Self-referencing relationship within the employee master.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Employee\'s date of birth. Required for age verification, H-2A visa compliance, minor labor law compliance, and benefits eligibility determination.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the employee\'s country of origin or citizenship. Required for H-2A visa program reporting, MSPA worker disclosure, and workforce diversity analytics. Examples: MEX, GTM, JAM, USA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports GDPR data subject access request timelines.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the employee is assigned. Examples include Crop Operations, Livestock Operations, Equipment & Fleet, Supply Chain, Quality & Compliance, Finance, Human Resources, Research & Development. Used for labor cost allocation in SAP CO module.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee\'s designated emergency contact person. Required for all field workers, equipment operators, and processing facility staff per OSHA agricultural safety standards and H-2A worker protection requirements.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the employee\'s designated emergency contact. Used by farm safety officers and HR in the event of a workplace injury, illness, or emergency in field or processing facility environments.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee\'s employment relationship with the enterprise. Drives access control, payroll processing, and benefits eligibility. Active = currently employed; On_Leave = approved leave of absence; Terminated = employment ended; Suspended = temporarily suspended pending investigation; Retired = formally retired.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement. Permanent = year-round staff; Seasonal = hired for specific agricultural seasons (planting, harvest); Temporary = short-term non-seasonal; Contract = third-party contracted labor; Intern = internship program. Critical for H-2A visa tracking and MSPA compliance.']
  everify_case_number varchar [note: 'The case number assigned by the DHS E-Verify system upon submission of the employee\'s I-9 data for electronic employment eligibility verification. Required for federal contractors and many agricultural employers. Enables audit trail for USCIS compliance reviews.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the employee as recorded on government-issued identification. Used for payroll, compliance documentation, and I-9 verification.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-reported gender of the employee. Used for EEO-1 reporting, workforce diversity analytics, and OSHA recordkeeping requirements.']
  h2a_visa_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the H-2A temporary agricultural worker visa. Triggers automated alerts for visa renewal or worker departure compliance. Critical for DOL and USCIS audit readiness. Null for non-H-2A employees.']
  h2a_visa_number varchar [note: 'Visa document number for employees admitted under the H-2A Temporary Agricultural Worker program. Used to track visa validity, DOL job order compliance, and APHIS worker protection requirements. Null for non-H-2A employees.']
  hire_date date [note: 'The official date the employee commenced employment with the enterprise. Used for tenure calculations, benefits eligibility vesting, seniority-based scheduling, and seasonal rehire tracking.']
  i9_verification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the employee\'s Form I-9 employment eligibility verification. Mandatory for all US employees under IRCA. Tracks whether identity and work authorization documents have been reviewed and verified. Reverification_required indicates documents are approaching expiration.']
  is_seasonal_rehire_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire in a future agricultural season based on prior performance, compliance record, and voluntary departure. Supports seasonal workforce planning and H-2A returning worker preference programs.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the employee as defined in the HR system. Examples include Field Agronomist, Equipment Operator, Livestock Technician, Pesticide Applicator, Harvest Supervisor, Processing Facility Worker, Farm Manager. Used for OSHA recordkeeping and pesticide applicator license tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and SOX audit trail compliance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name of the employee as recorded on government-issued identification. Used for payroll, compliance documentation, and I-9 verification.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Primary mobile phone number for the employee. Used for emergency notifications, scheduling communications, and field operations coordination. Critical for reaching seasonal and migrant farm workers.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in the US). Used for I-9 verification, E-Verify compliance, tax reporting, and MSPA worker disclosure requirements.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Compensation structure for the employee. Salaried = fixed annual compensation; Hourly = paid per hour worked; Piece_Rate = paid per unit of agricultural output (e.g., per bin of fruit picked); Daily = fixed daily rate. Piece-rate is common in harvest operations and has specific FLSA compliance requirements.']
  personal_email varchar [note: 'Personal email address of the employee. Used for communications with seasonal and H-2A workers who may not have enterprise email, and for post-termination correspondence.']
  pesticide_applicator_license_number varchar [note: 'State-issued pesticide applicator license number for employees authorized to apply restricted-use pesticides (RUPs). Required by EPA and state lead agencies for compliance with FIFRA. Null for employees not performing pesticide applications.']
  pesticide_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s state-issued pesticide applicator license. Triggers recertification workflows to ensure continuous compliance with EPA FIFRA and state regulations. Null for non-applicator employees.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'The name the employee prefers to be called in day-to-day operations. May differ from legal name. Used in operational communications and scheduling systems.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the employee\'s primary spoken language. Critical for ensuring GAP, HACCP, and pesticide safety training is delivered in the worker\'s language as required by OSHA and EPA Worker Protection Standard. Examples: es (Spanish), en (English), ht (Haitian Creole).']
  successfactors_employee_code varchar [note: 'The employee identifier assigned in SAP SuccessFactors HR system. Used for cross-system integration with payroll, talent management, and labor cost allocation modules. This is the system-of-record identifier from the HR platform.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The date the employee\'s employment was formally ended. Null for active employees. Used for final pay calculations, benefits termination, access revocation, and seasonal rehire eligibility assessment.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the employee\'s departure from the enterprise. Critical for seasonal rehire eligibility, unemployment insurance claims, and workforce planning analytics. End_of_season is common for H-2A and seasonal agricultural workers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_resignation|end_of_season|layoff|termination_for_cause|retirement|death|contract_end — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_authorization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s legal authorization to work in the United States. H-2A is the primary agricultural guest worker visa program. Drives compliance workflows for USCIS, DOL, and APHIS requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: us_citizen|permanent_resident|h2a_visa|ead|tn_visa|other_visa|asylum|daca — promote to reference product]']
  work_email varchar [note: 'Official enterprise email address assigned to the employee. Primary digital contact channel for HR communications, system access notifications, and training assignments.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary farm, field complex, or processing facility where the employee is assigned. Used for labor cost allocation by location, OSHA site-specific recordkeeping, and field operations scheduling. Aligns with Granular Farm Management location hierarchy.']

  Note: 'Master record for all agricultural enterprise personnel — permanent staff, seasonal farm workers, agronomists, equipment operators, livestock technicians, processing facility workers, and administrative staff. Captures full identity, employment classification (H-2A visa, migrant, domestic, salaried, hourly), job title, department, hire date, termination date, employment status, work authorization type, I-9 verification status, emergency contact, and SAP SuccessFactors employee ID. SSOT for all workforce identity data across farm operations and processing facilities. Supports E-Verify compliance, MSPA worker disclosure, and seasonal rehire tracking.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each authorized headcount position within the agricultural enterprise organizational structure. Primary key for the position data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  crop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: Agricultural positions are crop-specific (corn detasseler, strawberry picker, tobacco harvester). Linking position to crop supports H2A job order preparation (DOL requires crop specification), crop-sp']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm operation, processing facility, grain elevator, or distribution center to which this position is physically assigned.']
  farm_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_operation. Business justification: Agricultural workforce headcount planning and H-2A job order filing are done at the farm operation level. Linking position to farm_operation enables operation-specific budgeted headcount tracking, DOL']
  parent_position_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the supervisory position directly above this position in the organizational hierarchy, enabling reporting line traversal.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: In agriculture, positions belong to specific crop or livestock enterprise profit centers for headcount budgeting and labor cost P&L reporting. Budgeted headcount by profit center is a standard ag ente']
  budgeted_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of authorized headcount slots budgeted for this position within the current fiscal planning period. Supports workforce planning, labor budget management, and variance analysis against actual staffing levels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this position record was first created in the system, used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance recordkeeping purposes.']
  eeo_job_category varchar [note: 'EEO-1 job category classification for this position as defined by the EEOC, required for annual EEO-1 Component 1 workforce data reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive_senior_officials|first_mid_officials|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|administrative_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers_helpers|service_workers — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this position becomes or became effective within the organizational structure, marking the start of its authorized existence for headcount planning, DOL H-2A job order validity, and labor compliance purposes.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement for this position, distinguishing between permanent year-round roles, seasonal agricultural positions, temporary placements, contract workers, and H-2A guest worker positions under the DOL temporary agricultural worker program.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this position is scheduled to expire or be eliminated from the authorized headcount plan. Nullable for permanent positions; populated for seasonal, temporary, or project-based roles with a defined end date.']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the position under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) indicating whether the role is exempt, non-exempt, or qualifies for the agricultural exemption under FLSA Section 13(b)(12), which affects overtime pay eligibility and minimum wage requirements.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budgeted Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) allocation for this position, expressed as a decimal fraction of a standard full-time schedule (e.g., 1.00 = full-time, 0.50 = half-time). Used for headcount planning, labor cost budgeting, and workforce capacity analysis.']
  h2a_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position qualifies for staffing under the DOL H-2A Temporary Agricultural Worker visa program, which allows agricultural employers to bring foreign nationals to the U.S. to fill temporary or seasonal agricultural jobs. Required for DOL H-2A job order filings.']
  housing_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position includes employer-provided housing as required under DOL H-2A regulations, which mandate that agricultural employers provide free housing to H-2A workers and domestic workers who are not reasonably able to return to their residence within the same day.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the duties, responsibilities, and working conditions for this position, as published in DOL H-2A job orders, internal job postings, and USDA labor compliance documentation.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad functional grouping that classifies the position into an agricultural job family for workforce planning, compensation benchmarking, and labor cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agronomy|livestock|equipment_operations|food_processing|administration|supply_chain|quality_compliance|research_development — promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this position record was most recently updated, supporting change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  min_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for this position, used in job postings, H-2A job order filings, and candidate screening within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting.']
  min_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this position, as specified in job postings and DOL H-2A job order filings.']
  min_wage_tier varchar [note: 'Applicable minimum wage tier for this position, distinguishing between federal minimum wage, state minimum wage, DOL Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) for H-2A positions, piece-rate compensation structures common in agricultural harvesting, or negotiated collective bargaining rates.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation pay grade band assigned to this position, used for salary range management, compensation benchmarking, and labor cost budgeting within the enterprise pay structure.']
  physical_demands_rating varchar [note: 'DOL Dictionary of Occupational Titles (DOT) physical demands rating for this position, classifying the level of physical exertion required. Used for ADA accommodation assessments, OSHA ergonomic risk evaluation, and H-2A job order physical requirements disclosure.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the position within the enterprise, used in DOL H-2A job order filings, USDA labor compliance reporting, and integration with SAP SuccessFactors and payroll systems.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the authorized position indicating whether it is active and available for staffing, frozen due to budget constraints, pending organizational approval, or eliminated from the headcount plan.']
  remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements. Most field operations, livestock handling, and equipment operation positions are not remote-eligible; administrative and analytical roles may qualify.']
  requires_cdl boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position requires a valid Commercial Driver\'s License (CDL) for operating agricultural transport vehicles, grain trucks, or heavy equipment on public roads, as regulated by FMCSA.']
  requires_gap_training boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position requires completion of Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) training as part of USDA GAP/GHP certification requirements and FSMA Produce Safety Rule compliance. Applicable to field workers, supervisors, and food handling personnel.']
  requires_haccp_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position requires HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) food safety certification, applicable to roles in food processing, packing, and quality assurance functions subject to FDA FSMA and USDA FSIS regulations.']
  requires_osha_certification varchar [note: 'Specifies the OSHA safety training certification required for this position: OSHA 10-hour General Industry or Construction, OSHA 30-hour for supervisory roles, OSHA Agriculture standard (29 CFR Part 1928), or none required. Used for workforce compliance tracking and training planning.']
  requires_pesticide_license boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position requires a valid state-issued pesticide applicator license as mandated by the EPA Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA) and state pesticide regulations. Positions such as Pesticide Applicator and Crop Protection Specialist typically require this certification.']
  seasonal_end_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1–12) in which seasonal positions typically conclude staffing, used for annual workforce planning, H-2A visa petition duration, and labor demand forecasting aligned with crop harvest completion and post-harvest handling.']
  seasonal_start_month varchar [note: 'Calendar month (1–12) in which seasonal positions typically begin staffing, used for annual workforce planning, H-2A visa petition timing, and labor demand forecasting aligned with crop planting and harvest cycles.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Department of Labor Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code assigned to this position, required for DOL H-2A job order filings, OSHA recordkeeping, and USDA labor compliance reporting (e.g., 45-2041 for Graders and Sorters, Agricultural Products).']
  title varchar [note: 'Official human-readable job title for the position as it appears in organizational charts, offer letters, and DOL H-2A job order filings (e.g., Field Crew Supervisor, Pesticide Applicator, Irrigation Technician, Livestock Handler, Grain Elevator Operator, Food Safety Auditor).']
  transportation_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position includes employer-provided transportation between housing and the worksite, as required under DOL H-2A regulations for guest worker positions and migrant labor compliance.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining agreement (CBA) applicable to this position, if any. Used for wage rate determination, benefit entitlement, and labor relations compliance tracking.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element code for project-based labor cost allocation, used when the position supports specific capital projects (CAPEX) such as facility construction, irrigation system installation, or precision agriculture technology deployment.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the standard work schedule pattern for this position, distinguishing between standard daytime schedules, rotating shifts for processing facilities, seasonally variable hours common in agricultural operations, on-call arrangements, and piece-rate flexible schedules.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount positions within the agricultural enterprise organizational structure, including job classification taxonomy. Defines each budgeted role — field crew supervisor, pesticide applicator, irrigation technician, livestock handler, grain elevator operator, food safety auditor — including position code, job family (agronomy, livestock, equipment operations, food processing, administration), pay grade band, FTE allocation, FLSA exemption status, DOL occupational classification (SOC code), H-2A eligible flag, minimum wage tier, physical demands rating, required certifications (pesticide applicator license, OSHA 10/30, HACCP), organizational unit assignment, and the farm operation or processing facility to which the position belongs. SSOT for position definition and job classification taxonomy. Supports workforce planning, DOL H-2A job order filings, USDA labor compliance, and labor cost allocation.'
}

Table workforce.payroll {
  payroll_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payroll runs are entity-specific for W-2 filing, state tax remittance, and multi-entity payroll reconciliation. In ag holding structures with multiple farm LLCs, payroll must be attributed to the corr']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which labor costs are allocated, enabling crop enterprise budget tracking and farm operation cost analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record for whom this payroll record is generated. Links to the workforce employee master.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payroll journal entries debit specific GL accounts (wages expense, payroll tax expense, piece-rate labor). SAP HCM/FI integration requires payroll→GL account mapping for labor cost posting, period-clo']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: H2A program requires three-fourths guarantee wage calculations and AEWR compliance reporting by growing season. Linking payroll records to growing_season enables season-level labor cost reporting, DOL']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each payroll run generates a corresponding GL journal entry. Linking payroll records to journal entries enables payroll-to-GL reconciliation, audit trails for SOX compliance, and period-close verifica']
  payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.payment_run. Business justification: Payroll net pay disbursements in agriculture are executed via payment runs (ACH batch, check run). Linking payroll to the payment_run that executed disbursement is required for payment reconciliation,']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Payroll processing for agricultural workers must reference the authorized position to validate pay grade, FLSA exemption status, AEWR compliance, H-2A eligibility, and minimum wage tier. payroll.job_c']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Payroll postings in agriculture carry profit center assignments for crop/livestock enterprise P&L reporting. SAP-style ag payroll requires profit_center_id on payroll records to allocate labor costs t']
  aewr_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the effective hourly wage paid to the employee meets or exceeds the DOL Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) for the applicable state and period. Critical H-2A compliance indicator.']
  aewr_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The DOL-published Adverse Effect Wage Rate applicable to the employee\'s state and job classification for the pay period. Used as the minimum wage floor for H-2A guest workers and compliance benchmarking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail and SOX financial reporting controls.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll record. Typically USD for domestic operations; supports international agricultural operations.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employee\'s engagement type, distinguishing permanent staff, seasonal farm workers, H-2A guest workers, and migrant laborers. Drives compliance rule application and wage rate determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: permanent|seasonal|h2a_guest_worker|migrant|contract|part_time|temporary — promote to reference product]']
  farm_operation_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific farm operation or production unit (e.g., field, orchard, processing facility) where the employee worked. Enables labor cost allocation to crop enterprise budgets in Granular Farm Management.']
  federal_income_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of federal income tax withheld from the employee\'s gross wages for the pay period per IRS withholding tables and the employee\'s W-4 elections.']
  gross_wages decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total pre-deduction earnings for the pay period, including regular pay, overtime premium, piece-rate earnings, and any other compensation components before tax and other deductions.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The base hourly wage rate applied to this payroll record. For H-2A workers, must meet or exceed the Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) published by DOL for the applicable state and crop activity.']
  housing_deduction decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from gross wages for employer-provided housing furnished to H-2A workers or other agricultural employees. Must comply with DOL reasonable cost or fair rental value limits.']
  job_classification_code varchar [note: 'The DOL Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) or internal job code for the employee\'s role during the pay period (e.g., crop worker, equipment operator, agronomist). Drives AEWR rate selection and compliance rule application.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance.']
  medicare_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employee share of Medicare (HI) tax withheld under FICA for the pay period, including the Additional Medicare Tax for high earners where applicable.']
  net_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'The actual take-home amount disbursed to the employee after all tax withholdings and authorized deductions are subtracted from gross wages.']
  overtime_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total overtime premium earnings for the pay period, calculated at 1.5x the regular rate for hours exceeding 40 per week per FLSA requirements.']
  overtime_hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked beyond the FLSA 40-hour weekly threshold during the pay period. Required for FLSA overtime premium pay calculation and DOL wage and hour compliance reporting.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the employee is paid. Determines pay period boundaries and is critical for H-2A three-fourths guarantee calculations and FLSA overtime compliance.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the payroll processing period. Defines the boundary for wage calculations, overtime determination, and H-2A three-fourths guarantee assessment.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the payroll processing period. Used for DOL wage and hour compliance, H-2A three-fourths guarantee tracking, and labor cost allocation.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'The compensation structure applied to this payroll record. Piece-rate is common for harvest operations; hourly applies to FLSA overtime calculations; salary for permanent staff.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date on which wages were disbursed to the employee. Required for DOL wage payment timeliness compliance and state wage payment law adherence.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which net pay is disbursed to the employee. Direct deposit is standard; check and pay card are common for seasonal and H-2A workers without bank accounts.']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the payroll record within the SAP SuccessFactors payroll processing lifecycle, from initial calculation through final payment or reversal.']
  piece_rate_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total earnings derived from piece-rate compensation (harvest pay) for the pay period, calculated as piece_rate_units multiplied by piece_rate_per_unit. Supports harvest labor cost analysis.']
  piece_rate_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'The monetary rate paid per piece-rate unit (e.g., per bin, per pound, per row) for harvest or production work. Confidential compensation data used for piece-rate earnings calculation.']
  piece_rate_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of piece-rate units (e.g., bins picked, rows harvested, pounds collected) completed by the employee during the pay period. Drives piece-rate earnings calculation for harvest pay.']
  regular_hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total non-overtime hours worked by the employee during the pay period. Used for FLSA compliance, H-2A three-fourths guarantee tracking, and labor cost allocation to farm operations.']
  sap_payroll_doc_number varchar [note: 'The payroll document reference number generated by SAP S/4HANA payroll processing, used for reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.']
  social_security_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Employee share of Social Security (OASDI) tax withheld under FICA for the pay period, calculated at the statutory rate on wages up to the annual wage base.']
  state_income_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of state income tax withheld from the employee\'s gross wages for the pay period per applicable state tax authority withholding requirements.']
  three_fourths_guarantee_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The minimum number of hours the employer is obligated to offer the H-2A worker under the three-fourths guarantee provision (75% of contract period workdays). Used for compliance tracking and potential back-pay liability assessment.']
  three_fourths_guarantee_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer has met the H-2A three-fourths guarantee obligation for this pay period. A False value triggers back-pay liability calculation for the shortfall hours.']
  transportation_deduction decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount deducted from gross wages for employer-provided transportation to/from the worksite. Subject to DOL H-2A regulations limiting deductions to actual cost and prohibiting deductions that reduce wages below AEWR.']
  work_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the employee performed work during the pay period. Determines applicable state income tax withholding, state minimum wage, and H-2A AEWR rate.']

  Note: 'Periodic payroll processing records for all agricultural employees. Captures pay period start/end dates, gross wages, piece-rate earnings (harvest pay), overtime hours (FLSA), H-2A adverse effect wage rate (AEWR) compliance flag, deductions (taxes, housing, transportation), net pay, payment method, and SAP payroll document reference. Supports DOL wage and hour compliance, H-2A three-fourths guarantee tracking, and labor cost allocation to crop enterprise budgets.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual time and attendance record in the agricultural workforce management system.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP cost center to which labor hours and costs are allocated. Supports farm operation financial reporting and COGS tracking.']
  crew_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm work crew or labor gang this employee was assigned to during this time entry. Supports crew-level productivity reporting and H-2A group compliance tracking.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Time entries for facility-based agricultural workers (packing house sorters, grain elevator operators) require a facility link for labor cost allocation by facility, FSMA compliance, and H2A three-fou']
  field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agricultural field or land parcel where the labor was performed. Enables labor cost allocation to specific crop operations and fields.']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: Labor cost by season: agricultural payroll and enterprise budgeting require labor hours to be allocated to a growing season. FSMA traceability and crop insurance APH calculations also require season-l']
  herd_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Time entries for livestock care are allocated to specific herds for labor cost-per-head reporting. time_entry has field_id for crop labor but no herd_id for livestock labor. Herd-level labor cost allo']
  payroll_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.payroll. Business justification: Time entries are the source records that feed payroll processing. Once payroll is run, each time entry should reference the payroll record it was included in, enabling audit trails from gross pay back']
  pen_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to livestock.pen_location. Business justification: Pen-level time tracking enables cost-of-gain calculations per pen, a core feedlot profitability metric. Labor hours per pen feed into pen close-out reports used by feedlot managers and lenders. This i']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee whose time is being recorded. Links to the workforce employee master record in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.shift. Business justification: A time_entry records actual hours worked during a specific shift. shift is the master reference table. time_entry.shift_type is a denormalized STRING that duplicates shift.shift_type — once shift_id F']
  work_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.work_assignment. Business justification: Time entries capture actual hours worked against a scheduled work assignment. One work_assignment (shift/task) generates many time_entry records. This FK links actual attendance to the planned assignm']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the farm work order or field operation task this time entry is associated with. Links labor hours to specific crop or livestock operations.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why this time entry was amended. Required when is_amended is True. Supports compliance audit trails and payroll dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missed_punch|task_correction|hours_adjustment|pay_type_change|field_reassignment|supervisor_correction|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the supervisor during the approval or rejection of the time entry. Documents reasons for adjustments, exceptions, or rejections for audit purposes.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supervisor approved or rejected this time entry. Required for FLSA recordkeeping compliance and payroll processing authorization.']
  break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total unpaid break time in minutes deducted from the shift. Includes meal breaks and rest periods as required by OSHA and applicable state labor laws.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the employee clocked in to begin their work shift. Captured from time clock, mobile app, or supervisor entry in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the employee clocked out to end their work shift. Null if the shift is still in progress or if clock-out was not recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was first created in the system. Required for FLSA recordkeeping (3-year retention) and SOX audit trail compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this time entry record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international agricultural operations.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the time entry record, formatted as TE-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with payroll systems and compliance audits.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the time entry record. Drives payroll processing eligibility and supervisor approval routing in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the work location at clock-in, captured via mobile device or GPS-enabled time clock. Supports field-level labor allocation and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the work location at clock-in, captured via mobile device or GPS-enabled time clock. Supports field-level labor allocation and FSMA traceability requirements.']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total gross wages earned for this time entry before deductions. Calculated from regular hours, overtime hours, and/or piece-rate units. Feeds into SAP SuccessFactors payroll processing.']
  h2a_guarantee_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'The guaranteed minimum hours applicable to H-2A workers for this period, representing 75% of the total workdays in the contract period. Used to verify employer compliance with H-2A three-fourths guarantee obligation.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The applicable hourly wage rate for this time entry at the time of work. For H-2A workers, reflects the Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) as published by DOL. Stored for historical payroll audit purposes.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry has been amended or corrected after initial submission. Amended records require supervisor re-approval and are flagged for payroll audit review.']
  is_overtime boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry includes overtime hours subject to premium pay rates. Set to True when overtime_hours is greater than zero.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or identifier of the specific work location within the farm operation (e.g., barn, greenhouse, packing shed, orchard block). Complements the field_id for non-field locations.']
  overtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked beyond the standard FLSA threshold (40 hours/week or applicable state law). Triggers overtime pay rate calculation in payroll.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Method of compensation applicable to this time entry. H-2A adverse wage rate applies to H-2A visa workers per DOL requirements. Drives payroll calculation logic in SAP SuccessFactors.']
  payroll_period varchar [note: 'The payroll period (ISO week format YYYY-Www) to which this time entry belongs. Used for FLSA workweek aggregation, H-2A three-fourths guarantee calculations, and payroll batch processing.']
  piece_rate_per_unit decimal(10,4) [note: 'Dollar amount paid per piece-rate unit completed. Must meet or exceed FLSA minimum wage when divided by total hours worked. Stored for payroll audit and compliance verification.']
  piece_rate_unit_type varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for piece-rate work completed (e.g., bin, row, acre, head of livestock). Required for accurate piece-rate wage calculation and productivity analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bin|row|acre|head|bushel|flat|box|pallet — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  piece_rate_units decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of piece-rate units completed during this time entry (e.g., bins picked, rows planted, animals processed). Used for piece-rate pay calculation and productivity tracking.']
  regular_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total regular (non-overtime) hours worked during this time entry period. Used for standard wage calculation and H-2A three-fourths guarantee tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system from which this time entry was captured. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between SAP SuccessFactors, mobile field apps, and physical time clocks.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agricultural work activity performed during this time entry (e.g., planting, spraying, harvesting, irrigation, livestock feeding, pruning, scouting). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planting|spraying|harvesting|irrigation|livestock_feeding|pruning|scouting|land_preparation|post_harvest_handling|equipment_operation|administrative — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this time entry record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for data lineage, payroll reconciliation, and audit trail purposes.']
  visa_type varchar [note: 'Worker visa or employment classification type for this time entry. H-2A classification triggers adverse effect wage rate (AEWR) enforcement and three-fourths guarantee calculations per DOL regulations.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the agricultural work was performed. Primary date dimension for payroll period calculations and FLSA workweek compliance.']

  Note: 'Individual time and attendance records for agricultural workers capturing daily clock-in/clock-out, field assignment, task type (planting, spraying, harvesting, livestock feeding), hours worked, break time, overtime flag, piece-rate units completed, and supervisor approval status. Integrates with SAP SuccessFactors time management and supports FLSA compliance, H-2A three-fourths guarantee calculations, and labor cost allocation to specific fields and crop operations.'
}

  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the work assignment. \'draft\' = created but not confirmed; \'scheduled\' = confirmed and upcoming; \'active\' = currently in progress; \'completed\' = finished; \'cancelled\' = voided before start; \'suspended\' = paused due to weather, REI hold, or safety issue.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work assignment by the nature of the agricultural task. Determines applicable compliance requirements (e.g., spray_crew triggers REI tracking, pesticide applicator license verification). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field_crew|equipment_operator|spray_crew|harvest_crew|livestock_handler|irrigation_crew|scouting|administrative|maintenance_crew|transport_driver — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this work assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance record-keeping requirements.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Number of workers assigned to this work assignment. Used for labor capacity planning, field productivity analysis, and compliance with minimum crew size requirements for certain operations.']
  days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days on which this assignment recurs within the schedule period (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI\'). Supports recurring shift scheduling and weekly labor planning.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this work assignment expires or is completed. Null for open-ended assignments. Critical for H-2A visa compliance and seasonal labor contract management.']
  gap_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned worker must have completed Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) training before performing this assignment. Supports FSMA Produce Safety Rule and GLOBALG.A.P. certification compliance.']
  haccp_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned worker must have completed Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) training before performing this assignment. Required for processing facility and post-harvest handling assignments.']
  labor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor category for this assignment. Drives compliance requirements: \'h2a_visa\' triggers H-2A program compliance; \'migrant\' triggers MSPA compliance; \'contract\' triggers third-party labor tracking.']
  location_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional location details or directions relevant to the assignment (e.g., \'Enter via north gate, report to row marker 12A\', GPS waypoint reference). Supplements the field_id or facility_id reference.']
  osha_safety_cert_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid OSHA safety certification is required for the worker performing this assignment. Applies to equipment operators, chemical handlers, and confined space entry assignments.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or piece-rate wage applicable to this work assignment. Used for labor cost calculation, OPEX reporting, and H-2A Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) compliance verification.']
  pay_rate_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the pay rate (e.g., \'hourly\' for time-based wages, \'piece_rate\' for production-based pay such as per-bin or per-bushel). Required for accurate payroll calculation.']
  pesticide_applicator_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment requires the assigned worker to hold a valid pesticide applicator license. Enforces EPA and state-level pesticide application compliance for spray crew assignments.']
  ppe_required varchar [note: 'Description of the Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required for this assignment (e.g., \'Chemical-resistant gloves, respirator, coveralls\'). Derived from SDS and pesticide label requirements. Supports OSHA and EPA WPS compliance.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level of this work assignment. \'critical\' = time-sensitive operations (e.g., frost protection, emergency harvest); \'high\' = planned peak operations; \'medium\' = standard scheduled work; \'low\' = non-urgent tasks.']
  rei_expiry_time timestamp [note: 'The date and time after which workers may safely re-enter the field following a pesticide application. Null when rei_restricted is False. Enforces EPA Worker Protection Standard REI compliance in scheduling.']
  rei_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work assignment is subject to a Restricted Entry Interval (REI) restriction due to a recent pesticide application in the assigned field. When True, field re-entry is prohibited until the REI expiry timestamp. Critical for EPA Worker Protection Standard compliance.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the scheduling template or shift schedule this assignment belongs to (e.g., \'Spring Planting Rotation 2024\', \'Harvest Season Day Shift\'). Supports workforce planning and schedule management.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours planned for this work assignment shift or period. Used for labor budgeting, OPEX planning, and comparison against actual hours worked.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work assignment is seasonal in nature (True) or year-round permanent (False). Drives H-2A visa eligibility determination and seasonal labor budget classification.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the assigned shift ends. Used for overtime calculation, labor cost allocation, and compliance with FLSA agricultural hour regulations.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'The shift rotation pattern for this assignment. \'fixed\' = same shift daily; \'rotating\' = alternating shifts; \'split\' = two separate work periods in a day; \'on_call\' = available as needed; \'flex\' = variable hours within a window.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the assigned shift begins. Used for time-and-attendance tracking, REI compliance (field re-entry after pesticide application), and labor cost calculation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this work assignment becomes effective. Used for seasonal labor planning, H-2A visa compliance date tracking, and field operation scheduling.']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific agricultural task or operation to be performed during this assignment (e.g., \'Apply pre-emergent herbicide to corn fields 12A-12D\', \'Hand-harvest strawberries — rows 1-50\'). Supports traceability of who performed which operation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this work assignment record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP SuccessFactors, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Operational assignment and shift scheduling of employees and crews to specific farm tasks, fields, equipment, or livestock operations. Captures assignment type (field crew, equipment operator, spray crew, harvest crew, livestock handler), assigned field or facility, assigned employee or crew, start and end dates, shift start/end times, shift pattern (rotating, fixed, split), days of week, crew size, supervisor, seasonal validity, schedule name, and assignment status. SSOT for all labor scheduling and field assignment data. Supports workforce planning, labor scheduling across planting and harvest seasons, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance by scheduling field re-entry after pesticide applications, and full traceability of who performed which agricultural operation and when.'
}

  asset_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: Operator certifications (CDL, pesticide applicator, equipment operator licenses) are specific to equipment categories in agriculture. This FK enables pre-assignment compliance checks — verifying an em']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center.   employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or agricultural worker who holds this certification or license. Links to the workforce employee master record in SAP SuccessFactors.']

Linking workforce_certification to farm_operation enables opera']

Business justification: Certification costs (pesticide license fees, OSHA card fees, GAP training costs) tracked in workforce_certification.cost_amount must be posted to specific GL expense accounts. Agriculture operations r']
  input_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Pesticide applicator certifications authorize use of specific restricted-use pesticides in the input catalog. Linking certification to input catalog item supports EPA/state regulatory compliance verif']

  quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Linking workforce_certification to the quality_cert']
  cdl_class varchar [note: 'DOT-defined class of Commercial Driver License (CDL): \'A\' (combination vehicles over 26,001 lbs), \'B\' (single vehicles over 26,001 lbs), or \'C\' (vehicles carrying 16+ passengers or hazardous materials). Applicable only when certification_type is CDL. Null for non-CDL certifications.']
  cdl_endorsements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of DOT endorsement codes on the CDL (e.g., \'H\' for hazardous materials, \'T\' for double/triple trailers, \'N\' for tank vehicles, \'X\' for tanker with hazmat). Applicable only when certification_type is CDL.']
