  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification or license. \'active\' indicates valid and in good standing; \'expired\' indicates past expiration date without renewal; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily invalidated by issuing authority; \'revoked\' indicates permanently cancelled; \'pending_renewal\' indicates renewal application submitted but not yet approved.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of professional certification, license, or compliance credential held by the worker. Examples include pesticide applicator license, OSHA 10-hour card, OSHA 30-hour card, HACCP certification, GAP training, forklift operator, CDL, food handler permit, GLOBALG.A.P. worker training, and CEU completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pesticide_applicator|osha_10|osha_30|haccp|gap_training|forklift_operator|cdl|food_handler|globalg.a.p._worker|ceu_completion|first_aid_cpr|h2a_orientation|ppe_training|other — promote to reference product]']
  ceu_hours_completed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) hours the worker has completed toward the current renewal requirement. Compared against ceu_hours_required to determine renewal eligibility and compliance standing.']
  ceu_hours_required decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) hours required for renewal of this certification within the current certification period. Particularly relevant for pesticide applicator license renewals mandated by state departments of agriculture.']
  cost_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred by the enterprise for obtaining or renewing this certification, including examination fees, training course fees, and license application fees. Used for workforce training budget tracking and labor cost allocation in SAP SuccessFactors and SAP CO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., and SOX compliance.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the scanned copy or digital image of the certification document, license card, or training completion certificate stored in the enterprise document repository.']
  exam_pass_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to pass the certification examination as defined by the issuing authority. Used to validate exam_score against the passing threshold and confirm certification eligibility.']
  exam_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the worker on the certification examination, expressed as a percentage or raw score per the issuing authority\'s scoring system. Used for training effectiveness analysis and compliance documentation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or license expires and is no longer valid. Null for certifications with no expiration (e.g., some OSHA cards). Critical for compliance monitoring, renewal alerts, and audit readiness.']
  is_renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requires periodic renewal to remain valid. True = renewal required (e.g., pesticide applicator licenses, CDL, food handler permits); False = lifetime credential with no renewal requirement (e.g., some OSHA completion cards).']
  is_restricted_use_pesticide_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pesticide applicator license authorizes the worker to purchase and apply Restricted Use Pesticides (RUP) as defined by EPA under FIFRA. True = authorized for RUP; False = limited to general use pesticides only. Critical for EPA compliance and pesticide procurement controls.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification or license has been independently verified against the issuing authority\'s records (e.g., state pesticide applicator license lookup, DOT FMCSA CDL verification, OSHA card validation). True = verified; False = self-reported or pending verification.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or license was officially issued or awarded by the issuing authority. Used to calculate credential age and determine renewal eligibility windows.']
  job_role_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the worker\'s job role or position for which this certification is required or relevant (e.g., \'PEST_APPLICATOR\', \'FORKLIFT_OPR\', \'CDL_DRIVER\', \'FOOD_HANDLER\', \'AGRONOMIST\'). Used for workforce compliance gap analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional remarks, special conditions, restrictions, or compliance notes associated with this certification record (e.g., \'License restricted to aerial application only\', \'Renewal pending state board review\', \'CEU credits from approved provider only\').']
  osha_card_type varchar [note: 'Specific OSHA safety training card type: \'10_hour_general\' (OSHA 10-hour General Industry), \'30_hour_general\' (OSHA 30-hour General Industry), \'10_hour_construction\' (OSHA 10-hour Construction), or \'30_hour_construction\' (OSHA 30-hour Construction). Applicable only when certification_type is osha_10 or osha_30.']
  pesticide_applicator_category varchar [note: 'EPA-defined certification category for pesticide applicator licenses, specifying the type of pesticide use authorized (e.g., \'field crops\', \'ornamental and turf\', \'right-of-way\', \'aquatic\', \'seed treatment\', \'soil fumigation\'). Applicable only when certification_type is pesticide_applicator. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field_crops|ornamental_turf|right_of_way|aquatic|seed_treatment|soil_fumigation|forest|public_health|regulatory_pest_control|demonstration_research — promote to reference product]']
  pesticide_applicator_class varchar [note: 'EPA classification of the pesticide applicator license: \'private\' (applies restricted-use pesticides on own or employer\'s agricultural land), \'commercial\' (applies pesticides for hire or on others\' property), or \'public\' (applies pesticides as a government employee). Null for non-pesticide certifications.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or license was most recently renewed. Tracks the renewal history and confirms the current validity period was established through a formal renewal process.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the renewal application must be submitted to avoid lapse in certification status. May differ from expiration_date if the issuing authority requires advance submission (e.g., 90 days prior to expiration for CDL renewals).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this certification record was ingested or originated (e.g., \'sap_successfactors\' for HR-managed credentials, \'granular\' for farm management system records, \'manual_entry\' for paper-based credentials entered by HR staff, \'external_registry\' for records pulled from state licensing databases).']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the worker completed the required training course or examination that resulted in this certification. May differ from issue_date if there is a processing delay between training completion and official credential issuance.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, institution, or accredited body that delivered the training or examination leading to this certification (e.g., \'Purdue Extension\', \'National Pesticide Safety Education Center\', \'OSHA Training Institute Education Center\', \'NSF International\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was last modified. Tracks changes to certification status, renewal dates, or verification information for audit and data lineage purposes.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification or license was last verified against the issuing authority\'s official records. Used to track verification currency and schedule re-verification for audit readiness.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the HR staff member, compliance officer, or supervisor who performed the credential verification. Supports audit trail requirements for GLOBALG.A.P., FSMA, and state department of agriculture audits.']

  Note: 'Master registry of all professional certifications, licenses, and compliance credentials held by agricultural workers. Covers pesticide applicator licenses (EPA private/commercial/public categories, state license number, issuing state agency, certification categories for field crops, ornamental, right-of-way), OSHA 10/30-hour safety cards, HACCP certification, GAP training completion, forklift operator certification, CDL (commercial driver license), food handler permits, GLOBALG.A.P. worker training certificates, and continuing education units (CEU). Tracks certification type, issuing authority, issue date, expiration date, renewal status, license number, CEU hours completed, state agency reference, and license category. SSOT for ALL worker credential and license data — including pesticide applicator licenses. Supports EPA pesticide applicator compliance, state department of agriculture audits, OSHA verification, GLOBALG.A.P. certification readiness, and CDL/DOT compliance.'
}

Business justification: Pesticide applicator, GAP, HACCP, and WPS training providers in ag are managed as procurement vendors with contracts and invoices.   workforce_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.workforce_certification. Business justification: Training events that result in certifications (pesticide applicator license, OSHA card, GAP/HACCP certificate) should reference the master certification record they fulfill or renew. Adding workforce_']
  actual_attendee_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of employees who attended this training event session. Used for compliance reporting, cost allocation, and training effectiveness analysis.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual duration of the training session in minutes as delivered. Used for CEU (Continuing Education Unit) credit calculation, compliance reporting, and pesticide applicator license renewal tracking.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the training session actually concluded. Used with actual_start_timestamp to compute actual duration for compliance and audit purposes.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the training session actually commenced. Distinct from scheduled date; used for compliance evidence and duration calculation.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the employee on the post-training assessment or exam, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to determine pass/fail status and competency level.']
  certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a training completion certificate was issued to the employee upon successful completion of this training event. True = certificate issued; False = not issued.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier printed on the training completion certificate issued to the employee. Used for audit verification, regulatory inspections, and pesticide applicator license renewal documentation.']
  ceu_credits decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) credits awarded upon successful completion of this training event. Applicable for pesticide applicator license renewal and professional development tracking.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date the employee successfully completed the training event. The SSOT completion date for compliance evidence, certificate issuance, and qualification expiration calculation.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Per-attendee completion status for this training event. Enrolled = registered not yet attended; In_Progress = partially completed; Completed = successfully finished; Failed = did not meet pass threshold; Withdrawn = voluntarily withdrew; No_Show = did not attend without withdrawal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance record integrity.']
  curriculum_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the training curriculum or course materials used for this event (e.g., \'v2.3\', \'2024-Q1\'). Ensures audit trail integrity when regulatory requirements change and curriculum is updated.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training was delivered to attendees. Values: classroom (instructor-led in a room), field (on-site farm/field instruction), online (e-learning/LMS), blended (combination), video (recorded/self-paced video), on_the_job (supervised practical training).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee was enrolled or registered for this training event. Used to track lead time between enrollment and completion, and for compliance deadline management.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the training event or course offering (e.g., TRN-GAP001, TRN-HACCP02). Used for scheduling, audit documentation, and regulatory reporting.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training event session. Scheduled = planned but not yet started; In_Progress = currently underway; Completed = session finished; Cancelled = session cancelled; Postponed = rescheduled.']
  event_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the training session (e.g., \'GAP Awareness Training\', \'HACCP Food Safety Fundamentals\', \'WPS Pesticide Safety\', \'H-2A Worker Rights Orientation\'). Used in reports, certificates, and audit documentation.']
  h2a_orientation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training event fulfills the H-2A visa program worker rights orientation requirement under DOL regulations. True = qualifies as H-2A orientation; False = does not fulfill H-2A orientation requirement.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code in which the training was delivered (e.g., \'en\', \'es\', \'es-MX\'). Critical for H-2A and migrant worker compliance — FSMA and EPA WPS require training in a language workers understand.']
  max_attendees varchar [note: 'Maximum number of attendees permitted for this training event session. Used for scheduling, capacity planning, and ensuring adequate trainer-to-trainee ratios for hands-on safety training.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum assessment score percentage required to pass this training event and receive credit or certification. Varies by training type and regulatory requirement.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Originally planned duration of the training session in minutes. Used for scheduling, workforce planning, and comparison against actual duration.']
  qualification_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the qualification, certification, or compliance credit earned from this training event expires and retraining is required. Critical for pesticide applicator CEU renewal, OSHA certifications, and GLOBALG.A.P. annual training requirements.']
  retraining_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the employee must complete retraining for this training type. Populated when retraining_required is True. Used for compliance deadline tracking and workforce scheduling.']
  retraining_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is required to retake this training (e.g., due to failed assessment, regulatory change, incident, or qualification expiration). True = retraining required; False = no retraining needed.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the training event was originally scheduled to occur. Used for workforce planning, compliance scheduling, and audit trail.']
  trainer_certification_number varchar [note: 'License or certification number of the trainer, particularly for pesticide safety and applicator training where a certified trainer is required by EPA and state regulations.']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the trainer or instructor who delivered the training. Captured for audit documentation when the trainer may be external or when trainer_id is not available. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and EPA WPS compliance records.']
  training_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to deliver this training event, including trainer fees, materials, venue, and external provider charges. Used for labor cost allocation (OPEX) across farm operations and processing facilities in SAP CO module.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training by regulatory or operational category. Examples include GAP, HACCP, WPS_PESTICIDE_SAFETY, OSHA_HAZCOM, H2A_WORKER_RIGHTS, SEXUAL_HARASSMENT_PREVENTION, EQUIPMENT_OPERATION, EMERGENCY_RESPONSE, FOOD_SAFETY, PESTICIDE_APPLICATOR_CEU. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GAP|HACCP|WPS_PESTICIDE_SAFETY|OSHA_HAZCOM|H2A_WORKER_RIGHTS|SEXUAL_HARASSMENT_PREVENTION|EQUIPMENT_OPERATION|EMERGENCY_RESPONSE|FOOD_SAFETY|PESTICIDE_APPLICATOR_CEU — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this training event record was most recently modified. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  wps_handler_type varchar [note: 'EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) classification of the employee for pesticide safety training purposes. Agricultural Worker = works in treated areas; Pesticide Handler = mixes/loads/applies pesticides; Early Entry Worker = enters treated areas during REI; Not Applicable = WPS training not required for this role.']

  Note: 'Training sessions and per-employee attendance/completion records for the agricultural workforce. Covers GAP awareness, HACCP food safety, pesticide safety (WPS/REI/PHI compliance), OSHA hazard communication, H-2A worker rights orientation, sexual harassment prevention, equipment operation, and emergency response training. Captures training type, delivery method (classroom, field, online), trainer, location, date, duration, and per-attendee enrollment date, completion status, assessment score, certificate issued flag, and resulting qualification expiration date. SSOT for all training delivery, enrollment, and completion evidence. Supports FSMA worker training requirements, GLOBALG.A.P. audit documentation, EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) compliance, SQF/BRC certification, and pesticide applicator license CEU renewal tracking.'
}

Business justification: Safety events (pesticide exposure, WPS violations) in agriculture are often discovered during or triggered by a quality inspection. Linking safety_event to work_assignment enables analysis of which tasks, fields, and shift patterns generate the most incidents — critical for ']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'The body part(s) injured or affected by the safety incident (e.g., \'right hand\', \'lower back\', \'eyes\', \'respiratory system\'). Required for OSHA 300 log and workers compensation claim documentation.']

  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the workers compensation claim through its lifecycle from filing through resolution. Drives return-to-work program management and insurance reserve tracking.']

  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety event by the nature of the hazard or mechanism of injury. Drives regulatory reporting routing and corrective action protocols. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pesticide_exposure|equipment_injury|heat_illness|fall|animal_handling_injury|near_miss|chemical_exposure|grain_bin_entrapment|electrical|struck_by|caught_in|overexertion|vehicle_collision|fire_explosion — promote to reference product]']
  injury_illness_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the nature of the injury or illness sustained (e.g., \'laceration to right index finger requiring 3 stitches\', \'organophosphate pesticide exposure causing nausea and dizziness\'). PHI — restricted access. Required for OSHA 300 log.']
  insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the workers compensation insurance carrier handling the claim. Used for insurance premium optimization, carrier performance tracking, and agricultural insurance management.']
  job_title_at_incident varchar [note: 'The employee\'s job title at the time of the incident. Captured as a snapshot because the employee\'s current title may change after the incident. Used for occupational injury pattern analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the safety event record. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and data lineage requirements.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific location where the incident occurred (e.g., \'North grain bin #3, loading platform\', \'Field 12-B near irrigation pivot\'). Supplements location_type with precise operational context.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the physical location where the incident occurred within the agricultural operation. Used for hazard hotspot analysis and BMP safety program targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: field|grain_bin|processing_facility|livestock_barn|greenhouse|equipment_yard|office|storage_warehouse|irrigation_site|loading_dock|cold_storage — promote to reference product]']
  medical_treatment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the highest level of medical treatment received for the injury or illness. Determines OSHA recordability threshold and workers compensation claim type. First aid only incidents are generally not OSHA recordable.']
  osha_300_log_entry_number varchar [note: 'The sequential case number assigned to this incident on the OSHA 300 Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses. Required for OSHA recordable incidents. Used for annual OSHA 300A summary and regulatory audit.']
  osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA criteria for entry on the OSHA 300 Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses. Drives annual OSHA 300A summary reporting and EMR calculation.']
  pesticide_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pesticide, herbicide, fungicide, or other agricultural chemical was involved in the incident. Triggers EPA and state pesticide regulatory reporting requirements and PHI/REI compliance review.']
  ppe_description varchar [note: 'Description of the PPE that was in use or should have been in use at the time of the incident (e.g., \'chemical-resistant gloves, face shield, Tyvek suit\'). Relevant for pesticide exposure and chemical handling incidents.']
  ppe_in_use boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee was wearing or using required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at the time of the incident. Critical for root cause analysis and PPE compliance program evaluation.']
  reported_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time the safety incident was formally reported to the supervisor or safety officer. OSHA requires incidents to be reported within 24 hours (fatality/hospitalization within 8 hours). Used to measure reporting lag compliance.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'The date the employee returned to full or modified duty following the injury or illness. Used for return-to-work program management, lost workday calculation, and workers compensation claim closure.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary root cause category identified through incident investigation. Drives corrective action prioritization and BMP safety program improvements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unsafe_act|unsafe_condition|equipment_failure|inadequate_training|ppe_failure|environmental|procedural_violation|management_system_failure|human_error — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative from the root cause analysis (RCA) investigation describing the underlying factors that contributed to the incident. Supports corrective action development and safety program improvement.']
  severity_classification varchar [note: 'OSHA-defined severity tier for the incident. Determines OSHA 300 log recordability, workers compensation claim eligibility, and EMR impact. Near-miss events are tracked for proactive safety management but are not OSHA recordable.']
  subrogation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers compensation claim has been flagged for subrogation — recovery of claim costs from a liable third party (e.g., equipment manufacturer, chemical supplier). Supports insurance cost recovery efforts.']
  task_being_performed varchar [note: 'Description of the specific agricultural task or activity the employee was performing when the incident occurred (e.g., \'pesticide application\', \'grain bin entry\', \'livestock loading\'). Critical for root cause analysis and SOP review.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Names of witnesses present at the time of the incident. Stored as a delimited string for investigation documentation. Confidential — used for internal investigation purposes only.']
  workers_comp_claim_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a workers compensation claim has been filed in connection with this safety incident. Triggers the workers compensation claim tracking workflow.']
  workers_comp_claim_number varchar [note: 'The claim number assigned by the insurance carrier for the workers compensation claim associated with this incident. Used for claim tracking, insurance premium management, and EMR calculation.']

  Note: 'OSHA-reportable incidents, near-miss events, safety observations, and associated workers compensation claims for agricultural operations. Captures incident date/time, location (field, grain bin, processing facility, livestock barn), event type (pesticide exposure, equipment injury, heat illness, fall, animal handling injury, near-miss), affected employee(s), severity classification (first aid, OSHA recordable, lost-time, fatality), root cause analysis, corrective actions, OSHA 300 log entry reference, and — when a claim is filed — workers comp claim number, insurance carrier, claim amount, medical treatment type, lost workdays, subrogation flag, return-to-work date, and claim status. SSOT for the full safety lifecycle from incident through workers compensation resolution. Supports OSHA 300/300A annual reporting, experience modification rate (EMR) management, BMP safety programs, agricultural insurance premium optimization, and return-to-work program management.'
}

  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record in the workforce.employee product for the H-2A or migrant worker covered by this compliance record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: H2A labor compliance requires tracking which facility workers are housed at or assigned to, for DOL audit reporting and OSHA housing inspections. housing_facility_code is a denormalized plain text col']
  farm_operation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_operation. Business justification: H-2A and MSPA labor compliance records are audited at the farm operation level by DOL. Linking labor_compliance directly to farm_operation enables operation-level DOL audit trails, housing compliance ']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: H2A labor compliance records track inbound/outbound transport costs and housing costs that must be posted to GL accounts for DOL-required financial reporting and tax purposes. Agriculture H2A program ']
  growing_season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to crop.growing_season. Business justification: H2A compliance season scoping: DOL audit reporting, AEWR compliance verification, housing inspection records, and three-fourths guarantee calculations are all season-scoped. Labor compliance records m']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: H-2A labor compliance requires documented housing and field sanitation inspections (EPA, OSHA, DOL). Linking labor_compliance to the quality inspection record that covers housing/sanitation replaces t']
  payroll_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.payroll. Business justification: Labor compliance records (H-2A AEWR compliance, three-fourths guarantee) must reference the payroll record they validate. labor_compliance.actual_hourly_wage and aewr_compliance_status are compliance ']
  work_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.work_assignment. Business justification: H-2A and MSPA labor compliance records must be traceable to the specific work assignment they cover, enabling task-level compliance verification (REI restrictions, pesticide applicator requirements, P']
  actual_hourly_wage decimal(8,2) [note: 'The actual hourly wage rate paid to the worker during the contract period. Must meet or exceed the AEWR hourly rate and applicable state minimum wage for DOL compliance.']
  actual_hours_offered decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours of work actually offered to the H-2A worker during the contract period. Compared against three-fourths guarantee hours to determine if the employer met the wage guarantee obligation.']
  aewr_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the employer\'s actual wage payments meet the DOL-mandated Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) for this worker. Non-compliant status triggers remediation workflow and DOL reporting obligations.']
  aewr_hourly_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'The Adverse Effect Wage Rate (AEWR) applicable to this worker\'s job classification and state, as published annually by DOL. The minimum hourly wage the employer must pay H-2A workers to avoid adverse effect on domestic workers.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Date the H-2A or migrant worker arrived at the work site or employer-provided housing. Used to calculate three-fourths guarantee period start and AEWR wage compliance windows.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for documenting compliance exceptions, corrective actions, audit findings, or other relevant notes related to this worker\'s H-2A or MSPA compliance record. Used by compliance officers and HR staff.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The official end date of the H-2A job order contract period as approved by DOL. Defines the end of the three-fourths guarantee calculation period.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The official start date of the H-2A job order contract period as approved by DOL. Defines the beginning of the three-fourths guarantee calculation period.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the worker\'s country of origin or citizenship. Required for H-2A visa processing, USCIS petition, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor compliance record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and DOL record retention compliance.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Date the H-2A or migrant worker departed the work site or employer-provided housing. Used to close the compliance period and finalize three-fourths guarantee hour calculations.']
  dol_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance record has been flagged for or is currently under a DOL Wage and Hour Division audit or investigation. Triggers enhanced documentation retention and legal review workflow.']
  dol_job_order_number varchar [note: 'The official job order number assigned by the U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) for the H-2A temporary agricultural worker petition. Required for all H-2A program filings and DOL audit readiness.']
  epa_housing_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer-provided housing facility meets EPA environmental and pesticide exposure standards applicable to agricultural worker housing. Required for DOL H-2A housing certification.']
  housing_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of workers authorized to occupy the assigned housing unit per OSHA and DOL housing standards. Used for occupancy compliance and state inspection reporting.']
  housing_occupancy_end_date date [note: 'Date the worker vacated the employer-provided housing unit. Used to close the housing assignment record and finalize rent deduction calculations.']
  housing_occupancy_start_date date [note: 'Date the worker began occupying the employer-provided housing unit. Used to track housing provision obligations and calculate any permissible rent deduction periods.']
  housing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employer-provided housing structure assigned to the worker. Used for OSHA and EPA housing standard compliance categorization and state migrant labor housing inspection reporting.']
  housing_unit_number varchar [note: 'Specific unit, room, or bunk number within the employer-provided housing facility assigned to this worker. Used for occupancy tracking and housing inspection compliance.']
  inbound_transport_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred by the employer for inbound transportation of the H-2A worker from the country of origin or place of recruitment to the worksite. Must be reimbursed or paid directly per DOL requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor compliance record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and DOL audit readiness.']
  mspa_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the MSPA written disclosure document was provided to the worker. Required for DOL audit trail and MSPA compliance verification.']
  mspa_disclosure_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required MSPA written disclosure of working conditions, wages, housing, and transportation was provided to the migrant or seasonal agricultural worker prior to recruitment or at time of recruitment.']
  mspa_registration_number varchar [note: 'DOL-issued MSPA farm labor contractor (FLC) registration number, if a third-party contractor is involved in recruiting or employing the migrant worker. Required for MSPA compliance and contractor accountability.']
  osha_housing_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer-provided housing facility meets OSHA temporary labor camp standards (29 CFR 1910.142) as of the last inspection. Required for DOL H-2A housing certification.']
  outbound_transport_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred by the employer for return transportation of the H-2A worker from the worksite to the country of origin upon contract completion or early termination. Required by DOL H-2A regulations.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor compliance program governing this worker record: H-2A (temporary agricultural visa), MSPA (Migrant and Seasonal Agricultural Worker Protection Act), or both where applicable.']
  rent_deduction_amount decimal(8,2) [note: 'Dollar amount deducted from the worker\'s wages for employer-provided housing, if applicable. Must comply with DOL permissible deduction limits and must not reduce wages below AEWR. Reported on worker pay stubs.']
  surety_bond_number varchar [note: 'Surety bond number held by the farm labor contractor (FLC) as required under MSPA to ensure payment of wages and compliance with worker protection obligations. Applicable when a third-party FLC is involved.']
  three_fourths_guarantee_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum number of hours the employer is obligated to offer the H-2A worker under the three-fourths guarantee rule — equal to 75% of the total workdays in the contract period multiplied by the daily hours in the job order. Critical for DOL wage compliance.']
  transportation_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer provided inbound and/or outbound transportation for the H-2A worker as required by DOL regulations. Employer must provide or pay for transportation from the worker\'s home country to the worksite and return.']
  visa_petition_number varchar [note: 'USCIS-assigned petition number (Form I-129) for the H-2A temporary agricultural worker visa. Used for USCIS and APHIS compliance tracking and audit.']
  visa_petition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the H-2A visa petition filed with USCIS. Drives compliance workflow and worker eligibility for employment.']
  workers_comp_coverage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the H-2A or migrant worker is covered by the employer\'s workers\' compensation insurance policy as required by DOL H-2A regulations and applicable state law.']
  workers_comp_policy_number varchar [note: 'The insurance policy number for the workers\' compensation coverage applicable to this worker. Required for DOL audit documentation and state compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'H-2A temporary agricultural worker visa compliance, MSPA migrant labor protection, and employer-provided housing management records. Tracks DOL job order number, visa petition status, worker country of origin, arrival/departure dates, three-fourths guarantee hours tracking, AEWR wage compliance verification, MSPA disclosure records, housing facility assignments (facility, unit, assigned workers, occupancy dates, housing type, capacity, rent deductions, inspection dates, OSHA/EPA housing standard compliance status). SSOT for all DOL-regulated worker welfare obligations including housing provision and assignment, transportation, wage guarantees, and migrant worker protections. Critical for DOL audit readiness, APHIS/USCIS compliance, state migrant labor housing inspections, and seasonal agricultural labor program management.'
}

Table workforce.crew {
  crew_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the agricultural work crew record. Primary key for the crew master entity. MASTER_RESOURCE role — serves as the SSOT for crew identity across field labor scheduling, piece-rate payroll, H-2A housing assignment, and DOL MSPA disclosure.']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Harvest and production crews are organized around specific commodities (strawberry picking crew, grain harvest crew). Piece-rate calculation, PPE requirements, and GAP training requirements are commod']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Crew labor costs are tracked by cost center for field operations accounting. crew has denormalized cost_center_code; proper FK enables crew-level cost center reporting, piece-rate labor cost allocatio']
  farm_operation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the home farm operation or facility to which this crew is primarily assigned. Used for labor cost allocation, H-2A housing assignment, and field scheduling within the farm\'s operational boundary.']
  farm_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: Crews are permanently associated with a farm unit for H-2A housing assignment, MSPA compliance, and operational management. Farm-unit-level crew reporting drives labor cost allocation and regulatory h']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Crews working in HACCP-controlled processes (packing lines, cold storage, processing) operate under a specific HACCP plan. Linking crew to haccp_plan enables verification that all crew members have be']
  home_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary facility (packing house, processing plant, or farm office) where this crew is administratively based. Used for payroll jurisdiction, workers\' compensation coverage assignment, and facility-level labor reporting.']
  leader_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Every agricultural work crew has a designated crew leader who is an employee. crew.leader_mspa_registered (BOOLEAN) confirms the crew leader concept exists but the actual employee reference is missing']
  organic_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: NOP organic certification requires that crews working certified organic operations are trained in organic handling requirements and prohibited substances. Certifying agents audit crew assignments agai']
  parent_crew_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on crew (parent_crew_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Harvest and field crews in agriculture are assigned to specific crop or livestock enterprise profit centers for crew-level labor cost P&L reporting. Agriculture enterprise accounting requires crew-to-']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this crew master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance recordkeeping requirements.']
  crew_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the crew, used in field operations, payroll systems, and DOL job order references. Unique across the enterprise. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors or Granular Farm Management FMIS.']
  crew_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the agricultural work crew (e.g., \'Harvest Crew Alpha\', \'Spray Team 3\', \'Packing Line B\'). Used in scheduling boards, field assignment reports, and safety briefing documentation.']
  crew_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the crew record. \'active\' indicates the crew is currently deployed or available for assignment. \'seasonal_standby\' indicates the crew exists but is outside its operational season window. \'disbanded\' indicates the crew has been permanently dissolved.']
  crew_type varchar [note: 'Operational classification of the crew based on its primary agricultural function. Drives labor scheduling rules, PPE requirements, piece-rate pay structures, and field assignment eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planting|harvest|spray|irrigation|livestock_handling|packing_line|equipment_maintenance — promote to reference product]']
  dol_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the MSPA written disclosure of working conditions was provided to crew members. Required for DOL audit documentation and MSPA compliance recordkeeping.']
  dol_disclosure_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required DOL MSPA written disclosure of working conditions, wages, and housing has been provided to all crew members at the time of recruitment. Mandatory for migrant and seasonal agricultural worker crews under MSPA.']
  gap_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all members of this crew are required to complete GAP training before deployment. Enforced for crews working in fresh produce fields subject to FSMA Produce Safety Rule and GLOBALG.A.P. certification audits.']
  h2a_crew_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew is composed primarily of H-2A temporary agricultural workers under a DOL-certified job order. Triggers H-2A-specific compliance workflows including housing inspection, inbound/outbound transportation reimbursement, and three-fourths guarantee hour tracking.']
  h2a_job_order_number varchar [note: 'DOL-issued job order number associated with this crew\'s H-2A petition. Required for MSPA crew disclosure, housing assignment, and three-fourths guarantee hour compliance reporting. Null for domestic-only crews.']
  haccp_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HACCP food safety training is required for all members of this crew. Mandatory for packing line crews, post-harvest handling teams, and any crew working in USDA/FDA-regulated processing facilities.']
  housing_facility_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employer-provided housing facility assigned to this crew for H-2A or migrant worker housing compliance. Links to housing inspection records, OSHA housing standards compliance, and EPA housing requirements under WPS.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this crew master record. Supports change data capture (CDC) for Databricks Silver Layer incremental processing and audit trail compliance.']
  leader_mspa_registered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned crew supervisor/leader holds a valid MSPA farm labor contractor or crew leader registration with the DOL. Required when the crew leader recruits, solicits, or hires migrant or seasonal agricultural workers.']
  mspa_registration_number varchar [note: 'MSPA registration number of the farm labor contractor (FLC) associated with this crew, if applicable. Required for DOL compliance when a third-party FLC recruits or manages the crew. Null for directly-employed crews.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this crew not captured in structured fields (e.g., specific field access restrictions, equipment preferences, or seasonal rotation notes).']
  osha_safety_briefing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew requires a documented OSHA safety briefing prior to each field deployment. Mandatory for crews operating heavy equipment, working at heights, or handling hazardous materials. Supports OSHA 300 log compliance.']
  pesticide_applicator_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether at least one crew member must hold a valid state-issued pesticide applicator license for this crew\'s assigned tasks. Enforced for spray crews and IPM scouting teams applying restricted-use pesticides.']
  piece_rate_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew is eligible for piece-rate compensation in addition to or in lieu of hourly wages. Drives payroll calculation logic in SAP SuccessFactors and ensures AEWR floor compliance for H-2A workers on piece-rate pay.']
  piece_rate_unit_type varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used for piece-rate pay calculation for this crew (e.g., \'bin\' for tree fruit harvest, \'row\' for transplanting, \'acre\' for mechanical harvest). Drives piece-rate earnings calculation in payroll and productivity reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bin|bucket|flat|row|acre|box|bag|head — promote to reference product]']
  ppe_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the standard PPE required for this crew type (e.g., \'chemical-resistant gloves, respirator, eye protection\' for spray crews; \'cut-resistant gloves, steel-toe boots\' for harvest crews). Supports safety briefing documentation and OSHA compliance.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code representing the primary language spoken by the majority of crew members (e.g., \'es\' for Spanish, \'en\' for English, \'ht\' for Haitian Creole). Drives language selection for safety briefings, WPS handler training, GAP training materials, and DOL MSPA disclosure documents.']
  rei_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the current REI restriction for this crew expires, after which field re-entry is permitted. Populated when rei_restricted is True. Supports automated field assignment eligibility checks.']
  rei_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this crew is currently restricted from entering treated fields due to an active pesticide Restricted Entry Interval (REI). Prevents unsafe field entry and supports EPA WPS compliance enforcement.']
  safety_briefing_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent documented safety briefing conducted for this crew. Used to enforce safety briefing frequency requirements, support OSHA audit readiness, and trigger re-briefing alerts before field deployment.']
  season_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this crew\'s operational season concludes. Triggers seasonal standby status transition, H-2A departure processing, and final piece-rate pay reconciliation.']
  season_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this crew becomes operationally active for the current agricultural season. Used to validate work assignments, H-2A contract period alignment, and seasonal labor planning windows.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Default shift schedule pattern assigned to this crew for labor scheduling and time entry generation. Drives shift start/end time defaults in work assignment records and payroll period calculations.']
  target_crew_size varchar [note: 'Planned or budgeted number of workers for this crew at full operational capacity. Used in workforce planning, H-2A petition headcount calculations, housing unit allocation, and transportation logistics.']
  transportation_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer provides transportation for this crew between housing and the worksite. Required disclosure under H-2A program and MSPA. Drives transportation cost allocation and DOL audit compliance.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining agreement (CBA) applicable to this crew, if unionized. Drives CBA-specific wage rates, grievance procedures, and overtime rules in payroll processing.']
  wps_handler_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether crew members require EPA Worker Protection Standard pesticide handler training. Mandatory for spray crews and any crew working in fields during or after pesticide application within the REI window.']

  Note: 'Master record for agricultural work crews and gangs — the fundamental operational unit for field labor deployment in row crop, specialty crop, and livestock operations. Captures crew code, crew name, crew type (planting crew, harvest crew, spray crew, irrigation team, livestock handling team, packing line crew, equipment maintenance team), assigned supervisor/crew leader (employee reference), home farm operation or facility, default shift pattern, target crew size, current active member count, active status, seasonal validity start/end dates, and primary language spoken. Crews are the primary unit of labor scheduling, field assignment, productivity tracking, and piece-rate pay calculation in agricultural operations. SSOT for crew identity and composition. Supports H-2A crew-based housing and transportation management, piece-rate pay calculations by crew, DOL crew disclosure requirements under MSPA, and crew-level safety briefing documentation. Crew membership is tracked via employee-to-crew assignment with effective dates to support seasonal rotation and mid-season crew reassignment.'
}

Table workforce.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift']
  commodity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Harvest and packing shifts in agriculture are commodity-specific — strawberry harvest shifts differ from grain harvest shifts in duration, crew size, and seasonal windows. Labor planning and shift sch']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Shifts in agriculture are assigned to cost centers for labor cost allocation and payroll default coding. shift already has a denormalized cost_center_code plain column; replacing with a proper FK enab']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, farm, or processing plant where this shift operates. Used for location-based workforce planning.']
  parent_shift_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shift (parent_shift_id)']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the role or position responsible for supervising this shift. Used for accountability and escalation.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total allocated break time within the shift in minutes. Includes meal breaks and rest periods as required by labor regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  differential_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional pay rate multiplier or percentage applied to this shift (e.g., 1.15 for 15% night differential). Used for payroll calculation.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the shift in hours, including any built-in break periods. Used for labor cost calculation and compliance tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this shift definition is retired or replaced. Null for currently active shifts. Supports historical tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this shift definition becomes active and available for scheduling. Supports shift configuration versioning.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Standard end time for the shift in HH:mm format (24-hour clock). Represents the scheduled conclusion of the work period.']
  h2a_visa_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift is eligible for H-2A temporary agricultural workers under U.S. Department of Labor regulations. Critical for migrant labor compliance.']
  hazard_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification for this shift based on exposure to hazards (machinery, chemicals, weather, livestock). Used for safety planning and insurance.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of labor type for cost accounting. Direct labor is production-related, indirect supports operations, administrative is overhead, supervisory is management.']
  maximum_crew_size varchar [note: 'Maximum number of workers that can be accommodated during this shift based on facility capacity, equipment availability, or operational constraints.']
  minimum_crew_size varchar [note: 'Minimum number of workers required to operate this shift safely and effectively. Used for workforce planning and scheduling.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this shift record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization with SAP SuccessFactors.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special instructions, or scheduling considerations for this shift. Free-text field for planners and supervisors.']
  overtime_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hours worked during this shift qualify for overtime compensation under labor regulations.']
  paid_break_minutes varchar [note: 'Portion of break time that is compensated. Used for payroll calculation and labor cost allocation.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required certifications or licenses for workers assigned to this shift (e.g., Pesticide Applicator License, Forklift Certification, HACCP, GAP). Used for compliance verification.']
  safety_training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether workers must complete specific safety training before being assigned to this shift. Used for OSHA compliance and risk management.']
  season_end_date date [note: 'Last date this shift is active for seasonal operations. Null for year-round shifts.']
  season_start_date date [note: 'First date this shift becomes active for seasonal operations. Null for year-round shifts.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift is only active during specific seasons or harvest periods. Used for workforce planning and H-2A visa scheduling.']
  shift_category varchar [note: 'Operational category indicating the primary work area or function for this shift.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the shift (e.g., DAY1, NIGHT, HARVEST-A). Used for external reference and scheduling systems.']
  shift_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the shift purpose, responsibilities, and operational context. Used for workforce communication and training.']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Morning Shift, Night Harvest, Processing Day Shift).']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift. Active shifts are available for scheduling, inactive are temporarily disabled, suspended are on hold pending review, archived are historical records.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift based on operational purpose. Regular for standard operations, seasonal for peak periods, harvest for crop collection, processing for facility operations, maintenance for equipment service, emergency for unplanned needs.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Standard start time for the shift in HH:mm format (24-hour clock). Represents the scheduled beginning of the work period.']
  weather_dependent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift is subject to cancellation or modification based on weather conditions (e.g., field harvest shifts).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this shift record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift. Referenced by shift_id.'
}

Table workforce.training_enrollment {
  training_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the training_enrollment association']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee who is enrolled in or attended the training event']
  training_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the training event session the employee is enrolled in or attended']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by this specific employee on the post-training assessment or exam. Per-employee outcome that belongs to the enrollment relationship, not the training event master record.']
  certificate_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a training completion certificate was issued to this specific employee upon completing this training event. Per-employee outcome moved from training_event to the enrollment association.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier printed on the training completion certificate issued to this specific employee for this training event. Per-employee outcome moved from training_event to the enrollment association.']
  ceu_credits decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit credits awarded to this specific employee upon successful completion of this training event. Per-employee outcome moved from training_event to the enrollment association.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date this specific employee successfully completed the training event. Per-employee outcome that belongs to the enrollment relationship, not the training event master record.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Per-employee completion status for this training event. Values: Enrolled (registered, not yet attended), Completed (attended and passed), Failed (attended but did not meet passing threshold), No-Show (registered but did not attend), Waived (exempted by supervisor). Belongs to the enrollment, not the training event master.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the employee was enrolled or registered for this training event. Belongs to the enrollment relationship, not to the employee or the training event master record.']
  gap_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has completed required Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) training as mandated by FSMA Produce Safety Rule. Required for all workers involved in covered produce activities including harvesting, packing, and handling. [Moved from employee: This boolean on employee is a denormalized summary flag. The authoritative per-event completion record now lives in training_enrollment.completion_status. This flag can be derived from enrollment records and should be removed from employee to avoid data inconsistency.]']
  haccp_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has completed Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) training. Required for employees working in food processing facilities, packing houses, and post-harvest handling operations under FDA and USDA FSIS regulations. [Moved from employee: Same as gap_training_completed — this is a denormalized summary flag on employee that duplicates what is now tracked per-enrollment in training_enrollment.completion_status. Should be derived, not stored on the employee master.]']
  osha_safety_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee holds a current OSHA safety certification (e.g., OSHA 10-hour or 30-hour General Industry or Construction card). Required for supervisory roles and equipment operators in farm and processing facility environments. [Moved from employee: Denormalized certification flag on employee that duplicates per-enrollment certificate_issued and completion_status in training_enrollment. Should be derived from enrollment records for OSHA-type training events.]']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Enrollment event between an employee and a training_event session. It captures the per-employee participation record for each training session attended, including enrollment, completion, assessment outcome, and certificate issuance. Each record links one employee to one training_event and holds all attributes that exist only in the context of that specific employee\'s participation in that specific session. Serves as the SSOT for individual compliance evidence required by FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., EPA Worker Protection Standard, SQF/BRC certification, and pesticide applicator license CEU renewal audits.. Existence Justification: In agricultural workforce management, employees attend multiple training events over their employment lifecycle (GAP, HACCP, WPS, OSHA, H-2A orientation, etc.), and each training event session is attended by multiple employees simultaneously. This is a genuine operational M:N relationship: the business actively manages individual enrollment records, tracks per-employee completion status, assessment scores, and certificate issuance for each training event, and must produce compliance evidence per employee per training event for FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., EPA WPS, and pesticide applicator license audits. The relationship is not derivable from existing FKs — it is an independently managed operational record.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the GL account record in the enterprise data lakehouse. Primary key for all financial posting references across AP, AR, fixed assets, and period-end close.']
  account_currency varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this GL account is maintained (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Accounts may be maintained in local currency, group currency, or hard currency. Supports multi-currency consolidation for international agricultural operations.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Grouping classification that controls the number range and field selection for GL accounts within SAP S/4HANA (e.g., current assets, fixed assets, operating expenses, COGS). Supports chart of accounts structuring and financial reporting hierarchies for crop enterprise EBITDA analysis.']
  account_long_description varchar [note: 'Extended narrative description of the GL account purpose, usage guidelines, and applicable business transactions. Supports SOX documentation requirements and auditor review.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The short descriptive name of the GL account as it appears in the chart of accounts and financial statements. Used for display in trial balance, P&L, and balance sheet reports.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric GL account number as defined in the chart of accounts (CoA). Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA G/L account number (SAKNR) and Oracle NetSuite account number. Used in all financial postings, journal entries, and regulatory filings.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account: active (available for posting), inactive (temporarily disabled), blocked (posting prevented per SOX controls), or archived (retired from use but retained for historical reporting). Drives account availability in financial transaction processing.']
  account_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the account type for agricultural enterprise reporting (e.g., current_asset, non_current_asset, current_liability, long_term_liability, operating_revenue, other_income, cogs, operating_expense, depreciation, interest_expense, tax_expense). Supports EBITDA line-item decomposition and CAPEX/OPEX segregation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current_asset|non_current_asset|current_liability|long_term_liability|operating_revenue|other_income|cogs|operating_expense|depreciation|interest_expense|tax_expense — promote to reference product]']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Fundamental classification of the GL account per the accounting equation: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Drives debit/credit normal balance behavior and financial statement placement. Corresponds to SAP account type (KTOKS) and Oracle NetSuite account type.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the chart of accounts to which this GL account belongs. An enterprise may maintain multiple charts of accounts (e.g., operational CoA, group CoA for consolidation). Corresponds to SAP KTOPL field.']
  commodity_allocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether costs or revenues posted to this GL account can be allocated to specific agricultural commodities (e.g., corn, soybeans, wheat, livestock). Enables commodity-level COGS and profitability analysis per crop enterprise. Supports USDA farm program reporting and Granular Farm Management integration.']
  consolidation_account_number varchar [note: 'The group-level GL account number used for intercompany elimination and consolidated financial statement preparation. Maps local chart of accounts entries to the group chart of accounts for IFRS 10 consolidation reporting. Corresponds to SAP ALTKT field.']
  cost_center_required boolean [note: 'When True, a cost center assignment is mandatory for all postings to this GL account. Ensures cost accountability by organizational unit (e.g., field operations, irrigation, crop protection, livestock). Supports crop enterprise budgeting and OPEX tracking in SAP CO.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost element classification: primary cost elements map directly to P&L GL accounts (e.g., seed costs, fertilizer COGS, labor); secondary cost elements are used for internal allocations and activity-based costing. Supports crop enterprise EBITDA reporting and COGS allocation by commodity.']
  cost_element_type varchar [note: 'Numeric or coded type of the SAP CO cost element (e.g., 1=primary costs/cost-reducing revenues, 3=accrual/deferral per surcharge, 11=revenues, 12=sales deductions, 21=internal settlement, 41=overhead rates, 42=assessment, 43=internal activity allocation). Drives cost allocation behavior in crop enterprise budgeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|3|11|12|21|41|42|43 — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA or Oracle NetSuite). Supports audit trail requirements under SOX Section 404 and GAAP/IFRS record-keeping standards.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date on which this GL account was deactivated or retired from active use. Null for currently active accounts. Supports chart of accounts lifecycle management and historical financial statement reconstruction.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this GL account became active and available for financial postings. Used for chart of accounts versioning and historical financial reporting. Supports GAAP/IFRS comparative period reporting.']
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'Controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this GL account. Corresponds to SAP FSTAG field. Ensures data completeness for cost center, profit center, and commodity allocation fields on agricultural financial postings.']
  financial_statement_section varchar [note: 'Indicates which primary financial statement this account appears in: balance sheet (B/S) for assets, liabilities, and equity; profit and loss (P&L) for revenues and expenses; cash flow statement; or statement of changes in equity. Critical for GAAP/IFRS dual-ledger reporting and EBITDA computation.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP functional area classification used for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., production, sales, administration, research and development, procurement). Enables P&L by function reporting per IFRS IAS 1 and supports crop enterprise EBITDA analysis by business function.']
  is_balance_sheet_account boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this GL account is a balance sheet account (True) or a profit and loss account (False). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances at year-end; P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings. Corresponds to SAP XBILK field.']
  is_capex_account boolean [note: 'Flags whether this GL account is designated for capital expenditure (CAPEX) tracking, including farm equipment purchases, land improvements, irrigation infrastructure, and precision agriculture technology investments. Supports CAPEX vs OPEX segregation for financial planning and tax depreciation.']
  is_cogs_account boolean [note: 'Flags whether this GL account is used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) allocation. Supports commodity-level COGS tracking for crop enterprises (e.g., corn, soybeans, wheat) and livestock operations. Critical for gross margin analysis and EBITDA reporting by crop enterprise.']
  is_ebitda_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is included in EBITDA calculations for crop enterprise performance reporting. Accounts flagged as EBITDA-relevant are included in operating income before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization line items. Supports management reporting and investor relations.']
  is_gaap_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is active and relevant under US GAAP reporting requirements. Supports dual-ledger GAAP/IFRS accounting where certain accounts may apply to one framework but not the other. Used in GAAP-basis financial statement preparation and SOX compliance.']
  is_ifrs_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is active and relevant under IFRS reporting requirements. Supports dual-ledger GAAP/IFRS accounting for consolidated financial statements filed with international regulators. Used in IFRS-basis financial statement preparation.']
  is_opex_account boolean [note: 'Flags whether this GL account is designated for operational expenditure (OPEX) tracking, including seed costs, fertilizer, crop protection chemicals, labor, and irrigation operating costs. Supports CAPEX/OPEX segregation for EBITDA reporting and crop enterprise budgeting.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is a reconciliation account for a subledger (AP, AR, fixed assets, inventory). Reconciliation accounts cannot be posted to directly; postings flow from the subledger. Corresponds to SAP MITKZ field. Critical for SOX financial controls.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this GL account record was most recently updated. Tracks chart of accounts maintenance activity and supports SOX change management controls and audit trail documentation.']
  line_item_display boolean [note: 'When True, individual line items posted to this GL account are stored and displayable for detailed audit trail and drill-down analysis. Corresponds to SAP XKRES field. Required for SOX audit trail requirements and financial controls documentation.']
  normal_balance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance per double-entry bookkeeping rules. Assets and expenses are normally debit; liabilities, equity, and revenues are normally credit. Used for trial balance validation and period-end close controls.']
  open_item_management boolean [note: 'When True, individual line items posted to this account are managed as open items that must be explicitly cleared (e.g., GR/IR clearing accounts, bank clearing accounts, advance payment accounts). Corresponds to SAP XOPVW field. Essential for AP/AR reconciliation and period-end close.']
  posting_block boolean [note: 'When True, prevents any new financial postings to this GL account. Used during period-end close, account restructuring, or SOX-mandated account freeze periods. Corresponds to SAP XSPEB field. Critical for financial controls and audit integrity.']
  profit_center_required boolean [note: 'When True, a profit center assignment is mandatory for all postings to this GL account. Ensures segment-level profitability reporting by crop enterprise, livestock operation, or business unit. Supports IFRS 8 operating segment disclosure and management reporting.']
  reconciliation_account_type varchar [note: 'When is_reconciliation_account is True, identifies which subledger this account reconciles: accounts payable (vendor), accounts receivable (customer), fixed assets, or inventory. Null or not_applicable for non-reconciliation accounts. Supports period-end close and SOX subledger-to-GL reconciliation controls.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'Three-digit code that determines the assignment field populated in line items posted to this GL account (e.g., 001=posting date, 003=document number, 009=external document number, 014=assignment field). Corresponds to SAP ZUAWA field. Controls how line items are sorted and displayed in account statements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this GL account master data originates: SAP S/4HANA FI module or Oracle NetSuite GL. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and reconciliation between ERP systems.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Specifies the tax relevance of the GL account for input/output tax determination (e.g., input tax, output tax, not tax-relevant, tax-exempt). Corresponds to SAP MWSKZ field. Relevant for agricultural input tax recovery on seed, fertilizer, and equipment purchases per applicable tax regulations.']

  Note: 'General Ledger (GL) chart of accounts master — the authoritative SSOT for all GL account definitions across the agricultural enterprise. Captures account number, account type (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), account group, cost element classification, P&L vs balance sheet indicator, currency, consolidation account mapping, IFRS/GAAP dual-ledger flags, and active status. Supports EBITDA reporting by crop enterprise, commodity-level COGS tracking, and SOX financial controls. Referenced by all financial postings across AP, AR, fixed assets, and period-end close.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the cost center record in the enterprise data platform (Silver Layer). Primary key for the cost_center data product.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: cost_center has company_code as a STRING. Cost centers belong to a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP CO. Normalizing to a proper FK ensures referential integrity between the controlling area']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the manager accountable for this cost center\'s budget and expenditures, as maintained in SAP SuccessFactors HR. Used for budget approval workflows, variance reporting, and SOX financial controls accountability. Stored as employee identifier, not personal name.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO activity type code representing the primary activity performed by this cost center (e.g., \'MACH\' for machine hours, \'LABOR\' for labor hours, \'IRRIG\' for irrigation hours, \'SPRAY\' for spraying operations). Used for activity-based cost allocation and internal price calculation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status of the cost center master record per SOX internal controls. Draft: initial creation not yet submitted. Pending_approval: submitted for authorization. Approved: authorized by finance controller and available for use. Rejected: returned for correction. Ensures segregation of duties and financial control compliance.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP business area code representing a distinct area of operations for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting (e.g., \'CROP\' for crop operations, \'LVST\' for livestock, \'SUPL\' for supply chain). Enables cross-company-code segment reporting per GAAP ASC 280 and IFRS 8.']
  capex_budget_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved annual CAPEX budget amount for this cost center in the controlling currency. Represents planned capital investments (equipment, infrastructure, land improvements) for the fiscal year. Supports CAPEX tracking and ROI analysis per GAAP ASC 360 / IFRS IAS 16.']
  commodity_type varchar [note: 'Primary agricultural commodity or livestock type associated with this cost center for COGS allocation and crop enterprise budgeting (e.g., \'corn\', \'soybeans\', \'wheat\', \'cattle\', \'hogs\', \'poultry\', \'cotton\', \'rice\'). Enables commodity-level profitability analysis and WASDE-aligned reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corn|soybeans|wheat|cattle|hogs|poultry|cotton|rice|other — promote to reference product]']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling Area code to which this cost center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. All cost centers within the same controlling area share the same chart of accounts and fiscal year variant.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the cost center indicating its primary business purpose. Drives overhead allocation rules, COGS allocation by commodity, and OPEX tracking. Values: production (direct crop/livestock production), administration (G&A overhead), research_and_development (R&D per GAAP ASC 730), field_operations (planting, harvesting, irrigation), supply_chain (logistics, warehousing), overhead (indirect shared services).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the cost center within the SAP S/4HANA CO controlling area. This is the business-facing identifier used in journal entries, purchase orders, and management reports (e.g., \'CC-FIELD-001\', \'CC-ADMIN-HQ\').']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the cost center\'s purpose, scope, and operational activities (e.g., \'Manages all field operations costs for the 5,000-acre corn production unit in Iowa, including planting, fertilization, crop protection, and harvest activities\'). Supports management reporting and organizational documentation.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the cost center as maintained in SAP S/4HANA CO master data (e.g., \'North Field Operations – Corn Belt\', \'Livestock Feed Management – Hog Unit 3\'). Used in financial reports and management dashboards.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center master record. Active: available for cost postings. Inactive: temporarily disabled. Locked: blocked for new postings (e.g., period-end close). Pending_approval: awaiting authorization per SOX controls. Archived: historical record no longer in use.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this cost center operates (e.g., \'USA\', \'BRA\', \'ARG\', \'CAN\'). Supports multi-national agricultural operations, IFRS/GAAP consolidated reporting, GDPR compliance for international operations, and country-of-origin tracking per COOL regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost center master record was first created in the source system or data platform. Supports audit trail requirements, SOX financial controls, and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
